# 3.29 DL Scheduler

## 3.29.0-3 DL Scheduler Document Owner Deputy Lucy Liu (ID: 11173642)

## 3.29.1 Scheduling Overview

### 3.29.1.0-1 Scheduling Overview Overview of the DL Scheduler Process (ID: 11173650)

**3.29.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173657`)

Scheduling Overview
11173657:
Scheduling Overview
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a UML activity diagram illustrating the DL Scheduler process.
The diagram starts with a filled circle labeled "[DL Scheduler START]".
From there, it transitions to a state labeled "DL Scheduler IDLE".
An event, "[Event triggers the Slot-based DL scheduler processing for the next slot]", leads from "DL Scheduler IDLE" to an action labeled "Slot Selection".
"Slot Selection" leads to a decision point.
If the condition "[SS Burst slot]" is met, the flow proceeds to an action labeled "PBCH Scheduler (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4096)".
From "PBCH Scheduler (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4096)", there is a transition labeled "[DL Data Subframe]" that leads to an action labeled "PDSCH/PDCCH Scheduler (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4097)".
After "PDSCH/PDCCH Scheduler (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4097)", the flow reaches a decision point.
From the "DL Scheduler IDLE" state, there is also a transition labeled "[otherwise]" that loops back to "DL Scheduler IDLE".
From the decision point after "PDSCH/PDCCH Scheduler (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4097)", there is a transition to a state labeled "[DL Scheduler END]".
The diagram concludes with a filled circle labeled "[DL Scheduler END]".
There is also a transition labeled "[otherwise]" from the decision point after "PDSCH/PDCCH Scheduler (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4097)" that loops back to the "DL Scheduler IDLE" state.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12741.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The trigger to start the slot-based processing of the DL scheduler is specified in 5G\_L2\_350\_replaced([11150971](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24fcSPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The slot type selection is specified in the corresponding chapter: subframe type Selection (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2293\_replaced([11114641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpmfaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Please check the validity TAG to select what is specified for each subfeature.

Basic NR Cell Utilization PM Counters are assessed (5G\_L2\_249\_replaced([11192308](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28i13_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

On a SS burst slot both PBCH scheduler and PDSCH/ PDCCH scheduler processing are executed.

### 3.29.1.0-2 Scheduling Overview Basic tasks of the PBCH Scheduler (3GPP) (ID: 11173661)

**3.29.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173669`)

Scheduling Overview
11173669:
Scheduling Overview
The top level of the PBCH scheduler is depicted in the following flow chart. The execution of the PBCH scheduler shall guarantee that the PBCH can be scheduled in the NR-slot which are reserved for the broadcast messages in DL. Broadcast Messages are scheduled on SS block NR-slot according to the NR-slot selection algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2295\_replaced([11114654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpmfbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart depicting a process related to SS burst slots and PBCH scheduling.
The flowchart begins with a rectangular box containing the text: "a SS Burst slot has been selected by the slot Selection Algorithm]".
Following this, there is an oval shape with the text "Beam Control for SS Burst Slot".
A diamond shape follows, representing a decision point. To the left of this diamond, a rectangular box contains the text: "[before 5GC000425-Q] Next SS block slot is slot #0 of SFN%4=0]". To the right of the diamond, another rectangular box contains the text: "[before 5GC000425-Q]otherwise]".
Below the diamond, there is an oval shape with the text "PBCH Scheduling and MIB Transmission".
Finally, another diamond shape is present, followed by a rectangular box containing the text "[PBCH Scheduling END]".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12743.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

It is for the PBCH scheduler to select the beam indices of the SS Burst inclusive the mapping to the corresponding pattern IDs and the scheduling of the MIB. Pattern ID and MIB data shall be forwarded finally to L1. For any detail refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4107\_replaced([11173746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1ePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The PBCH Scheduler is triggered each time that a SS Burst NR-slot is selected by the NR-slot selection algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2295\_replaced([11114654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpmfbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The beam control and PBCH scheduling (and sending of MIB information to 5G-L1-DL) is done for every SS Burst NR-slot

### 3.29.1.0-3 Scheduling Overview Basic tasks of the DL Scheduler (xPDSCH/xPDCCH) (ID: 11173674)

**3.29.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11173681`)

Scheduling Overview
11173681:
Scheduling Overview
The top level of the DL scheduler is depicted in the following flow chart. Execution of the DL scheduler shall be triggered for 5GTF-subframes/NR-slots which allow the transfer of user data in DL.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process.
The flowchart begins with a rounded rectangle labeled "Start".
From "Start", an arrow points down to a rectangular box containing the text "Evaluation of the available resources for dynamic allocation on xPDSCH".
From this box, an arrow points down to another rectangular box containing the text "DL scheduling of 5G UEs".
Finally, an arrow points down from the "DL scheduling of 5G UEs" box to a rounded rectangle labeled "End".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12745.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Basically, the DL scheduler subdivides the tasks into two steps and starts with the evaluation of the available resources on xPDSCH. Restrictions, which may come e.g. from a blanked sub-spectrum of the xPDSCH, shall be considered by the assignment of the physical resources to the 5G UEs.

After evaluation of the available resources the DL scheduler shall select the X-Pol beam, which is directed to the 5G UEs for which the resources shall be granted. In addition to the X-Pol beam selection, the DL scheduler shall determine the transport block size by means of the PRBs which are assigned by the DL scheduler to the 5G UEs, the transmission scheme of the DL transmissions, and the corresponding 5G UE-specific MCS. More details regarding the evaluation steps for the scheduling of 5G UEs are described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13518\_replaced([11173699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1cPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in the subsequent sections.

Note: the description provided in this requirement may go beyond what is supported in the first subfeatures. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13518\_replaced([11173699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1cPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall point to the detailed requirements which specify what has to be supported in each subfeature.

---

### 3.29.1.0-4 Scheduling Overview RF calibration support for DL (Before CNI-86060 (23R2)) (ID: 11173686)

**3.29.1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11173695`)

Scheduling Overview
[Before CNI-86060]

For digital RF beamforming, due to DL calibration requirements from RF, the DL scheduler shall consider an algorithm to reserve rdDlCalSlotNum slots of a minimum of rdDlCalMinSymbolNum consecutive symbols for DL calibration purpose within period of rdCalDuration seconds.

A minimum timing, rdCalMinTime (in ms), is needed between two consecutive slots carrying DL calibration symbols.

This RF calibration dedicated radio resource shall not be used for any data transmissions. The 5G-L2-PS shall send DlData\_PatternConfigReq message to 5G-L1-DL with parameter of calibrationBitmap to indicate which symbols shall be reserved for calibration in each 5GNR-slot, which shall be all symbols of a slot.

The TD scheduler shall use 5GNR data slots with data symbols for RF calibration, or 5GNR unused symbols in SSB slots if there are at least rdDlCalMinSymbolNum consecutives unused symbols (preferred solution).

5G-L2-PS shall maintain a counter slotCountForDlCalibration to trigger DL calibration actions. Counter shall be updated every scheduling period. At startup and every rdCalDuration seconds counter is reinitialized to rdDlCalSlotNum.

At first DL calibration opportunity (SSB slots in highest priority, or DL data slot), TD scheduler checks if a DL calibration is needed. A calibration is needed when slotCountForDlCalibration counter is not null and when previous DL calibration was triggered more than rdCalMinTime ms before. If a slot is used for DL calibration, counter slotCountForDlCalibration is decreased by 1.

rdCalDuration : see 5G\_L2\_3732\_replaced([11183498](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27j-afvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdDlCalMinSymbolNum : see 5G\_L2\_1047\_replaced([11191371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28dWRfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdDlCalSlotNum : see 5G\_L2\_3733\_replaced([11182139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27Y_TfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdCalMinTime : see 5G\_L2\_11402\_replaced([11183349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iwSfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For data symbols used as calibration symbols: TD scheduler could manage those calibration opportunities when no users are scheduled or enforce them if needed. 5G-PS-L2 shall set data symbols (Dd in frame format) for calibration in DlData\_PatternConfigReq message (in calibrationBitmap set bits associated to RF calibration symbols to 1). 5G-L2-PS shall transmit the message DlData\_PdcchSendReq only in case it contains a non-zero RB assignment and omit the message otherwise.

NOTE:

* UL and DL calibrations are independent of each other (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12925\_replaced([11194247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvz1FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UL).
* Any data scheduled together with calibration will be lost.

No-Beamforming is applied, therefore there is no need for RF calibration even for TDD.

If numOfSubSectors > 0, RF calibration not required.

[End CNI-86060]

Note: for the definition of DlData\_PatternConfigReq please refer to 5G\_L1\_IF\_54\_replaced([11512418](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRpM9_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and for DlData\_PdcchSendReq please refer to the 5G\_L1\_IF\_70\_replaced([11512527](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRqbEfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.29.1.0-5 Scheduling Overview Procedure for DL Scheduling of 5G UEs (ID: 11173699)

**3.29.1.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `13457861`)

Scheduling Overview
13457861:
Scheduling Overview
The scheduling of the 5G UEs in DL shall be processed in several steps on 5GTF-subframe/NR-Slot basis. An overview of the procedural steps is given generically by the subsequent figure:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process. The flowchart begins with a rounded rectangle labeled "Start". Following this, there are several rectangular boxes representing steps in the process. The first box reads "DL Candidate Set 1". The next box contains two lines of text: "DL Time Domain Scheduling" and "-> DL Candidate Set 2". After this, there is a box labeled "DL xPDCCH Scheduling". This is followed by a box that says "DL Frequency Domain Scheduling". The next box is labeled "xPUCCH Scheduling". Finally, the flowchart concludes with a rounded rectangle labeled "End". The boxes are arranged vertically, connected by implied downward arrows.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12750.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The following procedure applies for the DL scheduling of 5G UEs:

DL Candidate Set 1:

The set of 5G UEs selected by the DL Pre-Scheduling as candidate to be scheduled in DL (DL Candidate Set 1, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21950\_replaced([11173713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1cvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is the input to the DL Time Domain Scheduling.

DL Candidate Set 2:

The set of 5G UEs selected by DL time domain or [Before CB013712-A]spatial domain scheduling [End CB013712-A]as candidate to be scheduled in DL. It is the input to the DL frequency domain scheduling.

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false, 5G-L2-PS shall skip DL time domain scheduling, DL PDCCH scheduling and DL frequency domain scheduling in DL slots which are reserved for LTE according to the current DL sharing pattern, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2304\_replaced([11171916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). However, 5G-L2-PS shall perform PUCCH scheduling even in DL slots reserved for LTE in order to schedule PUCCH receptions for periodic CSI reports and SR.

DL Time Domain Scheduling:

The time domain scheduler shall evaluate the scheduling metric of all 5G UEs which are eligible for the scheduling in DL (Candidate Set 1).

For analog beamforming, a scheduling metric per x-pol beam is defined from all the 5G UEs that are served by the corresponding beam based on the scheduling metrics of the 5G UEs. The x-pol beam with the highest metric is then selected for scheduling. With the selection of the x-pol beam the set of 5G UEs, which are available for the resource assignment on PDSCH are determined. These are the 5G UEs which are accessible via the selected x-pol beam in the order of the scheduling metric.

For digital beamforming, all 5G UEs from candidate set 1 are available for the resource assignment on PDSCH.

In case PCCH data need to be scheduled, 5G-L2-PS shall refer 5G\_L2\_823\_replaced([11173532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26b9CvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and shall decide further on UE scheduling in current slot.

The 5G UE with the highest scheduling metric shall be taken into account first in FD scheduling continued by the 5G UEs in the order of descending scheduling metric. The 5G UEs which are in the position for the scheduling in FD scheduler form the DL Candidate Set 2. For any details refer to the dedicated chapter: DL Time Domain Scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4228\_replaced([11175135](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0mPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[Before CB013712-A]DL Spatial Domain Scheduling:

If NRBTS.NRCELL.actDlMuMimo in PsCell\_CellSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is true, all 5G UEs from candidate set 2 from time domain scheduling shall be handled by DL spatial domain scheduling for spatial domain multiplexity on DL. Multiple candidate set 2 (CS2) will be created by spatial domain scheduling if spatial domain multiplexity can be scheduled. [End CB013712-A]

DL xPDCCH Scheduling:

The xPDCCH scheduler allocates the resources (CCEs) on the physical DL control channel for carrying the grants dedicated to the 5G UEs, which are finally selected for FD scheduling. A pre-defined PDCCH format shall be used for carrying the grants to the 5G UEs. For any details refer to the dedicated chapter: DL PDCCH Scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4414\_replaced([11178004](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzvg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

DL Frequency Domain Scheduling:

The scheduling of the 5G UEs of Candidate Set 2 in FD scheduler includes details about the resource allocation in terms of number of allocated PRBs/RBGs as well as the location of the physical resources within the carrier, the transmission scheme, the MCS and hence the transport block size. The frequency domain scheduler is responsible for the allocation of the physical resources over all carriers and provides the allocation details per carrier. Both, the DL PDCCH scheduler and the PDSCH scheduler have to allocate the resources to the same 5GTF-subframe/NR-Slot. For any details refer to the dedicated chapter: DL Frequency Domain Scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4477\_replaced([11178984](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

xPUCCH Scheduling:

The xPUCCH scheduling is determined by the DL scheduler for the DL HARQ ACK/NACK signalling, the BSI and CSI reporting and SR signaling.

Note: This description is meant to provide an overview on the overall 5G UEs DL Scheduling flow. The detailed requirements in the dedicated chapters specify what has to be supported in each subfeature. The validity TAG at the top of each requirement indicates whether it is valid for a certain subfeature or not.

---

### 3.29.1.0-6 Scheduling Overview DL Pre-Scheduling of 5G UEs (ID: 11173713)

**3.29.1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11173723`)

Scheduling Overview
11173723:
Scheduling Overview
The schedulability check of the 5G UEs in DL shall be performed only when the status of any of the RRC CONNECTED 5G UEs change.

[vsd\_desc]=== Diagram Content ===
Start
Pre-scheduling:
Schedulability Check due to 5G UE status change
-> Candidate Set 1
End
Status change of the RRC CONNECTED 5G UEs
(Event driven - DL related)
=== Diagram Metadata ===
dc:creator: dems1196
Content-Type: application/vnd.visio[/vsd\_desc][vsd\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/attachments/m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file12752\_0.vsd[/vsd\_path]

The following procedure applies to the DL pre-scheduling of 5G UEs.

DL Pre-Scheduling:

The DL scheduler evaluates the fundamental schedulability of the RRC CONNECTED 5G UEs in the pre-scheduling. The evaluation shall be triggered whenever the conditions of any of the 5G UEs change. Examples for triggering the pre-scheduler are beyond other constrains the change of the buffer filling level (empty/non-empty) and the change of the 5G UEs’ synchronicity (out-of-sync/in-sync). The 5G UEs, which are eligible for scheduling shall be assigned to the candidate set:

* pre-scheduler candidate set.

In reverse, whenever a 5G UE isn’t any longer eligible for scheduling the corresponding 5G UE shall be removed from the above mentioned candidate set. From the pre-scheduler candidate set, a subset of UEs is added to the CS1 list which is the input for the TD scheduler. For any details refer to the corresponding DL Pre-Scheduling chapter of this document.

Note: This description is meant to provide an overview on the overall DL Pre-Scheduler flow. The detailed requirements in the DL Pre-Scheduling chapter (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4183\_replaced([11174302](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) specify what has to be supported in each subfeature. The validity TAG at the top of each requirement indicates whether it is valid for a certain subfeature or not.

---

### 3.29.1.0-7 Scheduling Overview DL Scheduling of 5G UEs per L2 PS instance with traffic prioritization (ID: 11173728)

**3.29.1.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11173736`)

Scheduling Overview
Required for the subsequent scheduler tasks is that the UE context for each UE contains the serving X-Pol beam index, see beam index definition in the beam management chapter (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2183\_replaced([11112736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZEBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: Neither analog nor digital beamforming is supported on FDD carriers.

Furthermore the pre-requisite is that the DL pre-scheduler has determined the set of active UEs (Candidate set 1) as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4183\_replaced([11174302](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Candidate set 1 according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is input for the TD scheduler.

The overall processing of the DL scheduler (in each L2 PS instance) shall be according to the following steps:

Step 1: Beam selection for analog beamforming by the TD scheduler

For analog beamforming, the TD scheduler determines the X-Pol beam (best beam) to be used in the next scheduling slot taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource request classes into account, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4251\_replaced([11175632](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The high level prioritization concept defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9465\_replaced([11174010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuXqrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the request class definition details are specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4148\_replaced([11174036](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuYRtfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that step 1 is skipped for digital beamforming since no beam selection is required.

This step does not apply to FDD.

[CB012734-E]  If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE, the step1 shall be skipped as beam selection for analog beamforming is already performed in Pre-scheduler as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27542\_replaced([11174581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudKOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB012734-E]

Step 2: UE selection and carrier assignment by the TD scheduler

For analog beamforming, the TD scheduler determines the set of UEs served by the best beam (rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected). [CB012734-E]  If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE, all UEs from candidate set 1 according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are considered. [End CB012734-E]

For digital beamforming, all UEs from candidate set 1 according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are considered.

For CA UEs with distributed PUCCH allocation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2885\_replaced([11123447](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqjhoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), all UEs with PUCCH resources for HARQ A/N (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2884\_replaced([11123433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqi6nfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are considered.

Out of those, for each carrier the set of UEs which are candidate to be scheduled in that carrier (CS2 carrier specific lists) is determined taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource request classes into account, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4258\_replaced([11175719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The high level prioritization concept defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9465\_replaced([11174010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuXqrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the request class definition details are specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4148\_replaced([11174036](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuYRtfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Overload Control may limit UE selection. See 5G\_L2\_1820\_replaced([11212319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-k2IvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

if NRBTS/NRDU/flexibleCAMode is set to NumberCellAmount (value 3) and there are two cells setup in the same L2-PS core :

* For CA FDD Pcell with distributed PUCCH allocation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2885\_replaced([11123447](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqjhoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), in DPA process slot of Pcell (Measurement Processing cycle in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2936\_replaced([11123865](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) may limit UE selection. detail to be decided in CP3 level design.
* serving cell may trigger overload control eariler compare with one cell per L2-PS core, since one cell per L2-PS core have more free time to handle other activity besides schedule (like different cell L2-PS message exchange , L2-PS interface message with other component handling), which will additional cut down available schedule time. detail to be decided in CP3 level design

Step 3: HARQ handling and logical channel prioritization and multiplexing per carrier

HARQ handling per carrier may be an integral part of the TD scheduler UE selection procedure as the UE selection procedure directly determines the UE that will have to perform retransmission on a carrier. HARQ handling is specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4525\_replaced([11184113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvP0a_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If a new transmission shall be done in a carrier the required scheduler task for the definition of logical channel prioritization and multiplexing shall be aligned to the request classes to create an overall consistent traffic prioritization concept. This is specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4557\_replaced([11186962](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXJRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

No HARQ logical channel handling and no retransmissions are needed for the artificial PDSCH load.

Step 4: DCI creation, PDCCH scheduling and PUCCH scheduling

According to the outcome of the TD scheduler the following scheduling tasks shall be executed:

For each carrier the DCI for each transmission on PDSCH shall be created and scheduled for transmission on the PDCCH in the same slot as the PDSCH transmission.

The PUCCH scheduling procedure shall assign feedback resources for HARQ A/N resources (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4563\_replaced([11187069](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvYXRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

This step is not applicable for the artificial PDSCH load.

On a FDD NR carrier, separate DCI  search spaces (USS) are used for DL and for UL.

---

## 3.29.2 Broadcast Control Channel scheduling for Master Information Block and System Information transmissions

### 3.29.2.1 PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission

#### 3.29.2.1.0-1 PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission SS-Block messaging and MIB messages (ID: 11173749)

**3.29.2.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12578089`)

PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission
The PBCH scheduler controls the transmission of a SS-Block including the proper broadcasting of the MIB messages.

L3 delivers the MIB (only the first 24 bits) to 5G-L2-PS via PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq message on cell basis (PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq is cell specific). 5G-L2-PS stores the MIB payload from PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq and takes it into use in the next SS burst slot.

The PBCH scheduler is run every SS burst slot, and shall send a DlData\_SsBlockSendReq message to 5G-L1-DL , or in case of **[CB009543-A] TDD** [End CB009543-A] supercell mode, multiple DlData\_SsBlockSendReq messages are sent to each L1 subcell ID configured for supercell,  to trigger the transmission of SS-Block(s) on a slot. For each cell for which L3 has delivered the MIB information, 5G-L2-PS shall do the following:\*\*\*\*

The first 24 bit (3 Bytes) of the MIB payload contains only IEs that are constant during the whole MIB TTI (80msec). 5G-L2-PS shall only update the field *systemFrameNumberMSB (Higher part of the System Frame Number;  Size = 6 bits) in this section of MIB, using sfnOffset and sfnLength IEs from PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq to locate it;* systemFrameNumberMSB  = floor(SFN/(2^4)).\*\*

The 8 last bits (1 Byte) of the MIB Payload are *L1 Signal Values corresponding to IE that could be modified during the MIB TTI periodicity namely 4 LSB of* systemFrameNumber, halfFrameIndicator , *ssb-IndexExplicit. 5G-L2-PS shall build the* L1 Signal Value part of the MIB as followed:\*\*\*\*

* Update the field *systemFrameNumberLSB (lower 4 bits of the System Frame Number) in the MIB, mapped on bits 4 to 7 of the Byte3.* systemFrameNumberMSB  = SFN%(2^4)\*\*
* Update the field *halfFrameIndicator (set to SubframeId/ 5) in the MIB,mapped on bit 3 of the Byte3. The subframeId is defined in 38.211, Chapter 4.*

**-for FR2 configuration :**

- Update the field *ssb-IndexExplicit (3 MSB of the SS block Index, i.e. (SS Block Index / 8)) in the MIB, mapped on bits 0 to 2 on the Byte3. The SS Block Index is given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1970\_replaced([11111961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

**- for FR1 configuration :**

- Update the field *KssbMSB in the MIB,mapped on bit 5 of the Byte3. set according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4110\_replaced([11173790](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuWchPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*

- Set the field *reserved in the MIB, mapped on bit 6-7 of the Byte3, to 0.*

* set the activeSsBlocks according to the number of SS block transmitted on this SS burst slot. activeSsBlocks is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4114\_replaced([11173803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuWch_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* copy the updated MIB in the dataPayload field.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | MIB Payload |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | *MSB* |  |  |  |  |  |  | *LSB* |  |
|  | ***MIB Payload with 80 msec of TTI*** |  |  |  |  |  |  |  | Byte0 |
|  | Byte1 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | Byte2 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| L1 signal  FR1 | *7* |  |  | *4* | *3* | *2* |  | *0* | Byte 3 |
| *systemFrameNumberLSB* |  |  |  | *HFI* | *ssb-indexExplicit* |  |  |  |  |
| L1 signal  FR2 | *7* |  |  | *4* | *3* | *2* | *1* | *0* | Byte 3 |
| *systemFrameNumberLSB* |  |  |  | *HFI* | *KssbMSB* | *reserved* |  |  |  |

SS block bursts are transmitted periodically on half-radioframe such that (10\*SFN) % (ssBurstSetPeriod) =ssBlockOffset and int(Subframe/5) = HalfFrameIndicator

With

 ssBurstSetPeriod  is set to accoding to the IE ssBurstSetPeriod from PsCell\_CellSetupReq

 ssBlockOffset is hardcoded to 0

 HalfFrameIndicator can be either 0 or 1 depending on MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/NRDSSLTE/ssbPosition. Otherwise, HalfFrameIndicator is hardcoded at 0.

SS block index mapping onto a Slot index is given in 5G\_UP\_IF\_1429\_replaced([11512163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tTpwjvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When Single SS block is transmitted on the cell, 5G-L2-PS shall only trasnmit SS Block Index =0.

**Note1: MIB payload is created at C-Plane layer, and has a size of 3 bytes. U-Plane appends 1 byte to it which contains SFN LSB, HFI and ssb-indexExplicit.**

**Note2: activeSsBlocks shall be set to 0x80 if only a single SS block is transmitted on the cell.**

**Note3: For the defination of PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_155\_replaced([10967642](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UT6hfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), DlData\_SsBlockSendReq (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_69\_replaced([11512511](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRp0B_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), PsCell\_CellSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

---

#### 3.29.2.1.0-2 PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission SS/PBCH using Precoding vector (ID: 11173770)

**3.29.2.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173773`)

PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission
Obsoleted by PR433819: SS/PBCH using Precoding vector switching (PVS)

---

**3.29.2.1.0-2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173778`)

PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission
When the SS/PBCH is transmitted over 2 antennas, the SS/PBCH are precoded using the Precoding vector switching (PVS).  PVS is an 5G-L1-DL algorithm that improves the SS/PBCH detection rate by switching of precoding vector for each SS/PBCH burst.

For this algorithm L1 needs the assistance of 5G-L2-PS to detect the first SS/PBCH block of the SS/PBCH burst :

* L2-PS needs to maintain a rrmPrecodingVectorIndex bit, initially it shall set to 1
* On the first SS/PBCH slot of a SS/PBCH burst, 5G-L2-PS shall toggle the rrmPrecodingVectorIndex bit. The new value of rrmPrecodingVectorIndex is computed as(rrmPrecodingVectorIndex +1) modulo 2.
* On any other SS/PBCH slot, 5G-L2-PS shall not change the rrmPrecodingVectorIndex bit.
* On any SS/PBCH slot, 5G-L2-PS shall set PrecodingVectorIndex IE of DlData\_SsBlockSendReq message ( 5G\_UP\_ALG\_84\_replaced([11095019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmh9S_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_91\_replaced([11095060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmikV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to 0.

With non-beamforming antenna applied in a cell, specifically when NRBTS.NRCELL.actBeamforming in DlCell\_SetupReq = FALSE as well as 4Tx in DL with NRBTS.NRCELL.dlMimoMode= {1, 3}  are met [CB008985-A] or MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actSupercell = True  [End CB008985-A] . The following requirement is applied:

* When the SS/PBCH is transmitted over 4 antennas, If rdSyncSignalsTxMode == 2, the SS/PBCH are precoded using the Precoding Vector Switching (PVS).  PVS is an 5G-L1-DL algorithm that improves the SS/PBCH detection rate by switching of precoding vector for each SS/PBCH burst, as specified in 5G\_L1\_5G\_UP\_ALG\_84\_replaced([11095019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmh9S_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_91\_replaced([11095060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmikV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For this algorithm, L1 needs the assistance of 5G-L2-PS to detect the first SS/PBCH block of the SS/PBCH burst. Specifically, when rdSynSignalsTxMode (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8454\_replaced([11110258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpB3qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) = 2 for SSB transmission on 4Tx antenna using PVS approach
* L2-PS needs to maintain a variable called rrmPrecodingVectorIndex, initially it shall set to 3.
* On the first SS/PBCH slot of a SS/PBCH burst, 5G-L2-PS shall update the value of rrmPrecodingVectorIndex. The new value of rrmPrecodingVectorIndex is computed as (rrmPrecodingVectorIndex +1) modulo 4.
* On any other SS/PBCH slot of a SS/PBCH burst, 5G-L2-PS shall not change the value of rrmPrecodingVectorIndex.
* On any SS/PBCH slot, 5G-L2-PS shall set PrecodingVectorIndex IE of DlData\_SsBlockSendReq message (5G\_UP\_ALG\_84\_replaced([11095019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmh9S_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_91\_replaced([11095060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmikV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with 0.

Note: for more detail about DlData\_SsBlockSendReq and precodingVectorIndex please refer to 5G\_L1\_IF\_69\_replaced([11512511](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRp0B_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.2.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11173783`)

PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission
SS/PBCH using Precoding vector

---

**3.29.2.1.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173787`)

PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission
On any SS/PBCH slot, 5G-L2-PS shall set PrecodingVectorIndex IE of DlData\_SsBlockSendReq (5G\_UP\_ALG\_84\_replaced([11095019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmh9S_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_91\_replaced([11095060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmikV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) message with 0.

[CB013440-A]

If NIDD parameters configuration NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans = True and (NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode = [Before CB013440-A-UPD002] 1 or 3 [CB013440-A-UPD002] 4 or 6 [End CB013440-A-UPD002], 5G\_UP\_13733\_replaced([11353784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucB9oPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ,

- gNB-DU shall indicate precoding of a pair of cross polarizations as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30439\_replaced([11095083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmikXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

gNB-DU shall support online change of NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans  and NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode. [End CB013440-A]

#### 3.29.2.1.0-3 PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission cellWithSib1 handling (ID: 11173790)

**3.29.2.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12521534`)

PBCH scheduling and MIB transmission
If the SIB1 is not transmitted on the cell (5G\_CP\_OTH\_993\_replaced([11608652](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y4w3XPwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and for frequency carrier [Before CB009173-B]lower than 6GHz[CB009173-B] in FR1[End CB009173-B], 5G-L2-PS shall set KssbMSB to 1.

If the SIB1 is transmitted on the cell (5G\_CP\_OTH\_993\_replaced([11608652](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y4w3XPwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and for frequency carrier [Before CB009173-B]lower than 6GHz[CB009173-B] in FR1[End CB009173-B], 5G-L2-PS shall set KssbMSB to the value of the fifth bit of kSSB counting from the LSB (i.e. to the value of kSSB right shifted by 4 bits).

Note: CNI-57700 remove the celltype online change. The only way left to update the Kssb/cellWithSib1 is via PsCell\_CellSetupReq.

Note: In [Before CB009173-B]above 6GHz[CB009173-B] FR2[End CB009173-B], kSSB has a size of 4 bits transmitted on the L3 part of MIB. It is up to C-Plane to insure those bits are correctly set.

* For [Before CB009173-B]below 6Ghz[CB009173-B] FR1[End CB009173-B], kSSB has a size of 5 bits splits such as the 4 LSBs of kSSB are transmitted on the L3 part of the MIB (part set by C-PLAN), and the 5th bit, corresponding to the MSB of kSSB, is transmitted along the L1 Signal part of MIB.
* **KkSSB is also used to indicate to the UE whether the SIB is transmitted or not on the cell: a value of  Kssb higher than 11 in [Before CB009173-B]above 6GHz[CB009173-B]FR2[End CB009173-B] and higher than 23  for [Before CB009173-B]below 6GHz[CB009173-B]FR1[End CB009173-B] indicates an absence of SIB1.**

---

##### 3.29.2.1.1 Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz

##### 3.29.2.1.1-1 Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz

**3.29.2.1.1-1.0-4**  (ID: `11173908`)

Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz
In case of

rrmBeamformingType =digitalBf  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1886\_replaced([11111439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) AND NRBTS.NRCELL.actBeamforming == TRUE AND NRBTS.NRCELL.beamSet.nrBtsBeamRefinementP2== TRUE

5G-L2PS shall trigger 5G-L1-DL to transmit CSI-RS for beam refinement.

For this purpose, in each slot transmitting SS/PBCH blocks, the message DlData\_CsiRsSendReq shall be sent to the L1 subcell which is dedicated to transmit CSI-RS for beam refinement.

To select which subcell is used to transmit CSI-RS for beam refinement, see 5G\_L2\_41\_replaced([11141431](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23ogsfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before CB009175-A]

NOTE: The configuration with a single C2 subcell is not supported.

[CB009175-A]

NOTE: The configuration with C2 subcell is no more supported.

[End  CB009175-A]

NOTE: The time allocation of SS/PBCH blocks within the slot depends on the parameter ssBlockConfiguration, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_57\_replaced([11094835](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmgvIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for CaseB and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_59\_replaced([11094846](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmgvIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for CaseC.

Content of message DlData\_CsiRsSendReq :

Global Parameters:

subcellId - Id of the L1 subcell intended to transmit CSI-RS for beam refinement:

* See 5G\_L2\_41\_replaced([11141431](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23ogsfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

sfn - System Frame Number

slot - Current SS/PBCH block

numOfCsiResources - 4 \* number of SS/PBCH blocks within current slot.

Note:

* If number of actiavted SS/PBCH (nActivatedSsPbch, see 5G\_UP\_82\_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is even, numOfCsiResources is always 8, otherwise, the last SS/PBCH slot transmits only a single SS/PBCH block and numOfCsiResources = 4 for this slot.
* In case of additional CSI-RS for other purpose, this number has to be increased accordingly.

Parameters per CSI-RS Resource (repeated for each CSI-RS resource, i.e., numOfCsiResources times)

startSymbol - to be stepped through all symbols used for SS/PBCH block transmission. The symbols used for SS/PBCH block transmission are different for odd and even slots, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_57\_replaced([11094835](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmgvIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The number of SS/PBCH blocks being transmitted is given by the number of activated SS/PBCH (nActivatedSsPbch, see 5G\_UP\_82\_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

csiRsScramblingSequenceInt - Physical Cell ID

density - as given by parameter NRBTS.NRCELL.csirsBeamMgmt.csirsBmMgmtDensity

densityDot5PrbLocation - shall be left into default value. Parameter is only valid for density 0.5 which is not used for beam management

if NRBTS/NRCELL-actSpecificBwpSize is false and (actPRBBlanking is false or nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL=0), then

startPrb - as given by the parameter csirsBeamMgmt.startPrb; it has to be 0 + (multiple of 4)

if NRBTS/NRCELL-actSpecificBwpSize is true, then

startPrb - as given by the parameter csirsBeamMgmt.startPrb; It isn't fixed to 0, the csirsBeamMgmt.startPrb should follow start position of specific BWP.

if actPRBBlanking is true and nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL>0, then

startPrb - as given by the parameter csirsBeamMgmt.startPrb; It isn't fixed to 0, the csirsBeamMgmt.startPrb should follow start position of BWP and for the largest dedicated BWP (n=1) case should be aligned with nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL.

numOfPrb - as given by the parameter csirsBeamMgmt.numOfPrb; it has to be a multiple of 4

csiRsConfig - Configuration table row number in 38.211, table 7.4.1.5.2-1. For L1 RSRP measurement and single antenna port, it is always row 1. In 5G18A, no other value is supported for CSI-RS transmission for beam refinement (although it can be different for CSI-RS transmission for other purpose).

freqDomainAllocationKi - Bitmap; the content shall be derived from parameter NRBTS.NRCELL.csirsBeamMgmt.csirsBmMgmtReIndex such that this index gives the first bit to be set to 1; see also 38.211, subclause 7.4.1.5.3 as well as 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2674\_replaced([11303102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqixyPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) which defines the very same setting for the RRC reconfiguration.

csiTransmitPower - Transmit power of CSI-RS expressed in relative dBFS, see [5G\_UP\_ALG\_2576\_replaced([11118605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDybfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))].

pwrReductionPerCsiRsResource\_dB - set to 0 in operational mode but can be set to other values for testing purpose; in that case, the value shall be configurable by I&V and read from respective configuration setting.

trsInfo - set to false

NOTE: For Compilation of message for pattern IDs see 5G\_L2\_484\_replaced([11155945](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248IOfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 3.29.2.1.1.0-1 Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz Overview (ID: 11173803)

**3.29.2.1.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11173817`)

Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz
The number of SS/PBCH blocks to be transmitted, number of activated SS/PBCH (nActivatedSsPbch, see 5G\_UP\_82\_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), can be derived from the basic beam set, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1933\_replaced([11111686](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpPTD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) but is also provided at cell setup with message PsCell\_CellSetupReq.

If a cell is configured in supercell mode [CB009543-A] in TDD [End CB009543-A], then

parameters to be provided in DlData\_SsBlockSendReq messages are listed in the 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 SS/PBCH block sections below.

[CB009543-A]

If a cell is configured in supercell mode in FDD, then

parameters to be provided in DlData\_SsBlockSendReq messages are listed in the 1 SS/PBCH block sections below.

[End CB009543-A]

otherwise

parameters to be provided in DlData\_SsBlockSendReq message are listed in the 1, 2, 4, 6, 8 SS/PBCH block sections below.

The setting of the remaining parameters of this message do not depend on the actual SS/PBCH block.

---

###### 3.29.2.1.1.0-2 Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz 1 SS/PBCH block (ID: 11173820)

**3.29.2.1.1.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11173835`)

Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz

# of SS burst sets = 1

[Before CB011031-A]

1. SS burst sets (SS/PBCH ID 0):

activeSsBlocks: 0x80

threeLsbSsbIndex: 0

for FDD, the above is the only setting valid for all bandwidths.

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE, threeLsbSsbIndex is 2 according to CPlane configuration (5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_5166\_replaced([11361928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wZeXJ_vjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[End CB011031-A]

[CB011031-A]

For FR1 TDD:

If NRCELL.ssbPositionsInBurst is not configured or NRCELL.ssbPositionsInBurst = "0000 0001":

1. SS burst sets (SS/PBCH ID 0):

activeSsBlocks: 0x80

threeLsbSsbIndex: 0

Else if NRCELL.ssbPositionsInBurst = "0000 0100" (applicable to FR1 TDD only):

1. SS burst sets (SS/PBCH ID 2):

activeSsBlocks: 0x80

threeLsbSsbIndex: 2

Else

Other SS Burst set configurations are not supported yet

For FR1 FDD:

1. SS burst sets (SS/PBCH ID 0):

activeSsBlocks: 0x80

threeLsbSsbIndex: 0

The above is the only setting valid for all bandwidths.

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE, threeLsbSsbIndex is 2 according to CPlane configuration (5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_5166\_replaced([11361928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wZeXJ_vjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[End CB011031-A]

Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz

# of SS burst sets = 1

1. SS burst set (SS/PBCH IDs 0 and 1):

activeSsBlocks: total 8 bits, the most significant 2 bits equal to the value (11b & (! beamsMuted) ), others set to 0

threeLsbSsbIndex: 0

threeLsbSsbIndex: 0, (first ss Block index in a slot is 0)

threeLsbSsbIndex: 0, (first ss Block index in a slot is 0)

###### 3.29.2.1.1.0-5 Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz 4 SS/PBCH blocks (ID: 11173855)

**3.29.2.1.1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11173860`)

Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR1 below 6GHz

# of SS burst sets = 2

[CB008985-A] If supercell mode is not enabled [End CB008985-A]

1. SS burst set (SS/PBCH IDs 0 and 1):

activeSsBlocks: total 8 bits, the most significant 2 bits equal to the value (11b & !beamsMuted[0..1]), others set to 0

threeLsbSsbIndex: 0

1. SS burst set (SS/PBCH IDs 2 and 3):

activeSsBlocks: total 8 bits, the most significant 2 bits equal to the value (11b & !beamsMuted[2..3]), others set to 0

threeLsbSsbIndex: 2

[CB008985-A]

If supercell mode is enabled, then

1. SS burst sets (SS/PBCH ID 0):

activeSsBlocks: 0x80

threeLsbSsbIndex: 0, (first ss Block index in a slot is 0)

1. SS burst sets (SS/PBCH ID 1):

activeSsBlocks: 0x40

threeLsbSsbIndex: 0, (first ss Block index in a slot is 0)

1. SS burst set (SS/PBCH IDs 2):

activeSsBlocks: 0x80

threeLsbSsbIndex: 2, (first ss Block index in a slot is 2)

1. SS burst set (SS/PBCH IDs 3):

activeSsBlocks: 0x40

threeLsbSsbIndex: 2, (first ss Block index in a slot is 2)

[End CB008985-A]

# of SS burst sets = 3

[CB008985-A] If supercell mode is not enabled [End CB008985-A]

activeSsBlocks: total 8 bits, the most significant 2 bits equal to the value(11b & !beamsMuted[0..1]), others set to 0

threeLsbSsbIndex: 2

activeSsBlocks: total 8 bits, the most significant 2 bits equal to the value (11b & !beamsMuted[4..5]), others set to 0

##### 3.29.2.1.2 Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR2 above 6GHz

###### 3.29.2.1.2.0-1 Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR2 above 6GHz Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks (ID: 11173919)

**3.29.2.1.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13478991`)

Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR2 above 6GHz
13478991:
Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR2 above 6GHz
The activated (nActivatedSsPbch) and not muted SS/PBCH (see definition in 5G\_UP\_82\_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be transmitted in all slots for which configuration assumes presence of the SS/PBCH blocks.
The 5G-L2-PS compute the slot in a radio frame (rfSlotIdx)) to broadcast a SS/PBCH as follows:
[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
rfSlotIdx = | (halfFrameOffset + ssPbchStartSymb(ssPbchIdx)) \* 2μcommon / (14 \* 2μssPbch) |
The formula calculates `rfSlotIdx` based on several variables: `halfFrameOffset`, `ssPbchStartSymb`, `ssPbchIdx`, `μcommon`, and `μssPbch`. The absolute value is taken of the entire fraction.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12797.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
With
[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "μcommon". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letter "μ" is a Greek letter, mu, and it is followed by the English word "common".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12797.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]:
Numerology associated to the Sub Carrier Spacing of the channel bandwidth and defined at the NIDD level.
[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a table with two columns. The first column is labeled "Numerology" and the second column is labeled "$SCS\_{common}$ (kHz)". The table has four rows of data.

| Numerology | $SCS\_{common}$ (kHz) |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | 15 |
| 1 | 30 |
| 2 | 60 |
| 3 | 120 |
| [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "µs.SPbch" in a blurry, light-colored font against a white background. The text is horizontally aligned and appears to be a single line.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12797.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]: |  |
| Numerology associated to the Sub Carrier Spacing of SS/PBCH. |  |
| [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a table with two columns and five rows. The first column is labeled "Numerology" and contains the numbers 0, 1, 3, and 4. The second column is labeled "SCSs/PBCH (kHz)" and contains the numbers 15, 30, 120, and 240. |  |

| Numerology | SCSs/PBCH (kHz) |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | 15 |
| 1 | 30 |
| 3 | 120 |
| 4 | 240 |
|  |  |
| [image-5][image\_desc]halfFrameOffset[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12797.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]: |  |
| Number of OFDM symbol corresponding to the half frame of SS/PBCH SCS according to the Half Frame Indicator (HFI) added by the 5G-L2-PS in the dataPayload parameter of the DlData\_SsBlockSendReq message. |  |
| [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation defines a variable called `halfFrameOffset` using a piecewise function. |  |
| The equation is: |  |
| `halfFrameOffset = {` |  |
| `0, HFI = 0` |  |
| `5 x 14 x 2^μssPbch, HFI = 1` |  |
| `}` |  |
| The equation states that `halfFrameOffset` is equal to 0 when `HFI` is 0. When `HFI` is 1, `halfFrameOffset` is equal to the product of 5, 14, and 2 raised to the power of `μssPbch`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12797.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] |  |
| [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "ssPbchStartSymb" in a blurry, italicized font. The text is dark gray and appears on a light, slightly yellow background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12797.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]: |  |
| The first OFDM symbol index for candidate SS/PBCH block determined according to the SS/PBCH SCS, the SS/PBCH mapping case and the activated (nActivatedSsPbch) and not muted SS/PBCH (see 5G\_UP\_82\_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The SS/PBCH which are configured but not activated or activated and muted are not scheduled, but the Resource Elements overlapped by them are not allowed for any traffic. |  |
| For the SS/PBCH mapping case D, the first OFDM symbol index is computed as follows |  |
| [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in an italicized font. The formula is: |  |
| $ssPbchStartSymb(ssPbchIdx) = 4 \times (1 + ssPbchIdx \mod 4) + 4 \times \left\lfloor \frac{ssPbchIdx \mod 4}{2} \right\rfloor + 28 \times \left( \left\lfloor \frac{ssPbchIdx}{4} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{ssPbchIdx}{16} \right\rfloor \right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12797.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8] |  |
| For the SS/PBCH mapping case E, the first OFDM symbol index is computed as follows |  |
| [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation is: |  |
| `ssPbchStartSymb(ssPbchIdx) = 8 + 4 * ssPbchIdx mod 8 + 4 * floor(ssPbchIdx mod 8 / 4) + 56 * (floor(ssPbchIdx / 8) + floor(ssPbchIdx / 32))`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12797.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9] |  |
|  |  |
| Note: SS/PBCH transmission is requested from L1 via DlData\_SsBlockSendRequest (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4108\_replaced([11173749](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1efvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Physical Broadcast Channel Send') only for the nActivatedSsPbch. |  |
| [CB013712-B] Not valid for NewFR1 [End CB013712-B] |  |

**3.29.2.1.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173969`)

Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR2 above 6GHz

* groupPresence
* ssBlockConfiguration (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_1991\_replaced([11273930](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpEyEfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/beamSet-beamsMuted

**3.29.2.1.2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13477612`)

Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR2 above 6GHz
For analog beamforming for eCPRI RUs, L2 PS shall indicate beam of the corresponding SSB beam through ceAxcIndex and patternId in DlData\_SsBlockSendReq as:

* ceAxcIndex eAxC index of SSB for D2 subcell type according to  5G\_UP\_1972\_replaced([11377324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueVqtPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4705\_replaced([11193210](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvqrJPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* patternId map beam Id to pattern Id according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1909\_replaced([11111524](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpOE7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

13478993:
Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for FR2 above 6GHz
Supported configurations are indicated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1903\_replaced([11111484](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpOE5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
Slots available for 64 SS Block opportunities as specified in 3GPP are shown below.
SS/PBCH block opportunities numbered 0 to nActivatedSsPbch - 1 are used. 
nActivatedSsPbch is the number of activated SS/PBCH (see 5G\_UP\_82\_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**.**

## 3.29.3 DL Traffic prioritization concept

### 3.29.3.1 Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types

#### 3.29.3.1.0-1 Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types DL TD scheduler request classes and prioritization concept (ID: 11174010)

**3.29.3.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174014`)

Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types
The DL TD scheduler decisions shall be based on a prioritization concept using (resource) request classes for different traffic types:

The concept is based on the following principles:

· The resource request classes shall represent sets of RNTIs with different scheduling priorities. The scheduling priorities depend on the type of resource that has to be scheduled.

· The request classes are strictly ordered according to their request class priority, i.e. RNTis in request classes with higher priority are prioritized over RNTIs in request classes with lower priority.

· RNTIs in higher request classes are prioritized over RNTIs in lower request classes independent of their request class subpriorities.

· RNTIs within the same request class are ordered according to different request class sub-priorities. The concept for this ordering depends again on the different traffic request classes.

· RNTIs with higher request class sub-priority within a request class are prioritized over RNTIs with lower request class sub-priority.

· The request class priority will be also re-used in Bearer Prioritization and Multiplexing (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4537\_replaced([11185465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvTew_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

A resource request class is 'component carrier specific', if its TD-metric (request class prio + sub-prio) is component carrier specific.

A resource request class is 'not component carrier specific' if its TD-metric (request class prio + sub-prio) is not component carrier specific.

Note that for non CA UE the differentiation of 'component carrier specific' or 'not component carrier' specific is not required.

A 5GUE can be a member of zero, one or more request classes per slot. Depending on the dynamic scheduling decisions and traffic properties the membership of a 5GUE, may change in every slot.

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9891\_replaced([11174018](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuYRsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) the list of DL resource request class priorities.

Note:   
For future releases additional request classes will be added e.g. to support extended QoS concepts and related different priorities will be assigned to the extended set. This might change the defined priorities from above.

---

#### 3.29.3.1.0-2 Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types Full list of TD resource request class priorities (ID: 11174018)

**3.29.3.1.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13355404`)

Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types
Please find as below the list of resource request class priorities used:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Request class** | **Request class priority** | **O&M parameter for request class priority** | **Component Carrier specific / Not Component Carrier specific** | **Request class description** |
| systemInformation | 101 | None | Component Carrier specific | Request class for the management of the System Information broadcasts (SIB1/SI-Msgs). The prioDlSib1AndSiMsg is hardcoded in the 5G-L2-PS. Refer to [11174046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuYRt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |
| paging | 100 | None | Component Carrier specific | Request class for the management of the paging broadcasts. The prioDlSib1AndSiMsg is hardcoded in the 5G-L2-PS. Refer to [11174054](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuYRufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |

| harqRetransDrb | 80 | prioDlHarqRetransDrb | Component Carrier specific | HARQ retransmission for non-GBR, deprioritized 5QI1/65/66 VoIP and deprioritized non-5QI1 GBR. Refer to [11174143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuZf0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |
| dlMacCe | 70 | prioDlMacCe | Component Carrier specific | DL MAC CE: TAC DL MAC CE: SCell Activation/Deactivation DL MAC-CE: TCI state Indication for UE-specific PDCCH Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4168\_replaced ([11174160](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuZf1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. |

Refer to [13355606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dJcJ6PBUEfC3ocOh_n7w7A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for details [End CB014699-B]. |

Refer to [13355654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_XETQQfBXEfC3ocOh_n7w7A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for details [End CB014699-B]. |
| criticalGbrTraffic | 64 | prioDlCriticalGbrTraffic | Component Carrier specific | [CB014699-C] Critical GBR excluding 5QI1/65/66.  Refer to [13365675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_y96RkfaQEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for details [End CB014699-C]. |
| criticalDrbTraffic | 62 | prioDlCriticalDrbTraffic | Component Carrier specific | [CB014699-F] Critical DRB traffic. Refer to [13393326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_lHKq4wZMEfGozuGzRUXHpg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for details [End CB014699-F] |
| srb1Traffic | 60 | prioDlSrb1Traffic | Component Carrier specific | SRB1 traffic 5QI5/69 SIP Signaling Refer to [11174170](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuZf1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |
| srb3Traffic | 55 | prioDlSrb3Traffic | Component Carrier specific | SRB3 traffic. Refer to [11174182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuZf2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |
| srb2Traffic | 50 | prioDlSrb2Traffic | Component Carrier specific | SRB2 traffic Refer to [11174192](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuZf2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |
| dcGbrTraffic | 47 | prioDlDcGbrTraffic | Component Carrier specific | DC-GBR traffic. Refer to [11174200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuZf3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |
| voipTraffic | 45 | prioDlVoipTraffic | Component Carrier specific | 5QI1 conversational voice traffic OR 5QI 65/66 Mission critical voice traffic. Refer to [11174210](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |
| gbrTraffic | 43 | prioDlGbrTraffic | Component Carrier specific | GBR excluding 5QI1/65/66.  Refer to [11174223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |
| drbTraffic | 40 | prioDlDrbTraffic | Component Carrier specific | DRB traffic (currently only one QoS) Refer to [11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details. |
| deprioritizedVoip | 25 | prioDlDeprioritizedVoip | Component Carrier specific | Deprioritized [Before CB014699-B] 5QI1/65/66 conversational [CB014699-B] critical voip traffic [End CB014699-B] / deprioritize voip traffic. Refer to [11174262](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for details. |
| deprioritizedGbr | 20 | prioDlDeprioritizedGbr | Component Carrier specific | [CB014699-C] Deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-C], deprioritized DC-GBR and deprioritized GBR excluding 5QI1/65/66**. **O&M parameter for request class priority are hidden parameters; the values are restricted to the value defined in** Request class priority column.\*\*\*\*

[CB014699-B] Note: SRB3 is not prioritized for UE with critical traffic [End CB014699-B].

---

### 3.29.3.2 Determination of the Request Classes

#### 3.29.3.2.0-1 Determination of the Request Classes For all request classes (ID: 11174036)

**3.29.3.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174041`)

Determination of the Request Classes
UEs are ordered according to their request class sub-priorities. In case the request class subpriorities are equal those UEs with equal request class sub-priorities are ordered in random order.

Notes:

there is no SRB traffic in 5G18A NSA systems, thus, request classes harqRetransSrb1, harqRetransSrb2, srb1Traffic, srb2Traffic are not applied.

For NSA call, there is no support of RACH Msg4. Thus, the request class harqRetransMsg4 is not supported in NSA mode.

For NSA operation, srb3Traffic and harqRetransSrb3 are supported in user configured SgNB subcells cells.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-2 Determination of the Request Classes Request class systemInformation (ID: 11174046)

**3.29.3.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11174051`)

Determination of the Request Classes
There is no sub-priority management for the common channels. When a set to SI-RNTI are present for a given slot (associated to different SS/PBCH indexes), all resources PDCCH/PDSCH are booked and availlable in the SI scheduling contexts.

Note: The common channel (SI-RNTI) must have the highest priodity, as their scheduling could not be delayed and their presences in the slot enforce the selection of the SS/PBCH indexes allowed.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-3 Determination of the Request Classes Request class paging (ID: 11174054)

**3.29.3.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11174058`)

Determination of the Request Classes
There is no sub-priority management for the common channels. When a set to P-RNTI are present for a given slot (associated to different SS/PBCH indexes), all resources PDCCH/PDSCH are booked and availlable in the Paging scheduling contexts.

Note: The common channel (P-RNTI) must have the highest priodity after the one of the SI-RNTI, as its scheduling could not be delayed and its presence in the slot enforces the selection of the SS/PBCH indexes allowed.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-4 Determination of the Request Classes Request class rachMsg2 (ID: 11174063)

**3.29.3.2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13370272`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the transmission of RACH Msg2 (RAR). This set of UEs creates the request class rachMsg2. [CB015279-A] This request class shall also include the RAR that are created by the Scell and sent to Pcell. Such RAR messages are created during CFRA, which, in turn was initiated by PDCCH order [End CB015279-A] Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time to the end of the RAR response window and the peakMetric as sub-priority for the request class rachMsg2. The time to the end of the RAR response window should have a higher weight, i.e. it shall be inversely proportional to remaining time until the end of the window. This could be reached as follows:

sub-priority = (M \* x) + y

where x is an integer which increases as the UE approaches to the end of the RAR response window, M is the maximum possible peakMetric + epsilon (small float value), and y is the peakMetric. Typical values are M=1 and epsilon = 0.001.

peakMetric(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1615\_replaced([11107907](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is the reported received power of the PRACH .

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-5 Determination of the Request Classes Request class harqRetransMsg4 (initial Tx and reTx) (ID: 11174072)

**3.29.3.2.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11174080`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending Msg4 transmissions in the subcell cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransRach for Msg4 (re-)transmission UEs. Within this set the time to the end of the contention resolution window determines the sub-priorities. SRB0 [CNI-81991] or SRB1 [End CNI-81991] messages combined with Msg4 shall be treated in the same class as Msg4 transmission. Standalone SRB0 messages shall be treated with the lowest (zero) sub-priority.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-6 Determination of the Request Classes Request Class pdcchOrder (ID: 13445827)

**3.29.3.2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `13445863`)

Determination of the Request Classes
[CB015279-A]

UEs eligible for PDCCH order shall belong to this priority class. If multiple are eligible for PDCCH order, then they shall be prioritized based on when a free preamble is requested for them. This means earlier a preamble is requested higher will be their corresponding priority among UEs that are eligible for PDCCH order. Refer to [13345951: Timing Alignment via CFRA](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_L9hUMOSSEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). The priority of the request class is given in  [11174018: Full list of TD resource request class priorities](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuYRsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[End CB015279-A]

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-7 Determination of the Request Classes Request class criticalHarqRetransSrb1 (ID: 13355435)

**3.29.3.2.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `13355436`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending critical HARQ retransmissions with Msg4/RRCReconfiguration messages meant for RRC re-establishment, SRB1/SRB2 or 5QI5/69 SIP signaling content in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalHarqRetransSrb1 for critical SRB1/SRB2 retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of Msg4/RRCReconfiguration messages meant for RRC re-establishment, critical SRB1/SRB2 and critical 5QI5/69 HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities within the set.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-8 Determination of the Request Classes Request class criticalHarqRetransVoip (ID: 13355439)

**3.29.3.2.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `13355440`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending critical VoIP(5QI-1/65/66) retransmissions in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalHarqRetransVoip for critical VoIP retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities, i.e. the sub-priority is higher when more retransmissions have already been performed.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-9 Determination of the Request Classes Request class criticalHarqRetransGbr (ID: 13365666)

**3.29.3.2.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `13365667`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending critical GBR (excluding 5QI-1/65/66) retransmissions in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalHarqRetransGbr for critical GBR retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of critical HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities, i.e. the sub-priority is higher when more retransmissions have already been performed.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-10 Determination of the Request Classes Request class criticalHarqRetransDrb (ID: 13393162)

**3.29.3.2.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `13393163`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending critical non-GBR retransmission in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalHarqRetransDrb for critical non-GBR. Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities, i.e. the sub-priority is higher when more retransmissions have already been performed.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-11 Determination of the Request Classes Request class harqRetransSrb1 (ID: 11174085)

**3.29.3.2.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11174088`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending HARQ retransmissions with Msg4/RRCReconfiguration messages meant for RRC re-establishment, SRB1 **or 5QI5 SIP signaling content in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransSrb1 for SRB1 retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of Msg4/RRCReconfiguration messages meant for RRC re-establishment, SRB1 and 5QI5 HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities within the set.**

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-12 Determination of the Request Classes Request class harqRetransSrb3 (ID: 11174092)

**3.29.3.2.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `11174097`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending HARQ retransmissions with SRB3 content in the subcell cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransSrb3 for SRB3 retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of SRB3 HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities within the set.

For NSA 3X operation with SgNB, harqRetransSrb3 is 'Component Carrier Specific', as for srb1 and srb2.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-13 Determination of the Request Classes Request class harqRetransSrb2 (ID: 11174101)

**3.29.3.2.0-13.0-1**  (ID: `11174105`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending SRB2 retransmissions in the subcell cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransSrb2 for SRB2 retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of SRB2 HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-14 Determination of the Request Classes Request class harqRetransDcGbr (ID: 11174110)

**3.29.3.2.0-14.0-1**  (ID: `11174113`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending prioritized DC-GBR retransmissions in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransDcGbr for DC-GBR retransmission UEs. Within this set, the subpriorities are defined by the delay term, calculated according to the head of line packet delay of the MAC-PDU waiting for re-transmission.

The head-of-line (HOL) packet delay of the MAC-PDU waiting for re-transmission shall be the sum of the HOL delay for the DC-GBR DRB at the time of scheduling the initial transmission (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30375\_replaced([11175600](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) plus the delay accumulated since then until the time of scheduling the PDSCH retransmission slot

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-15 Determination of the Request Classes Request class harqRetransVoip (ID: 11174118)

**3.29.3.2.0-15.0-2**  (ID: `11174127`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending VoIP [Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] retransmissions in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransVoip for [Before CB008118-A]5QI-1[CB008118-A]5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] VoIP retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities, i.e. the sub-priority is higher when more retransmissions have already been performed.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-16 Determination of the Request Classes Request class harqRetransGbr (ID: 11174130)

**3.29.3.2.0-16.0-2**  (ID: `11174139`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending [Before CB008118-A]non-5QI-1[CB008118-A] non-VoIP [End CB008118-A] GBR retransmissions in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransGbr for [Before CB008118-A] non-5QI-1 [CB008118-A] non-5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] GBR retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities, i.e. the sub-priority is higher when more retransmissions have already been performed.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-17 Determination of the Request Classes Request class harqRetransDrb (ID: 11174143)

**3.29.3.2.0-17.0-3**  (ID: `11174156`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending [Before CB008118-A]non-GBR, RAT1(dlMacCe) retransmission(including NBR category and normal non-GBR category),deprioritized 5QI-1 VoIP  or deprioritized GBR [CB008118-A] non-GBR, RAT1(dlMacCe) retransmission(including NBR,MC-nGBR (5QI70) and normal non-GBR category),deprioritized 5QI-1/65/66 VoIP or Congestion deprioritized GBR (to distinguish the negative token bucket deprioritized GBR) [End CB008118-A][CB008600-B]or deprioritized DC-GBR[End CB008600-B] retransmissions in the cell.  This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransDrb for non-GBR, deprioritized 5QI-1 VoIP, RAT1(dlMacCe) retransmission,  and deprioritized GBR [CB008600-B]and deprioritized DC-GBR[End CB008600-B] retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities, i.e. the sub-priority is higher when more retransmissions have already been performed. As an exception, sub-priority of RAT1 retransmission shall be set to highest in this request class.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-18 Determination of the Request Classes Request class dlMacCe (ID: 11174160)

**3.29.3.2.0-18.0-1**  (ID: `11174165`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the transmission of a DL MAC CE (for TAC and SCell Activation/Deactivation) or the transmission of a DCI format 1\_1 indicating a DL BWP different from the active DL BWP. This set of UEs creates the request class dlMacCe.

Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time since the MAC CE transmission request or the BWP switch indication was received by the scheduler: oldest MAC CE or BWP switch indication trigger received shall be sent first (FIFO principle).

Note: the supported DL MAC CEs are: UL TAC.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-19 Determination of the Request Classes Request class criticalSrb1Traffic (ID: 13355606)

**3.29.3.2.0-19.0-1**  (ID: `13355607`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of critical SRB1/SRB2 or critical 5QI5/69 SIP signaling in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalSrb1Traffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since critical SRB1/SRB2 or critical 5QI5/69 data arrived in the buffer. The oldest request shall have the highest priority. Note: 5QI-69 signaling is used to provide Mission Critical push to talk services (i.e., 5QI-5 is not used for signaling)

criticalSrb1Traffic traffic class is 'component carrier specific': Critical SRB TD-Metrics are calculated only for the Pcell and a UE is a candidate only in the CS2 List of the PCell, with such a priority.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-20 Determination of the Request Classes Request class criticalVoipTraffic (ID: 13355654)

**3.29.3.2.0-20.0-1**  (ID: `13355655`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of critical VoNR(5QI1/65/66) bearer in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalVoipTraffic.  Within this class, the scheduler assigns sub-priorities based on the time the head-of-line VoIP packet has resided in the UE's buffer. The oldest packet shall have the highest priority.

Note: Critical VoNR(5QI-1/65/66) traffic shall have priority over non-critical VoNR traffic or non-GBR (critical or non-critical) traffic. 5QI5 SIP signaling is required to establish and maintain the VoNR(5QI-1) call. 5QI69 SIP signaling is required to establish and maintain Mission critical PTT (5QI65/66) call and both 5QI5 and 5QI69 maintain the voice traffic and shall have the same priority as SRB1 signaling.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-21 Determination of the Request Classes Request class criticalGbrTraffic (ID: 13365675)

**3.29.3.2.0-21.0-1**  (ID: `13365676`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of critical GBR data**(excluding 5QI-1/65/66) in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalGbrTraffic. Within this class the sub-priority is based on the critical GBR scheduling criteria (refer to [11175420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For the UE with multi critical GBR bearers, the sub-priority is based on the scheduling criteria of the critical GBR bearer with the highest GBR Delay metric.**

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-22 Determination of the Request Classes Request class criticalDrbTraffic (ID: 13393326)

**3.29.3.2.0-22.0-1**  (ID: `13393327`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the first transmission of critical non-GBR data in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalDrbTraffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the proportional fair metric calculated for the UE based on the channel quality of the cell and the total critical DRB traffic sent in the past on any cell. The proportional fair metric is calculated according to [11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[CB014699-D] Nominal bit rate configuration is ignored (if configured) for critical non-GBR bearers.

If a critical GBR bearer (positive token) is de-prioritized due to GBR congestion, it is treated as critical non-GBR traffic and moved to the criticalDrbTraffic request class [End CB014699-D].

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-23 Determination of the Request Classes Request class srb1Traffic (ID: 11174170)
