**3.29.3.2.0-23.0-2**  (ID: `11174179`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of SRB1 or [Before CB008118-A] 5QI5 [CB008118-A] 5QI5/69 [End CB008118-A] SIP signaling in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class srb1Traffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since SRB1 or [Before CB008118-A]5QI5[CB008118-A] 5QI5/69 [End CB008118-A] data arrived in the buffer. The oldest request shall have the highest priority.

[CB008118-A]Note: 5QI-69 signalling are used to provide Mission Critical push to talk services(i.e., 5QI-5 is not used for signaling)[End CB008118-A]

srb1Traffic traffic class is 'component carrier specific': SRB TD-Metrics are calculated only for the Pcell and a UE is a candidate only in the CS2 List of the PCell, with such a priority. Anyway the scheduling of SRB is not restricted to the Pcell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4557\_replaced([11186962](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXJRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details). Note: the alternative of candidating the UE also in its Scells with SRB priority would be more time-consuming as it requires (once SRB is scheduled by one CC) to reduce the UE priority in the CS2 lists of the other CCs (and re-sort the lists).

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-24 Determination of the Request Classes Request class srb3Traffic (ID: 11174182)

**3.29.3.2.0-24.0-1**  (ID: `11174187`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the first transmission of SRB3 in any of the allocated subcell cell. This set of UEs creates the request class srb3Traffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since SRB3 data arrived in the buffer. The oldest request shall have the highest priority.

srb3Traffic traffic class is 'component carrier specific': SRB TD-Metrics are calculated only for the Pcell and a UE is a candidate only in the CS2 List of the PCell, with such a priority. Anyway the scheduling of SRB is not restricted to the Pcell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4557\_replaced([11186962](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXJRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details). Note: the alternative of candidating the UE also in its Scells with SRB priority would be more time-consuming as it requires (once SRB is scheduled by one CC) to reduce the UE priority in the CS2 lists of the other CCs (and re-sort the lists).

Note: SRB3 is introduced in SgNB for NSA 3X operation. Theoretically, SRB1 and SRB3 have the same/identical priority level, and currently SRB1 and SRB3 will not happen simultaneously for a UE. From practical implementation point of view, in order to differentiate the priority level of SRB1 and SRB3, here we gave different value of Request class priority.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-25 Determination of the Request Classes Request class srb2Traffic (ID: 11174192)

**3.29.3.2.0-25.0-1**  (ID: `11174197`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the first transmission of SRB2 in any of the allocated subcells cells. This set of UEs creates the request class srb2Traffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since SRB2 data arrived in the buffer. The oldest request shall have the highest priority.

srb2Traffic traffic class is 'component carrier specific': SRB TD-Metrics are calculated only for the Pcell and a UE is a candidate only in the CS2 List of the PCell, with such a priority. Anyway the scheduling of SRB is not restricted to the Pcell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4557\_replaced([11186962](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXJRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details). Note: the alternative of candidating the UE also in its Scells with SRB priority would be more time-consuming as it requires (once SRB is scheduled by one CC) to reduce the UE priority in the CS2 lists of the other CCs (and re-sort the lists).

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-26 Determination of the Request Classes Request class dcGbrTraffic (ID: 11174200)

**3.29.3.2.0-26.0-1**  (ID: `11174204`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of DC-GBR bearer in any of the allocated cells.  This set of UEs creates the request class dcGbrTraffic.  Within this class the sub-priorities are based on DC-GBR scheduling criteria.  See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30364\_replaced([11175564](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj35_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

[CB008600-F]

Note: DC-GBR data traffic takes priority and is not subject to the DTX optimization mechanism controlled by NRBTS/NRCELL/actDtxOptimizedScheduler.

[END CB008600-F]

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-27 Determination of the Request Classes Request class voipTraffic (ID: 11174210)

**3.29.3.2.0-27.0-2**  (ID: `11174218`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] radio bearer in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class voipTraffic.  Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the length of time that the head of line VoIP packet has been in the UE's buffer. The oldest packet shall have the highest priority.

Note: [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] conversional voice traffic shall have priority over non-GBR DRB traffic. 5QI5 SIP signaling is required to establish and maintain the voice call. [CB008118-A] 5QI69 SIP signalling is required to establish and maintain Mission critical PTT (5QI65/66) call [End CB008118-A], [Before CB008118-A] and [CB008118-A] both 5QI5 and 5QI69 [End CB008118-A] maintain the voice traffic and shall have the same priority as SRB1 signalling.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-28 Determination of the Request Classes Request class gbrTraffic (ID: 11174223)

**3.29.3.2.0-28.0-3**  (ID: `11174236`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of GBR data [Before CB008118-A] (excluding 5QI-1) [CB008118-A] (excluding 5QI-1/65/66) [End CB008118-A] in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class gbrTraffic. Within this class the sub-priority is based on the GBR scheduling criteria. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8015\_replaced([11175420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. For the UE with multi GBR bearers, the sub-priority is based on the scheduling criteria of the GBR bearer with the highest GBR Delay metric.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-29 Determination of the Request Classes Request class drbTraffic (ID: 11174242)

**3.29.3.2.0-29.0-8**  (ID: `13365308`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the first transmission of DRB data in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class drbTraffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the proportional fair metric calculated for the UE based on the channel quality of the ~~subcell~~ cell and the total DRB traffic sent in the past on any ~~subcell~~ *cell. The proportional fair metric is calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

This class shall be divided into multiple categories and with order:

1- NBR Category: if NRCELL.actNbrForNonGbrBearers = TRUE, this category shall be additionally created and contain the UE having nonGBR bearers with DL nominal bitrate configured and with positive token bucket, and not de-prioritized according to NBR congestion control algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22592\_replaced([11183359](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvJtzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , which sub-priorities are based scheduling metric, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21642\_replaced([11175514](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kujQ2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: When NRCELL.actNbrForNonGbrBearers = TRUE and NRBTS.NRCELL.actTdHighBufUEPrio=DL or DLAndUL, buffer length alone can be used as the selection criterion for high-buffer UEs, regardless of NBR prioritization or token availability. As such, a high-buffer UE could be an NBR bearer or a non-GBR bearer, depending solely on buffer status.

2- Pairing group category for SB BF UEs: if NRCELL.actDlMuPairingGroup = TRUE, all SB BF UEs with request class drbTraffic shall be placed under these categories if they are in pairing groups and selected by the bucket algorithm defined in [12447143: Bucket algorithm for selecting DL pairing groups](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EGVwwAmBEfCT35inaBGo7g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

3- Large buffer non-GBR Category:

* If NRCELL.actLargeBufferUe is equal to DL or ULAndDL, the UEs which satisfy the following conditions shall be added into this Category:
* Total amount of buffered non-GBR data is larger than NRCELL.dataBufferThresholdHighDl, and
* Rank used shall be NOT smaller than rdRankThresholdDl (5G\_UP\_ALG\_33200\_replaced([11194162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvzOBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and
* Proposed MCS rrmDlProposedMcs calculated with full BW allocation shall be NOT smaller than rdMcsThresholdDl (5G\_UP\_ALG\_33198\_replaced([11194152](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvzOAvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* The gNB shall remove the UE from this Category on the following condition:
* Total amount of buffered non-GBR data is smaller than or equal to NRCELL.dataBufferThresholdLowDl.
* When NRCELL.dataBufferThresholdLowDl is set to 0, it means UE will be kept as "Large buffer non-GBR Category" until all the data buffer have been drained. After UE is removed from "Large buffer non-GBR Category", the PF-metric is re-initialized. The sub-priority scheduling criteria for large buffer non-GBR category, please refer to [12687136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_WIKNEVVUEfC45IKMrv0u7Q).
* The deprioritized NBR due to congestion is not qualified for Large buffer non-GBR category.
* The deprioritized GBR due to congestion is not qualified for Large buffer non-GBR category.
* **Note: Radio link quality is not checked for the exit of "Large buffer non-GBR Category" status to avoid the risk of ping-pong by radio link quality fluctuation.**

4- Normal nonGBR Category: all other UEs with nonGBR bearers, which sub-priorities are based on the proportional fair metric calculated for the UE based on the channel quality of the cell and the total DRB traffic sent in the past on any cell. The proportional fair metric is calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Congestion deprioritized GBR shall be treated in the normal nonGBR category and have specific scheduling weight: rdCongDepriorGbrSchedulWeight for SchedWeightFactor defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22255\_replaced([11175240](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For rdCongDepriorGbrSchedulWeight refer to (5G\_UP\_ALG\_31697\_replaced([11193461](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [CB015725-B]For DL ES, this applies only when rdDlEsEnh( refer to [13365298: rdDlEsEnh](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_XH4FAfZzEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is equal to True.[End CB015725-B]

If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0, HwVariant used is not ABIL, and the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31301\_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is greater than or equal to the value set for **activation threshold of rdNominalBitrateOlcDlPerCent (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31829\_replaced([11216807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxa-pPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then, the NBR category bearers are deprioritised in bulk to normal nonGBR category, irrespective of the NBR congestion control algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22592\_replaced([11183359](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvJtzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

When the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31301\_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is below the value set for **deactivation threshold of rdNominalBitrateOlcDlPerCent (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31829\_replaced([11216807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxa-pPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then all those bearers that were NBR category previously, or are newly setup during the overloaded duration when the countermeasure is active, are eligible for NBR categorisation and are repriorised as per individual scheduling metric and availability of data in buffer.**

Note: Within drbTraffic UEs, If UE uses Distributed PUCCH Allocation Algorithm (based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2885\_replaced([11123447](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqjhoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), since the PUCCH resource is already allocated, considering k1 value as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2473\_replaced([11116092](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), these UEs are given priority over other drbTraffic UEs.

If *NRBTS/NRCELL/actDtxOptimizedScheduler  is set to true, the TD scheduler shall not assign this request class based on the above conditions but on the algorithm described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8232\_replaced([11174950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kufmc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) instead.*

Note: The prioritization of NBR configured nonGBR bearers is only applicable within drbTraffic class. If *NRBTS/NRCELL/actDtxOptimizedScheduler is set to true and if UEs having NBR bearers with positive token bucket are part of dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic class, they are considered same as other normal nonGBR traffic UEs within that class. They are again prioritized only after the UE is moved back to drbTraffic class.*

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-30 Determination of the Request Classes Request class deprioritizedVoip (ID: 11174262)

**3.29.3.2.0-30.0-3**  (ID: `13374180`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that do not meet the packet aggregation criteria (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4356\_replaced ([11177988](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzvgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and require the first transmission of 5QI1/65/66 or [CB014699-B] Critical 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB014699-B] radio bearer in any of the allocated cells.  This set of UEs creates the request class deprioritizedVoip. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the length of time that the head of line VoIP packet has been in the UE's buffer.  The priority is highest for packets that have been in buffer longest.

Note: Deprioritzed VoIP [CB014699-B] (Critical or non-critical) [End CB014699-B] traffic that has not met the packet aggregation criteria and shall have priority lower than non-GBR DRB traffic.  Deprioritized VoIP traffic that is being HARQ retransmitted will have priority harqRetransDrb.

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-31 Determination of the Request Classes Request class deprioritizedGbr (ID: 11174274)

**3.29.3.2.0-31.0-4**  (ID: `13377800`)

Determination of the Request Classes
The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of deprioritized GBR [CB014699-C] or deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-C] data (excluding 5QI1/65/66) in any of the allocated cells.  Deprioritized GBR/[CB014699-C] deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-C] traffic is data for GBR bearer(s) that has/have a negative token bucket,i.e. the bearer(s)' s data transmission rates had/have exceeded the bearer(s)’s GBR value. **This set of UEs creates the request class deprioritizedGbr.**  Within this class the sub-priority is based on the GBR scheduling criteria (refer to[11175420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

Deprioritized DC-GBR traffic is data for DC-GBR bearer(s) and the bearer(s)' s data transmission rates have exceeded the bearer(s)’s GFBR value (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30379\_replaced([11175610](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This set of UEs is contained in request class deprioritizedGbr. Within this class the sub-priority is based on the DC-GBR scheduling criteria. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30365\_replaced ([11175567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj36PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

[CB014699-C] The sub-priority metric for the deprioritized critical GBRs (excluding critical 5QI-1/65/66) is computed according to their GBR scheduling criteria, see ([11175420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for details [End CB014699-C].

For the UE which belongs to deprioritizedGbr due to multiple DRBs (DCGBR/GBR), the sub-priority is based on the scheduling criteria of the bearer (GBR/DCGBR) with the highest GBR/DC-GBR delay metric.

[Before CB014699-C]

Note: Deprioritized GBR is non-5QI 1/65/66 GBR traffic that has a negative token bucket, i.e. the bearer’s transmission rate has exceeded the bearer’s GBR value that was provided by the AMF.  Deprioritized GBR traffic being HARQ retransmitted will have harqRetransDrb priority.

[CB014699-C]

Note: Deprioritized GBR (non 5QI1/65/66) or deprioritized critical GBR (critical non 5QI-1/65/66) traffic that has a negative token bucket, i.e. the bearer’s transmission rate has exceeded the bearer’s GBR value that was provided by the AMF. Deprioritized GBR or deprioritized critical GBR traffic being HARQ retransmitted will have harqRetransDrb priority [End CB014699-C].

---

#### 3.29.3.2.0-32 Determination of the Request Classes Request class dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic (ID: 11174294)

**3.29.3.2.0-32.0-1**  (ID: `11174297`)

Determination of the Request Classes
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actDtxOptimizedScheduler is set to true, the TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the first transmission of non-GBR DRB data (except 5QI5) in any of the allocated subcells cells. This set of UEs creates the request class dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic and drbTraffic based on the algorithm described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8232\_replaced([11174950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kufmc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The PF metric shall be used as sub-priority is the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic class as well. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the proportional fair metric calculated for the UE based on the channel quality of the subcell and the total DRB traffic sent in the past on any subcell. The proportional fair metric is calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actDtxOptimizedScheduler is set to true, UEs in prioDlDtxOptimizedDrbTraffic priority class shall not be scheduled unless one of the conditions described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8234\_replaced([11174999](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kufmf_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) applies or unless one of the conditions described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9471\_replaced([11175687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is verified. UEs in prioDlDtxOptimizedDrbTraffic priority class are in a transition state and shall be switched to prioDlDrbTraffic priority class based on the conditions defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8231\_replaced([11174946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kufmcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic class is component carrier specific.

More details on this process can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8231\_replaced([11174946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kufmcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

## 3.29.4 DL Pre-Scheduling

### 3.29.4.0-1 DL Pre-Scheduling DL Scheduling restrictions under Remote Interference Mitigation (ID: 11174307)

**3.29.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174311`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
RIM DL muting and UL overload restrictions for DL scheduling

---

**3.29.4.0-1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12643422`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
In the RIM mitigated slots [CB014153-B] for which all PDSCH symbols are mitigated [End CB014153-B] where RIM DL muting shall be applied (i.e.: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29888\_replaced([11161119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktSFjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ‘Determine RIM DL muting applicability’ conditions are fulfilled) or in the special slots where PDSCH skipping in special slot due to UL overload is applied (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32690\_replaced([11216120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) PDSCH transmission shall not be scheduled except the following type of traffic: SIBs, Paging, Msg2, Msg4 [05568582-26R2]or if the DCI is a DCI-based BWP switch ([11111082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) as there is no PDSCH linked to this DCI.[End 05568582-26R2]

If**RIM DL muting shall be applied  [CB014153-B] for all PDSCH symbols of the slot [End CB014153-B] and**the NRRIM\_PROFILE.highPrioRbTransInMutedSlots is set to true, then additionally any UE with transmission including request class equal to or higher than gbrTraffic (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9891\_replaced([11174018](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuYRsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Full list of TD resource request class priorities') can be scheduled.\*\*\*\*

Note: see the rationale of DL mitigation in 5G\_UP\_5478\_replaced([11360543](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucvIOfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11174332`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
RIM DL power reduction restrictions for DL scheduling

---

**3.29.4.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11174337`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
In the RIM DL mitigated slots where RIM DL power reduction shall be applied (i.e.: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29878\_replaced([11161157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktSslPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ‘Determine RIM DL power reduction applicability’ conditions are fulfilled), the UEs which fulfils at least one of the following conditions shall be excluded from scheduling:

* UEs with TA (as defined in  5G\_UP\_ALG\_18019\_replaced([11150417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksTOG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ‘computation of NTA’) higher than determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29909\_replaced([11161178](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktSsmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Determine RIM DL power reduction parameters for transmission'.
* UEs for which MCS calculated according to CQI without the correction related to RIM DL power reduction (so, effectively, CQI being the rrmCorrAvgCqi+rrmRIMReductionCqi where rrmCorrAvgCqi is as provided 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7557\_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and rrmRIMReductionCqi as provided by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29926\_replaced([11126166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV_vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is lower than determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29909\_replaced([11161178](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktSsmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Determine RIM DL power reduction parameters for transmission'.

---

### 3.29.4.0-2 DL Pre-Scheduling DL Scheduling restrictions for tdLte frame structure (ID: 12789896)

**3.29.4.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12789897`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
For the last two DL slots of the 5GC001116 tdLte frame structure with a 0 ms shift pattern, when the parameter NRCELL.actTwoDlSlotsMutInTdLte is set to true, L2PS shall not schedule PDCCH and PDSCH transmissions, except for the following types of traffic:

* SIBs
* Paging
* Msg2
* Msg4
* [05568582-26R2]DCI is a DCI-based BWP switch ([11111082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) as there is no PDSCH linked to this DCI.[End 05568582-26R2]

[CNI-152230-A] Note: this requirement is initially introduced via CNI-152230-A and afterwards backported to earlier release. [End CNI-152230-A]

---

### 3.29.4.0-3 DL Pre-Scheduling Pre-scheduler Candidates Based on Buffer Utilization (ID: 11174341)

**3.29.4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11174346`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
The DL Pre-scheduler shall assign the 5G UEs to the pre-scheduler candidate Set for which 5G-L2-LO indicates a buffer utilization greater than 0. In case the DL transmission buffer of the 5G UE exhausts and 5G-L2-LO indicates a buffer utilization of 0, the DL Pre-scheduler shall remove the corresponding 5G UE from the pre-scheduler candidate set.

It shall assume that a UE's DL buffer utilization is greater than 0 if there is at least one DL buffer level with bufferedBytes > 0 associated with the UE's CRNTI. The DL Pre-scheduler shall assume that a UE's DL buffer utilization is 0 if there is no DL buffer level with bufferedBytes > 0 associated with the UE's CRNTI. The DL buffer level is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: Exceptions for beam failure recovery are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22395\_replaced([11174363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: Linked to both 5GC000533 and 5GC000535 features to avoid creating a dependency on their mutual implementation order.

According to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2670\_replaced([11120289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqPYmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for CA UEs over SCell 5G-L2-PS, DL pre-scheduler shall assign the 5G UEs in Scell active state to the pre-scheduler candidate set in case UE's DL buffer utilization on Scell side is greater than 0 for DRB bearers, remove the corresponding UEs from the pre-scheduler candidate set in case UE's DL buffer utilization is 0.

---

### 3.29.4.0-5 DL Pre-Scheduling Measurement Gap (MG) handling for SA (ID: 11174355)

**3.29.4.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11174359`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
UE is not eligible to DL scheduling between "starting time of MG (subframe) –(minus) K0 for DL –(minus) mgta" and "starting time of MG (subframe) +MGL-mgta" to control the DL transmission during MGL period(refer Table 9.1.2-4: in 3GPP 38.133 to calculate number of interrupted slots).

UE is not eligible to DL PDSCH transmission between "starting time of MG (subframe) - ceiling(K1 for DL ACK/NACK \* (scs of PDSCH / scs of PUCCH)) - mgta" and "starting time of MG (subframe) + MGL-mgta” to control the DL ACK/NACK in UL(refer Table 9.1.2-4: in 3GPP 38.133 to calculate number of interrupted slots). Note: For UE over P(S)Cell both scs of PDSCH and PUCCH are same to P(S)Cell scs, for UE over Scell scs of PUCCH will be P(S)Cell scs considering PUCCH over PCell only, while scs of PDSCH will be Scell scs.

Whenever UE receives new v which is different from current one, UE will setup the new received measurement gap and perform the measurement accordingly.

5G-L2-PS sends PsUser\_UserModifyResp to CP-RT to ACK/NACK corresponding measurement gap setup.

When 5G-L2-PS receives a PsUser\_UserModifyReq with measurement gap released configured (MeasConfig presents, MeasGapConfigStatus = 0), measurement gap indicated by the previously received measGapConfig should be released.

L2-PS shall take this configuration in to effect when the relevant RRC Reconfiguration Complete (PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq) is received to avoid scheduling complexity between L2-PS and UE due to MG.

* once UE is able to receive RRC reconfiguration --> UE is not in MGL period (while the time of receiving MeasCapConfigStatus = 0 at L2-PS shall be no later than the time of receiving RRC reconfiguration with MeasGapConfig release at UE), MG configuration will be released.

Since feature interaction with Carrier Aggregation (CA) feature is considered for future sub-features, K0/K1 values shall be decided from PSCell configuration in this sub-feature. This requires further tuning when CA is considered.

For FDD-TDD(FR1) CA, K0 is always 0, while available K1 are determined according 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4595\_replaced([11187331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvaMe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PCell and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4598\_replaced([11187352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvaMf_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Scell.

If a UE uses Distributed PUCCH Allocation Algorithm as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2884\_replaced([11123433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqi6nfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), K1 is determined for each of the Serving cells based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2939\_replaced([11123879](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: PsUser\_UserModifyReq/Resp please refer to the 5G\_L2\_IF\_447\_replaced([10975990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEIdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for the PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq definition see 5G\_L2\_IF\_542\_replaced([10977666](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VN5fPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and for the measGapConfig please see 5G\_L3\_5360.

---

### 3.29.4.0-6 DL Pre-Scheduling Beam failure recovery - DL data scheduling (ID: 11174363)

**3.29.4.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `12464191`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
[Before CNI-137616-B] If rdActCr3574 == 1 (5G\_L2\_1340\_replaced([11187360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28AqU_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): [End CNI-137616-B]

After a beam failure recovery, if a MAC CE PDCCH TCI State Indication has to be scheduled as defined in the requirement  [11153186: TCI update for UE according to dedicated configuration state](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksk64PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), L2-PS shall suspend any DL scheduling by CORESET ID ≠ 0 and suspend DL data scheduling for SCell between the receiving time of Msg3 with CRNTI (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3436\_replaced([11152921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and until the updated beam ID can be applied (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2032\_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to the CORESET ID = value defined in (5G\_L2\_837\_replaced([11173906](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26fnYfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Scheduling shall be conducted by CORESET ID = 0 during this duration. If the MAC layer monitoring is not ongoing (5G\_UP\_ALG\_31669\_replaced([11153868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS shall flush all DL data in HARQ buffers at the point in time when the target beam can be used for scheduling by CORESET ID ≠ 0, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2032\_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: Linked to both 5GC000533 and 5GC000535 features to avoid creating a dependency on their mutual implementation order.

---

### 3.29.4.0-7 DL Pre-Scheduling Beam failure recovery - Initialization of DL precoder, layer and HARQ (ID: 11174373)

**3.29.4.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11174376`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
If, following a BFR procedure, DL number of layers and DL precoder initialization is triggered with 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3440\_replaced([11152973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DL HARQ processes related to the still pending retransmissions are released except for SRB1 buffer if MAC layer monitoring is ongoing, including the corresponding DL HARQ buffer indices (dlHarqBufferIndex)

---

### 3.29.4.0-8 DL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set (ID: 11174380)

**3.29.4.0-8.0-6**  (ID: `12647023`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
When the DL-DCCH is triggered and monitored by the RRC layer (where the new configurations are available after DL-xCCH procedure completion indication from the RRC layer), DL Pre-Scheduler shall exclude a UE from pre-scheduler candidate set during the reconfiguration transient phase, i.e., between the reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq and PsUser\_UserReconfCompletedReq,

OR

between N DPA scheduling cycle after the start notification of step-wise SCell Addition [CB010741-C] / Upgrade [End CB010741-C] (refer to ([11120331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqP_pPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) until end notification of SCell Addition [CB010741-C] / Upgrade [End CB010741-C] (refer to ([11120344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqP_p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

OR

between N DPA scheduling cycle after the start notification of SCell release (refer to ([11120617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqSb7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) until end notification of SCell release (refer to ([11120624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqSb8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

OR

between N DPA scheduling cycle after the start notification of PUCCH format 3 DMRS sequence modification until end notification of this modification.

if at least one of the following conditions is fulfilled:

(a) if the DMRS sequence of PUCCH format 3 or the modulation type of PUCCH format 3 (IE pi2Bpsk of pucchFormatConfigF3) **or max code rate of PUCCH format 3 (IE maxCodeRate of pucchFormatConfigF3)** is being reconfigured and the resulting HARQ feedback would be carried in a PUCCH F3, i.e.\*\*\*\*

* in slots with periodic CSI occurrence, or
* in slots with already 2 HARQ A/N bits**.**

(b) if HARQ-ACK slot is conflicted with P(S)Cell beam report during Scell addition/release for FR1 TDD CA with the update of *appliedBeamRefinementP2 ([11112003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpSWUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

(c) if the periodic SR configuration and/or the periodic CSI report configuration of a UE are reconfigured and the HARQ feedback would fall into a slot with the occurrence of such an affected SR or CSI report considering the new and the old configuration. Rationale is that gNB doesn't know when exactly the UE will apply the new configuration and hence the multiplexing of HARQ feedback with SR or periodic CSI shall be prevented during the short transient phase to avoid the loss of the HARQ feedback.

(d) if HARQ-ACK slot is conflicted with P(S)Cell CSI report during Scell addition/release for FR1 CA with PCell's CSI-RS resource downgrade or upgrade *(refer to ([11159253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Scenario 1).*

(e) if HARQ-ACK slot is conflicted with any serving cell CSI report which changes its UCI bit number (refer to ([11159253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Scenario 2) or beam report.

(f) If maxPayloadSize of PUCCH-ResourceSet1 is being reconfigured (refer to ([11312363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrhpKPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TDD PCell, and ([11312350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrhpJfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for FDD PCell) and the resulting HARQ feedback would be transmitted on a PUCCH F3, i.e., in slots with periodic CSI occurrence. (Rationale: there is ambiguity of maxPayloadSize during the transient period of this reconfiguration and gNB can’t decide on which PUCCH format3 resource the multiplexed UCI (HARQ-AN + pCSI) will be transmitted.)

(g) If HARQ-ACK slot is conflicted with releasing SCell periodic CSI report for channel acquisition or beam management (if configured). i.e: from 3CC to 2CC, *maxPayloadSize is not changed, only handle HARQ-ACK slot conflicting with SCell-2 periodic CSI report which is released.*

(h) if PCell's HARQ-ACK slot is conflicted with beam report for PCell/SCell during step-wise SCell Addition [CB010741-C] / Upgrade [End CB010741-C] for FR1 TDD CA with the update of *appliedBeamRefinementP2 ([11112003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpSWUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

(i) if PCell's HARQ-ACK slot is conflicted with CSI report for PCell/SCell during step-wise SCell Addition [CB010741-C] / Upgrade [End CB010741-C] for FR1 CA with PCell's CSI-RS resource downgrade or upgrade *(refer to ([11159253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Scenario 1).*

**Note: PsUser\_UserModifyReq/Res is specified in ([10975990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEIdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)). PUCCH Format type 3 will be configured by NRBTS.NRCELL.NRCELL\_FDD.pucchFormat and PUCCH F3 max code rate will be configured by NRBTS.NRCELL.pucchF3MaxCodeRate.**

---

### 3.29.4.0-9 DL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set due to SCell release (OBSOLETED, COMBINED to 5G_UP_ALG_4196_replaced( 11174380 )) (ID: 11174409)

**3.29.4.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11174417`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
DL Pre-Scheduler shall exclude a UE from pre-scheduler candidate set during the reconfiguration transient phase of SCell release, i.e. between N DPA scheduling cycle after the start notification of SCell release (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2689\_replaced([11120617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqSb7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) until end notification of SCell release (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2692\_replaced([11120624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqSb8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if at least one of the following conditions is fulfilled:

(a) if the max code rate of PUCCH format 3 (IE cellConfigurationForModification.pucchConfig.pucchFormatConfigF3.maxCodeRate) is being reconfigured and the resulting HARQ feedback would be carried in a PUCCH F3, i.e.

* in slots with periodic CSI report for channel acquisition or beam management (if configured), or
* in slots with already 2 HARQ A/N bits.

(b) if cellConfigurationForModification.appliedBeamRefinementP2 is being reconfigured and the resulting HARQ feedback would be carried in a PUCCH F3, i.e:

* in slots with P(S)Cell periodic CSI report for beam management.

(c) if the periodic SR configuration and/or the periodic CSI report configuration of a UE are reconfigured and the HARQ feedback would fall into a slot with the occurrence of such an affected SR or CSI report considering the new and the old configuration. Rationale is that gNB doesn't know when exactly the UE will apply the new configuration and hence the multiplexing of HARQ feedback with SR or periodic CSI shall be prevented during the short transient phase to avoid the loss of the HARQ feedback.(placeholder for SR or CSI report reconfiguration in SCell release, because FGCR-12120 is not supported in CA yet).

(d) if HARQ-ACK slot is conflicted with P(S)Cell CSI report during SCell release for FR1 CA with Pcell's CSI-RS resource upgrade (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3495\_replaced([11159253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Scenario 1).

(e) if HARQ-ACK slot is conflicted with any serving cell CSI report which changes its UCI bit number (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3495\_replaced([11159253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Scenario 2).

(f) if the maxPayloadSize of PUCCH-ResourceSet1 is being reconfigured (refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_3596\_replaced([11312363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrhpKPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TDD PCell, and 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_3944\_replaced([11312350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrhpJfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for FDD PCell) and the resulting HARQ feedback would be carried in a PUCCH F3, i.e:

* in slots with periodic CSI report for channel acquisition and beam management. (Rationale: there is ambiguity of maxPayloadSize equal to 8 or 12 or 16 for TDD PCell, and due to [Before CB009854-AA]presence/absence [CB009854-AA] any change [End CB009854-AA] of this IE for FDD PCell during SCell release, but without multiplexing with pCSI report, UE would always use HARQ AN PUCCH Format 3 resource set 1 for PCell only Scheduling)

(g) if HARQ-ACK slot is conflicted with releasing SCell periodic CSI report for channel acquisition or beam management (if configured). i.e: from 3CC to 2CC, maxPayloadSize is not changed, only handle HARQ-ACK slot conflicting with SCell-2 periodic CSI report which is released.

Note:

The overall handling is to avoid A/N bits multiplexing with periodic CSI report which is changing its UCI bits size or to avoid using F3 format which is changing its configuration.

---

### 3.29.4.0-10 DL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from DL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set due to A-CSI scheduling with large k2 timing and no k1 available (ID: 11174422)

**3.29.4.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11174426`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
If current slot N is eligible for PDSCH scheduling with at least one search space that could address C-RNTI and slot N is marked as A-CSI eligible and a list of UEs considered for A-CSI scheduling  was received from the UL scheduler ( see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3503\_replaced([11159340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5069\_replaced([11202276](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcHOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicating the UL scheduling time N’ with k2aCsi timing, and when N’ < N:

For each UE indicated by UL scheduler as scheduled for A-CSI

-If the current slot N doesn’t have associated a k1aCsi value in the slot context (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2502\_replaced([11116669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp6BY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then:

if N+k1 <= N’+k2aCsi, remove the scheduling eligibility for the UE in current slot.

-else:

consider (mark) the UE eligible for DL scheduling with k1aCsi Harq-Ack timing.

---

### 3.29.4.0-11 DL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from DL pre-scheduler candidate set due to HARQ-AN bit size restriction (ID: 11174430)

**3.29.4.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11174435`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
For CA UE with PUCCH allocated by DPA (Distributed PUCCH Allocation), in any serving cell, if c-DAI [Before PR704798] /t-DAI [End PR704798] pre-allocated by DPA for current slot N is invalid, then UE shall be removed from DL pre-scheduler candidate set in slot N. see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2944\_replaced([11123902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the setting of c-DAI / t-DAI during PUCCH pre-allocation by DPA.

---

### 3.29.4.0-12 DL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from DL pre-scheduler candidate set due to BetaOffsets update (ID: 11174440)

**3.29.4.0-12.0-4**  (ID: `11174457`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
The gNB-DU shall exclude a UE from the DL pre-scheduler candidate set during a User Reconfiguration Transient Phase in UL, if all the following are true:

* semiStatic betaOffset configuration for UE
* Any of betaOffsetAck indexes (betaOffsetAckIndex1, betaOffsetAckIndex2, betaOffsetAckIndex3) is updated
* UE only has data pending in DRB buffers or HARQ pending for DRB buffers to retransmission, including VoIP traffic, GBR traffic [CB008600-F],prioritized DC-GBR traffic, deprioritized DC-GBR traffic[End CB008600-F] and nonGBR traffic.

For SRB data (Request class srb1Traffic, srb3Traffic and srb2Traffic) , pending HARQ for SRB retransmission(Request class harqRetransSrb1, harqRetransSrb3, harqRetransSrb2) and MAC CE(Request class dlMacCe) shall be permitted to send in the transient phase except for the conditions defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4196\_replaced([11174380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008600-F]

For DC-GBR and pending HARQ for DC-GBR retransmission shall be permitted to send in transient phase except the conditions defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4196\_replaced([11174380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). And to prevent UL data scheudling blocked by too many UL HARQ feedback for DL data transmissions in transient phase, L2PS shall consider DL HARQ feedback in corresponding UL slot when schedule the DL transmission, that is, L2PS shall skip the DL data scheduling for DC-GBR with HARQ feedback in a UL slot with previous UL slot is not suitable for UL data scheduling defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4196\_replaced([11174380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008600-F]

Notes:

* UL scheduler shall skip the scheduling UL PUSCH in the slot for HARQ feedback/pCSI per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23614\_replaced([11195156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv57wPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* User Reconfiguration Transient Phase defined as period between the reception of a user modify request message and reception of a user reconfiguration complete request message.
* dynamicBetaOffsetInd reflects UE Capability. The enableDynamicBetaOffsets value reflects cell level configuration. Both are specified by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3500\_replaced([11159329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Two ways for semiStatic configuration: [1] UE does not support dynamic (dynamicBetaOffsetInd = false); [2] UE supports dynamic  (dynamicBetaOffsetInd = true), but cell does not (enableDynamicBetaOffsets = 'disable')
* betaOffsetAck indexes are updated for SAg per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21585\_replaced([11205427](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwpikvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

### 3.29.4.0-13 DL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from DL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set on Scell due to reconfiguration (ID: 11174462)

**3.29.4.0-13.0-2**  (ID: `11174469`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
DL Pre-Scheduler shall exclude a UE from pre-scheduler candidate set on the Scell during the reconfiguration transient phase from any [Before CB008830-C-UPD001] mcs-table [CB008830-C-UPD001] below [End CB008830-C-UPD001] change in DL on the Scell

[CB008830-C-UPD001]

- mcs-table

- DMRS configuration type

[End CB008830-C-UPD001]

Rationale: According to 3gpp 38.213 chapter 10.1, a Type3-PDCCH CSS set configured by SearchSpace in PDCCH-Config with searchSpaceType = common for DCI formats with CRC scrambled by INT-RNTI, SFI-RNTI, TPC-PUSCH-RNTI, TPC-PUCCH-RNTI, or TPC-SRS-RNTI and, only for the primary cell, C-RNTI, MCS-C-RNTI, or CS-RNTI(s).

---

### 3.29.4.0-14 DL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from DL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set on Scell due to Measurement Gaps (ID: 11174474)

**3.29.4.0-14.0-1**  (ID: `11174477`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
For E-UTRA-NR dual connected/SA UEs, if Measurement Gaps are setup as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2291\_replaced([11114624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpmfY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the pre-scheduler shall not select UEs whose PDCCH for PDSCH grant, or PDSCH, or PUCCH for this PDSCH's harq report drop in the interrupted slots to candidate set 1, i.e. CS1.

For the UEs skipped scheduling by MG, when the interrupted slots end, 5G-L2-PS shall add them back. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detail.

---

### 3.29.4.0-15 DL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from DL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set due to multiple K2, periodic CSI report and PUCCH A/N interworking (ID: 12702228)

**3.29.4.0-15.0-1**  (ID: `12702229`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
For a given UE, the DL pre-scheduler shall exclude the UE from the DL candidate set 1, i.e. CS1 when all following conditions are satisfied.

* Multiple K2 is being used (see 5G\_UP: [11348263: Multiple K2 Mode](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
* Denoted the PDSCH slot as N, which is processed by DL scheduler in current TTI, then special slot is N+2, the second UL slot after special slot is N+4.
* Before current TTI, gNB requests to receive a periodic CSI report on slot N+4 ([12709934](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_vnt_oGXGEfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

Rationale:

* Avoid scheduling a PDSCH UE with K1=4 while a CSI report is already scheduled with K1>=5. In this case, multiplexing PUCCH A/N with CSI report on UL slot N+4 might be problem. Revelant requirement: [12496661: k1 set for multiple K2 mode](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_zDHnAxRaEfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

### 3.29.4.0-16 DL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from DL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set due to Out-of-Order Execution in DL Pipelining (ID: 13462723)

**3.29.4.0-16.0-1**  (ID: `13462951`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
When DL pipelining functionality is enabled and a UE was considered for scheduling (i.e., part of the CS2 list or to-be-scheduled list) in a previous slot for which post-processing is not yet complete due to the out-of-order execution of slot scheduling phases (see [13423834](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_cvOa8RiUEfGFMKAOrP5qqw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), then the gNB-DU shall exclude that UE from DL candidate set 1 (CS1) in the current slot.

---

### 3.29.4.0-17 DL Pre-Scheduling Check UE DRX status (ID: 11174481)

**3.29.4.0-17.0-1**  (ID: `11174486`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
A particular UE can become candidate for dynamic packet scheduling only if it is in DRX active where it decodes signalling on the PDCCH (active time, i.e., there are no restrictions for transmission to/from the UE). Even if the gNodeB has data for transmission towards a dedicated UE, the gNodeB would be not able to transfer data as long as the UE does not decode resource assignments on PDCCH.

For the UEs, when PsUser\_UserModifyReq is received and contains the ‘measGapConfig’ structure, IE 'measGapConfigStatus' is set to 'setup' and DRX release configuration is also included in the same message, if the relevant RRC Reconfiguration Complete (PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq) hasn't yet been received from CP-RT, L2-PS shall consider both Measurement Gaps and DRX are active in this period. That is, such a UE can become candidate for dynamic packet scheduling only in slots that are not interrupted by gaps and when the DRX status is active.

---

### 3.29.4.0-19 DL Pre-Scheduling Use_3GPP_RAR_Window (ID: 11174494)

**3.29.4.0-19.0-2**  (ID: `11174503`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
[Before PR709206-23R4]

If NIDD parameter NRCELL.tmpActFeat2 is not configured or NRCELL.tmpActFeat2.bit6 is set 0, then Use\_3GPP\_RAR\_Window is activated; else it is deactivated.

[PR709206-23R4]

If NIDD parameter NRCELL.disableEarlyRar is FALSE, then Use\_3GPP\_RAR\_Window is activated; else it is deactivated.

[End PR709206-23R4]

---

### 3.29.4.0-20 DL Pre-Scheduling Pending RA-Msg2 addition (ID: 11174508)

**3.29.4.0-20.0-2**  (ID: `11174516`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
The DL Pre-scheduler shall include all 5GUEs with pending RACH Msg2 in the pre-scheduler candidate set.

Note that there is no support of RA-Msg4(CRI) or SRB0 in NSA mode.

See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17990\_replaced([11153010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjswvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the description of the triggers for the scheduling of a RA-Msg4.

See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3453\_replaced([11153402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the description of what triggers the scheduling of SRB0.

RA-Msg2 can only be sent within the RA response window.

[Before PR709206-23R2]

(raResponseWindowSize, provided by PsCell\_CellGroupSetupReq (please see 5G\_L2\_IF\_3117\_replaced([10966509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UPCB_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))), which starts 3 5GTF-subframes/NR-slots after RA preamble reception.

[PR709206-23R2]

gNB shall determine RAR window as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23061\_replaced([11174520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kucjKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End PR709206-23R2].

If 5G-L2-PS was not able to schedule RA-Msg2 within the RA response window, then the UE context and temporary C-RNTI shall be released.

---

### 3.29.4.0-21 DL Pre-Scheduling Random Access Response window identification (ID: 11174520)

**3.29.4.0-21.0-1**  (ID: `11174525`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
If Use\_3GPP\_RAR\_Window is activated as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23245\_replaced([11174494](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kucjJPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

· gNB shall determine 3GPP compliance RAR window starting slot (first slot with a valid Type1 PDCCH occasion after the end of the RA preamble) as descrived below,

Else

· gNB shall start RAR window at 3 5GTF-subframes/NR-slots after RA preamble reception, and window length is defined by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL-raResponseWindow.

End If

Determine 3GPP compliance RAR window

The RAR window is started from the first symbol of the earliest PDCCH candidate in RACH Search Space after the last symbol of the received RO (PRACH occasion).

1) The last symbol of RO is the end symbol of "1 time occasion" as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1400\_replaced([11105488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koLjHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/1416/1418/1420/1422/1424/1426/1428/1430/1432.

2) Type1 PDCCH CSS configuration (periodicity, slotOffset, duration, monitoringSymbolWithinSlot) is determined as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4049\_replaced([11168607](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktxNtPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2190\_replaced([11281596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpawVPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2187\_replaced([11281536](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpaJSfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_6113\_replaced([11362011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc5gTfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3) Determine PDCCH candidate SFN and slot

· Per 3GPP 38.213 10.1, a UE determines that a PDCCH monitoring occasion(s) exists in a slot with number SlotPO in a frame with number SFNPO, if (SFNPO \* N + SlotPO - slotOffset) mod periodicity = 0. N is the number of slots per frame in terms of NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELLGRP-scs. UE monitors PDCCH candidates for duration consecutive slots, starting from slot SlotPO, and does not monitor PDCCH candidates for the next (periodicity − duration) consecutive slots. The gNB shall determine the SFN and slot of PDCCH candidates in the same way as UE accordingly in terms of NRBTS/NRCELLGRP-scs.

4) Determine PDCCH candidate starting symbol

· The PDCCH candidate starting symbol set shall be determined from monitoringSymbolWithinSlot. E.g. the parameter value “symbol\_x\_y\_z” indicates the starting symbol can be x or y or z.

5) Determine PDCCH candidate length

· The CORESET linked with Type1 PDCCH CSS is determined as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4049\_replaced([11168607](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktxNtPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2190\_replaced([11281596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpawVPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2187\_replaced([11281536](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpaJSfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_6113\_replaced([11362011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc5gTfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The number of OFDM symbols of a PDCCH candidate shall be determined from duration of the CORESET.

6) Determine starting point of RAR window

· If the gNB is FDD mode, the first symbol of the earliest PDCCH candidate on Type1 PDCCH CSS is the starting point of the RAR window (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14386\_replaced([11174884](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kue_ZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

· If the gNB is TDD mode, the first valid slot (not declared as uplink slot) with first symbol of the earliest PDCCH candidate on Type1 PDCCH CSS is the starting point of the RAR window, where all the symbols of the PDCCH candidate shall be DL symbol based on the configured slot format pattern defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2313\_replaced([11114697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

7) Determine ending point of RAR window for gNB

· The RAR window shall end at the last symbol of a slot because gNB schedules the Msg2/Msg3 from a slot point of view as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2487\_replaced([11116530](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp4zR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

· UE RAR window length is defined by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL-raResponseWindow in unit of slot. If UE RAR window ends at the last symbol of a slot, gNB hold the same view as UE for the RAR window ending point; otherwise, gNB shall take RAR window ending at the last symbol of the second last slot in UE RAR window.

8) Determine “raResponseWindowSize”

raResponseWindowSize is the number of slots of the RAR window.

slot of 6): RAR window starting slot,

slot of 7): RAR window ending slot.

---

### 3.29.4.0-22 DL Pre-Scheduling Pending re-transmission (ID: 11174529)

**3.29.4.0-22.0-1**  (ID: `11174534`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
The DL Pre-scheduler shall include all 5GUEs with pending retransmissions from any carrier into the pre-scheduler candidate set.

---

### 3.29.4.0-23 DL Pre-Scheduling Stop Scheduling (ID: 11174539)

**3.29.4.0-23.0-2**  (ID: `11174547`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
Upon reception of PsUser\_UserStopReq, DL Prescheduler shall, for the designated UE :

* remove the UE from the DL

-  pre-scheduler candidate set,

if present.

* declare the UE ineligible for scheduling and prevent the UE from being added to the DL

-  pre-scheduler candidate set

This is handled by considering the status of the UE (suspended/stopped or not) when the DL Prescheduler checks if a UE can be included in the DL

* pre-scheduler candidate List.

[CB008140-AD]: For the designated UE which existed connection with SCell(s) located in ext-gNB, L2 PS in prime-gNB shall notify SCell(s) located in ext-gNB to stop data scheduling and DPA measurement. [End CB008140-AD]

By the upcoming PsUser\_UserDeleteReq (common to both these scenarios) the DL HARQ processes related to the still pending retransmissions are released, including the corresponding DL HARQ buffer indices (dlHarqBufferIndex).

If the numOfSCellToStop in the PsUser\_UserStopReq is zero, L2-PS shall follow the procedure described above. Otherwise, L2-PS shall mark the SCells in the scellToStopList as stopped. By the upcoming PsUser\_UserModifyReq, the DL HARQ processes related to the still pending retransmissions are released, including the corresponding DL HARQ buffer indices (dlHarqBufferIndex).

DL Prescheduler shall, for the designated UE on SCell [CB008140-AD]

Which located in the same gNB with PCell: [End CB008140-AD]:

* Declare UE is ineligible for scheduling on target SCell
* Shall not calculate TD metric for this UE in CS1 list (digital beamforming) or rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected (analog beamforming) on target SCell.
* Stop scheduling further HARQ re-transmissions for target SCell.

Note:

for PsUser\_UserStopReq\Resp see 5G\_L2\_IF\_461\_replaced([10976567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VHLxvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .

for PsUser\_UserDeleteReq\Resp see 5G\_L2\_IF\_467\_replaced([10976708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VHy4Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .

for PsUser\_UserModifyReq see 5G\_L2\_IF\_447\_replaced([10975990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEIdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .

for rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4101\_replaced([11173728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1dPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .

---

### 3.29.4.0-24 DL Pre-Scheduling Stop Scheduling for inter-gNB (ID: 11174551)

**3.29.4.0-24.0-1**  (ID: `11174556`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
[CB008140-CC]

When prime-L2-PS receives a notification to delete/stop non-GBR bearer(s) of ext-SCell(s) (for the related PDU session modification procedure, please see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_3808\_replaced([11630380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y83UR_wOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for the related PDU session deletion procedure, please see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_4264\_replaced([11634178](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y9mUC_wOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), it shall notify corresponding ext-L2-PS instance(s) to stop scheduling of the corresponding non-GBR bearer(s).

[End CB008140-CC]

When ext-L2-PS receives from prime-gNB a notification to stop the ext-SCell UE data scheduling (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4208\_replaced([11174539](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kucjLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4222\_replaced([11174838](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kueYWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

* Shall not calculate TD metric for this UE in CS1 list (digital beamforming) or rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected (analog beamforming) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4101\_replaced([11173728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1dPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) on ext-SCell.

- ext-L2-PS shall stop scheduling further HARQ re-transmissions.

[CB008140-CC]:

When ext-L2-PS receives from prime-gNB a notification to stop the ext-SCell non-GBR bearer(s) data scheduling, the DL pre-scheduler in ext-L2-PS shall execute the following operations for that UE:

* Declare non-GBR bearer is ineligible for scheduling on the ext-SCell
* Shall not calculate the TD metric scheduling weight factor (as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9885\_replaced([11175183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to the stopped data scheduling ext-SCell non-GBR bearer(s) for this UE in CS1 list (digital beamforming) or rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected (analog beamforming) on ext-SCell.
* Shall not calculate the FD resource distribution to the stopped data scheduling non-GBR bearer(s) of initial transmission UEs as per as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10158\_replaced([11181155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008140-CC]

---

### 3.29.4.0-25 DL Pre-Scheduling SCell cell load status determination for CA activation decision (ID: 11174560)

**3.29.4.0-25.0-1**  (ID: `11174564`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
5G-L2-PS shall calculate its cell load based on the number of UE with non-GBR bearers with data in pre-scheduler candidates list.

The cell load cellLoadForCADecision is initialized to 0 when cell is setup and is updated every slot (n) by an exponential filter defined below:

cellLoadForCADecision(n) = (1-rdPreschedulingUENumFilterConst) \* cellLoadForCADecision(n-1) + rdPreschedulingUENumFilterConst \* instanceCellLoadForCADecision(n)

with:

instanceCellLoadForCADecision(n): number of UE with non-GBR bearers with data in pre-scheduler candidates list in slot (n).

rdPreschedulingUENumFilterConst: filter constant of the exponential filter (R&D parameter defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8868\_replaced([11193322](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvr5RPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Two R&D parameters for threshold of high load and low load are defined:

rdlowCellLoadThreshold (R&D parameter defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8871\_replaced([11193331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvr5RvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdhighCellLoadThreshold (R&D parameter defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8873\_replaced([11193342](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvr5SPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

SCell 5G-L2-PS shall initialize the SCell cell load status to low load and update the cell load status as below:

If current SCell cell load status is low load and cellLoadForCADecision(n) > rdhighCellLoadThreshold, change the SCell cell load status to high load;

If current SCell cell load status is high load and cellLoadForCADecision(n) < rdlowCellLoadThreshold, change the SCell cell load status to low load;

In any other case, keep the SCell cell load status unchanged.

The update is triggered every time when SCell need to notify cell load status to P(S)Cell, and then piggyback in DPA measurement result according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8848\_replaced([11123534](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqkIs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note:

1. Cell load in this requirement is refer to number of UEs under pre-scheduling, it's a different concept comparing to cell load determined by PRB utilization.
2. SCell cell load status is reported to P(S)Cell according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8848\_replaced([11123534](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqkIs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and then further used by P(S)Cell to exclude SCells with high load from SCell activation candidate list according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8840\_replaced([11121235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqWtXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.29.4.0-26 DL Pre-Scheduling Beam Selection for Analog Beamforming in FR2 (ID: 11174568)

**3.29.4.0-26.0-1**  (ID: `11174573`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
Overview

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174577`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
For analog beamforming, in FR2, if NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27553\_replaced([11193947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvxY0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), DL pre-scheduler shall perform beam selection based on UEs in pre-scheduler candidate set during the UE selection for CS1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/actHighNumberOfUes = FALSE or rdActEnhBeamSelection = FALSE, then beam selection shall not be performed in the DL Pre-scheduler but in DL TD scheduler considering the UEs in DL candidate set 1 (CS1) as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4250\_replaced([11175627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-2**  (ID: `11174581`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
Beam Selection Based on UEs in Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11174585`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
Beam Selection

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174590`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
In FR2, If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27553\_replaced([11193947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvxY0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), analog beam selection is also performed together with UE selection for CS1  as below:

* Beam selection for all traffic except drbTraffic: When a UE/Tx belonging to the traffic class except drbTraffic is the first UE eligible to be added to CS1 from DL pre-scheduler candidate set as per conditions in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then the beam for the slot, namely selectedBeam(n), shall be set to the beam of the corresponding UE/Tx.
* Beam selection for drbTraffic class: When a UE/Tx belonging to drbTraffic class is the first UE eligible to be added to CS1 from DL pre-scheduler candidate set as per conditions in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then, the beam selection shall be performed based on UEs in drbTraffic class as below:
* The beam for the slot, namely selectionBeam(n), shall be set to the beam with the highest priority (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27547\_replaced([11174594](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudKPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) among all the beams which have atleast one UE belonging to drbTraffic eligible to be added to CS1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Once the beam for the slot is determined, namely selectionBeam(n), then, further UEs shall be added to CS1 only if their beam is same as selectedBeam(n) as stated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11174594`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
Beam Priorities for NonGBR Traffic

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11174599`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
DL pre-scheduler shall prioritize the beams for the beam selection of nonGBR traffic as below.

* The higher the value of beamMetric (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27543\_replaced([11174602](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the higher is the priority of the beam.
* If two beams have same value of beam metric, then the beam with higher value of beamWeight shall be considered to have more priority.

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11174602`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
Beam Metric Computation for NonGBR Traffic

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11174612`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
11174612:
DL Pre-Scheduling
For a given beam i, its beam metric, namely beamMetric(i), shall be determined as below

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
beamMetricᵢ = beamWeightᵢ / rrmAvgBeamSchedRateᵢ[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12944.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where:

* beamWeight(i) = sum of the scheduling weights (NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight) of all nonGBR bearers with non-empty buffer configured for the UEs in the beam i. In the beamWeight calculation, only the nonGBR bearers of UE(s) which are eligible to be scheduled in the slot n (i.e. UEs which are considered in DL pre-scheduler candidate set) shall be considered.
* rrmAvgBeamSchedRate(i) shall represent the percentage of slots that the beam gets selected for scheduling DL data transmission nonGBR bearers. It shall be calculated via an IIR filter as below

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation defines `rrmAvgBeamSchedRate(t)` in terms of previous values and a coefficient.
The equation is:
`rrmAvgBeamSchedRate(t) = (1 - rdBeamSelectionFilterCoeff) * rrmAvgBeamSchedRate(t-1) + rdBeamSelectionFilterCoeff * instBeamSchedRate(t)`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12944.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

where:

* rdBeamSelectionFilterCoeff is the R&D parameter described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27558\_replaced([11193957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvxY1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* rrmAvgBeamSchedRate(i) for every beam i calculated via an IIR filter shall be updated every DL scheduling slot 't' with the instantaneous value, namely instBeamSchedRate(i). In a given DL slot, instBeamSchedRate(i) of beam i shall be 1 if the corresponding beam i is selected for scheduling in the slot, otherwise, it shall be 0. At the beginning, rrmAvgBeamSchedRate for all beams shall be initialised to 1/n where n is the number of SSB beams configured in the cell. The slots without DL NR data scheduling shall be excluded in the rrmAvgBeamSchedRate calculation. Note, the NR DL slots with only artificial load generated shall also be considered as slots without DL NR data scheduling.

Note: This requirement states the functional perspective of the beamMetric, beamWeight and rrmAvgBeamSchedRate calculation. However, it shall be further described in detail in L2 EFS on how these parameters are efficiently computed for performance reasons.

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11174618`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
Interaction between Beam selection and CS1 Candidate UEs Optimization

---

**3.29.4.0-26.0-2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11174622`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
In FR2, if analog beam selection is performed in PRE scheduler (i.e. NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE), then the CS1 candidate UEs search optimization (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23553\_replaced([11193808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvvjtfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be disabled (i.e. behaviour shall be same as when rdCs1CandidateUENumCoeff = 0) irrespective of the configured value of the rdCs1CandidateUENumCoeff (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23553\_replaced([11193808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvvjtfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

### 3.29.4.0-27 DL Pre-Scheduling Selecting high TD metric and high buffer UEs to CS1 list (ID: 12550648)

**3.29.4.0-27.0-3**  (ID: `13462060`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
If NRBTS/NRCELL.bestNK.actBestNList is set to DL or DL\_UL, follow below algorithm to create CS1 list;

Configuration Parameters

* Max NK UEs = BestNKListRatio × maxCS1ListSize (instantaneous value of maxCS1ListSize per OLC) (see [12517582](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_U2PXwBiDEfC3upM2pE5-4w))
* NRBTS/NRCELL/bestNK.actBestKList is set to DL or DL\_UL.
* Max K UEs = max(1, BestKRatio × Max NK)
* BestNKListRatio (DL): NRBTS/NRCELL.bestNK.dlBestNListRatio
* BestKRatio (DL): NRBTS/NRCELL.bestNK.dlBestKListRatio

Step 0: Clean Up Best NK List

* Remove UEs from the Best NK List (proposed in previous slot) if they fail prescheduling checks (as per 11174302).
* If a non-GBR UE was added due to newTx and transitions to reTx, remove it.
* DRX Off state, measurement gap UEs are not allowed to be in the best NK List.
* The resulting size is referred to as BestNKListSize (DL)
* If Best NK List becomes empty, fall back to legacy CS1 creation (as per 11174627) and skip steps below.
* [CB014670-C] When UEs have been considered for scheduling or scheduled in previous slot(s) but their post-scheduling has not been performed due to out-of-order scheduling (see [13423834](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_cvOa8RiUEfGFMKAOrP5qqw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), then the gNB-DU shall remove those UEs from the bestNK list. [End CB014670-C]

Step 1: Add High-Priority UEs

* Add UEs with priority higher than drbTraffic, and UEs within drbTraffic class that are marked as prioritized (e.g., prioritized NBR, UEs in pairing group categories). (as per [11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Step 2: Add High Buffer (K) UEs

* From the remaining Best NK List, add up to (Max K-1) UEs with the highest buffer.
* Ensure at least one slot remains for a future RR UE (from Step 4), i.e., do not fill all available CS1 list entries here.
* Deduplication: UEs added in this step must be tracked so that they are excluded in subsequent steps.
* Do not exceed maxCs1ListSize.

Step 3: Add High TD-Metric (N) UEs

* From the remaining Best NK List, add up to (min(BestNKListSize (DL), Max NK) - actual\_K\_added) UEs with highest TD metric to CS1 list.
* Exclude any UEs already added/reserved in Step 2 .
* Do not exceed maxCs1ListSize.

Step 4: Add Remaining UEs via Round Robin (RR)

* Among non-GBR UEs, ensure at least one RR UE is selected if not already done in previous steps.
* From UEs with drbTraffic or lower priority class, add UEs via Round Robin. (as per [11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Ensure at least one UE is added via RR if no such UE has been added so far (to prevent starvation of new UEs).
* Exclude all UEs already present in CS1 list.
* Continue until CS1 list reaches maxCs1ListSize.

Deduplication Enforcement

* At each step, UEs already added to the CS1 list in earlier steps must be excluded from selection.

Note: From the algorithm, ensure that among the non-GBR UEs in the CS1 list:

* [At least one RR UE is included, if applicable.]
* If only two non-GBR UEs are allowed, select one K UE and one RR UE (Do not select two K UEs).
* If three non-GBR UEs are allowed, select one K UE, one RR UE, and one N UE.

Note: If the Carrier Aggregation feature is enabled, then for each CA UE with distributed PUCCH allocation, the Best NK List should also be considered when calculating cs1AttemptRatioServingCellx. (please see [11123479: UE Measurement Cycle](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqjhpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

[CB014670-C] Note: It is possible that the bestNK list created from the slot N-1 is not available for pre-scheduling of slot N, when DL pipelining solution is enabled due to the possibility of post scheduling of slot N-1 be executed after the PRE and TD scheduling of slot N. Hence, this requirement shall use the latest available best NK list and it might be from the older slot too. [End CB014670-C]

---

### 3.29.4.0-28 DL Pre-Scheduling UEs selection for CS1 (ID: 11174627)

**3.29.4.0-28.0-14**  (ID: `13409524`)

DL Pre-Scheduling
13409524:
DL Pre-Scheduling
Pre-scheduler shall select schedulingCapacity (5G\_L2\_1833\_replaced([11212618](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrWvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_1846\_replaced([11212907](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-ogjPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) UEs to form CS1.
CS1Size = schedulingCapacity. 
CA UE shall be considered in each serving cell as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4185\_replaced([11174341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
UEs for CS1 are selected as follows:
 CS1 shall be initialized as an empty set.
 Each UE should be assigned to one priority class as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9465\_replaced([11174010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuXqrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If a UE is candidate for several priority classes, it shall be assigned only to the highest among them.
 The pre-scheduler shall skip UEs in their MG interrupted slots as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25378\_replaced([11174474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kub8H_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When the interrupted slots end, those skipped UEs shall be eligible for pre-scheduling again.
\* If RIM DL mitigation is applied in current slot, then additional limitations as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13704\_replaced([11174307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ‘DL Scheduling restrictions under Remote Interference Mitigation’ shall be considered.
\* If NRCELL.actTwoDlSlotsMutInTdLte is set to true, then additional limitations as described in [12789896: DL Scheduling restrictions for tdLte frame structure](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y-yXQJOiEfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) shall be considered.
 Starting with the highest priority class, all classes are handled in decreasing priority order:
      - [CB014699-F] For the criticalDrbTraffic request class, the UEs eligible for CS1 are chosen in a round robin manner [End CB014699-F].
     If NRBTS/eirpControl/actEpic is enabled and if the current class is drbTraffic or lower priority
 If the UE transmission impacts a segment under EIRP control in which the allowance planned for the current slot (see [12526294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-_HiABrIEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is 0 or less then remove the UE from pre-scheduler candidate set.
 If NRBTS.actSliceAwareScheduler = 1 and the current class is drbTraffic
 If the UE has no non-GBR DRB (excluding 5QI-5) with non-empty buffer that belongs to a configured resource group (RG) **with nonzero weight ([image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmRGWeighti ≠ 0". The text is in a light color against a white background. The font is italicized and appears to be a sans-serif font. The characters are slightly blurred, but the text is still legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_12958.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]).**
 If the UE has at least one deprioritized VoIP DRB with nonempty buffer
