#### 3.29.4.1.0-5 DTX optimized scheduling Computation of the averaged maximum number of UEs in the CS1 list (ID: 11174992)

**3.29.4.1.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12578427`)

DTX optimized scheduling
The averaged maximum number of UEs in the CS1 list , *avgMaxNumUeCS1, is computed as follows:*

[Before CNI-137616-B]

If *rdEnableCpuOverloadControl (see 5G\_L2\_1279\_replaced([11189958](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28TlQ_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) set to false**:*\*\*

***avgMaxNumUeCS1* = \*rdMaxNumUeTdDl (see 5G\_L2\_3697\_replaced([11182022](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27YYNvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**\*\*\*

else **[End CNI-137616-B]**

***avgMaxNumUeCS1 = avgCs1Size as defined in 5G\_L2\_1841\_replaced([11212749](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-n5cPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))***

---

#### 3.29.4.1.0-6 DTX optimized scheduling Scheduling of UEs with the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class and request class switching (ID: 11174999)

**3.29.4.1.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11175008`)

DTX optimized scheduling
A UE in the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8549\_replaced([11174294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is eligible for scheduling if any of the following conditions is satisfied:

1. The UE has data to transmit for any other request class
2. The UE with the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class is marked eligible for scheduling as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8236\_replaced([11175012](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kugNgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB009333-F]

1. When NRBTS/NRCELL/actEnhDtxOptimizedScheduler is set to true and NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler.rlcStatusPduSupportEnabled is set to true and UE  with the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class has pending UL RLC Status PDU data indicated for any of non-GBR DRB (via buffer status report). UE with pending UL RLC Status PDU data is considered as Ue with delay sensitive user data (see 5G\_UP\_4682\_replaced([11348297](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubUL5fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) but no dedicated request class is created for this purpose.

[End CB009333-F]

[CB009333-B]

1. When multi cell micro-DTX synchronization slot occurs as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16826\_replaced([11175052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kugNjvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler/multiCellSyncEnabled is set to true and NRBTS/NRCELL/actEnhDtxOptimizedScheduler is set to true.

[End CB009333-B]

If any of those conditions is fulfilled, the UE shall then be set temporarily to the appropriate request class but related DTX optimized scheduling timers shall not be stopped.

UEs fulfilling only the condition 2 above shall be switched to dlDrbTraffic class (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) before being scheduled.

Note: A UE in the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class is also eligible for scheduling if one of the conditions described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9471\_replaced([11175687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is verified

UEs shall fall back to the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class if the data in its buffer when leaving the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class set is not emptied, unless a different priority class shall be used. Otherwise, if all associated buffers are emptied, all associated DTX optimized scheduling timers shall be stopped and the UE shall be moved out of the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class.

---

#### 3.29.4.1.0-7 DTX optimized scheduling DTX optimized scheduling timers handling (ID: 11175012)

**3.29.4.1.0-7.0-5**  (ID: `11175048`)

DTX optimized scheduling
11175048:
DTX optimized scheduling
Upon reception of new non-GBR DRB data for a UE (associated buffers increased) and upon entering the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8549\_replaced([11174294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall:

* Measure retention delay for each bearer until it exceeds dtxMaxRetentionDelay (see NOTE)

When NRBTS/NRCELL/actEnhDtxOptimizedScheduler is set to true and NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler/drxSupportEnabled is set to true and UE has the configured DRX profile the length of max retention delay timer shall be aligned with the n-th slot before the end of nearest DRX OnDuration window, where n is controlled by R&D parameter rdDtxDrxAvoidanceOffset (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16567\_replaced([11193557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtuePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The UE shall be marked eligible for scheduling if retention delay for any bearer exceeded dtxMaxRetentionDelay (see NOTE), regardless of the reception of new non-GBR DRB data.

If all of the associated non-GBR DRB buffers of a UE are emptied due to its scheduling with the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class or another higher priority request class then the associated DTX optimized scheduling timer shall be stopped.

Example cases of max retention delay are summarized below:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two charts side-by-side, illustrating different scheduling triggers. Both charts have a horizontal axis labeled 't' representing time, and a purple line indicating 'Initial data' on the left side.
The left chart, titled "Scheduling triggered by max retention delay", shows several light blue bars representing data points over time. A yellow horizontal line labeled "dtx MaxRetention Delay" extends from the first data point. A dashed vertical line connects the end of this delay to a red 'x' mark labeled "Scheduling", which is positioned above a tall, dark blue bar. This dark blue bar signifies a scheduled event occurring after the maximum retention delay has passed.
The right chart, titled "Scheduling triggered by sensitive data", also shows data points over time. The first data point is a light blue bar. Following this, there are two more bars, one red and one dark blue, positioned closely together. A yellow horizontal line labeled "dtx MaxRetention Delay" originates from the first data point. A dashed vertical line extends from the end of this delay to a red 'x' mark labeled "Scheduling", with a dotted yellow arrow indicating a forward direction. Below the red and dark blue bars, text reads "Delay sensitive data". This chart suggests that sensitive data, represented by the red and dark blue bars, can trigger scheduling even if the maximum retention delay has not yet been reached.
In summary, the image contrasts two scheduling mechanisms: one triggered by a fixed time delay ("max retention delay") and another that can be preempted by the arrival of "sensitive data".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13008.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

1- The first scenario shows that, the UE is switched to a higher priority request class only after the expiration of the max retention delay. In the meantime, data is buffered as the UE stays in the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class

2 - The second scenario shows that if any higher priority / delay sensitive data is received before the expiration of the max retention delay, the UE shall still use the appropriate request class. If the buffered data can all be scheduled with the delay sensitive data, the associated DTX timer shall be stopped. Otherwise, the UE shall continue applying the DTX related timer as specified above.  The same behavior happens when the UE is co-scheduled.

Note1: If the slot at expiration of max retention delay is blanked due to RF high temperature (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18282\_replaced([11175086](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kugNkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the scheduling will be delayed to the first available slot after it.

NOTE : dtxMaxRetentionDelay is parameter read from  NRBTS/NRDRB.dtxMaxRetentionDelay

for current bearer or if not present or if NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler.drbDifferentiationEnabled is set to false, from: NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler.dtxMaxRetentionDelay

[CNI-124437]

Special value NRBTS/NRDRB.dtxMaxRetentionDelay=0  indicates that retention delay for given bearer shall not be applied  for the corresponding DRB (no additional delay is allowed for this DRB by the Micro-DTX optimized scheduler).

Special value NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler.dtxMaxRetentionDelay=0 indicates that retention delay at cell level shall not be applied (but still retention delay could be applied for selected DRBs if NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler.drbDifferentiationEnabled=true and corresponding DRBTS/NRDRB.dtxMaxRetentionDelay>0).

[End CNI-124437]

---

#### 3.29.4.1.0-8 DTX optimized scheduling Determination of multi cell micro-DTX synchronization slot for a UE (ID: 11175052)

**3.29.4.1.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11175083`)

DTX optimized scheduling
11175083:
DTX optimized scheduling
Multi cell micro-DTX synchronization slot for a scheduling is the last multi cell micro-DTX synchronization opportunity slot before expiration of retention delay for any bearer of a UE  having data to be scheduled (as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8236\_replaced([11175012](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kugNgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Each UE has one sync slot (or once per bearer if NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler/drbDifferentiationEnabled is enabled) where it can be scheduled earlier than maxRetentionDelay but it may be also scheduled earlier if there are other scheduling opportunities (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8234\_replaced([11174999](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kufmf_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9471\_replaced([11175687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Multi cell micro-DTX synchronization opportunity slot is determined as every slot that is starting in multiple of  NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler/dtxRoundingFactor ms after (system frame number 0 + NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler/dtxRoundingOffset) ms.

Example of synchronization slots for single UE:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the behavior of a DTX (Discontinuous Transmission) timer in a wireless communication system, specifically for a User Equipment (UE) labeled UE1. The diagram shows time slots on the horizontal axis and different states or events represented by colored bars and lines.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Legend:**
\* **Green bars (tall):** Synchronization opportunity slot.
\* **Light blue bars:** Data in the buffer for UE1.
\* **Dark blue bars:** Data scheduled for UE1.
\* **Green bars (long, horizontal):** uDTX (User Equipment Discontinuous Transmission) state.
**Diagram Elements and Events:**
The diagram depicts two main scenarios over time:
**Scenario 1 (Left side):**
\* **Data in buffer:** A light blue bar indicates data is present in the buffer.
\* **Synchronization opportunity slot:** A tall green bar appears, representing a synchronization opportunity.
\* **Data scheduled:** A dark blue bar appears, indicating data is scheduled for transmission.
\* **dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer:** A horizontal orange line with an arrow extending to the right, labeled "dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer". This timer seems to be active during this period.
\* **uDTX state:** A long green horizontal bar indicates the UE is in the uDTX state.
\* **Annotation:** A blue line points to the synchronization opportunity slot and the data in the buffer, with the text "Data still left in the buffer after scheduling, DTX timer not canceled". This suggests that even after scheduling data, if there's still data in the buffer, the DTX timer is not canceled.
**Scenario 2 (Right side):**
\* **Data in buffer:** A light blue bar indicates data is present in the buffer.
\* **Synchronization opportunity slot:** A tall green bar appears.
\* **Data scheduled:** A dark blue bar appears, indicating data is scheduled for transmission.
\* **dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer:** Another horizontal orange line with an arrow and an 'X' mark, labeled "dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer". The 'X' mark indicates the timer has expired.
\* **uDTX state:** A long green horizontal bar indicates the UE is in the uDTX state.
\* **Annotation 1:** A blue line points to the synchronization opportunity slot and the data in the buffer, with the text "DTX timer expired, UE moved out of micro-DTX state". This implies that the expiration of the DTX timer leads to the UE exiting a micro-DTX state.
\* **Annotation 2:** A blue line points to the end of the data scheduled and the absence of data in the buffer, with the text "No data left in the buffer after scheduling, DTX timer canceled". This suggests that when all scheduled data is transmitted and no data remains in the buffer, the DTX timer is canceled.
The horizontal axis is labeled "Time (slots)", indicating that the diagram represents events occurring over discrete time slots. The diagram illustrates how the DTX timer's behavior is influenced by the presence of data in the buffer and the scheduling of transmissions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13012.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Example of synchronization slots for two UEs (TBD)

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a timeline of data scheduling and synchronization opportunities for two User Equipments (UEs), labeled UE1 and UE2. The timeline is divided into slots.
The legend at the top explains the different colored bars and labels:
- **Light green bars:** Represent "synchronization opportunity slots".
- **Light blue and orange bars:** Represent "UE1 UE2 data in the buffer". The light blue bar likely corresponds to UE1 and the orange bar to UE2, or vice versa, but this is not explicitly stated.
- **Dark blue and brown bars:** Represent "UE1 UE2 data scheduled". Similar to the buffered data, the dark blue bar likely corresponds to UE1 and the brown bar to UE2, or vice versa.
- **Green bars:** Represent "uDTX" (likely discontinuous transmission).
The diagram shows several events marked with red "X" symbols and labeled "dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer". These timers appear to indicate a maximum delay for data retention.
Here's a breakdown of the timeline:
1. **Initial State:** There are long green bars representing uDTX at the beginning of the timeline. Above these, there are buffered data bars (light blue and orange) and scheduled data bars (dark blue and brown). A "dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer" is shown starting from the beginning of the buffered data and ending at the first synchronization opportunity slot.
2. **First Synchronization Opportunity:** A light green bar indicates a synchronization opportunity. Before this slot, there is buffered data and scheduled data. The "dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer" is shown to expire at this synchronization opportunity slot, marked by a red "X".
3. **Second Synchronization Opportunity:** Another light green bar represents a subsequent synchronization opportunity. Before this slot, there are buffered data and scheduled data. A "dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer" is shown starting from the end of the previous synchronization opportunity and ending at this current synchronization opportunity slot, also marked by a red "X".
4. **Third Synchronization Opportunity:** A third light green bar indicates another synchronization opportunity. Before this slot, there are buffered data and scheduled data. A "dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer" is shown extending from the previous synchronization opportunity to this one, ending with a red "X".
5. **Later Slots:** The diagram continues to show uDTX, buffered data, and scheduled data in subsequent slots, with the "dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer" continuing to be indicated.
The horizontal axis is labeled "Time (slots)". The diagram visually represents how data buffering, scheduling, and synchronization opportunities interact over time, with the "dtxMaxRetentionDelayTimer" acting as a constraint or trigger for certain actions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13012.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[CB011114-B]Note: If a synchronization opportunity slot is blanked due to RF high temperature (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18282\_replaced([11175086](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kugNkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the scheduling shall skip this slot.[End CB011114-B]

---

### 3.29.4.2 Slot blanking due to RF high temperature

#### 3.29.4.2.0-1 Slot blanking due to RF high temperature Slot blanking flow in L2-PS (ID: 11175091)

**3.29.4.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12883411`)

Slot blanking due to RF high temperature
On cell scope, CB013489 shall provide the exact same functionality and use the same input method(notification) / the same PM counters as CB011114.

If blanking indicator due to RF high temperature (see [11611355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5LuEvwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) received, L2-PS shall start the slot blanking for corresponding cell as follows:

- L2-PS shall calculate achievedMutingRatio for each DL/special slot (see [11183727](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMxFvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- L2-PS shall perform slot blanking based on the achievedMutingRatio for each DL/special slot (see [11175107](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0kvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE, NR slots (see [11171912](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are applicable for achievedMutingRatio calculation and slot blanking.

On LTE side, PRB blanking performed (see [12790942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_DNY2UZQ4EfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

L2-PS shall stop the slot blanking immediately while blanking cancel indicator due to RF high temperature (see [11611437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5MVQfwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) received.

---

#### 3.29.4.2.0-2 Slot blanking due to RF high temperature Slot blanking (ID: 11175107)

**3.29.4.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175112`)

Slot blanking due to RF high temperature
If (achievedMutingRatio< tgtMutingRatio) AND the current slot does not contain priority traffic (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18294\_replaced([11175117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS shall blank the current slot PDSCH traffic.

Else

normal schedule algorithm same as legacy (slot blanking due to high temperature is not applied).

Where tgtMutingRatio is received from CPRT (see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_14488\_replaced([11611421](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5MVMvwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 3.29.4.2.0-3 Slot blanking due to RF high temperature Priority traffic (ID: 11175117)

**3.29.4.2.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `13430522`)

Slot blanking due to RF high temperature
If the slot blanking candidate cells sent by CPRT include multiple cells (more than one cells connected with same RF) and NRBTS/allowGBRMutingForRfCooling is true.

Priority traffic: SSB/refinement beam, SIB1, OSI, Paging, CSI-RS, TRS, RIM-RS, RACH MSG2/MSG4 (including both new and reTx).

Else if the slot blanking candidate cells sent by CPRT include multiple cells (more than one cells connected with same RF) and NRBTS/allowGBRMutingForRfCooling is false.

Priority traffic: SSB/refinement beam, SIB1, OSI, Paging, CSI-RS, TRS, RIM-RS, RACH MSG2/MSG4 (including both new and reTx),[CB014699-E] critical GBR, critical VoNR, critical non-GBR, [End CB014699-E] DC-GBR, GBR.

Else if the slot blanking candidate cells sent by CPRT not include multiple cells (more than one cells connected with same RF) and NRBTS/allowGBRMutingForRfCooling is true

Priority traffic: SSB/refinement beam, SIB1, OSI, Paging, CSI-RS, TRS, RIM-RS, RACH MSG2/MSG4, HARQ retransmission, MAC CE, DL SRB data.

Else if the slot blanking candidate cells sent by CPRT not include multiple cells (more than one cells connected with same RF) and NRBTS/allowGBRMutingForRfCooling is false

Priority traffic: SSB/refinement beam, SIB1, OSI, Paging, CSI-RS, TRS, RIM-RS, RACH MSG2/MSG4, HARQ retransmission, MAC CE, [CB014699-E] DL critical SRB data, critical GBR, critical VoNR, critical non-GBR, [End CB014699-E] DL SRB data, DC-GBR, GBR.

Note: Paging with short message only is considered as non-Priority traffic since it does not use any PDSCH resource.

---

## 3.29.5 DL Time Domain Scheduling

### 3.29.5.1 Time domain scheduling for traffic prioritization - Overview

**3.29.5.1.0-1**  (ID: `11175143`)

Time domain scheduling for traffic prioritization - Overview
The determination of candidate set 2 by the DL TD scheduler shall be done by:

 The presence of the common channels (SI-RNTI/P-RNTI) in the CS1 list, which enforces the beams selections when the number of beams per slot is rectricted.

 For analog beamforming, first Beam Selection (step 1 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4101\_replaced([11173728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1dPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4251\_replaced([11175632](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and subsequently.

 UE Selection (step 2 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4101\_replaced([11173728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1dPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4258\_replaced([11175719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Both procedures shall take the traffic prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of UE traffic into account.

---

### 3.29.5.2 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL

#### 3.29.5.2.0-1 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Definition of per UE and per carrier scheduling metric for DL (ID: 11175151)

**3.29.5.2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13343471`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
13343471:
Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The proportional fair (PF) scheduling metric per UE and per carrier shall be applied in the time domain for the prioritization of the 5G UEs of Candidate Set 1.

Individual PF scheduling metrics shall be calculated per configured carrier a 5G UE is allowed to be allocated to as per equation 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-1 or in case NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSchLowBufferOptimization is set to true (FR1 only) as per equation 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2.

if NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSchLowBufferOptimization is set to false

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-03211424.png[/image\_path][/image-0](5G_UP_ALG_4232_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-1)

otherwise (NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSchLowBufferOpt is set to true,

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-03211441.png[/image\_path][/image-1](5G_UP_ALG_4232_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2)

with:

i: Index of the UE

j: Index of the Carrier

t: NR slot the calculation relates to

rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t]:  The current DL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in bytes per slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4242\_replaced([11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha [i,j,t]:    The current DL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in bytes per slot with exponentiation of alpha (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4242\_replaced([11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,t-1]:  The average DL data rate of the 5G UE i over all carriers in bytes per slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4243\_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-1)

rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,j,t-1]:  The average DL data rate of the 5G UE i over carrier j in bytes per slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4243\_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2)

rrmUeSchedWeight[i,t]:   The UE scheduling weight as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9885\_replaced([11175183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRate[i,j,t]:   The current DL data rate which might be downgraded in case of low buffer utilization of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in bytes per slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

PF metric shall only be calculated for carriers where the UE is eligible for scheduling as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4240\_replaced([11175300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCtvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB015010-A] For the Friends, the above metric shall be modified as described in [13343401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_bEmCQN8GEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End CB015010-A]

5G-L2-PS shall only calculate individual PF scheduling metrics for configured carrier in current 5G-L2-PS instance for FDD and FR1 TDD CA UE or for F+F CA UE according to carriers configured in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2667\_replaced([11120269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqPYlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2670\_replaced([11120289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqPYmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: For analog beamforming, this metric shall be applied in addition for the selection of the x-pol beam.

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-2 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Definition of UE scheduling weight (ID: 11175183)

**3.29.5.2.0-2.0-9**  (ID: `13416676`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
13416676:
Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The overall UE scheduling weight rrmUeSchedWeight is defined as the product of multiple scheduling weight factors as per equation 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9885\_replaced([11175183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-1

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = False

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmUeSchedWeight[i, t] = (\sum\_{k} W[i, k, t]) \cdot W\_{DL,DRX\_{active}}(i, t) \cdot W\_{CA,DSS}(i, t) \cdot W\_{PL,DPA}(i, t)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13037.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9885\_replaced([11175183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-1)

with:

i:   Index of the UE

k:   Index of the non-GBR bearer [CB014699-F] or critical non-GBR bearer [End CB014699-F] or NBR bearer **in W[i,k,t]**

t:   NR slot the calculation relates to

W[i,k,t]:  the schedWeightFactor of NBR bearer or non-GBR bearer k for 5G UE i at slot t with data in buffer as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22255\_replaced([11175240](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

WDL,DRX\_Active[i,t]:        the DRX scheduling weight factor as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4237\_replaced([11175249](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

WCA,DSS[i,t]:          the CA DSS scheduling weight factor as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9913\_replaced([11175257](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbq_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

WPf, DPA[i,t]: the DL CA DPA scheduling weight factor as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8329\_replaced([11175264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Else

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmUeSchedWeight[i,t] = \max \left{ \frac{rdRGMinLowShareDL}{rdRGTinyWeightReciDL} \sum\_{k \in K} (W[i, k, t] \cdot rrmRGWeights\_{(k)}(t)) \right} \cdot W\_{DL,DRXactive}(i, t) \cdot W\_{CA,DSS}(i, t) \cdot W\_{Pf,DPA}(i, t)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-01301027.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

(5G\_UP\_ALG\_9885\_replaced([11175183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-1)

with:

i:    Index of the UE

k:    Index of the non-GBR bearer or NBR bearer **in** W[i,k,t] (belonging to a configured slice s of the cell)\*\*\*\*

K:    Indices of all non-GBR bearers [CB014699-F] or critical non-GBR bearer [End CB014699-F] or NBR bearer which belong to all configured slices of the cell

t:    NR slot the calculation relates to

W[i,k,t]:  the schedWeightFactor of NBR bearer or non-GBR bearer k for 5G UE i at slot t with data in buffer as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22255\_replaced([11175240](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

WDL,DRX\_Active[i,t]:  the DRX scheduling weight factor as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4237\_replaced([11175249](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

WCA,DSS[i,t]:  the CA DSS scheduling weight factor as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9913\_replaced([11175257](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbq_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

WPf, DPA[i,t]:  the DL CA DPA scheduling weight factor as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8329\_replaced([11175264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmRGWeights(k): RG weight of the RG s to which the non-GBR bearer k belongs as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For references, rdRGMinLowShareDL ([11193687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and rdRGTinyWeightReciDL ([11193780](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvvjqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[CB014699-D] if bearer k identified as critical non-GBR traffic (criticalDrbTraffic, see [13393326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_lHKq4wZMEfGozuGzRUXHpg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), the rrmRGWeights(k) shall be assigned with calculated maximum value (refer to [13415176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_F0x3RhOYEfG3gf_qmG8Vww?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) to ensures that critical non-GBR can obtain a larger share of resources independent of the configured slice target share or quota [End CB014699-D].

If NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to value DL or DLAndUL and NRCELL.actPtpMetric is set to true, then gNB-DU shall calculate a PTP (Packet Tail Prioritization) metric for each user i and shall multiply the calculated PTP metric to the [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmUeSchedWeight[i, t]". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be part of a mathematical formula or code. The letters are black and slightly blurred, suggesting the image may be a low-resolution scan or screenshot.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121225.png[/image\_path][/image-2], derived above, as described below:

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmUeSchedWeight[i, t]". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be part of a mathematical formula or code. The letters are black and slightly blurred, suggesting the image may be a low-resolution scan or screenshot.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121225.png[/image\_path][/image-3] = [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmUeSchedWeight[i, t]". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be part of a mathematical formula or code. The letters are black and slightly blurred, suggesting the image may be a low-resolution scan or screenshot.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121225.png[/image\_path][/image-4] \* PTP\_metric(i,t)

For PTP\_metric(i,t) calculation refer to [12784805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qvlxco-nEfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qvlxco-nEfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9").

When ext-L2-PS receives from prime-gNB a notification to stop the specifc non-GBR bearer(s) data scheduling of the ext-SCell, the DL TD-scheduler in ext-L2-PS shall exclude the NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight for the specific non-GBR bearer(s) to calculate rrmUeSchedWeight[i,t] for the ext-SCell UE.

Note: rrmUeSchedWeight is used for the initialization of rrmAvgDlDataRate[i,t0] as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2. A specific value may be applied for time varying scheduling weight factors.
