#### 3.29.5.2.0-3 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Scheduling weight for non-GBR bearer (ID: 11175240)

**3.29.5.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175245`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
If UE i belongs to NBR category within drbTraffic Class (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

SchedWeightFactor[i,k,t]: nbrDl[i,k], where k is index of NBR configured non-GBR bearer with data in buffer for UE with rrmNbrTokenBucket[i,k,t] > 0

Else

SchedWeightFactor[i,k,t]:  schedulWeight[i,k], where k is index of non-GBR bearer k for UE i at slot t with data in buffer

where,

nbrDl[i,k] is given by the NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.nbrDl of NBR configured nonGBR bearer k for UE i

schedulWeight is given by NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight of non-GBR bearer k for UE i

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-4 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL DRX scheduling weighting factor (ID: 11175249)

**3.29.5.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175252`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
It is beneficial to schedule the DRX UEs with data for new DL transmission in active time. For this purpose, weighting factor WDL, DRX\_active is introduced for DRX UEs. The weighting factor WDL, DRX\_active shall increase the UE’s PF metric for new transmission in the time domain scheduler by pre-defined scaling factor when ON DURATION Timer is running for the UE.

Usage of this weighting factor WDL, DRX\_active shall be enabled/disabled via MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/drxWactDlEnabled.

The actual value of WDL, DRX\_active shall be an R&D configurable parameter drxWactDl.

When drxWactDlEnabled is enabled, the values WDL, DRX\_active as configured via parameter drxWactDl shall be used. Otherwise WDL, DRX\_active = 1 shall be assumed. If DRX has not been enabled for the cell or has not been configured for a UE i at all, also WDL, DRX\_active = 1 shall be assumed. By default, R&D configurable parameter drxWactDl should be 1.1.

For the initialization of rrmAvgDlDataRate[i,t0] as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2, WDL, DRX\_active = 1 shall be assumed.

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-5 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL CA DSS scheduling weighting factor (ID: 11175257)

**3.29.5.2.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11175261`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
For FDD-TDD  (FR1) CA UE or for F+F CA UE with P(S)Cell on DSS carrier or with a 5MHz P(S)Cell, 5G-L2-PS(of DSS P(S)Cell or with 5 MHz P(S)Cell) shall apply a DSS CA specific weighting factor WCA,DSS given by rdNbCaPfMetricWeight [range 0...10, default 0.5] defined in 5G\_L2\_1386\_replaced([11184521](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27r6MvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: if all the serving cells are either DSS cells or 5 MHz cells (including some cells as 5 MHz and some cells DSS), then rdNbCaPfMetricWeight shall not be applied.

In other cases, WCA,DSS = 1 shall be assumed.

Rationale: NR-allocated resources on a narrowband FDD carrier shared with LTE are scarce, especially PDCCH. So, the best solution is to avoid splitting DRB buffer and enforce per NIDD configuration that all non-Voice DRB data is transmitted via the high-bandwidth serving cell (NRBTS/NRCELL/primaryPathMaxQueuingDelay=0ms). However, especially for 15 and 20Mhz and lower TDD bandwidths than 100 MHZ, some operators may insist on split bearer to maintain the single UEs peak throughput KPI (NRBTS/NRCELL/primaryPathMaxQueuingDelay=10ms - Def.). In order to be able to de-prioritize the CA UEs against non-CA UEs in case there is a mix of both UE types, this R&D parameter is introduced.

Note: For CA cases where PCell is DSS or 5MHz FDD, the intention is to push as much data via Scells as possible and utilize the good PCell coverage only for signaling, VoNR, GBR and any PUSCH transmissions, as explained in the rationale.

However, for CA cases where SCell is DSS or 5MHz FDD, the Scell was explicitly added as Scell by the operator. So, operator’s expectation is to give fair chance in DSS Scell or 5MHz FDD even to CA UEs compared to non-CA UEs. Therefore, WCA,DSS = 1 in DSS or 5MHz FDD Scells.

This requirement applies to both SA and NSA.

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-6 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL DL CA DPA scheduling weighting factor (ID: 11175264)

**3.29.5.2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11175269`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
For the CA UEs with a PUCCH resource assigned by DPA in a given slot, it is beneficial to increase the probability of scheduling such UEs, as otherwise the scarce PCell’s PUCCH resources are wasted.

For this purpose, weighting factor is introduced for CA UEs with a PUCCH resource assigned by DPA in a given slot.

The weighting factor WPf, DPA shall increase the UE’s PF metric for new transmission in the time domain scheduler by pre-defined scaling factor, but only in slots and on carriers where DPA has assigned a PUCCH resource for this UE.

The actual value of WPf, DPA shall be an R&D configurable parameter rdDpaPfWeightTd, defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8833\_replaced([11193293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvrSOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For UEs which PUCCH resource have not been assigned by DPA, WPf, DPA = 1 shall be assumed.

If average cs2 list size per subcell before FD Scheduler avgCs2SetSizePerSubcellBeforeFd is smaller than or equal to rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10585\_replaced([11179018](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku3Z4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or FDM is disabled, WPf, DPA = 1 shall be assumed.

Rationale: if on average less UEs are scheduled than possible for the scheduler, then CA UEs will be scheduled in any case, and there is no need for boosting them in TD scheduler, while deboosting in FD scheduler would cause impairments in the fairness.

If Multi User MIMO is activated, then on top of the above condition

 MAC-PS shall sum up the CS2 list sizes of all MU-MIMO user groups per subcell before FD scheduler and apply the averaging filter defined below to the result. The result of the averaging shall be stored as avgMuMimoCs2SetSizePerSubcellBeforeFd.

* MAC-PS shall sum up the CS2 list sizes of all MU-MIMO user groups per subcell after FD scheduler and apply the averaging filter defined below to the result. The result of the averaging shall be stored as avgMuMimoCs2SetSizePerSubcellAfterFd.

 if avgMuMimoCs2SetSizePerSubcellAfterFd is equal to averagedavgMuMimoCs2SetSizePerSubcellBeforeFd or FDM is disabled, WPf, DPA = 1 shall be assumed.

Note: the rationale for the this condition is the volatility in the number and size of MU MIMO user groups when UEs are moving.

When calculation the  averaged CS2 set size for any of avgCs2SetSizePerSubcellBeforeFd , avgMuMimoCs2SetSizePerSubcellBeforeFd or vgMuMimoCs2SetSizePerSubcellAfterFd the following filter shall be used:

The averaged CS2 set size is initialized to 0 when the (sub)cell is setup and is updated every slot (n) by an exponential filter defined below:

avgCs2SetSize(n) = (1-rdCs2SizeFilterConst) \* avgCs2SetSize(n-1) +rdCs2SizeFilterConst\*Cs2SetSize(n)

with:

avgCs2SetSize(n): averaged CS2 set size;

rdCs2SizeFilterConst: filter constant of the exponential filter (R&D parameter defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11016\_replaced([11193357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvr5S_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Cs2SetSize(n):  CS2 set size per subcell

* before FD scheduler in case of avgCs2SetSizePerSubcellBeforeFd or avgMuMimoCs2SetSizePerSubcellBeforeFd ,
* after FD scheduler in case of  avgMuMimoCs2SetSizePerSubcellAfterFd.

For the initialization of rrmAvgDlDataRate[i,t0] as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2, WPf, DPA = 1 shall be assumed as it is expected that the long term average data rate is not impacted by the scheduling weight (see note).

The scope of the scaling defined in this requirement ("only Scells" or "PCell and Scells") is controlled by the parameter rdDpaActPfboostPcell according 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8884\_replaced([11193312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvr5QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: The influence of this PF boosting in TD scheduler on scheduling fairnes is mitigated in the FD scheduler by assigning less PRBs to the UE, cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8331\_replaced([11181145](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85e_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-7 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Packet Tail Prioritization Metric for UEs with non-GBR bearers (ID: 12784805)

**3.29.5.2.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `12786479`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
12786479:
Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
If NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to a value DL or DLAndUL and NRCELL.actPtpMetric is set to true, gNb-DU shall calculate a Packet Tail Prioritization metric for an user meeting the following conditions listed below:

* the user must be a FR1 user
* the user must belong to largest BWP if multiple BWP is configured in the cell
* the user must have one or more non-GBR DRBs from drbTraffic class, including the NBR category, large buffer non-GBR category and then normal non-GBR (including the deprioritizedNBR and deprioritizedGbr due to excess data, deprioritizedVoip, dtxOptimizedTraffic, Subband beamforming UEs belonging to drbTraffic class) category.

gNB-DU shall calculate the PTP\_metric as shown below:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in italics. The formula is:
Ptp\_Metric[u,t] = min(rdMaxPtpMetric, max(1/TPtp(u), rdMinPtpMetric))[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121307.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "TP tp(u)". The text is in a dark color with a slight blue and red outline, giving it a glowing effect. The letters are clear and legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121308.png[/image\_path][/image-1] is the number of slots estimated for scheduling all the data for a UE u and shall be computed as shown below:

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$T\_{Ptp}(u) = \frac{data(u)}{tbsMax(u)}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121308.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

where:

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "data(u)" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are dark blue with a slight red and green outline, giving them a chromatic aberration effect. The text is centered in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121309.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is the amount of total outstanding data in buffer for the user u

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "tbsMax(u)". The text is in a dark blue color with a slight gradient and a white outline. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121310.png[/image\_path][/image-4]is the maximum TBS for UE u assuming the UE can get all the PRBs in the cell or the BWP the UE may belong to (largest in this case) using the rrmDlProposedMcs (calculated here [11125301: Determination of the DL MCS index](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) ).

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rdMax PtpMetric". The text is in a dark blue color with a slight gradient and is set against a light, blurry background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121318.png[/image\_path][/image-5] is the upper bound of the PTP metric, such that it does not lead to very large PTP metric (and hence TD metric) for any user. See [12784864: rdMaxPtpMetric](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_8icOYY-vEfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "radialinptometric". The text is blurry and appears to be in a stylized font with a gradient effect.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121318.png[/image\_path][/image-6] is the lower bound for the PTP metric, ensuring that the multiplier is not too small and is only applied to UEs with small enough data in buffer. See [12784860: rdMinPtpMetric](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_xQEiQo-vEfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Rationale: With exhaustive scheduling, gNB-DU shall try to drain the buffered bytes for the user as soon as possible to improve the average user throughput. The sooner the trailing bytes can be drained, better for the user throughput. The proposed method increases the priority of the users with trailing bytes in accordance to the number of slots each user would require to drain the trailing bytes considering the current data rate for the user assuming the user can get all the PRBs in the cell/BWP .

Note: The default value of [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "radialinptometric". The text is blurry and appears to be in a stylized font with a gradient effect.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121318.png[/image\_path][/image-7] =0.1 ensures that the TD metric modification is only done to those small buffer users that require maximum up to 10 slots to drain out the data.[image-8][image\_desc]The image is a line graph titled "PTP metric". The x-axis is labeled "Slots worth traffic" and ranges from 0 to 14. The y-axis is labeled "PTP metric" and ranges from 0 to 1.2. The graph shows a downward trend, starting at a PTP metric of 1 at 1 slot worth of traffic and decreasing as the slots worth of traffic increase. There are data points plotted at approximately (1, 1), (2, 0.5), (3, 0.33), (4, 0.25), (5, 0.2), (6, 0.18), (7, 0.16), (8, 0.15), (9, 0.14), (10, 0.14), (11, 0.14), and (12, 0.14).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09121430.png[/image\_path][/image-8]

The graph above shows the relation of the PTP metric with the slots required to drain the buffered traffic for an user.

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-8 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Convergence behavior of UE per carrier TD scheduling PF metric in DL and Average Throughput calcuation values (ID: 11175273)

**3.29.5.2.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11175288`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
11175288:
Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The implementation of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))'s PF metric formula (\*\*) for different schedulingWeights of non-GBR bearers, as well as the calculated average data rate (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4243\_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) on different UEs shall lead to a quick and reliable convergence of PF metric.

The example for equal QCIs and scheduling weights among UEs below shall be a guideline for convergence for the following multi-UE, multi DRB configuration.

All UEs are started with the same full buffer rate at the same time using a filter constant of 1000. For initialization the Initial MCS is 10 with rank 1. The number of PRBs is 273.

* UE 1 (blue) with 2 bearers with QCI 9 ( schedulWeight 1 )
* UE 2 ( orange hidden behind pink) with 1 bearer QCI 9 ( schedulWeight 1 )
* UE 3 (green hidden behind pink) with 1 bearer QCI 9 ( schedulWeight 1 )
* UE 4 (pink) with 1 bearer QCI 9 ( schedulWeight 1 )

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains two plots side-by-side.
The left plot is titled "PF Metric value 5G\_RAN\_UP\_11869". The y-axis is labeled from 0 to 9, and the x-axis is labeled from 0 to 4000. There are two lines plotted: a blue line that stays constant at approximately 2 from x=0 to x=600 and then increases slightly, and a pink line that starts at approximately 1 at x=0 and increases steadily to approximately 4.5 at x=4000. There is also a single pink data point at y=8.5 at x=0. A legend at the bottom indicates that the data points are labeled 1, 2, 3, and 4, with corresponding colors blue, yellow, green, and pink.
The right plot is titled "UE Average TP value [bytes] 5G\_RAN\_UP\_11896". The y-axis is labeled from 0 to 25000, and the x-axis is labeled from 0 to 4000. There are two lines plotted: a blue line that starts at approximately 23000 at x=0, stays constant until x=700, and then decreases to approximately 12000 at x=4000. A pink line starts at approximately 23000 at x=0 and decreases steadily to approximately 5000 at x=4000. A legend at the bottom indicates that the data points are labeled 1, 2, 3, and 4, with corresponding colors blue, yellow, green, and pink.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13047.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Above PF Metric and average throughput are internal values determined for each DL transmission/scheduling opportunity (time axis) described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4114\_replaced([11173803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuWch_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The UE's actual DL Throughput for the example is given in the next figure (average with a sliding window of 60 scheduling opportunities) on time axis:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a line graph titled "UE Actual TP [bytes] (Sliding Average 60 iterations)". The y-axis represents "bytes" and ranges from 0 to 25000. The x-axis represents iterations and ranges from 0 to 4000. There are four lines plotted on the graph, indicated by a legend at the bottom: "1" (blue dots), "2" (yellow dots), "3" (green dots), and "4" (pink dots).
The blue line (1) starts at approximately 22500 bytes, remains constant until around iteration 600, then drops sharply to approximately 9500 bytes and stays constant until the end of the graph.
The pink line (4) starts at 0 bytes, remains constant until around iteration 600, then jumps to approximately 5000 bytes and stays constant until the end of the graph.
The yellow line (2) and green line (3) are very faint and appear to be at or near 0 bytes for the entire duration of the graph, with a slight upward spike around iteration 4000.
The graph shows a significant drop in "UE Actual TP" for the blue line around iteration 600, and a corresponding increase for the pink line. The yellow and green lines show minimal activity.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13047.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

A stable DL throughput for all UEs shall be achieved quick after a certain time when PF Metric for all UE's has reached to a common value. UEs with a higher number of DRBs are scheduled first until PF metric is equal.

In the example UE 1 with 2 bearers is finally having doubled throughput compared to the other UEs with only 1 bearer. UE 1 with a higher number of bearers has a higher priority than the UE 2,3 and 4 having only 1 bearer. UE 1 is scheduled as long alone until PF Metric for the other UE has approached the same level than UE 1.

The example for different QCIs and scheduling weights among UEs below shall be a guideline for convergence for the following multi-UE, multi DRB configuration.

All UEs are started with the same full buffer rate at the same time using a filter constant of 1000. For initialization the Initial MCS is 10 with rank 1. The number of PRBs is 273.

* UE 1 (blue) with 2 bearers QCI 7 and QCI 9 ( schedulWeight 10 and 1 )
* UE 2 ( orange) with 1 bearer QCI 8 ( schedulWeight 5 )
* UE 3 (green) with 1 bearer QCI 9 ( schedulWeight 1 )
* UE 4 (pink) with 1 bearer QCI 6 ( schedulWeight 20 )

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains two line graphs side-by-side, both with a white background and a grid.
The left graph is titled "PF Metric value 5G\_RAN\_UP\_11869". The y-axis is labeled from 0 to 50 in increments of 5. The x-axis is labeled from 0 to 4000 in increments of 500. There are four lines plotted:
- A light blue line starting at approximately 11 at x=0, increasing to approximately 18 at x=500, and continuing to increase to approximately 32 at x=4000.
- An orange line starting at approximately 5 at x=0, increasing to approximately 10 at x=500, and continuing to increase to approximately 25 at x=1500, then leveling off to approximately 25 at x=4000.
- A green line starting at approximately 1 at x=0, increasing slowly to approximately 3 at x=1000, then increasing more rapidly to approximately 32 at x=3500, and leveling off to approximately 32 at x=4000.
- A pink line starting at approximately 45 at x=0, decreasing to approximately 20 at x=500, then increasing slowly to approximately 27 at x=4000.
Below the left graph, there is a legend with four colored dots and numbers: "1" (light blue), "2" (orange), "3" (green), and "4" (pink).
The right graph is titled "UE Average TP value [bytes] 5G\_RAN\_UP\_11896". The y-axis is labeled from 0 to 25000 in increments of 5000. The x-axis is labeled from 0 to 4000 in increments of 500. There are four lines plotted:
- A pink line starting at approximately 22500 at x=0, decreasing to approximately 21000 at x=500, and continuing to decrease slowly to approximately 15000 at x=4000.
- A light blue line starting at approximately 21000 at x=0, decreasing to approximately 12000 at x=500, and continuing to decrease to approximately 7500 at x=4000.
- A green line starting at approximately 22500 at x=0, decreasing sharply to approximately 12000 at x=500, and continuing to decrease to approximately 1000 at x=4000.
- An orange line starting at approximately 4500 at x=0, decreasing to approximately 4000 at x=500, and remaining relatively constant at approximately 4000 from x=500 to x=4000.
Below the right graph, there is a legend with four colored dots and numbers: "1" (light blue), "2" (orange), "3" (green), and "4" (pink).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13047.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Above PF Metric and average DL throughput are internal values determined for each DL transmission/scheduling opportunity described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4114\_replaced([11173803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuWch_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The UE's actual DL throughput for the example is given in the next figure (average with a sliding window of 60 scheduling opportunities):

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a line graph titled "UE Actual TP [bytes] (Sliding Average 60 iterations)". The y-axis represents "bytes" and ranges from 0 to 25000. The x-axis represents "iterations" and ranges from 0 to 4000. There are four lines plotted on the graph, each representing a different data series, indicated by colored dots in the legend at the bottom:
\* **Line 1 (Blue dots):** This line starts at approximately 7500 bytes around iteration 600, stays constant until around iteration 1600, then drops to approximately 6500 bytes, and remains at this level until around iteration 3400, where it slightly increases and then decreases again towards the end of the graph.
\* **Line 2 (Yellow dots):** This line starts at 0 bytes and remains there until around iteration 1600, where it jumps to approximately 3500 bytes and stays constant until the end of the graph.
\* **Line 3 (Green dots):** This line remains at 0 bytes for most of the graph, with a slight increase to around 500 bytes starting from iteration 3400.
\* **Line 4 (Pink dots):** This line starts at approximately 22500 bytes around iteration 0, drops sharply to around 15000 bytes at iteration 600, and then remains at this level with a slight dip around iteration 1600 and another dip around iteration 3500.
The graph shows how the "UE Actual TP" (likely Throughput) changes over iterations, with a sliding average applied.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13047.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

A stable DL throughput for all UEs shall be achieved moderately fast after a certain time when PF Metric for all UE's has reached to a common value. UEs with QCI leading to small scheduling weights will take longer to be scheduled, while UE with higher scheduling weights are served first.

PF Metric and average throughput figures are re-initialized after a change in the non-GBR service configuration is happening on the UE.

A PF Metric, average throughput and actual throughput simulation tool based on MS Excel shall be available (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22284\_replaced([11186903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXJI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-9 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Definition of per UE and per carrier DL scheduling metric for UEs with carrier aggregation (ID: 11175292)

**3.29.5.2.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11175297`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The proportional fair (PF) scheduling metric (rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i, j, t]) specified for the UE with a single carrier (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be applied individually for each of the carriers j, which belongs either to the PCell or to one of the SCells of the UEs’ serving carriers.

Consequently, all the calculated PF terms affect implicitly the evaluation of the per beam and per carrier metric for analog beam forming (5GC000527-B) as well as the final x-pol beam selection according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The proportional fair (PF) scheduling metric (rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i, j, t]) of any carrier for UE in bad radio conditions and with rrmPauseUeInDlScheduling=TRUE as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4351\_replaced([11177959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be excluded from computation of PF metric and thus shall be reset to 0 for that corresponding carrier, and PF scheduling metric is calculated by using the initial value of rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] for that corresponding carrier as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4242\_replaced([11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the first time slot after the R&D parameter rdPeriodDlScheduling timer defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4351\_replaced([11177959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) expires.

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-10 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Carriers eligible for scheduling (ID: 11175300)

**3.29.5.2.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11175304`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
If rrmBufferStatusSplitNeeded == 1, the buffer status split determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2833\_replaced([11122261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqeCHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) needs to be taken into account for CA UEs. Thus, the PF metric shall only be calculated for active carriers having a buffer split factor > 0 as determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2841\_replaced([11122293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqepJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If UE is requested for pausing in scheduling as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4351\_replaced([11177959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) by means of the parameter rrmPauseUeInDlScheduling (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3068\_replaced([11129535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq8jN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) TD metric per carrier defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4231\_replaced([11175148](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0m_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall not be computed.

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-11 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Definition of the current 5G UE DL Data Rate (ID: 11175309)

**3.29.5.2.0-11.0-4**  (ID: `12641954`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The current DL data rate of a 5G UE, which is valid in NR slot t for the carrier j, shall be evaluated based on the 5G UEs’ current MCS (rrmDlMcs[i, j, t] provided by the link adaptation and hence the MCS before MCS downgrade) and the maximum number of PRBs (rrmMaxNumPrbDl[i, j, t]) that can be allocated for the 5G UE. Furthermore, the rank of the DL transmission shall be taken into account for the evaluation of the current DL data rate. Corresponding to the MCS, the number of PRBs and the rank of the DL transmission, so the transport block size shall be computed with the transport block size table defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4635\_replaced([11192394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvkkgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using nb\_symb =13.

For FDD NR and TDD NR: nb\_cdm\_no\_data = the number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data which is decided with current MCS according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3054\_replaced([11129150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). nb\_dmrs\_symb shall be determined from additional DMRS configuration which is derived from the latest PsUser\_SetupReq or PsUser\_UserModifyReq message.

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE [CB007372-AA] when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-AA], 5G-L2-PS shall use nb\_symb=11, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2382\_replaced([11115299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kptNEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB007372-AA] On an FDD carrier shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true, 5G-L2-PS shall use nb\_symb=12 [CB007372-B] if no coreset overlaps the LTE shared area ([12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), otherwise nb\_symb=11 [End CB007372-B] [End CB007372-AA]

rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] = f(rrmDlMcs[i,j,t],rrmMaxNumPrbDl[i,j,t], rrmDlRank[i,j,t])

rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[i,j,t]  = (rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t])^(αDL)

Rationale: This requirement modifies the PF term “rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot” to “rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha”. The form “rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot” assumes DL alpha αDL= 1, while the form “rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot^αDL” allows DL alpha to either remain 1 or be set to some other value. This feature enables DL alpha to be modified for DL PF calculation based on the configurable TD metric alpha.

It is left to implementation to optimize the “rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot^αDL” exponentiation calculation, lookup table, or other better approach that can be considered.

with:

i: Index of the 5G UE

j: Index of the carrier

t: NR slot the calculation relates to

The current 5G UE DL data rate shall be provided in bytes per NR slot.

Note that in 5G18A release, all PRBs are always allocated in DL.

The maximum number of PRBs is defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For the first time slot after the R&D parameter rdPeriodDlScheduling timer defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4351\_replaced([11177959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) expires the current DL data rate of a 5G UE, rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] while j is any carrier which has been paused in scheduling before, shall be re-initialized. according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3045\_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), i.e.,

rrmDlMcs[i, j, t] is the default MCS (dllaIniMcs), rrmMaxNumPrbDl[i, j, t]) is defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), rrmDlRank[i,j,t] is the Rank1 initial transmissions in DL.

αDL is configurable TD metric alpha for DL, defined by NRCELL.dlsPfAlpha. It allows operator flexibility to favor cell edge or cell center users.

αDL> 1 favors scheduling of UEs with good channel conditions (like in cell center) at the expense of others. scheduler becomes more and more a maximum rate scheduler with a higher value set.

αDL< 1, favors scheduling of UEs with bad channel conditions (like in cell edge) at the expense of others, scheduler becomes more and more a rate fair scheduler with a lower value set.

αDL= 1 set as a default value, which means to disable configurable TD metric alpha by default.

If MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/eirpControl.schedulWeightFactorEnabled is TRUE and EIRP control is active for a given cell segment (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2614\_replaced([11118932](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqG1u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the current DL data rate of a 5G UE shall be adjusted considering that the maximum number of PRB it is allowed to be scheduled is proportional to the rrmNormalizedEirpBudget in the given cell segment of the beam it is associated to (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8084\_replaced([11118921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqG1uPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If EIRP control is active simultaneously in different cell segments, only the smallest rrmNormalizedEirpBudget is considered. No adjustment is performed if rrmNormalizedEirpBudget is greater than one (ie the beam is not concerned by the power reduction).

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-12 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Calculation of the average 5G UE DL Data Rate (ID: 11175317)

**3.29.5.2.0-12.0-4**  (ID: `12811425`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
12811425:
Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The average data rate of the 5G UE in DL shall be calculated per NR slot based on the size of the granted transport blocks as long as the 5G UE is part of the Candidate Set 1 either on a per UE basis (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4243\_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-1) or on a per carrier basis (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4243\_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2). NR slots during that the 5G UE is not scheduled but is included in the pre-scheduler candidate set are considered as well for the calculation of the average DL data rate. The number of slot since last scheduling during which the UE was included in pre-scheduling candidate set shall be counted in rrmNumSlotSinceLastDlSched and an exponential filter shall calculate rrmAvgDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot, which is the average TBS in byte per NR slot.

rrmAvgDlUeDataRate computation on a per UE basis:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
`rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,t] = (1 - 1/β)^n × rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[it-n] + 1/β × Σ_{j} rrmDlSumOfTransportBlockSize [i,j,t]`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13056.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4243\_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-1)

with:

i: Index of the 5G UE

t: NR slot the calculation relates to

n:  rrmNumSlotSinceLastDlSched

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract representation of the letter "B". The letter is rendered in a gradient of colors, transitioning from a light beige on the left to shades of blue, white, and purple in the center, and finally to a dark black on the right. The overall impression is one of soft, diffused light and color.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13056.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]:  rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst

rrmDlSumOfTransportBlockSize[i,j,t]: the aggregated traffic volume in bytes for all the 1st transmission transport blocks that are scheduled before FD-POLITE attempts (see [12533165: FD-POLITE widening PRB usage by reducing UE MCS and Power](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_jqYSRBw4EfCQJ5daVHVkgg))) for the 5G UE with index i on carrier j during the NR slot t as long as the 5G UE is in Candidate Set 1.

rrmAvgDlUeDataRate computation on a per carrier basis:

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt = (DL or DL\_UL) {

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is:
$rrm Avg DIUe Data Rate[i, j,t] = (1 - \frac{1}{\beta})^n \* rrm Avg DIUe Data Rate[i,j,t-n] + \frac{1}{\beta} \* instDIUeData Rate [i, j, t]$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/5115dd2f-0731-4c71-91d2-76b86ab04646.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[CB014044-B-UPD003]  
[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula is:
rrm AvgDlUe Data Rate[i, j,t] = max (avg DlUe Data Rate Lower Threshold[i,j,t], rrmAvgDlUe Data Rate[i, j, t])[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/644f3fc7-941b-478d-bf54-c5744babf87c.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

[End CB014044-B-UPD003]

(5G\_UP\_ALG\_4243\_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2)

}

Else {

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
`rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,j,t] = (1 - 1/β)^n × rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,j,t-n] + 1/β × rrmDlSumOfTransportBlockSize[i,j,t]`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13056.003.png[/image\_path][/image-4](5G_UP_ALG_4243_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2)

}

[CB014044-B-UPD003] Rationale for introducing lower limit on rrmAvgDlUeDataRate: With the fast decaying filter coefficient used for averaging UE data rate, the UE average data rate reaches quite lower values within a shorter period and possibly due to DRX state of UEs, it might be needed to decay the UE data rate for more than 100 ms which introduces higher fluctuation in the cell average metric. So, it was agreed that introducing a lower limit on the UE average data rate which can reduce the fluctuation in cell average metric leading to better comparision of new incoming UEs. [End CB014044-B-UPD003]

with:

i: Index of the 5G UE

t: NR slot the calculation relates to

n:  rrmNumSlotSinceLastDlSched

[image-5][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract representation of the letter "B". The letter is rendered in a gradient of colors, transitioning from a light beige on the left to shades of blue, white, and purple in the center, and finally to a dark black on the right. The overall impression is one of soft, diffused light and color.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13056.002.png[/image\_path][/image-5]:   rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst

[CB014044-B-UPD003]

avgDlUeDataRateLowerThreshold[i,j,t]: The lower bound on the avgDataRate in DL for the UE i in carrier j in slot t. It shall be calculated as rdMinExpectedBwSharePerUe \* rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[i,j,t]. Please refer to  [12811470: rdMinExpectedBwSharePerUe](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_E6Q9oKQ4EfCBvZf8dodgmQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for rdMinExpectedBwSharePerUe and [11175309: Definition of the current 5G UE DL Data Rate](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha definition.

[End CB014044-B-UPD003]

rrmDlSumOfTransportBlockSize[i,j,t]: the aggregated traffic volume in bytes for all the 1st transmission transport blocks that are scheduled before FD-POLITE attempts (see [12533165: FD-POLITE widening PRB usage by reducing UE MCS and Power](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_jqYSRBw4EfCQJ5daVHVkgg))) for the 5G UE with index i on carrier j during the NR slot t as long as the 5G UE is in Candidate Set 1.

instDlUeDataRate[i,j,t]: The instantaneous DL UE data rate of the UE i on carrier j during the NR slot t calculated as described in [12517558](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_KGcFoBhhEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

The filter constant rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst shall be implemented by an R&D parameter. For an FDD cell, it shall be rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConstFdd, see [12533643: rdAvgDlUEDataRateFilterConstFdd](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_SBKmwBxiEfCQJ5daVHVkgg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). For a TDD FR1 cell, it shall be rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConstTddFr1, see [12552044: rdAvgDlUEDataRateFilterConstTddFr1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_JBPwoSTBEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and for a TDD FR2 cell, see 5G\_L2\_1023\_replaced([11181966](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27XxK_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For the initialization of the exponential filter, the average data rate shall be derived from the scheduling conditions in the previous NR slot in the carrier j as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The exponential filter shall be initialized the 1st time that the PF metric has to be computed after the UE setup.

The exponential filter shall be re-initialized in the following cases:

* when the UE re-enters the pre-scheduler candidate set with new data in DRB buffer(coming back from nullBuffer state) after staying out for longer than MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL.avgDataRateReinitProhibitTimer; Reinitialization is done at most only once after the UE returns to nonNullBuffer state from nullBuffer state.
* with each change in the set of the non-GBR bearers and hence with each change of the overall schedulWeight.

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-13 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Definition of Instantaneous DL Data Rate (ID: 12517558)

**3.29.5.2.0-13.0-2**  (ID: `12821545`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
12821545:
Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The instantaneous DL data rate of a 5G UE i, namely instDlUeDataRate[i,j,t], in bytes per slot, which is valid in NR slot t for the carrier j, shall be evaluated as function of 5G current UE data rate of the UE along with the FD scheduling cost and/or TD scheduling cost due to the scheduling of the UE in the slot. In the slot when the UE is not scheduled, it shall be 0.

For the calculation of (instDlUeDataRate[i,j,t]):

When NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt = (DL or DL\_UL) [Before PR864769]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is:
`inst DIUe DataRate[i, j, t] = max(current DIUeDataRateWithTdCost[i, j, t], current DIUe DataRateWithFdCost[i, j, t])`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/592052bf-94f6-4fcd-844d-7b39b614921e.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR864769]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is:
instDlUe Data Rate[i, j, t] = max(currentDlUeData RateWithTdCost[i, j, t], current DlUe Data RateWithFdCost[i, j, t], rrmDlSumOfTransport BlockSize[i, j, t])[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/4b24a242-14de-4494-9e3b-307d4e4964cc.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End PR864769]

with

* currentDlUeDataRateWithTdCost[i,j,t] is the cost of scheduling the UE i in TD scheduler on carrier j in NR slot t calculated as below: [Before PR864769]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is:
`currentDlUeDataRateWithTdCost[i, j, t] = rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i, j, t] / maxNumFdUe[j, t]`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/3975abbf-e223-484f-b617-243f6042aae6.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[PR864769]

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is:
`current DlUe Data RateWithTdCost[i, j,t] = rrmCurrent DlUe Data Rate Bytes Per Slot[i,j,t]/maxNumFdUe[j,t]`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/f6bb0ada-d390-40dd-8e03-d34dac194dbc.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

[End PR864769]

* fdSchedulingCost[i,j,t] is the cost of scheduling the UE i in FD scheduler on carrier j in NR slot t calculated as below: [Before PR864769]

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a single line of mathematical text. The text is:
currentDlUeDataRateWithFdCost[i, j, t] = rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[i, j,t] \* allocFdResources[i, j, t]/usableFdResources[j,t][/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/dd97f783-9c89-4a12-8fb9-97e062b350bf.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

[PR864769]

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is:
`currentDlUeDataRateWithFdCost[i, j,t] = rrmCurrentDlUeDataRateBytesPerSlot[i, j, t] * allocFdResources[i, j,t]/usableFdResources[j, t]`
The equation appears to be defining a variable `currentDlUeDataRateWithFdCost` at indices `i`, `j`, and `t`. This variable is calculated by multiplying `currentDlUeDataRateBytesPerSlot` at the same indices by `allocFdResources` at indices `i`, `j`, and `t`, and then dividing the result by `usableFdResources` at indices `j` and `t`. The term `rrm` at the beginning of the right-hand side might be a constant or a function. The indices `i`, `j`, and `t` suggest that this equation is part of a larger system involving multiple entities or dimensions, possibly related to data rates, resource allocation, and time.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/b595691d-0182-4fe2-98f7-b3f0883e95d8.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

[End PR864769]

* [Before PR864769]rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[i,j,t] is the the current DL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in slot t in bytes per slot as the described in [11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).
* [PR864769]rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot [i,j,t] is the the current DL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in slot t in bytes per slot as the described in [11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). [End PR864769]
* allocFdResources[i,j,t] is the number of allocated PDSCH FD resources before FD-POLITE attempts (see [12533165: FD-POLITE widening PRB usage by reducing UE MCS and Power](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_jqYSRBw4EfCQJ5daVHVkgg))) for the UE i in the carrier j in NR slot t.
* usableFdResources[i,j,t] is the maximum number of available PDSCH FD resources in the carrier j in NR slot t.
* maxNumFdmUe[j,t] is the maximum number of allowed PDSCH allocation/grants in the slot t considering the BBRM pooling and overload control limitations on the cell/carrier j

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-14 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Initialization of the average UE DL Data Rate (ID: 11175354)

**3.29.5.2.0-14.0-5**  (ID: `12737712`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
12737712:
Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
For the initialization of the exponential filter, the average data rate rrmAvgDlDataRate[i,t0] shall be derived from the scheduling conditions in the previous NR slot in the carrier j.
 
If NRBTS.NRCELL.actPfMetricReinitSchedUes = false {
For this purpose, the average PF metric among all the 5G UEs in Candidate Set 1 served by carrier j shall be calculated where the 5G UE i is configured:
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j, t₀] = (1/N) \* Σ\_{all UEᵢ in CS1} mmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i, j, t₀ - 1]
This equation defines a variable `initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier` at indices `j` and `t₀` as the average of another variable `mmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier` over all User Equipments (UEs) in a set called CS1, at indices `i`, `j`, and `t₀ - 1`. The average is taken by summing the values for all UEs in CS1 and dividing by `N`, which presumably represents the total number of UEs in CS1.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13058.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-1)
with
 
j: Index of carrier
N: number of UEs i in candidate set 1
rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i, j, t0-1]: the UE i DL scheduling PF metric as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
}
Else{
Basically, the initial scheduler metric is an average metric over the scheduled UEs with DRB priority class further time-averaged using an IIR filter (of the relevant direction, in this case DL).
if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False or
NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to a value other than 1 (for DL only) or 3 (for both DL and UL) // ES OFF
{
**[CB014044-UPD002]**
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL\_UL)
{
[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is:
initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t] = (1 - rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst) × initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t-1] + rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst × (Σi∈S rrmDIUeSchedMetricPerCarrierExclVarFactors[i, j,t – 1] / sizeof(S))[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/08a2c6a6-c684-4e75-97c2-1ce30754430d.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
}
Else
**[End CB014044-UPD002]**
{
[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula appears to be a recursive definition for a variable named `initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier` at time `t` and carrier `j`.
Here's a breakdown of the formula:
`initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t]` is defined as:
`= (1 - rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst) * initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j, t-1]) + rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst`
This part of the formula suggests a smoothing or filtering operation, where the current value is a weighted average of the previous value and a constant.
The formula is then multiplied by a fraction:
`X` (multiplication symbol)
`ΣίειrrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier [i,j,t-1]`
`--------------------------------------------------`
`Number of Scheduled DL UEs of DRB priority class in slot (t-1)`
The numerator is a summation over `i` belonging to set `El` of `rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier` at time `t-1` and carrier `j`. The denominator is the "Number of Scheduled DL UEs of DRB priority class in slot (t-1)". This fraction likely represents some form of normalization or adjustment factor based on the number of scheduled users.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13058.002.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
}
i: User index
j:Carrier index
t: time index
[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains text that appears to be from a technical document, likely related to telecommunications or computer networking. The text is in a serif font and is italicized.
The text reads: "1: {All UEs Scheduled of DRB priority class in slot (t-1)}"
This text likely refers to a condition or a state within a system, where "UEs" (User Equipment) are scheduled for a "DRB" (Data Radio Bearer) of a specific priority class in a particular time slot, denoted as "t-1".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13058.003.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
[CB014044-UPD002] S: All UEs scheduled of DRB priority class (excluding NBR sub-category) in slot t-1 [End CB014044-UPD002]
[Before CB014044-UPD002] rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst: R&D parameter. Refer to 5G\_L2\_9328\_replaced([11183534](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27j-ePvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB014044-UPD002]
In case there are no UEs scheduled in the previous slot (t-1) of DRB priority class **[CB014044-UPD002](If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL_UL): excluding NBR sub-category)**[End CB014044-UPD002] and there are no UEs of DRB priority class **[CB014044-UPD002](If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL_UL): excluding NBR sub-category)**[End CB014044-UPD002] in CS1 list, then the value of 0 is input to the filter in the current slot, **[Before CB014044-UPD002] i.e., set*****\*****[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is a fraction set equal to zero. The numerator is a summation of "rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i,j,t-1]" over the set "i ∈ I". The denominator is "Number of Scheduled DL UEs of DRB priority class in slot (t-1)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13058.004.png[/image\_path][/image-4]* ***[End CB014044-UPD002] i.e., filter is decayed.****In case there are no UEs scheduled in the previous slot (t-1) of DRB priority class**[CB014044-UPD002](If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL_UL): excluding NBR sub-category)**[End CB014044-UPD002] and there were UEs of DRB priority class**[CB014044-UPD002](If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL_UL): excluding NBR sub-category)**[End CB014044-UPD002] in CS1 list but just didn’t get scheduled, then the current value of initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t-1] is input to the filter in the current slot,**[Before CB014044-UPD002] i.e., set****\******[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is presented as a fraction on the left side of an equals sign, and a term on the right side.
The numerator of the fraction is: $\Sigma\_{i \in I} rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i,j,t-1]$
The denominator of the fraction is: Number of Scheduled DL UEs of DRB priority class in slot (t-1)
The right side of the equals sign is: $initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t-1]$
The formula appears to be calculating a metric related to scheduled downlink user equipment (UEs) per carrier, possibly for a specific DRB priority class in a given time slot.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13058.005.png[/image\_path][/image-5]**[End CB014044-UPD002] i.e., filter is frozen.**}
else if NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to a value 1 (for DL only) or 3 (for both DL and UL) // ES ON
{
The [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "initDlWeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i, t]". The text is blurry and appears to be in a programming context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07161905.png[/image\_path][/image-6] to be used for calculating the rrmAvgDlDataRate[i,t0], shall be derived from the users scheduled in the slot based on whether there are users of DRB priority class scheduled and if all or few of these users got their buffered bytes drained out and the status of the remaining PRBs after the scheduling in this slot is over, as shown below.
If there are UEs of DRB priority class[PR877549] (If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL\_UL): excluding NBR sub-category)[End PR877549] scheduled in this slot (t-1), for whom their respective buffered bytes are not drained out in slot (t-1) then:
[PR877549]
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt = (DL or DL\_UL):
[image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is:
init DIU eSchedMetric Per Carrier[j, t] = (1-rdPfMetricReInitFilterConst) \* init DIU e SchedMetricPerCarrier[j, t -1] + rdPf Metric ReInitFilterConst \* (Σi∈S' rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier ExclVar Factors[i, j,t – 1] / sizeof(S'))[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/07de975f-7e10-431a-b286-94583b12d28e.png[/image\_path][/image-7]
with
S': List of scheduled DL UEs of DRB priority class (excluding NBR sub-category) scheduled in slot (t-1) whose bufferedbytes have not got drained.
Else (NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt is neither DL nor DL\_UL):
[End PR877549]
[image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a dark blue font on a white background. The formula is presented on two lines.
The first line reads:
`initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t] = (1 - rdPfMetricReinitFilterConstant) * initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t-1] +`
The second line reads:
`rdPfMetricReinitFilterConstant * Σi ∈ scheduled DL UEs of DRB priority class whose buffered bytes not got drained rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i,j, t - 1] / Number of Scheduled DL UEs of DRB priority class in slot (t-1) whose buffered bytes not got drained`
The formula appears to be related to a scheduling metric in a telecommunications context, possibly involving downlink (DL) user equipment (UEs) and radio bearer (DRB) priority classes. The terms `initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier`, `rdPfMetricReinitFilterConstant`, and `rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier` suggest specific metrics and constants used in such systems. The summation symbol (Σ) indicates an aggregation over a set of scheduled DL UEs belonging to a particular DRB priority class. The fraction represents a ratio related to buffered bytes and scheduled UEs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07170934.png[/image\_path][/image-8]
Rationale: This prevents the low throughput UEs from influencing the cell-level metric. The UEs with low throughput (lower average data rate) drains out the buffer sooner relative to the UEs with higher throughput. This is because such small users are favored as they have low buffer and thus can be drained out faster. Once buffer emptied, it end up leading to reinitialize their individual exponential filters used for calculating the average data rate more often and thereby spiking their PF metrics, which when included in calculating the above cell-level metric causes the higher throughput UEs to miss scheduling more often due to comparatively lower PF metric. This negatively impacts the overall throughput for these UEs. So the denominator in second term above shall consider only those UEs for whom the buffer is still not emptied.
If at the end of slot (t-1) all the UEs of DRB priority class[PR877549] (If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL\_UL): excluding NBR sub-category)[End PR877549] scheduled have their respective buffered bytes drained out but there were still free PRBs remaining, then the [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains the text "initDlWeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i, t]". The text is blurry and appears to be in a programming context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07161905.png[/image\_path][/image-9] shall be recalculated as shown below:
[image-10][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in dark blue text on a white background. The formula is:
initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t] =
(1-rdPfMetricReinitFilterConstant) \* initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j, t - 1] +
rdPfMetricReinitFilterConstant \* refSchedWeightDrb \*
Number of Prbs in channel bandwidth
Number of Remaining Prbs in slot (t - 1)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07171027.png[/image\_path][/image-10]
Rationale: Since this is a cell-level metric, a reference scheduling weight is used. When a new UE is arriving to a cell that does not have all PRBs occupied, then a new UE may be assumed to get all the remaining PRBs. In this case, the new UE's PF metric would converge to channel bandwidth/remaining PRBs.
If at the end of slot (t-1) all the UEs of DRB priority class[PR877549] (If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL\_UL): excluding NBR sub-category)[End PR877549] scheduled have their respective buffered bytes drained out and there are no free PRBs remaining, then the [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains the text "initDlWeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i, t]". The text is blurry and appears to be in a programming context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07161905.png[/image\_path][/image-11] shall remain unchanged from the previous value.
Rationale: The event of all DRBs are drained out and all PRBs are occupied should be a very rare event. In this case, it is preferred to ignore such slots, because we cannot easily estimate what the new UE's PF metric will converge to.
where
[image-12][image\_desc]The image contains the text "refSchedWeightDrb" in a dark blue, italicized font against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07171028.png[/image\_path][/image-12]: cell-wide reference 5QI scheduler weight of a bearer belonging to drbTraffic class. Set to 1 by default.
i: User index
j: Carrier index
t: time index

}
[CB014044-UPD002] [Before PR863926]rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst : R&D parameter. Refer to [12737015](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3lQpQndyEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). [PR863926] If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL\_UL): rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst refers to R&D parameter rdPfMetricReinitFilterConstLongHistory(refer to [12737015](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3lQpQndyEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)). otherwise,  rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst refers to legacy R&D parameter rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst (see 5G\_L2\_9328\_replaced([11183534](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27j-ePvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End PR863926][End CB014044-UPD002]**[CB014044-UPD002] rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrierExclVarFactors[i,j,t-1]: Same as rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier of UE i in carrier j in slot t-1(see [11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) with the exception that the rrmUeSchedWeight ((see [11175183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) used in the calculation shall consider only NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight of the non-GBR bearers of the UE with data in buffer, excluding the variable factors (rrmRGWeight, DRX, CA DSS, DL CA DPA weight factors) **[End CB014044-UPD002]**\**}
Formula (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2) defines the initial average DL data rate for a UE i all its active (PSCell + #Active SCells)  component carriers:
[image-13][image\_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image, a corrupted file, or a deliberately black image with no discernible content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-04022052.png[/image\_path][/image-13](5G_UP_ALG_9944_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2)
  rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,t0] = max(rdMinExpectedBwShare* rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[i,t0] \* MaxPRBs, rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,t0]), (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2\_1)
i:  Index of the 5G UE
j: Index of the Carrier
t0: NR slot where initialization occurs

# CCDL: number of active component carriers for UE i

rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha [i,j,t0]:    The current DL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in bytes per slot with exponentiation of alpha (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4242\_replaced([11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t0] : the average PF metric on carrier j as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**-1**
rrmUeSchedWeight[i,t0]  : the **UE scheduling weight factor as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9885\_replaced([11175183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**
rdMinExpectedBwShare: the minimum fraction of the bandwidth that assume any UE can used for receive, set as R&D parameter with range between [0,1], default value is 1/200, which corresponding to 0.5% of the channel bandwidth in the worst case.
MaxPRBs: Maximum number of PRBs of bandwidth on carrier i, refer to requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .
For FR1 CA UE, 5G-L2-PS shall only calculate the initial average DL data rate on a per carrier basis as per (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-3)
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(DL or DL\_UL) **[CB014044-UPD002] and NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to a value other than 1 (for DL only) or 3 (for both DL and UL) // Exhaustive scheduling is disabled** [End CB014044-UPD002]***\**\*[Before CB014044-UPD002]**
[image-14][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a serif font. The formula is:
rrm Avg DIU e Data Rate[i, j, t₀] = (rrmCurrent DIUe Data Rate\_Bytes Per Slot\_alpha [i, j, t₀]) / (rdInitDlUe SchedMetricScaling Factor \* initDlUeSched Metric PerCarrier[j, t₀]) \* rrmUeSchedWeight[i, t₀][/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/aec6d9fa-490e-4010-9121-8e6198accc0a.png[/image\_path][/image-14](5G_UP_ALG_9944_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-3)
**[CB014044-UPD002]**
[image-15][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$rrm Avg DIU\_e Data Rate[i, j, t\_0] = \frac{rrmCurrent DIU\_e Data Rate\_Bytes Per Slot\_alpha[i, j, t\_0]}{rdInitDlU\_e SchedMetricScaling Factor \* init DlU\_e SchedMetric PerCarrier [j, t\_0]} \* rrmU\_e SchedWeight ExclVar Factors[i, t\_0]$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/72cdae9b-71d3-40d6-8c3b-110a5c4fea90.png[/image\_path][/image-15](5G_UP_ALG_9944_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-3)
**[End CB014044-UPD002]**
Else
[image-16][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-04022054.png[/image\_path][/image-16](5G_UP_ALG_9944_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-3)
    rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,j,t0] = max (rdMinExpectedBwShare*rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[i,j,t0] \* MaxPRBs, rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,j,t0]), (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-3\_1)
with
i:  Index of the 5G UE
j: Index of the Carrier
t0: NR slot where initialization occurs
rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha [i,j,t0]: The current DL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in bytes per slot with exponentiation of alpha (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4242\_replaced([11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
initDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t0] : the average PF metric on carrier j as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9944\_replaced([11175354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**-1**rrmUeSchedWeight[i,t0]  : the* *UE scheduling weight factor as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9885\_replaced([11175183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**[CB014044-UPD002]**rrmUeSchedWeightExclVarFactors[i,t0]: the* *UE scheduling weight factor excluding variable factors. ~~It shall be calculated as the sum of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight of non-GBR bearer(s) k for UE i at slot t0 with data in buffer.~~* *It shall be calculated as the sum of SchedWeightFactor (see[11175240: Scheduling weight for non-GBR bearer](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) of nonGBR bearer(s) k for UE i at slot t0with data in buffer. For UEs belonging to NBR category within drbTraffic Class ([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), NBR configured nonGBR bearers with rrmNbrTokenBucket[i,k,t0] > 0 shall only be considered.**[End CB014044-UPD002]**rdInitDlUeSchedMetricScalingFactor:    The scaling factor for the initial average scheduler metric defined in [12523685: rdInitDlUeSchedMetricScalingFactor](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_cpzJoBoCEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
rdMinExpectedBwShare: the minimum fraction of the bandwidth PRB that assume any UE can used for receive, set as R&D parameter with range between [0,1], default value is 1/200, which corresponding to 0.5% of the channel bandwidth in the worst case.
MaxPRBs: Maximum number of PRBs of bandwidth on carrier i, refer to requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .**Rationale: The intent is to give UEs entering CS1 list with new data a scheduling priority metric comparable to their converged data throughput considering UEs already present in the Scheduled UEs list to ensure a fair scheduling with those but yet give it a high-enough metric such that it can get scheduled almost immediately. Considering the long term averaging factor for the data rate, a too high throughput value may starve the UE of grants while a too low value would give UE excessive priority for a long period of time.*\*
 
For the first UE which is configured in L2-PS the average data rate is initialized based on dllaIniMcs (NIDD):
 
[image-17][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13058.008.png[/image\_path][/image-17]
 
MaxPRBs is defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (known as Maximum number of PRBs).

#### 3.29.5.2.0-15 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Definition of the PF numerator in case of Low Buffer Optimization (ID: 11175386)

**3.29.5.2.0-15.0-2**  (ID: `11175396`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
Rationale: The Proportional Fair (PF) metric is biased to prefer UEs with low data-rate. This includes Non-GBR UEs with a low data rate due to its traffic model and not due to its radio conditions if they have some data to send. This results in high PF metric for these UEs and yields reduced MCS and lower PRB utilization to take its low buffer fillings into account. Additionally, these low TP UEs stay with high PF metric (they got scheduled but actually did not send much) while other UEs with high buffer load are neglected.

In case of [Before PR742905-B]NRBTS/NRCELL/tmpActFeat2.Bit20 [PR742905-B]NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSchLowBufferOptimization [End PR742905-B] is set to true, this requirement changes the calculation of the current 5G UE DL Data Rate which is the numerator of the PF-metric to take a low utilization and other constraints into account. Please note that this is meant only for FDD and TDD FR1 and a PDL rule is in place to avoid usage of [Before PR742905-B] NRBTS/NRCELL/tmpActFeat2.Bit20 [PR742905-B]NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSchLowBufferOptimization [End PR742905-B] is set to true for TDD FR2 which is using different concepts compared to FR1 FDD and TDD.

[Before PR742905-B] if NRBTS/NRCELL/tmpActFeat2.Bit20 is set to true

[PR742905-B]if NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSchLowBufferOptimization is set to true (FR1 only) [End PR742905-B]

the rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is replaced by rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRate which is at maximum the same as rmCurrentDlUeDataRate in case of high buffer occupation but might be downgraded in case of low buffer utilization. This correction is achieved by an additional term which consist of the maximum of three parts. At first too high latency should be avoided by the rrmLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer term. Then the main idea is put into place by taking the Non-GBR buffer occupation of the currently evaluated UE into account. Additionally, we assure a minimum of the PF-metric numerator by defining a fraction of rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate as a minimum (Eq. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).1).

rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] =

min (

max (

rrmLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t],

sum[k: logical Channel Id of DRB of UE i]{bufferedBytes[k, i]} \*

min(rdDlSchLowBufferFdmAssumption, rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl),

(1 - rdDlSchLowBufferMaxPenalty/100.0) \* rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t]

),

rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t]

)   (Eq. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).1)

with:

i: Index of the 5G UE

j: Index of the carrier

k:         logical Channel Id of DRB of UE

t: NR slot the calculation relates to

rdDlSchLowBufferMaxPenalty is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26546\_replaced([11193877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwxwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and rdDlSchLowBufferFdmAssumption is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26553\_replaced([11193888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwxwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). bufferedBytes is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The current 5G UE DL data rate rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4242\_replaced([11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). And rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10610\_replaced([11179079](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku3Z6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The rrmLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer is attempting to mitigate too high additional latency for the three different use cases. These three cases are:

1) Initial Tx shall not be degraded/delayed, especially to avoid Ping and TCP ramp-up delay problems.

2) Trailing bits of a data burst should be finished rather fast, so if you have transmitted bigger data packets the final transmission should not be delayed ideally.

3) A general aging approach so that the UEs in general do not suffer for too long.

rrmLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] is defined as follows:

If rrmAvgDlUeDataRate has been reset (according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4243\_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): (First TB of a traffic burst case):

rrmLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] =

(1 - rdDlSchLowBufferReinitPenalty/100.0) \* rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t]

(Eq. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).2)

otherwise (Aging)

rrmLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] =

rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRatePost\_BytesPerSlot[t - rrmNumSlotSinceLastDlSched] /

((1.0 - 1.0/rdDlSchLowBufferFilterConst) ^ rrmNumSlotSinceLastDlSched)

(Eq. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).3)

end

rrmNumSlotSinceLastDlSched is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4243\_replaced([11175317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) e.g. it is the number of slots since the last time the UE was in CS1 list, evaluated for scheduling. rdDlSchLowBufferReinitPenalty is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26706\_replaced([11193897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwxxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and rdDlSchLowBufferFilterConst is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26708\_replaced([11193908](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwxxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The a-posteriori downgraded DL Ue data rate is calculated after the scheduling decision and it is dependent if there was a transmission in the current DL slot or not as follows:

If the UE i has been effectively granted over the air for the current slot on the carrier j:

rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRatePost\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] =   
 (1 - rdDlSchLowBufferTbTxPenalty/100.0) \* tbSize\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] \* min(rdDlSchLowBufferFdmAssumption, rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl)

(Eq. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).4)

otherwise

rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRatePost\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] = rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t]

(Eq. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).5)

end

Please note that it is up to implementation to calculate rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRatePost either directly after the scheduling (like defined here) so at time [t] but then used in later slots when UE is again in CS1 list or prior to the scheduling but then with respect to the last time the UE was in CS1 list [t - rrmNumSlotSinceLastDlSched].

The R&d parameter rdDlSchLowBufferTbTxPenalty is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26710\_replaced([11193917](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwxyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  tbSize\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] is the tbSize for this UE's initial transmission in this DL slot (t). The maximum number of UEs to be scheduled (FDMed) in DL (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl) is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10610\_replaced([11179079](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku3Z6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-16 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL DL PF low buffer optimization additional explanation (ID: 11175398)

**3.29.5.2.0-16.0-2**  (ID: `11175414`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
11175414:
Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
Rationale: The time flow in the low buffer optimized PF algorithm and additional comments are explained within this informational requirement. For the functional description please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before PR742905-B] if NRBTS/NRCELL/tmpActFeat2.Bit20 is set to true (FR1 only) the current workflow is depicted below for the time slot t.

[PR742905-B]The light blue squares are basically new behavior and the yellow ones are either modified behavior or at least used once within the scheduler.[End PR742905-B]
