The rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is replaced by rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRate which is at maximum the same as rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate in case of high buffer occupation but might be downgraded in case of low buffer utilization and for that the buffer occupancy is needed.

These values, rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate (calculated as before) and the buffer occupancy are two of the main inputs of this algorithm. A third part tries to mitigate negative effects additionally expected in form of latency for these low buffer UEs.

In order to do so, this rrmLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer is calculated and it consists also of three parts. We try to avoid downgrading for UEs just arriving or having a new burst coming after some time of being without data. This is done by checking if the average data rate (legacy behavior) was reset. So that for these UEs are not  downgraded . A second term checks the actually used transport-block size from the last time. This can be directly derived from the last time the UE was scheduled so basically at the end of the depicted graph after scheduling is done, defined for t and used in the next slot the UE is again in CS1 list. In case the UE is not scheduled the already calculated rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRate is used for rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRatePost instead. This after scheduling (a-posterori) updated rrmDowngradedDlUeDataRatePost is then used the next time the UE enters the CS1 list after aging is applied which rather fast increases the numerator of the PF term too (left hand side of the figure). Please note that the Equation numbers depicted are 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).1 till 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26702\_replaced([11175386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).5.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process. The flowchart starts with a "Start" oval, followed by several rectangular process boxes and decision diamonds.
Here's a breakdown of the flowchart's elements:
\* **Start:** An oval shape indicating the beginning of the process.
\* **Calculate rrmCurrentDIUeDataRate:** A yellow rectangle representing a calculation step.
\* **Calculate Buffer occupancy:** Another yellow rectangle for a calculation.
\* **Aging as a general means to avoid too high latency:** A green rectangle providing context or a comment.
\* **Used to avoid latency for TCP slow start and ping:** Another green rectangle providing context.
\* **Aging (Eq.3): rrmLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer:** A blue rectangle representing a calculation or variable.
\* **No:** A red arrow indicating a negative decision path.
\* **rrmAvgDIUeDataRate reset?:** A yellow diamond representing a decision point.
\* **Yes:** A red arrow indicating a positive decision path.
\* **New calculation: rrmLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer (Eq.2):** A blue rectangle for a calculation.
\* **Only one path used:** A blue arrow with text, indicating a specific path is taken.
\* **Calculate (Eq.1) rrmDowngradedDIUeDataRate:** A blue rectangle for a calculation.
\* **If not scheduled update a-posteriori value with Eq1 calculated one:** A green rectangle providing context.
\* **PF-Metric calculation:** A yellow rectangle for a calculation.
\* **Used to avoid latency of last trailing data block:** A green rectangle providing context.
\* **rrmDowngradedDIUeDataRatePost(t) = rrmDowngradedDIUeDataRate(t) (Eq.5):** A blue rectangle representing an assignment or calculation.
\* **No:** A red arrow indicating a negative decision path.
\* **Yes:** A red arrow indicating a positive decision path.
\* **UE got scheduled:** A yellow diamond representing a decision point.
\* **Calculate (Eq.4) rrmDowngradedDIUeDataRatePost from used TBS:** A blue rectangle for a calculation.
\* **Update of rrmDowngradedDIUeDataRatePost:** A blue rectangle representing an update step.
\* **End:** An oval shape indicating the end of the process.
The flowchart depicts a logic flow that involves calculating data rates, buffer occupancy, and latency avoidance metrics, with decision points based on whether a data rate has been reset or if a UE has been scheduled.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13064.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-17 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Calculation of GBR scheduling criteria (ID: 11175420)

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-2**  (ID: `11175423`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR scheduling criteria formula

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11175436`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR time domain (TD) scheduling criteria has the following characteristics:

 Does not apply to 5QI1/65/66 which is prioritized higher than other GBR bearers. In this requirement GBR refers to non-5QI1/65/66 GBR bearers.

 The data rate for GBR bearers is monitored via a token bucket.  If the GBR data rate exceeds the token bucket size (i.e. token bucket becomes negative) the GBR bearer will be depriotized below non-GBR bearers. [CB008118-B]This criteria for GBR bearer deprioritization is not applicable for GBR congestion handling [End CB008118-B].

 GBR bearers shall be considered in the calculation of the TD criteria only if there is data waiting for initial transmission.

The calculation of the downlink GBR TD scheduling criteria shall be as follows:

C DL,i (t) = PF DL, i, GBR (t) \* C2 DL, I, m (t)

where

PF DL, i, GBR (t)     GBR time domain proportional fair term as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8018\_replaced([11175441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

C2 DL, I, m (t)       GBR specific scheduling criteria as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8020\_replaced([11175459](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

with

i           index of the UE

m          index of GBR bearer of UE i

t          current slot

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-3**  (ID: `11175441`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR proportional fair calculation

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175445`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The downlink GBR proportional fair (PF) metric provides an indication of the GBR UE’s current channel conditions compared to its average channel conditions.  Unlike the non-GBR PF metric the GBR PF metric does not measure the UE’s actual data rate.  For GBR the data rate is independent of the channel conditions and is dictated by the GFBR value provided by the AMF.  The GBR PF metric instead quantifies the UE’s channel conditions and is used to increase the GBR UE’s priority when its channel conditions are better than average and lower the UE’s priority when its channel conditions are worse than average.  The GBR PF metric value shall be calculated as follows:

PFDL,I,GBR (t) = rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot (i, t) / rrmAvgDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot (i, t-1)

where

rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot (i, t)     current wideband DL data rate of UE i, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4242\_replaced([11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmAvgDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot (i, t-1)       average wideband DL data rate of UE i, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8021\_replaced([11175451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

with

i      index of the UE

t      current slot

Note that the GBR PF value is only calculated for the UE's PCell carrier.  GBR traffic is not transmitted over CA SCells.

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-4**  (ID: `11175451`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR average data rate calculation

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12533652`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The GBR PF average wideband throughput in DL shall be calculated as follows:

rrmAvgDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot (i, t) =

(1 – (1 / rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst) ) \* rrmAvgDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot (i, t-1) +

(1 / rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst) \* rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot (i, t)

Where

rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot       Current DL wideband throughput, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4242\_replaced([11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst                   Filter constant given by R&D parameter, [Before CB014044-B] see 5G\_L2\_1023\_replaced([11181966](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27XxK_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CB014044-B] For an FDD cell, it shall be rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConstFdd, see [12533643: rdAvgDlUEDataRateFilterConstFdd](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_SBKmwBxiEfCQJ5daVHVkgg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). For a TDD FR1 cell, it shall be rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConstTddFr1, see [12552044: rdAvgDlUEDataRateFilterConstTddFr1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_JBPwoSTBEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). And for a TDD FR2 cell, see 5G\_L2\_1023\_replaced([11181966](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27XxK_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  [End CB014044-B].

with

i     index of the UE

t    current slot

rrmAvgDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot shall be updated in every slot that the GBR UE is included in the CS1 list.

For initialization of the average data rate filter, rrmAvgDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSloti (t-1) shall be set equal to rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot (i, t).  This will result in an initial PF value of 1.

Note that the GBR average data rate is only calculated for the carrier with the GBR bearer, i.e. the Pcell carrier.

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-5**  (ID: `11175459`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR delay metric calculation

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-5.0-5**  (ID: `11175482`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR delay metric calculation shall be performed as follows.  Note that GBR for the following requirement refers to non-5QI1/65/66 5QIs.

C2 DL,i,m = DMV DL,i,m (t)     for GBR bearers if data is eligible for initial transmission (buffer size is non-zero) and the GBR data rate has not been exceeded, i.e. token bucket filling level is positive (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8026\_replaced([11175497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kujQ1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True

If the GBR m of UE i belongs to a configured Resource Group (RG)

C2 DL,i,m =  rrmRGWeighti, m

Else

C2 DL,i,m =  0

Else  C2 DL,i,m = 1

for GBR bearers if data is eligible for initial transmission (buffer size is non-zero) and whose transmissions have exceeded the GBR data rate, i.e. token bucket filling level is negative (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8026\_replaced([11175497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kujQ1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  In this case the bearer will be put into the deprioritizedGbr traffic request class.[CB008118-B] This criteria for GBR deprioritization is caused by excessive data but is not applicable for GBR congestion deprioritize handling. [End CB008118-B]

C2 DL,i,m = 0                         otherwise, i.e. no data is awaiting initial transmission

where

DMV UL,i,m (t)       Delay Metric Value according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8024\_replaced([11175486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kujQ1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmRGWeighti, m     RG weight of the RG to which the GBR m of UE i belongs, as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

with

i           index of the UE

m         index of GBR bearer of UE i

t          current slot

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-6**  (ID: `11175486`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR delay metric value (DMV) calculation

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11175492`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR Delay Metric Value (DMV) is based on a unique linear curve for each GBR 5QI.  The slope and the maximum value of the curve are determined by the O&M parameter delayTarget (DT) for that 5QI.  The shorter the DT the steeper the curve and the higher the maximum DMV. All curves increase until the maximum packet delay budget specified by 3GPP (500ms for 5QI-76) is reached.  At that point all curves flatten out and have zero slope for delay times exceeding 500ms.  See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8053\_replaced([11196209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwBQdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for an example set of DMV curves.

Each GBR 5QI has a maximum DMV that is calculated as follows:

maxDMV5QI = maxDMV \* (1 – (confDT5QI – minDT) / maxDT)

where

maxDMV    200:   limit of the DMV that any 5QI may reach when ConfDT5QI > minDT

minDT                50:   minimum recommended packet delay budget specified by 3GPP 23.501 for non-Delay Critical GBR 5QIs

maxDT              500:   maximum recommended packet delay budget specified by 3GPP 23.501

confDT5QI           min (configured delayTarget for the GBR 5QI, 500ms)

The maxDMV5QI values should be calculated during cell start-up and whenever the value of the configured 5QI delayTarget is modified.  Note that delayTarget refers to NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.delayTarget which is defined for each GBR 5QI profile.

Finally, the GBR DMV is defined as follows:

DMV DL,i,m (t) = max ( 1,  maxDMV5QI  \* (tdelay,m (t) / maxDT) )        for 0 <=  tdelay,m (t) <= maxDT

DMV DL,i,m (t) = maxDMV5QI                                                        for tdelay,m (t) > maxDT

where

tdelay,m (t) = t – tarrival,m,k             head-of-line packet delay of bearer m at time t

with

tarrival,m,k             arrival time of oldest packet k of DRB m at PDCP

t currrent slot

i index of the considered UE

m index of the GBR bearer

k index of the head-of-line packet corresponding to the considered GBR bearer m

Note – a PDCP packet may be segmented at the RLC / MAC layer.  The segments shall inherit the PDCP packet’s timestamp for the purposes of calculating the delay metric.

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-7**  (ID: `11175497`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR rate monitoring with token bucket

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11175499`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The gNB shall maintain a token bucket variable Bi, j for each GBR bearer j of UE i.  Bi, j shall be initialized to zero when the GBR bearer is established and incremented by the product PBRi, j kbps x 1 ms for each ms that elapses, where PBRi, j is the prioritized bit rate equal to GBRMAC (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8059\_replaced([11196286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwB3g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the GBR bearer.

However, the value of Bi, j shall not exceed the bucket size.  If Bi, j is larger than bucket size j, it shall be set equal to the bucket size.  Bucket size j is equal to PBRi, j x BSDi, j where BSD is the bucket size duration which is specified for each GBR 5QI profile by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.schedulBSD.  Logically the token bucket variable (Bi, j) should be updated every ms after Bi, j is initialized.  However, this can be optimized by updating Bi, j at the beginning of every slot that the bearer is being considered for scheduling as follows:

Bi, j(Tcurrent) = min ( Bi, j(Tprevious) + PBRi, j \* diff(ti), PBRi,j \* BSDi, j )

where

diff(ti) = (tcurrent – tprevious)       represents the time duration since the last update of Bi, j.

The Bi, j variable is also updated when a PDSCH transmission containing a MAC SDU for the GBR bearer is sent to the UE.  Specifically, the token bucket for GBR bearer j is updated according to the amount of data transmitted to the GBR bearer:

Bi, j = Bi, j – dataSizeSentForGbrBearer

where

dataSizeSentForGbrBearer       number of data bits that were sent for the GBR bearer.   See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8036\_replaced([11185589](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUF2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-8**  (ID: `11175504`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
GBR token bucket lower limit

---

**3.29.5.2.0-17.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11175509`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
A lower limit shall be applied to the token bucket monitoring GBR bearers in DL.  The token bucket filling level which corresponds to a GBR bearer shall be restricted to a lower limit in the negative value range. The lower limit shall prevent the token bucket algorithm from continuously decrementing the token bucket filling level if the GBR bearer is scheduled for an extended time duration above the specified GBR, which might easily happen in an empty cell. Such a lower limit of the token bucket filling level will shorten the time the GBR bearer is not scheduled with the specified GBR when the number of UEs in the cell increases and the negative filling level of the token bucket would prevent the scheduling of the GBR bearer.

The time the GBR bearer comes back into the regular scheduling shall be a multiple of the bearer’s delayTarget and GBR value. The following equation defines the the lower bound of the token bucket.

Bi, j, min = -5 \* delayTargeti, j \* GBRMAC,i, j

where

delayTargeti, j       delay target of the GBR bearer which is specified for each GBR 5QI by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.delayTarget

GBRMAC,i, j             GBRMAC value of the GBR bearer.  See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8059\_replaced([11196286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwB3g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

with

i        index of the UE

j        index of the GBR bearer

When the token bucket filling level is updated, it shall be limited to this lower bound:

Bi, j = max (Bi, j, min , Bi, j)

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-18 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Calculation of NBR scheduling criteria (ID: 11175514)

**3.29.5.2.0-18.0-1**  (ID: `11175517`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
NBR scheduling criteria

---

**3.29.5.2.0-18.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12478643`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
**[CB013709-B] If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0, the HwVariant used is not ABIL, and the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31301\_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is greater than or equal to the value set for** activation threshold of rdNominalBitrateOlcDlPerCent (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31829\_replaced([11216807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxa-pPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then, gNB-DU shall skip the following actions for each NBR bearer i of UE j, since all the NBR bearers are deprioritized in bulk to normal nonGBR category. When the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31301\_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is below the value set for **deactivation threshold of rdNominalBitrateOlcDlPerCent (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31829\_replaced([11216807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxa-pPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then gNB-DU shall resume the below steps for each NBR bearer i of UE j.** [End CB013709-B]***\****\*

TD scheduler shall monitor the data rate of each of the NBR bearer i of UE j via a token bucket , namely rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j). The details are described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22092\_replaced([11175535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj34PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The resulting rrmNbrTokenBucket is eventually used for the categorization of UEs within drbTraffic class as explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

TD sub-prioritization of UEs in drbTraffic class shall follow below criteria:

* The sub-prioritization metric for all categories of drbTraffic class (namely NBR category, pairing group category, large buffer non-GBR category and normal non-GBR category, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be calculated as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with a difference in the calculation of rrmUeSchedWeight as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9885\_replaced([11175183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Within drbTraffic class, UEs belonging to NBR category shall always be prioritized over UEs belonging to pairing group category and large buffer non-GBR category, and UEs belonging to large buffer non-GBR category shall always be prioritized over UEs belonging to the normal non-GBR irrespective of their calculated sub-prioritization metric in the determination of candidate set 2 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4229\_replaced([11175140](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0mfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The calculated sub-prioritization metric shall only be used to prioritize the UEs within the given category of the drbTraffic class.

---

**3.29.5.2.0-18.0-2**  (ID: `11175535`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
Maintenance of NBR Token Bucket

---

**3.29.5.2.0-18.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175539`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
NBR Rate Monitoring with Token Bucket

---

**3.29.5.2.0-18.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11175544`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The TD scheduler shall maintain a token bucket variable rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j) [bits] for each NBR bearer i of UE j. It is updated as below

* when the non-GBR bearer with NBR rate (NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.nbrDl is non-zero) is established, rrmNbrTokenBucket shall be initialized to zero.
* Ideally, after rrmNbrTokenBucket is initialized, rrmNbrTokenBucket shall be incremented every slot by the product of rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) x slotDuration\_msec (For DL CA, PCell and SCell(s) should use its own cell slotDuration\_msec.) and reduced whenever data is successfully transmitted for the NBR bearer. However, this can be optimized by updating rrmNbrTokenBucket only when one of the conditions below are satisfied:
* At the beginning of every slot, the UE configured with NBR bearer(s) is being considered for scheduling (i.e. added to CS1 list)
* For DL CA, the rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) on PCell and SCells should be calculated since PCell setup for UE**. The rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) on PCell and SCells should be re-calculated when SCell activation or deactivation or any serving cell overheating status change.**
* Whenever the initial PDSCH transmission containing a MAC SDU for the NBR bearer i is sent to the UE j

rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j,t) = rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j,t-1) + rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) \* elapsedTime\_msec -  txDataForNbrBearer\_bits(i,j,t)

* The upper and lower limits of rrmNbrTokenBucket, namely nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit and nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit shall always be observed while updating the NBR token bucket variable. In addition, whenever the nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit or nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit is changed, and the current level of rrmNbrTokenBucket is beyond the allowed range, rrmNbrTokenBucket shall be clipped accordingly such that it is within the new allowed range.

where,

* rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) is the prioritised bit rate [kbits/sec] of DL NBR bearer i of UE j derived from NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.nbrDl [Mbps]
* nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22093\_replaced([11175549](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj34_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22094\_replaced([11175556](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj35fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* elapsedTime\_msec represents the time elapsed in msec since the last update of rrmNbrTokenBucket.
* txDataForNbrBearer\_bits is cumulative number of initial Tx data bits that were sent for the NBR bearer in slot t. This shall include initial Tx data bits from all the transmissions for NBR bearer i.e. both as part of positive/negative token bucket categories. In addition, in a given slot, NBR bearer with positive token bucket might be allocated in two steps i.e. NBR positive token bucket FD resource allocation as explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21689\_replaced([11179765](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ERvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22204\_replaced([11185658](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUs6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additional FD resource allocation as part of [CB014861-C]the Exhaustive Scheduling (see [12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ)) if enabled, otherwise[End CB014861-C] WRR as explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22150\_replaced([11185666](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUs7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: The NBR token bucket is reduced whenever initial Tx is scheduled for the bearer. As a result, when the HARQ Tx reaches max re-transmissions and results in HARQ failure, it will result in over-estimating the data successfully transmitted for the NBR bearer. However, as it is expected to be rare, this case is ignored for simplicity.

---

**3.29.5.2.0-18.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11175549`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
NBR Token Bucket Upper Limit

---

**3.29.5.2.0-18.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175551`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
For a given DL NBR bearer i of UE j, the upper limit of the NBR bucket variable, namely nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit, is given by

nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit [bits] =  rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) \* schedulBSD\_msec(i)

where,

rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) is the prioritised bit rate [bits/sec] of DL NBR bearer i of UE j derived from NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.nbrDl [Mbps]

schedulBSD\_msec(i) is the bucket size duration in secs which is derived from DRB profile by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.schedulBSD [msec]

---

**3.29.5.2.0-18.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11175556`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
NBR Token Bucket Lower Limit

---

**3.29.5.2.0-18.0-2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175561`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
For a given DL NBR bearer i of UE j, the lower limit of the NBR bucket variable, namely nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit, is given by

nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit [bits] =  - rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) \* rdDlNbrTokenBucketLowerLimit

where,

rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) is the prioritised bit rate [kbits/sec] of DL NBR bearer i of UE j derived from NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.nbrDl [Mbps]

rdDlNbrTokenBucketLowerLimit is an R&D parameter defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22408\_replaced([11193596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvuVgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-19 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Calculation of LargeBuffer scheduling criteria (ID: 12687136)

**3.29.5.2.0-19.0-2**  (ID: `12816983`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The TD sub-prioritization metric for large buffer UEs shall follow the criteria below:

* If the number of large buffer candidate UEs in CS1, as calculated in [11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_G7w6UPvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (maxNumUeToAddLargeBuffer), exceeds the configured rdMaxNumLargeBufferUesPerSlotDl(see [11194173](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_yurrEPvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), a round-robin mechanism shall be used to select rdMaxNumLargeBufferUesPerSlotDl UEs as large buffer UEs. These selected UEs shall be assigned the large buffer sub-priority as their TD sub-prioritization metric. The remaining unselected large buffer candidate UEs from the CS1 list shall be treated as normal non-GBR UEs.
* Within the drbTraffic class:
* UEs in the NBR category, if present, shall always be prioritized over UEs in the large buffer non-GBR category.
* [CB012971-C] UEs in the DL pairing group category, if present, shall always be prioritized over UEs in the large buffer non-GBR category. [End CB012971-C]
* UEs in the large buffer non-GBR category shall always be prioritized over UEs in the normal non-GBR category, regardless of their calculated sub-prioritization metrics when determining candidate set 2 (see [11175140](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_KSNRxvvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.29.5.2.0-20 Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL Calculation of DC-GBR scheduling criteria (ID: 11175564)

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-1**  (ID: `11175567`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
DC-GBR scheduling criteria formula

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175572`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
DC-GBR time domain (TD) scheduling criteria has the following characteristics:

 The data rate for DC-GBR bearers is monitored via averaging window(AW)( refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30379\_replaced([11175610](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  If the DC-GBR data rate exceeds the GFBRMAC, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31820\_replaced([11175619](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the DC-GBR bearer will be deprioritized to request class deprioritizedGbr(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8011\_replaced([11174274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 DC-GBR bearers shall be considered in the calculation of the TD criteria only if there is data waiting for initial transmission.

The calculation of the downlink DC-GBR TD scheduling criteria shall be as follows:

C DL,i (t) = PF DL, i, DC-GBR (t) \* C2 DL,i,m(t)

where

PF DL, i, DC-GBR (t)                DC-GBR time domain proportional fair term as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30367\_replaced([11175577](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj36vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

C2 DL,i,m(t)                         DC-GBR specific scheduling criteria as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30371\_replaced([11175593](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj39PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

with

i           index of the UE

m          index of DC-GBR bearer of UE i

t          current slot

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-2**  (ID: `11175577`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
DC-GBR proportional fair calculation

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175582`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The downlink DC-GBR proportional fair (PF) metric provides an indication of the DC-GBR UE’s current channel conditions compared to its average channel conditions.  Unlike the non-GBR PF metric the GBR PF metric does not measure the UE’s actual data rate.  For DC-GBR the data rate is independent of the channel conditions and is dictated by the GFBR value provided by the AMF.  The DC-GBR PF metric instead quantifies the UE’s channel conditions and is used to increase the DC-GBR UE’s priority when its channel conditions are better than average and lower the UE’s priority when its channel conditions are worse than average.  The DC-GBR PF metric value shall be calculated as PFDL,I,GBR (t), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8018\_replaced([11175441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that the DC-GBR PF value is only calculated for the UE's PCell carrier.  DC-GBR traffic is not transmitted over CA SCells.

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-3**  (ID: `11175584`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
DC-GBR average data rate calculation

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175589`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The DC-GBR PF average wideband throughput in DL shall be calculated as the GBR PF average wideband throughput, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8021\_replaced([11175451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

rrmAvgDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot shall also be updated in every slot that the DC-GBR UE is included in the CS1 list.

Note that the DC-GBR average data rate is only calculated for the carrier with the DC-GBR bearer, i.e. the Pcell carrier.

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-4**  (ID: `11175593`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
DC-GBR delay metric calculation

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175596`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
DC-GBR delay metric calculation shall be performed as follows.

If DC-GBR bearer is eligible for initial transmission (buffer size is non-zero) and the DC-GBR data rate has not been exceeded, i.e. DC-GBR data rate is less than or equal to the DC-GBR DRB GFBR(guaranteedFlowBitRateDownlink) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30365\_replaced([11175567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj36PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

C2 DL,i,m = delayTermi, m (t)

Else if DC-GBR bearer is eligible for initial transmission (buffer size is non-zero) and whose transmissions have exceeded the DC-GBR data rate, i.e. DC-GBR data rate is greater than the DC-GBR DRB GFBR(guaranteedFlowBitRateDownlink) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30365\_replaced([11175567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj36PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

**[Before CB008600-F]**

C2 DL,i,m = 1

**[CB008600-F]**

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True

C2 DL,i,m =  rrmRGWeighti, m

Else

C2 DL,i,m = 1

**[End CB008600-F]**

In this case the bearer will be put into the deprioritizedGbr traffic request class.

Else , i.e. no data is awaiting initial transmission:

C2 DL,i,m = 0

where

delayTermi, m (t)          Delay Term according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30375\_replaced([11175600](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**[CB008600-F]**

rrmRGWeighti, m RG weight of the RG to which the DC-GBR m of UE i belongs, as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**[End CB008600-F]**

with

i           index of the UE

m         index of DC-GBR bearer of UE i

t          current slot

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-5**  (ID: `11175600`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
DC-GBR delay term calculation

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11175604`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
The DC-GBR delay term (delayTerm) is based on a specific linear function for each DC-GBR 5QI. The slope and the maximum value reached depend on the Packet Delay Budget (PDB) and Priority Level of the 5QI.

If (HOL\_delay <= confDT5QI)

delayTermi, m (t) = maxDW5QI, m \* (HOL\_delaym (t) / confDT5QI, m )

Else (i.e. HOL\_delay >  confDT5QI)

If (discardTimer <=  confDT5QI )

delayTermi, m (t) = maxDW5QI, m

else if (discardTimer > confDT5QI)

if (HOL\_delay <= discardTimer)

delayTermi, m (t) = maxDW5QI, m  + slope\_factor \* maxDW5QI, m \* ((HOL\_delaym (t) - confDT5QI, m )/ (discardTimer5QI, m- confDT5QI, m ))

Else (i.e. HOL\_delay > discardTimer)

delayTermi, m (t) = (1 + slope\_factor)\*maxDW5QI, m

where

confDT5QI, m           5QI-dependent configured delay target of DRB m, refer to NRBTS/NRDRB.delayCriticalGbrDrbParam.dlRanPdb

maxDW5QI, m              5QI-dependent delay term/weight value reached for DRB m when its HOL packet delay becomes equal to confDT, that is, maxDW5QI, m = 100/( NRBTS/NRDRB/priorityLevel \* 0.1).

discardTimer5QI, m    5QI-dependent packet discard timer of DRB m, i.e. refer to NRDRB.discardTimer, typically equal to or larger than dlRanPdb to still deliver slightly delayed packets

HOL\_delaym (t) = t - tarrival, m, k      Head-of-line packet delay of DRB m at time t

slope\_factor          RD parameter rdSlopeFactorForDelayTerm, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30419\_replaced([11194081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvym8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

with

tarrival,m,k             arrival time of oldest packet k of DRB m at PDCP

t currrent slot

i index of the considered UE

m index of the DC-GBR bearer

k index of the head-of-line packet corresponding to the considered GBR bearer m

Note:  a PDCP packet may be segmented at the RLC / MAC layer.  The segments shall inherit the PDCP packet’s timestamp for the purposes of calculating the delay metric.

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-6**  (ID: `11175610`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
DC-GBR data rate monitoring with averaging window(AW)

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11175614`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
L2PS shall maintain an averaging window (AW) for DC-GBR data rate monitoring AvgTpi, j for each DC-GBR bearer j of UE i. AvgTpi, j is the moving average of throughput achieved for the DC-GBR bearer and it should be calculated as the sum of data transmitted in DL for that bearer during the last period of AW duration divided by the AW duration. The length of this averaging window shall be decided in CPlane according to NIDD parameter NRDRB.delayCriticalGbrDrbParam.avgWindow of corresponding DC-GBR bearer or from NGAP message.

AvgTpi, j shall be initialized to zero when the DC-GBR bearer is established, and it should be updated every period after it is initialized. For the traffic in invalid slots, like UL slots in TDD cell, or the slots before the DC-GBR DRB starts transmitting data (for AW initialization), it shall be assumed that the transmitted DC-GBR data is zero.

Note: The unit of period can be decided in CP3 phase according to CPU load usage and memory usage.

Note: Though the NRDRB.avgWindow support online modification, but it will only apply to new bearer or new Ue in Cplane. So the length of averaging window for one DC-GBR will not be modified after it is established.

Note: The DC-GBR data rate value store in averaging window shall consider the overhead, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31820\_replaced([11175619](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-7**  (ID: `11175619`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
**Additional overhead considerations for GFBR and MDBV**

---

**3.29.5.2.0-20.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11175622`)

Definition of per UE scheduling metric for DL
gNB-DU shall calculate the internal value of GFBR (namely GFBRMAC) [CB008600-F] and MDBV (namely MDBVMAC) [End CB008600-F] to be used for the data rate monitoring on the DC-GBR bearers on top of the GFBR [CB008600-F] and MDBV values [End CB008600-F] received for the DC-GBR QoS flows from the core network as below:

GFBRMAC = GFBR \* (100+NRDRB.l2OHFactorDl)/100

[CB008600-F] MDBVMAC = MDBV \* (100+NRBTS/NRDRB.l2OHFactorDl)/100 [End CB008600-F]

where

GFBR is the DL guaranteed flow bit rate of the DC-GBR bearer received from core network

[CB008600-F] MDBV is the maximum burst data volume of the DC-GBR bearer given by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB.delayCriticalGbrDrbParam.dlMdbv, if a valid value is not configured from core network, else, the value from the core network is taken. [End CB008600-F]

---

### 3.29.5.3 X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account

#### 3.29.5.3.0-1 X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account Determining the X-pol beam for scheduling (ID: 11175632)

**3.29.5.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11175659`)

X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account
11175659:
X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account
[CB012734-E] If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE, this procedure is not required as beam selection for analog beamforming is already performed in Pre-scheduler as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27542\_replaced([11174581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudKOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB012734-E]

For analog beamforming, the TD scheduler determines the X-Pol beam to be used in the next scheduling slot as follows:

In case PCCH data need to be scheduled, 5G-L2-PS shall refer 5G\_L2\_823\_replaced([11173532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26b9CvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and shall decide further on UE scheduling in current slot.

For each UE in CS1 according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2-PS shall determine its highest priority resource request over all its serving cells. The priority of a resource request is defined by the priority of its resource request class (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9465\_replaced([11174010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuXqrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and by a sub-priority which is the relative priority of a resource request within its resource request class (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4148\_replaced([11174036](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuYRtfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If there is at least one CS1 candidate then

If the class of the highest priority resource request among all the candidates is drbTraffic, then

Do the "Beam Selection Algorithm for drbTraffic".

else

Do the "Beam Selection Algorithm for non-drbTraffic"

Beam selection algorithm for non-drbTraffic:

L2-PS selects the Beam of the UE with highest priority resource request among all the candidates. The index of this beam is denoted with kselectedBeam.

Beam selection algorithm for drbTraffic:

Step 2.1:

For each UE i of CS1 belonging to the drbTraffic request class and each carrier j configured to the UE except the carriers where beam assigned to UE is considered muted(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2240\_replaced([11113816](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpejkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS shall calculate the PF metric (rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i, j]) as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If rrmBufferStatusSplitNeeded == 1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2851\_replaced([11122336](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the buffer status split determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2834\_replaced([11122266](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqeCH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) needs to be taken into account for CA UEs. Thus, the PF metric shall only be calculated for active carriers having a buffer split factor > 0 as determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2841\_replaced([11122293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqepJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In the active carriers with buffer split factor = 0, the UE is not considered a candidate of the drbTraffic request class.

Step 2.2:

The TD scheduler shall calculate for each beam k in the set of beams of the L2 PS instance and each carrier j in the set of all carriers, the maximum of the metrics calculated in step 2.1 among all the UEs assigned to the same beam k which belong to drbTraffic request class of carrier j. The resulting metrics are denoted by rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerBeamPerCarrier:

rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerBeamPerCarrier[k,j] =  [image-0][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13116.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]{ rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i,j]}

k = beam index

CS1(k, drbTraffic, j) = set of UEs assigned to beam k and member of the request class drbTraffic for carrier j

i = UE index

j = carrier index

Step 2.3:

The TD scheduler shall determine a metric per beam k by the following operation:

rrmDlUeSumSchedMetricPerBeam[k]  = [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13116.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]{ rrmDlUeSchedMetricPerBeamPerCarrier[k,j] }

k = beam index

j = carrier index, j = 1 … Nc

Nc is the number of carriers handled by the L2 PS instance

Step 2.4:

The X-Pol beam to be used in the next scheduling slot is equal to the X-Pol beam that has the highest value of the metrics calculated in step 2.3.

kselectedBeam = [image-2][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13116.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]{ rrmDlUeSumSchedMetricPerBeam[k] }

kselectedBeam is the beam index of the beam that is used in the next scheduling slot.

k = beam index

B = set of beams handled by the L2 PS instance.

End Beam selection algorithm for drbTraffic.

---

#### 3.29.5.3.0-2 X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account Beam restriction when all the PUCCH symbols are reserved (ID: 11175663)

**3.29.5.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11175673`)

X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account
[CB012734-E] If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE, this procedure is not required as beam selection for analog beamforming is already performed in Pre-scheduler as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27542\_replaced([11174581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudKOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB012734-E]

[Before PR777253]

For analog beamforming, if CSI-report is expected on the slot n + K1 and F2 PUCCH token is available on the corresponding serving Cell, the beam of CSI-report UE will be selected.

Otherwise if at least one PUCCH token is available on slot n+K1, beam shall be selected as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4251\_replaced([11175632](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)); If all PUCCH tokens are reserved, the beam of corresponding PUCCH tokens shall be selected and UE from those beams shall added to candidate set 2 according to their priority metric.

[PR777253]

If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/actPucchBeamConstraintFR2 = TRUE:

For analog beamforming,

* If there is atleast one rnti candidate in CS1 which does not require PUCCH scheduling (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26785\_replaced([11174828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kueYWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or atleast one PUCCH symbol in slot n+k1 does not have PUCCH resource allocation across all CCs within the cellgroup, then there is no restriction required on the considered beams and beam selection shall be performed as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4251\_replaced([11175632](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Else, beam(s) of the allocated PUCCH symbols in the slot n+k1 shall only be considered (not all configured beams) for the beam selection described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4251\_replaced([11175632](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else:

For analog beamforming, if CSI-report is expected on the slot n + K1 and F2 PUCCH token is available on the corresponding serving Cell, the beam of CSI-report UE will be selected.

Otherwise if at least one PUCCH token is available on slot n+K1, beam shall be selected as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4251\_replaced([11175632](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)); If all PUCCH tokens are reserved, the beam of corresponding PUCCH tokens shall be selected and UE from those beams shall added to candidate set 2 according to their priority metric.

[End PR777253]

---

#### 3.29.5.4 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account

##### 3.29.5.4.0-1 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account DTX optimized specific CS1 UE handling (ID: 11175683)

**3.29.5.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175687`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
Conditionnal DTX optimized UEs removal from the CS1 list

---

**3.29.5.4.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175691`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
5G-L2-PS shall check whether the data in dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8549\_replaced([11174294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be scheduled or kept retained, i.e. if any of the following conditions is satisfied.

• One or more UEs with a higher priority request class has been selected in the CS1 list

• The slot will be used for control channels other than PDCCH (SSBs, TRS, SIBs, Paging - SA cells) and has available PRBs for PDSCH

• The size of the CS1 list is larger than or equal to rdDtxCS1SizeThreshold (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9392\_replaced([11193397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvsgVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If none of the conditions above are satisfied, 5G-L2-PS shall check if the estimated DTX PRB load of the cell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8238\_replaced([11175696](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is higher than or equal to NRBTS/NRCELL/dtxOptimizedScheduler.dtxPrbThreshold and apply the condition below.

If none of the conditions above are satisfied, the CS1 list shall be emptied.

---

**3.29.5.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11175696`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
Computing of PRB cell load for DTX optimized scheduling

---

**3.29.5.4.0-1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11175703`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
For each dedicated DL BWP defined in the cell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1809\_replaced([11110718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpHXNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall:

1. Estimate the PRB demand (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8943\_replaced([11175708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for all UEs in the CS1 list that can be scheduled in the DL BWP. This includes UEs attached to a smaller, nested dedicated DL BWP.

*Note: The PRB demand shall not be calculated if there is any traffic in the initial DL BWP (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9471\_replaced([11175687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*

2. Estimate the number of PRBs available for PDSCH in this DL BWP as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4278\_replaced([11176996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3. The load of this DL BWP shall be calculated by dividing the result of 1. by the result of 2.

*Note: 5G-L2-PS shall compute the load starting from the smallest dedicated DL BWP to the largest one in order to reuse the estimated PRB demand.*

Note: Due to the nature of this ratio, slots with a smaller number of available PRBs (i.e. special slots in TDD) will be favored for scheduling.

**[CB012605-E]**

NOTE: This section shall also be valid when layer PRB pooling is enabled (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE).

**[End CB012605-E]**

The cell load shall be computed as the maximum of the dedicated DL BWP loads. Such calculation shall be only performed when none of the other conditions described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9471\_replaced([11175687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are reached.

---

**3.29.5.4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11175708`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
Computing of PRB Demand for DTX optimized scheduling

---

**3.29.5.4.0-1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175713`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
The number of PRBs equivalent to the buffered DL data prbDtxDemand shall be computed by using the non-GBR loads of the UE present in the CS1 list.

The aggregated DL PRB demand for a given BWP of a cell shall be calculated as the sum of the maximum number of PRBs needed to transmit the buffered data (as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of all the UEs with this BWP, or a nested BWP, as active BWP.

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-2 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account DL CS1 list truncation considering the PDCCH capacity (ID: 13346611)

**3.29.5.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13361389`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
When NRCELL.actPdcchLeastBlockingAlg is enabled and rdActCs1ListTruncationAndQuotaAllocation is True, gNB-DU shall truncate the n-GBR UEs prioritized in the TD metric order in the CS1 list to a smaller list based on the available CCEs to DL as mentioned in [13346612: CS1 list truncation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_VjDU4uVfEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Note: rdActCs1ListTruncationAndQuotaAllocation enables or disables the CS1 list truncation algorithm and it is an R&D parameter ([13347077: rdActCs1ListTruncationAndQuotaAllocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6q93UeWOEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-3 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account Composing CS2 list (ID: 11175719)

**3.29.5.4.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12465055`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
On carrier basis, 5G-L2-PS shall select the set of UE candidates to be scheduled on the carrier (CS2 List). CS2 list is provided to the corresponding FD scheduler for resource allocation. The FD scheduler shall allocate resources on that carrier for selected UEs out of those in CS2 list. The selection is done as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4292\_replaced([11177281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For each CS2 list, the below descriptions define how to select to be scheduled UEs from each CS2 list by FD scheduler.

If CS2 of that carrier is empty, scheduling continues with the next carrier until all the carriers or all the UEs from rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4101\_replaced([11173728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1dPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are scheduled.

In case PCCH data need to be scheduled, 5G-L2-PS shall refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4292\_replaced([11177281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and shall decide further on UE scheduling in current slot.

In case paging need to be scheduled, 5G-L2-PS shall get all of paging FD resource from corresponding cell DB by its subcell index and schedule paging according to PDCCH and/or PDSCH allocation result stored in paging FD resource.

For analog beamforming, the TD scheduler determines all the UEs assigned to the beam kbestBeam. The set is denoted by rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected.

For digital beamforming, the set rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected is equal to the set of UEs from the CS1 list.

TD Scheduler shall sort the UEs from rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected into the carrier specific CS2 lists in a way that each CS2 list contains the list of all the UEs that can be served in scheduled slot on this carrier. If UE has a best beam being a beam muted in the cell(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2240\_replaced([11113816](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpejkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), UE is removed from the CS2 set for the cell. **Each UE can be assigned with one priority resource. If the UE selection as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4292\_replaced([11177281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) fails for a UE with retransmission priority, [Before**PR819230] the respective UE is not removed from CS2 but re-added with lowest priority to the CS2 list [PR819230] L2-PS shall evaluate adding the UE to the CS2 list as UE with new transmission according to requirement [12462320: Converting re-transmission to new transmission](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TDyy8Ar_EfCT35inaBGo7g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) but with drbTraffic priority [End PR819230]

5G-L2-PS shall further update CS2 lists for all following conditions:

* Carrier Aggregation handling, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15565\_replaced([11175731](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* DL MU-MIMO handling, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15566\_replaced([11175739](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* lack of PDSCH resources, refer to **5G\_UP\_ALG\_15568\_replaced([11175747](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kultEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**
* FDD carrier shared with LTE (DSS), refer to **5G\_UP\_ALG\_15572\_replaced([11175768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kultF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**
* Several Dedicated BWP, refer to **5G\_UP\_ALG\_4261\_replaced([11175776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kultGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**
* UEs with DRB traffic in FR2 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26820\_replaced([11175784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kultG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The TD scheduler may limit the sorted cs2List further (see 5G\_L2\_7049\_replaced([11213344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-rj0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (FR1) and 5G\_L2\_20046\_replaced([11213374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-rj2PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (FR2)).

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-4 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account Composing CS2 list for DL CA handling (ID: 11175731)

**3.29.5.4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175734`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
If carrier aggregation feature is enabled, CS2 lists for multiple carriers shall be created according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4295\_replaced([11177311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP_vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When all CS2 lists are created, this shall be passed to the corresponding FD schedulers. The order how the Component Carriers are selected for the FD-Scheduling is left to implementation.

If rrmBufferStatusSplitNeeded == 1, a CA UE shall be a candidate for CS2 list of a carrier only if its buffer split factor > 0 for this carrier as deteremined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2841\_replaced([11122293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqepJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), unless it has resource requests with higher priority than drbTraffic which are pending on active carriers with buffer split ratio = 0 (like retransmisisons).

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-5 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account Composing CS2 list for DL MU-MIMO (Beam based) (ID: 11175739)

**3.29.5.4.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `13458077`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
If the DL MU-MIMO (NRBTS.NRCELL.actDlMuMimo = True and conduct mode only) is enabled, TD scheduler shall create up to 8 CS2 lists and each CS2 list only includes single UE for full downlink bandwidth allocation. TD scheduler shall send these CS2 list(s) to FD scheduler for frequency resource allocation. For each CS2 list, the FD scheduler shall schedule one UE from the list with a unused DL subcell (8 DL subcells are available with 2 ABILs) in frequency domain by using full downlink bandwidth.

If the DL MU-MIMO (NRBTS.NRCELL.actDlMuMimo = True and conduct mode only) is enabled, the SD scheduler will send multiple CS2 lists to FD scheduler for resource allocation if users can be paired (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4362\_replaced([11141946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr17HfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For each CS2 list, the FD scheduler shall select UE(s) to be scheduled from the list and schedule them in frequency domain by using all available downlink bandwidth.

For each CS2 list, the below descriptions define how to select to be scheduled UEs from each CS2 list by FD scheduler.

If CS2 of that carrier is empty, scheduling continues with the next carrier until all the carriers or all the UEs from rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4101\_replaced([11173728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1dPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are scheduled.

[CB013712-A] Not valid for NewFR1 [End CB013712-A]

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-6 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account Composing CS2 list for lack of PDSCH resource (ID: 11175747)

**3.29.5.4.0-6.0-8**  (ID: `12816286`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
If the number of PDSCH symbols used in the initial transmission exceeds the number of available PDSCH symbols in the considered slot:

If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced(11184268) are fulfilled.

UE is still allowed to be added to CS2 list.

If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) AND the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced(11184268) are not fulfilled (non-adaptive retransmission) AND NRBTS/NRCELL/actPdcchSharingWithPdsch is set to TRUE AND the initial transmission was scheduled with PDCCH symbol(s) shared with PDSCH (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25284\_replaced(11183082)).

UE is still allowed to be added to CS2 list. (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25287\_replaced(11183112) for rationale)

If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) and for a FDD cell which is shared with LTE and actLteNrDssLteCatM equals true:

UE is still allowed to be added to CS2 list.

For any other cases

UE shall not be scheduled

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true OR **[Before CB015668-A]** NIDD:NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt is true **[CB015668-A]** actTddCsiRsOpt is true (note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**and NIDD:ueSpecCsiRsSlotsRatio is equal to 1 and cell is not TDD eCPRI 7-2e:

If the transmission is NOT msg4, and  (the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) AND the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced(11184268) are not fulfilled (non-adaptive retransmission)), or the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to False),

If last symbol is used in the initial transmission, but the cell does not have ZAB privilege and last symbol is not possible for the retransmission, then the HARQ process is freed.

Note, when NIDD:ueSpecCsiRsSlotsRatio is equal to one, the last symbol will be reserved for UE specific CSI-RS slots as legacy.

Nevertheless the 5G-L2-PS triggers the dynamic evaluation of the CORESET duration pattern due to lack of PDSCH resources (see requirment 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3998\_replaced(11169802)). If retransmission is blocked in this slot, the number of HARQ retransmissions for the corresponding HARQ ID shall be incremented. TD scheduler shall take this into consideration when assigning priority resource request to the UE and shall not assign HARQ priority resource request to the UE in case HARQ retransmission is not possible.

If the re-transmission is blocked, L2-PS shall evaluate adding the UE to the CS2 list as UE with new transmission according to requirement 12462320: Converting re-transmission to new transmission

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-7 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account Composing CS2 list for DSS (ID: 11175768)

**3.29.5.4.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `12790544`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
On an FDD carrier shared with LTE [CB007372-M-UPD005] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M-UPD005], when Flexible Rate matching is enabled ([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) UEs which are not configured by L3 to support  Flexible Rate matching (rateMatchingResrcSetSemi-Static = FALSE ([11374757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wa-L8fvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be excluded from CS2 list in slot 0/5.

[CB007372-M-UPD005] When NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true, UEs using dedicated BWP which are not configured by L3 to support Flexible Rate matching (rateMatchingResrcSetSemi-Static = FALSE, ([11374757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wa-L8fvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall not be scheduled (DL) using dedicated BWP in slots 0/5. [End CB007372-M-UPD005]

If Flexible Rate matching is disabled (rrmLteDLPrbSharing=FALSE, [11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false then all UEs shall be excluded from CS2 list in slot 0/5.

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-8 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account Composing CS2 list with Several Dedicated BWP (ID: 11175776)

**3.29.5.4.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11175780`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
If n >= 2, and if maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot > 1,

While composing CS2 list according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4258\_replaced([11175719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), before the last UE candidate is selected, 5G-L2-PS shall check if at least one 1st BWP UE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is already selected.

* If so, then 5G-L2-PS shall select the last UE candidate according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4258\_replaced([11175719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).   
  NOTE: That is no change compared to parameter actMultipleDedicatedBWPs / actMultipleDedicatedBWPs = FALSE.
* If not, and If maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot == 2, the 5G-L2-PS shall select the next kth BWP UE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) according to the TD metric as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4258\_replaced([11175719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), where k ϵ{1, …, n-1}.   
  NOTE: That is if 2nd BWP UE has higher TD metric than 1st BWP UE, 2nd BWP UE shall be selected.
* If not, and if maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot > 2, the 5G-L2-PS shall select the next 1st BWP UE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) according to the TD metric. If no 1st BWP UE is eligible for scheduling, the 5G-L2-PS shall select the 2nd BWP UE, according to the TD metric. And so on.

NOTE: That is kth BWP UE is only selected if (k-1)th BWP UE is not eligible for scheduling.

NOTE: This enables the FD scheduler to allocate PDSCH resources in the larger dedicated BWP exclusive resource.

NOTE: If maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot == 1, CS2 does not need to include any large BWP capable UE.

NOTE: If maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot == 2, CS2 does not need to include any 1st BWP capable UE but instead next higher TD metric of any "large" BWP UE.

If NRBTS.NRCELL.actDlMuMimo is true received at cell setup, 5G-L2-PS shall create the CS2 list in the same manner as defined in this requirement, as if MU-MIMO is disabled.   
NOTE: In this manner the MU-MIMO gains can be realized in most scenarios.

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-9 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account Composing CS2 List for DRB traffic in FR2 (ID: 11175784)

**3.29.5.4.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11175788`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
Note that this requirement is only applicable for FR2, and hence only valid for analog beamforming.

For UEs belonging to the drbTraffic request class and the set rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected (rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected is the set of UEs served by the beam which is determined by the beam selection algorithm described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4251\_replaced([11175632](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall sort the UEs into the carrier specific CS2 lists in a way that the CS2 list for each carrier contains the list of all the UEs that can be served in scheduled slot on the corresponding carrier. For instance, if carrier j is not active for a UE u, the CS2 list for carrier j shall not contain UE u. The sorting of UEs is specified below

* If actHighNumberOfUEs=TRUE and rdActSinglePfMetricPerUeOnLoad=TRUE
* If totalActiveUeLoad is less than rdSinglePfMetricPerUeThreshold
* The UEs are sorted in each carrier specific CS2 list in order of their PF metrics. The PF metric calculation for each CC is specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Else if totalActiveUeLoad is equal to or greater than rdSinglePfMetricPerUeThreshold
* The UEs are sorted in the carrier specific CS2 lists in the order of the PF metrics computed at the Pcell of the UE. While creating carrier specific CS2 lists, the gNB-DU shall be aware of UEs that can be served by the carrier.

For instance, with 3 UEs in drbTraffic request class in the set  rrmDlCandidateSet1Selected, if the PF metric at their respective Pcells follow the following order UE2>UE3>UE1, then the same sequence of ordering shall be followed while adding these UEs to the respective carrier specific CS2 lists.  If carrier j is not serving UE3, then the CS2 list for carrier j shall follow the ordering UE2>UE1, and this ordering shall be followed while adding the UEs in the CS2 list for carrier j.

* Else if actHighNumberOfUEs=FALSE or rdActSinglePfMetricPerUeOnLoad=FALSE
* The UEs are sorted in each carrier specific CS2 list in order of their PF metrics. The PF metric calculation for each CC is specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4232\_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The algorithm to calculate totalActiveUeLoad is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26307\_replaced([11122140](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqdbAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and it provides the current load in the system by taking into account, for each beam, the number of UEs and number of active CCs for each UE.

The threshold denoted by rdSinglePfMetricPerUeThreshold is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26824\_replaced([11193927](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwxyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the activation flag denoted by rdActSinglePfMetricPerUeOnLoad is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26826\_replaced([11193936](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvxY0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-10 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account Limit Scheduling Opportunities of UEs with few DRB Data in DL Buffer (ID: 11175793)

**3.29.5.4.0-10.0-2**  (ID: `11175802`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
This workaround aims to mitigate the DL throughput degradation of UEs with a high amount of data in DL buffer which share the PDSCH resources with other UEs having few data in DL buffer.

The basic idea is to reduce the DL scheduling opportunities for the UEs with few DRB data in buffer and schedule them on DL slots with less PDSCH symbols (i.e. special slots containing the DL/UL switch point). Which DL slot is permitted within the radio frame is configured by an R&D parameter (rdDlPermitSchedSlotBitmap, 5G\_L2\_1356\_replaced([11182270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27aNafvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The activation of the functionality is controlled by an R&D parameter (rdDlSuppressionEnabled, 5G\_L2\_1353\_replaced([11182257](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27aNZvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

By filling each CS2 list, if the workaround is activated, the DL TD scheduler shall check all the below conditions for each UE j in CS1 list which is eligible to be moved to that CS2 list:

1. [PR726955] UE is not configured with 5QI1 bearer (See Note) [End PR726955]
2. The UE has no pending DL transmissions with priority class higher than drbTraffic;
3. The UE has few data in DRB buffers:

SUM[i: logical Channel Id of DRB of UE j]{bufferedBytes[i, j]} < rdDlSuppressionLowThreshold\_bytes;

4. CS1 list size <= rdDlSuppressionCs1ListSize;

5. There’s at least one UE k in CS1 list which has an high amount of data in DRB buffers:

SUM[i: logical Channel Id of DRB of UE k]{bufferedBytes[i, k]} > rdDlSuppressionHighThreshold\_bytes;

6. Current DL slot is not the permitted one according to rdDlPermitSchedSlotBitmap setting;

When all these conditions are met the UE j is not added to the CS2 List.

bufferedBytes is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). rdDlSuppressionCs1ListSize, rdDlSuppressionLowThreshold and rdDlSuppressionHighThreshold are respectively described in 5G\_L2\_1354\_replaced([11182262](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27aNZ_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_1357\_replaced([11182275](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27aNavvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_1358\_replaced([11182281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27aNa_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[PR726955]

Note:  The condition that checks for a 5QI1 bearer ensures that any allocation of UEs that are setup with VoNR bearers are not blocked, and not just allocations which contain voice data. Otherwise, problems might occur with the restrictive DRX profiles often used with VoNR configured. This is because the non-voice packets can not be scheduled due to being in DRX sleep in the only slot where they are allowed. Previously, an UE with a VoNR bearer might be scheduled in slots that coincide with their DRX\_Sleep duration and this can cause the UE to be dropped altogether.

[End PR726955]

---

##### 3.29.5.4.0-11 UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account Converting re-transmission to new transmission (ID: 12462320)

**3.29.5.4.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `12463104`)

UE Selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the Prioritization and Sub-Prioritization Concept of Resource Requests into account
Whenever UE is selected for DL transmission in the slot because of the re-transmission data and amount or arrangement of spectral (resource blocks, resource groups) or time domain (symbols) resources is such that re-transmission cannot be executed  then if all of the following conditions are satisfied:

* UE has new data to be transmitted
* UE satisfies criteria of being scheduled for new transmission due to RIM power reduction conditions as specified in the requirement [11174332: RIM DL power reduction restrictions for DL scheduling](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)
* UE satisfies criteria of being scheduled for new transmission in RIM DL muted slots due to enabled NRRIM\_PROFILE.highPrioRbTransInMutedSlots as specified in the requirement [11174311: RIM DL muting and UL overload restrictions for DL scheduling](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

UE should be removed from the CS2 list and added again as UE with new transmission with the non-reTx priority corresponding to original reTx priority (e.g.: 'harqRetransGbr' is converted to 'gbrTraffic') and processed together with the other UEs selected for the new transmission.

Otherwise (conditions are not satisfied), UE is not scheduled in this slot.

---

##### 3.29.5.4.1 Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes

###### 3.29.5.4.1.0-1 Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes Definition of a PDSCH Area (ID: 11175812)

**3.29.5.4.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175815`)

Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes
A PDSCH area is defined as a set of contiguous RB within the BW according to the CORESET#0 and the dedicated BWP configuration.

---

###### 3.29.5.4.1.0-2 Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes Common PDSCH Area (ID: 11175821)

**3.29.5.4.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175826`)

Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes
The common PDSCH Area is the smallest PDSCH area and overlaps in the frequency domain with the RBs occupied by the CORESET#0.

The common PDSCH Area is nested in all other PDSCH areas.

The L2PS shall initialize the number of available RBs in the common PDSCH area as the number of RBs in the CORESET#0 which are:

* not a blanked PRBs
* not containing any SS/PBCH resource element

---

###### 3.29.5.4.1.0-3 Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes Dedicated BWP PDSCH Areas (ID: 11175829)

**3.29.5.4.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175833`)

Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes
One dedicated BWP PDSCH Area shall be created per dedicated DL BWP configured in the cell.

The L2PS shall initialize the number of available RBGs as the number of RBGs contained in the dedicated BWP mapped to the PDSCH area as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), which are considered available:

* according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4284\_replaced([11177074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kusa1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in SSB slots
* according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4288\_replaced([11177156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kutB3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in TRS slots
* which do not contain any blanked PRB in other slots

---

###### 3.29.5.4.1.0-4 Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes Mapping UEs in CS2 list to PDSCH areas (ID: 11175839)

**3.29.5.4.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175842`)

Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes
Each UE in the CS2 list shall be mapped to one PDSCH area according to the RB scheduling restriction that applies given its active BWP, the expected DCI format, search space and CORESET to be used :

* UEs using DCI format 1\_0 scheduled from a common search space associated to CORESET#0 shall be mapped to the common PDSCH Area, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4489\_replaced([11179193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* UEs using DCI format 1\_1 shall be mapped to the PDSCH Area which is defined by their active BWP (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4492\_replaced([11179250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
