Note: it is assumed that DCI format 1\_0 is not scheduled from a UE specific search space or from a common search space associated to another CORESET than CORESET#0.

---

###### 3.29.5.4.1.0-5 Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes Maintenance of maximum available RBs in the PDSCH areas (ID: 11175847)

**3.29.5.4.1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11175850`)

Determining the PDSCH areas and their sizes
L2PS shall maintain the number of estimated available RB/RBGs for each created PDSCH area considering that:

* PDSCH areas are nested in one another such that one UE selected from a smaller PDSCH area will consume RB/RBGs from all larger PDSCH areas
* RB/RBGs exclusive to a PDSCH area would be consumed first for UEs associated to that PDSCH areas but are allowed to consume RB/RBGs from smaller PDSCH areas if those exclusive RB/RBGs are exhausted.
* the granularity of allocation from each PDSCH area

---

##### 3.29.5.4.2 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-1 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation overview (ID: 11175859)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175863`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
As explained in 5G\_UP\_10879\_replaced([11350699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU8vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB-DU shall define PDSCH sub-areas as contiguous PRBs area within the carrier bandwidth to cope with RAT1 contiguous restriction such that each PRB RAT1 allocation belongs to a single PDSCH sub-area.

An external DL signal (see 5G\_UP\_10881\_replaced([11350711](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU9PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), a Nr DL signal (see 5G\_UP\_10887\_replaced([11350744](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU-vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or a DL PRB allocation (5G\_UP\_10903\_replaced([11350777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU__vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) will split the carrier bandwidth into 2 or more PDSCH sub areas if its location belongs to a PRBs area not adjacent to one of the sides of the carrier bandwidth: the gNB-DU shall create the new PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23623\_replaced([11175867](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and for each PDSCH sub area shall identify the lowest PRB and compute its size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

gNB-DU shall determine in which PDSCH sub-area the PRBs are distributed and shall update, within each PDSCH sub-area, the number of remaining available PRBs in the CORESET#0 and in each dedicated BWP as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23464\_replaced([11176110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) according to UE’s bearers priority. A UE cannot be allocated if there is not enough PRBs left in any PDSCH subareas in the required BWP and the sum of the number of PRBs allocated within each PDSCH sub area shall not exceed the size of each PDSCH sub area (see all the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Figures and drawings materials (under UP algorithm, UP system level and user scenarios modules) can be found under this link:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=V8ishU&cid=f5e66487%2D25f0%2D4542%2Dad5e%2D9f60bf6b2d30&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20UP%20Algorithms%2F3%20L2RT%20Algorithms%2FCB008927%5FPDSCH%5Fsub%5Fareas&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

---

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-2 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation number of PDSCH sub areas (ID: 11175867)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175871`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
PDSCH sub areas creation: use cases, PRBs size condition, number supported

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12719391`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Due to the existence or the scheduling of a DL signal, gNB-DU splits the carrier bandwidth into 2 or more PDSCH sub areas (if several occurences), and shall initialize the size of each PDSCH sub area. The DL signal creating PDSCH sub areas can be:

1) external (non Nr) DL signal through a static configuration. This static configuration is applicable for all the slots. The PRBs which are blanked by the external DL signal split the bandwidth into 2 PDSCH sub areas and don't belong to the PDSCH sub areas which are above and below since any RAT1 PDSCH grant cannot allocate these PRBs. gNB-DU shall initialize the size of the PDSCH sub areas for each slot in the TD scheduler.

2) Nr DL signal. The location of this DL signal is configurable at cell level and is applicable for specific slots. In the slots where the Nr DL signal is scheduled, gNB-DU shall create the PDSCH sub areas in the same way than an external signal, that is one above and one below the PRBs carrying the Nr DL signal and shall initialize the size of these PDSCH sub areas. For SSB slot , see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23462\_replaced([11176070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB012623-B]For SSB interference mitigation(not Nr DL signal), refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). [End CB012623-B] For a FDD cell shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and when LTE CAT-M is enabled, the number of LTE CAT-M PRBs areas varies according to the slot and is known after cell setup procedure before any scheduling, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26285\_replaced([11175892](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3) DL PRBs allocation:

* If a PDSCH DL RAT1 grant is allocated in the FD scheduler (that is with a startPrb and a number of PRB), this allocation has the same effect than a DL signal, because any new DL RAT1 allocation shall be allocated above or below this allocation and automatically splits the current PDSCH sub area into 2 new PDSCH sub areas. In order to avoid to create plenty of PDSCH sub areas, gNB-DU (at DL FD scheduler level) shall reserve a number of PRBs for any DCI 1\_1 RAT1 PDSCH grant and shall not allocate a startPrb (see below the difference between PRBs reservation and PRBS allocation): gNB-DU shall perform the startPrb allocation at the end of the FD scheduler when all the PRBs have been reserved for all the possible UEs scheduled (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23995\_replaced([11182407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* since the CORESET #0 (or CORESET X (with X>0)) is not adjacent to one of the side of the carrier bandwidth, when for the first time in a slot a DCI 1\_0 is scheduled (common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING),  rach msg2,  rach msg4 (initial and retransmission) or any other DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant), gNB-DU shall split and update the existing PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24042\_replaced([11175880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and shall create an additional PDSCH sub area if there is enough PRBs (see the conditions about the size of a PDSCH sub area below).  Contrary to the DL signal, the PRBs of the CORESET #0 or CORESET#X (with X>0)**belongs to a PDSCH sub area, because these PRBs can be allocated to a DL DCI 1\_1 RAT1 PDSCH grant if available.**
* If a dynamicswitch RAT0 is allocated (for initial transmission, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24995\_replaced([11181383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and for retransmission see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), since a DCI 1\_1 RAT1 PDSCH grant can be allocated either below or above the RBGs allocated for a dynamicswitch RAT0 and since the RBGs are contiguously allocated in a contiguous way inside each PDSCH sub area, for each existing PDSCH sub area where RBGs are allocated for a dyamicswitch RAT0, gNB\_DU shall update the size of each existing PDSCH sub area (below the RBGs allocated) for the other RAT1 allocations and shall create another PDSCH sub area  (above the RBGs) under certain conditions: see all the details in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: If there is no Dl signal, the PDSCH sub area is the carrier bandwidth itself. When a DL signal splits a PDSCH sub area (which can be the carrier bandwidth) into 2 PDSCH sub areas: the existing PDSCH sub area (which is the largest one) and a new additional one. The limits and the size of the PDSCH sub area are clarified in the requirements below 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The blanked PRBs do not belong to a PDSCH sub area.

gNB-DU shall create a new PDSCH sub area if the size of this new PDSCH sub area is equal or greater than *rdMinNbPRBsPdschSubArea PRBs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24749\_replaced([11193819](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwKsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) otherwise there is no new PDSCH sub area. But gNB-DU shall still update the size of the existing PDSCH sub area. NOTE:* *When* rdMinNbPRBsPdschSubArea PRBs > 1 PRB, gNB-DU shall apply the check for the creation of the PDSCH sub area due to static DL signal such as in SSB slot or with CAT-M PRBs area.\*\*\*\*

When **there is no common channel no rach msg2, no rach msg4 scheduled no DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in the to-be-scheduled list in a slot, gNB-DU shall not modify the number of existing PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23605\_replaced([11175956](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7PfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).** But gNB-DU shall update the size of a PDSCH sub area due to PRB blanked at the edge of the carrier bandwidth or due to specific bandwidth.\*\*\*\*

gNB-DU shall follow rules provided by the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23683\_replaced([11176637](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  in order to select a PDSCH sub area where PRBs are reserved for a RAT1 allocation for a UE between several PDSCH sub areas.

In TRS slots or in SSB slot with TRS symbols, or in CSI-IM slots when CSI-IM is rate matched (that is **[Before CB015668-A]** NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true **[CB015668-A]** actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true(note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**), **if FR1 TDD FD beamforming** (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), for each PDSCH area, gNB-DU shall update the startPrb and the size of the PDSCH sub area as a multiple of a PRB granularity (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11175880`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
CORESET #0 use cases in a PDSCH sub area

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12632569`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
When a DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant in CSS mode is scheduled for the first time in a slot, gNB-DU shall handle 3 different use cases (if /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is TRUE):

 as soon as gNB-DU schedules a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) in the to-be-scheduled list at TD scheduler level and the true start PRB of the common channel is already known (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall re initialize the size of the existing PDSCH area which contains the common channel and a part of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10904\_replaced([11350781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8APvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23458\_replaced([11176013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and shall create an additional PDSCH sub area if there is enough PRBs (see the conditions about the size of a PDSCH sub area, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24041\_replaced([11175871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If another rach msg2, rach msg4 or DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled in the same slot, gNB-DU shall not create another PDSCH sub area and shall not modify the size of existing PDSCH sub area: gNB-DU shall allocate PRBs for this rach msg2, rach msg4 or DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in the PDSCH sub area which contains the CORESET #0 or in the other PDSCH sub area within the CORESET #0 if PRBs area available (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23558\_replaced([11176156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23683\_replaced([11176637](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 **When a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) [CB007372-M] and when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M] and when LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true) and at least one LTE CAT-M PRBs area is present in the slot, if gNB-DU schedules a rach msg2 or a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (which is not a rach msg4 or a common channel) in the PDSCH sub area between the lowest PRB available at the bottom of the carrier bandwidth and the PRB below the lowest blanked PRB of the lower CAT-M PRBs area (because this PDSCH sub area contains the largest consecutive PRBs area within the CORESET #0 (or X with X>0) as required in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16468\_replaced([11181355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall update the size of this existing PDSCH area which contains the largest PRBs area within the CORESET #0 (or X with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10906\_replaced([11350792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8AvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23460\_replaced([11175983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and shall create an additional PDSCH sub area if there is enough PRBs (see the conditions about the size of a PDSCH sub area, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24041\_replaced([11175871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), otherwise if gNB-DU schedules a rach msg2 in a PDSCH sub area above a CAT-M PRBs area, the rach msg2 shall start with the PRB above the highest blanked PRB of this CAT-M PRBs area (which is the start Prb of this PDSCH sub area too).**

 as soon as gNB-DU schedules a rach msg2 (Note: in the case where the FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) [CB007372-M] and when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M], the slot does not contain any LTE CAT-M PRBs area - the slot with CAT-M PRBs area(s) has been previously described specifically in the paragraph above) or a rach msg4 in the to-be-scheduled list at TD scheduler level for the first time in a slot and there is no common channel scheduled in this slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall update the size of the existing PDSCH area which contains completely or partially the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10906\_replaced([11350792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8AvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, 5G\_UP\_10912\_replaced([11350814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8BvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23460\_replaced([11175983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and shall create an additional PDSCH sub area if there is enough PRBs (see the conditions about the size of a PDSCH sub area, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24041\_replaced([11175871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If another rach msg2, rach msg4 or DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled in the same slot, gNB-DU shall not create another PDSCH sub area and shall no modify the size of existing PDSCH sub area: gNB-DU shall allocate PRBs for this rach msg2 or rach msg4 in the PDSCH sub area which contains the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23469\_replaced([11176138](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If**all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are not mapped to the configured CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10912\_replaced([11350814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8BvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall handle the rach in the same way, except that the startPrb of the CORESET #0 is replaced by the startPrb of the CORESET X (with X>0)  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23460\_replaced([11175983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ).\*\*\*\*

 as soon as gNB-DU schedules a DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant in CSS mode which requires a number of reserved PRBs for its allocation (Note: in the case where the FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS), the slot does not contain any LTE CAT-M PRBs area - the slot with CAT-M PRBs area(s) has been previously described specifically in the paragraph above) and there is no common channel no rach msg2 and no rach msg4 scheduled in this slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall check that a PDSCH sub area creation can be successfully performed (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23607\_replaced([11176609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If this check is successful, gNB-DU shall update the size of the existing PDSCH area which contains completely or partially the CORESET #0 and CORESET#X (with X>0)  (see 5G\_UP\_10910\_replaced([11350804](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8BPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **, 5G\_UP\_10912\_replaced([11350814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8BvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23611\_replaced([11176043](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniT_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and shall create an additional PDSCH sub area if there is enough PRBs (see the conditions about the size of a PDSCH sub area, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24041\_replaced([11175871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If this check is failed, gNB-DU shall not allocate PRBs for this DCI 1\_0 in the slot, shall not modify the existing PDSCH sub areas and shall postpone the DCI 1\_0 to the next slot. If**all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are not mapped to the configured CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10912\_replaced([11350814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8BvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall handle the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in the way except that the startPrb of the CORESET #0 is replaced by the startPrb of the CORESET X (with X>0)  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23460\_replaced([11175983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ).\*\*\*\*

The assumption is that the CORESET 0/ SSB /initial BWP are in the lowest part of the carrier bandwidth if dedicated BWPs are bottom aligned.

[CB007372-M] Note: in the case where the FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true and LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM=true) there are no LTE CAT-M PRBs within the CORESET#0 area. **[End CB007372-M]**

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11175888`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
static and configurable multi PDSCH sub areas

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175892`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
FDD DSS with LTE Cat-M enabled

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12643254`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12643254:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
For a FDD cell shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and when LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true) according to 5G\_UP\_10884\_replaced([11350715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU9fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB-DU shall create several PDSCH sub areas in the slot according to the number of the CAT-M NBs PRBs areas (their number and their locations are known before the TD scheduler step, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4279\_replaced([11177002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_CP\_IF\_NTW\_2693\_replaced([11367978](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__Cd5fvvmEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

Provided there is enough PRB for a PDSCH sub area creation (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24041\_replaced([11175871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

 If there is only one CAT-M PRBs area,

o for the PDSCH sub area which is below CAT-M PRBs area, gNB-DU shall initialize its startPrb to the lowest PRB available at the bottom of the carrier bandwidth (for instance CRB0 if no PRB blanking) and its size to difference between the PRB below the lowest blanked PRB of this CAT-M PRBs area and its startPrb.

o for the PDSCH sub area which is above CAT-M PRBs area, gNB-DU shall initialize its startPrb to the PRB above the highest blanked PRB of this CAT-M PRBs area and its size to the difference between  the highest PRB available at the top of the carrier bandwidth and its startPrb.

 If there is more than one CAT-M PRBs area, additionally for the PDSCH sub areas which are between 2 CAT-M PRBs area, gNB-DU shall initialize its startPrb to the PRB above the highest blanked PRB of the lower CAT-M PRBs area and its size to the difference between the PRB below the lowest blanked PRB of the higher CAT-M PRBs area and its startPrb.

Note: A slot which matches with a MBSFN subframe does not have any CAT-M PRBs area (see 5G\_UP\_10884\_replaced([11350715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU9fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which exclude common channels and rach msg4 with CAT-M PRBs area in the same slot [CB007372-M] when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M].

When there is at least one CAT-M PRBs area in a slot [CB007372-M] and when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M], when gNB-DU schedules a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (rach msg2 or DCI 1\_0 type 3 transient phase) for the first time in a PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24042\_replaced([11175880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall set at the bottom of the CORESET 0 within the PDSCH sub area (start Prb of the CORESET 0 or the PRB above the highest blanked PRB of the lower CAT-M PRBs area according where is located the largest PRBs area within the CORESET 0 see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16468\_replaced([11181355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall schedule any other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in the PDSCH sub area (if enough remaining PRBs in the CORESTE 0 within the PDSCH sub area) just above the previous DCI 1\_0 allocated in the PDSCH sub area.

For the PDSCH sub area which contains the SSB, gNB-DU shall split this existing PDSCH sub area into 2 PDSCH sub areas and initialize the startPrb and the size of the two PDSCH sub areas as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23462\_replaced([11176070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different sub-areas related to PDSCH grants and LTE CAT-M PRBs. The diagram uses colored blocks and text labels to represent these concepts.
On the left side, there are several vertical colored blocks. From left to right, these are:
- A thin blue block labeled "CO ET#0".
- A wider yellow block labeled "initial BWP".
- A wider orange block labeled "dedicated B".
- A light orange block.
There are also thick red horizontal bars that appear to segment the vertical blocks.
On the right side, text labels and connecting lines explain the relationships between the blocks and the PDSCH/PRB areas.
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a vertical bracket spanning the top portion of the colored blocks, above the first red bar.
- "DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grant" is positioned to the right of "PDSCH sub area 1".
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a vertical bracket spanning the middle portion of the colored blocks, between the first and second red bars.
- "3 LTE CAT-M PRBs areas" is positioned to the right, with lines connecting to the middle section of the diagram.
- "PDSCH sub area 3" is indicated by a vertical bracket spanning the lower portion of the colored blocks, between the second and third red bars.
- "DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant" is positioned to the right of "PDSCH sub area 3".
- Below the third red bar, there are labels "No PDSCH sub area" and "Not enough PRBs", with a small vertical bracket indicating a very small or non-existent area.
The diagram visually represents how different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and dedicated blocks are allocated within different PDSCH sub-areas, and how these relate to DCI grants and LTE CAT-M PRBs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13171.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11175903`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
blanked PRBs area in TRS slots (work around)

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175929`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11175929:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In TRS slots, when all the followig conditions are fullfilled:

* the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True for the cell
* the TRS rate matching is at RE level: NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking = True
* the TRS bandwidth configured is 52 PRBs: NRCELL.csirsTrackingPeriod.rbAllocation = 52
* PRBs blanking is configured at the bottom of the carrier bandwidth: NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0
* multi BWP is configured for the cell: NRBTS.actMultipleDedicatedBWPs = True or NRBTS.actPowerSavingBWP = True
* there are PRBs overlapped between the TRS bandwidth and the blanked PRBs area at the bottom of the carried bandwidth: NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > NRCELL.csirsTrackingPeriod.startRB

The gNB-DU shall handle the TRS shift in the BWPs different from the large dedicated BWP (BWP1). Since this 3GPP use case is not supported (see 5G\_UP\_IF\_2101\_replaced([11517191](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUeP5vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall create a blanked PRBs area on these shifted PRBs. This start PRB of this additional blanked PRBs area is the PRB just above the highest TRS PRB (PRB = 52) and the number of this additional blanked PRBs area

* the start PRB (additional blanked PRBs area) = NRCELL.csirsForTracking.startRB + NRCELL.csirsForTracking.rbAllocation
* the number of PRBs (additional blanked PRBS area) = NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL - NRCELL.csirsForTracking.startRB

[PR798480] gNB-DU shall mute TRS signal in the low edge blanked PRBs area (that is the PRBs between NRCELL.csirsForTracking.startRB and (NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDl-1)). [End PR798480]

When the gNB-DU computed this additional blanked PRBS area during cell setup phase, gNB-DU shall split in each TRS slot the carrier bandwidth into 2 PDSCH sub areas:

 For the PDSCH sub area which is below this additional blanked PRBs area, gNB-DU shall initialize its startPrb to the CRB which is just above the highest blanked PRB of the low edge blanked PRBs area and its size to the value of the lowest blanked PRB in this additional blanked PRBS area reduced the value of the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area.

 For the PDSCH sub area which is above this additional blanked PRBs area, gNB-DU shall initialize its startPrb to the PRB above the highest blanked PRB in this additional blanked PRBs area and its size to the carrier bandwidth (dlBandwidth, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) reduced by the value of startPrb of the PDSCH sub area.

[Before PR798480]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource allocations, likely in a cellular communication context. The diagram uses colored bars to represent these BWPs and text labels to identify them and explain various areas and concepts.
On the left side, there are several vertical colored bars of varying heights and colors, stacked next to each other. From left to right, these bars are:
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A tall orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP 1".
- A green bar labeled "BWP-2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
Below these bars, there are red horizontal bars. The leftmost red bar extends under the blue, yellow, and orange bars. Another red bar is positioned to the right of the light blue bar, and a purple bar is situated between the light blue bar and this red bar.
To the right of the colored bars, there are vertical arrows and text labels indicating different areas and measurements.
- A red horizontal bar at the top is associated with the label "PRBs blanking".
- A long vertical arrow points from this top red bar down to another red horizontal bar. This entire vertical section is labeled "PDSCH sub area 2".
- Below "PDSCH sub area 2", there is another red horizontal bar. This bar is associated with the label "additional blanked PRBs area (PDSCH)".
- A vertical arrow points from the "additional blanked PRBs area (PDSCH)" down to a section labeled "PDSCH sub area 1".
- Within "PDSCH sub area 1", there is a label "52 PRBS TRS" with a vertical double-headed arrow indicating a measurement.
- Below "PDSCH sub area 1", there are two more labels: "Low edge" and "PRBs blanking", each with a small vertical double-headed arrow.
On the far right, there is a blue line that starts from the "PDSCH sub area 1" region and extends upwards and to the right, with text labels associated with it: "nb PRBs area overlapping", "between TRS and", and "low edge blanked PRBs area".
The overall diagram visually represents how different BWPs might be configured and how certain Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) are utilized or blanked out in different sub-areas of a PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) transmission.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13173.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR798480]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource allocations, likely in a cellular communication context. The diagram uses colored bars of varying heights to represent these BWPs.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Allocation Bars):**
\* **CORESET#0:** Represented by a light blue bar on the far left.
\* **Initial BWP:** A yellow bar, slightly taller than CORESET#0.
\* **Dedicated BWP-1:** A tall orange bar, the tallest among the initial BWPs.
\* **BWP-2:** A green bar, shorter than Dedicated BWP-1 but taller than Initial BWP.
\* **PS BWP-3:** A light blue bar, shorter than BWP-2.
\* **Purple Bar:** A dark purple bar, positioned to the right of PS BWP-3. It has a hatched pattern at its base.
\* **Red Bars:** Two red bars are present. One is at the very top, spanning a significant width. Another red bar is positioned above the purple bar and extends to the right. There are also red bars at the bottom, below the initial BWPs and BWP-2.
**Right Side (Annotations and Areas):**
\* **High Edge PRBs blanking:** Indicated by dashed orange lines and text above the top red bar.
\* **PDSCH sub area 2:** A vertical annotation indicating a region above the top red bar.
\* **Additional blanked PRBs area (PDSCH):** Text and dashed orange lines indicating a region between the top red bar and the red bar above the purple bar.
\* **52 PRBs TRS:** Text and a vertical arrow pointing to the purple bar, indicating its size and that it represents Transmission Reception Symbols (TRS).
\* **PDSCH sub area 1:** A vertical annotation indicating a region below the red bar above the purple bar and above the hatched portion of the purple bar.
\* **Low edge PRBs blanking:** Indicated by dashed orange lines and text below the hatched portion of the purple bar.
\* **Nb PRBs area overlapping between TRS and low Edge blanked PRBs area:** A blue line and text pointing to a region that appears to be a combination of the TRS area and the low edge blanking area.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and specific resource elements like TRS are allocated and how certain areas might be blanked or used for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13173.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End PR798480]

figure 1: 2 PDSCH sub areas below and above the additional blanked PRBs area (for PDSCH)

Note1: For the PDSCH sub area below this additional blanked PRBs area which contains the CORESET #0, the CORESET 0 may overlap with this additional blanked PRBs area, but since there is no DCI 1\_0 scheduled in TRS slot, then the size and the number of remaining PRBs is initialized to 0 and there is no impact.

Note2: In FR1 TDD, if FR1 TDD FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), gNB-DU shall apply PRB\_GRANULARITY constraint for each PDSCH area on start PRB and the number of PRBs (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If there is a SSB in TRS slot (FDD, TDD non beamforming), the SSB is located in the PDSCH sub area below the additional additional blanked PRBs area. gNB-DU shall split this existing PDSCH sub area into 2 PDSCH sub areas and initialize the startPrb and the size of the two PDSCH sub areas as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23462\_replaced([11176070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If the SSB overlaps the additional additional blanked PRBs area, there is no blanking for the SSB itself. In this case, gNB-DU shall split theexisting PDSCH sub area  into 2 PDSCH sub areas: one above the additional blanked PRBs area and one below the SSB.

Note3: The additional blanked PRBs area only concerns the PDSCH, the other signal are not involved: the symbols for the PDCCH, TRS and CSI-RS are not blanked in these PRBs. If a SSB overlaps these PRBs, they are not blanked too.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating a radio frame structure, likely for a cellular communication system. The diagram is organized into columns representing time slots or symbols, numbered from 0 to 13 along the top. The left side of the diagram shows vertical bars labeled with "B", "W", "P", and numbers 1, 2, and 3, possibly indicating different types of resources or channels.
The main body of the diagram is divided into horizontal sections representing different types of data or control information within the radio frame. These sections are color-coded and labeled:
\* **Top Section (Green):** Labeled "PDSCH (no RM)". This section spans from symbol 2 to symbol 13.
\* **Middle Section (Red):** Labeled "additional blanked PRBs". This section is located below the "PDSCH (no RM)" section and spans from symbol 3 to symbol 11.
\* **Lower Section (Blue and Purple):** The blue section is labeled "SSB" and occupies symbols 3 to 7. A purple section labeled "T", "R", "S" is positioned to the right of the "SSB" section, spanning symbols 7 to 8. Another purple section with the same labels "T", "R", "S" is located to the right of the "SSB" section, spanning symbols 10 to 11.
\* **Bottom Section (Green):** This section is below the "additional blanked PRBs" and the "SSB" section, and extends from symbol 2 to symbol 13.
\* **Leftmost Section (Purple):** This section spans symbols 0 to 2 and is labeled with "P", "D", "C", "C", "H" vertically.
\* **Rightmost Section (Yellow):** This section spans symbol 13 and is labeled with "C", "S", "I", "R", "S" vertically.
The diagram visually represents the allocation of different resources within a radio frame, with specific regions designated for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel), blanked Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs), Synchronization Signal Blocks (SSB), and other control or reference signals. The labels on the left might correspond to different antennas or transmission paths.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13173.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent different resource allocations. From left to right, these are:
\* **Red bars:** These appear at the bottom and top, indicating "PRBs blanking" and "Low edge" respectively.
\* **Blue bar:** Labeled "CORESET#0".
\* **Yellow bar:** Labeled "initial BWP".
\* **Orange bar:** A tall bar labeled "dedicated BWP 1".

\* **Light blue bar:** Labeled "PS BWP-3".

\* **PDSCH sub area 3:** This is the largest area, spanning from the top red "PRBs blanking" to the "additional blanked PRBs area (PDSCH)".

\* **PRBs blanking:** This label is associated with the very bottom red bar.

The SSB and TRS are also shown as specific resource allocations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13173.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

figure 3: SSB overlaps the additional

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11175933`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
PDSCH sub areas steps;  reserved vs allocated PRBs

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175938`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
When a DCI 1\_0 (a common channel , a rach msg2 , a rach msg4) is scheduled in the CORESET #0 within a PDSCH sub area for the first time in a slot (see details in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24042\_replaced([11175880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall initialize the size and the startPrb of the existing and additional PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (carrying harq retransmission, DL MAC CE, SRBs) in the to-be-scheduled list at TD scheduler level (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode later in the FD scheduler is scheduled, gNB-DU shall first check the PRBs reservation in the existing PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23607\_replaced([11176609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the check is successful, gNB-DU shall initialize the size and the startPrb of the existing PDSCH sub area, shall count if there is enough PRBs to create a new PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24041\_replaced([11175871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and if enough PRBs, shall initialize the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), otherwise no creation of additional PDSCH sub area. Otherwise (if the check for PRBs reservation for the DCI 1\_0 has failed), gNB-DU shall not modify the existing PDSCH sub area and shall postpone the DCI 1\_0 to the next slot.

[CB008927-B] In a SSB slot, gNB-DU shall initialize the size and the startPrb of the two PDSCH sub areas split by the SSB at the latest at the end of the TD scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23462\_replaced([11176070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End CB008927-B]

[CB008927-L] If a dynamicswitch RAT0 (as retransmission) is allocated in the slot, gNB-DU shall initialize the size (and the startPrb if needed) of the existing PDSCH sub area and shall create another PDSCH sub area under certain conditions are fulfilled: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008927-L]

After the initialization of the startPrb and the size of the existing and additional PDSCH sub areas have been completed, each time a DCI 1\_0 requires PRBs for an allocation, gNB-DU shall count the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 within the PDSCH sub area  [CB008927-H] (which contains [CB008927-K] completely or partially [End CB008927-K] the CORESET #0  [CB008927-K] and CORESET#X (with X>0) [End CB008927-K] ) [End CB008927-H], shall check the constraints (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23607\_replaced([11176609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and update its value in variables:

* when a common channel is scheduled (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23467\_replaced([11176121](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* when a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 is scheduled (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23469\_replaced([11176138](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if no common channel in the same slot or 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23558\_replaced([11176156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if there is already a common channel in the same slot).
* when another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23471\_replaced([11176219](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

When a DCI 1\_1 is scheduled, gNB-DU shall count the number of remaining PRBs in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area, shall check the constraints (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and update its value in variables (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23567\_replaced([11176288](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kupXdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for mono dedicated BWP and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23569\_replaced([11176349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kupXfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for multi dedicated BWP).

[CB008927-L] If a dynamicswitch RAT0 (as initial transmission) is allocated in the slot, gNB-DU shall initialize the size (and the startPrb if needed) of the existing PDSCH sub area and shall create another PDSCH sub area under certain conditions are fulfilled (UEs in BWP2 …): see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008927-L]

wording definition between PRB reservation and PRB allocation or a RAT1 PDSCH grant:

A RAT1 PDSCH grant is allocated when its startPrb and its number of PRBs are by computed and provided the DL FD scheduler.

When only the number of PRBs is provided by the DL TD or FD scheduler (and not the startPrb) for a RAT 1 PDSCH grant, these PRBs are reserved and not allocated.

[CB008927-J] If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true, gNB-DU shall initialize to 0 a RBG pre-book bitmap for a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation for a slot at the beginning of the TD scheduling at least before any retransmission scheduling. When gNB-DU decides to allocate a UE  with  “dynamicswitch” RAT 0 allocation (for initial transmission see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24995\_replaced([11181383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and for retransmission see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23663\_replaced([11177504](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  gNB-DU shall set BIT x of this RBG pre book bitmap to 1 if the RBGs will be allocated later (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25098\_replaced([11182422](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and to 0 otherwise, the BIT x is indexed from MSB (BIT0) to LSB in the bitmap as it is done for the frequency domain assignment field for a RAT 0 allocation (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The RBG size is the one of the dedicated BWP (that is the largest dedicated BWP1 or the BWP2) where the UE belongs to (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25040\_replaced([11178662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End CB008927-J]

---

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-3 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation step1: initialization of the startPrb and the size of the PDSCH sub areas (ID: 11175942)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175946`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Variables and pre initialization

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175950`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
gNB-DU shall create variables for the startPrb and the size of each PDSCH sub area in order to know all the PRBs which can be allocated inside the PDSCH sub area:

* the size of the PDSCH sub area: rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] . At the first time, gNB-DU shall initialize with the carrier bandwidth size.
* the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area: rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]  At the first time, gNB-DU shall initialize to CRB0.

[CB008927-B] If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True (PRB blanking activated) or if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated) , gNB-DU shall initiailize the size of the PDSCH sub area as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24010\_replaced([11175959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7PvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008927-B]

[CB008927-D]

The size of the BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area: rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] if p>1. gNB-DU shall initialize at the first time with the BWP(p) size. (rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] is the size largest BWP, that is the size of PDSCH sub area: rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] ).

[CB008927-H] Note: If NRCELL.ueConnectionEstablishmentMode is set to useInitialBwp, gNB-DU shall initialize the size of the dedicated BWP(0) rmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][0]  to the size of the InitialBWP. [End CB008927-H]

If the dedicated BWPs are bottom aligned, gNB-DU shall set for the BWP(p) the same startPrb than the PDSCH sub area: rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] .

[End CB008927-D]

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11175956`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Initialization of the size of the PDSCH sub areas when no common channel, no rach msg2, no rach msg4, no DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode carrying harq retransmissions, DL MAC CE or SRBs in the TD scheduler list

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175959`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
mono BWP or dedicated BWP bottom aligned

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12816294`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12816294:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If in the CORESET #0 there is no common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING), no rach msg2, no rach msg4 and no DCI 1\_0 in CSS in CSS mode carrying harq retransmissions, DL MAC CE or SRBs in the TD scheduler list, gNB-DU shall not modify the number of existing PDSCH sub areas in mono dedicated BWP and dedicated BWP bottom aligned.

The size of the BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area: *rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] if p>1. gNB-DU shall initialize at the first time with the BWP(p) size.* (rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] is the size largest BWP, that is the size of PDSCH sub area: *rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] ).*\*\*

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True (PRB blanking activated),

* If the PDSCH sub area is up to the top of the carrier bandwidth (see 5G\_UP\_10888\_replaced([11350732](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU-PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_362\_replaced([11349089](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubbgqvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), for all the slots, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of blanked PRBs at the high edge of the carrier bandwidth from the size of the PDSCH sub area

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**- NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL*\*\*\*\*

* If the PDSCH sub area starts from the bottom of the carrier bandwidth (see 5G\_UP\_10888\_replaced([11350732](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU-PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_362\_replaced([11349089](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubbgqvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), for all the slots, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth from the size of the PDSCH sub area  for all the dedicated BWP(p).

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**- NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL*\*\*\*\*

if p >1, *rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**- NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL*\*\*\*\*

* gNB-DU shall initialize the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area to the number of blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth.

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL*

If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),  gNB-DU shall update the size and the starting PRB of the PDSCH sub area with the NIDD parameters defined for the specific bandwidth (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_7989\_replaced([11277346](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpMt5vvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl*

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl.cRbSizeDl*\*\*

If the dedicated BWPs are bottom aligned, gNB-DU shall set for the BWP(p) the same startPrb than the PDSCH sub area: *rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] .*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their sizes in relation to a PDSCH subarea. The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent these BWPs.
On the left side, there are several colored bars stacked next to each other, representing different BWPs. From left to right, these are:
- A thin blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A tall orange bar labeled "largest BWP-1".
- A green bar labeled "BWP-2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
At the bottom of these bars, there is a thick red bar.
On the right side of the diagram, there are text labels and arrows indicating sizes and relationships:
- At the top, there's a red horizontal line with dashed orange lines extending downwards. Text next to this indicates "PRBs blanking:" and "nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL".
- Below this, an arrow points from the top of the "largest BWP-1" bar to the top of the "BWP-2" bar, with text "Size of PDSCH sub area 1: rrmPdschSubAreaSize[1]".
- Further down, an arrow points from the top of the "BWP-2" bar to the top of the "PS BWP-3" bar, with text "size of BWP(2): rrmSizeBwplnPdschSubArea[1][2]".
- At the bottom, an arrow points from the top of the "PS BWP-3" bar to the bottom of the diagram, with text "size of BWP(3): rrmSizeBwplnPdschSubArea[1][3]".
- At the very bottom, there is another red horizontal line with dashed orange lines extending upwards. Text next to this indicates "PRB blanking" and "nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL".
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs are contained within or relate to a larger PDSCH subarea, with some blanking regions at the top and bottom.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13181.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

in TRS slots when **[Before CB015668-A]** **NRBTS.actTddCsirsOpt = false****[CB015668-A]**actTddCsirsOpt = false (note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]****, or in CSI-IM slots when CSI-IM is rate matched (that is if** [Before CB015668-A]**NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A]**actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator)** [End CB015668-A]**)**, if FR1 TDD FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), for each PDSCH area, gNB-DU shall update the startPrb and the size of the PDSCH sub area as a multiple of a PRB granularity (see PRB\_GRANULARITY definition in 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).\*\*\*\*

If PRB\_GRANULARITY > 1

{

old\_startPrb = *rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]*

old\_size = *rmPdschSubAreaSize[n]*

new\_startPrb = CEIL(*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] / PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY*

*rmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = FLOOR( (old\_size – (new\_startPrb - old\_startPrb))* / PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = new\_startPrb*

}

**Note: there is no DCI 1\_0 scheduled in TRS slots (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

For a FDD cell shared with LTE (FDD DSS), gNB-DU shall update the PDSCH sub area (startPrb and size) in the same way when there is PRBs blanking.

When LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true), gNB-DU shall update the PDSCH sub area which is below the lowest CAT-M PRBs area or the one which is above the highest CAT-M PRBs area in the same way in the case of PRBs blanking.

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11175971`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
dedicated BWP top aligned

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175977`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11175977:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
The number of existing PDSCH sub area does not change. rrrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  is initialized in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True,

If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDl >0, for all the slots, gNB-DU shall reduce NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDLPRBs from the size of the PDSCH sub area.

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL > 0, for all the slots,  gNB-DU shall reduce NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL from the size of the PDSCH sub area for all dedicated BWP(p).

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL

if p >1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]   = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL

gNB-DU shall update the starting PRB of the PDSCH sub area and all dedicated BWP(p) in this way: rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl of the largest BWP

if p >1, rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl of each BWP(p).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their sizes within a PDSCH sub-area. The diagram uses colored blocks to represent these BWPs, arranged vertically.
On the left side, there are several colored blocks stacked vertically:
- A thin blue block labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow block labeled "initial BWP".
- A large orange block labeled "largest BWP-1".
- A green block labeled "BWP-2".
- A light blue block labeled "PS BWP-3".
To the right of these blocks, there are vertical arrows and text annotations indicating sizes and starting points.
The annotations are as follows:
- At the top, there's a red block, and below it, text indicating "PRBs blanking:" and "nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL".
- Below the "PS BWP-3" block, there's an arrow pointing down and text "size of BWP(3): rrmSizeBwplnPdschSubArea[1][3]".
- Further down, there's a dashed line and an arrow, with text "rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[1][3]".
- Below that, another arrow and text "size of BWP(2): rrmSizeBwplnPdschSubArea[1][2]".
- Following that, a dashed line and an arrow, with text "rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[1][2]".
- Below that, an arrow and text "Size of PDSCH sub area 1: rrmPdschSubAreaSize [1]".
- At the bottom, there's another red block, and above it, text "PRB-blanking" and "rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[1]", and below it, "nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL".
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs are allocated within a larger PDSCH sub-area, with specific parameters defining their sizes and starting positions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13183.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
