**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11175983`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Initialization of the size of the PDSCH sub areas when a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 is scheduled in the TD scheduler list

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175986`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
mono BWP or dedicated BWP bottom aligned

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12643280`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12643280:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is TRUE, as soon as a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 is in the to-be-scheduled list at TD scheduler level (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

**Note: gNB-DU shall follow this requirement too when a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and:**

-when there is no LTE CAT-M PRBs area in the slot (either the flag NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to false or the slot is without CAT-M PRBs area -for instance a MBSFN subframe)

* or when LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true) and at least one LTE CAT-M PRBs area is present in the slot, if gNB-DU schedules a rach msg2 (but not a rach msg4) in the PDSCH sub area between the lowest PRB available at the bottom of the carrier bandwidth and the PRB below the lowest blanked PRB of the lower CAT-M PRBs area (because this PDSCH sub area contains the largest consecutive PRBs area within the CORESET 0 as required in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16468\_replaced([11181355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **,**

When NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: in this artifact, actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator)  in TDD FR1 in a CSI-IM slot, the starting PRB of a rach msg2 or a rach msg4, if it is the first DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode scheduled in the CSI-IM slot, shall be modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY in order to keep the PDSCH sub areas (startPrb and size) multiples of PRB\_GRANULARITY (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then if it is not the case,  gNB-DU shall increase the value of starting RB of the rach msg2 / rach msg4 in this way (and then the start PRB is no more the start Prb of the CORESET 0).

[CB007372-M] Note: For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true, Coreset0 and InitialBWP resources are in NR dedicated area ([12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and do not collide with CAT-M PRBs [End CB007372-M]

**1) PDSCH sub area which contains (partially) CORESET #0 and the PRBs above**

When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0),

* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned which contains the rach msg2 or rach msg4 and the PRBs above the CORESET #0 with the position of the startPrb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) or the first PRB modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY above the startPrb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)  if NIDD parameters NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true in the CSI-IM slot**, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:**

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true, and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*(CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)***\***\*

*if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] -*(CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*\*\*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY*\*\*

else:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*startPrbCoresetCom**\***\*

*if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] -*startPrbCoresetCom\*\*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* startPrbCoresetCom\*\*

where startPrbCoresetCom is defined in this way

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note**: If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True (PRB blanking activated) or if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated), gNB-DU shall have first initialized the size of the existing PDSCH sub area as specified (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

* otherwise  gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area  and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned which contains the CORESET #0 and the PRBs above the CORESET #0 with the position of the startPrb of the CORESET #0  or the first PRB modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY above the startPrb of the CORESET #0 if NIDD parameters NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true andactTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true in the CSI-IM slot **, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth,    in this way:**

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true, and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*(CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)***\***\*

*if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] -*(CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*\*\*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY*\*\*

else:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*startPrbCoreset0**\***\*

***if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] -*startPrbCoreset0**\*\*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* startPrbCoreset0\*\*

**Note**: If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True (PRB blanking activated) or if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated), gNB-DU shall have first initiailized the size of the existing PDSCH sub area as specified (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).\*\*\*\*

**2) PDSCH sub area (which can contain partially CORESET #0) and contains the PRBs below the CORESET #0**

When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0),

gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (below the rach msg2 or rach msg4 in the CORESET #0) and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned with the position of the startPrb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) or the first PRB modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY above the startPrb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)  if NIDD parameters NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true in the CSI-IM slot, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]**= deltaCS0*\*\*

*if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]**= deltaCS0*\*\*

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true, and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] =* (CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*\*\*

– deltaCS0

else:

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] =* startPrbCoresetCom – deltaCS0\*\*

where startPrbCoreset0 and deltaCS0 are defined in this way:

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- deltaCS0 is the number of PRBs between the lowest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true, and CSI-IM slot,

deltaCS0 = (CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,

deltaCS0 =  (CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*

- NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),

deltaCS0 =  (CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*

- NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl

else:

deltaCS0 = startPrbCoresetCom

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,

deltaCS0 = startPrbCoresetCom - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),

deltaCS0 = startPrbCoresetCom - NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl

* otherwise **gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (below the CORESET #0)   and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned   with the position of the startPrb of the CORESET #0   or the first PRB modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY above the startPrb of the CORESET #0 if NIDD parameters NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true andactTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true in the CSI-IM slot**the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth,    in this way:\*\*\*\*

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]**= deltaCS0*\*\*

***if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]*\*= deltaCS0**\*\*\*

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true **,** and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] =* (CEIL( *startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)\*\*\*\*

– deltaCS0

else:

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] =* startPrbCoreset0 – deltaCS0\*\*

where startPrbCoreset0 and deltaCS0 are defined in this way:

startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0. DeltaCS0 is the number of PRBs between the lowest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true, and CSI-IM slot,

deltaCS0 = (CEIL( startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*

else:

deltaCS0 = startPrbCoreset0

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true, and CSI-IM slot,

deltaCS0 = (CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,

deltaCS0 =  (CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*

- NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),

deltaCS0 =  (CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*

- NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl

else:

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,

deltaCS0 = startPrbCoreset0 - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),

deltaCS0 = startPrbCoreset0 - NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated sub-areas within a communication system. The diagram uses colored blocks and text labels to represent these components.
On the left side, there are three vertical colored blocks stacked on top of each other, representing different BWPs. The bottom block is red, the middle block is yellow, and the top block is blue. The blue block is labeled "CORESET#0". The yellow block is labeled "initial BWP". The largest block, colored orange, is labeled "dedicated BWP" and extends vertically from the top of the yellow block downwards.
To the right of these blocks, there are horizontal dashed lines and text labels indicating different sub-areas and parameters. A long vertical black arrow originates from the top of the "dedicated BWP" block and extends upwards, with the label "PDSCH sub area 1" positioned near its top.
Below the "PDSCH sub area 1" label, there is a purple horizontal block. To the right of this purple block, there is a vertical double-headed arrow with the label "Rach msg2" positioned between the arrows. The top of this arrow aligns with the top of the purple block, and the bottom of this arrow aligns with a dashed horizontal line.
Below this dashed line, another vertical black arrow originates and extends downwards, with the label "PDSCH sub area 2" positioned near its bottom. This arrow's top aligns with the bottom of the "Rach msg2" arrow.
Further to the right, there are more labels and dashed lines. A dashed horizontal line is labeled "startPrbCoreseto" and has a vertical arrow pointing upwards to it from a point aligned with the top of the purple block. Below "startPrbCoreseto", there is a label "deltaCSO" with a vertical double-headed arrow pointing to it. The top of this arrow aligns with the "startPrbCoreseto" line, and the bottom aligns with another dashed horizontal line.
Below the "deltaCSO" arrow, there is a dashed horizontal line labeled "PRBs blanking" with a vertical double-headed arrow pointing to it. The top of this arrow aligns with the bottom of the "deltaCSO" arrow, and the bottom aligns with the top of the red block on the left.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13186.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

figure below: when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0), position of the rach msg 2 at the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) and not at the start of the CORESET 0

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G NR (New Radio). It depicts different frequency resource blocks (PRBs) allocated to various components.
On the left side, there are vertical bars representing different areas and bandwidth parts (BWPs):
- **IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area**: This is the first area, colored purple.
- **Coreset dedicated 1**: This is a dedicated area for CORESET (Control Resource Set), colored light blue.
- **CORESET#0**: This is another CORESET, colored cyan.
- **initial BWP**: This is the initial Bandwidth Part, colored yellow.
- **dedicated BWP**: This is a dedicated Bandwidth Part, colored orange.
To the right of these bars, there are lines and labels indicating relationships and sizes of these resources, particularly in relation to PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) sub-areas and CORESETs.
Key elements and labels include:
- **PDSCH sub area 1**: Indicates a region for PDSCH transmission.
- **rrmPdschSubAreaSize[1]**: A label associated with PDSCH sub area 1, likely representing its size.
- **delta (in PRBs) between CORESET 0 and CORESET COMMON**: This indicates a difference in PRBs between CORESET 0 and a common CORESET.
- **rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[1]**: A label indicating the size of CORESET 0 within PDSCH sub-area 1.
- **rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[2]**: A label indicating the size of CORESET 0 within PDSCH sub-area 2.
- **PDSCH sub area 2**: Indicates another region for PDSCH transmission.
- **Rach msg 2**: Refers to a Random Access Channel (RACH) message.
- **start Prb CORESET COMMON**: Indicates the starting PRB for a common CORESET.
- **start Prb CORESET 0**: Indicates the starting PRB for CORESET 0.
- **rrmPdschSubAreaSize[2]**: A label associated with PDSCH sub area 2, likely representing its size.
There is also a green shaded rectangle, possibly representing a specific allocation or a time-frequency resource. The diagram uses dashed orange lines to denote boundaries or reference points for the different allocations and measurements. The blue lines with arrows show the relationships between different resource sizes and positions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13186.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

If  all **the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are not mapped with the CORESET #0   (Note: for instance in FDD DSS NSA mode)  , if a rach msg2 is in the TD scheduler list, gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the existing PDSCH sub area and shall create a new PDSCH sub area in the same way except that the startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET COMMON and DeltaCS0 is the number of PRBs between the lowest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the lowest PRB of the CORESET** X (with X>0)  (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).\*\*\*\*

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11176003`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
dedicated BWP top aligned

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176008`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176008:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is TRUE, as soon as a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 is planned to be scheduled in a slot in TD scheduler list,

startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0. DeltaCS0 is the number of PRBs between the highest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the highest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). SizeCoreset0 is the size of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). dlBandwidth is the carrier bandwidth size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

deltaCS0 = dlBandwidth  - (startPrbCoreset0 + SizeCoreset0)

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True,

if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL > 0, deltaCS0 = deltaCS0 - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL

If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDl >0, for all the slots, NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL PRBs are reduced from the PDSCH sub area.

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

gNB-DU shall update the size of the PDSCH sub area which contains the CORESET #0 and the PRBs below the CORESET #0 with the position of the highest PRB of the CORESET #0 in this way:

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - deltaCS0

gNB-Du shall update the size of the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area with the position of the highest PRB the CORESET #0 in this way:

if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]   = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - deltaCS0

gNB-DU shall initialize the startPrb of this PDSCH sub area and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area are initialized in this way:

rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

if p >1, rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl of each BWP(p).

gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area which is above the CORESET #0 and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area with the position of the highest PRB of the CORESET #0 in this way:

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]   = deltaCS0

if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]   = deltaCS0

rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] = startPrbCoreset0 + SizeCoreset0

if p >1, rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = startPrbCoreset0 + SizeCoreset0

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and control resource sets (CORESETs) in a communication system, likely related to 5G NR. The diagram uses colored blocks to represent these components and arrows with labels to indicate their relationships and sizes.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Vertical Blocks (representing time or frequency resources):**
\* **Red Bar (Top and Bottom):** These appear to denote the overall boundaries or limits of the system's resource allocation.
\* **Blue Block (Leftmost):** Labeled "CORESET#0". This is the first control resource set.
\* **Yellow Block:** Labeled "initial BWP". This represents the initial bandwidth part.
\* **Orange Block:** Labeled "dedicated BWP 1". This indicates a dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **Green Block:** Labeled "BWP-2". This is another bandwidth part.
\* **Light Blue Block:** Labeled "PS BWP-3". This likely refers to a "Physical Signal" or "Paging Signal" bandwidth part.
\* **Purple Block:** This block is positioned to the right of the light blue block and appears to represent a portion of the "CORESET #0 size".
**Horizontal Labels and Arrows:**
\* **"deltaCSO"**: An arrow pointing downwards from the top red bar to the top of the purple block, indicating a difference in Carrier-Specific Offset (CSO).
\* **"Rach msg2"**: A wavy red line with an arrow pointing downwards, positioned below "deltaCSO" and above the top of the purple block. This likely signifies the location or duration of a Random Access Channel (RACH) message 2.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 2"**: An arrow pointing downwards from the top red bar to the top of the purple block, indicating a sub-area for Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH).
\* **"CORESET #0 size"**: An arrow spanning the height of the purple block, indicating its size.
\* **"StartPrbCoreseto"**: An arrow pointing downwards from the top of the purple block to the top of the light blue block, indicating the starting Physical Resource Block (PRB) for CORESET#0.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 1"**: A vertical arrow extending from the top of the diagram down to the bottom red bar, indicating a sub-area for PDSCH.
\* **"PRBs blanking"**: An arrow pointing downwards from the bottom of the light blue block to the bottom red bar, indicating a region where PRBs are blanked or unused.
The diagram visually represents how different control and data channels are allocated within the available bandwidth, with specific BWPs and CORESETs defined and positioned relative to each other.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13188.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

TBC: don't know if the top aligned supports the configuration no search space mapped on CORESET 0

If the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is not mapped with the CORESET #0 , if a rach msg2 is in the TD scheduler list, gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the existing PDSCH sub area and shall create a new PDSCH sub area in the same way except that the startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET COMMON and DeltaCS0 is the number of PRBs between the highest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the highest PRB of the CORESET COMMON (see 5G\_UP\_xxxx).

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11176013`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Initialization of the size of the PDSCH sub areas when a common channel is scheduled in the TD scheduler list

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176017`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
mono BWP or dedicated BWP bottom aligned

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12703765`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12703765:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is TRUE, as soon as a common channel (SIB1, SI PAGING) is in the to-be-scheduled list at TD scheduler level and the start PRB and the number of PRBs of a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) are known and allocated by TD scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned which contains the common channel (see 5G\_UP\_10904\_replaced([11350781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8APvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the PRBs above the common channel with the position of the startPrb of the common channel, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*deltaCC**\***\*

***if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpPdschInSubArea[n][p]*\*= rrmSizeBwpPdschInSubArea[n][p]**-*startPrbCC****\****

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* startPrbCC\*\*

Note**: If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True (PRB blanking activated) or if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated), gNB-DU shall have first initiailized the size of the existing PDSCH sub area as specified (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (below the common channel) and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned with the position of the startPrb of the common channel, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth,  in this way:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]**=*deltaCC\*\*\*\*

***if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpPdschSubArea[n+1][p]*\*= deltaCC**\*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] =* startPrbCC – deltaCC\*\*

where startPrbCC and deltaCC are defined in this way:

startPrbCC is the lowest PRB allocated for the common channel (see 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

deltaCC is the number of PRBs between  the lowest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the lowest PRB allocated for the common channel.

deltaCC = startPrbCC

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,

deltaCC = startPrbCC - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),

deltaCC = startPrbCC - NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl

Note: When NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator), in TDD FR1 in a CSI-IM slot, the starting PRB startPrbCC of the a common channel shall be modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY in order to keep the PDSCH sub areas (startPrb and size) multiples of PRB\_GRANULARITY (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and channels within a communication system. The diagram is oriented vertically, with several colored rectangular blocks representing different components.
On the left side, from bottom to top, there is a thick red rectangle labeled "PRBs blanking". Above this, there is a purple rectangle. To the left of the purple rectangle, there are three stacked blocks: a blue rectangle labeled "CORESET#0", a yellow rectangle labeled "initial BWP", and a tall orange rectangle labeled "dedicated BWP".
Vertical arrows and dashed lines indicate different regions and parameters. A long vertical arrow on the right, originating from the top of the "dedicated BWP" block and extending upwards, is labeled "PDSCH sub area 1".
Below "PDSCH sub area 1", there is a region marked by a double-headed arrow and dashed lines. This region is labeled "common Channel".
Further down, another double-headed arrow and dashed lines define a region labeled "PDSCH sub area 2". To the right of this region, there are labels "startPrbCC", "deltaCC", and "PRBs blanking", with corresponding arrows indicating their positions or extents relative to the diagram. The "startPrbCC" label is associated with the top dashed line of the "common Channel" region. The "deltaCC" label is positioned between the bottom dashed line of the "common Channel" region and the top dashed line of the "PDSCH sub area 2" region. The "PRBs blanking" label is associated with the bottom dashed line of the "PDSCH sub area 2" region.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13191.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**Note: When a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) [CB007372-M] and NRCELL.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M], there is no startPrb randomization for the common channel** (DOPR rule: NRCELL.actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP shall be set to false when NRCELL.lteNrDssMode is different from 'none' and [CB007372-M] and NRCELL.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M]), so the their start Prb is the same than the CORESET #0 (or CORESET#X (with X>0) **).**\*\*\*\*

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11176031`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
dedicated BWP top aligned

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176039`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176039:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is TRUE (and NRCELL.sibStartPrbRandomDisabled = False)

as soon as the start PRB and the number of PRBs of a common channel are known and allocated by TD scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

startPrbCC is the lowest PRB allocated for the common channel and nbPrbCC is the number of the PRBs allocated for the common channel (see 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

deltaCC is the number of PRBs between the highest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the highest PRB allocated for the common channel.

dlBandwidth is the carrier bandwidth size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The PDSCH subarea size is initialized with the size of the carrier bandwidth: rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  = dlBandwidth andedicated BWP(p) with their respectve bandwidth size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

deltaCC = dlBandwidth -  (startPrbCC + nbPrbCC)

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True,

if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0, deltaCC = deltaCC - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDl >0, for all the slots, NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL is withdrawn to the size of the PDSCH sub area.

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area which contains the common channel and the PRBs below with the position of the lowest PRB of the common channel in this way:

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - deltaCC

For the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area: thei size is updated with the position of the highest PRB the common channel in this way:

if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]   = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - deltaCC

gNB-DU shall initialize the startPrb of this PDSCH sub area and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area in this way:

rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

if p >1, rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl of each BWP(p).

gNB-DU shall udate the size and the startPrb of  the PDSCH sub area which is above the common channel and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area with the position of the highest PRB of the CORESET #0 in this way:

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]   = deltaCC

if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]   = deltaCC

rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] = startPrbCC + nbPrbCC

if p >1, rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = startPrbCC + nbPrbCC

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated areas within a communication system. The diagram uses colored rectangular blocks to represent these BWPs, arranged horizontally and vertically.
On the left side, several colored blocks are stacked and partially overlap. From top to bottom, these are:
- A thin cyan block labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow block labeled "initial BWP".
- A tall orange block labeled "dedicated BWP 1".
- A tall green block labeled "BWP-2".
- A light blue block labeled "PS BWP-3".
To the right of these blocks, there are labels and arrows indicating different regions and parameters:
- At the top, a red bar spans across the top of the diagram, with a dashed orange line below it and the text "PRBs blanking" to its right.
- Below the red bar, an arrow points downwards, labeled "deltaCC".
- Further down, there's a purple rectangular block extending from the "PS BWP-3" block.
- To the right of the purple block and below "deltaCC", the text "PDSCH sub area 2" is present.
- Below "PDSCH sub area 2", there's a dashed orange line and an arrow pointing downwards, labeled "common channel: nbCC".
- Below the "common channel: nbCC" label, the text "startPrbCC" is positioned.
- A long vertical dark blue line extends from near the top of the diagram down to the bottom.
- Along this vertical line, the text "PDSCH sub area 1" is placed roughly in the middle.
- At the bottom, another red bar spans across the bottom of the diagram, with a dashed orange line above it and the text "PRBs blanking" to its right.
The diagram visually represents the allocation and relationships between different bandwidth parts and sub-areas, likely in a cellular communication context like 5G NR. The colors differentiate the various BWPs, and the labels and arrows define their extents and functional significance.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13193.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `11176043`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Update of the size of the PDSCH sub areas when a number of PRBs for a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is requested to be reserved for the first time

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12816327`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12816327:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
The check described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23607\_replaced([11176609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) must be successful in order to schedule a DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant in CSS mode for the first time in a slot. Otherwise no PRB cannot be reserved for this DCI 1\_0 and the scheduling of the DCI 1\_0 is postponed to the next slot.

Note: Since FD resources (including CCE and PUCCH pre bookings too) for any DCI 1\_0 allocations are done at the end of the TD scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23663\_replaced([11177504](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the check described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23607\_replaced([11176609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is expected to be successful and gNB-DU shall update the existing PDSCH sub area and create a new one (if enough PRBs, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24041\_replaced([11175871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) due to DCI 1\_0 scheduling in the slot at this step.

When NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\*, in TDD FR1, the starting PRB of a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode, if it is the first DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode scheduled in the CSI-IM slot, shall be modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY in order to keep the PDSCH sub areas (startPrb and size) multiples of PRB\_GRANULARITY (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then if it is not the case,  gNB-DU shall increase the value of starting RB of the DCI 1\_0 in this way (and then the start PRB is no more the start Prb of the CORESET 0 or of the CORESET#X (with X>0).

1) When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0),

gNB-DU shall update the existing PDSCH sub area with:

* the start Prb equal to the start Prb of the CORESET #0 or the first PRB modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY above the startPrb of the CORESET #0 if NIDD parameters NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]*****\* in the CSI-IM slot* \*when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher (or equal) than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) otherwise the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)**  or the first PRB modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY abovethe startPrb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)  if NIDD parameters NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\* in the CSI-IM slot
* and the size from the size of the existing PDSCH sub area and the highest start Prb between the start Prb of the CORESET 0 and the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)

If startPrbCoreset0 >= startPrbCoresetCom,

{

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\* and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*(CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)***\***\*

*if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] -*(CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*\*\*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY*\*\*

else:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*startPrbCoreset0**\***\*

*if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] -*startPrbCoreset0\*\*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* startPrbCoreset0\*\*

}

else

{

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\* and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*(CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)***\***\*

*if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] -*(CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)*\*\*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY*\*\*

else:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*startPrbCoresetCom**\***\*

*if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] -*startPrbCoresetCom\*\*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* startPrbCoresetCom\*\*

}

where startPrbCoresetCom and startPrbCoreset0 are defined in this way

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

* startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: the PRB blanking at low edge, the specific bandwidth at low edge have already been handled in the existing PDSCH sub area.

gNB-DU shall create a new additional PDSCH sub area below this new DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode which contains only CORESET#X (with X>0) PRBs when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher (or equal) than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) otherwise only CORESET 0 PRBs and PRBs for dedicated BWP(s) and shall update the size and the startPrb of this additional PDSCH sub area with the position of the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area updated which contains the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]**= deltaCS0*\*\*

*if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]**= deltaCS0*\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] =* rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] – deltaCS0\*\*

where deltaCS0 is the number of PRBs between the highest start Prb between the start Prb of the CORESET 0 and the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) and the lowest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot.

deltaCS0 = *rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]*

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,

deltaCS0 = *rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL*

If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),

deltaCS0 = *rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] - NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl*

Otherwise

{

Note:

{ gNB shall follow this part too when a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and:

-when there is no LTE CAT-M PRBs area in the slot (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true or false)

* or when LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true) and at least one LTE CAT-M PRBs area is present in the slot, if gNB-DU schedules a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (type3 transient phase) in the PDSCH sub area between the lowest PRB available at the bottom of the carrier bandwidth and the PRB below the lowest blanked PRB of the lower CAT-M PRBs area (because this PDSCH sub area contains the largest consecutive PRBs area within the CORESET 0 as required in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16468\_replaced([11181355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **}**

If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is TRUE, gNB-DU shall create a new PDSCH sub area in the same way than 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23460\_replaced([11175983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and shall update the size for the existing PDSCH sub area when PRBs are reserved successfully for the first DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant in CSS mode if no common channel, no rach msg2 and no rach msg4 are in the TD scheduler list for the slot.

The existing PDSCH sub area contains the CORESET #0 and starts with the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 in low2high direction if mono BWP or multi dedicated BWP bottom aligned. The new PDSCH sub area is below the CORESET #0 if mono BWP or multi dedicated BWP bottom aligned.

gNB-DU shall handle the PRBs blanking at Low Edge or the specific bandwidth **and shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub areas and the dedicated BWP(p) if multi dedicated BWP bottom aligned**in the same way than in the case specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23460\_replaced([11175983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

}

[image-5][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different sub-areas within LTE CAT-M PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks). The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent these PRBs and labels various sections.
On the left side, there are several vertical bars of different colors: blue, yellow, orange, and a small green rectangle. These bars are labeled with text such as "CORE 0", "Initial BWP", and "Dedicated BW".
There are three horizontal red rectangles placed at different vertical positions, overlaying the colored bars. These red rectangles appear to demarcate specific regions.
Lines connect these red rectangles and the colored bars to text labels on the right side of the image.
The text labels and their corresponding connections are:
- "No PDSCH sub area" and "Not enough PRBS" are connected to the uppermost red rectangle.
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is connected to the middle red rectangle.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is connected to the lowermost red rectangle.
- "PDSCH sub area 3" is connected to a region below the lowermost red rectangle, specifically to the green rectangle and the orange bar.
- "DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant" is also associated with "PDSCH sub area 3".
- "3 LTE CAT-M PRBs areas" is a general label connected to the three PDSCH sub-areas.
The diagram visually represents how the total PRBs are divided into different sub-areas, some of which are designated for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) and others that might not have enough PRBs or PDSCH sub-areas. The "CORE 0", "Initial BWP", and "Dedicated BW" labels likely refer to different configurations or bandwidth parts within the LTE system.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13195.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

If all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are not mapped with the CORESET #0 (Note: for instance in FDD DSS NSA mode), when PRBs are reserved for the first DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant in CSS mode if no rach msg2 is in the TD scheduler list for the slot, gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the existing PDSCH sub area and shall create a new PDSCH sub area in the same way than 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23460\_replaced([11175983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) except that the startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET COMMON and DeltaCS0 is the number of PRBs between the lowest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the lowest PRB of the CORESET X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `11176070`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Initialization of the PDSCH sub areas in SSB slot (without CSI-RS for beam refinement)

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11176074`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
mono BWP or dedicated BWP bottom aligned

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176080`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176080:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In a SSB slot,

* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area **[CB008927-D] and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned** [End CB008927-D] above the SSB (see 5G\_UP\_10892\_replaced([11350748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU-_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the position of the startPrb of the SSB **[CB008927-B], the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth,** [End CB008927-B] in this way:***\****\*

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] -*startPrbSSB - nbPrbsSSB\*\*\*\*

**[CB008927-D] *if p > 1,rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**-*startPrbSSB - nbPrbsSSB* \*[End CB008927-D]******\****

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* startPrbSSB + nbPrbsSSB\*\*

**[CB008927-B] Note**: If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True (PRB blanking activated) or if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated), gNB-DU shall have first initiailized the size of the existing PDSCH sub area as specified (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  **[End CB008927-B]**\*\*\*\*

* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (below the SSB)  **[CB008927-D] and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned** [End CB008927-D]  with the position of the startPrb of the SSB **[CB008927-B], the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth,** [End CB008927-B] in this way:***\****\*

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]**= deltaSSB*\*\*

**[CB008927-D] *if p > 1,rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]\*\*= deltaSSB*** [End CB008927-D]***\****\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] =* startPrbSSB – deltaSSB\*\*

where startPrbSSB and deltaSSB are defined in this way:

startPrbSSB is the lowest PRB of the SS/PBCH block (see 5G\_UP\_10925\_replaced([11350670](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). DeltaSSB is the number of PRBs between the lowest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the lowest PRB of the SSB block.

deltaSSB = startPrbSSB

nbPrbsSSB is the number of blanked PRBs by the SSB (see 5G\_UP\_10925\_replaced([11350670](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

**[CB008927-B]**

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,

deltaSSB = startPrbSSB - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),

deltaSSB = startPrbSSB - NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl

**[End CB008927-B]**

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a concept related to cellular network resource allocation, likely within the context of 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts several rectangular blocks of varying colors and heights, representing different resource elements or configurations.
On the left side, there are three vertical bars stacked from bottom to top:
1. A red bar at the very bottom.
2. A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP" (Bandwidth Part).
3. A taller orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP" and also containing the text "dedicated BWP" rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise.
4. A thin blue rectangle on the left edge of the yellow bar, labeled "CORESET#0".
To the right of these bars, there is a light blue rectangular block.
Several labels and arrows indicate relationships and regions:
\* An arrow pointing downwards from the top of the diagram to the top of the light blue block is labeled "PDSCH sub area 1".
\* An arrow pointing downwards from the top of the light blue block to the bottom of the light blue block is labeled "SSB nbPrbsSSB PRBS".
\* A dashed orange line below the "SSB nbPrbsSSB PRBS" label is associated with the text "startPrbSSB".
\* An arrow pointing downwards from the bottom of the light blue block to a dashed orange line further down is labeled "deltaSSB".
\* A dashed orange line below the "deltaSSB" label is associated with the text "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* A dashed orange line at the very bottom of the diagram is associated with the text "PRBs blanking".
The diagram visually represents how different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and control resource sets (CORESETs) might be configured, and how these relate to different sub-areas for Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) transmission, potentially involving Synchronization Signal Block (SSB) resources and blanking periods.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13198.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**[CB008927-I] In SSB slot with TRS symbols, if FR1 TDD FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), for each PDSCH area, gNB-DU shall update the startPrb and the size of the PDSCH sub area as a multiple of a PRB granularity (see PRB\_GRANULARITY definition in 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which is equal to:**

if PRB\_GRANULARITY > 1,

{

old\_startPrb = *rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]*

old\_size = *rmPdschSubAreaSize[n]*

new\_startPrb = CEIL(*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] / PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY*

*rmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = FLOOR( (old\_size – (new\_startPrb - old\_startPrb))* / PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = new\_startPrb*

}

**Note: there is no DCI 1\_0 scheduled in SSB slots (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

**[End CB008927-I]**

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11176082`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
dedicated BWP top aligned

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176090`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176090:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
For SSB slot,

startPrbSSB is the lowest PRB of the SS/PBCH block (see 5G\_UP\_10925\_replaced([11350670](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). DeltaSSB is the number of PRBs between the highest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the highest PRB of the SSB block.

nbPrbsSSB is the number of blanked PRBs by the SSB (see 5G\_UP\_10925\_replaced([11350670](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

dlBandwidth is the carrier bandwidth size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall initialize the size of the PDSCH sub area with the size of the carrier bandwidth: rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  = dlBandwidth andedicated BWP(p) with their respectve bandwidth size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

deltaSSB = dlBandwidth - (startPrbSSB + nbPrbsSSB)

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True,

if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0, deltaSSB = deltaSSB - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDl >0, for all the slots, gNB-DU shall reduce NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL PRBs  from the size of the PDSCH sub area.

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

gNB-DU shall initialize the size of the PDSCH sub area which is below the SSB with the position of the lowest PRB of the common channel in this way:

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - deltaSSB

gNB-DU shall update the size of the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area with the position of the highest PRB the common channel in this way:

if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]   = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - deltaSSB

gNB-DU shall initialize the startPrb of this PDSCH sub area and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area in this way:

rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

if p >1, rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl of each BWP(p).

gNB-DU shall initialize the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area which is above the SSB and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area with the position of the highest PRB of the CORESET #0 in this way:

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]   = deltaSSB

if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]   = deltaSSB

rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] = startPrbSSB + nbPrbsSSB

if p >1, rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = startPrbSSB + nbPrbsSSB

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-7**  (ID: `11176094`)

Initialization of the PDSCH sub areas in SSB slot with CSI-RS for beam refinement

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11176106`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176106:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In FR1 TDD, if beam refinement / management is activated (NRCELL.beamSet.nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 = true), [PR769456] and the PRB pooling is not activated (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = false) [End PR769456], gNB-DU shall create one PDSCH sub area below the lowest PRB of the SSB if CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement are above the SSB (NRCELL.csirsBeamMgmt.csirsBmMgmtSubband = 2 (aboveSSPBCHBlock)) or above the highest PRB of the SSB if CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement are above the SSB (NRCELL.csirsBeamMgmt.csirsBmMgmtSubband = 1 (belowSSPBCHBlock)) (see 5G\_UP\_13027\_replaced([11350762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU_fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their relation to CSI-RS, SSB, and PRB blanking. The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent these BWPs.
From left to right, the bars are:
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP-1".
- A green bar labeled "BWP-2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
- A purple bar.
- A teal bar.
There are several annotations and labels associated with these bars:
- "CSI-RS for Beam refinement" is indicated by a double-headed arrow spanning from the top of the teal bar down to the top of the purple bar.
- "SSB" is indicated by a double-headed arrow spanning the height of the purple bar.
- Below the bars, there is a red area labeled "PRB blanking".
- To the right of the diagram, there are text descriptions:
- "PDSCH sub area 1"
- "Size of BWP(1) in PDSCH sub area 1: rrmPdschSubAreaSize[1]"
- "size of BWP(2) in PDSCH sub area 1:: rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[1][2]"
- "size of BWP(3) in PDSCH sub area 1:: rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[1][3]"
- "nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL"
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs might be configured and their associated functionalities or measurements. The dashed orange lines indicate the boundaries and relationships between these elements.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13202.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR769456] If beam refinement / management is activated (NRCELL.beamSet.nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 = true) and the PRB pooling is activated (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = true), gNB-DU shall create 2 PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_13027\_replaced([11350762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU_fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

* if CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement are above the SSB (NRCELL.csirsBeamMgmt.csirsBmMgmtSubband = 2 (aboveSSPBCHBlock)), the first PDSCH sub area is below the lowest PRB of the SSB and the another one is above the highest PRB of the CSI-RS for beam refinement.
* if CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement are below the SSB (NRCELL.csirsBeamMgmt.csirsBmMgmtSubband = 1 (belowSSPBCHBlock)), the first PDSCH sub area is above the highest PRB of the SSB and the other one is below the lowest PRB of the CSI-RS for beam refinement.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their relation to other elements like PDSCH sub-areas, CSI-RS, and SSB. The diagram uses colored bars of varying heights on the left to represent these BWPs.
On the left side, there are four vertical bars:
- The leftmost bar is yellow and labeled "initial BWP".
- Next to it is an orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP-1".
- To the right of that is a green bar labeled "BWP-2".
- The rightmost bar is light blue and labeled "PS BWP-3".
On the right side, there are text labels and arrows indicating different regions and their properties:
- At the top, there's a red horizontal bar. Below it, text reads "PRBs blanking: nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL".
- Below this, there's a region labeled "PDSCH sub area 2". An arrow points from this region to the text "Size of BWP(1) in PDSCH sub area 1: rrmPdschSubAreaSize[2]".
- Further down, there's a region labeled "CSI-RS for Beam refinement". An arrow points to this region from the text "size of BWP(2) in PDSCH sub area 2:: rrmSizeBwplnPdschSubArea[2][2]".
- Below the CSI-RS region, there's a region labeled "SSB".
- At the bottom, there's a region labeled "PDSCH sub area 1".
There are also dashed orange lines that act as horizontal guides, aligning different elements across the diagram. Two small purple and teal colored rectangles are positioned to the right of the "PS BWP-3" bar, aligned with some of the dashed lines.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13202.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End PR769456]

The variables rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] , rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n], rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] (if p > 1) of the PDSCH sub area(s) are updated in a similar way (including PRBs blanking, specific bandwidth and PRB granularity if there are TRS symbols and FD beamforming) as it is done in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23462\_replaced([11176070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in a SSB slot .

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12719427`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12719427:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In a slot with PRB block muted due to interference mitigation(IM)(refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)),

with definition:

startPrbIM is the lowest PRB of the PRB block muted due to SSB interference mitigation.

deltaPrbIM is the number of PRBs between the highest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the highest PRB of the muted PRB block.

nbPrbsIM is the number of PRB block muted by interference mitigation(IM).

dlBandwidth is the carrier bandwidth size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall initialize the size of the PDSCH sub area with the size of the carrier bandwidth: *rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= dlBandwidth.*\*\*

* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned**above the PRB block muted due to interference mitigation with the position of the startPrb of the muted PRB block, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:**

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] -*startPrbIM - nbPrbsIM\*\*\*\*

*if p > 1,rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**= rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]**-*startPrbIM - nbPrbsIM**\***\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* startPrbIM + nbPrbsIM\*\*

Note**: If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True (PRB blanking activated) or if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated), gNB-DU shall have first initialized the size of the existing PDSCH sub area as specified (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (below the PRBs muted due to interference mitigation)  and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area if multi BWP bottom aligned with the position of the startPrb of the PRBs muting, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:

deltaPrbIM = startPrbIM

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,

deltaPrbIM = startPrbIM - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1]  = NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL*

If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true,

deltaPrbIM = startPrbIM - NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]**= deltaPrbIM*\*\*

*if p > 1,rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]**= deltaPrbIM*\*\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated sub-areas for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) transmission, along with a concept of PRB (Physical Resource Block) muting due to interference management (IM).
The diagram shows several vertical bars representing different BWPs:
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0" on the left.
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP" to the right of the blue bar.
- A tall orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP" to the right of the yellow bar.
To the right of these bars, there are horizontal dashed lines indicating different regions and their associated labels:
- The top region is labeled "PDSCH sub area 1". An arrow spans from the top of the "dedicated BWP" bar down to a dashed line, indicating the extent of this sub-area.
- Below "PDSCH sub area 1", there is a smaller region indicated by an arrow and labeled "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)". This region is positioned between two dashed lines.
- Further down, there is a dashed line labeled "startPrbIM".
- Below "startPrbIM", there is another region labeled "PDSCH sub area 2" with an arrow indicating its extent. This arrow also points to a label "deltaPrbIM".
- The bottom region is labeled "PRB blanking" and is indicated by a red bar at the very bottom of the diagram.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs might be allocated and how certain PRBs within the PDSCH transmission might be muted due to interference management, with a specific sub-area defined for this muting and a starting point for the PRB muting indicated.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07301607.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-8.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12719429`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12719429:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
For a slot with PRB block muted due to SSB interference mitigation(IM)(refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)),

with definition:

startPrbIM is the lowest PRB of the PRB block muted due to SSB interference mitigation.

deltaPrbIM is the number of PRBs between the highest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the highest PRB of the muted PRB block.

nbPrbsIM is the number of PRB block muted by interference mitigation(IM).

dlBandwidth is the carrier bandwidth size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

deltaPrbIM = dlBandwidth - (startPrbIM + nbPrbsIM)

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True,

If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDl >0, for all the slots, gNB-DU shall reduce NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL PRBs  from the size of the PDSCH sub area.

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**- NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL*\*\*\*\*

If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL > 0, for all the slots,  gNB-DU shall reduce NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL from the size of the PDSCH sub area for all dedicated BWP(p).

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL

deltaPrbIM = deltaPrbIM - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL

if p >1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]   = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL

gNB-DU shall initialize the size of the PDSCH sub area which is below the muted PRB block for IM with the position of the lowest PRB of muted PRB block in this way:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**- deltaPrbIM - nbPrbsIM*\*\*\*\*

gNB-DU shall update the size of the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area with the position of the highest PRB the common channel in this way:

if p > 1, *rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]*= rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - deltaPrbIM - nbPrbsIM\*\*

gNB-DU shall initialize the startPrb of this PDSCH sub area and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area in this way:

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL\*\*

if p >1, *rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl of each BWP(p).*

gNB-DU shall initialize the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area which is above the PRB block muted for IM and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area with the position of the highest PRB of the CORESET #0 in this way:

*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]*= deltaPrbIM\*\*

if p > 1, *rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]*= deltaPrbIM\*\*

*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] = startPrbIM + nbPrbsIM*

if p >1, *rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = startPrbIM + nbPrbsIM*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their relationship to PRB (Physical Resource Block) blanking and PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) sub-areas. The diagram is oriented horizontally, with different colored vertical bars representing the BWPs.
From left to right, the colored bars are:
- A narrow blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP 1".
- A green bar labeled "BWP 2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP 3".
Above and to the right of these bars, there are labels and arrows indicating different regions and parameters:
- "PRB blanking" is indicated by dashed orange lines at the top and bottom of the diagram.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a vertical arrow pointing downwards, with "deltaPrbIM" labeled between two horizontal dashed orange lines.
- Below "deltaPrbIM", there is a label "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)" with a vertical arrow pointing downwards.
- "startPrbIM" is labeled to the right of the bottom dashed orange line of the "PDSCH sub area 2" region.
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a large vertical arrow spanning from the "startPrbIM" level down to the bottom "PRB blanking" line.
The overall diagram visually represents how different BWPs are allocated and how PRB blanking and specific PRB configurations (like those muted due to IM) define different sub-areas within the PDSCH transmission.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07301615.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
