**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-8.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12726611`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12726611:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In a slot with PRB block muted due to interference mitigation(IM)(refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), the start PRB and the number of PRBs of a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) are known and allocated by TD scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH subarea and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH subarea if multi BWPs are bottom aligned according to following scenarios:
\* Definitions:
\* startPrbIM is the lowest PRB of the PRB block muted due to interference mitigation.
\* deltaPrbIM is the number of PRBs between the lowest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the lowest PRB of the PRB block muted due to SSB interference mitigation.
\* nbPrbsIM is the number of PRBs muted due to IM.
\* startPrbCC is the lowest PRB allocated for the common channel (see 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
\* deltaCC is the number of PRBs between the lowest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the lowest PRB allocated for the common channel.
\* nbPrbsCC is the number of PRBs reserved for common channel.
\* rrmDlBwpSize[p] is the bwp size of each bwp[p] for downlink if p>1.
\* dlbandwidth is cell bandwidth size for downlink.
1. The common channel overlaps with PRB block muted due to IM, and the startPrb of common channel(startPrbCC) is lower than startPrb of PRB block muted due to IM(startPrbIM). Refer to figure 1.
\* NB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area(above the PRBs muted due to IM) in this way:
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] -  startPrbIM - nbPrbsIM
if p > 1,rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  -  startPrbIM - nbPrbsIM
rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = startPrbIM + nbPrbsIM
Note**: If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True (PRB blanking activated) or if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated), gNB-DU shall have first initialized the size of the existing PDSCH sub area as specified (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**
\* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (below the common channel) at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]  =  deltaCC
if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpPdschSubArea[n+1][p]  = deltaCC
rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] = startPrbCC – deltaCC
deltaCC = startPrbCC
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,
deltaCC = startPrbCC - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL
If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),  
 deltaCC = startPrbCC - NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different resource allocation concepts, likely within a cellular communication system. It uses colored blocks and text labels to represent various components and their relationships.
The diagram features several vertical colored bars on the left side:
- A cyan bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these bars, horizontal colored blocks represent different sub-areas or channels:
- A light blue horizontal block is positioned above a pink horizontal block.
- The pink block is labeled "Common Channel".
- The light blue block is positioned to the right of the pink block and extends further to the right.
Several text labels and arrows indicate specific regions and parameters:
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a large vertical arrow spanning a significant portion of the upper right area.
- Below "PDSCH sub area 1", there's a label "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)" with a smaller vertical arrow pointing to a region between two dashed orange lines.
- A dashed orange line is labeled "startPrbIM".
- Another dashed orange line below "startPrbIM" is labeled "startPrbCC".
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by another vertical arrow in the lower right area.
- Next to the "PDSCH sub area 2" arrow, there is a label "deltaCC".
- At the very bottom, a red horizontal bar is shown, and below it, a dashed orange line is labeled "PRB blanking".
The overall layout suggests a hierarchical or segmented view of resource blocks (PRBs) and their allocation for different purposes like control signaling (CORESET#0), initial bandwidth parts (initial BWP), dedicated bandwidth parts (dedicated BWP), common channels, and data channels (PDSCH sub areas). The labels also indicate potential interference management (IM) and carrier aggregation (deltaCC) concepts.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08041854.png[/image\_path][/image-0]figure 1
2. The common channel overlaps with PRB block muted due to IM, and the startPrb of common channel(startPrbCC) is higher than startPrb of PRB block muted due to IM(startPrbIM). Refer to figure 2.
\* NB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area(above the common channel) in this way:
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] - startPrbCC - nbPrbsCC
if p > 1,rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - startPrbCC - nbPrbsCC
rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = startPrbCC + nbPrbsCC
Note**: If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True (PRB blanking activated) or if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated), gNB-DU shall have first initialized the size of the existing PDSCH sub area as specified (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**
\* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (below the PRBs muted due to IM) at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]  =  deltaPrbIM
if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpPdschSubArea[n+1][p]  = deltaPrbIM
rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] = startPrbIM – deltaPrbIM
deltaPrbIM = startPrbIM
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and if NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0,
deltaPrbIM = startPrbIM - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL
If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),  
 deltaPrbIM = startPrbIM - NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl
[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and channels within a communication system. The diagram uses colored bars to represent these components and arrows with labels to indicate their relationships and sizes.
On the left side, there are three vertical bars:
1. A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
2. A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
3. An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these bars, there are two rectangular blocks:
1. A pink block labeled "Common Channel".
2. A light blue block positioned below and slightly to the right of the pink block.
Several horizontal dashed lines are present, indicating different levels or boundaries. Arrows connect these lines and are accompanied by text labels:
\* A large black arrow spans from the top of the diagram down to a dashed line, with the label "PDSCH sub area 1".
\* Below "PDSCH sub area 1", there is a smaller black arrow spanning between two dashed lines, labeled "nbPrbsCC". To the right of this arrow, the text "startPrbCC" is aligned with the lower dashed line.
\* Below the "nbPrbsCC" section, there is another dashed line. Between this dashed line and the one below it, there is text "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)".
\* Below the "PRB muted due to IM" section, there is another dashed line. An arrow spans between this dashed line and the one below it, labeled "PDSCH sub area 2". To the right of this arrow, the text "startPrbIM" is aligned with the upper dashed line of this section.
\* Further down, there is a dashed line with the label "deltaPrbIM" aligned with an arrow that spans between this dashed line and the one below it.
\* At the bottom of the diagram, a red horizontal bar is present. Below this red bar, there is a dashed line with the label "PRB blanking".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08041932.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
figure 2
3. The common channel  and Muted PRBs don't overlap and are separated from each other. Refer to figure 3.
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = dlbandwidth
deltaCC = startPrbCC
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] - startPrbIM - nbPrbsIM
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1] = startPrbIM - startPrbCC
~~if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = rrmDlBwpSize[p]~~
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0
deltaCC = deltaCC - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2] = deltaCC
if p>1:
rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+2][p] = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2]
if  rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] > startPrbCC and  rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]< startPrbIM
rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p] = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - startPrbCC
else if rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] > startPrbIM
rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p] = rmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1](or: startPrbIM-startPrbCC)
if rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] > startPrbIM + nbPrbsIM
rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - startPrbIM - nbPrbsIM
[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and sub-areas within a communication system, likely related to 5G NR. The diagram uses colored bars to represent BWPs and horizontal dashed lines to delineate sub-areas and specific points.
On the left side, there are three vertical colored bars stacked from left to right:
1. A cyan bar labeled "CORESET#0".
2. A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
3. An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these bars, there are several horizontal dashed lines and text labels indicating different regions and parameters:
\* The topmost dashed line is associated with "PDSCH sub area 1".
\* Below this, a blue bar is shown, representing a portion of the PDSCH.
\* A double-headed arrow spans from the top of the blue bar to a dashed line below it, with the text "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)" next to it.
\* The dashed line below the blue bar is labeled "startPrbIM".
\* Below "startPrbIM", there is another double-headed arrow indicating "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* A pink bar labeled "Common Channel" is positioned below the blue bar and to the left of the "PDSCH sub area 2" label.
\* The dashed line below "PDSCH sub area 2" is labeled "startPrbCC".
\* Below "startPrbCC", there is a double-headed arrow indicating "PDSCH sub area 3" and the text "deltaCC" is placed within this arrow's span.
\* The lowest dashed line is labeled "PRB blanking".
\* A red bar is shown at the very bottom, extending across the width of the diagram, likely representing the "PRB blanking" region.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and channels are allocated and how certain resource blocks (PRBs) might be muted or blanked within specific sub-areas.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08042014.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Refer to [12719426: mono BWP or dedicated BWP bottom aligned](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EHEPA2wlEfCRGai1q1VTeQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for this scenario.
[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different resource allocation concepts in a communication system, likely related to cellular networks. It uses colored blocks and text labels to represent various components and their relationships.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Colored Blocks (representing resource allocations):**
\* **Blue Block (leftmost):** Labeled "CORESET#0". This likely represents a Control Resource Set (CORESET) used for control signaling.
\* **Yellow Block:** Labeled "initial BWP". This signifies an initial Bandwidth Part (BWP), which is a subset of the total bandwidth.
\* **Orange Block (tallest):** Labeled "dedicated BWP". This indicates a dedicated Bandwidth Part, possibly for user data.
\* **Pink Block (within a light blue rectangle):** Labeled "Common Channel". This represents a channel allocated for common use, such as system information or initial access.
\* **Light Blue Rectangle (enclosing the pink block):** This rectangle seems to define the extent of the "Common Channel" allocation.
\* **Red Bar (bottom):** Labeled "PRB blanking". This indicates a region where Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) are intentionally not used or are blanked.
**Text Labels and Annotations:**
\* **"PDSCH sub area 1"**: This label, with a large vertical arrow pointing from the top of the diagram down to the level of the "Common Channel", indicates a region designated for Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) data transmission.
\* **"PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)"**: This text, positioned to the right of the "Common Channel" block, explains that a certain number of PRBs ("nbPrbsIM PRBs") are muted due to Intermodulation (IM) interference.
\* **"startPrbCC"**: This label, with a dashed line pointing to the left edge of the "Common Channel" block, likely indicates the starting PRB index for the Common Channel.
\* **"startPrbIM"**: This label, with a dashed line pointing to the right of the "PRB muted due to IM" text, likely indicates the starting PRB index for the IM-affected region.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 2"**: Similar to "PDSCH sub area 1", this label, with a vertical arrow pointing from the level of the "Common Channel" down to the "PRB blanking" level, denotes another region for PDSCH data.
\* **"deltaPrbIM"**: This label, with a vertical arrow between the "startPrbCC" and "deltaPrbIM" lines, likely represents the number of PRBs between the start of the Common Channel and the start of the IM-affected region.
\* **"PRB blanking"**: This label, positioned at the bottom of the diagram, corresponds to the red bar and indicates the region of blanked PRBs.
The diagram visually represents how different types of resources (control, common channels, dedicated data) and interference management strategies (PRB muting, blanking) are organized within a given spectrum. The vertical arrows and dashed lines indicate relative positions and extents of these allocations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08042020.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
figure 4
5. The PRBs muted due to IM overlaps with common channel and PRBs muted due to IM is completed contained within common channel  refer to figure 5.
Refer to [11176017: mono BWP or dedicated BWP bottom aligned](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for this scenario.
[image-4][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to cellular networks. It uses colored bars and text labels to represent different components and their relationships.
Here's a detailed breakdown:
**Visual Elements:**
\* **Colored Bars:**
\* A tall, orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP" is the widest and tallest element on the left.
\* A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP" is positioned to the left of the orange bar and is shorter.
\* A cyan bar labeled "CORESET#0" is to the left of the yellow bar and is the shortest of these three.
\* A pink bar labeled "Common Channel" is located to the right of the orange bar.
\* A light blue bar is to the right of the pink bar.
\* A thick red bar is at the very bottom, spanning across the width of the initial bars.
\* **Text Labels:**
\* "CORESET#0" (on the cyan bar)
\* "initial BWP" (on the yellow bar)
\* "dedicated BWP" (on the orange bar)
\* "Common Channel" (on the pink bar)
\* "PDSCH sub area 1" (above and to the right of the Common Channel)
\* "PRB muted due to IM" (to the right of the Common Channel and light blue bar)
\* "(nbPrbsIM PRBs)" (below "PRB muted due to IM")
\* "startPrbIM" (below "(nbPrbsIM PRBs)")
\* "PDSCH sub area 2" (below "PDSCH sub area 1")
\* "deltaPrbIM" (to the right of "PDSCH sub area 2")
\* "startPrbCC" (to the right of the light blue bar)
\* "PRB blanking" (at the bottom right, aligned with the red bar)
\* **Arrows and Dashed Lines:**
\* Two vertical double-headed arrows indicate the extent of "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* Horizontal dashed orange lines delineate the boundaries of these sub-areas and other regions.
\* A vertical double-headed arrow is placed between the "PRB muted due to IM" text and the "PDSCH sub area 2" text, indicating a specific range.
**Interpretation of the Diagram:**
The diagram appears to represent the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) in a communication system.
\* **CORESET#0, initial BWP, dedicated BWP:** These likely represent different configurations or bandwidth parts (BWPs) used for control or initial data transmission. The bars suggest a hierarchical or sequential usage, with CORESET#0 being the smallest, followed by initial BWP, and then the dedicated BWP being the largest.
\* **Common Channel:** This is a shared resource, possibly for control signaling or broadcast information.
\* **PDSCH sub area 1 and PDSCH sub area 2:** These denote different regions within the PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) where data can be transmitted. The diagram shows these sub-areas are separated.
\* **PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs) and startPrbIM:** This indicates a section of PRBs that are intentionally muted or unavailable due to Interference Management (IM). `nbPrbsIM` likely refers to the number of PRBs affected, and `startPrbIM` indicates the starting PRB index of this muted region.
\* **deltaPrbIM:** This likely represents the size or duration of the PRB muting.
\* **startPrbCC:** This might indicate the starting PRB for the Common Channel.
\* **PRB blanking:** This refers to a region where PRBs are completely blank or unused. The red bar at the bottom visually represents this blanking.
In essence, the diagram illustrates how different parts of the radio spectrum (PRBs) are allocated for various functions, including control signaling (CORESET#0, Common Channel), initial data transmission (initial BWP), dedicated data transmission (dedicated BWP), and how certain PRBs might be intentionally muted or left blank for specific reasons like interference management.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08042020.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
figure 5

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-8.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12726610`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
**dedicated BWP top aligned**

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-3.0-8.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12727500`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12727500:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In a slot with PRB block muted due to interference mitigation(IM)(refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), the start PRB and the number of PRBs of a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) are known and allocated by TD scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH subarea and all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH subarea if multi BWPs are top aligned according to following scenarios:
\* Definitions:
\* startPrbIM is the lowest PRB of the PRB block muted due to interference mitigation.
\* deltaPrbIM is the number of PRBs between the highest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the highest PRB of the PRB block muted due to SSB interference mitigation.
\* nbPrbsIM is the number of PRBs muted due to IM.
\* startPrbCC is the lowest PRB allocated for the common channel (see 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
\* deltaCC is the number of PRBs between the highest PRB of the carrier bandwidth which can be allocated in the slot and the highest PRB allocated for the common channel.
\* nbPrbsCC is the number of PRBs reserved for common channel.
\* rrmDlBwpSize[p] is initialized as bwp size for downlink bwp[p] if p >1.
\* dlbandwidth is cell bandwidth size for downlink.
1. The common channel overlaps with PRB block muted due to IM, and the startPrb of common channel(startPrbCC) is lower than startPrb of PRB block muted due to IM(startPrbIM). Refer to figure 1.
\* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area (below the common channel) at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way
deltaPrbIM = dlBandwidth - (startPrbIM + nbPrbsIM)
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True,
If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDl >0, for all the slots, gNB-DU shall reduce NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL PRBs  from the size of the PDSCH sub area.
*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**- NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL****\*
If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL > 0, for all the slots,  gNB-DU shall reduce NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL from the size of the PDSCH sub area for all dedicated BWP(p).
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL
deltaPrbIM = deltaPrbIM - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL
if p >1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] - deltaPrbIM - nbPrbsIM - (startPrbIM - startPrbCC)
if p >1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - deltaPrbIM - nbPrbsIM - (startPrbIM - startPrbCC)
gNB-DU shall initialize the startPrb of this PDSCH sub area and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area in this way:*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] = \*NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL**
if p >1, *rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl of each BWP(p).*
\* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (above the PRBs muted due to IM) in this way:
*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]*= deltaPrbIM**if p > 1, *rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]*= deltaPrbIM*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] = startPrbIM + nbPrbsIM*if p >1,* rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = startPrbIM + nbPrbsIM*[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their relationship to PDSCH sub-areas and PRB blanking. The diagram is organized vertically, with different colored bars representing various BWPs.
From left to right, the bars are labeled:
- CORESET#0 (blue)
- initial BWP (yellow)
- dedicated BWP 1 (orange)
- BWP 2 (green)
- PS BWP 3 (light blue)
To the right of the PS BWP 3 bar, there is a pink bar labeled "Common Channel".
Above and below the BWPs, there are horizontal red bars labeled "PRB blanking".
Several annotations and arrows indicate relationships and measurements:
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a bracketed region above the "Common Channel" and "PS BWP 3" bars.
- "delta PrbIM" is shown as a vertical double-headed arrow within the "PDSCH sub area 2" region, indicating a measurement.
- "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)" is indicated by a vertical double-headed arrow pointing to a region below "PDSCH sub area 2".
- "startPrbIM" and "startPrbCC" are marked with horizontal dashed lines below the "PRB muted due to IM" annotation.
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a large vertical double-headed arrow spanning from below the "startPrbCC" line down to the lower "PRB blanking" region.
The overall diagram visually represents how different BWPs are allocated and how they relate to specific sub-areas for PDSCH transmission and periods of PRB blanking.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08051051.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
figure 1
2. The common channel overlaps with PRB block muted due to IM, and the startPrb of common channel(startPrbCC) is higher than startPrb of PRB block muted due to IM(startPrbIM). Refer to figure 2.
\* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area (below the PRBs muted due to IM) at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = dlBandwidth*deltaCC = dlBandwidth - (startPrbCC + nbPrbsCC)
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True,
If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDl >0, for all the slots, gNB-DU shall reduce NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL PRBs  from the size of the PDSCH sub area.*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**= rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**- NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL*****If NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL > 0, for all the slots,  gNB-DU shall reduce NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL from the size of the PDSCH sub area for all dedicated BWP(p).
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL
deltaCC = deltaCC - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL
if p >1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] - deltaCC - nbPrbsCC - (startPrbCC- startPrbIM)
if p >1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - deltaCC - nbPrbsCC - (startPrbCC- startPrbIM)
gNB-DU shall initialize the startPrb of this PDSCH sub area and the dedicated BWP(p) within this PDSCH sub area in this way:
*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] =* NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL**
if p >1, *rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl of each BWP(p).*
\* gNB-DU shall update the size and the startPrb of the additional PDSCH sub area (above the common channel) in this way:
*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]*= deltaCC**if p > 1, *rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]*= deltaCC**
*rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] = startPrbCC + nbPrbsCC*
if p >1, *rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = startPrbCC + nbPrbsCC*
[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated parameters, likely within a cellular communication system. The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent the BWPs and horizontal dashed lines with labels to indicate specific regions and values.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**BWPs (Vertical Bars):**
\* **CORESET#0:** Represented by a teal blue bar on the far left.
\* **initial BWP:** Represented by a yellow bar to the right of CORESET#0.
\* **dedicated BWP 1:** Represented by an orange bar to the right of the initial BWP.
\* **BWP 2:** Represented by a green bar to the right of dedicated BWP 1.
\* **PS BWP 3:** Represented by a light blue bar to the right of BWP 2.
\* **Common Channel:** Represented by a pink bar positioned above and slightly overlapping the PS BWP 3.
**Regions and Parameters (Horizontal Dashed Lines and Labels):**
\* **PRB blanking:** Two horizontal dashed lines at the very top and very bottom of the diagram, indicating regions where Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) are blanked.
\* **PDSCH sub area 2:** A region indicated by a dashed line above the Common Channel and PS BWP 3.
\* **deltaCC:** A vertical double-headed arrow between the "PRB blanking" line at the top and the "PDSCH sub area 2" line, labeled "deltaCC". This likely represents a difference in carrier components or similar.
\* **nbPrbsCC:** A vertical double-headed arrow between the "PDSCH sub area 2" line and the "startPrbCC" line, labeled "nbPrbsCC". This could indicate the number of PRBs in a specific configuration.
\* **PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs):** Text indicating a region where PRBs are muted due to Intermodulation, with a vertical double-headed arrow pointing to it and labeled "nbPrbsIM PRBs". This arrow originates from a dashed line below the "nbPrbsCC" arrow.
\* **startPrbCC:** A horizontal dashed line below the "nbPrbsCC" arrow, marking the start of a specific PRB configuration.
\* **startPrbIM:** A horizontal dashed line below the "PRB muted due to IM" region, marking the start of another PRB configuration.
\* **PDSCH sub area 1:** A large vertical double-headed arrow spanning from the "startPrbIM" line down to the "PRB blanking" line at the bottom, labeled "PDSCH sub area 1". This indicates a significant region related to PDSCH.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs might be allocated or configured within a larger spectrum, with specific regions defined by parameters like "deltaCC", "nbPrbsCC", and "startPrbCC/IM". The "PRB muted due to IM" suggests a scenario where certain PRBs are intentionally made inactive.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08051103.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
figure 2
3. The common channel  and Muted PRBs don't overlap and are separated from each other. Refer to figure 3.​​​​​​
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = dlbandwidth
if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = rrmDlBwpSize[p]
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL > 0
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL
if p > 1, rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] =  rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL
deltaCC = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] - startPrbCC - nbPrbsCC
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2] = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] - startPrbCC
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1] = startPrbCC - startPrbIM - nbPrbsIM
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = startPrbIM
if p>1:
rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+2][p] = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2]
if  rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] > deltaCC + nrPrbsCC and rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] < deltaCC + nrPrbsCC + rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]
rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p] = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - deltaCC - nrPrbsCC
else if rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] > deltaCC + nrPrbsCC + rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]
rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p] = rmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]
if rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] > rmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1] + rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2] + nbPrbsIM
rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] - rmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1] - rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2] - nbPrbsIM
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True and NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0
rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL
If NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true (specific bandwidth activated),  
*rmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] =  NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl*

---

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-4 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation step2: computation of remaining PRBs in PDSCH sub areas (ID: 11176110)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176113`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Useful variables for PDSCH sub areas:

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12642511`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Within each PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall provide any RAT1 PDSCH grant with a number of reserved PRBs when it is needed within its BWP (CORESET #0 is considered as a BWP), provided the sum of the number of reserved PRBs shall not exceed the size of the PDSCH sub area. The sum of the number of PRBs reserved for all the PDSCH sub areas shall not exceed the total amount usable DL PRB granted to the cell. All the constraints are listed in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Within each PDSCH sub area, each time a number of PRBs is reserved for a DCI 1\_1 or DCI 1\_0 RAT1 grant, gNB-DU shall withdraw this number from the remaining numbers of PRBs for each BWP or CORESET #0.

Number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area: *rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]. gNB-DU shall not compute  in this variable the number of PRBs which are reserved through DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in the CORESET #0 (or the CORESET X (with X>0) ). If multi BWP configured, only the PRBs reserved through DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the largest BWP are computed. gNB-DU shall initialize this variable with the size of the PDSCH sub area (*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]*=* rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] ). When the size of a PDSCH sub area is modified, gNB-DU shall update this variable.\*\*\*\*

Size of the CORESET #0 within the PDSCH sub area: *rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]. If the CORESET #0 belongs to the PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall initialize this variable to the CORESET #0 size (*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] = *SizeCoreset0). In the case of common channel scheduling in the slot, gNB-DU shall update this size of the PDSCH sub area which contains the common channel and the one below.*\*\*

**Note:** When a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) [CB007372-M] when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M] and when LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true),\*\*\*\*

* when there is one LTE CAT-M PRBs area in the slot, the size of the CORESET #0  (or X with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area size  (*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n])*can be lower than the size of the CORESET 0 due to overlapping of the LTE CAT-M PRBs area.\*\*
* when there are several  LTE CAT-M PRBs areas in the slot, the size of the CORESET #0 (or X with X>0)  within the PDSCH sub area size (*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]) is lower than the size of the CORESET 0 and between 2 LTE CAT-M PRBs areas, the size of the CORESET #0  within the PDSCH sub area size is equal or below the size of the PDSCH sub area.*

[CB007372-M]   
 Note2: When a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true the CORESET #0 is not impacted by LTE CAT-M if NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true.   
[End CB007372-M]

Number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 (or X with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area: *rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]. If the CORESET #0 (or X with X>0) belongs to the PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall initialize this variable to the CORESET #0 size within the PDSCH sub area size, otherwise set to 0. When the size of a PDSCH sub area containing the CORESET #0 (or X with X>0) is modified, gNB-DU shall update this variable.*

Number of remaining PRBs in the BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area: *rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]. gNB-DU shall compute in this variable only the number of PRBs allocated of a UE which belongs to this BWP(p). gNB-DU shall initialize this variable to the size of the BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area . When the size of a BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area is modified, gNB-DU shall update this variable.*

**Note: If NRCELL.ueConnectionEstablishmentMode is set to useInitialBwp, the size of the dedicated BWP(0) is the size of the InitialBWP.**

The assumption is that the CORESET #0/ SSB /initial BWP are in the lowest part of the carrier bandwidth  if mono BWP or dedicated BWP bottom aligned.

SizeCoreset0 is the Size of CORESET #0 (or X with X>0 when DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are mapped to this CORESET)

Refer to previous variables 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11176121`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Common channel scheduled in CORESET #0

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12642535`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12642535:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
When a common channel (SIB1, SI PAGING) is in the to-be-scheduled list at TD scheduler level and the start PRB and the number of PRBs of a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) are known and allocated by TD scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

* for the PDSCH sub area which contains the common channel (see 5G\_UP\_10904\_replaced([11350781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8APvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

- When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0),

gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the size of the CORESET 0 within this PDSCH sub area with the initial size of the CORESET #0, the position of the startPrb of the CORESET #0 and the CORESET#X (with X>0) and the position of the startPrb of the common channel, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] =* SizeCoreset0 - startPrbCC  + startPrbCoreset0 - startPrbCoresetCom\*\*

* otherwise,

{

gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the size of the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the initial size of the CORESET #0, the position of the startPrb of the CORESET #0 and the position of the startPrb of the common channel, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] =* SizeCoreset0 - (startPrbCC - startPrbCoreset0)\*\*

Note: When NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator), in TDD FR1 in a CSI-IM slot, the starting PRB startPrbCC of the a common channel shall be modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY in order to keep the PDSCH sub areas (startPrb and size) multiples of PRB\_GRANULARITY (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: When a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) [CB007372-M] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M], there is no start Prb randomization for the common channel which starts from the bottom of the CORESET 0.

- gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the size of the CORESET #0, the position of the startPrb of the CORESET #0 and the position of the startPrb of the common channel, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth,  and the number of the PRBs allocated to the common channel in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] =* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – nbPrbCC\*\*

*- there is no update for* the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area.\*\*

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] does not change.*

}

* For the additional PDSCH sub area below the common channel for mono BWP and dedicated BWP(p) bottom aligned,

- When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0), gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the size of the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the position of the startPrb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) and the position of the startPrb of the common channel, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:

*rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1] =* startPrbCC - startPrbCoresetCom\*\*

- otherwise,

{

gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the size of the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the initial size of the CORESET #0, the position of the startPrb of the CORESET #0 and the position of the startPrb of the common channel, the blanked PRBs at the low edge of the carrier bandwidth due to PRB blanking or specific bandwidth, in this way:

*rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1] =* startPrbCC - startPrbCoreset0\*\*

- gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the size of the CORESET #0 of ths PDSCH sub area in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1] =* rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1]\*\*

*- there is no update for* the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area.\*\*

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1] does not change.*

**Note: When a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) [CB007372-M] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M], there is no PRB for the CORESET 0 in the additional PDSCH sub area below the CORESET 0, because the common channel starts at the bottom of the CORESET 0.**

}

where nbPrbCC and SizeCoreset0 are defined in this way

nbPrbCC is the number of PRBs for the common channels (see 5G\_UP\_10875\_replaced([11350662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

SizeCoreset0 is the size of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11176138`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
rach msg2 or a rach msg4 without common channel in the same slot

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12677145`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12677145:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Each time a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 is scheduled in a slot (without common channel in the same slot),

1) For the PDSCH sub area which contains the rach msg2 or rach msg4 (see 5G\_UP\_10906\_replaced([11350792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8AvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  **and the PRBs above,**

- When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall update the variables in the same way for the PDSCH sub area which contains the rach msg2 or rach msg4,

Note: When it is the first time that a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 is scheduled, and when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0), the variable *rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] (which remains equal to the size of the CORESET 0) includes at its top the difference between the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) and the start of the CORESET 0 (see figure below).*

gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 extended with the upper part of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23458\_replaced([11176013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) within this PDSCH sub area with the size of this PRB area and the number of the PRBs allocated to the rach msg2 or rach msg4 in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] =* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – nbPrbRach\*\*

where nPrbRach is the number of PRBs allocated for the Rach msg2 or Rach msg4 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16521\_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- otherwise,

**Note: gNB-DU shall apply this part too when a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and :**

- when there is no LTE CAT-M PRBs area in the slot (either the flag NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to false or the slot is without CAT-M PRBs area -for instance a MBSFN subframe)

- or when LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true) and at least one LTE CAT-M PRBs area is present in the slot, if gNB-DU schedules a rach msg2 (but not a rach msg4) in the PDSCH sub area between the lowest PRB available at the bottom of the carrier bandwidth and the PRB below the lowest blanked PRB of the lower CAT-M PRBs area (because this PDSCH sub area contains the largest consecutive PRBs area within the CORESET 0 (or X with X>0) as required in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16468\_replaced([11181355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[CB007372-M] Note: For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true, Coreset0 and InitialBWP resources are in NR dedicated area and do not collide with CAT-M PRBs**[End CB007372-M]\*\*\*\*

gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the size of the CORESET #0   (or X with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area**and the number of the PRBs allocated to the rach msg2 or rach msg4 in this way:**

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] =* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – nbPrbRach\*\*

where

nPrbRach is the number of PRBs allocated for the Rach msg2 or Rach msg4 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16521\_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

*- there is no update for* the variables handling the size of the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area.\*\*

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] does not change*

*- there is no update for* the variables handling the number of remaining PRBs in the dedicated BWP within this PDSCH sub area.\*\*

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] does not change.*

}

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G NR. It depicts different areas and parameters associated with data transmission and control signaling.
On the left side, there are vertical bars representing different resource areas:
- **DCI 1\_0 PRBs area**: This is the leftmost bar, colored purple, and labeled "DCI 1\_0 PRBs area". Above it, the text "Coreset dedicated 1" is written vertically.
- **CORESET#0**: Adjacent to the purple bar is a cyan bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- **initial BWP**: Next to CORESET#0 is a yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- **dedicated BWP**: The rightmost bar is orange and labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these bars, there are horizontal dashed lines indicating boundaries and vertical arrows showing relationships between different elements.
Several text labels and annotations are present:
- **PDSCH sub area 1**: Located above the right side of the diagram.
- **PDSCH sub area 2**: Located below the right side of the diagram.
- **rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]**: A blue line points to the upper part of the diagram, indicating a parameter related to remaining PRBs for CORESET 0 in a PDSCH sub-area.
- **delta (in PRBs) between CORESET 0 and 1 For DCI 1\_0 PRBs area**: This text explains the difference in PRBs between CORESET 0 and 1 for the DCI 1\_0 PRBs area, with a double-headed arrow indicating this delta.
- **rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[1]**: Another blue line points to a middle section of the diagram, likely representing the size of CORESET 0 in a PDSCH sub-area.
- **DCI 1\_0 init type 3**: This text is centrally located, indicating a type of DCI initialization.
- **Rach msg2**: A label pointing to a section of the diagram, likely related to Random Access Channel (RACH) message 2.
- **rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[2]**: A blue line points to a lower section of the diagram, possibly indicating the size of CORESET 0 in another PDSCH sub-area.
- **start Prb CORESET 0**: A label indicating the starting Physical Resource Block (PRB) for CORESET 0.
- **start Prb CORESET 1**: A label indicating the starting Physical Resource Block (PRB) for CORESET 1.
There is also a green rectangular area shown within the "dedicated BWP" bar, which might represent allocated resources. The diagram visually represents the partitioning and configuration of resources for different functionalities within the cellular network.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13209.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

2) For the additional PDSCH sub area below  **the rach msg 2 or rach msg 4 in**the CORESET #0 **for mono BWP**and dedicated BWP(p) bottom aligned   ,***\****\*

**When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall update the variables in the same way for the PDSCH sub area which contains the rach msg2 or rach msg4,**

2.a) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0),

gNB-DU shall update the size of the CORESET#X (with X>0) and the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET#X (with X>0) within this PDSCH sub area with the difference between the  start Prb of the CORESET 0   (or the first PRB modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY above the startPrb of the CORESET 0  if NIDD parameters NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) in the CSI-IM slot**)  and the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) in this way:**

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, actTddCsiRsOpt = true, actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**= (CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)\*\*\*\*

- startPrbCoresetCom

else:

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**= startPrbCoreset0 - startPrbCoresetCom*\*\*

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] =* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]\*\*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way:

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

2.b) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0),

gNB-DU shall update the size of the CORESET #0 and the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the difference between the  start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)   (or the first PRB modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY above the startPrb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)  if NIDD parameters NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) in the CSI-IM slot)**and the start Prb of the CORESET #0 in this way:**

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, actTddCsiRsOpt = true, actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**= (CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)\*\*\*\*

- startPrbCoreset0

else:

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**= startPrbCoresetCom - startPrbCoreset0*\*\*

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] =* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]\*\*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way:

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

fig below: When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X=1 >0)

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G NR. It depicts different frequency resource blocks (PRBs) allocated to various functions.
On the left side, there are vertical bars representing different areas:
- A purple bar labeled "IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area".
- A cyan bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these bars, there are several horizontal dashed lines indicating boundaries and labels associated with these resources. Blue lines with arrows connect these boundaries to specific parameters or functions.
Key elements and labels include:
- "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2" are indicated, suggesting different regions for Physical Downlink Shared Channel transmission.
- "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[1]" and "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[2]" are labels pointing to the sizes of these PDSCH sub-areas.
- "delta (in PRBs) between CORESET 0 and CORESET 1 for DCI 1\_0 PRBs area" is a label indicating the gap in PRBs between two CORESETs within the DCI 1\_0 PRBs area.
- "rrmSizeCoresetOInPdschSubArea[1]" and "rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[2]" are labels that appear to define the size of CORESET 0 within specific PDSCH sub-areas.
- "Rach msg 2" is a label indicating a message related to Random Access Channel.
- "start Prb CORESET 1" and "start Prb CORESET 0" indicate the starting physical resource blocks for CORESET 1 and CORESET 0, respectively.
- A green shaded rectangle is positioned within the "dedicated BWP" area, possibly representing a scheduled resource.
The diagram visually represents the partitioning and sizing of radio resources, highlighting the relationship between different control and data channels, and their respective bandwidth parts (BWPs).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13209.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

**otherwise**the variables are not modified\*\*\*\*

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1], rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] do not change.*

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] =* 0\*\*

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `11176156`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
rach msg2, a rach msg4 or DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode with common channel in the same slot

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12643083`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12643083:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Each time a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled in a slot with already a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) in the same slot,

* If the DCI 1\_0 RAT1 PDSCH grant is allocated in the PDSCH sub area which contains the common channel (see 5G\_UP\_10904\_replaced([11350781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8APvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

- gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the previous value of this variable and the number of the PRBs allocated to the rach msg2, the rach msg4 or the DCI 1\_0 grant in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] =* rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – nbPrbDci10\*\*

- the other variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] ,* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] do not change.\*\*

* For the additional PDSCH sub area (below the CORESET #0 **for mono BWP and dedicated BWP(p) bottom aligned, the variables are not modified**

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1] ,* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] , *rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]  do not change.*\*\*

where:

nrbDci10 is the number of PRBs allocated for the Rach msg2,  Rach msg4 or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16521\_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note: When a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) [CB007372-M] and when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M], there is no start Prb randomization for the common channel which starts from the bottom of the CORESET 0 and then there is no PRB for the CORESET 0 in the additional PDSCH sub area below the CORESET 0

Note: rach msg2 is not expected to be in the same slot than a common channel [CB007372-M] if NRCELL.actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP=false (correcting with CB007372 but this is legacy behaviour) [End CB007372-M]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and channels within a system, likely related to wireless communication.
On the left side, there are three vertical bars representing different BWPs. The leftmost bar is blue and labeled "CORESET#0". To its right is a yellow bar labeled "initial BWP". The largest bar, colored orange, is labeled "dedicated BWP".
The diagram also shows horizontal lines and labels indicating different sub-areas and parameters.
A vertical arrow points from the top of the "dedicated BWP" bar upwards, with the label "PDSCH sub area 1" next to it.
Below the "dedicated BWP" bar, there are two colored rectangular blocks. The upper block is green, and the lower block is purple.
Between the green and purple blocks, there is a horizontal dashed line. Above this line, a vertical double-headed arrow spans a region labeled "rach msg2 nbPrbDci10".
Below the purple block, there is another horizontal dashed line. Between this line and the previous dashed line, there is a region labeled "common Channel".
Further to the right, there are two more vertical double-headed arrows with associated labels. The upper arrow spans a region labeled "rrmNbRemainingCoresetOPdschSubArea[1]". The lower arrow spans a region labeled "rrmSizeCoresetOPdschSubArea[1]". Below the lower arrow, there is a horizontal dashed line with the label "startPrbCC".
Finally, at the bottom of the diagram, another vertical arrow points downwards from a horizontal dashed line, with the label "PDSCH sub area 2" next to it.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13211.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

figure mono BWP

* If the DCI 1\_0 RAT1 PDSCH grant is allocated in the PDSCH sub area which does not contain the common channel and below the common channel for mono BWP and dedicated BWP(p) bottom aligned,

- gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the previous value of this variable and the number of the PRBs allocated to the rach msg2, the rach msg4 or the DCI 1\_0 grant in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] =* rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] – nbPrbDci10\*\*

- the other variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1] ,* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]  do not change.\*\*

* For the additional PDSCH sub area which contains the CORESET #0, the variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] ,* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n], rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] do not change.\*\*

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and channels within a communication system. The diagram is oriented vertically, with time or frequency progressing from bottom to top.
On the left side, there are three colored bars representing different BWPs:
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0" at the bottom.
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP" above the blue bar.
- A tall orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP" above the yellow bar.
To the right of these bars, there are horizontal dashed lines and text indicating different sub-areas and channels:
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a dashed line extending from the top of the orange bar upwards.
- Below "PDSCH sub area 1", there are two colored rectangular areas: a purple rectangle above a green rectangle.
- Associated with these colored rectangles are labels and arrows:
- "common Channel" is labeled next to the top of the purple rectangle, with a double-headed arrow indicating its vertical extent.
- "rach msg2 nbPrbDci10" is labeled next to the top of the green rectangle, with a double-headed arrow indicating its vertical extent.
- "rrmNbRemainingCoresetOPdschSubArea[2]" is labeled below the green rectangle, with a double-headed arrow indicating its vertical extent.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a dashed line extending from the bottom of the green rectangle downwards.
- On the far right, there are two labels with arrows:
- "startPrbCC" is labeled at the top right, with a double-headed arrow pointing to the vertical extent of the "rach msg2 nbPrbDci10" and "rrmNbRemainingCoresetOPdschSubArea[2]" labels.
- "rrmSizeCoresetOPdschSubArea[2]" is labeled below "startPrbCC", with a double-headed arrow indicating its vertical extent.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13211.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

figure mono BWP

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `11176171`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
rach msg2, a rach msg4 or DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in SSB slot without TRS symbol

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11176174`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
all the DCI 1\_0 are mapped on CORESET 0 or  CORESET#X (with X>0)) in the SSB slot

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176184`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176184:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Each time a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled in a SSB slot without TRS symbol (in FDD  non DSS and FR1 TDD), and if all the DCI 1\_0 are mapped on CORESET 0 or  CORESET#X (with X>0)) in the SSB slot

gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 (or a CORESET#X (with X>0)) within this PDSCH sub area with the previous value of this variable and the number of the PRBs allocated to the rach msg2, the rach msg4 or the DCI 1\_0 grant in this way:

rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] = rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – nbPrbDci10

where:

nrbDci10 is the number of PRBs allocated for the Rach msg2,  Rach msg4 or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16521\_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The other variables are not modified

rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] , rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  (with p>1) rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] do not change.

On the left side, there are three vertical bars representing different BWPs: "CORESET#0" in blue, "initial BWP" in yellow, and "dedicated BWP" in orange. The "dedicated BWP" is the tallest bar.
To the right of these bars, there are horizontal dashed lines indicating different sub-areas. The topmost area is labeled "PDSCH sub area 1". Below this, there are two colored rectangular blocks, a green one above a blue one. Between these blocks and the "PDSCH sub area 1" label, there is a vertical arrow with the text "rach msg2 nbPrbDci10" next to it. Below the blue block, there is a label "SSB". The bottommost area is labeled "PDSCH sub area 2".
There are also two vertical double-headed arrows on the right side of the diagram, indicating ranges. The upper arrow points to "↑rrmNbRemainingCoresetOPdschSubArea[1]", and the lower arrow points to "rrmSizeCoresetOPdschSubArea[1]". These arrows are positioned between the dashed lines that delineate the sub-areas.
The text in the image is:
CORESET#0
linitial BWP
dedicated BWP
PDSCH sub area 1
rach msg2 nbPrbDci10
