[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication system's resource allocation. It depicts several rectangular blocks representing different components or time/frequency resources.
On the left side, there are three vertical bars. The leftmost bar is blue and labeled "CORESET#0". To its right is a yellow bar labeled "initial BWP". The largest bar, colored orange, is labeled "dedicated BWP" and extends significantly higher than the other two.
To the right of these bars, there are horizontal dashed lines and labels indicating different areas and configurations. A long vertical arrow points from the top of the "dedicated BWP" bar down to a dashed line, with the label "PDSCH sub area 1" to its right.
Below "PDSCH sub area 1", there is a blue rectangle and a green rectangle stacked vertically. To the right of these rectangles, there are several dashed horizontal lines with associated labels:
- The top dashed line aligns with the top of the blue rectangle and has the label "SSB" to its right.
- Below the "SSB" line, there is a dashed line that separates the blue and green rectangles. To the right of this line, there is a label "rach msg2 nbPrbDci10".
- Below the green rectangle, there is another dashed line. To the right of this line, there is a label "rrmNbRemainingCoresetOPdschSubArea[2]".
- A long vertical arrow points from the dashed line below the green rectangle down to another dashed line, with the label "PDSCH sub area 2" to its right.
- To the far right, aligned with the dashed line below the green rectangle, there is a vertical double-headed arrow and the label "rrmSizeCoresetOPdschSubArea[2]".
The diagram visually represents how different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and control resource sets (CORESETs) are allocated, along with specific signaling channels like SSB and RACH messages, within different PDSCH sub-areas.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13214.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Figure: rach msg2 scheduled below the SSB

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11176189`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
the DCI 1\_0 for rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) and not on CORESET #0

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176204`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176204:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Each time a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled in a SSB slot without TRS symbol (in FDD  non DSS and FR1 TDD),  and when the DCI 1\_0 for rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

2.a) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) is mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area above the SSB, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET#X (with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area

rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] >= startPrbCoreset0 - startPrbCoresetCom

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29561\_replaced([11176174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G NR. It depicts different areas and their assignments using colored blocks and connecting lines with labels.
On the left side, there are vertical colored blocks representing different resource areas:
- A purple block labeled "IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area".
- A cyan block labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow block labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange block labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these blocks, there are horizontal dashed lines indicating boundaries and vertical arrows showing ranges.
Connecting to these areas are lines and text labels that explain the relationships and parameters:
- A blue line connects from the top of the "dedicated BWP" area downwards, pointing to "PDSCH sub area 1".
- Another blue line starts from a point above the "dedicated BWP" area and extends downwards, with a label "rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]".
- A text box explains "delta (in PRBs) between CORESET O and CORESET 1" and "For DCI 1\_0 PRBs area". This text is associated with a double-headed arrow indicating a difference in PRBs.
- Below this, there's a label "DCI 1\_0 init trans" and "type3", connected by a blue line to a point within the diagram.
- Further down, "SSB" is labeled, also connected by a blue line.
- Another blue line, labeled "rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]", originates from a point within the "dedicated BWP" area and extends downwards.
- Two more labels, "start Prb CORESET 0" and "start Prb CORESET 1", are shown at the bottom, with a double-headed arrow indicating a difference.
There are also labels "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2" indicating different sub-areas within the PDSCH.
The diagram uses color coding to differentiate resource allocations and employs lines and text to define relationships, parameters, and specific configurations like CORESETs, BWPs, and DCI transmissions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13216.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including rach msg 2 or rach msg 4) is mapped on CORESET 0 and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area below the SSB, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET 0 within the PDSCH sub area.

rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] >= startPrbCoreset0 - startPrbCoresetCom

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29561\_replaced([11176174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to 5G NR (New Radio). It depicts different blocks representing various resource areas and their configurations.
On the left side, there are several vertical colored bars:
- A light purple bar labeled "IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area" and "Coreset dedicated 1".
- A dark blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these bars, there are horizontal dashed lines and arrows indicating different sub-areas and their extents.
- A double-headed arrow labeled "PDSCH sub area 1" spans a large vertical range above the orange bar.
- Below this, there's a dark blue rectangular block, and below that, a light green rectangular block.
- A double-headed arrow labeled "PDSCH sub area 2" is positioned between the dark blue and light green blocks, indicating a smaller vertical range.
Further to the right, there are diagonal blue lines with text labels:
- An upper diagonal line labeled "rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[2]".
- A lower diagonal line labeled "rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[2]".
There are also labels and arrows indicating specific points or regions:
- "SSB" is labeled next to a vertical arrow pointing upwards from the dark blue block.
- "Rach msg 4" is labeled next to a vertical arrow pointing upwards from the light green block.
At the bottom right, there are two horizontal dashed lines with double-headed arrows and labels:
- The upper one is labeled "Start Prb CORESET 0".
- The lower one is labeled "Start Prb CORESET 1".
The diagram visually represents how different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and control resource sets (CORESETs) are allocated and how they relate to physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) sub-areas, synchronization signal blocks (SSBs), and random access (RACH) messages. The diagonal lines likely represent dynamic changes or relationships between these parameters.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13216.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

2.b) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including rach msg 2 or rach msg 4) is mapped on CORESET 0 and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area above the SSB,  gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET 0 within the PDSCH sub area.

rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] >= startPrbCoresetCom - startPrbCoreset0

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29561\_replaced([11176174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G NR (New Radio). It depicts different frequency resource blocks (PRBs) allocated for various functions.
On the left side, there are vertical bars representing allocated PRB ranges. From left to right, these are:
- A purple bar labeled "IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area".
- A cyan bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A green bar, and below it, a blue bar.
There are horizontal dashed lines indicating boundaries between different areas.
On the right side, there are labels and arrows pointing to specific regions or relationships between them:
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a double-headed arrow spanning a significant portion of the diagram.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a double-headed arrow below "PDSCH sub area 1".
- "SSB" (Synchronization Signal Block) is indicated by a double-headed arrow pointing to a lower region.
- "Rach msg 2" is indicated by a label with a wavy underline, positioned between "PDSCH sub area 1" and "SSB".
- Two blue lines originate from the top right and converge downwards, labeled:
- "rrmSizeCoresetolnPdschSubArea[1]"
- "rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[1]"
- A label "delta (in PRBs) between CORESET 0 and CORESET 1" is accompanied by a double-headed arrow indicating a difference in PRB allocation.
- Below this, "For DCI 1\_0 PRBs area" provides context for the delta measurement.
- At the bottom right, there are two labels:
- "start Prb CORESET 1"
- "start Prb CORESET 0"
The diagram visually represents how different control and data channels, as well as bandwidth parts (BWPs), are mapped onto the available PRBs, and highlights specific parameters like the size of CORESETs and the delta between them. The "Coreset dedicated 1" label is oriented vertically along the left edge of the diagram.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13216.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) is mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area below the SSB, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET#X (with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area

rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] >= startPrbCoresetCom - startPrbCoreset0

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29561\_replaced([11176174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to 5G NR (New Radio). It depicts several colored vertical bars representing different resource areas and their assignments.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
\* **Left Side:**
\* A tall, light purple bar labeled "IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area" and "Coreset dedicated 1". This suggests a dedicated area for PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks) associated with IDCI 1\_0 and a specific CORESET.
\* A shorter, cyan bar labeled "CORESET#0". This represents a CORESET (Control Resource Set) with index 0.
\* A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP" (Bandwidth Part). This indicates an initial bandwidth part configuration.
\* A tall, orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP". This signifies a dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **Middle Section:**
\* A dark blue rectangular block positioned below the "dedicated BWP" bar.
\* A light purple rectangular block positioned below the dark blue block.
\* Vertical arrows and dashed orange lines indicate different "PDSCH sub area" divisions. One is labeled "PDSCH sub area 1" and another "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* **Right Side:**
\* The text "SSB" (Synchronization Signal Block) is shown with a vertical arrow pointing to a region.
\* The text "DCI 1\_0 retrans type3" is displayed with a wavy red underline, indicating a retransmission of DCI (Downlink Control Information) of type 3 for IDCI 1\_0.
\* Two lines of text, "start Prb CORESET 1" and "start Prb CORESET 0", are shown with vertical arrows indicating specific starting PRB positions for CORESET 1 and CORESET 0, respectively.
\* A diagonal line originates from the "SSB" region and extends upwards and to the right, labeled "rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[2]". This likely represents a relationship between the size of CORESET 0 within a PDSCH sub-area and some other parameter.
\* Another diagonal line originates from below the "start Prb CORESET 0" label and extends upwards and to the right, labeled "rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[2]". This might indicate the number of remaining PRBs for CORESET 0 within a PDSCH sub-area.
The diagram visually represents how different control and data resources are allocated and segmented within a communication system's time-frequency grid.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13216.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-6**  (ID: `12729885`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
**rach msg2, a rach msg4 or DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in slot with PRBs muted due to interference mitigation**

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12729886`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
**all the DCI 1\_0 are mapped on CORESET 0 or** **CORESET#X (with X>0)) in the slot**\*\*\*\*

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12729887`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12729887:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Each time a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled in a slot with PRBs muted due to interference mitigation(IM, refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), and if all the DCI 1\_0 are mapped on CORESET 0 or  CORESET#X (with X>0)) in the slot

gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 (or a CORESET#X (with X>0)) within this PDSCH sub area with the previous value of this variable and the number of the PRBs allocated to the rach msg2, the rach msg4 or the DCI 1\_0 grant in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] =* rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – nbPrbDci10\*\*

where:

nrbDci10 is the number of PRBs allocated for the Rach msg2,  Rach msg4 or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16521\_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The other variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] ,* rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  (with p>1) *rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] do not change.*\*\*

On the left side, there are three vertical bars representing different BWPs:
1. **CORESET#0**: A cyan-blue bar on the far left.
2. **initial BWP**: A bright yellow bar next to CORESET#0.
3. **dedicated BWP**: A large orange bar to the right of the initial BWP.
To the right of these bars, the diagram shows different sub-areas and their associated resource quantities, indicated by vertical arrows and text labels.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated sub-areas within a PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) context. The diagram uses colored bars to represent these components and text labels to identify them.
On the left side, there are three vertical bars of different colors and lengths:
1. **CORESET#0**: A blue bar on the far left.
2. **initial BWP**: A yellow bar to the right of the blue bar, slightly shorter.
3. **dedicated BWP**: An orange bar to the right of the yellow bar, the longest of the three.
To the right of these bars, there are two horizontal sections labeled as PDSCH sub-areas:

\* **PDSCH sub area 2**: This is the lower section.
Within the diagram, there are also two smaller colored rectangles:
\* A light blue rectangle labeled "PRBs muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)". This rectangle is positioned to the right of the "dedicated BWP" bar and partially overlaps with the upper part of the "PDSCH sub area 1" region.
\* A green rectangle positioned below the light blue rectangle.
Several arrows and text labels indicate relationships and sizes:
\* An arrow pointing from the top of the diagram down to the bottom of the "PRBs muted due to IM" section is labeled "PDSCH sub area 1".
\* An arrow pointing from the bottom of the "PRBs muted due to IM" section down to the top of the "PDSCH sub area 2" section is labeled "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)".
\* An arrow pointing from the top of the green rectangle to the bottom of the light blue rectangle is labeled "rach msg2 nbPrbDci10".
\* An arrow pointing from the top of the "PDSCH sub area 2" section down to the bottom of the diagram is labeled "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* To the right of the "rach msg2 nbPrbDci10" label, there is a label "rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[2]".
\* Below that, there is a label "rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[2]".
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and specific PRB (Physical Resource Block) configurations, such as muted PRBs due to interference management (IM) and RACH (Random Access Channel) messages, are allocated within different sub-areas of the PDSCH.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08061420.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `12729888`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
the DCI 1\_0 for rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) and not on CORESET #0

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12729889`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12729889:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Each time a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled in a slot with PRBs muted due to IM(refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)),  and when the DCI 1\_0 for rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

2.a) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) is mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area above the PRBs muted by IM, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET#X (with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] >= startPrbCoreset0 - startPrbCoresetCom*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29561\_replaced([11176174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating different resource allocation areas and parameters related to cellular network communication, likely 5G NR. The diagram uses colored bars to represent different resource blocks (PRBs) and labels them with specific functions or configurations.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Colored Bars (representing PRBs):**
\* **Purple Bar (leftmost):** Labeled "DCI10 PRBs Area". This suggests a region of PRBs allocated for DCI (Downlink Control Information) transmission, specifically for a DCI format 10.
\* **Pink Bar:** Labeled "Coreset dedicated 1". This indicates a set of PRBs dedicated to a specific CORESET (Control Resource Set) for control signaling.
\* **Blue Bar:** Labeled "CORESET#0". This represents another CORESET, likely the primary or a reference CORESET.
\* **Yellow Bar:** Labeled "initial BWP". This signifies the initial Bandwidth Part (BWP), which is a subset of the carrier bandwidth.
\* **Orange Bar:** Labeled "dedicated BWP". This represents a dedicated BWP, possibly for a specific user or service.
\* **Green Bar:** This bar is positioned above a blue bar and is not explicitly labeled with a primary function, but it's part of the overall resource allocation.
\* **Light Blue Bar:** Labeled "PRBs muted due to IM". This indicates a block of PRBs that are muted or unavailable due to Interference Management (IM).
**Labels and Annotations:**
\* **"PDSCH sub area 1"**: This label points to a region above the green and blue bars, indicating a sub-area for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) transmission.
\* **"rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[1]"**: This text likely refers to the number of remaining PRBs in a PDSCH sub-area associated with CORESET 0.
\* **"delta(in PRBs) between Coreseto and Coreset1"**: This annotation, with a diagonal line connecting two points, indicates the difference in PRBs between CORESET 0 and CORESET 1.
\* **"For DCL1\_0 PRBs area"**: This text clarifies the context for the preceding delta measurement.
\* **"rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[1]"**: This likely refers to the size (number of PRBs) of the PDSCH sub-area associated with CORESET 0.
\* **"DCI 1\_0 init trans type3"**: This annotation likely describes a specific type of DCI transmission (DCI format 1\_0) and its initialization type.
\* **"PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)"**: This label reiterates the meaning of the light blue bar, specifying that the muted PRBs are due to IM and referring to the count of such PRBs as "nbPrbsIM PRBs".
\* **"PDSCH sub area 2"**: This label points to a region below the muted PRBs, indicating another sub-area for PDSCH transmission.
\* **"Start Prb Coreset0"**: This annotation marks the starting PRB index for CORESET 0.
\* **"Start Prb Coreset1"**: This annotation marks the starting PRB index for CORESET 1.
**Arrows:**
\* Vertical arrows are used to indicate the extent or size of different areas or the difference between specific points.
\* A diagonal arrow connects a point within "PDSCH sub area 1" to a point related to the "delta" annotation, visually representing the difference in PRBs.
The diagram visually represents how different control and data channels are mapped onto PRBs, with specific considerations for CORESETs, BWPs, and interference management.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08061428.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including rach msg 2 or rach msg 4) is mapped on CORESET 0 and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area below the SSB, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET 0 within the PDSCH sub area.

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] >= startPrbCoreset0 - startPrbCoresetCom*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29561\_replaced([11176174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

2.b) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including rach msg 2 or rach msg 4) is mapped on CORESET 0 and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area above the PRBs muted by IM,  gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET 0 within the PDSCH sub area.

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] >= startPrbCoresetCom - startPrbCoreset0*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29561\_replaced([11176174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) in a cellular network, likely related to 5G NR (New Radio). It depicts different areas and their assignments, along with specific parameters and conditions.
Here's a breakdown of the visual elements and text:
**Left Side: PRB Allocation Areas**
\* **Purple Bar (Leftmost):** Labeled "DCI10 PRBs Area Coreset dedicated 1". This indicates a region of PRBs designated for DCI (Downlink Control Information) transmission, specifically for a "Coreset dedicated 1".
\* **Blue Bar:** Labeled "CORESET#0". This represents another core resource set, "CORESET#0".
\* **Yellow Bar:** Labeled "initial BWP". This signifies the initial Bandwidth Part (BWP), a feature that allows for flexible bandwidth usage.
\* **Orange Bar:** Labeled "dedicated BWP". This represents a dedicated BWP, likely for specific services or configurations.
\* **Green Rectangle:** Positioned above a blue rectangle, it doesn't have a specific label but is part of the PRB allocation.
\* **Blue Rectangle:** Labeled "PRBs muted due to IM". This indicates a block of PRBs that are muted or unavailable due to Interference Management (IM).
**Right Side: Parameters and Annotations**
\* **Top Annotation:** "rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[1]". This likely refers to the number of remaining PRBs in a specific PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) sub-area for Coreset0.
\* **Blue Line Segment 1:** Connects from a point above the "PDSCH sub area 1" label to the "rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[1]" annotation.
\* **Annotation below Blue Line Segment 1:** "delta(in PRBs) between Coreset0 and Coreset1". This describes the difference in PRBs between Coreset0 and Coreset1.
\* **Annotation below that:** "For DCI 1\_0 PRBs area". This specifies the context for the preceding delta measurement.
\* **Blue Line Segment 2:** Connects from a point above the "PDSCH sub area 1" label to the "rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[1]" annotation.
\* **Annotation:** "rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[1]". This likely refers to the size of a PDSCH sub-area for Coreset0.
\* **Annotation:** "DCI 1\_0 init trans type3". This indicates a specific type of initial transmission for DCI 1\_0.
\* **Vertical Arrow 1 (Leftmost):** Spans from the top of the diagram down to the bottom of the "CORESET#0" bar. It is labeled "PDSCH sub area 1".
\* **Vertical Arrow 2:** Spans from the top of the "dedicated BWP" bar down to the top of the "PRBs muted due to IM" rectangle. It is labeled "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)". This annotation clarifies that the muted PRBs are due to Interference Management and quantifies them as "nbPrbsIM PRBs".
\* **Vertical Arrow 3 (Rightmost):** Spans from the top of the "dedicated BWP" bar down to the bottom of the diagram. It is labeled "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* **Dashed Orange Horizontal Lines:** These lines act as dividers or reference points across the diagram, indicating boundaries or specific PRB ranges.
\* **Bottom Right Annotations:**
\* "Start Prb Coreset1"
\* "Start Prb Coreset0"
These indicate the starting PRB index for Coreset1 and Coreset0, respectively.

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) is mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area below the PRB muted by IM, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET#X (with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] >= startPrbCoresetCom - startPrbCoreset0*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29561\_replaced([11176174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

5. **dedicated BWP**: An orange bar.
To the right of these bars, there are two stacked light blue and green bars. The light blue bar is labeled "PRBs muted due to IM", and the green bar is below it.
Several annotations and arrows indicate relationships and measurements:
\* An arrow labeled "PDSCH sub area 1" spans from the top of the orange bar to a dashed line above the light blue and green bars.
\* Below the "PDSCH sub area 1" annotation, there is text "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)". An arrow points from this text to the top of the light blue bar.
\* Below the "PRB muted due to IM" text, there is an arrow labeled "DCI 1\_0 retrains type3". This arrow points from a dashed line above the light blue bar to a dashed line above the green bar.
\* Below the "DCI 1\_0 retrains type3" annotation, there is an arrow labeled "rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[2]". This arrow points from the dashed line above the green bar to a dashed line further down.
\* Below the "rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[2]" annotation, there is an arrow labeled "rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[2]". This arrow points from the dashed line above the green bar to a dashed line even further down.
\* To the right of the "rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[2]" annotation, there is text "Start Prb Coreset1" next to a dashed line.
\* To the right of the "rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[2]" annotation, there is text "Start Prb Coreset0" next to a dashed line.
\* An arrow labeled "PDSCH sub area 2" spans from the bottom of the orange bar to the lowest dashed line.
The dashed orange lines appear to demarcate different sub-areas or reference points within the resource allocation.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08061432.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-7**  (ID: `11176208`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
rach msg2 or DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode without common channel in the same slot above a CAT-M PRBs area in FDD DSS

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `12643415`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12643415:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
For a FDD cell shared with LTE (FDD DSS), when LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true) and  [CB007372-M] when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false and  [End CB007372-M]  at least one LTE CAT-M PRBs area is present in the slot,

when gNB-DU schedules a rach msg2 (but not a rach msg4) or a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for the first time in the PDSCH sub area above a CAT-M PRBs area (because this PDSCH sub area contains the largest consecutive PRBs area within the CORESET 0 as required in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16468\_replaced([11181355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall update the size of the CORESET 0 with the number of PRBs of the CORESET 0 in this PDSCH sub area and shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 within this PDSCH sub area with the size of the CORESET #0 within the PDSCH sub area and the number of the PRBs allocated to the rach msg2 in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] =* rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – nbPrbRach\*\*

where

nPrbRach is the number of PRBs allocated for the Rach msg2 or the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]*is the number of PRBs of the CORESET 0 in this PDSCH sub area.\*\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating different sub-areas within a communication system, likely related to LTE CAT-M. The diagram features colored vertical bars representing resource blocks or time slots, with labels indicating their purpose.
On the left side, there are vertical bars colored blue, yellow, and orange. The blue bar is labeled "CO ET#0". The yellow bar is labeled "initial BWP". The orange bar is labeled "dedicated B".
Above these bars, there are horizontal red bars. The top red bar spans across the blue, yellow, and orange bars. Below this, there is a gap, and then another horizontal red bar.
The diagram also shows vertical arrows and dashed lines connecting these bars to text labels on the right side.
There are three labeled "PDSCH sub area" sections:
1. "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a vertical arrow spanning from the top red bar to the bottom of the yellow and orange bars.
2. "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a vertical arrow spanning between the two horizontal red bars.
3. "PDSCH sub area B" is indicated by a vertical arrow spanning from the bottom red bar to the bottom of the green bar.
On the right side, there are several text labels connected by blue lines to different parts of the diagram:
\* "3 LTE CAT-M PRBs areas" is connected to the top red bar and the area above the second red bar.
\* "rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[3]" is connected to the area between the two red bars.
\* "rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[3]" is connected to the area below the second red bar and above the third red bar.
\* "Rach msg2" is located below the "PDSCH sub area B" label.
A green bar is partially visible between the second and third red bars, positioned to the right of the yellow and orange bars.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13218.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

fig: rach msg2 in the PDSCH sub area 3 above a CAT-M PRBs area

If all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are not mapped with the CORESET #0 (Note: for instance in FDD DSS NSA mode)  , if a rach msg2 is in the TD scheduler list, gNB-DU shall select a PDCH sub area and shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET#X (with X>0) within this PDSCH sub area in the same way as described for the CORESET 0 in this requirement.

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8**  (ID: `11176219`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11176223`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
additional DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode when a common channel is scheduled in the slot

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12816348`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12816348:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In a slot where a common channel (SIB1, SI PAGING) is already scheduled,

1) When all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are mapped on CORESET #0

When PRBs are reserved for a DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant in CSS mode, gNB-DU shall update the variables in the same way than for a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 23558), just replace nbPrbRach by the number of PRBs reserved to the DCI 1\_0 PDSCH allocation.

2) When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

2.a) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) is mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area which contains the common channel, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET#X (with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true, NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\* and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**>= (CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY) - startPrbCoresetCom\*\*\*\*

else:

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] >= startPrbCoreset0 - startPrbCoresetCom*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G NR. It depicts different areas and channels represented by colored bars and labels.
On the left side, there are vertical bars representing different resource allocations:
- A purple bar labeled "IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area".
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A light purple bar above a green bar, both within the "dedicated BWP" area.
On the right side, there are labels and arrows indicating different sub-areas and parameters:
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a vertical arrow pointing upwards from a dashed orange line.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a vertical arrow pointing downwards from a dashed orange line.
- A blue line connects two points, with an arrow pointing to "rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]".
- A double-headed arrow indicates "delta (in PRBs) between CORESET O and CORESET 1" and "For DCI 1\_0 PRBs area".
- "DCI 1\_0 init trans" and "type3" are labeled below the delta.
- "common channel" is labeled with a wavy red underline.
- Another blue line connects two points, with an arrow pointing to "rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]".
- At the bottom, there are two labels with double-headed arrows: "start Prb CORESET O" and "start Prb CORESET 1".
The diagram uses color-coding to differentiate between various resource blocks and control regions. The vertical axis appears to represent the frequency domain (PRBs), and the horizontal axis might represent time or different control/data channels. The dashed orange lines likely denote boundaries between different resource regions or sub-areas.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13221.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including rach msg 2 or rach msg 4) is mapped on CORESET 0 and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area below the common channel, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET 0 within the PDSCH sub area.

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true, NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\* and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**>= (CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY) - startPrbCoresetCom\*\*\*\*

else:

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] >= startPrbCoreset0 - startPrbCoresetCom*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1).

- To the right of the blue bar is a yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".

Above these bars, a purple bar is shown extending upwards, labeled "Coreset dedicated 1".
To the right of the "dedicated BWP" bar, there is a green rectangular area.
The diagram also includes several text labels and lines indicating relationships and measurements:
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is labeled above the green area and extends to the right.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is labeled below "PDSCH sub area 1" and is associated with a vertical dashed line and arrows.
- "common channel" is written in red text, with a red dashed line and arrows pointing to a region between two blue lines.
- "Rach msg 4" is written in red text, with a red dashed line and arrows pointing to a region below "common channel" and between two blue lines.
- Two blue lines originate from the right side of the diagram and extend upwards and to the right. The upper line is labeled "rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[2]". The lower line is labeled "rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[2]".
- At the bottom right, there are two labels with associated dashed lines and arrows: "Start Prb CORESET 0" and "Start Prb CORESET 1".
The diagram uses dashed orange lines to indicate boundaries and measurements between different elements. The overall visual representation suggests a breakdown and allocation of resources, possibly for data transmission and control signaling in a cellular network.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13221.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

2.b) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including rach msg 2 or rach msg 4) is mapped on CORESET 0 and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area which contains the common channel, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET 0 within the PDSCH sub area.

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true, NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\* and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**>= (CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY) - startPrbCoreset0\*\*\*\*

else:

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] >= startPrbCoresetCom - startPrbCoreset0*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1)

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to 5G NR (New Radio). It depicts different types of resource blocks (PRBs) allocated to various channels and control information.
The diagram is structured vertically, with different colored bars representing different resource allocations. From left to right, the bars are:
\* **Purple Bar:** Labeled "IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area". This likely represents a set of PRBs used for IDCI (Intra-Device Communication Interface) or a similar control channel.
\* **Blue Bar:** Labeled "CORESET#0". This signifies a CORESET (Control Resource Set) designated as #0.
\* **Yellow Bar:** Labeled "initial BWP". This indicates an initial Bandwidth Part (BWP), which is a subset of the total system bandwidth.
\* **Orange Bar:** Labeled "dedicated BWP". This represents a dedicated BWP, likely for user data or specific services.
\* **Green Bar:** This bar is positioned to the right of the orange bar and extends downwards. It is not explicitly labeled with text but is visually distinct.
To the right of these bars, there are several annotations and lines indicating relationships and measurements:
\* **"PDSCH sub area 1"**: This label is associated with the upper portion of the diagram, suggesting a region for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) transmission.
\* **"rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[1]"**: This is a technical label, likely referring to the size of CORESET 0 within a PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]"**: Another technical label, possibly indicating the number of remaining PRBs for CORESET 0 in the PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"delta (in PRBs) between CORESET 0 and CORESET 1"**: This annotation points to a vertical gap between two levels, indicating a difference in PRBs between CORESET 0 and CORESET 1.
\* **"For DCI 1\_0 PRBs area"**: This text clarifies that the preceding "delta" measurement is relevant to the DCI 1\_0 PRBs area.
\* **"DCI 1\_0 init trans Type1 (SRB1)"**: This label describes a type of DCI (Downlink Control Information) transmission, specifically "init trans Type1" for "SRB1" (Signaling Radio Bearer 1). It is associated with a vertical segment.
\* **"common channel"**: This label, underlined with a wavy red line, indicates a region for a common channel. It is also associated with a vertical segment.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 2"**: Similar to "PDSCH sub area 1", this label refers to a lower region for PDSCH transmission.
\* **"start Prb CORESET 1"**: This indicates the starting PRB for CORESET 1.
\* **"start Prb CORESET 0"**: This indicates the starting PRB for CORESET 0.
The diagram uses dashed orange lines to denote boundaries and measurements between different resource allocations and control information areas. The overall visual representation suggests a breakdown of downlink resources, highlighting the allocation for control signaling (CORESETs, DCI) and data transmission (PDSCH, BWPs).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13221.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) is mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area below the common channel, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET#X (with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true, NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\* and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**>= (CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY) - startPrbCoreset0\*\*\*\*

else:

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1] >= startPrbCoresetCom - startPrbCoreset0*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1)

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely a cellular network. It depicts several colored rectangular blocks representing different resource areas and channels, arranged vertically and horizontally.
On the left side, there are vertical colored bars:
- A purple bar labeled "IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area".
- A cyan bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A light green bar positioned to the right of the orange bar, partially overlapping it.
- A light purple bar positioned below the light green bar.
Horizontal dashed lines and arrows indicate divisions and relationships between these areas.
On the right side, there are labels and lines pointing to specific regions of the diagram:
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a vertical arrow pointing upwards from a dashed line.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a vertical arrow pointing downwards from a dashed line.
- A blue line originates from the top right and points to the "dedicated BWP" area, with the label "rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[2]".
- A red wavy line is labeled "common channel".
- Another blue line originates from the "common channel" area and points downwards, with the label "DCI 1\_0 retrans type3".
- Two more blue lines originate from the "DCI 1\_0 retrans type3" area and point downwards, with labels "start Prb CORESET 1" and "start Prb CORESET 0".
- A final blue line originates from the bottom right and points upwards, with the label "rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[2]".
The diagram visually represents how different types of data or control information are allocated within specific resource blocks (PRBs) and bandwidth parts (BWPs), and how these allocations relate to different core sets and sub-areas.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13221.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

Otherwise in all the other use cases of DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1).

Note: When the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0) has the same value than the start Prb of the CORESET 0, gNB-DU shall handle all the DCI 1\_0 as they belong to the CORESET 0 and apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1).

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11176243`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
additional DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode when a rach msg2 or rach msg4 is scheduled without common channel  in the slot

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12816354`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12816354:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In a slot where a rach msg2 or rach msg4 is already scheduled without common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING),

1) When all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are mapped on CORESET #0 (or X with X>0)

gNB-DU shall update the variables in the same way than for a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 23469, just replace nbPrbRach by the number of PRBs reserved to the DCI 1\_0 PDSCH allocation.

2) When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

2.a) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) is mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area which contains the common channel, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET#X (with X>0) within the PDSCH sub area

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true, NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\* and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**>= (CEIL(startPrbCoreset0*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY) - startPrbCoresetCom\*\*\*\*

else:

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] >= startPrbCoreset0 - startPrbCoresetCom*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to 5G NR. It uses colored vertical bars to represent different resource blocks (PRBs) and their allocation to various functions.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Allocation Bars):**
\* **Purple Bar (Leftmost):** Labeled "IDCI 1\_0 PRBs area". This appears to be a block of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) allocated for a specific purpose related to "IDCI 1\_0".
\* **Cyan Bar:** Labeled "CORESET#0". This represents PRBs allocated for "CORESET 0".
\* **Yellow Bar:** Labeled "initial BWP". This indicates an "initial Bandwidth Part" allocation.
\* **Orange Bar:** Labeled "dedicated BWP". This signifies a "dedicated Bandwidth Part" allocation.
\* **Light Purple Bar:** This bar is positioned below the orange bar and extends to the right. It seems to represent a portion of the PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) area.
\* **Green Bar:** This bar is located below the light purple bar and to the right. It also appears to be part of the PDSCH area.
**Right Side (Annotations and Indicators):**
\* **Vertical Arrows and Dashed Lines:** These are used to indicate ranges, differences, and relationships between different resource blocks and areas.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 1"**: This annotation points to the upper portion of the PDSCH allocation.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 2"**: This annotation points to the lower portion of the PDSCH allocation.
\* **"rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]"**: This is a technical label, likely referring to the number of remaining PRBs for CORESET 0 within a specific PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"delta (in PRBs) between CORESET 0 and CORESET 1"**: This annotation, with a double-headed arrow, indicates the difference in PRBs between CORESET 0 and CORESET 1.
\* **"For DCI 1\_0 PRBs area"**: This clarifies the context for the "delta" measurement.
\* **"rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[1]"**: Another technical label, possibly indicating the size of CORESET 0 within a PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"DCI 1\_0 init type 3"**: This label specifies a type of "Downlink Control Information" (DCI) initialization.
\* **"Rach msg2"**: This indicates the allocation for "Random Access Channel (RACH) message 2".
\* **"start Prb CORESET 0"**: This marks the starting PRB for CORESET 0.
\* **"start Prb CORESET 1"**: This marks the starting PRB for CORESET 1.
\* **"Coreset dedicated 1"**: This label is positioned vertically along the leftmost purple bar, indicating a dedicated CORESET.
The diagram visually represents how different communication resources (PRBs) are segmented and allocated for various functions like control signaling (CORESETs, DCI) and data transmission (PDSCH), as well as initial and dedicated bandwidth parts. The annotations provide specific technical details about these allocations and their relationships.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13223.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note: gNB-DU may schedule a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) in the PDSCH sub area below the CORESET #0.

2.b) when the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0)

* When a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including rach msg 2 or rach msg 4) is mapped on CORESET 0 and request PRBs in the PDSCH sub area which contains the common channel, gNB-DU shall check the constraints in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally that the PRBs reservation does shall not exceed the CORESET 0 within the PDSCH sub area.

If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true, NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\* and CSI-IM slot,

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]**>= (CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY) - startPrbCoreset0\*\*\*\*

else:

*rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] >= startPrbCoresetCom - startPrbCoreset0*

where startPrbCoreset0 and startPrbCoresetCom are defined in this way

- startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

And if the checks are successful, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1)

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to 5G NR. It depicts different resource blocks (PRBs) allocated for various functions.
On the left side, there are vertical colored bars representing different resource areas:
- A purple bar labeled "DCI 1\_0 PRBs area Coreset dedicated 1".
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A green bar representing a portion of allocated resources.
Horizontal dashed lines indicate boundaries between different sub-areas.
On the right side, there are labels and arrows indicating relationships and values:
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a label and an arrow pointing to a region above the green bar.
- "rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]" is a label connected by a blue line to a region above the green bar.
- "delta (in PRBs) between CORESET O and CORESET 1" is a label with a double-headed arrow indicating a difference in PRBs between CORESET 0 and CORESET 1, specifically for the "DCI 1\_0 PRBs area".
- "rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]" is a label connected by a blue line to a region below the "delta" label.
- "DCI 1\_0 re trans Type1 (SRB1)" is a label with a downward arrow pointing to a region below the "rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1]" label.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is a label at the bottom left, with arrows indicating a region below the green bar.
- "Rach msg 4" is a label with a downward arrow pointing to a region below the "DCI 1\_0 re trans" label.
- "start Prb CORESET 1" is a label with a downward arrow pointing to a region below "Rach msg 4".
- "start Prb CORESET 0" is a label at the very bottom, with a downward arrow pointing to the lowest region.
The diagram visually represents the partitioning and allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) for different control and data channels, including DCI (Downlink Control Information), CORESET (Control Resource Set), BWP (Bandwidth Part), PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel), and RACH (Random Access Channel). The labels and arrows suggest a quantitative relationship or a specific configuration of these resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13223.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Note: gNB-DU may schedule a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode mapped on CORESET 0 in the PDSCH sub area below the CORESET#X (with X>0).

Otherwise in all the other use cases of DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode, gNB-DU shall apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1).

Note: When the start Prb of the CORESET#X has the same value than the start Prb of the CORESET 0, gNB-DU shall handle all the DCI 1\_0 as they belong to the CORESET 0 and apply the same statement than described above in paragraph 1).

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11176257`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
additional DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode when no rach msg2, no rach msg4 and no common channel is scheduled in the slot

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11176262`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
[CB008927-K] When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

When the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0), gNB-DU shall apply the same rules for a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) as in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26047\_replaced([11176243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowa_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) paragraph 2.a)

When the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0), gNB-DU shall apply the same rules for a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode mapped on CORESET 0 as in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26047\_replaced([11176243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowa_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) paragraph 2.b)

Otherwise [End CB008927-K] when PRBs are reserved for a DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant in CSS mode [CB008927-K] either mapped on CORESET 0 or mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0)  [End CB008927-K]  but no rach msg2 neither rach msg4 nor common channel is scheduled, gNB-DU shall update the variables in the same way than for a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 23469 [CB008927-K] in the PDSCH sub areas above or below the first DCI 1\_0 scheduled [End CB008927-K], just replace nbPrbRach by the number of PRBs reserved to the DCI 1\_0 PDSCH allocation.

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-4**  (ID: `11176268`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
additional DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode when no rach msg2, no rach msg4 and no common channel is scheduled in the slot when no DCI 1\_0 mapped on CORESET 0

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176273`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If [CB008927-K] all the [End CB008927-K] DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are not mapped on CORESET #0 [CB008927-K] (but on CORESET#X (with X>0) [CB008927-Q], for instance FDD DSS NSA configuration [End CB008927-Q],  see 5G\_UP\_10912\_replaced([11350814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8BvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008927-K], if PRBs are reserved for a DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant in CSS mode where no rach msg2 is scheduled in the slot, gNB-DU shall update the variables in the same way than for a rach msg2 or a rach msg4 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 23469, just replace nbPrbRach by the number of PRBs reserved to the DCI 1\_0 PDSCH allocation.

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-5**  (ID: `11176276`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
additional DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in the SSB slot without TRS symbol

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-8.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11176284`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176284:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
When the DCI 1\_0 for rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

When the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is higher than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0), gNB-DU shall apply the same rules for a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (Type3 PDCCH CSS) mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) as in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26046\_replaced([11176223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) paragraph 2.a)

When the start Prb of the CORESET 0 is lower than the start Prb of the CORESET#X (with X>0), gNB-DU shall apply the same rules for a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode mapped on CORESET 0 as in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26046\_replaced([11176223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuowafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) paragraph 2.b)

Otherwise when PRBs are reserved for a DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant in CSS mode either mapped on CORESET 0 or mapped on CORESET#X (with X>0) , gNB-DU shall update the variable handling the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0 (or a CORESET#X (with X>0)) within this PDSCH sub area with the previous value of this variable and the number of the PRBs allocated to the rach msg2, the rach msg4 or the DCI 1\_0 grant in this way:

rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] = rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – nbPrbDci10

where:

nrbDci10 is the number of PRBs allocated for the Rach msg2,  Rach msg4 or another DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16521\_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The other variables are not modified

rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] , rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  (with p>1) rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] do not change.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication system's resource allocation. On the left, there are three vertical bars representing different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and a CORESET. The first bar, labeled "CORESET#0" in blue, is the shortest. Next to it is a yellow bar labeled "initial BWP". The third and widest bar, colored orange, is labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these bars, the diagram shows different sub-areas and their associated parameters. There are two horizontal dashed lines indicating "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2". Between these areas, there are labels for "DCI 1\_0 type 3 nbPrbDci10" and "rach msg2 nbPrbDci10", with a smaller label "SSB" below them.
Vertical arrows indicate the size and remaining capacity of certain areas. One arrow points to "rrmNbRemainingCoresetOPdschSubArea[1]", and another, longer arrow points to "rrmSizeCoresetOPdschSubArea[1]". There are also green and blue rectangular blocks positioned to the right of the "dedicated BWP" bar, which appear to represent allocated resources within the PDSCH sub-areas. The green block is above the blue block.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13229.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `12816360`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12816360:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Each time a number of PRBs is reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Dedicated BWP within a PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] =* rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  - nbPrb\*\*

The other variables are not modified

*rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n],* rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n]*do not change*.\*\*\*\*

where:

nbPrb is this number of PRBs reserved gor the DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grant in dedicated BWP

**Note: in TRS slots when**[Before CB015668-A]**NRBTS.actTddCsirsOpt = false**[CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = false (note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]****)** or in SSB slots with TRS symbols, or in CSI-IM slots when CSI-IM is rate matched (that is if **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]*****\*)) if FR1 TDD FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), nbPrb is expected to be a multiple of  PRB\_GRANULARITY (see PRB\_GRANULARITY definition, see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).***\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system. It depicts several blocks of varying colors and sizes, representing different types of data or control information. These blocks are arranged vertically and horizontally, with labels and annotations indicating their purpose and relationships.
On the left side, there are three vertical blocks. The leftmost is a thin blue block labeled "CORESET#0". To its right is a wider yellow block labeled "initial BWP". The largest vertical block, colored orange, is labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these vertical blocks, there are horizontal blocks and annotations. At the top, a light orange horizontal block is positioned above a dashed line. To the right of this block, text reads "nbPrb reserved" and "DCI 1\_1 dedicated BWP", with a double-headed arrow indicating a range.
Below this, a dashed line separates it from the next section. A light green horizontal block is positioned above a light purple horizontal block. To the right of these blocks, text reads "PDSCH sub area 1" and "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[1]". A double-headed arrow with the label "DCI 1\_0" is positioned between two dashed lines, indicating a range below the green block and above the purple block.
Further down, below the purple block, text reads "Rach msg2", with a double-headed arrow indicating a range. Below this, another dashed line is labeled "PDSCH sub area 2", with a double-headed arrow extending downwards.
There is also text "rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[1]" positioned to the right, spanning across the area of the green and purple blocks and extending upwards.
The diagram uses color coding to differentiate the blocks: blue for CORESET#0, yellow for initial BWP, orange for dedicated BWP, light green for a portion of PDSCH sub area 1, and light purple for Rach msg2. The dashed lines and arrows are used to denote boundaries, ranges, and relationships between different resource allocations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13231.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For the additional PDSCH sub area (below the CORESET #0), the variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1] , rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1], rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n+1]*do not change\*\*

If the number of PRBs reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Dedicated BWP is selected in the PDSCH sub area below the CORESET #0 without a common channel, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1] =* rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1]  - nbPrb\*\*

For the other PDSCH sub area (above the CORESET #0), the variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] , rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n],* rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n]*do not change*.\*\*\*\*

On the left side, there are three vertical colored bars:
- A thin blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A tall orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these bars, there are several horizontal colored blocks stacked vertically:

These blocks are associated with labels and annotations indicating their function and position within the resource grid.
The text elements in the image are:

- initial BWP
- dedicated BWP
- PDSCH sub area 1
- DCI 1\_0
- Rach msg2
- nbPrb reserved
- DCI 1 1 dedicated BWP
- PDSCH sub area 2
- rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[2]
- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[2]
The diagram shows how different components are allocated. The "PDSCH sub area 1" spans the entire width of the diagram at the top. Below this, "DCI 1\_0" is indicated. Further down, "Rach msg2" is shown. Below "Rach msg2", there's a section labeled "nbPrb reserved" and then "DCI 1 1 dedicated BWP". Finally, "PDSCH sub area 2" is at the bottom, with associated labels "rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[2]" and "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[2]". The colored blocks visually represent the allocation of resources within these defined areas.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13231.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

If the number of PRBs reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Dedicated BWP is selected in the PDSCH sub area below the common channel, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1] =* rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1]  - nbPrb\*\*

*rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1],* rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1]*do not change*.\*\*\*\*
