*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] , rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n],* rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n]*do not change*.\*\*\*\*

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different blocks of resources, represented by colored rectangles, and associated labels and annotations.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Blocks):**
\* **Blue Rectangle:** Labeled "CORESET#0". This likely represents a Control Resource Set (CORESET) used for control signaling.
\* **Yellow Rectangle:** Labeled "initial BWP". This signifies an initial Bandwidth Part (BWP), a mechanism to adapt the bandwidth used by the device.
\* **Orange Rectangle:** Labeled "dedicated BWP". This indicates a dedicated Bandwidth Part, possibly configured for specific data transmission.
\* **Purple Rectangle:** Positioned to the right of the yellow and orange blocks, with no explicit label but aligned with "common Channel".
\* **Green Rectangle:** Positioned below the purple rectangle, also aligned with "common Channel".
\* **Peach Rectangle:** Positioned below the green rectangle, aligned with "nbPrb reserved".
**Right Side (Annotations and Labels):**
\* **"PDSCH sub area 1"**: Indicates a region designated for Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) data, specifically "sub area 1".
\* **"common Channel"**: A label pointing to the purple and green rectangles, suggesting these resources are used for common channels.
\* **"rach msg2 nbPrbDci10"**: This annotation likely refers to Random Access Channel (RACH) message 2, with specific parameters like "nbPrb" (number of Physical Resource Blocks) and "Dci10" (Downlink Control Information type 10). The arrows indicate the vertical extent of this resource allocation.
\* **"rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[2]"**: This is a complex label, possibly indicating remaining resources within CORESET#0 for PDSCH in "sub area 2", with a parameter "[2]". The arrows show the vertical extent.
\* **"startPrbCC"**: This label likely denotes the starting Physical Resource Block (PRB) for a Carrier Component (CC).
\* **"rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[2]"**: Similar to the previous annotation, this likely refers to the size of remaining resources within CORESET#0 for PDSCH in "sub area 2".
\* **"PDSCH sub area 2"**: Another designation for a PDSCH data region, "sub area 2".
\* **"rrmPdschSubAreaSize[2]"**: This annotation likely refers to the size of remaining PDSCH resources in "sub area 2".
\* **"nbPrb reserved"**: Indicates that a certain number of PRBs are reserved.
\* **"DCI 1\_1 dedicated BWP"**: This annotation refers to Downlink Control Information type 1\_1, associated with the dedicated BWP.
\* **"rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[2]"**: This annotation likely refers to the remaining number of PRBs for PDSCH in "sub area 2".
The diagram uses dashed orange lines and arrows to denote the boundaries and extents of different resource allocations and to connect labels to their corresponding graphical elements. The overall structure suggests a hierarchical or segmented allocation of radio resources for different communication functions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13231.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-10**  (ID: `11176304`)

DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in FDD DSS when CAT-M PRBs area present in the slot

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11176315`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176315:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Each time a number of PRBs is reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the dedicated BWP within a PDSCH sub area which has CATM-M PRBs area above or/and below, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of the PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area in this way:

rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] = rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  - nbPrb

The other variables rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n],  rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n]  do not change.

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-11**  (ID: `11176320`)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11176330`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176330:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In SSB slot, each time a number of PRBs is reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Dedicated BWP within a PDSCH sub area above the SSB, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area in this way:

rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] = rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  - nbPrb

where:

nbPrb is this number of PRBs reserved gor the DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grant in dedicated BWP

[CB008927-I] Note: In SSB with TRS symbols, if FR1 TDD FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), nbPrb is expected to be a multiple of  PRB\_GRANULARITY (see PRB\_GRANULARITY definition, see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End CB008927-I]

For the other PDSCH sub area (above the SSB), the variables are not modified

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `11176345`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176345:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In FR1 TDD, in SSB slot, if beam refinement / management is activated (NRCELL.beamSet.nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 = true) [PR769456] and the PRB pooling is not activated (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = false) [End PR769456], if the SSB is configured close to the bottom of the carrier bandwidth and if the CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement are above the SSB (NRCELL.csirsBeamMgmt.csirsBmMgmtSubband = 2 (aboveSSPBCHBlock) (see 5G\_UP\_10892\_replaced([11350748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU-_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall select the PDSCH sub area below the SSB in order to reserve a number of PRBs for a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the dedicated BWP and shall withdraw this number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area in this way:
rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] = rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  - nbPrb
where:
 nbPrb is this number of PRBs reserved gor the DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grant in dedicated BWP
[Before PR769456] For the other PDSCH sub area (above the SSB), the variables are not modified
rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  does not change.
[PR769456] If CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement are below the SSB (NRCELL.csirsBeamMgmt.csirsBmMgmtSubband = 1 (belowSSPBCHBlock)) (and no PRB pooling), gNB-DU shall allocate PRBs in the PDSCH sub area above the SSB. [End PR769456]
 
[PR766701] When rdMinNbPRBsPdschSubArea < 4 PRBs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24729\_replaced([11115147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kprX6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [PR769456] and the PRB pooling is not activated (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = false) [End PR769456], gNB-DU shall not create new PDSCH sub area either between SSB and the CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement or above the CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement (if they are located above the SSB) or below the CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement (if they are located below the SSB).  [End PR766701]
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts several blocks of varying colors and heights, representing different types of resources or bandwidth parts (BWPs).
On the left side, there are vertical bars. The leftmost bar is yellow and labeled "initial BWP". To its right is a taller orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
Above the "dedicated BWP" bar, there are two colored blocks: a teal block and a purple block. A vertical arrow spanning from the top of the teal block to the top of the purple block is labeled "CSI-RS for Beam refinement". A smaller vertical arrow pointing to the purple block is labeled "SSB".
Below the purple block, there is a peach-colored block. Below this, there is a text box containing "PDSCH sub area 1" and "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[1]". A vertical arrow spans from the top of the peach block to the bottom of the purple block, with the label "SSB" positioned between them.
Further down, there is another peach-colored block. Below this block, there is text that reads "nbPrb reserved" and "DCI 1\_1 dedicated BWP". A vertical arrow spans from the top of this lower peach block to the bottom of the "PDSCH sub area 1" block.
To the right of the diagram, there is text "rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[1]" with a double-headed arrow pointing to the area between the top of the lower peach block and the bottom of the purple block.
The diagram uses dashed orange lines to indicate boundaries and relationships between different elements. The overall visual representation suggests a hierarchical or segmented allocation of radio resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13237.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
[PR769456] In FR1 TDD, if beam refinement / management is activated (NRCELL.beamSet.nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 = true)  and the PRB pooling is activated (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = true), gNB-DU shall select one of the 2 PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23683\_replaced([11176637](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23462\_replaced([11176070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in order to reserve a number of PRBs for a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the dedicated BWP and shall withdraw this number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area in this way:
rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] = rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  - nbPrb
where:
 nbPrb is this number of PRBs reserved gor the DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grant in dedicated BWP
For the other PDSCH sub area, the variables are not modified
rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1]  does not change.
 
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated functionalities within a communication system. The diagram uses colored blocks and text labels to represent these components.
On the left side, there are two vertical blocks. The leftmost block is yellow and labeled "initial BWP". To its right is a wider orange block labeled "dedicated BWP".
Above the "dedicated BWP" block, there are two horizontal dashed lines indicating a range. Within this range, there is a light orange rectangular block. To the right of this block, text reads "nbPrb reserved" and "DCI 1\_1 dedicated BWP". A double-headed arrow spans this range.
Below the light orange block, there is another horizontal dashed line. Between this line and the one above it, there is a teal rectangular block. To the left of this block, text reads "PDSCH sub area 1" and "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[1]". A double-headed arrow spans this section.
Further down, there is a purple rectangular block. To the left of this block, text reads "SSB". A double-headed arrow spans the vertical space between the bottom of the teal block and the top of the purple block.
Below the purple block, there is another horizontal dashed line. Between this line and the one above it, there is a double-headed arrow indicating a range. To the left of this arrow, text reads "CSI-RS for Beam refinement".
At the very bottom, there is a horizontal dashed line. Below this line, text reads "PDSCH sub area 2". A double-headed arrow spans the vertical space from the bottom of the diagram up to this dashed line.
To the right of the diagram, spanning from the top of the light orange block down to the bottom of the teal block, there is text that reads "rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[1]". This text is underlined with a red wavy line.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13237.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
[End PR769456]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-13**  (ID: `11176349`)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-13.0-2**  (ID: `12729959`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12729959:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If one PDSCH sub area, or for a PDSCH sub area above the CORESET #0 or the SSB,[CB012623-B]or the area of PRB block muted by IM(refer to [12707684](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))[End CB012623-B], each time a number of PRBs is reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Dedicated BWP(p) within a PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of the PRBs of the dedicated BWP(p) from the number of remaining PRBs for the BWP(p) in this way:

*if p > 1, rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] =* rrrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  - nbPrbBwp(p)\*\*

*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n],* rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n], rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  do not change*. And the variable(s)* rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  of the other(s) dedicated BWP(p) when the DCI 1\_1 grant does not belong to these BWP(p).\*\*\*\*

where:

nbPrbBwp(p) is the number of PRBs with a  DCI 1\_1 in USS mode mapped to the Dedicated BWP(p) within a PDSCH sub area.

**Note: in TRS slots**when **[Before CB015668-A]** **NRBTS.actTddCsirsOpt = false****[CB015668-A]**actTddCsirsOpt = false (note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**, or in CSI-IM slots when CSI-IM is rate matched (that is if **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]*****\*), if FR1 TDD FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), nbPrbBwp(p) is expected to be a multiple of  PRB\_GRANULARITY (see PRB\_GRANULARITY definition, see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).***\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated control and data channels in a cellular communication system. The diagram is organized vertically, with different colored blocks representing different BWPs and their functions.
On the left side, there are several vertical bars representing different BWPs:
- A thin blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A thin yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A wide orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP 1".
- A wide light green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
To the right of these bars, the diagram shows different regions and their associated information:
- An orange rectangular block is positioned above the "dedicated BWP 1" bar, indicating "DCI 1\_1 RAT1 in USS mode For UEs belonging to BWP 1". This block spans a certain vertical range.
- A green rectangular block is positioned above the "dedicated BWP 2" bar, indicating "nbPrbBwp2 reserved For a UE belonging to BWP 2". This block is below the orange block.
- A purple rectangular block is positioned below the green block and partially overlapping with the "dedicated BWP 2" bar, indicating "DCI 1\_0 CSS-mode".
Vertical dashed lines with arrows indicate the extent of these regions and are associated with labels:
- An arrow pointing downwards from the top of the orange block to the top of the green block is labeled "DCI 1\_1 RAT1 in USS mode For UEs belonging to BWP 1".
- An arrow pointing downwards from the top of the green block to the top of the purple block is labeled "nbPrbBwp2 reserved For a UE belonging to BWP 2".
- Below the purple block, there are two labels associated with vertical arrows: "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2".
On the far right, there are two labels with horizontal red wavy lines, indicating parameters:
- "rrmBwpPdschSubAreaSize[1][2]" is positioned at the top, aligned with the top of the green block.
- "rrmNbRemainingBwpPdschSubArea[1][2]" is positioned below the first label, aligned with the bottom of the green block.
The diagram visually represents the allocation and configuration of resources for different types of user equipment (UEs) based on their BWP assignments and operational modes.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13239.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For the additional PDSCH sub area (below the CORESET 0 or the SSB), the variables are not modified

If the number of PRBs reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Dedicated BWP(p) is selected in the PDSCH sub area below the CORESET #0 without a common channel, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved of the dedicated BWP(p) from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area for the BWP(p) in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p] =* rrrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]  - nbPrbBwp(p)\*\*

The variable(s) *rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  of the other(s) dedicated BWP(p) when the DCI 1\_1 grant does not to these BWP(p).*

For the other PDSCH sub area (above the CORESET #0), the variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] , rrrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] , rmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n],* rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n]*do not change*.\*\*\*\*

If the number of PRBs reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Dedicated BWP(p) is selected in the PDSCH sub area below the common channel or the SSB, [CB012623-B]or the area of PRB block muted by IM(refer to [12707684](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))[End CB012623-B], gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved of the dedicated BWP(p) from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area  for the BWP(p)  in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1] =* rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1]  - nbPrb\*\*

*rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1],* rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1]*do not change*. And the variable(s) *rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]  of the other(s) dedicated BWP(p) when the DCI 1\_1 grant does not belong to these BWP(p).*\*\*\*\*

For the other PDSCH sub area (above the common channel or the SSB), the variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] , rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n],* rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n] , rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]*do not change*.\*\*\*\*

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-14.0-3**  (ID: `13241919`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
13241919:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If NRCELL.ueConnectionEstablishmentMode is set to useInitialBwp, PDSCH carrying SRBs frames can be allocated with DCI 1\_1 grants within the Initial BWP which is configured as a dedicated BWP (BWP 0). So gNB-DU shall handle the initial BWP as a dedicated BWP(p=0). The assumption is that the initial BWP is bottom aligned with the other dedicated BWPs.
if multi BWP, Initial BWP can be larger than the PS BWP-3.
If one PDSCH sub area (see note (*) below), or for a PDSCH sub area above the CORESET #0 or the SSB, each time a number of PRBs is reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Initial BWP configured as a dedicated BWP(0) within a PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of the PRBs of the dedicated BWP(0) from the number of remaining PRBs for the BWP(p) in this way:*if p = 0, rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][0] = *rrrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  - nbPrbInitBwp(0)*rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n], *rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n], rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  do not change*. And the variable(s) **for p >1, rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  of the other(s) dedicated BWP(p) if multi BWP*****where:
 nbPrbInitBwp(0) is the number of PRBs with a  DCI 1\_1 in USS mode mapped to the Initial Bwp(0) within a PDSCH sub area.
Note (*) : if there is one PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall handle the Initial BWP configured as a dedicated BWP(0) like a multi BWP configuration, that is there are 2 dedicated BWPs: the dedicated BWP and BWP(0).
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and control regions within a communication system. The diagram uses colored rectangles to represent these elements.
On the left side, there are three vertical colored bars. The leftmost is a thin blue rectangle labeled "CORESET#0". To its right is a yellow rectangle labeled "initial BWP". The widest and tallest bar is orange, labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of the "dedicated BWP", there are two horizontal yellow rectangles. The upper one is labeled "nbPrbInitBwp(0)" and "DCI 1\_1 SRB1". The lower one is a purple rectangle labeled "SSB".
The diagram also shows two vertical regions labeled "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2", separated by a horizontal dashed line. Arrows indicate the extent of these sub-areas. Dashed orange lines are used to denote boundaries and relationships between different elements.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13241.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
The CORESET 0 (or CORESET X with X>0) may not be completely contained by the smallest BWP (like PS BWP) and overlaps with the PRBs above these smallest BWPs (for instance when NRCELL.actInitialBWPFlexibility is set to true). In the upper PDSCH sub area,  gNB-DU shall withdraw any PRBs reservation and update the remaining PRBs of the Initial BWP, the PS BWP, the CORESET 0 and the other dedicated BWPs.
Notes:
- in this case, the SSB has to completely contained by all the dedicated BWPs, including the smallest dedicated BWPs.
- the size of Init BWP may be equal to the largest BWP1 or BWP2 too if BWP2 is UE capable BWP and is expected to the same size than an existing dedicated BWP
- the size of several small BWPs (and not only PS BWP) can be lower than the size of the CORESET 0
In this example below PDSCH sub area 1 is the upper PDSCH sub area and {PS BWP(3), CORESET 0, Init BWP(0) , BWP(2), BWP(1)} is the list of the dedicated BWP and CORESET 0 in increasing order of their respective size within the PDSCH sub area 1 that is:  rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[1][3] < rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[1] < rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[1][0] <= rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[1][2]
 < rrmPdschSubAreaSize[1]
[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their sizes within a PDSCH sub-area. The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent these BWPs.
On the left side, there are several vertical bars with labels:
- A light blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP-1".
- A green bar labeled "BWP-2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
To the right of these bars, there are annotations and arrows indicating sizes and relationships:
- An orange rectangle is shown below "PS BWP-3" and to the left of "nbPRBs PDSCH".
- Text "nbPRBs PDSCH" is positioned below the orange rectangle.
Several vertical arrows point from different levels of the diagram to text annotations on the right. These annotations describe the sizes of various BWPs within "PDSCH sub area 1":
- "Size of CORESET 0 in PDSCh sub area 1" with a corresponding variable "rrmSizeCoresetOlnPdschSubArea[1]".
- "size of PS BWP(3) in PDSCH sub area 1" with a corresponding variable "rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea [1][3]".
- "size of BWP(2) in PDSCH sub area 1" with a corresponding variable "rrmSizeBwpInPdsch SubArea [1][2]".
- "size of Init BWP(0) in PDSCH sub area 1" with a corresponding variable "rrmSizeBwpInRdschSubArea[1][0]".
Finally, there is a label "PDSCH sub area 1" with a corresponding variable "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[1]" on the right side of the diagram.
The diagram uses dashed orange lines to indicate horizontal boundaries or levels.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-11141142.png[/image\_path][/image-1]**Note: in TRS slots when**[Before CB015668-A]**NRBTS.actTddCsirsOpt = false **[CB015668-A]** actTddCsirsOpt = false(note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**, or in CSI-IM slots when CSI-IM is rate matched (that is if **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]*****\*) if FR1 TDD FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), gNB-DU shall grant any PDSCH grant as a multiple of a PRB granularity (PRB\_GRANULARITY definition see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*\*
so nbPrbBwp(p) is expected to be a multiple of  PRB\_GRANULARITY (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**Note: there is no DCI 1\_0 scheduled in TRS slots (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated configurations in a communication system. The diagram uses colored bars of varying heights to represent these BWPs.
On the left side, there are several vertical bars:
- A dark blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A tall orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A tall green bar labeled "BWP-2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
To the right of these bars, there are horizontal dashed lines and text indicating different configurations and sub-areas.
- A yellow horizontal bar is positioned above the "PS BWP-3" bar.
- Text associated with this yellow bar reads "nbPrbInitBwp(0)" and "DCI 1\_1 SRB1".
- A vertical arrow points from the top of the "dedicated BWP" bar down to the bottom of the yellow horizontal bar, with the label "PDSCH sub area 1" to its right.
Below the "PS BWP-3" bar, there is a light green horizontal bar.
- Text associated with this green bar reads "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0".
- A vertical arrow points from the bottom of the "BWP-2" bar down to the bottom of the light green horizontal bar, with the label "PDSCH sub area 2" to its right.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs are allocated and how they relate to control information (DCI) and data transmission areas (PDSCH sub areas). The heights of the bars likely represent the size or duration of these BWPs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13241.002.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
For the additional PDSCH sub area (below the CORESET 0 or the SSB), the variables are not modified
If the number of PRBs reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Initial BWP configured as a dedicated BWP(0) is selected in the PDSCH sub area below the CORESET #0 without a common channel, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved of the dedicated BWP(0) from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area for the BWP(p) in this way:
*for p =0, rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][0] = *rrrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][0]  - nbPrbInitBwp(0)**The variable(s)* rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  of the other(s) dedicated BWP(p) if muti BWP configured.*For the other PDSCH sub area (above the CORESET #0), the variables are not modified*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] , rrrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] , rmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n],  *rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n]*do not change\*.****If the number of PRBs reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Initial BWP configured as a dedicated BWP(0) is selected in the PDSCH sub area below the common channel or the SSB, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved of the dedicated BWP(p) from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area  for the BWP(p)  in this way:*for p=0, rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1][0] = *rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1][0] - nbPrbInitBwp(0)*rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1],  *rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n+1]*do not change*. And the variable(s)* rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]  of the other(s) dedicated BWP(p) if multi BWP configured.***\**
For the other PDSCH sub area (above the common channel), the variables are not modified
*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] , rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n],* rrmNbRemainingCoreset0PdschSubArea[n] , rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]*do not change*.**\*\*

From left to right, the first bar is a narrow blue rectangle labeled "CORESET#0". Next to it is a yellow bar labeled "Initial BWP". Following that is a tall orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP". Then, a green bar labeled "BWP-2" is shown. A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3" comes next. To the right of "PS BWP-3", there is a purple bar. Below the purple bar, there is a yellow bar.
There are also annotations and labels indicating different areas and configurations. An arrow on the right side points upwards and is labeled "PDSCH sub area 1". **3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-15**  (ID: `11176383`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in TRS slots with additional blanked PRBs area at TRS boundary

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-15.0-1**  (ID: `11176392`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176392:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In a TRS slot, when an additional blanking PRBs area is setup at TRS boundary (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30501\_replaced([11175903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU has created 2 PDSCH sub areas: one above the additional blanking PRBs area and one below and initialized the start PRB and the size for the both PDSCH sub areas. When a SSB is scheduled in a TRS slot and if the SSB is not adjacent or overlapping to the additional blanking PRBs area, gNB-DU shall create 3 PDSCH sub areas: one above the additional blanking PRBs area, one below the SSB and between the SSB and the additional blanking PRBs area.

If required, gNB-DU may apply the PRB\_GRANULARITY to the startPRB and the size of the both PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note1: in the case of SSB in a TRS slot, if gNB-DU applies the PRB\_GRANULARITY, one of PDSCH sub area (below or above the SSB) may not exist because not enough PRBs modulo PRB\_GRANULARITY.

When a PDSCH scrambled with a DCI 1\_1 RAT1 and belonging to a BWP(p) is scheduled in a PDSCH sub area (n), gNB-DU shall check if the constraints are fullfilled (including the PRB\_GRANULARITY if needed) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and if it is the case, shall update the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP(p) in the PDSCH sub area (n): rmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] in withdrawing the number of PRBs reserved of the DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grant.

Note2: in TRS slots, there is no DCI 1\_0 scheduled, so the variables rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]., rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] are non significant (and set to 0).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely a cellular network. It depicts different types of resource blocks (PRBs) and their allocation across various sub-areas and bandwidth parts (BWPs).
The diagram is structured vertically, with different colored bars representing allocated or reserved resources. Horizontal dashed lines and arrows indicate the extent of these allocations and the relationships between different elements. Text labels describe the purpose and characteristics of these resource blocks.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Vertical Bars (Left to Right):**
\* **Red Bar (Bottom):** Appears to be a base allocation or a boundary.
\* **Blue Bar:** Labeled "CORESET#0".
\* **Yellow Bar:** Labeled "initial BWP".
\* **Orange Bar:** Labeled "dedic d BWP 1". This bar extends significantly upwards.
\* **Green Bar:** Labeled "BWP-2".
\* **Light Blue Bar:** Labeled "PS BWP-3".
\* **Purple Bar:** Represents "52 PRBS TRS".
\* **Gray Bar:** Located below the purple bar, its purpose is not explicitly labeled but is associated with "nbPrb reserved" and "DCI 1\_1 BWP3".
\* **Light Orange Bar (Top Right):** Represents "nbPrb reserved" and "DCI 1\_1 BWP1".
**Text Labels and Annotations:**
\* **"PDSCH sub area 2"**: Indicates a specific region for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) allocation.
\* **"rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubAreaSize[2][1]"**: A parameter related to the size of a BWP within PDSCH sub-area 2.
\* **"PRBs blanking"**: Denotes resource blocks that are intentionally not used or are made unavailable.
\* **"rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[2][1]"**: A parameter indicating remaining PRBs in PDSCH sub-area 2 for a BWP.
\* **"nbPrb reserved"**: Indicates a number of PRBs that are reserved.
\* **"DCI 1\_1 BWP1"**: Refers to a specific type of Downlink Control Information (DCI) format for BWP 1.
\* **"rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[2]"**: A parameter indicating the starting PRB for PDSCH sub-area 2.
\* **"additional blanked PRBs area (PDSCH)"**: An area within PDSCH where additional PRBs are blanked.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 1"**: Another region for PDSCH allocation.
\* **"rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[1][3]"**: A parameter related to the size of a BWP within PDSCH sub-area 1.
\* **"rrmNbRemainingBwpInPrbsPdschSubArea[1][3]"**: A parameter indicating remaining PRBs in PDSCH sub-area 1 for a BWP.
\* **"DCI 1\_1 BWP3"**: Refers to a specific type of DCI format for BWP 3.
\* **"Low edge"**: Indicates the lower boundary of a resource allocation.
\* **"rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[1]"**: A parameter indicating the starting PRB for PDSCH sub-area 1.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and control information (DCI) are mapped onto physical resource blocks, with specific areas designated for PDSCH and indicating blanked or reserved resources. The parameters like "rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea" and "rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea" suggest a dynamic resource management system.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13243.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Figure 1: DCI1\_1 PDSCH granted in PDSCH sub area 2 BWP(1) and another one in PDSCH sub area 1 in BWP(3)

The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent these blocks, with text labels and arrows indicating their purpose and relationships.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Vertical Bars (representing resource blocks):**
\* **Red Bars:** These appear at the top and bottom, and also as a horizontal bar connecting some of the vertical blocks. They seem to represent "PRBs blanking" or areas where resources are not allocated for data transmission.
\* **Yellow Bar:** Labeled "d BWP 1", this is a significant block, likely representing a Bandwidth Part (BWP) for data transmission.
\* **Green Bar:** A smaller green bar is positioned above the "dedicated BWP 1" label, possibly indicating a specific type of resource within that BWP.
\* **Blue Bar:** Labeled "CORESET#0", this is a distinct block, likely representing a Control-Resource-Set for control channel reception.
\* **Light Yellow Bar:** Labeled "initial BWP", this block is adjacent to CORESET#0 and also appears to be a Bandwidth Part.
\* **Orange Bar:** Labeled "dedicated", this bar is positioned between the "initial BWP" and "BWP-2".
\* **Green Bar:** Labeled "BWP-2", this is another Bandwidth Part.
\* **Light Blue Bar:** Labeled "PS BWP-3", this is a third Bandwidth Part, possibly with a "Physical Signal" designation.
\* **Purple Bar:** This bar is positioned to the right of the BWPs and has no direct label on it, but it is associated with the "SSB" label.
\* **Light Blue Bar (smaller):** Located to the right of the purple bar, this is associated with the "SSB" label.
\* **Green Rectangle:** A small green rectangle is positioned below the light blue bar and to the right of the purple bar.
**Text Labels and Annotations:**
\* **"d BWP 1"**: Indicates a Bandwidth Part 1.
\* **"CORESET#0"**: Refers to Control-Resource-Set 0.
\* **"initial BWP"**: Denotes the initial Bandwidth Part.
\* **"dedicated"**: Suggests a dedicated resource allocation.
\* **"BWP-2"**: Indicates Bandwidth Part 2.
\* **"PS BWP-3"**: Indicates Physical Signal Bandwidth Part 3.
\* **"52 PRBS TRS"**: "PRBS" likely stands for Physical Resource Blocks. "TRS" could refer to Transmission Reception Slot or similar. The number 52 indicates a quantity of PRBs.
\* **"SSB"**: Stands for Synchronization Signal Block.
\* **"PRBs blanking"**: Indicates areas where PRBs are not used. There are two such annotations, one at the top and one at the bottom.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 3"**: Refers to a sub-area within the PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) allocation.
\* **"additional blanked PRBs area (PDSCH)"**: Indicates an area of blanked PRBs specifically related to PDSCH.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 2"**: Another sub-area of the PDSCH.
\* **"rrmNbRemainingBwpInPrbsPdschSubArea[1][2]"**: This is a technical parameter or variable name, likely related to remaining Bandwidth Part resources in a PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 1"**: The first sub-area of the PDSCH.
\* **"rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[1][2]"**: Another technical parameter, likely related to the size of a Bandwidth Part within a PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"nbPrb reserved DCI 1\_1 BWP2"**: Indicates a number of PRBs reserved for DCI (Downlink Control Information) in BWP2.
\* **"Low edge"**: Refers to the lower boundary of a particular section.
\* **"rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[1]"**: A technical parameter indicating the start of PRBs for PDSCH sub-area 1.
**Arrows:**
\* Vertical arrows with double arrowheads indicate the extent of different areas or sub-areas (e.g., PDSCH sub area 3, PDSCH sub area 2, SSB).
\* Dashed orange lines connect labels to specific regions or boundaries within the diagram.
\* A blue line connects "rrmNbRemainingBwpInPrbsPdschSubArea[1][2]" to a specific point on the diagram, likely indicating a measurement or reference.
The diagram visually represents how different types of resources (control, data, synchronization signals) are organized and potentially allocated within a given frequency band, with specific areas designated for blanking or reserved for control information. The concept of Bandwidth Parts (BWPs) is central, showing how different BWPs can be defined and utilized.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13243.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-16**  (ID: `12729942`)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-4.0-16.0-1**  (ID: `12729943`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12729943:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In a slot with PRBs muted due to interference mitigation(IM, refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), each time a number of PRBs is reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Dedicated BWP within a PDSCH sub area above the PRBs muted by IM, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] =* rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  - nbPrb\*\*

where:

nbPrb is this number of PRBs reserved gor the DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grant in dedicated BWP

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system. It features several colored blocks representing different types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and sub-areas.
On the left side, there are three vertical blocks:
- A blue block labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow block labeled "initial BWP".
- A large orange block labeled "dedicated BWP".
To the right of these blocks, there are horizontal dashed lines and text annotations indicating different resource regions and their properties:
- The top section shows an orange block, likely representing reserved resources. Text above it reads "nbPrb reserved" and "DCI 1\_1 dedicated BWP". A double-headed arrow indicates the extent of this region.
- Below the orange block, there is a region labeled "PDSCH sub area 1". A double-headed arrow spans this area, with text "rrmPdschSubAreaSze[1]" below it. Another annotation "rrmNBRemainingPrbsPdschSubarea[1]" is placed to the right of this region.
- In the middle, a blue block is shown, representing a portion of the allocated resources.
- Below the blue block, there is a region labeled "PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs)". A double-headed arrow indicates the extent of this muted region.
- The bottom section shows a region labeled "PDSCH sub area 2". A double-headed arrow indicates the extent of this area.
The diagram visually represents how different bandwidth parts and sub-areas are defined and potentially utilized for data transmission and control signaling, with specific mentions of reserved and muted resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08061451.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For the additional PDSCH sub area, the variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1]*does not change\*\*

If the number of PRBs reserved with a DCI 1\_1 in USS mode in the Dedicated BWP is selected in the PDSCH sub area below the PRBs muted by IM, gNB-DU shall withdraw the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP from the number of remaining PRBs for the PDSCH sub area in this way:

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1] =* rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1]  - nbPrb\*\*

For the other PDSCH sub area, the variables are not modified

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]*does not change*.*\*\*

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and sub-areas within a Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH).
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
\* **Left Side:** Three vertical bars represent different BWPs:
\* A cyan bar labeled "CORESET#0".
\* A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
\* A tall orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
\* **Right Side:** The diagram shows two horizontal dashed lines dividing the PDSCH into sub-areas, with associated labels and arrows indicating sizes or quantities.
\* **PDSCH sub area 1:** This is the upper section. An arrow indicates its total size.
\* **PRB muted due to IM (nbPrbsIM PRBs):** Below "PDSCH sub area 1", a blue rectangular block represents a portion of the PDSCH that is muted due to Interference Management (IM). An arrow indicates the size of this muted portion.
\* **PDSCH sub area 2:** This is the lower section. An arrow indicates its total size.
\* **rrmNBRemainingPrbsPdschSubarea[2]:** To the right of "PDSCH sub area 2", this text likely refers to the remaining number of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) in sub-area 2, possibly related to a Remaining Minimum Requirement (RRM) or a specific configuration.
\* **nbPrb reserved DCI 1\_1 dedicated BWP:** Below the muted PRBs and within "PDSCH sub area 2", an orange rectangular block represents reserved PRBs for a dedicated BWP, associated with a Downlink Control Information (DCI) format 1\_1.
\* **rrmPdschSubAreaSize[2]:** At the bottom, this text likely refers to the size of sub-area 2, possibly related to RRM. A diagonal line connects the bottom of the reserved PRBs to this label, suggesting a relationship or a calculation.
The diagram visually represents how the total PDSCH resource is divided into different sub-areas and how certain PRBs might be utilized or reserved for specific purposes, such as CORESET, initial BWP, dedicated BWP, and reserved PRBs for DCI, while also accounting for PRBs muted due to IM.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08061453.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

---

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-5 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation step3: constraints in PDSCH sub areas (ID: 11176397)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11176401`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
introduction and variables

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12730008`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
gNB-DU shall follow several constraints for any PRB reservation in PDSCH sub areas**and for each dedicated BWP if multi BWP which are computed for each PDSCH sub area.**

refer for variables definition in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 (or in the PRBs area in the CORESET X (with X>0) allowed for the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode) within a PDSCH sub area n. If there is no PRB belonging to the CORESET #0, it is equal to 0. Otherwise it is computed in this way:

nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n)**= rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] - rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]**

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) witin the PDSCH sub area n. It does not contain the PRBs reserved to PDSCHs granted in the CORESET #0 (or the PRBs area in the CORESET X (with X>0) allowed for the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode). It does not contain the PRBs reserved to PDSCHs granted in the lower BWP.

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) = *rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]\*\*\*\*

for p > 1 nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) = *rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] -* rmNbRemainingBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]\*\*

If NRCELL.ueConnectionEstablishmentMode is set to useInitialBwp, nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(0,n) = *rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][0] -* rmNbRemainingBwpInPdschSubArea[n][0]\*\*

Note that the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP can overlap the CORESET #0 (or the PRB area of the CORESET X (with X>0) used for DCI 1\_0 PRB allocation if they are not mapped to the CORESET #0), that is nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) > *rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] - rrmSizeCoreset0PdschSubArea[n]*

numPdschPrbInSsbSlots: the total number of PRBs that can be scheduled in SSB slot defined in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2040\_replaced([11277571](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpN8BfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

numPdschPrbInTrsSlots: the total number of PRBs that can be scheduled in a slot with at least one symbol for CSI-RS for tracking defined in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2041\_replaced([11277590](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpN8B_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

nbPrbsSSB is the number of blanked PRBs by the SSB (see 5G\_UP\_10925\_replaced([11350670](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt6vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB012623-B]nbPrbsMutedIMFinal is the number of muted PRBs by interference mitigation.(refer to [12719425: Initialization of the PDSCH sub areas for SSB interference mitigation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EHEPAWwlEfCRGai1q1VTeQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and refer to [12709089: Basic functionality](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_pAtj4GOsEfCtwYrh0KQ_DA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for nbPrbsMutedIMFinal). If PRB muting by IM overlaps with common channel, it only counts the PRBs not covered by common channel. if PRB muting by IM is separated with common channel, nbPrbsMutedIMFinal = nbPrbsIM. If PRB muting by IM completely contained within common channel, nbPrbsMutedIMFinal =0.[End CB012623-B]

Each time a number of PRBs is reserved in the dedicated BWP, gNB-DU shall check the constraints:

* about the maximum number of PRBs within a PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24759\_replaced([11176409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* about the total number of PRBs limitation due to PRB pooling (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24760\_replaced([11176430](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* about the maximum number allowed of PRBs in SSB and TRS slots (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24761\_replaced([11176571](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-k_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If PRBs reservation do not fullfill these contraints, gNB-DU shall either reduce the PRBs reservation in order to fullfill these constraints, either re schedule the UE in the following slot.

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11176409`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
constraints on number of PRBs on PDSCH sub areas

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12816628`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12816628:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
gNB-DU shall not provide more PRBs than the PDSCH sub area contains.
- gNB-DU shall check that the PRBs reservation shall not exceed the number of remaining PRBs in the PDSCH sub area in mono BWP or the largest BWP if multi BWP
(1) *rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] >=0*
(1bis) *rmNbRemainingBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] >=0*
- gNB-DU shall check that the PRBs reservation shall not exceed the number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET #0
(2) *rrmNbRemainingCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] >= 0*
- for each PDSCH sub area,**gNB-DU shall check that the the sum of the number of PRBs reserved in all the dedicated BWP(p) and in the CORESET #0 does not exceed the size of the PDSCH sub area.**
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]*****\* and in a CSI-IM slot,
(3)* *[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the letters "V" and "n" in a dark, possibly metallic, font. The letters are outlined in a lighter color, and there are hints of blue and orange hues within the letters, suggesting a reflective or iridescent surface. The background is dark and out of focus.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13248.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0],  [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. It shows a summation symbol $\sum$ with the index $(p)$ below it. **3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11176430`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12730138`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12730138:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
if slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true', gNB-DU shall not provide a number of PRBs in each slot above the total amount usable DL PRB granted to the cell (numAllocCellPrb) by the BBRM (see 5G\_UP\_11023\_replaced([11350907](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjGPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for all the PDSCH sub areas.
In the SSB slot, gNB-DU shall check that the sum of the number of PRBs reserved for all the dedicated BWP (p) of all the PDSCH sub areas and the PRBs of the SSB shall not exceed the PRBs limitation provided by BBRM
if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_RAN\_OP\_2268\_replaced([11692786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3pqm_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is true **(see 5G\_RAN\_OP\_2387\_replaced([11692644](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Yf9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg)))**
(1)  [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation symbol followed by indices "p,n" and then the text "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)". The summation symbol is a large sigma. The indices "p,n" are written below and to the right of the sigma. The text "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP" is written in a standard font, and "(p,n)" is enclosed in parentheses.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13251.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] + [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by 'n' as the index. The terms being summed are 'nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13251.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]  + nbPrbsSSB + numPrbCsiRsBm <=  min(numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth)
where dlBandwidth is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
otherwise
(1) [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation symbol followed by indices "p,n" and then the text "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)". The summation symbol is a large sigma. The indices "p,n" are written below and to the right of the sigma. The text "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP" is written in a standard font, and "(p,n)" is enclosed in parentheses.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13251.001.png[/image\_path][/image-2]  + [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by 'n' as the index. The terms being summed are 'nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13251.003.png[/image\_path][/image-3]  + nbPrbsSSB + numPrbCsiRsBm <= numAllocCellPrb
where**:**
 - numPrbCsiRsBm is the number of PRBs of CSI-RS for Beam Management for Beam Refinement: it is equal to 0 is if NRCELL.beamSet.nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 = false (no Beam refinement) otherwise refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2667\_replaced([11302851](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqiKqfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10892\_replaced([11350748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU-_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**.**
**[CB012623-B]**
In the slot with PRB muting by IM(refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), gNB-DU shall check that the sum of the number of PRBs reserved for all the dedicated BWP (p) of all the PDSCH sub areas and the PRBs finally muted(excluding PRBs overlapping with common channel) by IM shall not exceed the PRBs limitation provided by BBRM
if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_RAN\_OP\_2268\_replaced([11692786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3pqm_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is true **(see 5G\_RAN\_OP\_2387\_replaced([11692644](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Yf9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg)))**
(3)  [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation symbol followed by indices "p,n" and then the text "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)". The summation symbol is a large sigma. The indices "p,n" are written below and to the right of the sigma. The text "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP" is written in a standard font, and "(p,n)" is enclosed in parentheses.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13251.001.png[/image\_path][/image-4] + [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. where dlBandwidth is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- otherwise in the other slots, gNB-DU shall check that the sum of the number of layer PRBs reserved for all the dedicated BWP(p) of all the PDSCH sub areas and the layer PRBs allocated in the CORESET #0(or X with X>0)(of all the PDSCH sub areas if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled in the slot) shall not exceed the layer PRBs limitation provided by BBRM

**3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-4**  (ID: `11176544`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
constraints of PRB limitation due to Power boost on PDSCH sub areas

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176566`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176566:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If there is an eligible HARQ retransmission for power boost in the slot, FD resource availability check shall also include the condition that the sum of FD resources used  for PRBs for PDSCH allocation of all the UEs and PRBs for Power boosting  the eligible retransmission is less than rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs. 
   Eligibility of HARQ retransmission for power boost defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 25136.
   Definition of PRBs for PDSCH allocation for a UE and PRBs for Power boosting is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24935\_replaced([11185393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
   rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs  defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced([11177271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
- in the SSB slot, gNB-DU shall check that the sum of the number of PRBs reserved for all the dedicated BWP (p) of all the PDSCH sub areas and the PRBs of the SSB shall not exceed the rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs 
 
(1) [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by subscripts "p,n". The term being summed is "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)". The expression is written in a standard mathematical font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13253.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]   [PR782200] + [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation, denoted by the Greek letter sigma ($\Sigma$), with the index 'n' below it. The term being summed is "nbPrbsCoresetOBWP(n)". The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13253.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]  [End PR782200] + [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation, denoted by the Greek letter sigma ($\Sigma$), with the index $n$ below it. To the right of the sigma symbol, there is a function named `nbPrbsRbg` which takes the argument $n$ in parentheses. The entire expression is `$\sum_{n} nbPrbsRbg(n)$`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13253.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]  + numPrbCsiRsBm + powerBoostPrbs   <=
rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs 
where:
- powerBoostPrbs is PRBs used to power boost all the eligible UEs in the slot[As explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25138\_replaced([11185420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3t_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))].These PRBs are from rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced([11177271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which can have both granted and non granted resources.
Note: p and n indicate BWP, and PDSCH sub-area, respectively.
- numPrbCsiRsBm is the number of PRBs of CSI-RS for Beam Mangement for Beam Refinement: it is equal to 0 is if NRCELL.beamSet.nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 = false (no Beam refinement) otherwise refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2667\_replaced([11302851](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqiKqfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10892\_replaced([11350748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uboU-_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
- otherwise in the other slots, gNB-DU shall check that the sum of the number of PRBs reserved for all the dedicated BWP(p) of all the PDSCH sub areas and the PRBs allocated in the CORESET #0   (or X with X>0)  (of all the PDSCH sub areas if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled in the slot) shall not exceed the rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs 
(2)  [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by subscripts "p,n". The term being summed is "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)". The expression is written in a standard mathematical font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13253.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] + [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation denoted by the Greek letter Sigma ($\Sigma$) with a subscript 'n'. This is followed by the term 'nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n)'. The entire expression appears to be in a sans-serif font. The background is a light, slightly textured color, possibly white or off-white, with some subtle shading. The text is in a dark color, likely black or dark gray, and is slightly blurred, suggesting it might be a low-resolution image or a screenshot of a document.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13253.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]   + [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation denoted by the Greek letter sigma ($\Sigma$). The subscript of the summation is 'n'. The term being summed is 'nbPrbsRbg(n)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13253.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]  + powerBoostPrbs <= rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs
where nbPrbsRbg(n) is the number of PRBs of the RBGs of the dynamicswitch RAT0 in the PDSCH sub area n  (see pre booked RBG bitmap in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24772\_replaced([11175933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if there is a dynamicswitch RAT0 (as retransmission) in the slot.

**3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-5**  (ID: `11176571`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
constraints on SSB / TRS on PDSCH sub areas

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-5.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11176605`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176605:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
[Before CB008927-H][Before CB008927-D][CB008927-B]
gNB-DU shall not exceed the maximum number of PRBs in the dedicated BWP in SSB and TRS slots:
- in a SSB slot, without TRS. 
(1) nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) <= numPdschPrbInSsbSlots
- in a TRS slot.
(2)  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) <= numPdschPrbInTrsSlots
- in a SSB slot with TRS.
(3)  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) <= min(numPdschPrbInSsbSlots, numPdschPrbInTrsSlots)
[End CB008927-B]
[CB008927-D]
gNB-DU shall not exceed the maximum number of PRBs in all the dedicated BWP(p) allowed in SSB and TRS slots:
- in a SSB slot, without TRS.
(1)  [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by the subscript 'p'. The term being summed is 'nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p, n)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13255.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] <= numPdschPrbInSsbSlots
- in a TRS slot.
(2)   [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by the subscript 'p'. ###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-6 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation step3bis: PDSCH sub area creation checking when a number of PRBs for a DCI 1_0 in CSS mode is requested to be reserved  for the first time (ID: 11176609)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `12816646`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12816646:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In order to allow the scheduling of **a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode** which requests to reserve a number of PRBs in the CORESET #0 or in the CORESET#X (with X>0)**for the first time in the slot (and then a new PDSCH sub area can be created), gNB-DU shall start the computation of the size of the PDSCH area which is candidate to contain the CORESET #0 or the common PRB area between the CORESET 0 and the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*****\*
gNB-DU shall apply this check too
- when a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) without LTE CAT-M 
- when LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true) and at least one LTE CAT-M PRBs area is present in the slot, in the PDSCH sub area between the lowest PRB available at the bottom of the carrier bandwidth and the PRB below the lowest blanked PRB of the lower CAT-M PRBs area
newSizePdschSubArea* = rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n] -  deltaCS0\*
where
When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,** [Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true, NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true **[CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator)** [End CB015668-A]***\** *and in CSI-IM slot,
 
deltaCS0 =* (CEIL(startPrbCoreset0\*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY) if startPrbCoreset0 is higher or equal than startPrbCoresetCom**
 deltaCS0 = *(CEIL(startPrbCoresetCom*/ PRB\_GRANULARITY) \* PRB\_GRANULARITY)  if startPrbCoresetCom  is higher than startPrbCoreset**else:
deltaCS0 = startPrbCoreset0 if startPrbCoreset0 is higher or equal than startPrbCoresetCom
deltaCS0 = startPrbCoresetCom  if startPrbCoresetCom  is higher than startPrbCoreset0
Otherwise deltaCS0 is defined and computed in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23460\_replaced([11175983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
startPrbCoresetCom is the lowest PRB of the CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
 nbPrbDCI10 is the number of PRBs for the RAT1 PDSCH grant scrambled with DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode.
 
nbPrbsDedicatedBWP**, nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p) are the number of reserved PRBS in PDSCH sub area for single dedicated BWP and for multiple dedicated BWP when p >1 which are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**In order to validate the creation of another PDSCH sub area due to reservation of PRBS for a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode, the sum of already reserved PRBs shall not exceed the new size of the existing PDSCH sub area which will contain the CORESET #0 :
if multiBWP,
 
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. It shows a summation symbol $\sum$ with the index $(p)$ below it. To the right of the summation symbol is the term $nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p)$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13257.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] + nbPrbDCI10  <= newSizePdschSubArea
 
If this condition is fullfilled, gNB-DU shall continue the creation of the PDSCH sub area. Otherwise gNB-DU shall keep the existing PDSCH sub area as before, shall not create a new PDSCH sub area, shall not allocate a DCI 1\_0 in the slot and shall postpone it to the next slot.
