###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-7 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation FD resource tracking within multiple dedicated BWPs while resource reservation in respective PDSCH sub-areas (ID: 11176627)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11176632`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
The gNB-DU shall apply the following generic rules for resource tracking when there are multiple dedicated BWPs configured in the cell during PDSCH sub-area assignment:

- The size of each dedicated BWP ‘p’ within a sub-area ‘n’ is given by ‘rmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]’ when p > 1 and is initialized as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- When there is any reservation made in a BWP in a PDSCH sub-area ‘n’ the remaining PRBs in the BWP(p) is given ‘rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]’ and is calculated as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23464\_replaced([11176110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Only the number of PRBs reserved for a UE which belongs to this BWP(p) contributes towards this variable.

- The PRBs that belong to the CORESET#0 nested within each BWP(p) within a PDSCH sub-area(n) is common to all the BWPs and is given by ‘rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]’ and is initialized as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23464\_replaced([11176110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case there are reservation due to common channels (SIB/OSI/Paging) or initial BWP transmission (RA-msg2/SRB0/msg4/DCI\_1\_0) towards these PRBs, this size is updated and the remaining PRBs is given by ‘rmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]’. Note that the ‘rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]’ does not include the unused PRBs towards the CORESET#0 part in this BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub-area(n) as these are tracked by the above mentioned ‘rmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]’.

- Thus, effectively at any point of time, the total number of PRBs that can be used in the BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub-area(n) shall be given by ‘rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]’ + ‘rmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]’.

- When there are multiple dedicated BWPs (p>1) configured and given as BWP(i) where i ∈{1..p} and size of BWP(i) increase from p..1, each time a reservation is made for a UE in a BWP(i) within a PDSCH sub-area(n) where n>0, the remaining PRBs of all the nested BWP(j) where j ∈ {1..p-1} and sizeOf(j)>sizeOf(i) given by ‘rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][j]’ are also updated along with the remaining PRBs of the BWP(i) given by ‘rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][i]’.

-     The gNB-DU shall always track the exclusive resources within a BWP(i) (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4507\_replaced([11181223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). While reserving PRBs say 'N', in BWP(i) such that i ∈{1..p} if the amount of PRBs to be reserved is greater than the number of exclusive resources for the BWP(i), then corresponding number of resources given by (N - number of exclusive resources of BWP(i)) shall also be deducted from the immediate smaller nested BWP(j) such that j ∈ {i+1...p}. Rationale: This ascertains that the reservation is made from the respective exclusive PRBs first in each BWP(p) when the PDSCH sub-area(n) chosen contains the exclusive resources for the BWP(p).

---

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-8 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation PDSCH sub-area assignment rules for non MU-MIMO users (ID: 11176637)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `13371880`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
The gNB-DU shall assign each UE from the to-be-scheduled list, to a PDSCH sub-area, both during the TD (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and FD scheduler phases (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) whenever the FD resource reservation is made.

For each UE the PRB allocation happens at the end of FD resource distribution/reservation phase for the users in the respectively assigned PDSCH sub-areas.

The gNB-DU shall apply the following rules during PDSCH sub-area assignment:

- If the PRBs reserved for a UE exceed the size of the UE active BWP within the largest PDSCH sub-area, then the UE is reserved with the maximum PRBs within the PDSCH sub-area and the UE is assigned to this PDSCH sub-area. The UE is further removed from the resource reservation. Thus each user can be assigned to the respective UE's active BWP within only one PDSCH sub-area.

**- If a rach msg2, rach msg4 or any other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled in the same slot than a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING), gNB-DU shall allocate first in the PRBs of the CORESET 0 or CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) belonging to the PDSCH sub area below the common channel, otherwise, if there are not enough PRBs available, then gNB-DU shall allocate PRBs in the PRBs area of the CORESET 0 or CORESET#X (with X>0) belonging to the PDSCH sub area which contains the common channel and the PRBs above the common channel.**

**- If a rach msg2, rach msg4 or any other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode is scheduled in a SSB slot, gNB-DU shall allocate first in the PRBs of the CORESET 0 or CORESET#X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) belonging to the PDSCH sub area below the SSB, otherwise, if there are not enough PRBs available below the SSB, then gNB-DU shall allocate PRBs in the PRBs area of the CORESET 0 or CORESET#X (with X>0) above the SSB.**

-     [CB015725-E]If DL ES(refer to [12487237: Overview](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_h5BEsBA3EfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is disabled or if DL ES is enabled but rdDlEsEnh(refer to [13365298: rdDlEsEnh](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_XH4FAfZzEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is False,**[End CB015725-E] when there are multiple PDSCH sub-areas from which the needed resources can be granted then, gNB shall reserve the PRBs from the UE's respective active BWP within the smallest PDSCH sub-area. If there are more than one available smallest PDSCH sub-area then, gNB shall prefer and reserve the PRBs from that PDSCH sub-area that may contain the exclusive resources for the respective BWP. This facilitates larger contiguous free PRBs available for the subsequent UEs.**

- When there are multiple PDSCH sub-areas, and for FR1 FDD NIDD parameter NRCELL::numOfDeprioritizedDlRBGs is greater than 0, then gNB-DU shall give the least preference to reserve PRBs in the sub-area that contains the de-prioritized PRBs, if the remaining UEs could be satisfied in other sub-areas. If the UEs require more PRBs than in the prioritized region, then PRBs shall be reserved from the PDSCH sub-area that contains de-prioritized PRBs as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26762\_replaced([11182513](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- If a UE’s PRB reservation cannot be satisfied from any of the available PDSCH sub-area(s), then assign the PDSCH sub-area with the largest number of PRBs of the BWP of the UE so that the maximum resources can be assigned.

-     While reserving the PDSCH sub-area(s), gNB-DU shall adhere to the constraints described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Once the user is assigned to a PDSCH sub-area the UE is never re-assigned to another PDSCH sub-area as this could cause potential loss of resources within a PDSCH sub-area and hence avoided.

---

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-9 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation PDSCH sub-area assignment rules for MU-MIMO users (ID: 11176645)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-9.0-3**  (ID: `12520226`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12520226:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If Exhaustive Scheduling is enabled by setting NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling to 1 (for DL only) or 3 (for both DL and UL) then the following algorithm is skipped.

If the size of a PDSCH sub area (*rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]) is smaller than 20 PRBs, gNB-DU shall not create any MU MIMO VUE in this PDSCH sub area and shall ignore this requirement for this PDSCH sub area.*

With multiple PDSCH subareas, the gNB-DU shall determine the PDSCH subarea for a MU user as per the rules defined below:

[CB012971-C]

- If NIDD:actDlMuPairingGroup = True, [Before CB012971-C-UPD001] for SB BF UEs, which are MU eligible and able to be paired (see [11144070: Virtual UE construction](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), they shall be added into the PDSCH subarea which has the largest sizeSubArea(k) [CB012971-C-UPD001] for SB BF UE which is in pairing group and scheduled for non-GBR initialTx, it shall be added into the PDSCH subarea which has the largest number of remain PRB. Meanwhile, other UEs which are in the same pairing group and scheduled for non-GBR initialTx shall be assigned to the same pairing group [End CB012971-C-UPD001]. For other cases, go to following steps to decide allocation of PDSCH subarea.

[End CB012971-C]

- gNB-DU shall determine for each PDSCH subarea and each incoming MU eligible user UE(i), the correlation between UE(i) and UE(m) where m is the existing MU eligible user in the PDSCH subarea k and add the PRBs of those users for which the correlation value given by [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "corr(m, t)". The text is written in a dark color with a slight blur effect. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] is greater than the NRCELL/NRDLMUMIMO/dlMuMimoCorrThd as given below:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
totalNonPairableUePrbs(i) = Σ numPrbUe(m) where m ∈ SubArea(k) and corr(m,i) > corrThreshold
The formula calculates the total number of non-pairable User Equipment (UE) Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) for a given index 'i'. It sums up the `numPrbUe(m)` values for all 'm' that belong to a specific sub-area 'k' and satisfy the condition that the correlation `corr(m,i)` is greater than a predefined `corrThreshold`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

where:

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "corr(m, t)". The text is written in a dark color with a slight blur effect. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.001.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is the correlation value between UE(i) and UE(m) as given by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32977\_replaced([11143729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "d1M uM tmoCorrTha". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are in a dark color, possibly black or dark blue, against a light, slightly colored background. The overall image quality is low, with significant blur.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.003.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is given by NRCELL/NRDLMUMIMO/dlMuMimoCorrThd

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "numPrbUe(m)" in a blurry, light-colored font against a white background. The text is horizontally aligned and appears to be a function call or a variable name.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.004.png[/image\_path][/image-4] is the number of PRB allocated to UE(m)

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "totalNonPairableUePrbs(i)". The text is in a dark color and appears to be slightly blurred. The background is a light, gradient color.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.005.png[/image\_path][/image-5] is the sum of PRBs of all those users that cannot pair to the user i.

- If the existing user UE(m) passes the correlation check, then gNB-DU shall check if both the users have same DMRS Configuration Type failing which shall add the PRBs of UE(m) to [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "totalNonPairableUePrbs(i)". The text is in a dark color and is slightly blurry. The background is white with some light blue and yellow hues.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.006.png[/image\_path][/image-6]. Note that a UE can have both high correlation with UE(i) and different DMRS configuration Type, in which case the addition is done only once.

- gNB-DU shall calculate the number of potential PRBs that each candidate PDSCH subarea can provide for MU pairing as described below:

[image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font against a dark background. The equation reads:
`numPotentialPpbSubArea(k, t) = sizeSubArea(k) - totalNonPairablePrbs`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.007.png[/image\_path][/image-7]

where:

[image-8][image\_desc]The image contains the text "numPotentialPrbSubArea(k, t)". The text is in a light color against a white background, and the letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.008.png[/image\_path][/image-8] is the number of potential PRBs that can be allocated to UE(i) in sub area k due to DL MU-MIMO

[image-9][image\_desc]The image contains the text "sizeSubArea(k)". The text is in a light color against a white background, with a slight blur effect. The letters are in a sans-serif font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.009.png[/image\_path][/image-9] is the number of PRBs in subarea(k);

gNB-DU shall choose the subarea that has the maximum [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains the text "numPotentialPrbSubArea(k, i)". The text is blurry and appears to be in a programming context, possibly a function name.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.010.png[/image\_path][/image-10] for the user UE(i) and assign the user UE(i) to this subarea k. Note that the PDSCH subareas for a MU user is always one of the PDSCH subarea(s) (if multiple PDSCH subarea is available) across the largest BWP configured in the cell since MU-MIMO is applicable only to the largest BWP UEs in a slot. In case when [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text that reads "numPotentialPrbSubArea".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.011.png[/image\_path][/image-11] is equal for all the available PDSCH subareas, then L2-PS shall additionally check total [image-12][image\_desc]The image contains the text "totalNonPairableUePrbs" in a blurry, italicized font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.012.png[/image\_path][/image-12] within each PDSCH subarea and shall choose the PDSCH subarea with the minimum [image-13][image\_desc]The image contains the text "totalNonPairableUePrbs" in a blurry, italicized font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.012.png[/image\_path][/image-13] to facilitate more PRBs for the paired user, both to account for additional MU pairing with upcoming user(s) if any or as SU.

- It is possible that the estimated number of PRBs (given by [image-14][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" followed by a subscript "u". The text is dark blue and appears to be on a white background. The image is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.013.png[/image\_path][/image-14]) exceed the size of the chosen subarea k (since the estimation is based on the largest available PDSCH subarea according to step3 of the WRR algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23755\_replaced([11179805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ET_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), in which case gNB-DU shall set [image-15][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" with a subscript "w". The text is dark blue and appears to be on a white background. The image is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.014.png[/image\_path][/image-15] to the remaining number of PRBs available in the subarea k and reserve, at the same time returning back to the WRR the extra PRBs for further contention, provided that the [image-16][image\_desc]The image contains the text "numPotentialPrbSubArea(k, i)". The text is slightly blurry and appears to be in a programming or mathematical context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.015.png[/image\_path][/image-16]satisfies the UE(i) fair share derived from its scheduling weight given by [image-17][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression $W\_u^R$. The letters and subscripts are rendered in a stylized font with a chromatic aberration effect, giving them a slightly blurred and colorful appearance. The background is white, and the expression is enclosed within a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.016.png[/image\_path][/image-17]. Note that in this process, [image-18][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" with a subscript "w". The text is dark blue and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13263.017.png[/image\_path][/image-18]can be even 0. Rationale is that the MU user always has a chance to pair in a vUE hence it is alright to assign to the subarea k with the maximum number of potential PRBs for MU pairing. (see example in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26543\_replaced([11144828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr9P2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- While reserving the PDSCH sub-area(s), gNB-DU shall adhere to the constraints described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Once the user is assigned to a PDSCH sub-area the UE is never re-assigned to another PDSCH sub-area as this could cause potential loss of resources within a PDSCH sub-area and hence avoided.

---

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-10 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation PDSCH sub-area selection based on most exclusivity criteria (ID: 13476994)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `13476995`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
When exhaustive scheduling is enabled(refer to [12487239](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_859igBA3EfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and [rdDlEsEnh](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_XH4FAfZzEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) is set to true, the system shall implement a subarea assignment mechanism that utilizes a PRB exclusivity delta to guide the selection of candidate subareas. This mechanism aims to optimize resource allocation by identifying subareas with the highest number of exclusive PRBs relative to smaller Bandwidth Parts (BWPs).

1. Trigger Conditions:

The delta-based selection logic shall be invoked only when the following conditions are met:

* Multiple valid candidate subareas exist that can serve the needs of the UE.
* There is at least one smaller reference BWP available that contains active UEs to be distributed in the current slot.
* Reference BWP Selection:

To calculate the delta, the system shall identify a reference small BWP based on these criteria:

* Identify the first small BWP located sequentially after the current BWP.
* Verify the candidate small BWP has at least one remaining UE to be distributed in the current slot.
* Iterate to the next small BWP if the current candidate does not contain active UEs.
* Delta Computation:

The system shall compute a Delta value for each candidate subarea using the following logic:

* If a reference small BWP is identified:

Delta = Free PRBs(subArea, current BWP) - Free PRBs(subArea, reference small BWP)

* If no reference small BWP exists:

Delta = Free PRBs(subArea, current BWP)

1. Selection Criteria:
2. The system shall evaluate all candidate subareas and select the one associated with the highest calculated Delta value.

---

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-11 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation dynamicswitch RAT0 conditions for retransmission (ID: 11176710)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11176715`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Conditions to allocate a retransmission  as a “dynamicswitch” RAT0

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-11.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12683727`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12683727:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
gNB-DU shall list all the RBGs available for the retransmission in “dynamicswitch” RAT0 allocation in the same way it is done in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25001\_replaced([11181630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_VvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the use cases under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24997\_replaced([11181725](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8yfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for an initial transmission.

For a candidate retransmission as dynamicswitch RAT0, **gNB-DU shall check:**

* the functionality dynamicswitch have been activated (NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc  = true), the UE is configured as dynamicswitch.
* in the slot, there are already at least 2 PDSCH sub areas (note: not a slot with CSI-RS for beam refinement), and at least one RBG available (with the RBG size of the BWP where the UE belongs to, except if it is a partial RBG) in each PDSCH sub area.

- [CB014474-A] When there is no MU pairing in the slot, or the candidate UE is a non MU eligible UE [End CB014474-A],

- gNB-DU shall start with the RBGs contained by the PDSCH sub area where the number of remaining PRBs is the highest, and shall continue with the RBGs contained by the other PDSCH sub area(s). Note: gNB-DU shall select the RBG size of the dedicated BWP where the UE belongs to in case of multi BWP (see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and resource block groups (RBGs) in a communication system. The diagram uses colored bars to represent BWPs and stacked orange rectangles to represent RBGs.
On the left side, there are several colored bars labeled as follows:
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A large orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP 1".
- A large green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
On the right side, there are stacked orange rectangles representing RBGs. These are divided into two sections, "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2".
Text labels and arrows indicate relationships and quantities:
- "Last RBG" points to the top of the RBG stack in "PDSCH sub area 1".
- "Nb1 RBGs available" is associated with the upper portion of the RBGs in "PDSCH sub area 1".
- "RBGs Full size" is associated with the middle portion of the RBGs in "PDSCH sub area 1".
- A purple rectangle is shown within "PDSCH sub area 2", labeled "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0)". Arrows indicate the size of this purple rectangle.
- "RBGs Full size" is also associated with the RBGs in "PDSCH sub area 2".
- "Nb2 RBGs available" is associated with the lower portion of the RBGs in "PDSCH sub area 2".
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs might utilize or be allocated within the available RBGs, with specific sections designated for PDSCH sub-areas.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13266.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* gNB-DU shall check at each PDSCH sub area if, with the additional number of  RBGs available in this PDSCH sub area, the sum of the PRBs contained by the RBGs is greater than the number of PRBs needed for the retransmission: if it is not fulfilled, gNB-DU shall look for new RBGs available in another PDSCH sub area, otherwise  gNB-DU shall stop searching new RBGs in other PDSCH sub areas till there is no more PDSCH sub area.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different types of resource blocks (RBs) or physical resource blocks (PRBs) allocated for various purposes.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Vertical Bars (representing resource allocation):**
\* **Leftmost Column:**
\* A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3" (likely Physical Shared Bandwidth Part 3).
\* A green bar labeled "BWP-" (likely Bandwidth Part).
\* A yellow bar labeled "dedicated BWP-1" (likely dedicated Bandwidth Part 1).
\* A red bar at the bottom.
\* Another red bar at the top.
\* **Middle Column:**
\* A blue bar labeled "SSB" (likely Synchronization Signal Block).
\* **Rightmost Columns:**
\* Several orange rectangular blocks, stacked vertically. These appear to represent allocated or blanked resource blocks.
**Horizontal Dashed Lines and Arrows:**
\* These lines and arrows indicate the boundaries and extents of different resource areas.
\* "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a large vertical arrow spanning from the top red bar to a dashed line above the orange blocks in the middle right.
\* "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a vertical arrow spanning from a dashed line below the top red bar to a dashed line above the blue SSB block.
\* "PDSCH sub area 3" is indicated by a vertical arrow spanning from a dashed line below the SSB block to a dashed line above the bottom red bar.
\* "additional blanked PRBs area" is indicated by a double-headed arrow between two dashed lines, positioned below the "PDSCH sub area 1" boundary and above the "PDSCH sub area 2" boundary.
\* "Low edge PRBs blanking" is indicated by a double-headed arrow between two dashed lines, positioned below the "PDSCH sub area 3" boundary.
**Text Labels:**
\* "PS BWP-3"
\* "BWP-"
\* "dedicated BWP-1"
\* "PDSCH sub area 1"
\* "PDSCH sub area 2"
\* "SSB"
\* "PDSCH sub area 3"
\* "additional blanked PRBs area"
\* "Low edge PRBs blanking"
\* "Nb1 + Nb2 + Nb3"
\* "RBGs Pre booked"
**Blue Lines:**
\* Two blue lines connect the top of the diagram (near "Nb1 + Nb2 + Nb3") to the bottom of the diagram, indicating a range or relationship between different resource allocations.
The diagram visually represents how different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and specific signals like SSB are positioned relative to each other, and how certain resource areas are designated for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) or are blanked. The labels "Nb1 + Nb2 + Nb3" and "RBGs Pre booked" suggest a concept of pre-booked resource groups.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13266.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their allocation in a communication system. The diagram uses colored bars to represent these BWPs.
From left to right, the bars are:
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP 1".
- A green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
To the right of these bars, there are several stacked colored blocks representing resource allocations within specific sub-areas.
The diagram also includes text labels and arrows indicating relationships and quantities:
- "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated by a dashed line encompassing a section of the diagram.
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_1 BWPs" is indicated by a double-headed arrow pointing to a set of stacked colored blocks (light orange, green, light blue, purple).
- "Nb1 + Nb2", "RBGs", and "Pre booked" are text labels on the right side, connected by a blue line to the upper portion of the diagram.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by another dashed line lower down.
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0" is indicated by a double-headed arrow pointing to a single orange block.
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_1 BWP1" is indicated by a double-headed arrow pointing to a single orange block in the lower section.
The overall diagram appears to be a visual representation of how different BWPs are utilized and how resources are allocated within specific sub-areas, possibly for data transmission (PDSCH). The stacked colored blocks likely represent resource units like Resource Blocks (RBs) or Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) allocated to different control information (DCI) or BWPs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13266.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

- [CB014474-A] If ~~NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, and~~ the candidate user is a MU eligible retransmission UE.

* During the vUE construction with retransmission users as root UE, calculate the required number of PRBs i.e allowedPrbUe(i) for the retransmission MU eligible user as per [11144618: PDSCH allocation with RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8oyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) step (1)
* When Exhaustive scheduling (ES) is disabled (i.e NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to a value other than 1 (for DL only) or 3 (for both DL and UL)), determine the PDSCH sub area for the user as per the rules defined in [11176645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). Note: The requirement defined in [11176645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) gives the generic rules to determine the PDSCH subarea. However for the MU eligible users always the largest PDSCH subarea with remaining available PRBs is chosen in order to maximize the pairing opportunity as per current SW implementation. Else with ES enabled (i.e NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to 1 (for DL only) or 3 (for both DL and UL), the PDSCH subarea determination mentioned in [11176645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) is bypassed and the user must be always assigned to the PDSCH sub area with the maximum remaining available resources.Then go to the next steps.
* If the PDSCH sub area chosen in the above step can provide enough contiguous PRBs to satisfy the required number of PRBs i.e allowedPrbUe(i), then the user shall be assigned to this PDSCH sub area and shall be scheduled as "dynamicswitch" RAT1 and can participate in further pairing with potential new transmission and other retransmission MU eligible users,
* [PR869610] If the user is not a SVD-RZF MU paired retransmission user:[End PR869610]
* If PDSCH sub area chosen cannot provide enough contiguous PRBs to satisfy the required number of PRBs i.e allowedPrbUe(i), gNB-DU shall check for the other PDSCH sub area that whether, with the additional number of RBGs available in this PDSCH sub area, the sum of the PRBs contained by the RBGs (across the two PDSCH sub areas) can satisfy the number of PRBs needed for the retransmission (i.e allowedPrbUe(i)). The below mentioned constraints for BB pooling must be followed.
* If the above check passes, gNB-DU shall convert the user to dynamicswitch RAT0. Note that, there could be other paired users (initial or retransmission) in one or more vUE(s) which could also be candidate users to be converted to dynamicswitch RAT0 at the same time. gNB-DU shall perform the following steps:
  + In case there are only retransmission users paired in the vUE(s), gNB-DU shall decide whether one or all of these are candidates for dynamicswitch RAT0 based on the steps described above.
  + In case there are new transmission user(s), then for these new transmission users, gNB-DU shall decide if they can be also possible candidate for dynamicswitch RAT0 based on the minimum number of PRBs required to drain the buffered bytes, (since allowedPrbUe(i) is yet unknown when ES is disabled).
  + For both the above cases, if gNB-DU evaluated this user could also be a potential candidate, then:
  + gNB-DU shall select and allocate the RBGs within the PDSCH sub areas in the same way as it is done for an initial transmission (See [11181651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8wvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/bi_ku_8wvvsee-aqvopbp1qhq?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9"), [11181674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8xvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/bi_ku_8xvvsee-aqvopbp1qhq?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9")). These PRBs shall be made unavailable from the PDSCH sub areas and the size of the PDSCH subarea shall be updated (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The PDSCH sub areas are those where the user(s) has been converted to dynamicswitch RAT0, that includes the original PDSCH sub area when the user was allocated with dynamicswitch RAT1 allocation only and the subsequent PDSCH sub area where the users(s) has been extended to, after the conversion to dynamicswitch RAT0.
  + In the other/subsequent PDSCH sub area, for the new vUE creation and final MCS recalculation, gNB-DU shall follow the same rules as in the case of conversion into dynamicswitch RAT0, for initial transmission MU paired user (See [11181651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8wvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/bi_ku_8wvvsee-aqvopbp1qhq?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9"), [11181674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8xvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/bi_ku_8xvvsee-aqvopbp1qhq?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9")).
* If the above check fails, then the retransmission shall be dropped by gNB-DU in this slot and postponed. In order to harness the MU pairing that the user would otherwise offer, a new transmission for the same user shall be paired with the other constituent user(s) in the vUE(s). However this new transmission opportunity shall be re-assessed for dynamicswitch applicability with other potential candidates in the slot by gNB-DU. If there is another candidate UE that deserves to be prioritized, then gNB-DU shall pick the UE for the above procedures.
* [PR869610] If the user is a SVD-RZF MU paired retransmission user:
* If the PDSCH sub area chosen cannot provide enough contiguous PRBs to satisfy the required number of PRBs i.e allowedPrbUe(i), gNB-DU shall drop and postpone the retransmission to future. In order to harness the MU pairing that the user would otherwise offer, a new transmission for the same user shall be paired with the other constituent user(s) belonging to same Pairing group (PG).

[End PR869610 ]

[End CB014474-A]

* for the PRB pooling limitation criteria: gNB-DU shall check that the sum of the number of the PRBs from the RBGs reserved for the “dynamicswitch” RAT0 in addition of the number of PRBs of other signals shall not exceed the PRB limitation provided by BBRM in the slot as it is done in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) each time when in another PDSCH sub area some available RBGs are found.

(see example in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25644\_replaced([11176733](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If the sum of the PRBs contained by the RBGs is bigger (or equal) than the number of PRBs needed for the retransmission and the PRBs limitation (including the PRBs reserved by other UEs in RAT1) provided by BBRM is fulfilled if PRB pooling is activated (in FDD or if NRCELL.slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is set to True in FR1 TDD, see previous paragraph and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall select the retransmission with a “dynamicswitch” RAT0 for this UE and the check is successful. Otherwise, gNB-DU shall not select this UE for a “dynamicswitch” RAT0 as a retransmission and the check is failed.

If the check is successful, gNB-DU shall select the RBGs within a PDSCH sub area in the same way as it is done for an initial transmission and shall pre book the RBGs through a RBG bitmap as it is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25096\_replaced([11181674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8xvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Then if there is no common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING) scheduled in the slot, gNB-DU shall update the size of the CORESET #0 within the PDSCH sub area which contains the CORESET #0 where some RBGs are prebooked (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25184\_replaced([11176746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

gNB-DU shall update the size (and the startPrb) of the PDSCH sub areas which contain pre booked RBGs as it is described in the requirements below 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and gNB-DU shall create a new PDSCH sub area too above the highest PRB of the pre booked RBGs per PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

**3.29.5.4.2.0-11.0-2**  (ID: `11176733`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Example for RBG research for a dynamicswitch RAT0 retransmission

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-11.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176737`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
Example: a slot with 2 PDSCH sub areas due to DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grants (such as rach msg2 or 4) in multi BWP where a UE (belonging to the largest dedicated BWP1) is scheduled for a retransmission of an initial transmission "dynamicswitch" RAT0, see figure below.

 gNB-DU finds Nb1 RBGs available inside the PDSCH sub area 1 which contains a number of PRBs which is smaller than the number of PRBs required for the retransmission of the UE.

o scenario 1: This number of PRBs is greater than the PRB pooling limitation. The check fails here, and then the retransmission for this UE cannot be a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 and gNB-DU shall remove the UE from the CS2 list and postpone it to the next slot.

o scenario 2: This number of PRBs is smaller (or equal) than the PRB pooling limitation and then gNB-DU continues to the next step.

 then, gNB-DU shall look for other RBGs available in the second PDSCH sub area and find Nb2 RBGs. The sum of PRBs from (Nb1 + Nb2) RBGs is greater than the number of PRBs required for the retransmission, but smaller (or equal) than the PRB pooling limitation. gNB-DU checks The sum of PRBs from (Nb1 + 1) RBGs.

o scenario 3: The sum of PRBs from (Nb1 + 1) RBGs is greater than the PRB pooling limitation, then same conclusion as in scenario 1.

o scenario 4: The sum of PRBs from (Nb1 + 1) RBGs is smaller (or equal) than the PRB pooling limitation and above  the number of PRBs required for the retransmission => gNB -DU finds the rigth number of RBGs needed shared on 2 PDSCH sub areas, and shall select this UE for a retransmission in "dynamicswitch" RAT0.

o scenario 5: The sum of PRBs from (Nb1 + 1) RBGs is smaller (or equal) than the PRB pooling limitation and below the number of PRBs required for the retransmission => gNB-DU shall checks (Nb1 + 2) RBGs in the same way with the PRB pooling limitation and  the number of PRBs required for the retransmission in order to determine if this transmission is postponed to the next slot or selected as a  "dynamicswitch" RAT0 within the 2 PDSCH sub areas.

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `11176746`)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176760`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176760:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
In a slot [CB008927-Q] if a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and  [End CB008927-Q] when there is no common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) scheduled , but there are other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode such as a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or any DCI 1\_0 in transient phase,

[CB008927-Q] or if a FDD cell is shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and when a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING),  a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or any DCI 1\_0 in transient phase are scheduled, [End CB008927-Q],

if a “dynamicswitch” RAT0 (initial transmission or retransmission) is scheduled [CB008927-L] and no other RAT1 UEs are scheduled  [End CB008927-L] and its RBGs are pre booked, gNB-DU shall update the size of the CORESET #0 within the PDSCH sub area which contains this CORESET #0 with the lowest PRB of the pre booked RBGs in this PDSCH sub area and [Before CB008927-Q] the start PRB [CB008927-Q] the lowest PRB [End CB008927-Q] of the CORESET #0, provided it is smaller than the size of the CORESET 0, in this way:

rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] = MIN((startPrbRbg(n) - startPrbCoreset0), SizeCoreset0)

where

 startPrbCoreset0 is the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 SizeCoreset0 is the size of the CORESET #0 [CB008927-Q] (or X with X>0) [End CB008927-Q]  (see 5G\_UP\_10865\_replaced([11350646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [CB008927-Q] Note:  it can be the start Prb of the CORESET  or the PRB above the highest PRB of a CAT-M PRBs area (that is the start Prb of the PDSCH sub area). [End CB008927-Q]

 startPrbRbg(n) is the lowest PRB of the RBGs pre booked in this PDSCH sub area n.

 rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]  is the size of the CORESET #0 within the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

gNB-DU shall update the number of remaining PRBs for the CORESET 0 inside the PDSCH sub area with the new size of the PDSCH sub area in this way.

rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] =  rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]   - nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n)

Where:

-  nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- rrrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n]  is the number of remaining PRBs in te CORESET 0 inside the PDSCH sub area  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[CB008927-K] When the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3 PDCCH CSS , see 38.213 §10.1) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0) (and not on CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and , gNB-DU shall handle in the same way, gNB-DU shall not update the variables for the CORESET 0 and CORESET#X (with X>0), because there is no start Prb randomizaton for the DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grants. [End CB008927-K]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176769`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
PDSCH subarea update when vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot and the candidate UE for the dynamicswitch to RAT0 is either one among the paired users or not.

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12678834`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
When NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True,  the vUE(s) are contiguously allocated from the top of the PDSCH sub area. See PDSCH allocation rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23986\_replaced([11182340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for MU-MIMO users.

[CB014474-A] If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 is set to TRUE, then there can be multiple MU paired users that can be candidate for dynamicswitch RAT0. The following rules applies for each such candidate user.[End CB014474-A]

When vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot then irrespective of the candidate UE selected for dynamicswitch to RAT0 is among the UEs that are paired or not, if needed gNB-DU shall update the size of the PDSCH sub area in which the vUE(s) are scheduled by deducting the total number of PRBs reserved for the vUE(s).

When the candidate UE is not among the paired users in a vUE, the update of the PDSCH sub area is required to facilitate the implementation with application of the general rules of dynamicswitch to RAT0 for the selected non MU candidate UE in the reduced PDSCH sub area in the same manner without considering the PRBs reserved for the vUE(s) in the PDSCH sub area. See figure 2bis for example in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25683\_replaced([11176810](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMq_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or figure 1bis in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25686\_replaced([11176935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Similarly when vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot and if the candidate UE selected for dynamicswitch to RAT0 is one among the UEs that are paired (see rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25096\_replaced([11181674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8xvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall still update the size of the PDSCH sub area in which the vUE(s) are scheduled. In addition the PDSCH reservation within the vUE(s) could also be affected and the final update of the respective PDSCH sub area depends on the candidate user picked for dynamicswitch to RAT0.

When a paired user is selected as the candidate UE, gNB-DU shall check if the user is the largest contributing paired user within the vUE. If the user is the largest then converting this user to dynamicswitch RAT0 must not affect the PRB share of the other users in the vUE however the number of PRBs previously reserved using RAT1 given by nbPrbs(vUE) is affected and lower most PRB in nbPrbs(vUE) is determined by the lowest PRB of the lower most full size RBG of the candidate UE as shown in the figure 1bis, 1bis' in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25683\_replaced([11176810](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMq_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) which leads to the update of the PDSCH sub area.

The update of the PDSCH sub area is to facilitate other RAT1 UEs to utilize the updated PDSCH sub area for allocation purposes.

Once the RBGs and correspondingly the nbPrbs(vUE) worth of the RBGs are calculated and identifed, gNB-DU shall update the size of the PDSCH sub area if needed, by deducting the same from the respective PDSCH sub area size and update the remaining size as desribed below.

rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] remains the same.

*rmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  =* rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]  + *rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - nbPrbs(vUE)*\*\*

The remaining PRBs in the PDSCH sub area shall be updated as:

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) - nbPrbs(vUE)

**rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] =** rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-        nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n)**\*\*\*\*

where;

* *rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] and* rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]*are the start Prb and the size of the existing PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and after the above update* rmPdschSubAreaSize[n]*is the new size of the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*\*\*\*\*

- nbPrbs(vUE) gives the total number of PRBs of the vUE(s).

* nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) is the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758).

- *rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]*is the number of remaining PRBs inside the new updated PDSCH sub area n.\*\*

When there are multiple dedicated BWPs (p>1) configured and given as BWP(i) where i ∈{1..p}, the MU-MIMO paired users belong to largest BWP(i) where i=1, then;

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] =* *rmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  given below;*\*\*

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) - nbPrbs(vUE)

*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] =* *rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]**-  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n)*\*\*\*\*

where:

* nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) is the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758).
* *rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1]*is the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP(1) inside the new updated PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).\*\*

.

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11176778`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
RBGs pre booked for a dynamicswitch RAT0 at the top of a PDSCH sub area
