**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176782`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
PDSCH sub area update update (initial and retransmission dynamicswitch RAT0)

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11176790`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
updating the size of the PDSCH sub area due to pre booked RBGs for a dynamicswitch RAT0 starting at the top of a PDSCH sub area (initial and retransmission dynamicswitch RAT0)

[CB013945-A] Note: If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDSCH sub area update. Below requirement defines the rules for further update of the reduced PDSCH sub area for non-MU paired users depending on whether the initial PDSCH sub area size was updated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or not. [End CB013945-A]

1) Above the pre booked RBGs of a dynamicswitch RAT0

If multi BWP is configured (see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the candidate UE belongs to BWP2 and there are reserved PRBs in the largest dedicated BWP1 and there is an exclusive PRB area for the largest dedicated BWP1 at the top of the PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall create a new PDSCH sub area and update the start PRB and the size of this new PDSCH sub area  with the PRB above the highest PRB of the pre booked RBGs in the PDSCH sub area, the size and the start PRB of the existing PDSCH sub area  in this way:

rmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+2] = startPrbHighRbg(n)

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2]  = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]  + rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - startPrbHighRbg(n)

Where:

- startPrbHighRbg(n) is the PRB above highest PRB of the RBGs pre booked in this PDSCH sub area n

- rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] and rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  are the start Prb and the size of the existing PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Otherwise, in the case of retransmission, there is no RAT 1 UEs above the pre booked RBGs of the dynamicswitch RAT0 but if the remaining PRBs above the pre booked RBGs in a PDSCH sub area is greater than rdMinNbPRBsPdschSubArea  PRBs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24749\_replaced([11193819](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwKsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall create a new PDSCH sub area where the start PRB is the PRB above the highest PRB of the RBGs pre booked and the size is the number of remaining PRBs.

rmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+2] = startPrbHighRbg(n)

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2]  = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]  + rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  - startPrbHighRbg(n)

Where:

- startPrbHighRbg(n) is the PRB above highest PRB of the RBGs pre booked in this PDSCH sub area n

- rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] and rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  are the start Prb and the size of the existing PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note: This new PDSCH sub area can be:

* below a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING), gNB-DU shall handle variables of a CORESET #0 PRB area with the dedicated BWPs too.
* under blanked PRBs due to SSB, PRBs blanking, additional blanking PRBs area ...

2) Below the pre booked RBGs of a dynamicswitch RAT0

For an initial transmission or retransmission, gNB-DU shall update the size of the PDSCH sub area below the pre booked RBGs with the lowest PRB of the RBGs pre booked in this PDSCH sub area and the start PRB of the PDSCH sub area in this way:

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  = startPrbLowRbg(n) - rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]

Where:

- startPrbLowRbg(n) is the lowest PRB of the RBGs pre booked in this PDSCH sub area n

- rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] and rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  are the start Prb  and the size of the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

When all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including the common channel, the rach msg2 and rach msg4) are mapped on the CORESET 0 or when all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including the rach msg2) are mapped on the CORESET#X (with X>0), in a slot when there is no common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) scheduled , but there are other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode such as a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or any DCI 1\_0 in transient phase, gNB-DU shall update the size of the CORESET #0 (if present in the PDSCH sub area) within the PDSCH sub area which contains this CORESET as it is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25184\_replaced([11176746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The figures for the use cases with RBGs at the top of the PDSCH sub area are under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25683\_replaced([11176810](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMq_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11176795`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
remaining PRBs udate only for an initial transmission dynamicswitch RAT0

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11176806`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
updating the remaining PRBs in the PDSCH sub area due to RBGs pre booked for a dynamicswitch RAT0 starting at the top of a PDSCH sub area

[CB013945-A] Note: If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDSCH sub area update. Below requirement defines the rules for further update of the reduced PDSCH sub area for non-MU paired users depending on whether the initial PDSCH sub area size was updated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or not.[End CB013945-A]

1) If single dedicated BWP,

When it is an initial dynamicswitch RAT0 and the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 belong to the PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall update the number of  reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area by removing the number PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as  RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area and shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with the new number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area and the new size of the PDSCH sub area.

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) - nbReservedPrbsUe

rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n)

Where:

- nbReservedPrbsUe is the number of PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) is the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP witin the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  is the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] and rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  are the new start Prb and the new size of the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If the candidate UE has no reserved PRB in the PDSCH sub area for an initial transmission (pre booked RBGs in another PDSCH sub area than the PDSCH sub area where PRBs were reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1) or a retransmission, the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area of PRBs is not updated and gNB-DU shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with new size of the PDSCH sub area.

rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n)

Where:

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP witin the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  is the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  is the new size of the PDSCH sub area n(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

2) If multi dedicated BWP is configured,

2.a) if there is no exclusive PRB for the largest dedicated BWP1 at the top of the PDSCH sub area or no reserved PRBs in the largest dedicated BWP1 if the UE belongs to the BWP2 (see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

When it is an initial transmission for the dynamicswitch RAT0 and the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 belong to the PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall update the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) (where the candidate UE belongs to) within the PDSCH sub area n by removing the number of PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area and shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with the new or current number of reserved PRBs for all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area  and the new size of the PDSCH sub area.

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) - nbReservedPrbsUe

Where:

- p (1 or 2) is the number of the dedicated BWP where the candidate UE had reserved PRBs as RAT1

rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) for all the BWP(p)

Where:

- nbReservedPrbsUe is the number of PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area n

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) is the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  is the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP(p) inside the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  is the new size of the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If the candidate UE has no reserved PRB in the dediacted BWP(p) in the PDSCH sub area for an initial transmission  (pre booked RBGs in another PDSCH sub area than the PDSCH sub area where PRBS were reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1) or a retransmission, the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) (p is 1 or 2) within the PDSCH sub area of PRBs is not updated and gNB-DU shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with new size of the PDSCH sub area.

rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)

Where:

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  is the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP(p) inside the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  is the new size of the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

2.b) If multi BWP is configured (see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the candidate UE belongs to BWP2 and there are reserved PRBs in the largest dedicated BWP1 and there is an exclusive PRB area for the largest dedicated BWP1 at the top of the PDSCH sub area,

gNB-DU shall update the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) (where the candidate UE belongs to) within the PDSCH sub area n by removing the number PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area n,  shall remove the number of PRBs reserved for the UEs which belong to the largest dedicated BWP1 in this PDSCH sub area n and shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with the new or current number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) (except the largest dedicated BWP1) within the PDSCH sub area n

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(2,n) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(2,n) - nbReservedPrbsUe

previous\_nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n)

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) = 0 (in this PDSCH sub area n)

rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) for p >= 2 or p = 0

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) and rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] are set to 0 in this PDSCH sub area n since there is no more UEs of the BWP1 in this  PDSCH sub area n

Where:

- nbReservedPrbsUe is the number of PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) is the number of reserved PRBs  in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  is the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP(p) inside the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  is the new size of the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If the candidate UE has no reserved PRB in the dedicated BWP2 in the PDSCH sub area for an initial transmission (pre booked RBGs in another PDSCH sub area than the PDSCH sub area where PRBS were reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1) or a retransmission, the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area is not updated but gNB-DU shall remove the number of PRBs reserved for the UEs which belong to the largest dedicated BWP1 in this PDSCH sub area n and gNB-DU shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with the new or current number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) (except the largest dedicated BWP1) within the PDSCH sub area n.

previous\_nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n)

rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) for p >= 2 or p = 0

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) and rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] are set to 0 in this PDSCH sub area n since there is no more UEs of the BWP1 in this  PDSCH sub area n

Where:

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) is the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  is the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP(p) inside the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  is the new size of the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

gNB-DU shall update the number of reserved PRBs in the largest dedicated BWP(1) in the new PDSCH area created (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25680\_replaced([11176782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in updating the number PRBs reserved for the UEs belonging to the largest PDSCH sub area and shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with the new number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area n + 2.

Note: In ths new PDSCH sub area, the reserved PRBs belong only to the largest dedicated BWP1

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n+2) = previous\_nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n)

rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+2][1] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n+2)

Where:

- previous\_nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the largest dedicated BWP1 in the old PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n+2) is the number of reserved PRBs in the largest dedicated BWP1 in the new PDSCH sub area n+2 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+2][1]  is the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP1 inside the PDSCH sub area n+2 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

When all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including the common channel, the rach msg2 and rach msg4) are mapped on the CORESET 0 or when all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including the rach msg2) are mapped o the CORESET#X (with X>0), in a slot when there is no common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) scheduled , but there are other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode such as a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or any DCI 1\_0 in transient phase, gNB-DU shall update the variables of the CORESET #0 (if present in the PDSCH sub area) within the PDSCH sub area which contains this CORESET as it is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25184\_replaced([11176746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The figures for the use cases with RBGs at the top of the PDSCH sub area are under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25683\_replaced([11176810](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMq_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11176810`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
figures of use cases

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12678875`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12678875:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
figures of use cases when RBGs pre booked for a dynamicswitch RAT0 at the top of a PDSCH sub area

Note: These figures are partial view of the carrier bandwidth, only showing a PDSCH sub area which is below or above a SSB, a CORESET 0, an additional blanking PRBs area  ... The purpose is to illustrate better how this existing PDSCH sub area is updated (and split in 2 if needed) because of RBGs pre booking.

fig1: partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 above a SSB in multi BWP configuration (there is no PRB blanking at the top of the PDSCH sub area) . It is an initial transmission of a candidate UE.The candidate UE belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1. The reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 are moved and pre booked into RBGs at the top of the old PDSCH sub area 1. The other RAT1 UEs are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 1 above the DCI 1\_0 allocations (same start PRB, size updated). No new PDSCH sub area is created within the old PDSCH sub area 1.

The CORESET 0 can contain DCI 1\_0 mapped with CORESET#X (with X>0) when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0

in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the allocation of Resource Blocks (RBs) in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource allocations for various User Equipments (UEs).
The diagram shows several colored bars representing allocated RBs. From left to right, these are:
\* **CORESET#0**: A narrow blue bar.
\* **initial BWP**: A yellow bar.
\* **dedicated BWP**: A larger orange bar.
\* **dedicated BWP 2**: A green bar.
\* **PS BWP-3**: A light blue bar.
\* Following these, there are several stacked bars in light orange, light green, light purple, and a darker orange bar.
Text annotations explain the different sections and allocations:
\* **CORESET#0**, **initial BWP**, **dedicated BWP**, **dedicated BWP 2**, **PS BWP-3** are labels for the initial BWPs.
\* **nbPrbs reserved for the candidate UE in BWP1**: This text is associated with the large darker orange bar on the right, indicating a reserved number of PRBs for a specific UE in BWP1.
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 1**: A dashed line indicates this area.
\* **Nb 1 RBGs**: This text is positioned to the right of the diagram, likely referring to the total number of Resource Block Groups (RBGs) in a specific band.
\* **nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWPs**: This text is associated with the stacked bars below the "candidate UE" allocation, indicating reserved PRBs for other UEs in their dedicated BWPs.
\* **Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1**: This text is associated with a dashed line below the "Old PDSCH sub area 1" line, indicating the lowest PRB of pre-booked RBGs in a PDSCH sub-area.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with or without common channel)**: This text is associated with a dashed line below the "nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs" section, indicating reserved PRBs for DCI 1\_0.
\* **New PDSCH sub area 1**: A dashed line indicates this area.
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1**: This text is associated with the lowest dashed line, indicating the starting PRB of a PDSCH sub-area.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and specific UE allocations are managed within the available radio resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

fig1bis:single MU paired UE converted to dynamicswitch to RAT0

partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 above a SSB in multi BWP configuration (there is no PRB blanking at the top of the PDSCH sub area, but there could be a partial RBG) . The figure depicts the process of dynamicswitch to RAT0 for an initial transmission for a candidate UE which belongs to a vUE in BWP1, and is the largest UE in terms of PRB reservation within the pair.

* The vUE has total size of nbPrbs and reserved from the top of the PDSCH subarea in the BWP1.
* The reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 are moved and pre booked into RBGs, given by Nb1 at the top of the old PDSCH sub area 1.
* The nbPrbs for the vUE are readjusted to become equal to number of PRBs worth the size of the Nb1 RBGs provided that the reservations of the other paired UEs in the vUE are unaffected. Note that, for the candidate UE , Nb1 RBGs could also include RBG(s) reserved as SU in which case nbPrbs for the vUE shall include only the PRBs worth the part of the Nb1 RBGs that are reserved as MU.
* The startPrb and number of PRBs for the other paired users remain unaffected. The other RAT1 UEs are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 1 above the DCI 1\_0 allocations (same start PRB, size updated). No new PDSCH sub area is created within the old PDSCH sub area 1.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the allocation of Resource Blocks (RBs) in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). The diagram shows different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource allocations.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Allocation Blocks):**
\* **CORESET#0:** A blue block representing a Control Resource Set (CORESET) for initial access or control signaling.
\* **initial BWP:** A yellow block representing the initial bandwidth part.
\* **dedicated BWP:** An orange block representing a dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **dedicated BWP 2:** A green block representing a second dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **PS BWP-3:** A light blue block representing a third dedicated bandwidth part, possibly for a specific service (PS likely stands for Packet Switched).
\* **Candidate UE:** A vertical arrangement of blocks (orange, light blue, grey) labeled "Candidate UE". This suggests a potential allocation for a user equipment (UE) that is being considered for a resource assignment.
**Right Side (Resource Allocation Descriptions and Areas):**
\* **Nb1 RBGs:** A large orange block labeled "Nb1 RBGs". This likely represents a group of resource blocks allocated to a specific UE or service.
\* **Text associated with the orange block:** "new nbPrbs = number of Prbs worth Nb1 RBGs". This indicates that the size of this allocation is determined by a new number of physical resource blocks (PRBs) worth of Nb1 Resource Block Groups.
\* **"nbPrbs reserved For the candidate UE in BWP1 = old nbPRbs reserved for the VUE"**: This text describes a reservation of PRBs for a candidate UE within BWP1, which were previously reserved for a VUE (likely another type of UE or service).
\* **"Old PDSCH sub area 1"**: This text indicates a region of the spectrum allocated for a Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) in sub-area 1, which is now considered "old".
\* **"nbPrbs reserved For the other UEs in dedicated BWPs"**: This text indicates a reservation of PRBs for other UEs that are using dedicated BWPs.
\* **"nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with or without common channel)"**: This text refers to PRBs allocated for Downlink Control Information (DCI) format 1\_0, which can be used with or without a common channel.
\* **"Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1, also defines lowest PRB in new nbPrbs"**: This text explains that the lowest PRB of pre-booked RBGs in PDSCH sub-area 1 also defines the lowest PRB for the new PRB allocation.
\* **"New PDSCH sub area 1"**: This indicates a new allocation area for PDSCH in sub-area 1.
\* **"Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1"**: This marks the starting point of the PDSCH allocation in sub-area 1.
The diagram uses dashed lines to indicate boundaries and relationships between different resource allocations and areas. The vertical arrows show the extent of these allocations. The overall diagram visualizes how different BWPs and control information are mapped onto the available radio resources, with specific considerations for reserved PRBs and the definition of new resource allocations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

fig1bis': single MU paired UE converted to dynamicswitch to RAT0

partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 above a SSB in multi BWP configuration when there is PRB blanking at the top of the PDSCH area.

* Similar to fig1bis, after the conversion to dynamicswitch to RAT0 , the respective RBG is not included in the Nb1. However the nbPrbs does include those unaffected PRBs in this blanked RBG reserved for the other paired users in the vUE.
* The lowest PRB in the nbPrbs is determined by the lowest PRB in the lower most RBG included in Nb1 RBGs reserved as MU for the candidate UE if there are more RBG(s) for this UE that are reserved as SU as well, provided that the reservations of the other paired UEs in the vUE are unaffected.
* The startPrb and number of PRBs for the other paired users remain unaffected. The other RAT1 UEs are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 1 above the DCI 1\_0 allocations (same start PRB, size updated). No new PDSCH sub area is created within the old PDSCH sub area 1.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource blocks (RBs) or resource block groups (RBGs).
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Vertical Bars representing BWPs and Resource Allocation:**
\* **CORESET#0 (Blue):** This is the first element, likely representing a control resource set.
\* **initial BWP (Yellow):** This bar follows CORESET#0 and is labeled "initial BWP".
\* **dedicated BWP (Orange):** This is a larger bar, indicating a dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **dedicated BWP 2 (Green):** Another dedicated bandwidth part, positioned next to the first dedicated BWP.
\* **PS BWP-3 (Light Blue):** This represents a "Physical Shared Channel Bandwidth Part 3".
\* **Candidate UE (Vertical text in a light orange/pinkish block):** This block is segmented and labeled with vertical text "C", "a", "n", "d", "i", "d", "a", "t", "e", "U", "E". This likely represents resources allocated for a candidate user equipment.
**Horizontal Dashed Lines and Text Annotations:**
Several horizontal dashed lines divide the diagram and are accompanied by text explaining the resource allocation or reservation.
\* **Top Section:**
\* A red bar at the very top, possibly indicating a maximum resource limit or a control region.
\* A dashed line with the text "nbPrbs reserved For the candidate UE in BWP1 = old nbPRbs, reserved for the VUE". This indicates a reservation of "nbPrbs" (number of PRBs) for a candidate UE in a specific BWP.
\* A block labeled "Old PDSCH sub area 1" is associated with this section.
\* An orange block is labeled "new nbPrbs != number of Prbs worth Nb1 RBGs". This suggests a new calculation or allocation of PRBs for "Nb1 RBGs" (Resource Block Groups).
\* The text "Nb1 RBGs" is prominently displayed.
\* **Middle Section:**
\* A dashed line with the text "nbPrbs reserved For the other UEs in dedicated BWPs". This indicates a reservation of "nbPrbs" for other UEs using dedicated BWPs.
\* A dashed line with the text "Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1, also defines lowest PRB in new nbPrbs". This annotation explains that the lowest PRB of pre-booked RBGs in a PDSCH sub-area defines the lowest PRB in the new "nbPrbs".
\* **Bottom Section:**
\* A dashed line with the text "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with or without common channel)". This refers to "nbPrbs" related to "DCI 1\_0" (Downlink Control Information type 1-0), possibly indicating a reservation for control signaling.
\* A dashed line with the text "New PDSCH sub area 1".
\* A dashed line with the text "Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1". This marks the starting point of a PDSCH sub-area.
The diagram visually represents how different types of bandwidth parts and control information are mapped onto physical resource blocks, with specific reservations and definitions for different user equipment and channel types.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[CB014474-A]

fig1bis:  multiple MU eligible UEs converted to dynamicswitch RAT0.

partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 above a SSB in multi BWP configuration (there is no PRB blanking at the top of the PDSCH sub area, but there could be a partial RBG) . The figure depicts the process of dynamicswitch to RAT0 for multiple candidate initial transmission UEs which belong to a vUE in BWP1, and are the largest UEs in terms of PRB reservation within the pair.

* The candidate UEs are UE1 and UE2 which are present in vUE1 and vUE2 and collectively has nbPrbs worth of PRBs reserved to them in total (within vUE1 and vUE2) and reserved from the top of the PDSCH subarea in the BWP1.
* The reserved PRBs i.e nbPrbs of the candidate UEs as RAT1 are moved and pre booked into RBGs, given by Nb1 at the top of the old PDSCH sub area 1.
* The nbPrbs for the vUE are readjusted to become equal to number of PRBs worth the size of the Nb1 RBGs provided that the reservations of the other paired UEs in the vUE are unaffected. Note that, for the candidate UE , Nb1 RBGs could also include RBG(s) reserved as SU in which case nbPrbs for the vUE shall include only the PRBs worth the part of the Nb1 RBGs that are reserved as MU.
* The startPrb and number of PRBs for the other paired users remain unaffected. The other RAT1 UEs are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 1 above the DCI 1\_0 allocations (same start PRB, size updated). No new PDSCH sub area is created within the old PDSCH sub area 1.

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and user equipment (UE) allocations within a resource grid.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Resource Blocks (RBGs) and BWPs:**
\* **CORESET#0:** A blue bar on the far left, labeled "CORESET#0". This likely represents a control resource set.
\* **initial BWP:** A yellow bar next to CORESET#0, labeled "initial BWP". This is the initial bandwidth part allocated to a UE.
\* **dedicated BWP:** An orange bar next to the initial BWP, labeled "dedicated BWP". This is a dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **dedicated BWP 2:** A green bar, labeled "dedicated BWP 2". This indicates another dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **PS BWP-3:** A light orange bar, labeled "PS BWP-3". This might represent a Paging Service Bandwidth Part.
\* **Candidate UEs (UE1 and UE2):** Two vertical orange blocks labeled "Candidate UE1" and "Candidate UE2". These represent UEs that are candidates for some resource allocation. They are positioned within the "dedicated BWP" area.
\* **vUE1 and vUE2:** These labels indicate vertical positions or references for UE1 and UE2 respectively.
**Resource Allocation and Reservations:**
\* **Nb1 RBGs:** A large orange block on the right side of the diagram, labeled "Nb1 RBGs". This signifies a block of resource blocks.
\* **nbPrbs reserved For the candidate UEs in BWP1:** A dashed purple line indicates a reserved range of physical resource blocks (PRBs) for candidate UEs in BWP1.
\* **nbPrbs reserved For the other UEs in dedicated BWPs:** Another dashed purple line indicates reserved PRBs for other UEs in dedicated BWPs.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with or without common channel):** A dashed orange line indicates reserved PRBs for DCI (Downlink Control Information) format 1\_0, which can be used with or without a common channel.
\* **PDSCH sub area 1 available for non-MU RAT1 users:** A vertical arrow and text indicate a sub-area within the PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) that is available for non-Multi-User (MU) RAT1 (Radio Access Technology 1) users.
\* **Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1:** A dashed purple line marks the lowest PRB of pre-booked RBGs within PDSCH sub area 1.
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1:** A dashed orange line marks the starting PRB of PDSCH sub area 1.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and UEs are mapped onto the available radio resources, with specific areas reserved for control information and data transmission for different user types. The vertical arrows and dashed lines indicate the boundaries and extents of these resource allocations and reservations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06241156.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

fig1bis': multiple MU paired UEs converted to dynamicswitch to RAT0

partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 above a SSB in multi BWP configuration when there is PRB blanking at the top of the PDSCH area.

* Similar to fig1bis, after the conversion to dynamicswitch to RAT0 , the respective RBG is not included in the Nb1. However the nbPrbs does include those unaffected PRBs in this blanked RBG reserved for the other paired users in the vUE.
* The lowest PRB in the nbPrbs is determined by the lowest PRB in the lower most RBG included in Nb1 RBGs reserved as MU for the candidate UEs if there are more RBG(s) for this UE that are reserved as SU as well, provided that the reservations of the other paired UEs in the vUE are unaffected.
* The startPrb and number of PRBs for the other paired users remain unaffected. The other RAT1 UEs are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 1 above the DCI 1\_0 allocations (same start PRB, size updated). No new PDSCH sub area is created within the old PDSCH sub area 1.

[image-4][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and resource blocks (RBs) allocated to various user equipment (UEs) and control channels.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Allocation Blocks):**
\* **CORESET#0:** A blue block on the far left, likely representing a Control Resource Set for initial access or common control signaling.
\* **initial BWP:** A yellow block adjacent to CORESET#0, indicating the initial bandwidth part for a UE.
\* **dedicated BWP:** A larger orange block, representing a dedicated bandwidth part for a UE.
\* **dedicated BWP 2:** A green block, indicating another dedicated bandwidth part, possibly for a different UE or a different configuration.
\* **PS BWP-3:** A light blue block, followed by a light purple block, and a light green block. These likely represent a "Physical Shared Channel Bandwidth Part 3" and associated resource allocations.
**Middle Section (UE and Candidate UE Indicators):**
\* **vUE1:** A label indicating a virtual UE 1.
\* **Candidate UE1, Candidate UE2:** Two vertical orange blocks labeled "Candidate UE1" and "Candidate UE2" are shown within a larger blue-outlined area. These likely represent UEs that are candidates for receiving specific resources.
\* **UE1, UE2:** Labels "UE1" and "UE2" are positioned below the candidate UE blocks, indicating specific UEs.
\* **vUE2:** A label indicating a virtual UE 2.
**Right Side (Resource Allocation Descriptions and RBG Information):**
\* **nbPrbs reserved For the candidate UEs in BWP1:** This text, accompanied by a double-headed arrow, indicates a reservation of a certain number of physical resource blocks (PRBs) for candidate UEs within BWP1.
\* **Nb 1 RBGs:** This text, with a double-headed arrow, likely refers to a Resource Block Group (RBG) size or a count of RBGs.
\* **nbPrbs reserved For the other UEs in dedicated BWPs:** Another text annotation with a double-headed arrow, indicating PRB reservations for other UEs using dedicated BWPs.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with or without common channel):** This text, with a double-headed arrow, suggests PRB reservations for a specific type of Downlink Control Information (DCI) format 1\_0, which can be used with or without a common channel.
\* **Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area1:** This text indicates the starting point of pre-booked RBGs within a specific PDSCH sub-area.
\* **PDSCH sub area 1 available for non-MU RAT1 users:** This annotation describes the availability of a PDSCH sub-area for users of a non-Multi-User (MU) Radio Access Technology 1 (RAT1).
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1:** This text marks the starting physical resource block of PDSCH sub-area 1.
The diagram uses colored blocks to represent different resource allocations and BWPs, with vertical arrows and text annotations to explain the purpose and scope of these allocations. The horizontal dashed lines indicate boundaries or reference points for these allocations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06241204.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

[End CB014474-A]

fig 2:  partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 above a SSB in multi BWP configuration ( PRB blanking at the top of the PDSCH sub area) . It is an initial transmission of a candidate UE, but the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 don´t belong to this PDSCH sub area .The candidate UE belongs to the dedicated BWP2. The reserved PRBs of the RAT1 UEs which belong to the largest dedicated BWP1 are located at the top of the PDSCH sub area 1 in a new PDSCH sub area 3. The reserved PRBs of the RAT1 UEs which don´t belong to the largest dedciated BWP1 are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 1 above the SSB (same start PRB, size updated).The pre booked RBGs are at the top of the old PDSCH sub area 1 within the dedicated BWP2 (RAT1 UEs of BWP1 overlap BWP2).

[image-5][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) in a cellular network, likely for 5G New Radio (NR). The diagram uses colored bars of varying heights to represent different types of PRB allocations and their associated bandwidth parts (BWPs).
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (PRB Allocation Visualization):**
\* **CORESET#0 (Blue Bar):** Represents the control resource set 0, a fundamental component for initial access and control signaling.
\* **initial BWP (Yellow Bar):** Denotes the initial bandwidth part, used for initial communication with a User Equipment (UE).
\* **dedicated BWP (Orange Bar):** Indicates a dedicated bandwidth part allocated to a specific UE.
\* **dedicated BWP 2 (Green Bar):** Represents a second dedicated bandwidth part, likely for a different UE or a different service.
\* **PS BWP-3 (Light Blue Bar):** Stands for "Physical Shared Bandwidth Part 3," suggesting a shared resource allocation.
\* **Purple Bar:** Located below the light blue bar, it's part of the PS BWP-3 allocation.
\* **Green Bar (right side):** A tall green bar on the right side, positioned higher than the "dedicated BWP 2" bar.
\* **Orange Bar (top right):** A short orange bar at the top, positioned above the tall green bar.
\* **Stacked Bars (bottom right):** A stack of three smaller bars (green, light blue, purple) on the right side, aligned with the "PS BWP-3" label.
**Right Side (Explanations and Boundaries):**
\* **PRBs blanking (Orange dashed line with text):** Indicates a region where PRBs are intentionally not used or are reserved for specific purposes.
\* **nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWP 1 (Red dashed line with text):** Shows a reserved block of PRBs for other UEs within the dedicated BWP 1.
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 1 (Vertical black line with text):** Marks a boundary or region related to the Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) in an older configuration.
\* **New PDSCH sub area 3 (Text):** Refers to a new sub-area for PDSCH allocation.
\* **Nb1 RBGs (Text):** Likely refers to "Number of Resource Blocks Groups" or a similar grouping metric.
\* **Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1 (Purple dashed line with text):** Defines the upper boundary of pre-booked Resource Blocks within PDSCH sub area 1.
\* **Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1 (Purple dashed line with text):** Defines the lower boundary of pre-booked Resource Blocks within PDSCH sub area 1.
\* **nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWP 2 and 3 (Red dashed line with text):** Indicates reserved PRBs for other UEs within dedicated BWPs 2 and 3.
\* **SSB (Orange dashed line with text):** Likely refers to a Synchronization Signal Block, crucial for initial cell search.
\* **New PDSCH sub area 1 (Text):** Refers to a new sub-area for PDSCH allocation.
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1 (Orange dashed line with text):** Marks the starting point of PRBs for PDSCH sub area 1.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and reserved PRB blocks are organized within the overall spectrum, highlighting distinctions between initial, dedicated, and shared resources, as well as reserved blocks for other users and specific signaling components like SSB. The dashed lines indicate boundaries and regions of interest for PDSCH allocation and PRB usage.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.005.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

fig 2bis: partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 above a SSB in multi BWP configuration ( PRB blanking at the top of the PDSCH sub area) .

* It is an initial transmission of a candidate UE (non MU), but the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 don´t belong to this PDSCH sub area .
* The candidate UE belongs to the dedicated BWP2. The reserved PRBs of the RAT1 UEs which belong to the largest dedicated BWP1 are located at the top of the PDSCH sub area 1 for e.g a vUE in the largest dedicated BWP1 reserved from the top of the BWP1 ( Note that vUEs are allocated before the dynamicswitch candidate if any).
* The PDSCH sub area 1 is updated and the highest PRB of the new PDSCH sub area 1 is the PRB below the lower most PRB in the nbPrbs of the vUE(s) (See b. in the figure). This new PDSCH sub area 1 is then left for other proceedings as described in fig 2 above considering the dynamicswitch candidate in from BWP2. Note that the PDSCH sub area 1 is again updated (See c. in the figure). Note that new PDSCH sub area 3 formed (See d. in the figure).

[image-6][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their allocation within a system, likely related to cellular communication. The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent these BWPs and horizontal dashed lines to denote boundaries and allocations.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (BWPs):**
\* **CORESET#0:** Represented by a thin blue bar at the bottom left.
\* **initial BWP:** A yellow bar positioned to the right of CORESET#0.
\* **dedicated BWP:** A tall orange bar to the right of the initial BWP.
\* **dedicated BWP 2:** A tall green bar to the right of the dedicated BWP.
\* **PS BWP-3:** A light blue bar to the right of dedicated BWP 2, with a smaller purple bar and a very small light blue bar stacked on top of it.
**Right Side (Allocations and Labels):**
\* **PRBs blanking:** Indicated by a red bar at the very top, with a double-headed arrow pointing to a section labeled "nbPrbs reserved of the vUEs in dedicated BWP 1".
\* **a. Old PDSCH sub area 1:** A horizontal dashed line with a label indicating an allocation for "Old PDSCH" in "sub area 1".
\* **b. New PDSCH sub area 1:** Another horizontal dashed line with a label indicating an allocation for "New PDSCH" in "sub area 1".
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 2 and 3:** A double-headed arrow pointing to a section between two dashed lines, indicating reserved PRBs for other UEs in specific BWPs.
\* **SSB:** A label indicating the Synchronization Signal Block, with a double-headed arrow pointing to the lowest section of the stacked bars on the right.
\* **d. New PDSCH sub area 3:** A label indicating an allocation for "New PDSCH" in "sub area 3", with a double-headed arrow pointing to the top of a green bar.
\* **Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1:** A label indicating the highest PRB of pre-booked Resource Blocks (RBs) in PDSCH sub area 1, marked by a dashed line.
\* **Nb1 RBGs:** A label indicating "Nb1 RBGs" (Resource Blocks), with a double-headed arrow spanning between two dashed lines.
\* **Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGS in PDSCH sub area 1:** A label indicating the lowest PRB of pre-booked RBs in PDSCH sub area 1, marked by a dashed line.
\* **c. New PDSCH sub area 1:** A label indicating an allocation for "New PDSCH" in "sub area 1", with a double-headed arrow pointing to the bottom of a green bar.
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1:** A label indicating the starting PRB of PDSCH sub area 1, marked by a dashed line at the bottom.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and PDSCH allocations are structured and potentially overlap or are reserved within a given spectrum. The colors help differentiate the various BWPs, and the arrows and labels clarify the relationships and boundaries of these allocations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.006.png[/image\_path][/image-6]

fig 3:  partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 2 below DCI 1\_0 (with a common channel) in multi BWP configuration ( PRB blanking at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area) . It is an initial transmission of a candidate UE, the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 belong to this PDSCH sub area, the candidate UE belongs to the dedicated BWP2. The reserved PRBs of the RAT1 UEs are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 2 (same start PRB, size updated) The reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 are moved and pre booked into RBGs at the top of the old PDSCH sub area 2 (but below the DCI 1\_0 allocated below the common channel at the top of the old PDSCh sub area 2). No new PDSCH sub area is created within the old PDSCH sub area 2.

The CORESET 0 can contain DCI 1\_0 mapped with CORESET#X (with X>0) and the slot can be without common channel when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

[image-7][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource blocks (RBs) or physical resource blocks (PRBs).
The diagram is structured vertically, with different colored blocks representing different BWPs and their allocations. From left to right, the colored blocks are:
\* **Blue:** Labeled "CORESET#0". This likely represents a control resource set used for initial access and control signaling.
\* **Yellow:** Labeled "initial BWP". This is the first BWP allocated to a User Equipment (UE).
\* **Orange:** Labeled "dedicated BWP". This is a BWP dedicated to a specific UE.
\* **Green:** Labeled "dedicated BWP 2". This is another BWP dedicated to a UE, possibly a secondary or different type of dedicated BWP.
\* **Light Blue:** Labeled "PS BWP-3". "PS" might stand for "Physical Shared" or a similar term, indicating a shared BWP.
Horizontally, the diagram shows the allocation of PRBs within these BWPs. Several text labels and dashed lines indicate different resource allocation scenarios:
\* **"nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with common channel)"**: This label, associated with a dashed line, indicates a number of PRBs for DCI (Downlink Control Information) format 1\_0, which includes common channel information.
\* **"nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (below common channel)"**: Another label for DCI format 1\_0, specifically for resources below the common channel.
\* **"nbPrbs reserved for the UE in dedicated BWP 2"**: This text, with a dashed line pointing to the green "dedicated BWP 2" block, indicates reserved PRBs for a specific UE within that BWP.
\* **"Nb2 RBGs"**: This label, with a vertical arrow spanning a significant portion of the diagram, likely refers to a quantity of Resource Grid Blocks (RBGs) or a similar resource unit.
\* **"Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1"**: This text, with a dashed line, points to a specific PRB range, indicating the lowest PRB of pre-booked resources in a PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) sub-area.
\* **"Old PDSCH sub area 2"**: This label, with a dashed line, indicates an older or previous PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWPs in PDSCH sub area 2"**: This text, with a dashed line, indicates reserved PRBs for other UEs within dedicated BWPs in a PDSCH sub-area 2.
\* **"New PDSCH sub area 2"**: This label, with a dashed line, indicates a new PDSCH sub-area 2.
\* **"Start PRB of the PDSCH sub area 2"**: This label, with a dashed line, marks the starting PRB for PDSCH sub-area 2.
\* **"PRBs blanking"**: This label, with a dashed line, indicates a region where PRBs are blanked or unused.
There is also a small orange block within the "PS BWP-3" area, and a red block at the very bottom, possibly representing a control region or a different type of resource. The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and sub-areas are utilized and how PRBs are allocated or reserved for various purposes, including control signaling and data transmission for different UEs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.007.png[/image\_path][/image-7]

fig 3bis:  half view of the old PDSCH sub area 2 below DCI 1\_0 (with a common channel) in multi BWP configuration ( PRB blanking at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area) .

* It is an initial transmission of a candidate UE (non MU), the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 belong to this PDSCH sub area, the candidate UE belongs to the dedicated BWP2.
* The reserved PRBs, nbPrbs for the vUE(s) from BWP1 are located in the top of the old PDSCH sub area 2. The PDSCH sub area 2 is updated (See b. in the figures).
* The reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 are moved and pre booked into RBGs at the top of the new PDSCH sub area 2 (but below lower most PRB of the nbPrbs of the vUE(s)). The PDSCH sub area 2 is updated again (See c. figure).
* The reserved PRBs of the other RAT1 UEs are located at the bottom of the new PDSCH sub area 2. A new PDSCH sub area 4 is created (See d. in the figure).

[image-8][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR) or a similar technology. It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and sub-areas within the frequency domain, along with reserved and allocated resource blocks (RBs).
The diagram is structured vertically, with time or frequency progressing from left to right. On the left side, several colored blocks represent different BWPs:
\* **CORESET#0**: A blue block on the far left, labeled "CORESET#0".
\* **initial BWP**: A yellow block next to CORESET#0, labeled "initial BWP".
\* **dedicated BWP**: An orange block, labeled "dedicated BWP".
\* **dedicated BWP 2**: A green block, labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
\* **PS BWP-3**: A light blue block, labeled "PS BWP-3".
To the right of these BWPs, the diagram shows different sub-areas and resource allocations, indicated by horizontal dashed lines and colored blocks:
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with common channel)**: This is indicated by a dashed line and text at the top, suggesting a range of resource blocks reserved for DCI (Downlink Control Information) Type 1\_0, including common channels.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (below common channel)**: Another dashed line and text below the previous one, indicating resource blocks reserved for DCI Type 1\_0 below the common channel.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the vUEs in dedicated BWP 1**: Text and a dashed line pointing to a section of the diagram.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP 2**: Text and a dashed line pointing to a section of the diagram.
\* **d. New PDSCH sub area 4**: Text indicating a new Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) sub-area.
\* **Highest RB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 2**: Text and a dashed line pointing to the upper boundary of a green block.
\* **Nb2 RBGs**: Text indicating a quantity of resource groups.
\* **Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 2**: Text and a dashed line pointing to the lower boundary of the green block.
\* **b. New PDSCH sub area 2**: Text indicating a new PDSCH sub-area.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWPs in PDSCH sub area 2**: Text and a dashed line pointing to a section of the diagram.
\* **a. Old PDSCH sub area 2**: Text indicating an old PDSCH sub-area.
\* **PRBs blanking**: Text indicating blanked resource blocks at the bottom.
\* **c. New PDSCH sub area 2**: Text indicating another new PDSCH sub-area.
\* **Start PRB of the PDSCH sub area 2**: Text indicating the starting resource block for a PDSCH sub-area.
There are also smaller colored blocks within the main BWPs, such as a blue and orange block within the PS BWP-3, and a green block labeled "Nb2 RBGs" in the middle right section. An orange block and a light green block are also present below the "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP 2" label. A red block is at the very bottom, labeled "PRBs blanking".
The diagram uses color coding and dashed lines to visually represent the allocation and reservation of resources across different BWPs and sub-areas, likely for scheduling and control purposes in a wireless communication system.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.008.png[/image\_path][/image-8]

fig6: half view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 below a CAT-M PRBs area (FDD DSS)  and at the start of the CORESET 0. The candidate UE is an initial transmission. The other RAT1 UEs (DCI 1\_1 and DCI 1\_0) are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 1  (same start PRB, size updated).

[image-11][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G NR (New Radio). It depicts different types of resource blocks (PRBs) and their allocation across various sub-areas and user equipment (UE) configurations.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Blocks/Areas):**
\* **Red Bar:** At the very bottom, a red bar labeled "CAT-M PRBs area" indicates a specific region of PRBs designated for CAT-M (Category M) devices.
\* **Blue Bar:** Above the red bar, a blue bar labeled "CORESET #0" represents a Control Resource Set, a fundamental component for initial access and control signaling.
\* **Yellow Bar:** Next to the CORESET, a yellow bar labeled "initial BWP" signifies the initial Bandwidth Part, a flexible way to adapt the UE's bandwidth.
\* **Orange Bar (Left):** A tall, light orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP" represents a dedicated Bandwidth Part, likely allocated to a specific UE or service.
\* **Orange Bar (Right):** A shorter, darker orange bar is positioned to the right, representing a different allocation or usage of PRBs.
**Right Side (Labels and Definitions):**
\* **"PRBs blanking":** This label, with a dashed arrow pointing to the top of the diagram, indicates a region where PRBs are intentionally not used or are "blanked."
\* **"Old PDSCH sub area 3":** A dashed line and label indicate a previous or "old" sub-area for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) transmission.
\* **"Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 3":** This label, with a dashed line, defines the upper boundary of a set of pre-booked Resource Blocks within PDSCH sub-area 3.
\* **"Nb1 RBGs for dyn RATO Retransmission":** This label, with a dashed line, refers to a specific number of Resource Blocks (Nb1 RBGs) used for dynamic RAT (Radio Access Technology) handover retransmissions.
\* **"Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 3":** This label, with a dashed line, defines the lower boundary of the same set of pre-booked Resource Blocks.
\* **"nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWP":** This label, with a double-headed arrow spanning a portion of the "dedicated BWP" area, indicates the number of PRBs reserved for other UEs within that dedicated BWP.
\* **"New PDSCH sub area 3":** This label, with a dashed line, indicates a newer or current sub-area for PDSCH transmission.
\* **"Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 3":** This label, with a dashed line, marks the starting PRB of PDSCH sub-area 3.
The diagram visually represents how different PRB allocations and definitions relate to each other, likely to explain resource management strategies in a mobile network, possibly highlighting differences between older and newer configurations or specific use cases like CAT-M and dynamic RAT handovers.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.011.png[/image\_path][/image-11]

fig 9: partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 above an inner guardband in multi BWP configuration. The candidate UE is a retransmission. The candidate UE belongs to the dedicated BWP2. The reserved PRBs of the RAT1 UEs (retransmission) which belong to the largest dedicated BWP1 are located at the top of the PDSCH sub area 1 in a new PDSCH sub area 3, The reserved PRBs of the RAT1 UEs (retransmission) which don´t belong to the largest dedicated BWP1 are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 1 above the aditionnal blanking PRBs area(same start PRB, size updated). The pre booked RBGs are at the top of the old PDSCH sub area 1 within the dedicated BWP2 (no overlap in BWP2).

On the left side, there are three vertical bars representing allocated PRB resources. The leftmost bar is yellow and labeled "dedicated BWP". The bar next to it is green and labeled "dedicated BWP 2". To the right of these, there's an orange bar. Below these bars, there's a red bar labeled "Additional blanking PRBs area".
The diagram uses horizontal dashed lines to demarcate different regions and allocations. Arrows indicate the extent of these regions.
On the right side, text labels describe the purpose or status of these PRB allocations:
\* "PDSCH sub area 1" is indicated at the top.
\* "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 1" is associated with the top portion of the diagram.
\* "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP 2" is associated with the middle portion.
\* "Old PDSCH sub area 1" is a label pointing to a dashed line.
\* "Highest PRB position of the pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1" is a label associated with the upper part of the right-hand side.
\* "Nb1 RBGs for dynamicswitch RATO retransmission" is a label in the middle.
\* "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 2" is a label associated with the lower part of the left-hand side.
\* "Lowest PRB position of the pre booked RBGs In PDSCH sub area 1" is a label associated with the lower part of the right-hand side.
\* "new PDSCH sub area 1" is a label at the very bottom right.
The diagram visually represents how PRBs are divided and allocated, possibly showing a scenario where resources are reserved for specific users or functionalities, and how these allocations might change or be managed dynamically. The colors likely represent different types of allocations or users.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.012.png[/image\_path][/image-12]

fig10: partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 1 below an inner guardband and above a SSB in multi BWP configuration. The candidate UE is a retransmission. The candidate UE belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1. The pre booked RBGs are located at the top of the old PDSCH sub area 1. The other RAT1 UEs (retransmission) are located at the bottom of the updated PDSCH sub area 1 above the SSB (same start PRB, size updated). [image-13][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different areas and their associated resource blocks (PRBs) and user equipment (UE) configurations.
The diagram shows several colored rectangular blocks representing resource allocations. On the left, there are two vertical blocks labeled "dedicated BWP-1" (yellow) and "dedicated BWP 2" (green). Below these, a red horizontal block is labeled "Low edge blanked PRBs area".
To the right of these dedicated BWPs, there are several horizontal dashed lines and text annotations indicating different resource areas and their properties.
Key text elements and their likely meanings are:
\* **"Additional blanking PRBs area"**: This suggests a region of PRBs that are intentionally left blank or unused, possibly for interference management or specific signaling.
\* **"nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP 1"**: This indicates a number of PRBs reserved for a specific UE within its dedicated Bandwidth Part (BWP) 1.
\* **"Old PDSCH sub area 1"**: This refers to a previous or legacy sub-area for Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) resource allocation.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 3"**: This likely represents a newer or different sub-area for PDSCH resource allocation.
\* **"Highest PRB of the pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1"**: This annotation points to the highest-indexed PRB within a pre-booked set of Resource Block Groups (RBGs) in the old PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"Nb1 RBGs for dynamicswitch RATO retransmission"**: This suggests a number of RBGs allocated for retransmissions during a dynamic switch between Radio Access Technologies (RATs).
\* **"Lowest PRB of pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 1"**: This annotation points to the lowest-indexed PRB within the same pre-booked RBGs in the old PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWPs"**: This indicates PRBs reserved for other UEs that are also using dedicated BWPs.
\* **"New PDSCH sub area 1"**: This refers to a current or new sub-area for PDSCH resource allocation.
There is also an orange rectangular block positioned above the "nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWPs" section, which might represent a specific allocation or reservation within the PDSCH sub-areas. The diagram uses vertical arrows to indicate the extent of these areas and reservations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13284.013.png[/image\_path][/image-13]

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11176901`)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11176905`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
PDSCH sub area update update (initial and retransmission dynamicswitch RAT0)

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11176915`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
updating the size of the PDSCH sub area above the pre booked RBGs for a dynamicswitch RAT0 starting at the bottom of a PDSCH sub area (initial and retransmission dynamicswitch RAT0)

[CB013945-A] Note: If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDSCH sub area update. Below requirement defines the rules for further update of the reduced PDSCH sub area for non-MU paired users depending on whether the initial PDSCH sub area size was updated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or not.[End CB013945-A]

gNB-DU shall update the startPrb and the size of the PDSCH sub area above pre booked RBGs of a dynamicswitch RAT0 with the PRB above the highest PRB of the RBGs pre booked in this PDSCH sub area, the start PRB and the size  of the existing PDSCH sub area in this way:

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]  = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] + rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]  -  startPrbHighRbg(n+1)

rmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] = startPrbHighRbg(n+1)

Where:

- startPrbHighRbg(n+1) is the PRB above the highest PRB of the RBGs pre booked in this PDSCH sub area n+1

- rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] and rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]  are the start Prb  and the size of the old PDSCH sub area n+1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The figures for the use cases with RBGs at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area are under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25686\_replaced([11176935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11176921`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
remaining PRBs udate only for an initial transmission dynamicswitch RAT0

---

**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11176931`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
updating the remaining PRBs in the PDSCH sub area due to pre booked RBGs for a dynamicswitch RAT0 starting at the bottom of a PDSCH sub area when it is an initial dynamicswitch RAT0

[CB013945-A] Note: If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDSCH sub area update. Below requirement defines the rules for further update of the reduced PDSCH sub area for non-MU paired users depending on whether the initial PDSCH sub area size was updated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or not.[End CB013945-A]

1) If single dedicated BWP,

When it is an initial transmission for the dynamicswitch RAT0 and the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 belong to the PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall update the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area by removing the number of  PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area and shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with the new number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area n

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n+1) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n+1) - nbReservedPrbsUe

rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n+1)

Where:

- nbReservedPrbsUe is the number of PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area n+1

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n+1) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP witin the PDSCH sub area n+1 n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+1]  is the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area n+1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If the candidate UE has no reserved PRB in the PDSCH sub area for an initial transmission  (pre booked RBGs in another PDSCH sub area than the PDSCH sub area where PRBS were reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1)  or a retransmission, the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area is not updated and gNB-DU shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with new size of the PDSCH sub area.

rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n)

Where:

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) is the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP witin the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]  is the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

2) If multi dedicated BWP is configured,

When it is an initial transmission for the dynamicswitch RAT0 and the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 belong to the PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall update the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) (where the candidate UE belongs to) within the PDSCH sub area (n + 1) by removing the number of PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area and shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with the new or current number of reserved PRBs for all the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area  and the new size of the PDSCH sub area.

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n+1) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n+1) - nbReservedPrbsUe

Where:

- p (1 or 2) is the number of the dedicated BWP where the candidate UE had reserved PRBs as RAT1

rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) for all the BWP(p)

Where:

- nbReservedPrbsUe is the number of PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area n + 1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n+1) is the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area n +1  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][p]  is the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP(p) inside the PDSCH sub area n + 1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]  is the new size of the PDSCH sub area n + 1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If the candidate UE has no reserved PRB in the dediacted BWP(p) in the PDSCH sub area for an initial transmission  (pre booked RBGs in another PDSCH sub area than the PDSCH sub area where PRBS were reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1) or a retransmission, the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area of PRBs is not updated and gNB-DU shall update the number of remaining PRBs inside the PDSCH sub area with new size of the PDSCH sub area.

rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] =  rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]   -  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)

Where:

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24758)

- rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]  is the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP(p) inside the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  is the new size of the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

When all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including the common channel, the rach msg2 and rach msg4) are mapped on the CORESET 0 or when all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (including the rach msg2) are mapped o the CORESET#X (with X>0), in a slot when there is no common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) scheduled , but there are other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode such as a rach msg2, a rach msg4 or any DCI 1\_0 in transient phase, gNB-DU shall update the variables of the CORESET #0 (if present in the PDSCH sub area) within the PDSCH sub area which contains this CORESET as it is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25184\_replaced([11176746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The figures for the use cases with RBGs at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area are under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25686\_replaced([11176935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
