**3.29.5.4.2.0-12.0-2.0-3.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12678897`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
12678897:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
figures of use cases when RBGs pre booked for a dynamicswitch RAT0 at the bottom of a PDSCH sub area

Note: These figures are partial view of the carrier bandwidth, only showing a PDSCH sub area which is below or above a SSB, a CORESET 0,  an additional blanking PRBs area ... The purpose is to illustrate better how this existing PDSCH sub area is updated (and split in 2 if needed) because of RBGs pre booking.

fig 1:  partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 2 below DCI 1\_0 no common channel) in multi BWP configuration ( no PRB blanking at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area) . The candidate UE is an initial transmission , the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1  belong to this PDSCH sub area, the candidate UE belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1. The reserved PRBs of the RAT1 UEs are located at the top of the updated PDSCH sub area 2 ( start PRB updated, size updated) The reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 are moved and pre booked into RBGs at the bottom of the old PDSCH sub area 2. No new PDSCH sub area is created within the old PDSCH sub area 2.

The CORESET 0 can contain DCI 1\_0 mapped with CORESET#X (with X>0) when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent different types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated PRB allocations.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Allocation):**
\* **CORESET#0:** Represented by a thin blue bar on the far left. This likely refers to a Control Resource Set used for control signaling.
\* **Initial BWP:** A wide yellow bar, indicating the initial bandwidth part allocated to a User Equipment (UE).
\* **Dedicated BWP:** A wide orange bar, suggesting a dedicated bandwidth part for a specific UE.
\* **Dedicated BWP 2:** A wide green bar, another dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **PS BWP-3:** A wide light blue bar, possibly representing a "partial" or "secondary" bandwidth part.
\* **Orange Bar:** A segment of an orange bar, positioned below the PS BWP-3, indicating a specific allocation.
\* **Green Bar:** A segment of a green bar, positioned above the orange bar and below the PS BWP-3, indicating another allocation.
\* **Purple Bar:** A segment of a purple bar, positioned above the green bar, indicating a further allocation.
\* **Light Orange Bar:** A segment of a light orange bar, positioned below the green bar and above the orange bar.
**Right Side (Explanations and Boundaries):**
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel):** This text, with a red wavy underline, is associated with the purple bar, indicating a number of PRBs allocated for DCI (Downlink Control Information) format 1\_0, and it's not for a common channel.
\* **nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWPs in PDSCH sub area 2:** This text, also with a red wavy underline, is associated with the green and light orange bars, indicating PRBs reserved for other UEs in their dedicated BWPs within a specific PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) sub-area.
\* **New PDSCH sub area 2:** A label pointing to the upper boundary of the area described by the previous text.
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 2:** A label pointing to a lower boundary, below the green and light orange bars.
\* **nbPrbs reserved for the candidateUE in dedicated BWP 1:** This text, with a red wavy underline, is associated with the orange bar at the bottom, indicating PRBs reserved for a specific candidate UE in its dedicated BWP 1.
\* **Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 2:** This text, with a red wavy underline, is associated with the upper boundary of the large orange bar at the bottom, indicating the highest PRB of pre-booked Resource Blocks (RBs) in PDSCH sub-area 2.
\* **Nb2 RBGs:** A label indicating "Number of 2 Resource Blocks".
\* **Start PRB of the old PDSCH sub area 2:** This text, with a red wavy underline, is associated with the lower boundary of the large orange bar at the bottom, indicating the starting PRB of the old PDSCH sub-area 2.
The diagram uses dashed orange and purple lines to denote the boundaries of these allocated PRB groups. Vertical arrows are used to indicate the extent of these allocations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13294.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

fig 1bis:  partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 2 below DCI 1\_0 no common channel) in multi BWP configuration ( no PRB blanking at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area) . The candidate UE is an initial transmission , the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1  belong to this PDSCH sub area, the candidate UE belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1. The reserved PRBs of the RAT1 vUE(s) are located at the top of the old PDSCH sub area 2. The PDSCH sub area 2 is updated (See b. figure) ( start PRB updated, size updated). The reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 are moved and pre booked into RBGs at the bottom of the new PDSCH sub area 2. A new PDSCH sub area 4 is created within the new PDSCH sub area 2 (See c. in figure).

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) in a cellular network, likely 5G NR, for different types of data channels and bandwidth parts (BWPs). The diagram uses colored vertical bars to represent different BWPs and their associated PRB allocations.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (BWPs and Channels):**
\* **CORESET#0:** Represented by a thin blue bar at the very left. This likely refers to a Control Resource Set used for initial access or control signaling.
\* **initial BWP:** A yellow bar immediately to the right of CORESET#0. This is the initial bandwidth part allocated to a User Equipment (UE).
\* **dedicated BWP:** An orange bar to the right of the initial BWP. This indicates a dedicated bandwidth part for the UE.
\* **dedicated BWP 2:** A green bar to the right of the first dedicated BWP. This suggests another dedicated bandwidth part, possibly for different services or configurations.
\* **PS BWP-3:** A light blue bar to the right of dedicated BWP 2. "PS" might stand for "Partial" or "Primary/Secondary," and BWP-3 indicates a third bandwidth part.
\* **Orange Area (within PS BWP-3):** This area is further subdivided into smaller rectangular blocks with blue outlines. This section is labeled with various annotations related to PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) allocations.
**Right Side (PRB Allocation Descriptions and Labels):**
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel):** This label, with a wavy underline, indicates the number of PRBs associated with DCI (Downlink Control Information) format 1\_0, and it's specified that it's not for a common channel. This is positioned above the main orange area.
\* **nbPrbs reserved for the vUE(s) in PDSCH sub area 2:** This label, also with a wavy underline, points to a section within the orange area. It signifies PRBs reserved for the UE(s) within a specific PDSCH sub-area.
\* **a. Old PDSCH sub area 2:** This label, with a vertical arrow, defines the extent of an older PDSCH allocation within sub-area 2.
\* **c. New PDSCH sub area 4:** This label, with a double-headed arrow, indicates a new PDSCH allocation within sub-area 4.
\* **b. New PDSCH sub area 2:** This label, with a vertical arrow, defines the extent of a new PDSCH allocation within sub-area 2.
\* **Number of Prbs reserved as RAT1:** This label, with a wavy underline, is placed over a solid orange rectangular block. It indicates the number of PRBs reserved for a specific RAT (Radio Access Technology), likely for inter-RAT handover or sharing.
\* **Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 2:** This label, with a wavy underline, points to the upper boundary of a section within the orange area, indicating the highest PRB index for pre-booked Resource Block Groups (RBGs) in PDSCH sub-area 2.
\* **Nb2 RBGs:** This label, with a wavy underline, is positioned below the "Highest PRB" label and likely refers to a quantity of Resource Block Groups.
\* **Start PRB of the old PDSCH sub area 2:** This label points to the lower boundary of the orange area, marking the starting PRB for the old PDSCH allocation in sub-area 2.
The diagram visually represents how PRBs are partitioned and allocated for different purposes, highlighting the concept of bandwidth parts and specific channel allocations within those parts. The use of dashed lines and arrows helps to delineate the boundaries and relationships between different allocated resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13294.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[CB014474-A]

fig 1bis: multiple paired UEs converted to dynamicswitch RAT0

partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 2 below DCI 1\_0 (with a common channel) in multi BWP configuration ( no PRB blanking at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area).

The number of PRBs (nbPrbs) in the vUE' contains the PRBs worth 1 RBG or more that are available in the PDSCH subarea and are given to the candidate UEs UE1 and UE2 for dynamicswitch to RAT0. Both these users shall be assigned the same startPrb and nbPrbs. Note that there is no fresh vUE creation at this stage and these users are just co-scheduled. The Mu-MCS for these two candidate users and all the other users that participated in any vUE(s) with these two candidate users, shall be recalculated since the allocation sizes for these candidate users increase due to more PRBs allocated. The highest and lowest most PRBs in the RBGs assigend are shown in the figure. The PDSCH subarea size shall be updated based on the vUEs size. The unsused PRBs if any can be reassigned to any other RAT user belonging to this PDSCH subarea.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their allocation in a communication system. The diagram is organized horizontally, with different colored blocks representing different BWPs.
From left to right, the blocks are:
1. A blue block labeled "CORESET#0".
2. A yellow block labeled "initial BWP".
3. An orange block labeled "dedicated BWP".
4. A green block labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
5. A light blue block labeled "PS BWP-3".
6. A vertical arrangement of two blue rectangles, one labeled "Candidate UE1" and the other "Candidate UE2".
7. A light orange block.
8. A large orange block.
There are also several annotations and arrows indicating relationships and ranges:
\* An arrow pointing downwards from a dashed orange line to another dashed orange line, with the text "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel)" between them.
\* An arrow pointing downwards from a dashed orange line to a dashed purple line, with the text "Nb2 RBGs" next to it.
\* To the right of the orange block, there are two text annotations:
\* "Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 2" aligned with the top of the orange block.
\* "Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 2" aligned with the bottom of the orange block.
\* Below the light orange block, there is text "PDSCH sub area 2 for non MU RAT1 users" with a vertical arrow spanning from the top of the light orange block to a dashed orange line below it.
\* A dashed orange line at the bottom of the diagram is labeled "Start PRB of the old PDSCH sub area 2".
\* A small red text "vUE'" is located near the bottom of the "PS BWP-3" block and the light orange block.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06241246.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[End CB014474-A]

fig 2:  partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 2 below DCI 1\_0 (with a common channel) in multi BWP configuration ( no PRB blanking at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area) .

The candidate UE is an initial transmission non MU UE, the reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 belong to this PDSCH sub area, the candidate UE belongs to the dedicated BWP2.

The reserved PRBs of the RAT1 UEs (including the DCI 1\_0 below the common channel) are located at the top of the updated PDSCH sub area 2 ( start PRB updated, size updated) The reserved PRBs of the candidate UE as RAT1 are moved and pre booked into RBGs at the bottom of the old PDSCH sub area 2. No new PDSCH sub area is created within the old PDSCH sub area 2.

The CORESET 0 can contain DCI 1\_0 mapped with CORESET#X (with X>0) and slot can be without common channel when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and sub-areas within the Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH).
On the left side, there are vertical bars representing different BWPs: "CORESET#0", "initial BWP", "dedicated BWP", "dedicated BWP 2", and "PS BWP-3". These bars are color-coded: yellow for CORESET#0, orange for initial BWP, light green for dedicated BWP, darker green for dedicated BWP 2, and light blue for PS BWP-3.
The diagram shows horizontal dashed lines indicating different resource divisions and associated text labels.
Key text labels and their corresponding visual representations are:
\* **"nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 with common channel)"**: This label is associated with a purple colored block, indicating a certain number of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) allocated for DCI format 1\_0 with a common channel.
\* **"nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 Below common channel)"**: This label is associated with a light blue colored block, indicating PRBs for DCI format 1\_0 below the common channel.
\* **"nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWPs in PDSCH sub area 2"**: This label points to a section of the diagram that includes a light orange block and a portion of the darker green block, suggesting reserved PRBs for other UEs in their dedicated BWPs within a specific PDSCH sub-area.
\* **"Old PDSCH sub area 2"**: This label marks a horizontal boundary.
\* **"nbPrbs reserved for the candidate UE in dedicated BWP 2"**: This label points to a green colored block, indicating reserved PRBs for a specific candidate UE within its dedicated BWP 2.
\* **"New PDSCH sub area 2"**: This label indicates a new sub-area.
\* **"Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 2"**: This label is associated with a horizontal dashed line above the green block representing the candidate UE's allocation.
\* **"Nb2 RBGs"**: This label is placed between two horizontal dashed lines, likely indicating a quantity of resource blocks.
\* **"Start PRB of the old PDSCH sub area 2"**: This label is associated with the lowest horizontal dashed line in the diagram.
The diagram visually represents how PRBs are partitioned and allocated across different BWPs and PDSCH sub-areas, distinguishing between common channels, dedicated BWPs for other UEs, and dedicated BWPs for a specific candidate UE. It also highlights the concept of pre-booked resource blocks.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13294.003.png[/image\_path][/image-3]The same figure can be imagined with vUE(s) inline with the above figure 1bis.

fig 3:  partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 3 below DCI 1\_0 (with a common channel) in multi BWP configuration ( no PRB blanking at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area) . The candidate UE is a retransmission, the candidate UE belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1. The reserved PRBs (retransmission) of the RAT1 UEs (including the DCI 1\_0 below the common channel) are located at the top of the updated PDSCH sub area 3 ( start PRB updated, size updated) The pre booked RBGs are located at the bottom of the old PDSCH sub area 3. No new PDSCH sub area is created within the old PDSCH sub area 3.

The CORESET 0 can contain DCI 1\_0 mapped with CORESET#X (with X>0) and slot can be without common channel when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

[image-4][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource blocks (RBs).
On the left side, there are vertical colored bars representing different BWPs:
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
- A small peach-colored rectangle is positioned above and to the right of the green bar.
On the right side, there are text labels and horizontal dashed lines indicating different resource allocations and sub-areas:
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with common channel)" is indicated by a dashed line pointing to the top portion of the diagram.
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (below common channel)" is indicated by a dashed line pointing to a section below the previous one.
- "nbPrbs reserved for Ues in dedicated BWP 1 and BWP 2" is indicated by a dashed line pointing to the area encompassing the orange and green BWPs.
- "New PDSCH sub area 3" is a label with a vertical arrow pointing downwards.
- "Old PDSCH sub area 3" is a label with a vertical arrow pointing downwards.
- A horizontal dashed purple line is present.
- An orange rectangle is shown to the right, below the purple dashed line.
- "Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 3" is a label associated with the purple dashed line.
- "Nb3 RBGs for dyn RATO Retransmission" is a label below the previous one.
- "Start PRB of the old PDSCH sub area 3" is a label at the bottom.
The diagram visually represents how different types of data or control information are mapped to specific resource blocks within the allocated bandwidth, potentially showing a dynamic allocation strategy.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13294.004.png[/image\_path][/image-4]The same handling as depicted above is followed when there are vUE(s) in the slot and in the selection phase for dynamicswitch to RAT0 candidate, a retransmission UE selected as described above. If there are no other vUE(s) with retransmission constructed, this retransmission UE that was allocated in old PDSCH sub area 3 from low to high in RAT1 is upconverted to RBGs (given by Nb3), and new PDSCH sub area 3 is created as shown above**. Note: If there are also vUE(s) with retransmissions in the above scenario, first these vUE(s) must be reserved and PDSCH sub area updated. Refer to figure 1bis as a example where the vUE(s) are shown.**

fig4: partial view of the old PDSCH sub area 2 below a CAT-M PRBs area (FDD DSS). The candidate UE is a retransmission. The candidate UE belongs to the dedicated BWP. The pre booked RBGs are located at the bottom of the old PDSCH sub area 2. The PDSCH sub area 2 is updated (start Prb and size).

[image-5][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating resource allocation, likely within a cellular network context. The diagram uses colored blocks and text labels to represent different resource areas and their functions.
On the left side, there are three vertical colored blocks. The leftmost block is cyan and labeled "CORESE T#0". To its right is a yellow block labeled "initial BWP". The rightmost block is orange and labeled "dedicated BWP". These blocks appear to represent initial bandwidth parts (BWPs) and dedicated BWPs, possibly for different transmission modes or services.
To the right of these blocks, there are horizontal dashed lines and text labels indicating different sub-areas and their characteristics. A red horizontal block is positioned at the top, above the "CORESE T#0" block. A dashed purple line runs horizontally across the image, with a label "CAT-M PRBs area" above it and "PDSCH sub area 2" to its right, with a double-headed arrow indicating a range.
Below the purple dashed line, there is a large orange vertical block. To the right of this orange block, text labels describe its context. "Old PDSCH sub area 2" is indicated by a vertical arrow pointing from the purple dashed line down to a lower dashed orange line. Another label, "Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in PDSCH sub area 2", is positioned to the right of the upper part of the orange block, aligned with the purple dashed line.
Further down, to the right of the lower part of the orange block, the text "Nb1 RBGs for dyn RATO Retransmission" is displayed. A vertical arrow originates from the purple dashed line and points downwards, ending near the bottom of the orange block.
Finally, at the bottom of the diagram, a dashed orange line spans the width, and the text "Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 2" is placed to its right, aligned with the bottom of the orange block.
The diagram visually represents the partitioning and allocation of radio resource blocks (RBs) or physical resource blocks (PRBs) for different purposes, including initial access, dedicated data transmission, and retransmissions, within specific sub-areas of a PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel). The "CAT-M PRBs area" suggests a specific allocation for Category-M devices.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13294.005.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

###### 3.29.5.4.2.0-13 PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation Interaction of RAT1 with PDCCH symbol shared with PDSCH (ID: 11176983)

**3.29.5.4.2.0-13.0-1**  (ID: `11176992`)

PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
11176992:
PDSCH sub areas handling for DL RAT1 allocation
If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actPdcchSharingWithPdsch is set to True, the feature introduces dynamic PDCCH and multiplexing of PDSCH with PDCCH symbol, from symbol 0 and a single PDSCH UE to be scheduled per slot. For more details on the algorithm description refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25284\_replaced([11183082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvH4lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 
When NRCELL.actRAT1Alloc = True, and NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc = True, for UE which is dynamic switch capable, gNB-DU shall give higher preference for the UE to be scheduled as dynamic switch RAT0 instead of RAT1 PDSCH allocations. Since with RAT1, UE can be scheduled only in one of the PDSCH sub-areas which can have reduced peak throughput.
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams illustrating different resource allocation scenarios in a cellular network, labeled "Case 1" and "Case 2". Both diagrams depict a PDSCH Sub-area, which is a vertical rectangular region representing a portion of the downlink shared channel. Within this sub-area, various resource blocks (PRBs) are allocated for different purposes.
**Case 1: RAT1 PRB allocations from symbol 0 in one of the PDSCH Sub-areas ratematched around its own DCI**
This diagram shows a PDSCH Sub-area with the following allocations:
\* **Large BWP (Brownish-beige):** Occupies the top portion of the sub-area.
\* **CORESET 1 (Dark green):** A narrow vertical strip on the left side, overlapping with the Large BWP.
\* **SSB/PBCH (Yellow):** A horizontal rectangular block located below the Large BWP and partially overlapping with CORESET 1.
\* **Small BWP (Light blue):** A rectangular block below the SSB/PBCH.
\* **Small BWP dedicate CORESET2 (Red outline):** A narrow vertical strip on the left side, overlapping with the Small BWP.
**Case 2: dynamic switch RATO RBG allocation from symbol 0 ratematched around its own DCI**
This diagram also shows a PDSCH Sub-area with allocations, but with a different arrangement:
\* **Large BWP (Brownish-beige):** Occupies the top portion of the sub-area.
\* **CORESET 1 (Dark green):** A narrow vertical strip on the left side, overlapping with the Large BWP.
\* **CORESET 2 (Purple):** A narrow vertical strip to the right of CORESET 1, also overlapping with the Large BWP.
\* **SSB/PBCH (Yellow):** A horizontal rectangular block located below the Large BWP and overlapping with CORESET 1 and CORESET 2.
\* **Small BWP (Light blue):** A rectangular block below the SSB/PBCH.
Below the diagrams, a legend explains the color coding:
\* **Large BWP (Light green outline):** Represents the Large BWP.
\* **Large BWP dedicate CORESET1 (Dark green):** Represents the Large BWP dedicated to CORESET 1.
\* **Small BWP (Light blue outline):** Represents the Small BWP.
\* **Small BWP dedicate CORESET2 (Red outline):** Represents the Small BWP dedicated to CORESET 2.
\* **Case 1. RAT1 PRB allocations (Pink):** This color is used in the diagrams to represent allocations specific to Case 1.
\* **Case 2. RATO RBG allocations (Pink):** This color is also used in the diagrams to represent allocations specific to Case 2.
The text in the image is as follows:
**Case 1. RAT1 PRB allocations from symbol 0 in one of the PDSCH Sub-areas ratematched around its own DCI**
**CORESET 1**
**SSB/PBCH**
**Small BWP**
**Large BWP**
**Large BWP dedicate CORESET1**
**Small BWP dedicate CORESET2**
**PDSCH Sub-area**
**CORESET 2**
**Case 2. dynamic switch RATO RBG allocation from symbol 0 ratematched around its own DCI**
**CORESET 1**
**CORESET 2**
**SSB/PBCH**
**Case1. RAT1 PRB allocations**
**Case 2. RATO RBG allocations**[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13296.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

##### 3.29.5.4.3 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot

###### 3.29.5.4.3.1 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot)

###### 3.29.5.4.3.1.0-1 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot) previous releases (before 25R1) (ID: 11177008)

**3.29.5.4.3.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11177027`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot)
Note: Starting 25R1, due to cleaning purpose (GATEKEEPER), this requirement (Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot)) has been split in 2 parts:

* FDD, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24044\_replaced([11177034](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kusaxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* TDD, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24045\_replaced([11177044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kusax_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

L2-PS shall determine the available FD resource for the to-be-scheduled UEs in CS2.

If PRB blanking function is activated, the remaining FD resource for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized by the available PDSCH RBGs (number of RBGs in BW - number of RBGs contains at least one blanked PRBs), which remained for the DL after PRB blanking.

If actAsymBwGuardband is enabled, the remaining FD resource for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized by the available PDSCH RBGs (number of RBGs in BW - number of RBGs at guardband), which remained for the DL after PRB blanking. The guardband for CB007933 is specified in 5G\_UP\_4096\_replaced([11349195](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubcux_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the number of RBG(s) at guardband is specified in 5G\_UP\_3900\_replaced([11349231](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubcu0_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For a FDD cell shared with LTE when  the DL PRB sharing in subframe 0/5/9 is enabled (rrmLteDLPrbSharing=TRUE,  5G\_UP\_ALG\_4722\_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) FD resource shall be initialized only by the available PDSCH RBGs in these subframes according to values received in EN DSS DL CELL RESOURCE AREA ALLOCATION REQUEST as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4744\_replaced([11172253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuK2VPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). This applies for any kind of BWP.

For a FDD cell shared with LTE when Cat-M support is enabled (actLteNrDssLteCatM) FD resource shall be initialized only by the available PDSCH RBGs in the respective slot according to values received in EN DSS DL CELL RESOURCE ALLOCATION REQUEST as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4744\_replaced([11172253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuK2VPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the paging, SIB1-BR Cat-M NBs,  for the data Cat-M NB and for repeated MPBCH (if configured).  The PRB Area Allocation List shall be considered as well.

The remaining FD resource in SSB slot without TRS for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4284\_replaced([11177074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kusa1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The remaining FD resource in SSB slot with TRS for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4284\_replaced([11177074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kusa1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the remaining FD resource in TRS slot for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4288\_replaced([11177156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kutB3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The available FD resource shall be initialized as the number of RBGs in the BW as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), all RBGs may not be available for use.

The max number of useable RBG (full and non full RBG) is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

However the number of used RBG will be limited by the numAllocCellPrb which is computed every bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6781\_replaced([11211947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-StfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This will be checked during the RBG allocation specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The non-full last RBG and if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize is true, the non-full first RBG may also be used as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh is True and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is False;

* numAllocCellPrb received from the BBRM is in subcell PRB unit and the determines the total number of subcell PRBs that can be used by L2-PS for all scheduling across all subcells of the cell.
* as subcell PRB defines the PRB processing capacity on subcell level, a cell with two configured subcells may receive as many subcell PRBs as twice the number of physical cell PRB available in the slot (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7012\_replaced([11212774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxDLPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* for the 5G-L2-PS,  the available FD resource refers to the  physical cell PRBs (RBGs), thus, the total physical cell PRBs within the usable RBG shall not exceed the rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot, which is defined as:
* rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}.

For TDD FR1, when large BB pooling is enabled, i.e., slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is true;

* numAllocSubcell and subcell Id list received from the BBRM is the subcell unit and available subcells can be used by L2-PS for all scheduling across all subcells of the cell.
* numAllocCellPrb received from BBRM is in subcell PRB unit, the value of numAllocCellPrb is equal to available subcells multiple the number of physical PRB as dlBandwidth (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB010713-A] if available subcells is larger than one subcell. The value of numAllocCellPrb is equal to or less than the number of physical PRB as dlBandwidth if only primary subcell of the cell is assigned by BBRM. [End CB010713-A]
* the total physical cell PRBs within the usable RBG shall not exceed the rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot, which is defined as: rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}.

---

###### 3.29.5.4.3.1.0-2 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot) FDD (ID: 11177034)

**3.29.5.4.3.1.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `12705008`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot)
gNB-DU shall determine the available FD resource for the to-be-scheduled UEs in CS2.

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,

{ **if PRB blanking function is activated, the remaining FD resource for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized by the available PDSCH RBGs (number of RBGs in BW - number of RBGs contains at least one blanked PRBs), which remained for the DL after PRB blanking.**

if actAsymBwGuardband is enabled, the remaining FD resource for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized by the available PDSCH RBGs (number of RBGs in BW - number of RBGs at guardband), which remained for the DL after PRB blanking. The guardband for CB007933 is specified in 5G\_UP\_4096\_replaced([11349195](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubcux_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the number of RBG(s) at guardband is specified in 5G\_UP\_3900\_replaced([11349231](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubcu0_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true the available PDSCH RBGs are in NR dedicated area and more RBGs may be allocated in the LTE shared area (see [12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), [11172253: Handling of DL CELL RESOURCE AREA ALLOCATION REQUEST](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuK2VPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

for a FDD cell shared with LTE [CB007372-M] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M] when the DL PRB sharing in subframe 0/5/9 is enabled (rrmLteDLPrbSharing=TRUE,  5G\_UP\_ALG\_4722\_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) FD resource shall be initialized only by the available PDSCH RBGs in these subframes according to values received in EN DSS DL CELL RESOURCE AREA ALLOCATION REQUEST as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4744\_replaced([11172253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuK2VPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). This applies for any kind of BWP.

for a FDD cell shared with LTE [CB007372-G] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-G] when Cat-M support is enabled (actLteNrDssLteCatM) FD resource shall be initialized only by the available PDSCH RBGs in the respective slot according to values received in EN DSS DL CELL RESOURCE ALLOCATION REQUEST as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4744\_replaced([11172253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuK2VPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the paging, SIB1-BR Cat-M NBs,  for the data Cat-M NB and for repeated MPBCH (if configured).  The PRB Area Allocation List shall be considered as well.}

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

{if PRB blanking function is activated (if NRCELL.actPRBBlanking = True), gNB-DU shall initialize the FD resources (PRBs) available for any DL RAT1 allocation through the size of PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

if specific bandwidth is enabled (if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = True), gNB-DU shall initialize the FD resources (PRBs) available for any DL RAT1 allocation  through the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB007372-M] For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true the available PDSCH resources (PRBs) for DL RAT1 allocation are in NR dedicated area and more PRBs may be allocated in the LTE shared area (see [12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), [11172253: Handling of DL CELL RESOURCE AREA ALLOCATION REQUEST](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuK2VPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))  [End CB007372-M]

For a FDD cell shared with LTE [CB007372-M] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M] when the DL PRB sharing in subframe 0/5/9 is enabled (rrmLteDLPrbSharing=TRUE,  5G\_UP\_ALG\_4722\_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall initialize the FD resources (PRBs) available for any DL RAT1 allocation through the size of PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in these slots according to values received in EN DSS DL CELL RESOURCE AREA ALLOCATION REQUEST as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4744\_replaced([11172253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuK2VPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for any kind of BWP. gNB-DU shall handle these slots for the dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation too (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 24995)

for a FDD cell shared with LTE when Cat-M support is enabled (actLteNrDssLteCatM) [CB007372-G] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-G] , gNB-DU shall initialize the FD resources (PRBs) available for any DL RAT1 allocation (and the dynamicswitch RAT0) through the start Prb and the size of the PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26285\_replaced([11175892](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the respective slot according to values received in EN DSS DL CELL RESOURCE ALLOCATION REQUEST as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4744\_replaced([11172253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuK2VPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the paging, SIB1-BR Cat-M NBs,  for the data Cat-M NB and for repeated MPBCH (if configured).

**If a dynamicswitch RAT0 is allocated (as a retransmission, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall initialize the FD resources (PRBs) available for any DL RAT1 allocation through the start Prb and the size of PDSCH sub areas which are above and below the pre booked RBGs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24772\_replaced([11175933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the dynamicswitch RAT0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**.\*\*\*\*

The remaining FD resource in SSB slot without TRS for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4284\_replaced([11177074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kusa1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The remaining FD resource in SSB slot with TRS for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4284\_replaced([11177074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kusa1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the remaining FD resource in TRS slot for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4288\_replaced([11177156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kutB3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,

{ the available FD resource shall be initialized as the number of RBGs in the BW as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ([11178370: Setting of parameter Frequency Domain Resource Assignment in DL DCI Format 1\_1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

if slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), [11278751: 5G-CP-RT shall initialize the L1 max PRB resource for L1 sub pool for PRB pooling](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_EvoPVu3rEe-rL47x_rCCmw)), all RBGs may not be available for use.

The max number of useable RBG, including full and non full (first and last partial RBGs, if available) , is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))([11178370: Setting of parameter Frequency Domain Resource Assignment in DL DCI Format 1\_1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

However the number of used RBG will be limited by the numAllocCellPrb which is computed every bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6781\_replaced([11211947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-StfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [11211947: Slow PRB pooling Overview](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-StfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ([11212285: PRB Pooling Algorithm](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

This will be checked during the RBG allocation specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ([11181328: Resource Allocation Using FDM](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

}.

**If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,**

{gNB-DU shall initialize the available FD resource (PRBs) for any DL RAT1 allocation through the number, the location and the size of PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

if slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true'  (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_RAN\_OP\_2268\_replaced([11692786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3pqm_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), all PRBs may not be available for use. gNB-DU shall limit the number of used PRBs **of all the PDSCH sub areas and the sum of the PRBs coming from the RBGs of the dynamicswitch RAT 0 (as retransmission if allocated in the slot, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** by the numAllocCellPrb which is computed every bbPoolingPeriod (NRBTS.bbPoolEvalPeriodFdd for FDD) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).}\*\*\*\*

gNB-DU shall check this PRBs limitation during the FD resource allocation specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in the constraints for PRBs reservation in all the PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall handle the PRB blanking and the specific bandwidth through the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub areas, (see previously and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) }.

[CB014543-G]

Note: When partial ZAB is enabled (partialZABAllowed = True), the BBRM determines the set of ZAB-privileged cells according to the specification in ([12804289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Ui5xYp6QEfCkxIKMlo4L6w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). This privilege assignment may subsequently be overridden by the algorithm detailed in ([12825840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Onwec61_EfCyQasEtWkQ4A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[End CB014543-G]

---

###### 3.29.5.4.3.1.0-3 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot) FR1 TDD (ID: 11177044)

**3.29.5.4.3.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11177050`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot)
General limitations

---

**3.29.5.4.3.1.0-3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12710080`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot)
gNB-DU shall determine the available FD resource for the to-be-scheduled UEs in CS2.

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,

{if PRB blanking function is activated, the remaining FD resource for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized by the available PDSCH RBGs (number of RBGs in BW - number of RBGs contains at least one blanked PRBs), which remained for the DL after PRB blanking.

[CB012623-A]if NRCELL.actSsbIMForNonBF is activated, the available PDSCH RBGs shall exclude the PRBs qualified for muting according to [12709089](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_pAtj4GOsEfCtwYrh0KQ_DA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), which is (number of available RBGs in BW - number of RBGs contains at least one muted PRBs) [End CB012623-A]}

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

{if PRB blanking function is activated (if NRCELL.actPRBBlanking = True)[CB012623-B] or NRCELL.actSsbIMForNonBF is activated [End CB012623-B], gNB-DU shall initialize the FD resources (PRBs) available for any DL RAT1 allocation through the size of PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). }

The remaining FD resource in SSB slot without TRS for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4284\_replaced([11177074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kusa1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The remaining FD resource in SSB slot with TRS for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4284\_replaced([11177074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kusa1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the remaining FD resource in TRS slot for a non-initial BWP transmission UE shall be initialized according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4288\_replaced([11177156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kutB3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, the available FD resource shall be initialized as the number of RBGs in the BW as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

{gNB-DU shall initialize the available FD resource (PRBs) for any DL RAT1 allocation through the number, the location and the size of PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). }

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

{in CSI-IM slots when CSI-IM is rate matched (that is if **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]*****\*)* \*, if FR1 TDD multi beams with FD beamforming** (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), for each PDSCH area, gNB-DU shall initialize the startPrb and the size of the PDSCH sub area as a multiple of a PRB granularity (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If a DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (common channel(SIB1, SI, PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4, type3 DCI 1\_0 in transient phase) is scheduled for the first time in this CSI-IM slot, gNB-DU shall allocate a startPrb of the PDSCH scrambled with this DCI 1\_0 as a multiple of PRB granularity in order to have PDSCH sub areas following the PRB\_GRANULARITY constraints for DCI 1\_1  (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note: PRB\_GRANULARITY does not apply to the number of PRBs of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH allocations and the startPrb the other DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grants except thr first one (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If a dynamicswitch RAT0 is allocated (as a retransmission, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall initialize the FD resources (PRBs) available for any DL RAT1 allocation through the start Prb and the size of PDSCH sub areas which are above and below the pre booked RBGs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24772\_replaced([11175933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the dynamicswitch RAT0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**.**

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to false,

{if slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), all RBGs may not be available for use.

The max number of useable RBG (full and non full RBG) is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

However the number of used RBG will be limited by the numAllocCellPrb which is computed every bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6781\_replaced([11211947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-StfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This will be checked during the RBG allocation specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The non-full last RBG and if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize is true, the non-full first RBG may also be used as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.5.4.3.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11177059`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot)
Pooling limitations

---

**3.29.5.4.3.1.0-3.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12826811`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a slot (not SSB or TRS slot)
gNB-DU shall determine the available FD resource for the to-be-scheduled UEs in CS2.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, the maximum number of usable PRBs (defined as rrmMaxNumUsablePrb in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) will be dependent on the layer PRB capacity computed every pooling period and is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The maximum usable PRBs shall be considered as the remaining number of FD resources available for initialisation.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh is True and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is False;

* numAllocCellPrb received from the BBRM is in subcell PRB unit and the determines the total number of subcell PRBs that can be used by gNB-DU for all scheduling across all subcells of the cell.
* as subcell PRB defines the PRB processing capacity on subcell level, a cell with two configured subcells may receive as many subcell PRBs as twice the number of physical cell PRB available in the slot (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7012\_replaced([11212774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxDLPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* for the gNB-DU, the available FD resource refers to the physical cell PRBs **if PDSCH allocation is made with RAT1 (NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True) or RBGs with RAT0, thus, the total physical cell PRBs with RAT1 allocation or within the usable RBG when PDSCH allocation is made with RAT0** shall not exceed the rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot, which is defined as:\*\*\*\*
* rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}.

For TDD FR1, when large BB pooling is enabled, i.e., slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is true  (see 5G\_RAN\_OP\_2387\_replaced([11692644](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Yf9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg)));

* numAllocSubcell and subcell Id list received from the BBRM is the subcell unit and available subcells can be used by gNB-DU for all scheduling across all subcells of the cell.
* numAllocCellPrb received from BBRM is in subcell PRB unit, the value of numAllocCellPrb is equal to available subcells multiple the number of physical PRB as dlBandwidth (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if available subcells is larger than 1 subcell. The value of numAllocCellPrb is equal to or less than the number of physical PRB as dlBandwidth if only primary subcell of the cell is assigned by BBRM.
* the total physical cell PRBs within the usable RBG shall not exceed the rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot, which is defined as: rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}. }

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

{if slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_RAN\_OP\_2268\_replaced([11692786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3pqm_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), all PRBs may not be available for use.

gNB-DU shall initialize the available FD resource (PRBs) for any DL RAT1 allocation through the number, the location and the size of PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

However , gNB-DU shall limit the number of used PRBs of all the PDSCH sub areas and the sum of the PRBs coming from the RBGs of the dynamicswitch RAT 0 (as retransmission if allocated in the slot, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by the numAllocCellPrb which is computed every bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6781\_replaced([11211947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-StfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).}

gNB-DU shall check this PRBs limitation during the FD resource allocation specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in the constraints for PRBs reservation in all the PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For TDD FR1, when large BB pooling is enabled, i.e., slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is true;

* numAllocSubcell and subcell Id list received from the BBRM is the subcell unit and available subcells can be used by gNB-DU for all scheduling across all subcells of the cell.
* numAllocCellPrb received from BBRM is in subcell PRB unit, the value of numAllocCellPrb is equal to available subcells multiplied by the number of physical PRB as dlBandwidth (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if available subcells is larger than 1 subcell. The value of numAllocCellPrb is equal to or less than the number of physical PRB as dlBandwidth (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}:  if only primary subcell of the cell is assigned by BBRM.
* gNB-DU shall check that the total physical cell PRBs in all the PDSCH sub areas and the sum of the PRBs coming from the RBGs of the dynamicswitch RAT 0 (as retransmission if allocated in the slot, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **shall not exceed the rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot, which is defined as: rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). }**

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, gNB-DU shall check that the total physical cell PRBs in all the PDSCH sub areas and the sum of the PRBs coming from the RBGs of the dynamicswitch RAT 0 (as retransmission if allocated in the slot, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **shall not exceed the rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot, which is defined as: rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{rrmMaxNumUsablePrb(refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), dlBandwidth) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

The maximum usable PRBs shall be considered as the remaining number of FD resources available for initialisation.

[CB0014543-H]

If partialZABAllowed = True, gNB-DU shall determine  the maximum number of FD resources and respective numAllocCellPrb by executing the algorithm specified in ([12825840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Onwec61_EfCyQasEtWkQ4A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

[End CB0014543-H]

---

###### 3.29.5.4.3.2 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot

###### 3.29.5.4.3.2-1 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot

###### 3.29.5.4.3.2-1.0-1 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot previous releases (before 25R1) (ID: 11177162)

**3.29.5.4.3.2-1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11177183`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot
Note: starting 25R1, due to cleaning purpose (GATEKEEPER), this requirement (Determining the maximum number of FD resources in a TRS slot) has been split in 2 parts:

* FDD, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29715\_replaced([11177186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuto4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* TDD, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29717\_replaced([11177198](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuto5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For FDD:

In TRS slot, the remaining FD resource shall be initialized by the available PDSCH RBGs. 5G-L2-PS shall determine the available RBGs which is not occupied by PRB blanking and not greater than numPdschPrbInTrsSlots;

5G-L2-PS shall use the parameter numPdschPrbInTrslots value corresponding to a UE's active dedicated BWP (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1809\_replaced([11110718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpHXNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For FDD cell, if slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true', the total number of PRBs to be used in the TRS slot is limited to the total amount of usable DL PRB granted to the cell (numAllocCellPrb) by the BBRM. This grant is computed every bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6781\_replaced([11211947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-StfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Full RBGs and the non-full last RBG and if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize is true, the non-full first RBG may be used as far as the numAllocCellPrb limitation is respected.

For TDD FR1:

In TRS slot, the remaining FD resource shall be initialized by the available PDSCH RBGs. 5G-L2-PS shall determine the available RBGs [CB009915-A] which is not occupied by PRB blanking [End CB009915-A] and the total PRBs is not greater than numPdschPrbInTrsSlots;

For TDD FR1, to calculate the available RBGs in TRS slots, the RBGs which have partial overlapped PRBs with CSI-RS for tracking are not avaliable for PDSCH based on the csirsTracking configuration(csirsTrackingStartingPrb and csirsTrackingNumOfPrbs) in PsCell\_CellSetupReq.

For TDD FR1, if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

* the total number of PRBs used in the TRS slot shall not exceed the total amount of usable DL PRB granted to the cell by the BBRM. This grant is computed every bbPoolingPeriod (NRBTS.bbPoolEvalPeriod) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For PDSCH, full RBGs, the non-full last RBG and first non-full RBG when actSpecificBwpSize is true may be used as far as the total number of usable DL PRB limit allocated by the BBRM is respected.
* with NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh is True, the total number of usable physical PRB limit in the slot is given by the value of rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth(5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}. Rationale given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4279\_replaced([11177002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For TDD FR1, when large BB pooling is enabled, i.e., slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is true,

* the total number of PRBs used in the TRS slot are allocated in primary subcell of the cell, the primary subcell of the cell is always activated and granted by BBRM.
* the total number of usable physical PRB limit in the slot is given by the value of rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}.

[Before CB009915-A] Note:  PRB blanking is not supported in TDD FR1, so PRB blanking shall not be considered in this subfeature. [End CB009915-A]

Note: The SW implementation detail to calculate the avaliable RBG bitmap and PRBs in SSB slot is covered by CP3

---

###### 3.29.5.4.3.2-1.0-2 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot FDD (ID: 11177186)

**3.29.5.4.3.2-1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12826886`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot
If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, in TRS slot, the remaining FD resource shall be initialized by the available PDSCH RBGs. gNB-DU shall determine the available RBGs which is not occupied by PRB blanking and not greater than numPdschPrbInTrsSlots. If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, in TRS slot, if *numPdschPrbInTrslots (defined in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2041\_replaced([11277590](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpN8B_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is greater than zero, gNB-DU shall determine the number and the size of the PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and check that the number of the PRBs reserved and the sum of the PRBs coming from the RBGs of the dynamicswitch RAT 0 (as retransmission if allocated in the slot, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* *are not greater than* numPdschPrbInTrslots (check constraints on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).\*\*\*\*

gNB-DU shall use the parameter *numPdschPrbInTrslots value corresponding to a UE's active* *dedicated BWP (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1809\_replaced([11110718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpHXNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*\*\*

In TRS slots, **if the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, if the conditions listed in 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fullfilled, gNB-DU shall initialize the FD resources (PRBs) available for any DL RAT1 or dynamicswitch RAT1 and RAT0 allocation through the size of PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30501\_replaced([11175903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30520\_replaced([11176383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-gfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which are split due to the additional blanking PRBs area: the PRBs in this additional blanking PRBs area are blanked PRBs (but only for PDSCH signal) and the PDSCH sub areas are split below and above these PRBs. If required, gNB-DU shall apply the PRB\_GRANULARITY to the PDSCH sub areas and the PDSCH grants (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

For FDD cell, if slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true', the total number of PRBs including the sum of the PRBs coming from the RBGs of the dynamicswitch RAT 0 (as retransmission if allocated in the slot, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to be used in the TRS slot is limited to the total amount of usable DL PRB granted to the cell (numAllocCellPrb) by the BBRM. This grant is computed every bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6781\_replaced([11211947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-StfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, gNB-DU shall check this limitation in the constraints for PRBs reservation in the PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The PRB blanking and the specific bandwidth are already handled through the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub areas, (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and constraints 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,  full RBGs and the non-full last RBG and if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize is true, the non-full first RBG may be used as far as the numAllocCellPrb limitation is respected.

[CB0014543-G]

If partialZABAllowed =True, gNB-DU shall determine the maximum number of FD resources and corresponding numAllocCellPrb by executing the algorithm specified in ([12825840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Onwec61_EfCyQasEtWkQ4A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) ).

[End CB0014543-G]

---

###### 3.29.5.4.3.2-1.0-3 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot TDD FR1 (ID: 11177198)

**3.29.5.4.3.2-1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11177201`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot
General limitations

---

**3.29.5.4.3.2-1.0-3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12816661`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot
If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,

{In TRS slot, the remaining FD resource shall be initialized by the available PDSCH RBGs. gNB-DU shall determine the available RBGs which is not occupied by PRB blanking and the total PRBs is not greater than numPdschPrbInTrsSlots;}

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

{in TRS slot, gNB-DU shall determine the available FD resource (PRBs) for any DL RAT1 allocation through initializing the number and the size of the PDSCH sub areas (discarding the blanked PRBs due to PRB blanking, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) If numPdschPrbInTrsSlots is greater than zero (defined in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2041\_replaced([11277590](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpN8B_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall check that the number of the PRBs reserved in all the PDSCH sub areas are not greater than numPdschPrbInTrsSlots (check constraints on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).} **[End**

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,

{For TDD FR1, to calculate the available RBGs in TRS slots, the RBGs which have partial overlapped PRBs with CSI-RS for tracking are not avaliable for PDSCH based on the csirsTracking configuration(csirsTrackingStartingPrb and csirsTrackingNumOfPrbs) in PsCell\_CellSetupReq. }

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

{in TRS slots when **[Before CB015668-A]** **NRBTS.actTddCsirsOpt = false****[CB015668-A]** actTddCsirsOpt = false(note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**, if FR1 TDD multi beams with FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), for each PDSCH area, gNB-DU shall initialize the startPrb and the size of the PDSCH sub area as a multiple of a PRB granularity (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}\*\*\*\*

In TRS slots, **if the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, if the conditions listed in 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fullfilled, gNB-DU shall initialize the FD resources (PRBs) available for any DL RAT1 or dynamicswitch RAT1 and RAT0 allocation through the size of PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30501\_replaced([11175903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30520\_replaced([11176383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-gfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which are split due to the additional blanking PRBs area: the PRBs in this additional blanking PRBs area are blanked PRBs (but only for PDSCH signal) and the PDSCH sub areas are split below and above these PRBs. If required, gNB-DU shall apply the PRB\_GRANULARITY to the PDSCH sub areas and the PDSCH grants (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, Note: The SW implementation detail to calculate the avaliable RBG bitmap and PRBs in SSB slot is covered by CP3

---

**3.29.5.4.3.2-1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11177213`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot
Pooling limitations

---

**3.29.5.4.3.2-1.0-3.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12826894`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot
If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,

{For TDD FR1, if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

* the total number of PRBs used in the TRS slot shall not exceed the total amount of usable DL PRB granted to the cell by the BBRM. This grant is computed every bbPoolingPeriod (NRBTS.bbPoolEvalPeriod) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For PDSCH, full RBGs, the non-full last RBG and first non-full RBG when actSpecificBwpSize is true **may be used as far as the total number of usable DL PRB limit allocated by the BBRM is respected.**

* with NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh is True, the total number of usable physical PRB limit in the slot is given by **the value of rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth(5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}**. Rationale given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4279\_replaced([11177002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

For TDD FR1, when large BB pooling is enabled, i.e., slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is true,

* the total number of PRBs used in the TRS slot are allocated in primary subcell of the cell, the primary subcell of the cell is always activated and granted by BBRM.
* the total number of usable physical PRB limit in the slot is given by **the value of rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min{numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}. }**

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, the maximum number of usable PRBs (defined as rrmMaxNumUsablePrb in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) will be dependent on the layer PRB capacity computed every pooling period and is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

gNB-DU shall determine the available RBGs and total number of PRBs is not greater than min(numPdschPrbInTrsSlots, rrmMaxNumUsablePrb), and PDSCH is rate matched around CSI-RS for tracking.

The usable PRBs shall be considered as the remaining number of FD resources available for initialisation.

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

 {if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

o gNB-DU shall check that the total number of PRBs to be used for PDSCH in all the PDSCH sub areas in the TRS slot shall not exceed the total amount usable DL PRB granted to the cell (numAllocCellPrb) by the BBRM. This grant is computed every bbPoolingPeriod (NRBTS.bbPoolEvalPeriod) by the slow pooling algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). gNB-DU shall check this limitation in the constraints for PRBs reservation in the PDSCH sub areas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 when large BB pooling is enabled, i.e., slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true and subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is true,

o the total number of PRBs used in the TRS slot are allocated in primary subcell of the cell, the primary subcell of the cell is always activated and granted by BBRM.

o gNB-DU shall check that the total usable physical cell PRBs in all the PDSCH sub areas in the slot shall not exceed the rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot, which is defined as: rrmNumUsableCellPrbPerSlot = min(numAllocCellPrb, dlBandwidth) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1795\_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). }

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, the maximum number of usable PRBs (defined as rrmMaxNumUsablePrb in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) will be dependent on the layer PRB capacity computed every pooling period and is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

gNB-DU shall determine the available RBGs and total number of PRBs is not greater than min(numPdschPrbInTrsSlots, rrmMaxNumUsablePrb), and PDSCH is rate matched around CSI-RS for tracking.

The usable PRBs shall be considered as the remaining number of FD resources available for initialisation.

[CB0014543-H]

If partialZABAllowed =True, gNB-DU shall determine the maximum number of FD resources and corresponding numAllocCellPrb by executing the algorithm specified in ([12825840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Onwec61_EfCyQasEtWkQ4A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) )

[End CB0014543-H]

---

###### 3.29.5.4.3.2-2 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot

###### 3.29.5.4.3.2-2.0-1 Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot previous releases (before 25R1) (ID: 11177232)

**3.29.5.4.3.2-2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11177246`)

Determining the maximum number of FD resources in an SSB slot
Note: starting 25R1, due to cleaning purpose (GATEKEEPER), this requirement (Determining the maximum number of FD resources in any slot with several BWP per Cell) has been split in 2 parts:

* 'General limitations', see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29727\_replaced([11177251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* 'Pooling limitations', see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29728\_replaced([11177261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

5G-L2-PS shall determine the available FD resources separately a) for dedicated BWP n, and b) for each ith BWP exclusive resources versus (i+1)th BWP where i ϵ {1,2,…,n-1} (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
