**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11178239`)

3GPP NR DCI format
Determination of PDSCH start symbol for multiple dedicated BWPs in TDD FR1- Basic

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-7.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12732996`)

3GPP NR DCI format
PDSCH start symbol selection based on MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl for 3 symbol PDCCH in 20-40 MHz BWs in TDD FR1

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-7.0-1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12733001`)

3GPP NR DCI format
In case of NRCELL.actThreeSymbolPdcch is not disabled, NRCELL.actAdaptReTxResMcs is enabled and n > 1 BWPs are configured there is a possibility that symbol 0, 1 or 2  can have both PDSCH and PDCCH.

numPDSCH[k] which is the number of PDSCH UEs that is allowed for PDSCH-PDCCH sharing in a symbol k is determined as follows:

* If there is CCE split in symbol k=0,1:
* If PDSCH is shared with PDCCH in symbol k, then following condition must satisfy
* numPdsch[k]  <= MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl - rrmMaxNumFdmUlCceSplit - min(PDCCH\_DL[k], amount of RBGs in RAT0 or PRBs from PDSCH subarea (n) in RAT1 not covered by any CORESET)
* If no CCE split in symbol k = 0, 1, 2:
* If PDSCH is shared with DL PDCCH in symbol k, then following condition must satisfy in symbol k
* then numPDCCH[k]  + numPDSCH[k]   <= MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl
* If PDSCH is shared with an UL PDCCH symbol k, then following conditions must satisfy in symbol k:
* numPDSCH[k]<= min(rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlCceSplit, amount of RBGs in RAT0 or PRBs from PDSCH subarea (n) in RAT1 not covered by any CORESET) and
* The number of UL PDCCH in symbol k is limited to rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlCceSplit
* When three PDCCH symbols are available, PDSCH allocation starts with symbol 3 and do the following check to extend it to other PDSCH start symbols (0, 1 or 2)  in below order. To determine if PDSCH+PDCCH sharing is possible, each symbol is considered separately as follows when PDSCH is extended for each UE. Note that PDSCH start symbol extension from symbol 3 to symbols 0, 1 or 2 is not applicable to ZF MU paired UEs when NRCELL.NRDLMUMIMO.actDlMuPairingGroup is enabled.
* If  num of UEs for PDSCH-PDCCH sharing <= min(numPDSCH[0], numPDSCH[1],numPDSCH[2])
* Select PDSCH start symbol 0
* Else if num of UEs for PDSCH-PDCCH sharing <= min(numPDSCH[1],numPDSCH[2])
* Select PDSCH start symbol 1
* Else if  num of UEs for PDSCH-PDCCH sharing <= numPDSCH[2]
* Select PDSCH start symbol 2
* Else, select PDSCH start symbol 3

It may be beneficial to prioritize UEs for PDSCH+PDCCH sharing extension with lesser target pdschStartSymbol.

Same algorithm is applicable when 2 PDCCH symbols are available or 1 PDCCH symbol is available.

* When 2 PDCCH symbols are available, PDSCH start symbol can be 0, 1 or 2.
* When 1 PDCCH symbol is available, PDSCH start symbol can be 0 or 1.

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11178262`)

3GPP NR DCI format
Determination of PDSCH start symbol for multiple dedicated BWPs in TDD FR1 - base band pooling limitation

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-7.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12731810`)

3GPP NR DCI format
When NRCELL/actDynPdcchTdMode is set to perBWP in a TDD FR1 cell with n > 1 dedicated BWPs configured, there is a possibility that symbol 1 can have both PDSCH and PDCCH PRBs

To preserve the pool limit, the PDCCH resources shall be considered when deciding if symbol 1 can be used for PDSCH or not.

The assumption is to give the priority to the CORESET region determined through dynamic PDCCH symbol allocation algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16720\_replaced([11169210](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt2GNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) so at first the numAllocCellPrb(DL) in symbol 1 shall be used to derive the N\_CCE (usable) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17155\_replaced([11169994](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt7lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The leftover shall be used to limit PDSCH in symbol 1, thus calculations below shall use:

numAllocCellPrbsSymbol1(DL) = numAllocCellPrb(DL) – 6\* N\_CCE(usable)

* numAllocCellPrb(DL) received from BBRM (5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In cases below when symbol 1 is selected as starting symbol for PDSCH, the 5G-L2-PS shall use starting symbol 1 for PDSCH only if the sum of already allocated PDSCHs in symbol 1 are less than or equals to rnumAllocCellPrbsSymbol1(DL), otherwise symbol 2 shall be used as starting symbol for PDSCH.

In case new numAllocCellPrb <= 0, there is no room for PDSCH in that symbol and scheduling of PDSCH shall be performed using the next PDSCH start symbol using the whole numAllocCellPrb for PDSCH.

[CB012733-C] In case of NRCELL.actThreeSymbolPdcch is not disabled and n > 1 BWPs are configured there is a possibility that symbol 0, 1 or 2  can have both PDSCH and PDCCH.

When three PDCCH symbols are available, PDSCH allocation starts with symbol 3 and do the following check to extend it to other PDSCH start symbols (0, 1 or 2)  in below order. To determine if PDSCH+PDCCH sharing is possible, each symbol is considered separately as follows.

Suppose N0 = PDCCH REGs usable in symbol\_0, N1 = PDCCH REGs usable in symbol\_1 and N2 = PDCCH REGs usable in symbol\_2. N0, N1 and N2 are available from [11169994](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt7lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

If  PRBs used for PDSCH <= min(numAllocCellPrb(DL) - N2, numAllocCellPrb(DL) - N1, numAllocCellPrb(DL) - N0)

Select PDSCH start symbol 0

Else if PRBs used for PDSCH <= min(numAllocCellPrb(DL) - N2, numAllocCellPrb(DL) - N1)

Select PDSCH start symbol 1

Else if  PRBs used for PDSCH <= numAllocCellPrb(DL) - N2'

Select PDSCH start symbol 2

Else, select PDSCH start symbol 3

It may be beneficial to prioritize UEs for PDSCH+PDCCH sharing extension with lesser target pdschStartSymbol.

Same algorithm is applicable when 2 PDCCH symbols are available or 1 PDCCH symbol is available.

* When 2 PDCCH symbols are available, PDSCH start symbol can be 0, 1 or 2.
* When 1 PDCCH symbol is available, PDSCH start symbol can be 0 or 1.

[End CB012733-C]

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `12731688`)

3GPP NR DCI format
**Examples of PDSCH starting from symbol 0, 1, 2 or 3 (Large + PS BWP, Large + Small BWP or Large + PS + Small BWP)**

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12731689`)

3GPP NR DCI format
12731689:
3GPP NR DCI format
Following PDSCH start symbols are applicable for NRCELL.actThreeSymbolPdcch is not disabled and NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is enabled and  with Large + PS BWP, Large + Small BWP or Large + PS + Small BWP configurations.

Note: left hand side figures correspond to RAT0 allocations and right hand side figures correspond to RAT1 allocations.

Case 1:

Case 2:

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays three diagrams illustrating different configurations of "Large BWP" and "Small/PS BWP" with "CORESET1", "CORESET2", and "PDSCH". The diagrams are labeled "Legacy" and "CB012733".

Below this, a "Small/PS BWP" is shown, containing "CORESET2" and "PDSCH". The second "Legacy" diagram is similar but includes an "or" between the two configurations, suggesting an alternative arrangement.
The "CB012733" section shows a single diagram with a "Large BWP" that has a "CORESET1" and "PDSCH" configuration. Below this, a "Small/PS BWP" is depicted with "CORESET2" and "PDSCH".
The diagrams use color coding to differentiate the sections: light blue for "Large BWP", darker blue for "PDSCH", and a peachy color for "CORESET1", and green for "CORESET2". The vertical axis represents the bandwidth, with "Large BWP" being significantly larger than "Small/PS BWP". Each diagram is divided into vertical sections representing CORESET1 and CORESET2, which are control resource sets. Within these resource sets, PDSCH allocations for different User Equipments (UEs) are shown, along with a "PDSCH sub-area 1".
The diagrams are arranged horizontally. The first two diagrams are labeled "Legacy" and show two possible configurations. The third diagram is labeled "CB012733".
**Diagram 1 (Legacy - Configuration 1):**
\* **CORESET1:** Occupies a significant portion of the left side.
\* **CORESET2:** Occupies a smaller portion at the bottom left.
\* **PDSCH (UE1):** Occupies the top right area.

**Diagram 3 (CB012733):**

\* **CORESET2:** Occupies the bottom left, similar to the other diagrams.
\* **PDSCH (UE1):** Occupies the top right area.
\* **PDSCH (UE2):** Occupies the middle right area.
\* **PDSCH sub-area 1:** Occupies the lower middle right area.
\* **PDSCH (UE3):** Occupies the bottom right area.
The diagrams visually represent how PDSCH resources can be allocated to different UEs and potentially partitioned into sub-areas, with variations shown between legacy and the CB012733 system. The color coding (shades of blue and light blue) likely indicates different resource allocations or types. The diagrams are labeled "Legacy" and "CB0127333".
Each diagram represents a time-frequency resource grid, divided into several sections. The vertical axis likely represents time, and the horizontal axis represents frequency.
**Common elements across all diagrams:**
\* **CORESET1 and CORESET2:** These are highlighted in peach and green respectively on the left side of each diagram. They appear to represent control resource sets, possibly for initial access or control signaling. CORESET1 is positioned above CORESET2.
\* **PDSCH (UE1), PDSCH (UE2), PDSCH (UE3):** These represent Physical Downlink Shared Channels allocated to different User Equipment (UE). They are depicted as blue rectangular blocks within the resource grid.
\* **PDSCH sub-area 1:** This is a specific sub-area within a PDSCH allocation, shown in a lighter shade of blue.
**Diagram 1: Legacy (Left)**
\* This diagram shows a configuration where CORESET1 and CORESET2 occupy a portion of the time-frequency resources.
\* Above the CORESETs, PDSCH (UE1) is allocated.
\* Below PDSCH (UE1), PDSCH (UE2) is allocated.
\* Further down, PDSCH (UE3) is allocated.
\* A portion of PDSCH (UE2) is further subdivided into "PDSCH sub-area 1".
**Diagram 2: Legacy (Middle)**
\* This diagram is similar to the first "Legacy" diagram but includes an "or" connector, suggesting an alternative configuration.
\* The structure of CORESET1, CORESET2, PDSCH (UE1), PDSCH (UE2), PDSCH (UE3), and "PDSCH sub-area 1" is identical to the first "Legacy" diagram. The "or" might imply that the resource allocation for PDSCH (UE2) and PDSCH (UE3) can be arranged differently, or that the "sub-area 1" is an optional or alternative way to use the resources.
**Diagram 3: CB0127333 (Right)**
\* This diagram, labeled "CB0127333", shows a different resource allocation compared to the "Legacy" diagrams.
\* CORESET1 and CORESET2 are present on the left, similar to the other diagrams.
\* PDSCH (UE1) is allocated above the CORESETs.
\* Below PDSCH (UE1), PDSCH (UE2) is allocated.
\* Further down, "PDSCH sub-area 1" is shown, and below that, PDSCH (UE3) is allocated.
\* The key difference appears to be the relative positioning and size of "PDSCH sub-area 1" and PDSCH (UE3) compared to the "Legacy" diagrams. In this diagram, "PDSCH sub-area 1" seems to occupy a distinct block before PDSCH (UE3).
The diagrams visually represent how downlink shared channel resources can be partitioned and allocated to different users, with specific control resource sets and a designated sub-area for a particular PDSCH transmission. The "CB0127333" label likely refers to a specific release or feature set within a cellular standard, indicating a potential evolution or alternative to the "Legacy" configuration.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07111647.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

[image-10][image\_desc]The image displays a comparison of two different configurations, labeled "Legacy" and "CB012733," illustrating the allocation of resources within a "Large BWP" (Bandwidth Part) and a "Small/PS BWP" (Power Saving Bandwidth Part). Both configurations show the partitioning of these bandwidth parts into different channels, specifically "CORESET1," "CORESET2," and "PDSCH" (Physical Downlink Shared Channel).
In the "Legacy" configuration:
- The "Large BWP" is divided into three sections. The top section is entirely allocated to "PDSCH." The middle section is also allocated to "PDSCH." The bottom section is divided into two parts: a smaller upper part allocated to "CORESET1" and a lower part allocated to "CORESET2."
- The "Small/PS BWP" is shown as a subset of the "Large BWP." It is divided into two distinct regions. The upper region is labeled "CORESET1" and appears to be further subdivided into multiple instances of "CORESET1." The lower region is labeled "CORESET2" and also appears to be subdivided into multiple instances of "CORESET2."
In the "CB012733" configuration:
- The "Large BWP" is divided into three sections. The top section is entirely allocated to "PDSCH." The middle section is also allocated to "PDSCH." The bottom section is divided into two parts: a smaller upper part allocated to "CORESET1" and a lower part allocated to "CORESET2." This structure is similar to the "Legacy" configuration in the "Large BWP."
- The "Small/PS BWP" in this configuration is depicted differently. It shows a vertical arrangement of "CORESET1" and "CORESET2" regions. The "CORESET1" region is positioned above the "CORESET2" region. Both "CORESET1" and "CORESET2" are shown as being subdivided into multiple instances.
The key difference highlighted is in the "Small/PS BWP" allocation. The "Legacy" configuration shows a more horizontal or block-like division within the "Small/PS BWP," while the "CB012733" configuration shows a more vertical and potentially more granular subdivision of "CORESET1" and "CORESET2" within the "Small/PS BWP." The arrows on the left indicate the relative sizes of the "Large BWP" and the "Small/PS BWP," with the "Small/PS BWP" being significantly smaller than the "Large BWP."[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07111648.png[/image\_path][/image-10]       [image-11][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams side-by-side, labeled "Legacy" and "CB012733". Both diagrams illustrate resource allocation, likely for a communication system, showing different types of channels or data blocks assigned to different users (UE1, UE2, UE3) and control regions (CORESET1, CORESET2).
The "Legacy" diagram shows:
- A section labeled "CORESET1" repeated three times, colored in a light orange hue.
- A section labeled "CORESET2" repeated three times, colored in a light green hue.
- A section labeled "PDSCH (UE1)" colored in a dark blue hue.
- A section labeled "PDSCH (UE2)" colored in a dark blue hue.
- A section labeled "PDSCH sub-area 1" colored in a light blue hue.
- A section labeled "PDSCH (UE3)" colored in a dark blue hue.
The "CB012733" diagram shows:
- A section labeled "CORESET1" repeated three times, colored in a light orange hue.
- A section labeled "CORESET2" repeated three times, colored in a light green hue.
- A section labeled "PDSCH (UE1)" colored in a dark blue hue.
- A section labeled "PDSCH (UE2)" colored in a dark blue hue.
- A section labeled "PDSCH sub-area 1" colored in a light blue hue.
- A section labeled "PDSCH (UE3)" colored in a dark blue hue.
The primary difference between the two diagrams appears to be the arrangement and possibly the size of the CORESET regions relative to the PDSCH regions. In the "CB012733" diagram, the CORESET regions are more tightly grouped and appear to occupy a larger proportion of the left side of the diagram compared to the "Legacy" diagram. The PDSCH allocations seem to be in similar positions and sizes in both diagrams, with "PDSCH (UE1)" at the top, "PDSCH (UE2)" below it, "PDSCH sub-area 1" below that, and "PDSCH (UE3)" at the bottom.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07111649.png[/image\_path][/image-11]

[CB012733-D] When three PDCCH symbols are allocated in a slot in a BWP with CORESET duration pattern DLDLDL, DLULDL or ULDLDL in a 20 ms PDCCH slot pattern, if no DCIs are using symbol 2 in all BWPs, then gNB-DU shall allocate PDSCH start symbol 2 for a UE using PRBs in the CORESET region of symbol 2.

E.g. behaviors for RAT1 or RAT0 allocations.

[image-12][image\_desc]The image displays four diagrams illustrating different configurations of Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) allocations for User Equipment (UE) within a Bandwidth Part (BWP). Each diagram is a vertical bar representing the BWP, divided into horizontal sections for different UEs and control regions.
The leftmost diagram shows a "Large BWP" and a "Small/PS BWP (20 MHz)" indicated by a double-headed arrow on the left. This diagram is divided into three main horizontal sections, each labeled with a PDSCH allocation for a specific UE: "PDSCH (UE1)" at the top, "PDSCH (UE2)" in the middle, and "PDSCH (UE3)" at the bottom. Between the UE allocations, there's a region labeled "PDSCH sub-area 1". On the left side of the diagram, there are vertical bars representing control regions: "CORESET1" (peach color) and "CORESET2" (green color). These control regions are further subdivided, with "CORESET1" appearing above "CORESET2". There are two instances of "CORESET1" and two instances of "CORESET2" shown side-by-side in the control region. A blue bar labeled "CORESET1" is also shown within the "PDSCH (UE2)" area.
The second diagram from the left is similar to the first but has a slightly different arrangement of the control regions and PDSCH allocations. It also shows "PDSCH (UE1)", "PDSCH (UE2)", and "PDSCH (UE3)" with "PDSCH sub-area 1" in between. The control regions on the left are "CORESET1" and "CORESET2", with two instances of each. A blue bar labeled "CORESET1" is present within the "PDSCH (UE2)" area.
The third diagram from the left is identical to the first diagram.
The fourth diagram from the left is identical to the second diagram.
In summary, the diagrams illustrate how PDSCH resources can be allocated to different UEs, potentially utilizing different BWPs and control channel configurations (CORESETs). The presence of "sub-area 1" suggests a partitioning of the PDSCH resource.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-03031104.png[/image\_path][/image-12]

When three PDCCH symbols are allocated in a slot in a BWP with CORESET duration pattern DLDLDL in the 20 ms PDCCH slot pattern, if no DCIs are in symbol 1 and 2 for PDCCH in all BWPs, then gNB-DU shall allocate PDSCH start symbol 1 for a UE using PRBs in the CORESET region of symbol 1.

E.g. behaviors for RAT1 or RAT0 allocations.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays four diagrams illustrating different configurations of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and control resource sets (CORESETs) within a communication system, labeled "Case A (legacy)", "Case B (CB008367)", "Case C (CB008367)", and "Case D (CB008367)". Each diagram represents a frequency spectrum divided into resource blocks (RBs), with the horizontal axis indicating RB numbers from 0 to 13. The vertical axis indicates the total bandwidth, specified as 60 MHz (168 RBs) and 70 MHz (192 RBs).
Each diagram shows a "Large BWP" and a "Small BWP", with a "PDSCH" (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) block positioned within the "Large BWP". Additionally, "CORESET 1" and "CORESET 2" are depicted as control regions. CORESET 1 is consistently located at the beginning of the spectrum, while CORESET 2 is positioned adjacent to it, with varying widths and overlaps across the cases.
**Case A (legacy):**
- CORESET 1 occupies a small portion at the beginning of the spectrum.
- PDSCH is located within the Large BWP, above CORESET 1.
- Large BWP occupies the upper part of the spectrum.
- Small BWP occupies the lower part of the spectrum, below the Large BWP.
- CORESET 2 is located at the very beginning of the spectrum, overlapping with the start of the Small BWP.
**Case B (CB008367):**
- Two CORESET 1 blocks are shown side-by-side at the beginning of the spectrum.
- PDSCH is located within the Large BWP, above the CORESET 1 blocks.
- Large BWP occupies the upper part of the spectrum.
- Small BWP occupies the lower part of the spectrum.
- Two CORESET 2 blocks are shown side-by-side, adjacent to the CORESET 1 blocks, and overlapping with the start of the Small BWP.
**Case C (CB008367):**
- CORESET 1 is located at the beginning of the spectrum.
- PDSCH is located within the Large BWP, above CORESET 1.
- Large BWP occupies the upper part of the spectrum.
- Small BWP occupies the lower part of the spectrum.
- Two CORESET 2 blocks are shown side-by-side, adjacent to CORESET 1, and overlapping with the start of the Small BWP.
**Case D (CB008367):**
- Two CORESET 1 blocks are shown side-by-side at the beginning of the spectrum.
- PDSCH is located within the Large BWP, above the CORESET 1 blocks.
- Large BWP occupies the upper part of the spectrum.
- Small BWP occupies the lower part of the spectrum.
- CORESET 2 is located at the beginning of the spectrum, adjacent to the CORESET 1 blocks, and overlapping with the start of the Small BWP. A small grey shaded area is also present next to CORESET 2.
The diagrams highlight how the allocation of BWPs and CORESETs can vary, potentially impacting resource utilization and control signaling efficiency.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13535.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

NOTE 1: CORESET0 is not shown in large + small BWP case because it is only used for common channels.

NOTE 2: If power saving BWP has a BW > 20 MHz and has a dedicated CORESET > CORESET0, the case is similar to the coexistence of small and large BWP case, so it is omitted.

**A:**

Legacy case, all CORESETs have equal number of PDCCH symbols (1), starting symbol for PDSCH is 1.

**B:**

Extended legacy case, all CORESETs have equal number of PDCCH symbols (2), starting symbol for PDSCH is 2, additionally the RBGs not covered by any CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH.

**C:**

UEs scheduled solely in RBGs non-overlapping with small BWP's dedicated CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH, while other UEs shall start PDSCH from symbol 2.

**D:**

UEs scheduled solely in RBGs non-overlapping with large BWP's dedicated CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH, while other UEs shall start PDSCH from symbol 2.

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPdcchSharingWithPdsch is set to True, the PDSCH allocation in large dedicated BWPs can be extended to include one or more PDCCH symbols, based on the conditions given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25284\_replaced([11183082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvH4lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**else**

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays four diagrams, labeled Case A, Case B, Case C, and Case D, each illustrating a different configuration of resource allocation within a communication system. All diagrams share a common structure, depicting a frequency spectrum divided into Resource Blocks (RBs) ranging from 0 to 13. The total bandwidth is indicated as 70MHz (192 PRBs) and 60MHz (168 PRBs), with a distinction between "Large BWP" (Bandwidth Part) and "Small BWP".
Each diagram shows the allocation of different components:
- **PDSCH (UE1)**: This represents the Physical Downlink Shared Channel for User Equipment 1, shown as a blue rectangular block.
- **PDSCH SUB-AREA 1**: This is a larger green rectangular block, indicating a sub-area for PDSCH.
- **CORESET 1**: This is a narrow cyan block, representing a Control Resource Set 1.
- **CORESET 2**: This is a narrow red block with diagonal hatching, representing a Control Resource Set 2.
The specific arrangements of these components vary across the four cases:
**Case A:**
- CORESET 1 is positioned at the beginning of the spectrum, followed by PDSCH SUB-AREA 1.
- PDSCH (UE1) is located above PDSCH SUB-AREA 1, occupying a portion of the spectrum.
- CORESET 2 is not explicitly shown in this diagram, but the structure implies its potential placement.
**Case B:**
- Two CORESET 1 blocks are placed side-by-side at the beginning of the spectrum.
- PDSCH SUB-AREA 1 is located to the right of the CORESET 1 blocks.
- PDSCH (UE1) is positioned above PDSCH SUB-AREA 1.
- CORESET 2 is not explicitly shown.
**Case C:**
- One CORESET 1 block is placed at the beginning of the spectrum.
- PDSCH SUB-AREA 1 is located to the right of CORESET 1.
- PDSCH (UE1) is positioned above PDSCH SUB-AREA 1, and it extends further to the right than in Case A.
- CORESET 2 is not explicitly shown.
**Case D:**
- Two CORESET 1 blocks are placed side-by-side at the beginning of the spectrum.
- PDSCH SUB-AREA 1 is located to the right of the CORESET 1 blocks.
- PDSCH (UE1) is positioned above PDSCH SUB-AREA 1, and it extends further to the right than in Case B.
- CORESET 2 is not explicitly shown.
The diagrams also indicate the presence of a "Large BWP" and a "Small BWP" which are distinct bandwidth parts, suggesting different operational modes or configurations. The numbering at the bottom (0, 1, ..., 13) likely represents the Resource Block indices within the allocated bandwidth. The labels "70MHz (192PRBs)" and "60MHz (168PRBs)" indicate the total bandwidth and the number of physical resource blocks.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13535.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

NOTE 1: CORESET0 is not shown in large + small BWP case because it is only used for common channels.

NOTE 2: If power saving BWP has a BW > 20 MHz and has a dedicated CORESET > CORESET0, the case is similar to the coexistence of small and large BWP case, so it is omitted.

**A:**

Legacy case, all CORESETs have equal number of PDCCH symbols (1), starting symbol for PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area is 1.

**B:**

Extended legacy case, all CORESETs have equal number of PDCCH symbols (2), starting symbol for PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area is 2, additionally the PRBs not covered by any CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH.

**C:**

UEs scheduled solely in PRBs non-overlapping with small BWP's dedicated CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH in PDSCH aub-area, while other UEs shall start PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area from symbol 2.

**D:**

UEs scheduled solely in PRBs non-overlapping with large BWP's dedicated CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area, while other UEs shall start PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area from symbol 2.

[CB009422-E] In tdLte config0 frame structure, when [NRCELL.actPdcchPowerBoost](https://mind.emea.nsn-net.net/nrbts.nrcell.actpdcchpowerboost?search=actPdcchPowerBoost&selectedCategory=129&selectedSystemRelease=1391) is disabled, NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is enabled and when large BWP >40 MHz is configured with small BWP of size 20-40 MHz in slots configured  with 2 valid K2s and 1 valid K1 three PDCCH symbols are available in all the dedicated BWPs. In that case, PDSCH start symbol 1 can occur as in case B above. [End CB009422-E]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays four diagrams illustrating different cases of PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) and BWP (Bandwidth Part) configurations in a cellular communication system. Each diagram represents a different case: Case A (legacy), Case B (CB008367), Case C (CB008367), and Case D (CB008367).
The diagrams are arranged horizontally, with each case occupying a separate vertical section. The horizontal axis is labeled with numbers 0 and 13, likely representing Resource Blocks (RBs). The vertical axis indicates the allocation of resources, with labels like "60 MHz (144 RBs)" and "96 RBs" and "48 RBs" indicating the total bandwidth or number of RBs.
Each diagram shows different colored blocks representing PDSCH and PS BWP (Physical Downlink Shared Channel Bandwidth Part), and hatched areas labeled "CORESET 0" and "CORESET 1" representing control resource sets.
**Case A (legacy):**
This case shows two PDSCH blocks, one above the other, separated by a "Large BWP" region. Below the lower PDSCH block, there is a "PS BWP" region, and below that, another PDSCH block. The CORESET 1 region is on the left, and CORESET 0 is below CORESET 1. The total bandwidth is 60 MHz (144 RBs).
**Case B (CB008367):**
This case features a PDSCH block at the top, followed by a "Large BWP" region. To the left of the "Large BWP" and the PDSCH above it, there are two CORESET 1 regions side-by-side. Below the "Large BWP" region, there is a "PS BWP" region, and below that, a PDSCH block. The CORESET 0 regions are below the CORESET 1 regions. The total bandwidth is 96 RBs.
**Case C (CB008367):**
Similar to Case B, this case has a PDSCH block at the top, followed by a "Large BWP" region. However, there is only one CORESET 1 region on the left, adjacent to the PDSCH and "Large BWP". Below the "Large BWP", there is a "PS BWP" region, and below that, a PDSCH block. The CORESET 0 regions are below the CORESET 1 region. The total bandwidth is 96 RBs.
**Case D (CB008367):**
This case shows a PDSCH block at the top, followed by a "Large BWP" region. To the left of the "Large BWP" and the PDSCH above it, there are two CORESET 1 regions side-by-side. Below the "Large BWP" region, there is a "PS BWP" region, and below that, a PDSCH block. The CORESET 0 regions are below the CORESET 1 regions. The total bandwidth is 60 MHz (144 RBs).
The diagrams highlight how different configurations of PDSCH, BWP, and CORESETs are utilized across various cases, likely representing different operational scenarios or standards.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13539.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

NOTE 1: CORESET0 is not shown in large + small BWP case because it is only used for common channels.

NOTE 2: If power saving BWP has a BW > 20 MHz and has a dedicated CORESET > CORESET0, the case is similar to the coexistence of small and large BWP case, so it is omitted.

**A:**

Legacy case, all CORESETs have equal number of PDCCH symbols (1), starting symbol for PDSCH is 1.

**B:**

Extended legacy case, all CORESETs have equal number of PDCCH symbols (2), starting symbol for PDSCH is 2, additionally the RBGs  not covered by any CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH.

**C:**

UEs scheduled solely in RBG **non-overlapping with PS BWP's dedicated CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH, while other UEs shall start PDSCH from symbol 2.**

**D:**

UEs scheduled solely in RBGs non-overlapping with large BWP's dedicated CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH, while other UEs shall start PDSCH from symbol 2.

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actPdcchSharingWithPdsch is set to True, the PDSCH allocation in dedicated BWPs can be extended to include one or more PDCCH symbols, based on the conditions given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25284\_replaced([11183082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvH4lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**else**

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays four diagrams illustrating different cases of PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) allocation within a 60MHz bandwidth, divided into 144 PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks). Each diagram represents a different scenario, labeled Case A (Legacy), Case B, Case C, and Case D.
All diagrams share a common structure:
- A vertical axis representing frequency, labeled "60MHz (144PRBs)".
- A horizontal axis representing PRBs, numbered from 0 to 13.
- A green shaded area labeled "PDSCH SUB-AREA 1", occupying a significant portion of the bandwidth.
- Blue rectangles representing "PDSCH (UEx)" allocations, where 'x' denotes the User Equipment (UE) number.
- Vertical bars on the left side labeled "CORESET 0" (red hatched) and "CORESET 1" (blue striped), indicating control resource sets.
- A label "Large BWP" (Bandwidth Part) on the right side, indicating a larger bandwidth part.
- A label "PS BWP" (Partial or Paging Service Bandwidth Part) on the bottom right, indicating a smaller bandwidth part.
- A vertical arrow on the left indicating "48 PRBs" for the lower portion of the spectrum.
**Case A (Legacy):**
- CORESET 0 occupies PRBs 0.
- CORESET 1 occupies PRBs 1.
- PDSCH (UE2) is allocated above CORESET 1.
- PDSCH (UE3) is allocated above PDSCH (UE2).
- The PDSCH SUB-AREA 1 encompasses the remaining PRBs not used by CORESETs or PDSCH allocations for UEs.
**Case B ( & CB008367):**
- CORESET 0 occupies PRBs 0.
- Two instances of CORESET 1 occupy PRBs 1.
- PDSCH (UE1) is allocated above the CORESETs, occupying a large portion of the PDSCH SUB-AREA 1.
**Case C ( & CB008367):**
- CORESET 0 occupies PRBs 0.
- CORESET 1 occupies PRBs 1.
- PDSCH (UE1) is allocated above CORESET 1, occupying a large portion of the PDSCH SUB-AREA 1.
**Case D ( & CB008367):**
- CORESET 0 occupies PRBs 0.
- Two instances of CORESET 1 occupy PRBs 1.
- PDSCH (UE2) is allocated above the CORESETs.
- PDSCH (UE1) is allocated below PDSCH (UE2) and above CORESET 0.
- The PDSCH SUB-AREA 1 is split between the two PDSCH allocations.
The diagrams illustrate how different UEs and control channels are mapped within the available bandwidth, highlighting variations in resource allocation strategies across different cases.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13539.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

NOTE 1: CORESET0 is not shown in large + small BWP case because it is only used for common channels.

NOTE 2: If power saving BWP has a BW > 20 MHz and has a dedicated CORESET > CORESET0, the case is similar to the coexistence of small and large BWP case, so it is omitted.

**A:**

Legacy case, all CORESETs have equal number of PDCCH symbols (1), starting symbol for PDSCH in PDSCH-sub area is 1.

**B:**

Extended legacy case, all CORESETs have equal number of PDCCH symbols (2), starting symbol for PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area is 2, additionally the PRBs not covered by any CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area.

**C:**

UEs scheduled solely in PRBs non-overlapping with PS BWP's dedicated CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area, while other UEs shall start PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area from symbol 2.

**D:**

UEs scheduled solely in PRBs non-overlapping with large BWP's dedicated CORESET might use symbol 1 for PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area, while other UEs shall start PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area from symbol 2.

[CB009422-E] In tdLte config0 frame structure, when [NRCELL.actPdcchPowerBoost](https://mind.emea.nsn-net.net/nrbts.nrcell.actpdcchpowerboost?search=actPdcchPowerBoost&selectedCategory=129&selectedSystemRelease=1391) is disabled, NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is enabled and when large BWP >40 MHz is configured with PS BWP in slots configured  with 2 valid K2s and 1 valid K1 three PDCCH symbols are available in all the dedicated BWPs and PS BWP. In that case, PDSCH start symbol 1 can occur as in case B above. [End CB009422-E]

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-7.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `12696653`)

3GPP NR DCI format
12696653:
3GPP NR DCI format
[Before CB009422-E] In tdLte config0 frame structure, when large BWP >40 MHz is configured with small BWP of size 20-40 MHz and/or PS BWP of size 20 MHz, following PDSCH start examples shall occur in slots configured  with 2 valid K2s and 1 valid K1. This is an extension of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19210\_replaced([11178315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku1kuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to PDSCH starting from symbol 2**.**

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays four diagrams illustrating different cases of resource allocation, labeled "Case A: legacy", "Case B: CB009422", "Case C: legacy", and "Case D: CB009422". Each diagram represents a time-frequency resource grid, with the vertical axis labeled "Large BWP" on the left and "Small/PS BWP" on the right, and the horizontal axis representing time slots from 0 to 13.
The diagrams show allocations for different components:
- **CORESET0**: Represented by a dark grey block on the left side of the grid.
- **RESET2**: Represented by a light green block adjacent to CORESET0.
- **CORESET1**: Represented by a light orange block above RESET2.
- **CORESET2**: Represented by a light green block above CORESET1.
The remaining areas are allocated to **PDSCH** (Physical Downlink Shared Channel), depicted in a darker blue color.
**Case A: legacy** shows two PDSCH allocations in the "Large BWP" section.
**Case B: CB009422** also shows two PDSCH allocations in the "Large BWP" section, similar to Case A.
**Case C: legacy** shows three PDSCH allocations. The top one is labeled "PDSCH (UE1)", the middle one is labeled "PDSCH (UE 2)", and the bottom one is labeled "PDSCH sub-area 1". These are all within the "Large BWP" section.
**Case D: CB009422** also shows three PDSCH allocations, mirroring Case C with labels "PDSCH (UE1)", "PDSCH (UE 2)", and "PDSCH sub-area 1". These are also within the "Large BWP" section.
The "Small/PS BWP" section on the right side of the diagrams shows a different allocation pattern, with the PDSCH blocks being smaller and positioned differently compared to the "Large BWP" section. Specifically, in Case C and Case D, the "Small/PS BWP" section shows "PDSCH (UE 2)" and "PDSCH sub-area 1" in the upper and middle parts, and "PDSCH (UE 3)" in the lower part.
The diagrams illustrate how different control and data channels are mapped onto the time-frequency resources under different configurations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13541.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**A:**

Legacy case where the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false and, starting symbol for PDSCH is 3 when at least one CORESET is configured in 3 PDCCH symbols.

**B:**

The NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, and UEs scheduled solely in RBGs non-overlapping with large BWP's dedicated CORESET shall use symbol 2 for PDSCH, while other UEs shall start PDSCH from symbol 3.

**C:**

Legacy case where the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and, starting symbol for PDSCH is 3 when at least one CORESET is configured in 3 PDCCH symbols.

**D:**

The NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, and UEs scheduled solely in PRBs non-overlapping with large BWP's dedicated CORESET shall use symbol 2 for PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area, while other UEs shall start PDSCH in PDSCH sub-area from symbol 3.

Note1: In the examples CORESET1 is configured to large BWP, CORESET2 is configured to small/PS BWP for UE  specific SS and CORESET0 is used for common/type3 search spaces.

Note2: This requirement is obsolete from 26R1 as this case is replaced by 3 symbols in large BWP when small BWP of size 20-40 MHz and/or PS BWP of size 20 MHz is configured. This requirement is replaced by [11178274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku1ktvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[End CB009422-E]

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-8.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11178565`)

3GPP NR DCI format
11178565:
3GPP NR DCI format
The PDSCH PRBs allocated via the DL DCI format 1\_1 are based on a resource allocation type 0 [Before CB008927-A] only [CB008927-A] if /NRBTS/NRcell/actRat1Alloc is set to False [End CB008927-A]. With such scheme, the Frequency resource Assignment is a bitmap indicating the Resource Block Groups ( RBGs) that are allocated to the UE. The RBGs is a set of P consecutive PRBs. P is provided in 3GPP 38.214 Table 5.1.2.2.1-1. gNB-DU shall use the parameter rbgSizePdsch from pdschRat0RbgConfig corresponding to each UE's active dedicated BWP that is defined by the parameter cellBwpId  (NSA: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1840\_replaced([11111024](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJzc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), SA: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1842\_replaced([11111031](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJzdfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If a UE is eligible for UE power saving, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1866\_replaced([11111136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKaj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the UE’s active BWP is defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1868\_replaced([11111144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The total number of RBGs ([image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]) for a downlink bandwidth part i of size [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]PRBs is given by [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$N\_{RBG} = \lfloor \frac{(N\_{BW P,i}^{size} + (N\_{BW P,i}^{start} \mod P))}{P} \rfloor$
The equation defines $N\_{RBG}$ in terms of other variables, including $N\_{BW P,i}^{size}$, $N\_{BW P,i}^{start}$, and $P$. The floor function is indicated by the vertical bars surrounding the fraction.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]  where

-    the size of the first RBG is [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $RBG\_{0}^{size} = P - (N\_{BWp\_{i}}^{start} \mod P)$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

- the size of last RBG is[image-4][image\_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image, a corrupted file, or a deliberate black image. There is no discernible content, text, or objects within it.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] if  [image-5][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] and P otherwise,

- the size of all other RBGs is P.

-   [image-6][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] the size of the Ue active bandwidth part (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1823\_replaced([11110957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

-  [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry mathematical expression. The expression appears to be a fraction with "N\_start" in the numerator and "BWP\_i" in the denominator.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7] the start of the Ue active bandwidth part (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1823\_replaced([11110957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If the PRB blanking function is de-activated (MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = false) OR BWP is any non-largest BWP

All BWP RBGs can be used:

first usable RBG index = 0

last usable RBG index = [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N\_RBG - 1". The text is in a light color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8] (zero based RBG indexing)

If the PRB blanking function is activated (MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = true) AND BWP is any the largest dedicated BWP

first usable RBG index = ceil(nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL/P)

If nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL = 0

last usable RBG index = [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "N\_RBG - 1". The text is in a light blue and purple color with a slight blur.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9]

else

last usable RBG index = floor(([image-10][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N size BWP, i -". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10]nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL)/P)-1

When actSpecificBwpSize [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Nstart BWPi". The text is in a stylized font with a gradient effect, giving it a slightly blurry or glowing appearance. The "N" is larger and appears to be the start of a word or variable, followed by "start" in a smaller font above and to the right. Below this, "BWPi" is written in a similar font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11] might have non-zero value.

The bitmap is of size [image-12][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13547.013.png[/image\_path][/image-12]bits with one bitmap bit per RBG such that each RBG is addressable. The RBGs shall be indexed in the order of increasing frequency and starting at the lowest frequency of the bandwidth part.

Using the frequency assignment of Downlink scheduler in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4487\_replaced([11179166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4A_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB-DU shall set the BITx of frequency Domain Resource to 1 if the RBGx was allocated the Ue and to 0 otherwise, BITx indexed from MSB(BIT0) to LSB in the bitmap.

When the whole bandwidth part is allocated to an UE, all the bits of frequency Domain resource Assignment shall be set to 1.

For each scheduled UE, the bits of frequency Domain resource Assignment corresponding to the allocated RBGs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be set to 1.

If the PRB blanking function is activated (MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = TRUE), gNB-DU shall set the BITx of frequency Domain Resource to 0 if at least one of the PRB of the RBGx is blanked by cell configuration.

If NRBTS/NRCELL/NRCELL\_FDD/actAsymBwGuardband is enabled, gNB-DU shall set the BITx of frequency Domain Resource to 0 if at least one of the PRB of the RBGx is overlaping with the guardband. The guardband for CB7933 is specified in 5G\_UP\_3895\_replaced([11349191](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubcuxvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) CB007933/CB007813 is specified in 5G\_UP\_4096\_replaced([11349195](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubcux_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the impacted RBG is specified in 5G\_UP\_3900\_replaced([11349231](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubcu0_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If NRBTS/NRCELL/NRCELL\_FDD/actAsymBwGuardband is enabled and NRBTS/actMultipleDedicatedBWPs is enabled for 30/10MHz asymmetric BW combination, gNB-DU shall set the BITx of frequency Domain Resource to 0 if at least one of the PRB of the RBGx is overlapping with the guardband.

1) If P(the size of RBG, P is provided in 3GPP 38.214 Table 5.1.2.2.1-1 ) equals 8, BIT6 of frequency Domain Resource shall be set to 0.

2) If P equals 16, BIT3 of frequency Domain Resource shall be set to 0.

If the DL PRB sharing in subframe 0/5/9 is enabled (rrmLteDLPrbSharing=true, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4722\_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall set the BITx of frequency Domain Resource to 0 in the corresponding bitmap for subframe (0/5/9) if at least one of the PRB of the RBGx is reserved by LTE in given subframe (0/5/9) according to values received in EN DSS DL Cell Resource Area Allocation message (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4743\_replaced([11172249](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuK2U_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note: pdschRat0RbgConfig is from MRBTS/NRBTS/pdschRat0RbgConfig or NRBTS.BWP\_PROFILE.PDSCH.pdschRat0RbgConfig of the largest BWP.

11178658:

The PDSCH PRBs allocated via the DL DCI format 1\_1 are based on a resource allocation type 1 if /NRBTS/NRcell/actRat1Alloc is set to True. The frequency resource assignment is a resource indication value (RIV). The RIV value is a function of the number of contiguous PRBs ([image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]) and the starting PRB inside the bandwidth part (the [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]).
RIV field has a size of [image-2][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] bits
 
 
The resource indication value (RIV) is defined by
if [image-3][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] then
[image-4][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
else
[image-5][image\_desc]The image is completely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]
where
[image-6][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] 1 and shall not exceed[image-7][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]
 
Where:

If DCI 1\_1 is used to switch bandwidth part for a UE eligible for the feature (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1866\_replaced([11111136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKaj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N size" and "RW P" in a stylized font. The text is blurry and has a gradient effect, with colors ranging from blue to orange. The letters are outlined in black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9] is the size of the source downlink UE active bandwidth part (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1857\_replaced([11111082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall compute the RIV using:

The text appears to be "N size BW'P".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13549.015.png[/image\_path][/image-17] - NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL) -       [image-18][image\_desc]The image contains text that is blurry and difficult to read.
