**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11178662`)

3GPP NR DCI format
DL"dynamicswitch" resource allocation

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-8.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11178697`)

3GPP NR DCI format
11178697:
3GPP NR DCI format
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to true and if NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true

and if the UE supports dynamicSwitchRA-Type0-1-PDSCH UE capability (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_13162\_replaced([11282505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tpfo0fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

the PDSCH PRBs allocated via the DL DCI format 1\_1 are based on a resource allocation type "dynamicswitch" (see 38.212 §7.3.1.2.2) which can be either a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 or a "dynamicswitch" RAT1 allocation.

The size of the Frequency Domain Resource Assignment field is equal to {MAX(RIV\_field\_size;  NRBG) + 1} bits

where:

* RIV\_field\_size is the the size of the RIV field if the resource assignment was a type 1 and is defined under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23475\_replaced([11178569](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* NRBG is the total number of RBGs if the resource assignment was a type 0 and is defined under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If the Frequency Domain Resource Assignment field is  a "dynamicswitch" RAT1 allocation,

* the MSB bit is set to 1 and the RIV\_field\_size LSB bits is the RIV field which is a function of the number of contiguous PRBs ([image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13551.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]) and the starting PRB inside the bandwidth part (the [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13551.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1])  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23475\_replaced([11178569](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If the Frequency Domain Resource Assignment field is  a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation,

* the MSB bit is set to 0 and and the NRBG LSB bits is is a bitmap indicating the Resource Block Groups ( RBGs) that are allocated to the UE (as described in 5G\_UP\_AG\_23474). gNB-DU shall use the NIDD parameter BWP\_PROFILE/PDSCH.pdschRat0RbgConfig and the Table 5.1.2.2.1-1 in 38.214 §5.1.2.2.1 to determine the RBG size P corresponding to each UE's active dedicated BWP (see 5G\_UP\_AG\_23474).

The padding bits between MSB bit and LSB bits are set to 0 (if they exist).

If DCI 1\_1 is not used to switch bandwidth part,

* if the Frequency Domain Resource Assignment field is  a "dynamicswitch" RAT1 allocation,

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains text that is blurry and difficult to read. The text appears to be "N size BW'P".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13551.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is the size of the Ue active BWP (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1823\_replaced([11110957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as it is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23475\_replaced([11178569](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* if the Frequency Domain Resource Assignment field is  a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation,

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13551.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is the size and  [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N start BWP,i". The text is in a dark color and appears to be slightly blurred. The letters are arranged in two lines, with "N start" on the top line and "BWP,i" on the bottom line. The "N" on the top line is larger than the other letters and is positioned to the left of "start". The "BWP,i" on the bottom line is smaller than the letters on the top line.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13551.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] the start of the Ue active bandwidth part (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1823\_replaced([11110957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as it is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If DCI 1\_1 is used to switch bandwidth part for a UE eligible for the feature (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1866\_replaced([11111136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKaj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

* if the size of Frequency Domain Resource Assignment field calculated for the source downlink UE active bandwidth part is smaller than the size of Frequency Domain Resource Assignment field calculated for the target downlink UE active bandwidth part (which happens for a BWP switch from PS Power to BWP1 or BWP2, see 38.212 §7.3.1.2.2), gNB-DU shall use a RAT0 allocation for the DCI-based 1\_1 BWP switch and proceed as it is described for RAT0 BWp switch in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1857\_replaced([11111082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* otherwise, gNB-DU shall use a "dynamicswitch" RAT1 allocation for the DCI-based 1\_1 BWP switch,  [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains text that appears to be "N size RWP". The text is blurry and has a gradient effect.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13551.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] is the size of the source downlink UE active bandwidth part and proceed as it is described for RAT0 BWp switch in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1857\_replaced([11111082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23475\_replaced([11178569](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

gNB-DU shall set the value set for the FDRA IE in the DCI for the BWP switch as it is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1857\_replaced([11111082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N size BWP" in a stylized font. The letters are slightly blurred and have a colorful, iridescent effect. The "N" is large and in the foreground, while "size" is smaller and positioned above and to the right of the "N". "BWP" is even smaller and positioned below and to the right of the "N".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13551.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] (for dynamicswitch RAT0)  is the size of the UE active BWP part (or source BWP). (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1823\_replaced([11110957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and resp. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If the Frequency Domain Resource Assignment field is  a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation,

* gNB-DU shall handle the bitmap is of size [image-7][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13551.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]bits like it is described in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* gNB-DU shall follow the same for the PRB blanking (NRCELL/actPRBBlanking), the specific bandwidth (NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize) in the same way than  5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-9**  (ID: `11178704`)

3GPP NR DCI format
Setting of parameter Frequency Domain Resource Assignment in DL DCI Format 1\_0

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-9.0-6**  (ID: `12705100`)

3GPP NR DCI format
12705100:
3GPP NR DCI format
The PDSCH PRBs allocated via the DL DCI format 1\_0 are based on a resource allocation type 1 only. With such scheme, the frequency resource assignment is a resource indication value (RIV). The RIV value is a function of the number of contiguous PRBs ([image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]) and the starting PRB inside the bandwidth part (the [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]).
If the DCI 1-0 is granted over the common search space, typically for the msg2 or PDSCH during transient phase which is corresponding to the period between receiving PsUser\_UserModifyReq and receiving PsUser\_UserReconfCompletedReq, **PDSCH just after a beam failure recovery which is corresponding to the period from the receiving time of Msg3 with CRNTI until the updated beam ID can be applied (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2032\_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22395\_replaced([11174363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),** 
 
RIV field has a size of [image-2][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] bits
 
 
The resource indication value (RIV) is defined by
if [image-3][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] then
[image-4][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
else
[image-5][image\_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]
where
\* [image-6][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] 1 and shall not exceed[image-7][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]
 
 
 
If the DCI 1-0 is granted over the common search space, typically for the msg2 or PDSCH during transient phase which is corresponding to the period between receiving PsUser\_UserModifyReq and receiving PsUser\_UserReconfCompletedReq PDSCH just after a beam failure recovery which is corresponding to the period from  the receiving time of Msg3 with CRNTI until the updated beam ID can be applied (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2032\_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22395\_replaced([11174363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the NSizeBwp is equal to the number of RB of the Coreset0. The number of RB of the coreset0 corresponds to the 'number of bit set to 1 in coresetFreqDomain  of the CoresetId=0' multiply by 6 in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2134\_replaced([11308300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrDvE_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 
For scheduling RA-Msg4(CRI) alone or RA-Msg4(CRI) with SRB0/SRB1 message or any(re)transmission(s) for a UE without its dedicated configuration activated, NSizeBwp is equal to the number of RBs of the CORESET(0).
 
In case of PRB blanking function is supported and CORESET#0 is overlapping with blanked PRBs, CORESET#0 shall be configured but not map to any SS, CORESET#1 shall be configured and map to ra-Searchspace(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3935\_replaced([11168314](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktvYg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [image-8][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8] shall be set according to the CORESET in which the DCI is sent.  That is, [image-9][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9] is computed starting from the first RB of the Coreset#1 , i.e. for an allocation starting on first RB of Coreset#1 [image-10][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10] is = 0. First RB of Coreset#1 is determined by coresetFreqDomain of the CoresetId=1,  with RB of lowest frequency provided by coresetFreqDomain. Because the CORESET#0 is configured, so NSizeBwp used for RIV encoding is equal to the number of RB of the CORESET#0, irrespective of the CORESET in which the DCI is sent. **The number of RB of the coreset0 corresponds to the 'number of bit set to 1 in coresetFreqDomain of the CoresetId=0' multiply by 6 in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2134\_replaced([11308300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrDvE_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).** [image-11][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11] **shall not exceed “Min(NSizeBwp, coreset 1 size) -Rb start”.**\*\***5G-L2-PS shall compute the RIV using:
-[image-12][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.013.png[/image\_path][/image-12]  set to 0 as the PDSCH is granted starting on first PRB of the bandwidth part,
- [image-13][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.014.png[/image\_path][/image-13] set according to the number of PRB computed in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4487\_replaced([11179166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4A_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
If actRateMatchedCsiIm is set to 'true' and rateMatchedCsiImInMsg2Slot is set to 'true'  and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false the first RB for MSG2 scheduling shall be computed according to ZP CSI-RS configuration received from higher layers during cell setup procedure.
If ZP CSI-RS Starting Prb < NSizeBwp / 2
If (ZP CSI-RS Starting Prb + ZP CSI-RS number of Prbs) >= NSizeBwp / 2
Select [image-14][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.015.png[/image\_path][/image-14]in the way that satisfies all of the following criteria:
\* Allocated in the RA-RNTI schedulable RBs ().
\* Points to the largest area available in BWP and non-overlapping with the ZP CSI-RS (weather from firstPrbInSearspace up to ZP CSI-RS (ZP CSI-RS Starting Prb-1) or starting above ZP CSI-RS (ZP CSI-RS Starting Prb + ZP CSI-RS number of Prbs) up to last RB in BWP considering the abovementioned MSG2 scheduling size limit.
else
 [image-15][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.015.png[/image\_path][/image-15] = Max(ZP CSI-RS Starting Prb + ZP CSI-RS number of Prbs, firstPrbInSearspace)  - firstPrbInSearspace
else
 [image-16][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13555.016.png[/image\_path][/image-16] = 0,
where firstPrbInSearspace = the first CRB of the coreset mapped to the Searchspace
and Searchspace:
 RA-RNTI transmission =  Searchspace Type-1
 C-RNTI transmission =  Searchspace Type-3
If the DL PRB sharing in slots  0/5/9 is enabled (*rrmLteDLPrbSharing=true [CB007372-M] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M], refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4722\_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) L2PS shall not shedule any PDSCH PRBs allocated via the DL DCI format 1\_0 in slots 0 or 5.*
The rationale is that such slots are having only very limited number of available REs due to presence of LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH so that would additionally reduce efficiency of this type of DCI. [CB007372-M] With NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true PDSCH PRBs allocated via the DL DCI format 1\_0 are in area not shared with LTE. [End CB007372-M]
 
In case of FR1 CA, if in the FDD DSS PCell there are slots where ZP CSI-RS is configured, during transient phase for SCell Addition (5G\_UP\_ALG\_11907\_replaced([11120306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqPYnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) / SCell Release of the last configured SCell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_11908\_replaced([11120596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqSb5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
- when CAT-M support is disabled (actLteNrDssLteCatM = false), PCell 5G-L2-PS shall not schedule with DCI 1\_0 scrambled with C-RNTI any PDSCH PRBs which overlap with configured ZP CSI-RS (for more details see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4489\_replaced([11179193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
Note: When CAT-M support is enabled (actLteNrDssLteCatM = true), the PDSCH PRBs scheduled with DCI 1\_0 scrambled with C-RNTI may overlap with configured ZP CSI-RS (for more details see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16468\_replaced([11181355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
 
Note:
5G-L2-PS supports only the DCI format 1\_0 scheduled in the common search space of the initial bandwidth part and UE dedicated bandwidth part. If the DCI 1\_0 is transmitted over the UE Specific Search space, this requirement shall be revisited.

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-10**  (ID: `11178897`)

3GPP NR DCI format
Downlink Assignment Index in DCI

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-10.0-6**  (ID: `13106178`)

3GPP NR DCI format
13106178:
3GPP NR DCI format
This field is applicable when Harq-codebook = dynamic is configured

For each UE, the L2-PS shall maintain the current count DAI until the UlData\_PucchReceiveReq is sent to L1.

For each UE configured with CA, the L2-PS shall additionally maintain the total DAI.

If scheduled for the first time since HARQ feedback has been requested to L1 or after the DL scheduler starts using k1aCsi for the UE due to an A-CSI request issued by the UL scheduler  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4331\_replaced([11177668](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), current count DAI shall be reset to 1 and total DAI reset to 0.

If rdUsePucchF2OnlyForHarq is true (see 5G\_L2\_1189\_replaced([11181836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27XKEfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then count DAI shall be reset to 3 and total DAI to 2 such that the UE reports at least 3 bits of HARQ feedback over PUCCH format 2.

When a UE is scheduled, if configured with CA, total DAI shall be incremented by the number of serving cells it is allocated on.

For each scheduled transport block for a given UE in the slot according to sCellIndex order (according to C-Plane configuration, see 5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_4849\_replaced([11357911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wY_O9fvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the L2-PS shall fill the c-DAI = (current Count DAI - 1) mod 4 and T-DAI = (total DAI - 1) mod 4 in the DCI and increment the count DAI.

Determination of C-DAI and T-DAI values for CA UEs is commonly done for all carriers after FD scheduling. Rational is that there is no real-time alignment of scheduling results for FD schedulers running in parallel.

With FR1 TDD carrier aggregation support, 5G-L2-PS shall fill counter DAI and total DAI for DL PDSCH scheduling over all activated serving cells according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2944\_replaced([11123902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note: FR1 TDD CA must have NRBTS/NRDU.flexibleCAMode is set to 1.

If UE uses Distributed PUCCH Allocation Algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2885\_replaced([11123447](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqjhoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall fill counter DAI and total DAI for DL PDSCH scheduling over all activated serving cells according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2941\_replaced([11123890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For DCI 1\_0, only Count DAI is supported.

If UE is in TP Enabled::SAG ON state (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18906\_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), setting of 1st DAI in DCI 0\_1 is per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22970\_replaced([11205450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwpilfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

If a PUSCH on a serving cell doesn’t overlap with a PUCCH with HARQ-ACK information, L2-PS shall set the 1st DAI of the DCI 0\_1, which grants the PUSCH to value 3 (corresponds to [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font and appears to be blurred. The expression is:
$v\_{T-DAI}^{UL}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-11071433.png[/image\_path][/image-0]= 4).

If PUSCH is scheduled with DCI 0\_1 during BWP scanning procedure (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16104\_replaced([11111313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpMPyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB shall assume that no HARQ-ACK bits is multiplexed from DL DCI and set the 1st DAI value to 3

If UE would transmit a long PUCCH with HARQ-ACK information on P(S)cell for slot n,

* L2-PS shall set the 1st DAI of a DCI 0\_1 which grants the PUSCH on P(S)cell overlapping with the PUCCH to the below value;
* If UE is not pre-allocated PUCCH resource by distributed PUCCH algorithm, to C-DAI of the last DCI 1\_1 where the corresponding A/N still falls in slot n.
* If UE is pre-allocated PUCCH resource by distributed PUCCH algorithm and actK1Opt = 1 or actK1Opt = 2, to value "11" if maximum T-DAI is smaller than 9; otherwise to the maximum T-DAI (maximum T-DAI is calculated with all scheduling opportunity for PCell and SCell based on distributed PUCCH algorithm pre-allocation result). Note: For C-DAI and T-DAI setting refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2941\_replaced([11123890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* L2-PS shall set the 1st DAI of a DCI 0\_1 which grants the earliest PUSCH on SCell overlapping with the PUCCH to the below value;
* If UE is not pre-allocated PUCCH resource by distributed PUCCH algorithm, to value 3 (corresponds to [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font and appears to be blurred. The expression is:
  $v\_{T-DAI}^{UL}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-11071433.png[/image\_path][/image-1] = 4). Rationale: when this case happens, PUCCH resource is only allocated for PDSCH transmission on PCell, it's possibly too late for SCell to get C-DAI of the last DCI 1\_1 where the corresponding A/N still falls in slot n from PCell.
* If UE is pre-allocated PUCCH resource by distributed PUCCH algorithm and actK1Opt = 1 or actK1Opt = 2 [CB014871-SR-A] and is not configured with ~~simultaneousPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17~~ parallelTxPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17 [End CB014871-SR-A], to value "11" if maximum T-DAI is smaller than 9; otherwise to the maximum T-DAI (maximum T-DAI is calculated with all scheduling opportunity for PCell and SCell based on distributed PUCCH algorithm pre-allocation result). Note: For C-DAI and T-DAI setting refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2941\_replaced([11123890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB014871-SR-A]

* If UE is configured with TDD+FDD 2CC UL CA and ~~simultaneousPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17~~ parallelTxPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17 and the PUCCH resource is pre-allocated by distributed PUCCH algorithm, L2-PS shall set the DAI of the DCI0\_1 which grants PUSCH in SCell in the overlapping UL slot to 3 (corresponding to [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font and appears to be blurred. The expression is:
  $v\_{T-DAI}^{UL}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-11071433.png[/image\_path][/image-2] = 4).

[End CB014871-SR-A]

* If the PUCCH overlaps with PUSCH on SCell in the multiple slots, L2-PS shall set the 1st DAI of the DCI0\_1 which grants PUSCH on SCell in the multiple slots except the earliest one to value 3 (corresponds to [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font and appears to be blurred. The expression is:
  $v\_{T-DAI}^{UL}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-11071434.png[/image\_path][/image-3] = 4).

For more details see 3GPP 38.213 Chapter 9.1.3.2. Note, in short PUCCH case there is never A/N multiplexed in PUSCH.

In case that A-CSI is requested with the UL DCI, computation of the number of Harq-Ack bits shall be done according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4472\_replaced([11178969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note:

* For UE configured with UL CA and long PUCCH, according to 3GPP 38.213 Chapter 9.1.3.2:
* In case the 1st DAI of the DCI 0\_1 is set differently for overlapping PUSCH between PCell and SCell, UE only cares about the 1st DAI of the DCI 0\_1 granting the PUSCH which HARQ-ACK information is multiplexed in.
* If HARQ-ACK information is multiplexed in PUSCH granted by a DCI0\_0, UE doesn't care about the 1st DAI of the DCI0\_1, which grants PUSCH on other serving cell overlapping with the PUSCH granted by the DCI0\_0.

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-11**  (ID: `11178969`)

3GPP NR DCI format
1’st DAI in UL DCI with A-CSI

---

**3.29.6.2.1.0-3.0-11.0-2**  (ID: `11178978`)

3GPP NR DCI format
Number of Harq-Ack bits that are considered to be multiplexed with A-CSI on PUSCH is computed as follows.

Assuming the DCI slot N.

[PR712608, CB008246-C, CB008246-E]If UE is scheduled without 64qamLowSE table [End PR712608, CB008246-C, CB008246-E]

-If the UE is scheduled for A-CSI at DCI time N with k2aCsi timing and the slot N is configured with PDCCH token allocated to PDSCH (slot N is eligible to schedule PDSCH data) and k1 computed for the slot N is defined and equals to k2aCsi:

- Number of Harq-Ack bits considered to be multiplexed with A-CSI is OACK + 1, where OACK represents the number of Harq-Ack bits received from DL scheduled before the UL scheduler to start corresponding to the number of DL scheduling before slot N that accumulates Harq-Ack in the slot N+k2aCsi

o Note: that is, since there is insufficient time for DL scheduler to inform UL scheduler if a DL DCI is scheduled in the same slot, UL scheduler shall always consider that PDSCH is scheduled, with k1 = k2aCsi (in practice, this case is encountered in case of FR1 TDD)

- UL\_DAI in UL DCI is set accordingly

- When PUSCH reception request for N+k2aCsi is sent to 5G-L1-UL:

o L2-PS shall request L1 to decode OACK + 1 Harq-Ack bits multiplexed on PUSCH

 if no DL grant was sent to the UE in the slot N such that to request UE to multiplex Harq-Ack and PUSCH at time N+k2aCs

 DL scheduled shall only keep the first OACK bits as relevant and corresponding to the DL scheduling before time N.

 else:

 DL scheduled shall keep as relevant all OACK + 1 bits

-otherwise, number of Harq-Ack bits considered to be multiplexed with A-CSI is OACK , where OACK represents the number of Harq-Ack bits received from DL scheduled before the UL scheduler to start corresponding to the number of DL scheduling before slot N that accumulates Harq-Ack in the slot N+k2/k2aCsi

//note that this otherwise case covers all the other cases, including P-CSI on PUSCH

[PR712608, CB008246-C, CB008246-E]EndIf (UE is scheduled without 64qamLowSE table) [End PR712608, CB008246-C, CB008246-E]

[PR712608, CB008246-C, CB008246-E]If UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE table:

-If the UE is scheduled for A-CSI at DCI time N with k2aCsi timing and the slot N is configured with PDCCH token allocated to PDSCH (slot N is eligible to schedule PDSCH data) and k1 computed for the slot N is defined and equals to k2aCsi:

- Number of HARQ-Ack bits considered to be multiplexed with A-CSI is MAX(OACK + 1, 3), where OACK represents the number of Harq-Ack bits received from DL scheduled before the UL scheduler to start corresponding to the number of DL scheduling before slot N that accumulates Harq-Ack in the slot N+k2aCsi

o Note 1: that is, since there is insufficient time for DL scheduler to inform UL scheduler if a DL DCI is scheduled in the same slot, UL scheduler shall always consider that PDSCH is scheduled, with k1 = k2aCsi (in practice, this case is encountered in case of FR1 TDD)

o Note 2: As only Semistatic BetaOffsets set is supported in Cplane for 64qamLowSE table, minimum 3 HARQ-Ack bits are required in order not to puncture CSI part2 on PUSCH.

- UL\_DAI in UL DCI is set accordingly

- When PUSCH reception request for N+k2aCsi is sent to 5G-L1-UL:

L2-PS shall request L1 to decode Max(OACK + 1, 3) Harq-Ack bits multiplexed on PUSCH

 if no DL grant was sent to the UE in the slot N such that to request UE to multiplex Harq-Ack and PUSCH at time N+k2aCs

 DL scheduled shall only keep the first OACK bits as relevant and corresponding to the DL scheduling before time N.

 else:

 DL scheduled shall keep as relevant all OACK + 1 bits

-otherwise, if (the UE is scheduled for A-CSI with normal k2) AND (Harq-Ack bits are mutliplexed together with A-CSI on PUSCH)

- number of Harq-Ack bits considered to be multiplexed with A-CSI is Max(OACK, 3) , where OACK represents the number of Harq-Ack bits received from DL scheduled before the UL scheduler to start corresponding to the number of DL scheduling before slot N that accumulates Harq-Ack in the slot N+k2

o Note 2: As only Semistatic BetaOffsets set is supported in Cplane for 64qamLowSE table, minimum 3 HARQ-Ack bits are required in order not to puncture CSI part2 on PUSCH.

- UL\_DAI in UL DCI is set accordingly

- When PUSCH reception request for N is sent to 5G-L1-UL:

L2-PS shall request L1 to decode MAX(OACK, 3) Harq-Ack bits multiplexed on PUSCH

 if the number of Harq-Ack bits decided above is >0 and < 3

* L2-PS shall drop the tail of the UCI sequence that exceeds the decided OACK.

 else

* do nothing

EndIf ( UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE table)

[End PR712608, CB008246-C, CB008246-E]

---

## 3.29.7 DL Frequency Domain Scheduling

### 3.29.7.1 DL FDM Specific General Information

#### 3.29.7.1.0-1 DL FDM Specific General Information DL FDM specific information and parameters - Overview (ID: 11178994)

**3.29.7.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11178997`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
DL FDM allows the scheduling of multiple UEs in a single slot by giving each UE different frequency resources. This requirement summarizes the information and parameters that affect DL FDM:

 DL FDM Information (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10648\_replaced([11179002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 DL FDM Parameters (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10585\_replaced([11179018](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku3Z4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

#### 3.29.7.1.0-2 DL FDM Specific General Information DL FDM Information (ID: 11179002)

**3.29.7.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11179012`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
FDM information:

- FDM is currently supported in FR1 TDD and FDD

- DL FD resources for are counted with granularity of RBGs[CB008927-A] if the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, or PRBs  if the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true [End CB008927-A]

- In FR2, the behavior of FD scheduling is not changed by 5GC000679. Only a single UE is scheduled with full BW allocation as before.

---

#### 3.29.7.1.0-3 DL FDM Specific General Information DL FDM Parameters (ID: 11179018)

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11179022`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
The number of L1 DL subcells - rrmNumDlSubcells

---

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13455183`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
rrmNumDlSubcells = dlSubcellIdMax **+ 1**

(dlSubcellIdMax is defined in 5G\_UP\_1854\_replaced([11375881](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueQLJvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The number of L1 DL subcells available for PDSCH rrmNumDlSubcells depends on the DL subcell channel capacity (5G\_UP\_2554\_replaced([11390916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufWXWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [Before CB013712-A]and whether DL subcell is available for usage based on subcell pooling (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5230\_replaced([11210126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB013712-A]or large baseband pooling (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15314\_replaced([11212864](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxDySvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or **L1 ARM capacity based subcell deactivation (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18301\_replaced([11213855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxKf8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13411\_replaced([11212398](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxBWAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**with a maximum value of *dlSubcellIdMax+1 (*dlSubcellIdMax is defined in 5G\_UP\_1854\_replaced([11375881](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueQLJvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**\***\*

---

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11179040`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
The maximum number of UEs that can be FDMed per DL subcell - rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell

---

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-2.0-7**  (ID: `13470221`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
For **TDD FR1:**

**TDD FR1** rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell\*\*\*\*

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 Variant** | **Parameter** |  |  |  |  |
| *actFdmScheduling* | True |  |  | False |  |
| *actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot* | True | False | False | False |  |
| *pucchModeSelect* | longPucch | longPucch | ShortPucch | ShortPucch |  |
| Loner |  | 16 Note1 | 4 Note2 | 4 Note2 | 4 Note2 |
| Loki |  | 8            Note6 | 4 | 2 | 2 |
| Thor |  | 9 Note7 | 4 | 2 | 2 |
| GPU |  |  | 4 (Without pooling) |  |  |

Note1: 16 is the maximum number of PDSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIL, refer to 5G\_UP\_3179\_replaced([11396901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufzqYPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note2: rdMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcellTdd refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10745\_replaced([11193417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvsgWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note6: 8 is the maximum number of PDSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIO, refer to 5G\_UP\_3179\_replaced([11396901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufzqYPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note7: Since BB pooling is mandatory to be enabled on Thor BB HW, PDSCH capacity per subcell is not useful and not applicable anymore. In case of UE pooling is disabled on Thor BB HW, 9 is the maximum number of PDSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIP, refer to 5G\_UP\_3179\_replaced([11396901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufzqYPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In some special deployments, for example, TDD deployment with four cells on the ABIL (5GC002509) and TDD deployment with 3 cells on the ABIL (5GC002411), when L2 instances for UL and DL are deployed on the same core, the UP overload control might react and restrict the CS1. Instead of up to 16 UEs, the maximum number of UEs in this scenario is 9 per slot.

**rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell for an FR1 TDD Supercell**

If NRBTS.NRCELL.actSuperCell=TRUE

* For half-board deployment,
* If NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot=TRUE
  + rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell=16
* Else,
  + rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell=4
* Else, if the deployment is full-board and HwVariant=ABIO/ABIQ/ASOE/ASOF
* If NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot=TRUE
  + rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell=10
* Else,
  + rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell=4
* Else, if the deployment is full-board and HwVariant=ABIP/ASOG/ASOH/RINLINE2
* If NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot=TRUE
  + rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell=12
* Else,
  + rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell=4

For **FDD:**

**FDD rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell**

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 Variant** | *actFdmScheduling* | True |  |  | False |
| *actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot* | True | False | False | False |  |
| *pucchModeSelect* | longPucch | longPucch | ShortPucch | ShortPucch |  |
| Loner |  | 12 Note3 | 2 | 2 | 2 |
| Loki |  | 12 Note5 | 4 | 2 | 2 |
| Thor |  | 12 Note8 | 4 | 2 | 2 |
| [CB016062-AD] GPU [end CB016062-AD] |  | [ CB016062-F, CB016062-AD] 12  [End CB016062-F, CB016062-AD] | [CB016062-AD] 4 [End CB016062-AD] |  |  |

Note3: 14 is the maximum number of PDSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIL. For L1 ABIO capacity see 5G\_UP\_3192\_replaced([11397060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf0RbvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Value 12 has been chosen in order to limit the required PRB PUCCH F1 resource.

Note4: rdMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcellFdd, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10747\_replaced([11193426](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvsgW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note5: 12 is the maximum number of PDSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIO, refer to 5G\_UP\_3192\_replaced([11397060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf0RbvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note8: Since BB pooling is mandatory to be enabled on Thor BB HW, PDSCH capacity per subcell is not useful and not applicable anymore. In case of UE pooling is disabled on Thor BB HW, the default value of "rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell" should use same value as Loki BB HW, refer to 5G\_UP\_3192\_replaced([11397060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf0RbvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11179079`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
The maximum number of UEs to be FDMed in DL - rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl

---

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-3.0-9**  (ID: `13479136`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
If FDM is disabled (actFdmScheduling == False)

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = 1

Else

**TDD FR1:**

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = rrmNumDlSubcells \* rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell

If (actFdmScheduling == True && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && pucchModeSelect == longPucch && **L1 Variant == Loner)**

Then

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl, NRCELL/maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot, 16)

Note: 16 is the maximum number of PDSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIL in 21A, refer to 4.8.8.4 PDSCH capacity, 5G\_UP\_3179\_replaced([11396901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufzqYPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else If (NRBTS.NRCELL.actSuperCell==TRUE && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True)

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl, NRCELL/maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot)

Else If (L1 Variant == Loki or Thor or GPU) && actFdmScheduling == True && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == False && pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed == False )

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl, 4)

Else If (L1 Variant == Loki or Thor && actFdmScheduling == True && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed == False )

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl, NRCELL/maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot, 8)

Else If (L1 Variant == Thor && actFdmScheduling == True && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed == False && rdTriggerEcpri72eInterfaceSplit == False)

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl, NRCELL/maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot, 8 )

Else If (L1 Variant == Thor && actFdmScheduling == True && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && pucchModeSelect == longPucch               && scheUePoolingAllowed == False && rdTriggerEcpri72eInterfaceSplit == True)

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl, NRCELL/maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot, 9 )

Note:   rdTriggerEcpri72eInterfaceSplit is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25991\_replaced([11193867](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwKu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Else If (L1 Variant == Loki or Thor  or GPU && actFdmScheduling == True && scheUePoolingAllowed == True)**

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = maxNumPdschPerSlotByBBRM

Note: maxNumPdschPerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5264\_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl shall be further limited by rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlCceSplit  if applicable as per  5G\_UP\_ALG\_17531\_replaced([11179128](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4A9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min(rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlCceSplit, rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl)

**FDD:**

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = rrmNumDlSubcells \* rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell

If (L1 Variant == Loner && actFdmEnhancedScheduling == True && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && pucchModeSelect == longPucch)

Then

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl, NRCELL/maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot, 12)

Note: 14 is the maximum number of PDSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIL in 21A, refer to 4.8.8.4 PDSCH capacity, 5G\_UP\_3179\_replaced([11396901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufzqYPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Value 12 has been chosen in order to limit the required PRB PUCCH F1 resource.

Else If ((L1 Variant == Loki or Thor [CB016062-AD] or GPU [End CB016062-AD]  )&& actFdmScheduling == True && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == False && pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed == False)

Then

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl, 4)

Note: 4 is the maximum number of PDSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIO. For L1 ABIO capacity see 5G\_UP\_3192\_replaced([11397060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf0RbvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else If ((L1 Variant == Loki or Thor [CB016062-AD] or GPU [End CB016062-AD])  && actFdmScheduling == True && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed == False)

Then

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl, NRCELL/maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot, 12)

Else If ((L1 Variant == Loki or Thor [CB016062-F] or GPU [End CB016062-F] ) && actFdmScheduling == True &&scheUePoolingAllowed == True)

{

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = maxNumPdschPerSlotByBBRM

if (NRBTS/NRDU/flexibleCaMode is set to DualCellPerCore (value 3) && dual cells setup in the L2-PS core && sum of active CA UE number of the two cells (counted only in Pcell) is greater than rdFDMReducedThresholdActiveCAUEnumber && the slot is CA DPA process slot)

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = max (1, maxNumPdschPerSlotByBBRM \* DPASlotFDMReducedFactor)

Note:

DPASlotFDMReducedFactor is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7981\_replaced([11211886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9ruPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

maxNumPdschPerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5264\_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

DPA process slot stands for measurement processing cycle in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2936\_replaced([11123865](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdFDMReducedThresholdActiveCAUEnumber is defined in CP3.

}

In overload state, UP Overload control may restrict the rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl further (see 5G\_L2\_7048\_replaced([11213384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-rj2vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note the parameter NRCELL/maxNumPdschAllocationPerSlot is received at cell setup or reconfiguration and a new value is applied at next scheduling period.

Note: 1 <= rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl <= rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell \* MIN (rrmNumDlSubcells, rrmNumUlSubcellsPucch)

where the number of L1 UL subcells for PUCCH **rrmNumUlSubcellsPucch depends on the UL subcell channel capacity (5G\_UP\_2554\_replaced([11390916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufWXWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with a maximum value of *ulSubcellIdMax+1 (\*ulSubcellIdMax is defined in 5G\_UP\_1859\_replaced([11375909](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueQLKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))***\*

---

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11179128`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
The maximum number of UEs to be FDMed in DL per slot in a CCE split enabled slot for multiple dedicated BWPs in TDD FR1

---

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11179134`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
if (dlEcpriFdBeamforming == True && ulEcpriFdBeamforming == True && actFdmEnhancedScheduling == True )

When NRCELL.actDynPdcchTdMode is set to perBWP, then if CCE split is enabled in a slot, the maximum number of UEs FDMed in that slot (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlCceSplit)  is limited by the number of supported beams per symbol in DL (MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10617\_replaced([11179139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4A9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlCceSplit = floor(MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl\*rdFactorInCceSplitSlot) // rdFactorInCceSplitSlot is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17525\_replaced([11193576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtufPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `11179139`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
The maximum number of beams to be supported per stream in L1 DL subcell - rrmMaxNumBeamsDlStream

---

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12783545`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
Please note, the meaning of rrmMaxNumBeamsDlStream shall be interpreted as the maximum number of beams that one AxC/eAxC stream can support per symbol. For example, to schedule one 4x4 UE (with 4 AxC/eAxC streams), one beam direction will be counted on every AxC/eAxC stream. To schedule one 2x2 UE (with 2 AxC/eAxC streams), one beam direction will be counted on two AxC/eAxC streams each, the number of supported beams on other two streams is not changed.

**TDD** FR1:\*\*\*\*

The FD beamforming shall be considered enabled only when dlEcpriFdBeamforming = True and ulEcpriFdBeamforming = True (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2526\_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True.

If ( dlEcpriFdBeamforming == True && ulEcpriFdBeamforming == True && actFdmEnhancedScheduling== True ) then

rrmMaxNumBeamsDlStream is assigned the "Number of supported beams per symbol" value,

where:

For eCPRI FR1 TDD Radio Units that support beamforming, the Radio Unit provides two parameters:

 Number of supported beams per symbol in DL //MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl

 Number of supported sections per symbol in DL //MaxNumSectionsPerSymbolDl

The number of sections per symbol is currently ignored (in DL).  ORAN cases are not supported in this functionality.

Parameter informs of number of supported beams per symbol per eAxC.

It is known, that for Nokia RUs, rrmMaxNumBeamsDlStream >= rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell.

IF (MaxNumSectionsPerSymbolDl < (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl + 1) OR MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl < (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl + 1 ))

THEN L2PS shall printout a warning

Note. Such situation is unlikely to happen. All Nokia RUs, as part of required eCPRI functionalities, shall support a minimum number of beams/sections to ensure proper operation. Additionally, similar check is done in CPRT to protect the cell against such situations. See also 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2526\_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and RD parameters 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4032\_replaced([11326803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Ts0ClfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4036\_replaced([11326871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Ts0CqfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else  
 rrmMaxNumBeamsDlStream = 1

When supercell mode enabled, refer to 5G\_UP\_5471\_replaced([11353418](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub_hVfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, then L1 subcell used for UE allocation is found by evaluating the mapping table L1 subcell <-> beam ID.**

**FDD:**

[CB008577-A] If the cell has FDD beamforming enabled ([11111439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) :

- rrmMaxNumBeamsDlStream shall be: Number of supported beams per symbol in DL //MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl (refer to cplane [12783847: RU capability based configuration for NetConfigY (ORAN) fronthaul with BF WMP radio](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qCq88o7qEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

rrmMaxNumBeamsDlStream shall be larger or equal to (NIDD: +1) note, 1 is for

else: [End CB008577-A]

* rrmMaxNumBeamsDlStream = 1

The maximum number of UEs to be FDMed in DL initial BWP - rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlInitialBWP

**3.29.7.1.0-3.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11179161`)

DL FDM Specific General Information
[CB009550-B] If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is False [End CB009550-B]

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlInitialBWP = 1

### 3.29.7.2 DL resource assignment using FDM

#### 3.29.7.2.0-1 DL resource assignment using FDM DL PTRS in SA mode (ID: 11179171)

**3.29.7.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11179187`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
[PR708042 ] In FR2 SA  Mode, when DL PT-RS is enabled (see enableDlPtrs in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2239\_replaced([11282365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpeauvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), for PDSCH scheduled with dedicated BWP, the following restrictions on PRB allocation for PDSCH shall be applied:

 In UE's transient phase:

o The maximum number of allocated PRBs for PDSCH shall be less than NRB0.

[End PR708042]

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-2 DL resource assignment using FDM PDSCH granted with a DCI Format 1_0 (ID: 11179193)

**3.29.7.2.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `12619913`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12619913:
DL resource assignment using FDM
When the PDSCH is granted with a DCI Format 1\_0 into a common search space, with a RA-RNTI or TC-RNTI or P-RNTI (RRC Paging) or a C-RNTI, the PDSCH RB allocation is such that :

 The first RB allowed for the PDSCH allocation, corresponds to the first RB of the CORESET associated to the common search space **used to send the DCI Format 1\_0. This RB is the reference (RB0) for the encoding Frequency Domain Resource Allocation.**

For the RA-RNTI and the TC-RNTI, the Type1-PDCCH is used.

For the SI-RNTI, the Type0-PDCCH is used for the SIB1 and the Type0A-PDCCH for the SI-Msg(s).

For the P-RNTI, the Type2-PDCCH is used.

For the C-RNTI, the Type3-PDCCH is used.

The mapping between the search space and the Control Resource Set is defined in the PsCell\_CellSetupReq according to the Bandwidth Part selected. The SI-RNTI and P-RNTI use the initial BWP. The RA-RNTI, TC-RNTI and C-RNTI are using the UE active BWP (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4049\_replaced([11168607](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktxNtPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4051\_replaced([11168618](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktxNt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4053\_replaced([11168627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktxNufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The UE active BWP identification is done during the Random Access procedure according to the type of the RA procedure type (Contention Based or Contention Free) and the UE RRC configuration state (Initial BWP for the non RRC configured UEs or when the initial cell active BWP is the PsUser\_UserSetupReq is set to 0 ; Dedicated BWP for the RRC configured UEs or when the initial cell active BWP in the PsUser\_UserSetupReq is not equal to 0).

 Number of PRBs shall be lower or equal to the of size of the CORESET(0).

For RA-RNTI, TC-RNTI or C-RNTI, on top of the previous constraint, the number of available RB could be restricted by the nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL received in the PsCell\_CellSetupReq[prbBlanking] and according to the first RB position of the CORESET associated to the search space **used to send the DCI Format 1\_0**

The largest size allowed is computed as follow:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in white text on a black background. The formula is:
nRbmax = min(coreseto,size, min(endCrbbwp,nRbchBw-nbrof Blanked Prb-1) - startCrb + 1)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13607.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

*endCrb*bwp:\*\*

Last Common Resource Block (CRB) of the UE active BWP, computed from the data within the cellBwp structure received in the PsCell\_CellSetupReq (cellBwpId, downlinkBwp[location,bandiwth]) and the cellBwpId associated to the current UE active BWP

The UE active BWP is the dedicated BWP outside any Random Access procedure. Otherwise, the UE active BWP is defined during the search space selection according to the RNTI used (requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4036\_replaced([11168496](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktwmofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

*nRb*chBw:\*\*

Number of RB available in the channel bandwidth.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Frequency Range 1: nRB |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| SCSCommon (KHz) | Bandwith (MHz) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| 5 | 10 | 15 | 20 | 25 | 30 | 40 | 50 | 60 | 70 | 80 | 90 | 100 |  |
| 15 | 25 | 52 | 79 | 106 | 133 | 160 | 216 | 270 | - | - | - | - | - |
| 30 | 11 | 24 | 38 | 51 | 65 | 78 | 106 | 133 | 162 | 189 | 217 | 245 | 273 |
| 60 | - | 11 | 18 | 24 | 31 | 38 | 51 | 65 | 79 | 93 | 107 | 121 | 135 |

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Frequency Range 2: nRB |  |  |  |  |
| SCSCommon (kHz) | Bandwith (MHz) |  |  |  |
| 50 | 100 | 200 | 400 |  |
| 60 | 66 | 132 | 264 | - |
| 120 | 32 | 66 | 132 | 264 |

*nbrOfBlankedPrb:*

If the actPRBBlanking in the PsCell\_CellSetupReq is set to true, the *nbrOfBlankedPrb is set to nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL. Otherwise, it is set to 0*

*coreset*0,size:\*\*

The CORESET(0) is size is computed by the 5G-L2-PS from data received in the PDCCH structure of the PsCell\_CellSetupReq

*startCrb*n:\*\*

The first Common Resource Block (CRB) index of the CORESET(n) used to send the DCI Format 1\_0. The start CRB of a coreset is computed by the 5G-L2-PS from data received in the PDCCH structure of the PsCell\_CellSetupReq

 Multi RNTI PDSCH allocation in common search space(s).

For all common search spaces associated to the CORESET(0), all PDSCH allocations must be within the CORESET(0) bandwidth. The scheduler must restrict the PDSCH allocation or discard a RNTI if the allocation will use a RB outside the CORESET(0) bandwidth.

For all common search spaces associated to the CORESET(>0), all PDSCH allocations must be within the CORESET(>0) bandwidth starting at the first RB of the CORESET and restricted to the CORESET(0) size. The scheduler must restrict the PDSCH allocation or discard a RNTI if the allocation will use a RB outside the PDSCH area allowed and restricted by the number of CORESET(0) RBs.

 Additional constraints.

For RA-RNTI,TC-RNTI or C-RNTI, apart from the above constraint, the number of RBs and start PRB is defined as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16521\_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For SI-RNTI, the number of RBs is defined during the Link Adaptation of the SIB1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3716\_replaced([11164922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kte50_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or the SI-Msg(s) (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3802\_replaced([11166155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktjyX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For P-RNTI, the number of RBs is defined during the Link Adaptation of the Paging (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3915\_replaced([11167805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For FDD DSS cell, in slot where ZP CSI RS resources is configured 5G-L2-PS shall avoid scheduling PRBs which are overlapping with configured ZP CSI RS resources for the following:.

 When [CB007372-AA] NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false and [End CB007372-AA] actRateMatchedCsiIm is set to 'true' and rateMatchedCsiImInMsg2Slot is set to 'true':

RA-RNTI  transmission.

 [CB007372-AA] When NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true

RA-RNTI  transmission. [End CB007372-AA]

 When Cat-M support is **not enabled (actLteNrDssLteCatM), C-RNTI transmission during the reconfiguration transient phase (SA only), i.e. between the reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq and PsUser\_UserReconfCompletedReq.**

 When Cat-M support is not enabled (actLteNrDssLteCatM) and PCell is a FDD DSS cell:

transmission in PCell with DCI 1\_0 scrambled with C-RNTI during transient phase for SCell Addition (5G\_UP\_ALG\_11907\_replaced([11120306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqPYnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and SCell Release of the last configured SCell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_11908\_replaced([11120596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqSb5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); it is applicable for SA and NSA configurations.

ZP CSI RS resources configuration is received during cell setup procedure and corresponds to CSI-IM configuration.

The first RB allowed and Number of PRBs for the above PDSCH allocations shall be aligned with ZP CSI RS/CSI-IM configuration.

[CB007372-AA] Note: For FDD DSS cell with asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true), UE without dedicated configuration shall not be scheduled in the CSI-IM slots (see [11174627: UEs selection for CS1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB007372-AA]

The DL FD scheduler allocates the frequency resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If slow pooling of instantaneous throughput is enabled i.e. tddInstTputPoolingAllowed is TRUE (refer 5G\_RAN\_OP\_3986\_replaced([11693605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D33F-P9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), for every PDSCH scheduled with DCI\_1\_0, gNB-DU shall add the TBS(tbSize) value of the PDSCH to rrmUsedCellThroughputPerSlot (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29086\_replaced([11215102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to keep track of the cell's throughput per slot. Before every PDSCH transmission (including a retransmission), the scheduler shall also ensure that the throughput limit is not exceeded as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case that scheduling the current UE increases the throughput beyond the limit, the scheduler shall take necessary actions as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-3 DL resource assignment using FDM PDSCH granted with a DCI 1-1 (ID: 11179250)

**3.29.7.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11179255`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
PDSCH granted with a DCI 1-1 are granted onto the active UE bandwidth part (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1823\_replaced([11110957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The UE bandwidth part is defined such that the full carrier bandwidth [CB008917-A,CB008917-B] (if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize disabled) or specific BWP size (if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize enabled) [End CB008917-A,CB008917-B]

is contained in it, i.e. [CB008917-A,CB008917-B] (if NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize disabled) [End CB008917-A,CB008917-B] its size is equal to the DL bandwidth received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq.

If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the DL bandwidth of the active dedicated DL bandwidth part can be equal to or smaller than the full carrier bandwidth.

The DL FD scheduler allocates the frequency resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-4 DL resource assignment using FDM FD Resource Grid and Book-keeping with several BWP per Cell for Resource Allocation Type0 (RAT0) (ID: 11179260)

**3.29.7.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11179286`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11179286:
DL resource assignment using FDM
If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

5G-L2-PS shall perform FD resource book-keeping and allocation in a manner that different RBG sizes per UE, that is the parameter rbgSizePdsch (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), can be configured in the same slot. Details are up to implementation and CP3.

Example: The RBG size of the largest BWP capable UE#2 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) has 16 PRBs and is double of the RBG size of the largest BWP non-capable UE#1 of 8 PRB. After allocating odd number off RBGs of 8 PRB to UE#1 and then allocating RBGs of 16 PRBs to UE#2, free PRBs are needed in between for correct allocation of the RBG of 16 PRBs.

Example with three RBG sizes:
