#### 3.29.7.2.0-5 DL resource assignment using FDM Amount of symbols available for PDSCH (ID: 12646489)

**3.29.7.2.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11179291`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Amount of symbols available for PDSCH in a slot for multiple dedicated BWPs in TDD FR1

---

**3.29.7.2.0-5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11179296`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
When NRCELL/actDynPdcchTdMode is set to perBWP in a TDD FR1 cell with n > 1 dedicated BWPs configured, the number of symbols used by the PDCCH might be different between configured BWPs.

When calculating resource distribution of the PRBs the maximal size of PDCCH across all configured BWPs shall be used.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12646491`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
**Amount of symbols available for PDSCH in a RIM partially mitigated slots**

---

**3.29.7.2.0-5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12646492`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
In the RIM partially mitigated slots, for which 4 or 8 latter PDSCH symbols are mitigated where RIM DL muting shall be applied (i.e.: [11161119: Determine RIM DL muting applicability](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktSFjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) conditions are fulfilled), L2PS shall additionally consider lower number of available symbols for PDSCH scheduling when calculating resource distribution. Meaning that L2PS shall assume SLIVs with maximum length which does not overlap with muted symbols and exists on the dedicated TDRA list.

Note: see details of restrictions in [12646511: Restrictions for RIM DL partial slot muting](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__aOpckLEEfCH-cG2YrVgkA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-6 DL resource assignment using FDM Resource Reservation/Allocation Sequence for Multiple UEs (ID: 11179300)

**3.29.7.2.0-6.0-9**  (ID: `13360369`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resources for SIB1 and SI messages are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3747\_replaced([11165240](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktgH9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) & 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3800\_replaced([11166015](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktjyW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Messages sent to 5G-L1-DL are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3767\_replaced([11165512](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kthWE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note : If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, for each SIB1/SI message that is allocated in the slot, the layer PRBs shall be monitored as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For a list of UEs to be scheduled using FDM gNB-DU shall do the following (excluding SI-RNTIs):

When rdEnableTdIcic (specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30165\_replaced([11194010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is enabled, the UEs that will be eligible for FD resource allocation will be determined based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29945\_replaced([11179332](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,

Note: The term FD resources means RBGs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, after allocating FD resources to each UE in each step below, gNB-DU shall determine the number of layer PRBs consumed (**rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and use it to keep track of the used, as well as remaining layer PRB resources (**rrmNumUsedPdschLayerPrbPerSlot, **rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  gNB-DU shall also ensure that the constraint related to layer PRBs mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26597\_replaced([11214606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is maintained. In case allocating the required resources to a UE breaks the constraint, the UE shall be allocated the maximum possible resources while making sure that the minimum required FD resources are still provided to the UE. If such an allocation is not possible, the UE shall be skipped. The next UE in the list shall be checked for allocation as long as there are still layer PRB resources available. Further UE allocation and scheduling in all steps shall be stopped if there are no more layer PRB resources available for scheduling in the current slot.**\*\*\*\*

1. Give UEs with SRB transmission the amount of FD resources as indicated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

2.   Allocate resources to above UEs in the list using the random allocation algorithm 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3. Give UEs with 5QI5/69 transmission the amount of FD resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21155\_replaced([11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

4.   Give UEs with dcGbrTraffic request class the amount of FD resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30511\_replaced([11179689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5. Give UEs with 5QI1/65/66 voipTraffic request class transmission the amount of FD resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21155\_replaced([11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

6. If there are FD resources **available give UEs with**5QI1/65/66 deprioritizedVoip request class the amount of resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21155\_replaced([11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

7. If there are FD resources available give UEs with**non-5QI1/65/66 GBR bearers the amount of resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8033\_replaced([11179713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

8. If there are FD resources **available give UEs with NBR configured Non-GBR bearers which have positive token bucket (NBR Category), the amount of resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21689\_replaced([11179765](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ERvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

9. Calculate available FD resources for the **Non-GBR  bearers of initial transmission UEs (to be used in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) = the FD resources in the BW (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or, If slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), usable FD resource (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4279\_replaced([11177002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) - FD resources allocated in previous steps.**

10. If there are FD resources available, distribute these resources to non-GBR which includes normal non-GBR and NBR excessive data after distributed in step 7 and deprioritized GBR bearers **of initial transmission UEs as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, and Deprioritized DC GBR DRBs as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30509\_replaced([11179756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ERPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

11. Allocate resources to all UEs in the list as distributed in steps 3-8 using the random allocation algorithm 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

If there is an eligible HARQ retransmission for power boost in the slot, FD resource availability check shall also include the condition that the **sum of FD *resources used for\*PRB allocation for PDSCH of all the UEs*** and PRBs needed for Power boosting the eligible HARQ retransmission is less *than rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced ([11177271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))****\****

Otherwise:

{ / *if RAT1* /

Perform the PRB reservation for the following users with bearers from each traffic class in the following order. gNB-DU shall select a PDSCH sub area and **perform the resource reservation as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23683\_replaced([11176637](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in the UE active BWP within the** PDSCH sub-area selected. After each reservation the remaining PRBs are updated as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23464\_replaced([11176110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The next reservation shall proceed only if there are available PRBs in the UE active the BWP in the PDSCH sub-area(s). For FR1 TDD when FD beamforming is enabled, in TRS  whenactTddCsirsOpt = false (note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) or SSB slots with TRS symbols, or in CSI-IM slots when CSI-IM is rate matched (that is if **actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator)** gNB-DU shall reserve the PRBs as explained above for UEs with DCI\_1\_1, in multiples of PRB granularity (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as resource blocks. Due to maintaining PRB granularity in the resource reservation for a user, while calculating the remaining PRBs, the gNB-DU shall mark those PRBs in a resource block unavailable for further use, even if the resource block is partially reserved by this user which means the user got more allocation than it needed. Similarly, gNB-DU shall reduce the allocation for a user, if there are not enough PRBs left to maintain the PRB granularity, in which case the buffer may not be completely drained out.\*\*\*\*

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, after allocating FD resources to each UE in each step below, gNB-DU shall determine the number of layer PRBs consumed (**rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and use it to keep track of the used, as well as remaining layer PRB resources (**rrmNumUsedPdschLayerPrbPerSlot, **rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  gNB-DU shall also ensure that the constraint related to layer PRBs mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26597\_replaced([11214606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is maintained. In case allocating the required resources to a UE breaks the constraint, the UE shall be allocated the maximum possible resources while making sure that the minimum required FD resources are still provided to the UE. If such an allocation is not possible, the UE shall be skipped. The next UE in the list shall be checked for allocation as long as there are still layer PRB resources available. Further UE allocation and scheduling in all steps shall be stopped if there are no more layer PRB resources available for scheduling in the current slot.**\*\*\*\*

Note: During TD scheduler, the PRBs for the UEs in the to-be-scheduled list are already reserved as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and remaining PRBs are updated. The following list of UEs are handled after above UEs are handled at this stage in the FD scheduler as follows:

1. UEs with DL MAC CE (with DCI\_1\_1), the amount of FD resources as indicated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23850\_replaced([11179675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. [CB014699-B] UEs with 5QI1/65/66 criticalVoipTraffic request class, the number of PRBs as indicated (refer to [11179697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). Note that 5QI5/69 is treated as critical srb1Traffic request class [End CB014699-B].
2. [CB014699-C] UEs with criticalGbrTraffic request class, the number of PRBs as indicated (refer to [11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB014699-C].

4. [CB014699-F] UEs with criticalDrbTraffic request class, the number of PRBs as indicated (refer to [13404773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rJo3eQyeEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB014699-F].

1. UEs with dcGbrTraffic request class the amount of FD resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30511\_replaced([11179689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

6. UEs with 5QI1/65/66 voipTraffic request class the number of PRBs as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21155\_replaced([11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note that 5QI5 request class is treated as SRB1 traffic request class.

7. UEs with**5QI1/65/66 deprioritizedVoip request class the number of PRBs as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21155\_replaced([11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

8. UEs with non-5QI1/65/66 GBR bearers the number of PRBs as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21155\_replaced([11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

9. UEs with NBR configured non-GBR bearer which have positive token bucket (NBR Category 1), the number of PRBs as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21155\_replaced([11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

10.   After reserving and assigning the sub-area to the UEs in the list above, if there are still remaining PRBs as per the EIRP constraint check described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4315 ([11177531: EIRP constraint check](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwFI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for any UE within the respective active BWP within the PDSCH sub-area(s), then for UEs with bearers classified as [CB014699-F] critical non-GBR traffic class and [End CB014699-F] non-GBR bearer traffic class listed below, are subjected to resource reservation and respective sub-area assignment through FD scheduler resource distribution. Before FD scheduler resource distribution phase, there is no minimum reservation made at this stage distribution (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- [CB014699-F] UEs with critical non-GBR excessive data leftover after step-4 in the list [CB014699-F].

-   UEs with normal non-GBR and NBR excessive data after distributed in step 10.

-   UEs with deprioritized GBR, [CB014699-C] deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-C] bearers, and Deprioritized DC GBR bearers.

- Allocate a PDCCH candidate for the DCI for all the above (start CCE is determined as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4433\_replaced([11170857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuAeTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: The FD scheduler shall check if there are available PDCCH CCEs before reserving PRBs.

* Allocate the PUCCH resource as indicated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4330\_replaced([11177663](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In order to determine the smallest number of FD resources (see [11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), gNB-DU shall set the number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data computed in the table in the artifact [11129150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) according  to the inputs (DL DMRS configuration type = type1 or type2, NRCELL.dlDataDmrsFdm.actDlDataDmrsFdm, rrmDlProposedMcs and number of layers).

For UEs with multiple bearers in the to-be-scheduled list (e.g VoIP with GBR, VoIP with non-GBR etc) the UE priority is based on the highest traffic class while getting added to the to-be-scheduled list.

When a UE having a multiple bearer configuration excluding SRBs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4537\_replaced([11185465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvTew_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for generic rules for bearer prioritization and multiplexing), e.g one or more non-GBR bearers(s) that does not require resource reservation, along with a priority bearer that requires reservation at this stage then, gNB-DU shall only book-keep the estimated maximum PRBs to drain out the buffer for these non-GBR bearer(s) (See buffer requirements in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for multiple non-GBR bearers) and not reserve, along with the priority bearer requiring reservation.

In both cases, described above, gNB-DU shall estimate the total PRBs required to satisfy the UE needs across all the bearers it has with non-zero buffered bytes. Further gNB-DU shall take this total number of required PRBs, as the criteria to choose the sub-area as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23683\_replaced([11176637](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: if rdSubareaReselectActForMultiBearerUes ([12563046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ral0gisFEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))is set to False, then, in the latter case, though the PRBs for the non-GBR bearer(s) are contended and reserved later in the FD scheduler resource distribution phase, but sub-area is chosen as determined above based on the book-keeping,

else Book-keeping above mentioned does not necessarily mean that the PDSCH sub-area is assigned/chosen at this stage before contending for FD resources for the non-GBR bearer(s) in FD scheduler resource distribution phase. The book-keeping just helps in providing the maximum FD resource estimation for UEs with multi-DRBs with combination of GBR and non-GBR bearer(s) before a suitable PDSCH sub-area can be chosen. In the FD scheduler resource distribution phase, for such UEs the sub-area assignment shall additionally consider the book-keep PRBs for the bearers other than the non-GBR bearer(s) the UE has, as the total FD resource reservation must take into account the FD resources for the GBR bearer(s) contiguously to the FD resource reserved for the non-GBR bearer(s) in order to facilitate the PDSCH sub-area assignment as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23683\_replaced([11176637](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note that in the event of not sufficent FD resource could be reserved as per the book-keeping, the FD scheduler must ensure reservation for the book-keeped PRBs for the GBR bearer(s) the UE has.

**Rationale: This estimation of the maximum PRBs needed to drain out the buffer is to hint/guess a optimal sub-area that could provide the UE the contiguous allocation in case there are multiple sub-areas that could satisfy the UE needs. However it is left to implementation to reselect the PDSCH sub area if assigned already before this step and recompute it within the FD resource distribution phase.**

Note: rdSubareaReselectActForMultiBearerUes ([12563046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ral0gisFEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is by default set to disabled to avoid extra CPU processing which would result in subarea reselection activity.

After the PRBs are reserved and UEs assigned to respective PDSCH sub-area, FD scheduler shall allocate the resources to the UEs using the random allocation algorithm 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If Link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for DL is deactivated (See [12472220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HoCyEA1UEfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

{ if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = TRUE and rdEnableMcsLookUptableForTrsAndCsiImSlotsRat1 ([13416147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_4qemQRPxEfG3gf_qmG8Vww?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) is set to 1, in the slots where the PDSCH have to be rate matched (that TRS slots or CSI-IM slots), gNB-DU shall apply a reduction factor, based on the ratio of the rate matching  in these slots, to the rrmDlProposedMcs used to evaluate smallest number of FD resources in the Resource Distribution for Initial Transmission UEs as Per Priority Class (see [11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) ).

Since the rate matching pattern for a PRB in a TRS and CSI-IM slots is known at the cell setup procedure , gNB-DU shall compute lookup tables providing a ate Matched MCS based on 2 PRB’s, the rate matching ratio in the TRS and the CSI-IM slots, the modulation scheme (256QAM, 64QAM or 64qamLowse) and the rank and save these tables before any scheduling in the slot in order to save CPU processing time.

Rationale:

With the current implementation, the number of needed FD resource is computed with a rrmDlProposedMcs which is derived from the previous slot (slot n-1) and not the current slot (slot n), In the slots with rate matching (TRS, CSI-IM), a new rrmDlProposedMcs is re-computed later, is lower due to the rate matching, then with a RAT1 allocation, the new TBS recomputed cannot drain out the data with the FD resources initially estimated and some leftover bytes have to be re scheduled in the next slots.}

Else

If UE has rate matched REs in allocated PDSCH resources in TRS slot, the gNB-DU shall scale the PRB allocation as defined in [13413656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_7wuG8xJbEfG3gf_qmG8Vww?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

End If

If there is an eligible HARQ retransmission for power boost in the slot, FD resource availability check shall also include the condition that the sum of FD resources used for PRB allocation for PDSCH of all the UEs and PRBs needed for Power boosting the eligible HARQ retransmission is less than rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced(11177271) and in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26756\_replaced(11176544)(for initial transmission with RAT1) and in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27184\_replaced(11181601)(for initial transmission with RAT0) if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = TRUE)

Note: FD resource distribution algorithm can be either of the following:

* DL Exhaustive Scheduling (ES) if NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to DL or DLAndUL
* WRR if NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set otherwise

} / *otherwise if RAT1* /

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-7 DL resource assignment using FDM PRB adjustment for rate matching slots (ID: 13413656)

**3.29.7.2.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `13413657`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
In TRS slots, if the PDSCH allocation includes nonGBR traffic and Rate Matched (RM) REs, gNB-DU shall scale the PRB allocation to compensate for the RM REs as following.

Input:

PRB\_needed: PRB allocation for the UE before any considering for rate matched REs.

numREs\_needed: Number of REs available for PDSCH per PRB before rate matching

numREs\_rm: Number of REs available for PDSCH per PRB after rate matching.

Procedure:

PRB\_rm = ceil(PRB\_needed \* (numREs\_needed / numREs\_rm))

[PR879874-26R2]

(If link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for DL is enabled (see [12472220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HoCyEA1UEfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))) AND (UE buffer is expected to be drained in this slot) AND (cqiDerivedMcs is equal to maximum MCS of the MCS index table used) AND (there are remaining PRBs for the allocation after the above scale-up of PRBs), then the gNB-DU shall further scale the PRB\_rm as following to adjust for the code rate check.

PRB\_rm =  min(totalRemainingPrbs, roundUp((1+rdDlPrbScaleUpForMaxMcsTrsSlot)\*PRB\_rm))

[End PR879874-26R2]

where,

* totalRemainingPrbs are the remaining PRBs available for allocation in the slot.

- rdDlPrbScaleUpForMaxMcsTrsSlot is defined in [13458174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gzyxgS5qEfGxldjOZQLezQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

Output:

PRB\_rm: PRB allocation for the UE considering the rate matched Res.

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-8 DL resource assignment using FDM Time Domain Intercell Interference Coordination (TD-ICIC) (ID: 11179332)

**3.29.7.2.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11179337`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
TD-ICIC among the cells shall be performed when rdEnableTdIcic [5G\_UP\_ALG\_30165\_replaced([11194010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))] is enabled. When rdEnableTdIcic is enabled, each slot in a frame is represented by a bit in a bitmap. This bitmap is denoted by the parameter rdFirstDlSlot (specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30265\_replaced([11194020](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Depending on the status of the bit in the bitmap, the gNB-DU applies the following resource allocation restrictions for 'high' and 'normal' performing UEs (the classification of UEs into high and normal performing groups is based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8432\_replaced([11181306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the corresponding slot:

* For a slot, if its corresponding bit in rdFirstDlSlot is set to 1 and in that slot if the sum of FD resource requirements of the 'normal' UEs is greater than or equal to rdResourceUtilizationThresholdFirstSlot (specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30168\_replaced([11194030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the available channel bandwidth, then 'high-performing' UEs, even if selected by the TD scheduler, shall not be allocated any FD resources. This restriction is only applicable for 'high-performing' UEs having an initial transmission.
* For a slot, if its corresponding bit in rdFirstDlSlot is set to 0 and in that slot if the sum of FD resource requirements of the 'high-performing UEs' is greater than or equal to rdResourceUtilizationThresholdOtherSlots (specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30171\_replaced([11194040](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the available channel bandwidth, then 'normal' UEs, even if selected by the TD scheduler, shall not be allocated any FD resources. This is applicable for both initial and retransmission of normal performing UEs.

Note: It is recommended that when rdEnableTdIcic is enabled, then only the bits corresponding to the first DL slot after an UL slot can be set to 1 in rdFirstDlSlot.

[PR794578]

Exceptions to TD-ICIC

gNB-DU shall not apply the resource allocation restrictions imposed by TD-ICIC for the following UEs

* UEs with pending MSG2 transmission
* UEs in transient state

[End PR794578]

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-9 DL resource assignment using FDM Resource distribution to Msg2/Msg4/SRB0/... in initial bandwidth part (indeed CORESET(0) bandwidth) (ID: 11179343)

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11179347`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
FD resource calculation for UEs with RACH Msg2

---

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12742767`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
If more than one grant is allowed in the CORESET(0) bandwidth using a common search spaces (/MRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP set to true), and for each RA-RNTI, the number of RBs is computed according to:

·    The total number of UEs grouped based on the same (RA-RNTI, beam) (see [11177911: Prioritizing UEs for which RACH Msg2 with RAPID of same RA-RNTI in a slot](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

·    The RAR payload size is calculated as per the requirement [11152611: Assembling MAC PDU for RA Response (RA-Msg2)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kshQhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

·    The MCS and scaling factor are defined in the requirement [11152757: MCS and scaling factor for RA Msg2](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksh3lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

·    [05383414-26R1] ; The number of symbols PDSCH available in the target slot and defined in the common TDRA list in order to not overlap any CSI-IM, to include the DM-RS type A position and starting not before the symbol defined by the PDCCH slot pattern (see [13404901: RA-Msg2 symbol allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_MM2K8gylEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). [End 05383414-26R1].

·    [04795863-26R1] ; When the minimum number of RBs for the RA-Msg2 is configured (/NRBTS/NRCELL/nRbTargetForRaMsg2DL is configured to a non-zero value, the U-Plane must not allocate less RBs that the value specified to compute the TBS. If the value configured is greater than the CORESET(0) size, the U-Plane restricts the allocation to the number of RBs defined by the CORESET(0) size. [End 04795863-26R1].

Otherwise, only one transmission using a grant in the common search space is allowed (/MRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP set to false), and the U-Plane applies the following constraints

·    Only one RAR is scheduled per RA-RNTI.

·    The RAR payload size is 64 bits.

·    The MCS0 and scaling factor set to 1.0 are used.

·    In the SS/PBCH slots, the largest number of RBs available outside the SS/PBCH bandwidth is used if the C-Plane allocates enough RB for PDSCH according to the SS/PBCH block power configured by the operator. In all other slots the number of RB defined by the CORESET(0) size is used.

·    [05383414-26R1] ; The number of symbols PDSCH available in the target slot and defined in the common TDRA list in order to not overlap any CSI-IM, to include the DM-RS type A position and starting not before the symbol defined by the PDCCH slot pattern (see [13404901: RA-Msg2 symbol allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_MM2K8gylEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). [End 05383414-26R1].

If slow pooling of instantaneous throughput is enabled i.e. tddInstTputPoolingAllowed is TRUE (refer 5G\_RAN\_OP\_3986\_replaced([11693605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D33F-P9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), for every Msg2 transmission, gNB-DU shall add the TBS(tbSize) value of the Msg2 to rmUsedCellThroughputPerSlot (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29086\_replaced([11215102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to keep track of the cell's throughput per slot. Before every PDSCH transmission (including a retransmission), the scheduler shall also ensure that the throughput limit is not exceeded as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case that scheduling the current UE increases the throughput beyond the limit, the scheduler shall take necessary actions as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note : If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, for each Msg2 transmission that is allocated in the slot, the layer PRBs shall be monitored as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11179366`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
**FD resource calculation for Msg4/SRB0 message in Initial BWP (indeed CORESET(0) bandwidth) for regular RRC Setup**

---

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `11179583`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11179583:
DL resource assignment using FDM
**If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is False**
{
Only one UE can be scheduled in the initial BWP, and entire RBs of the CORESET(0) are allocated to schedule RA Msg4/SRB0 when the UE dedicated configuration is activated (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4628\_replaced([11192229](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvjWYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). When the UE dedicated configuration is not activated, including the UE in the initial BWP without dedicated configuration, the Link Adaptation of the DL-CCCH with or without the CRI MAC CE is specified in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18114\_replaced([11153428](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
}
**Else ( /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is TRUE**
{
L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources required to empty the SRBs' buffered data for a UE.
 
Allocate resources to MSG4/SRB0 considering the MCS corresponding to **[Before PR808039]NRBTS/NRSYSINFO\_PROFILE/SystemInformationTargetRate as the initial MCS(SIMCS)**[PR808039]NRBTS/NRCELL/dllaIniMcs as the initial MCS(dllaIniMcs)**[End PR808039], increase the MCS(*R\*MCS) stepwise to fulfill the below inequality.  For multiplexing Msg4 in the SI/Paging slot, MCS shall be restricted to rdMaxMcsForMsg4InSiPagingSlot(defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16823\_replaced([11193566](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtuevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to avoid the BLER.******\****[Before PR808039]**[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in a curly brace, indicating a set of conditions. The text is positioned on a white background with a black border on the right side.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13632.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10]: MCS value corresponding to NRBTS/NRSYSINFO\_PROFILE/SystemInformationTargetRate.**[PR808039]**[image-11][image\_desc]The image contains the text "dllalniMc s" in a blurry, light blue font against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13632.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11]: NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/dllaIniMcs used as Initial MCS for DL transmission**[End PR808039][PR808039] For scheduling RA Msg4/SRB0 in initial BWP when the UE dedicated configuration is activated, L2-PS shall consider the entire RBs of the CORESET(0). **[End PR808039]****Use the 38.214 table[Table 5.1.3.1-1: MCS index table 1 for PDSCH] to map MCS and target coding rate.
}**[CB013199-B]**If slow pooling of instantaneous throughput is enabled i.e. tddInstTputPoolingAllowed is TRUE (refer 5G\_RAN\_OP\_3986\_replaced([11693605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D33F-P9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), for every Msg4 transmission, gNB-DU shall add the TBS(tbSize) value of the Msg4 to rrmUsedCellThroughputPerSlot (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29086\_replaced([11215102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to keep track of the cell's throughput per slot. Before every PDSCH transmission (including a retransmission), the scheduler shall also ensure that the throughput limit is not exceeded as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case that scheduling the current UE increases the throughput beyond the limit, the scheduler shall take necessary actions as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**[End CB013199-B]\*\*
 
Note : If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, for each Msg4/SRB0 transmission that is allocated in the slot, the layer PRBs shall be monitored as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-3**  (ID: `12707562`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
FD resource calculation for Msg4/SRB0 message in initial BWP (coreset(0) BWP) for Smallest RRC Setup

---

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12708349`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12708349:
DL resource assignment using FDM
When NRBTS/NRCELL/actSmallestRRCSetup is True

L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources required to empty the SRBs' buffered data for a UE.

1. Allocate resources to MSG4/SRB0, considering the MCS corresponding to NRBTS/NRCELL/dllaIniMcs as the initial MCS (dllaIniMcs).
2. If [Before 04840817-26R1] NRBTS/NRCELL.tmpParam5[04840817-26R1] NRBTS/NRCELL.nRbTargetForRaMsg4DL [End 04840817-26R1]is set to a value other than 0, then the criteria to consider this value as the number of RBs to recompute Msg4 TBS is only when the following conditions are met.
3. If [Before 04840817-26R1] NRBTS/NRCELL.tmpParam5[04840817-26R1]  NRBTS/NRCELL.nRbTargetForRaMsg4DL [End 04840817-26R1] is less than or equal to [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "remainingRb\_{coreset}(0)". The text is in a light gray color and is slightly blurred. The text is written in a sans-serif font. The subscript "coreset" is smaller than the main text and is italicized. The number "0" is enclosed in parentheses and is also italicized.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13632.003.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
4. [Before 04840817-26R1] NRBTS/NRCELL.tmpParam5[04840817-26R1] NRBTS/NRCELL.nRbTargetForRaMsg4DL [End 04840817-26R1] is greater than RB resources calculated in step 1.
5. If the conditions are not met as in Step 2, then RB resources calculated in Step 1 shall be considered.

Rationale: To ensure there are enough DMRS resource elements for Msg4, along with the chosen coding rate (MCS) for UE to do better channel estimation when a small RRC setup is considered.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "remainingRb\_{coreset}(0)". The text is in a light gray color and is slightly blurred. The text is written in a sans-serif font. The subscript "coreset" is smaller than the main text and is italicized. The number "0" is enclosed in parentheses and is also italicized.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13632.003.png[/image\_path][/image-1]: Available RBs in coreset(0) for the allocation

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Msg4Srb0payloadsize". The text is in a light gray color against a white background. The font appears to be a sans-serif font. The text is slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13632.004.png[/image\_path][/image-2]: Msg4/SRB0 payload size in bits.

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "dllalniMc s" in a blurry, light blue font against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13632.012.png[/image\_path][/image-3]: NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/dllaIniMcs used as Initial MCS for DL transmission

Use 38.214 table[Table 5.1.3.1-1: MCS index table 1 for PDSCH] to map MCS and target coding rate.

}

If slow pooling of instantaneous throughput is enabled i.e. tddInstTputPoolingAllowed is TRUE (refer 5G\_RAN\_OP\_3986\_replaced([11693605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D33F-P9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), for every Msg4 transmission, gNB-DU shall add the TBS(tbSize) value of the Msg4 to rrmUsedCellThroughputPerSlot (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29086\_replaced([11215102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to keep track of the cell's throughput per slot. Before every PDSCH transmission (including a retransmission), the scheduler shall also ensure that the throughput limit is not exceeded as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case that scheduling the current UE increases the throughput beyond the limit, the scheduler shall take necessary actions as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note : If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, for each Msg4/SRB0 transmission that is allocated in the slot, the layer PRBs shall be monitored as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-4**  (ID: `12707822`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
FD resource calculation for SRB1 messages sent before dedicated configuration for UE is configured when small RRC setup is enabled

---

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12708377`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12708377:
DL resource assignment using FDM
When NRBTS/NRCELL/actSmallestRRCSetup is True

L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources required to empty the SRBs' buffered data for a UE for the SRB1 till the dedicated configuration is available (RRC Reconfiguration after the small RRC Setup message)

1. Allocate resources to SRB1 segments, considering the MCS corresponding to NRBTS/NRCELL/dllaIniMcs as the initial MCS (dllaIniMcs).
2. If [Before 04840817] NRBTS/NRCELL.tmpParam5 [04840817] NRBTS/NRCELL.nRbTargetForRaMsg4DL [End 04840817] is set to a value other than 0, then the criteria to consider this value as the number of RBs used to recompute SRB1 buffer to be emptied TBS are only when the following conditions are met.
3. if [Before 04840817] NRBTS/NRCELL.tmpParam5 [04840817] NRBTS/NRCELL.nRbTargetForRaMsg4DL [End 04840817] is less than or equal to [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "remainingRb\_{coreset}(0)". The text is in a light gray color and is slightly blurred. The text is written in a sans-serif font. The subscript "coreset" is smaller than the main text and is italicized. The number "0" is enclosed in parentheses and is also italicized.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13632.003.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
4. [Before 04840817] NRBTS/NRCELL.tmpParam5 [04840817] NRBTS/NRCELL.nRbTargetForRaMsg4DL [End 04840817] is greater than RB resources calculated in step 1.
5. If the conditions are not met as in Step 2, then RB resources calculated in Step 1 shall be considered.

Rationale: To ensure there are enough DMRS resource elements for all the segments of RRC Reconfiguration, along with the chosen coding rate (MCS) for UE to do better channel estimation when a small RRC setup is considered.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "remainingRb\_{coreset}(0)". The text is in a light gray color and is slightly blurred. The text is written in a sans-serif font. The subscript "coreset" is smaller than the main text and is italicized. The number "0" is enclosed in parentheses and is also italicized.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13632.003.png[/image\_path][/image-1]: Available RBs in coreset(0) for the allocation

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "dllalniMc s" in a blurry, light blue font against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13632.012.png[/image\_path][/image-2]: NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/dllaIniMcs used as Initial MCS for DL transmission

Use 38.214 table[Table 5.1.3.1-1: MCS index table 1 for PDSCH] to map MCS and target coding rate.

}
