**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11179600`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
This requirement describes the algorithm followed to allocate RA Msg2/Msg4 and Transient UE in CORESET(0). It is applicable to slots with or without SI/Paging.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11179605`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Slots with SI/Paging

---

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-5.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11179622`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is TRUE

gNB-DU shall allocate Msg2/Msg4/SRB1(Transient UE) contiguous to SIB/Paging allocation and shall ensure there is no gap in the resource allocation,  when the associated Common Search Space (CSS) is linked to the CORESET(0). When the CORESET(0) is not linked to the CSS, the PDSCH allocation is within the area defined by the first RB of the CORESET used in CSS and the number of RB defined by the CORESET(0).

[CB008927-A] If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, for the allocations of the rach msg2, rach msg4 and DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode around the SIB/Paging allocation in the CORESET #0 [CB008927-K] and in the CORESET#X (with X>0)[End CB008927-K]  within the PDSCH sub areas, gNB-DU shall follow the rules described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and shall start from the to-be-scheduled list update step in the TD scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End CB008927-A]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11179627`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Slots without SI/Paging

---

**3.29.7.2.0-9.0-5.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11179642`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is TRUE

gNB-DU shall allocate Msg2/Msg4/SRB1(Transient UE):

In case of bottom aligned BWPs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1809\_replaced([11110718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpHXNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

* from first available RBs in CORESET(0), and allocate contiguously in high direction when the the associated Common Search Space (CSS) is linked to the CORESET(0) and ensure there is no gap in the resource allocation.
* from first available RBs in CORESET(>0), and allocate contiguously in high direction when the the associated Common Search Space (CSS) is linked to the CORESET(>0) and ensure there is no gap in the resource allocation in the area allowed starting from teh first RB of the CORESET and restricted by the CORESET(0) size.

[CB008927-A] If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, for the allocations of the rach msg2, rach msg4 and DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode without SIB/Paging scheduling in the CORESET #0 [CB008927-K] and in the CORESET#X (with X>0)[End CB008927-K] within the PDSCH sub areas, gNB-DU shall follow the rules described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and shall start from the to-be-scheduled list update step in the TD scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End CB008927-A]

Note: [CB008927-E] If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, [End CB008927-E] No start PRB randomization needed for Msg2/Msg4/SRB1(Transient UE).

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-10 DL resource assignment using FDM Resource Distribution for Initial Transmission UEs as Per Priority Class (ID: 11179648)

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `13404773`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
**Resource distribution to critical non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs**

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13404774`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
The gNB-DU scheduler shall allocate prioritized resources to critical non-GBR traffic with upper boundary limited by NRCELL.prioritiPrbForCriticalNonGbrDl, which is configured as a maximum percentage of the total available resources in the cell or BWP.

Resources are distributed fairly among the competing critical non-GBR UEs. Each eligible UE is allocated as:

min (PRB Demand, PRBWeightedPrioritized)

where,

PRB Demand = PRB required to completely drain the UEs critical non-GBR data buffer.

PRBWeightedPrioritized = This represents the UEs fair share of the prioritized resource pool. It is calculated as:

round ((Wu / Σ Wj) \* min (PRB remaining, PRBBWP Limit))

where,

PRBBWP Limit: [Before CB014699-E-UPD002]This is the total number of PRBs available in the BWP multiplied by the prioritizedPrbForCriticalNonGbrDl percentage. [CB014699-E-UPD002] It is determined by multiplying the configured priority setting (NRCELL.prioritiPrbForCriticalNonGbrDl) by the size of the current Bandwidth Part and then subtracting any prioritized resources that have already been assigned to critical non-GBR traffic within that BW. [End CB014699-E-UPD002]

PRB remaining: This is the PRBs available for allocation in the UEs active BWP and all nested smaller BWP among all PDSCH subareas.

Wu/ΣWj: [CB014699-E-UPD002] The weight ratio used to calculate the proportional share of remaining resources for each critical non-GBR UE [End CB014699-E-UPD002].

* Wu is the scheduling weight of the specific UEs critical non-GBR bearers
* ΣWj is the sum of the weights of all [CB014699-E-UPD002] remaining [End CB014699-E-UPD002] critical non-GBR bearers competing for resources in that BWP.
* Please refer to [11179826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) to determine the scheduling weight for critical non-GBR bearers

Note:

[Before CB014699-E-UPD002]

* There is tradeoff to complexity for the weight ratio-based allocation, now the fair shared PRB can be larger than UE demand, but the delta part cannot be reallocated. Left to CP3 decision if further enhance to always exclude the allocated critical non-GBR resource from the sum () operation (i.e. ΣWj, PRBBWP Limit) to fully utilize the remaining resource from the configured resource pool.

[CB014699-E-UPD002]

* PRBBWP Limit a dynamic value that is updated iteratively within a single scheduling process. After the scheduler allocates a certain number of PRBs to the first UE, that number is recorded and when calculating the PRBBWP Limit for the second UE, the number of PRBs assigned to the first UE is subtracted from the initial prioritized pool.

[End CB014699-E-UPD002]

- Following the initial prioritized allocation, any remaining data for critical non-GBR traffic competes for leftover resources with non-critical non-GBR traffic (refer to [11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-2**  (ID: `11179653`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to SRBs of initial transmission UEs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `12553937`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to empty the SRBs' buffered data a UE has.

If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is set to FALSE

Note: SRB0/msg4 are considered as initial BWP transmission, thus the calculation indicated in previous line is not needed in their case.

This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4622\_replaced([11192072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the proposed MCS *rrmDlProposedMcs (calculated while assuming full BW allocation) to be greater than* (sum (max { bufferedBytes, rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes } for all SRBs with data*) + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes \* number of SRBs with data) \*\* 8*\*\*\*

If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is True **And for** UE with SRB1 in transient phase\*\*\*\*

Use **proposedMcsBased2PRB in the above algorithm instead of rrmDlProposedMcs.**

[PR826709] When actDlForceRankDowng is set to dlVoNRSrbVoNRCtrl/dlSrbOnly/dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrNonGbrBr, if proposedMcsBased2PRB is higher than maxMcsForDlSrb, proposedMcsBased2PRB shall be set to maxMcsForDlSrb. [End PR826709];

*proposedMcsBased2PRB : proposed MCS shall be calculated using 2 PRBs in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22781\_replaced([11125340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).* *Rationale: For the cases of traffic which are preferred not be segmented to reduce latency, proposed MCS shall be computed based on the assumption of 2 PRB allocation (instead of wideband allocation). In general, we would use the number of PRBs which has the highest SINR requirement. The 2 PRB allocation seemed to have the highest SINR requirement for any given MCS. So then the determined number of PRBs will always be sufficient to drain the data and hence avoid segmentation*\*\*

*(*bufferedBytes in *5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),* rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes **in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4543\_replaced([11185517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvTez_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), *rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes iin 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28749\_replaced([11187059](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvYXQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))***\*\*\*\*

If the UE works in high reliability LA mode(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15603\_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or UE is configured with lowSE MCS table for PDSCH, and the buffer contains RLC status PDU, the number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4622\_replaced([11192072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the proposed MCS *rrmDlProposedMcs (calculated while assuming full BW allocation) to be greater than* (sum (max { bufferedBytes, RLC status PDU size } for all SRBs with data*) +*rdRrmRLCStatusPDUProtectionSize\_bytes + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes \* number of SRBs with data) \*\* 8**\***

*Note, if proposedMCS is 0, rdRrmRLCStatusPDUProtectionSize\_bytes (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23155\_replaced([11193667](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8kPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*) shall be regarded as 0.\*\*

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-3**  (ID: `11179675`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to DL MAC CE of initial transmission UEs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11179683`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB-DU shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to send the DL MAC CE that the UE has.

[CB008927-A]If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

This is determined by as follows:

max{PRB\_max, [Before PR772594] 4 [PR772594] rdMinPrbRat1Alloc [End PR772594]};

Else,

PRB\_max ;

where,[End CB008927-A]

PRB\_max : the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TB for the DL MAC CE , with the proposed MCS rrmDlProposedMcs. gNB-DU shall consider the 5G-MAC subheader size, given in 5G\_L2\_1970\_replaced([11215653](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-5mSPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and the MAC CE payload size, given in 5G\_L2\_1971\_replaced([11215683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-5mTfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to determine the total bytes to be scheduled.

[PR772594] For rdMinPrbRat1Alloc , see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29029\_replaced([11193977](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvxY2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End PR772594]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-4**  (ID: `11179689`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to DC GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12689352`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB-DU shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to empty the DC-GBR buffered data for a UE. This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS with the proposed MCS rrmDlProposedMcs *to be greater than dcGbrDesiredBytesi,j \* 8.*

where

dcGbrDesiredBytesi,j = bufferedBytesi, j + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes

with

bufferedBytesi,j                                  bytes in the buffer associated with the DC GBR bearer.  See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes              MAC plus RLC header size as defined below

i                                                             index of UE with DC GBR bearer

j                                                             index of DC GBR bearer

NOTE: gNB-DU shall allocate an extra rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes for MAC and RLC headers which might be added to the DL buffer in 5G-L2-LO due to RLC segmentations in the previous slot (if scs = 15 or 30). This compensates for an inaccuracy of dcGbrBufferedBytesi,j  which can be up to rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes smaller than the buffer level in 5G-L2-LO since 5G-L2-PS receives LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd with 1 slot (if scs = 15 or 30) delay.

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes =7 (if scs = 15 or 30) for DC GBR bearer which are configured with RLC UM mode.  For scs = 15 or 30 it is the sum of the maximum MAC subheader size (3 bytes, see 38.321 Section 6.1.2) and the maximum RLC header size for RLC UM (4 bytes, see 38.322 Section 6.2.2.3).

[CB014474-C] If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True and if the bearer is configured as High Reliable (i.e the DRB is linked with NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN), then gNB-DU shall allocate the amount of FD resources as described below:

max(PRB(dcGbrDesiredBytesi,j), rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb)

where:

PRB(dcGbrDesiredBytesi,j) gives the number of PRBs required to construct a TBS with the proposed MCS rrmDlProposedMcs *to be greater than dcGbrDesiredBytesi,j \* 8.*

rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb gives the minimum number of FD resources for a HR DRB as described in [11193988](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[End CB014474-C]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-5**  (ID: `11179697`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to 5QI1 and 5QI5 bearer of initial transmission UEs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11179708`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to empty the [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 or 5QI5 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 or 5QI5/69 [End CB008118-A] buffered data a UE has.

This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4622\_replaced([11192072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the proposed MCS rrmDlProposedMcs to be greater than max{ bufferedBytes, rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes } for [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 or 5QI5 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 or 5QI5/69 [End CB008118-A] bearer + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes) \* 8

Where:

bufferedBytes is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes, rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes  is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4545\_replaced([11185562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUF1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note - Each slot’s PDSCH symbol number is configured at cell setup phase but that number can be changed dynamically by functions such as dynamic PDCCH.  To calculate the amount of FD resources required by [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 or 5QI5 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 or 5QI5/69 [End CB008118-A], DL scheduler must use the slot’s current PDSCH symbol number to make sure that [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 or 5QI5 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 or 5QI5/69 [End CB008118-A]  buffered data can be emptied.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-6**  (ID: `11179713`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to non-5QI-1 GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-6.0-4**  (ID: `12689358`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to meet the desired allocation for **non-5QI1/65/66 GBR bearers that have pending data.  This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS with the proposed MCS rrmDlProposedMcs *to be greater than gbrDesiredBytesi,j \* 8.***

where

gbrDesiredBytesi,j = bufferedBytesi, j + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes

with

bufferedBytesi,j                                             bytes in the buffer associated with the GBR bearer.  See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes              MAC plus RLC header size as defined below

i                                                                 index of UE with GBR bearer

j                                                                 index of GBR bearer

NOTE: 5G-L2-PS shall allocate an extra rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes for MAC and RLC headers which might be added to the DL buffer in 5G-L2-LO due to RLC segmentations in the previous slot (if scs = 15 or 30). This compensates for an inaccuracy of bufferedBytesi,j  which can be up to rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes smaller than the buffer level in 5G-L2-LO since 5G-L2-PS receives LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd with 1 slot (if scs = 15 or 30) delay.

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes = 7 (if scs = 15 or 30) for 5QI-2, 5QI-67 and 5QI-3 GBR bearer which are configured with RLC UM mode.  For scs = 15 or 30 it is the sum of the maximum MAC subheader size (3 bytes, see 38.321 Section 6.1.2) and the maximum RLC header size for RLC UM (4 bytes, see 38.322 Section 6.2.2.3).

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes = 8 (if scs = 15 or 30) for other**non-5QI1/65/66**GBR bearer which can be configured with RLC UM or AM mode.  For scs = 15 or 30 it is the sum of the maximum MAC subheader size (3 bytes, see 38.321 Section 6.1.2) and the maximum RLC header size for RLC AM (5 bytes, see 38.322 Section 6.2.2.4).\*\*\*\*

The number of PRB allocated to UE with DL non-5QI1/65/66 GBR bearer(s) is calculated by adding the number of PRBs allocated to all DL non-5QI1/65/66 GBR bearer(s) driving scheduling for the UE.

[CB014474-C] If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True and if the bearer is configured as High Reliable (i.e the DRB is linked with NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN), then gNB-DU shall allocate the amount of FD resources as described below:

max(PRB(gbrDesiredBytesi,j), rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb)

where:

PRB(dcGbrDesiredBytesi,j) gives the number of PRBs required to construct a TBS with the proposed MCS rrmDlProposedMcs *to be greater than gbrDesiredBytes*i,j \* 8.

rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb gives the minimum number of FD resources for a HR DRB as described in [11193988](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[End CB014474-C]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-7**  (ID: `11179736`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to Deprioritized GBR bearers of initial transmission UE

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-7.0-5**  (ID: `13378971`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Deprioritized GBR [CB014699-C] and/or deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-C] bearers (refer to [11175459](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuipzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and Deprioritized MC-GBR bearer (deprioritized MC-GBR bearer(5QI67) together with deprioritized GBR bearers in same scheduler request class deprioritizedGbr)**shall be treated as non-GBR bearers for FD resource distribution.  For the WRR algorithm that allocates RBGs if** /NRBTS/NRCELL/actRat1Alloc is set to False, else PRBs if /NRBTS/NRCELL/actRat1Alloc is set to TRUE **to UEs (outer loop WRR), the UE’s scheduling weight should be increased by rdDepriorGbrSchedulWeight(refer to[11193445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for each deprioritized GBR bearer that it contains.  For the downlink resource distribution requirements including the weighted round-robin requirements, the term non-GBR shall be interpreted as “non-GBR or deprioritized GBR/[CB014699-C] critical deprioritized GBR [End CB014699-C]”.**\*\*\*\*

If DL ES is enabled, the PRB resource allocation shall refer to [12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ).

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-8**  (ID: `11179756`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to Deprioritized DC-GBR bearers of initial transmission UE

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11179760`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Deprioritized DC-GBR bearers (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30371\_replaced([11175593](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj39PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be treated as non-GBR bearers for FD resource distribution.  For the WRR algorithm that allocates RBGs **if** /NRBTS/NRCELL/actRat1Alloc is set to False, else PRBs if /NRBTS/NRCELL/actRat1Alloc is set to TRUE to UEs (outer loop WRR**), the UE’s scheduling weight should be equal to rdDepriorGbrSchedulWeight=1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10990\_replaced([11193445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for each deprioritized DC GBR bearer that it contains.  For the downlink resource distribution requirements including  the weighted round-robin requirements, the term non-GBR shall be interpreted as “non-GBR or deprioritized GBR or deprioritized DC-GBR”.**

[CB014861-A]If DL ES is enabled, the PRB resource allocation shall refer to [12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ).[End CB014861-A]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-9**  (ID: `11179765`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution NBR configured non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `12539378`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to meet the desired allocation for NBR Category (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) bearers that have pending data. This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS with the proposed MCS rrmDlProposedMcs *to be greater than nbrDesiredBytes(i,j)*8.\*

*Where:*

nbrDesiredBytes(i,j) = min{bufferedBytes(i,j), bytesToReachNbrRate(i,j)} + rmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes

bufferedBytes(i,j)                             bytes in the buffer associated with NBR configured nonGBR bearer j of UE i.  See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

bytesToReachNbrRate(i,j) = max(rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j) , x*slotDuration\_msec*rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j))/8

bytesToReachNbrRate(i,j)  Tx bytes required for the NBR bearer j of UE i to reach its target NBR rate

i                                                      index of UE with NBR configured non-GBR bearer

j                                                      index of NBR configured non-GBR bearer

rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j)  and rrmNbrPbr\_kbps(i,j) *are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22083\_replaced([11175539](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj34fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and x is defined by an R&D parameter rdNbrOvershootFactor (5G\_UP\_ALG\_22424\_replaced([11193605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvuVg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*

The number of PRB allocated to UE with DL NBR bearers is calculated by adding the number of PRBs allocated to all DL NBR bearers driving scheduling for the UE.

The overall number of PRBs allocated to NBR bearers for the purpose of meeting DL NBR bitrate for a UE is restricted by the product of O&M parameter *NRBTS/NRCELL/maxPrbsPerNbrUe and number of PRBs usable for PDSCH for any bearer. If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), This limit* represents the maximum percentage of PDSCH resources within a given dedicated BWP that can be allocated for prioritized NBR bearer(s) of a given UE. In that case, this limit shall be computed corresponding to the number of PRBs usable for PDSCH for any bearer within the UE's active dedicated BWP.\*\*

~~For a group of UEs having the same beam, the sum of allocated FD resources should not exceed~~ ~~rrmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp~~~~,~~~~the remaining number of FD resources that can be allocated to each scheduled beam (based on EIRP, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4349\_replaced([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and all those UEs should be excluded from any further FD resource giveaways if FD scheduler already assigned the remaining number of FD resources for that beam.~~

[CB013505-A] When NRCELL/eirpControl/actEpic is disabled [End CB013505-A] the resource distribution for NBR configured non-GBR bearers shall consider EIRP restriction specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22486\_replaced([11181204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gh_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-10**  (ID: `11179776`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-10.0-5**  (ID: `13405092`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
If DL ES(refer to [12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ)) is deactivated, then // WRR is used(refer to [11179805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ET_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) ).

gNB-DU shall distribute the available resources calculated in ([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to each initial transmission UE with non-GBR data **belonging to either the drbTraffic which includes MC-nGBR(5QI70), normal non-GBR (including congestion deprioritized GBR) and NBR excessive data after distributed in step 9**[CB014699-F] or the criticalDrbTraffic [End CB014699-F] or the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class **and/or deprioritized GBR/DC-GBR** data.***\****\*

UEs with SRB traffic are excluded from remaining resource distribution described in this requirement.

The FD resources are distributed using a weighted round robin algorithm (See [11179805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ET_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else, then //DL ES is activated

gNB-DU  shall distribute the available resources calculated in ([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to each initial transmission UE with non-GBR data **belonging to either the drbTraffic which includes MC-nGBR(5QI70),  normal non-GBR (including congestion deprioritized GBR), Large buffer non-GBR and NBR excessive data after distributed in step 9**[CB014699-F] or the criticalDrbTraffic class [End CB014699-F] or the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class **and/or deprioritized GBR/DC-GBR** **data.**\*\*\*\*

UEs with SRB traffic are excluded from remaining resource distribution described in this requirement.

End If

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11**  (ID: `11179805`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Weighted Round-Robin algorithm for distributing resources for all non-GBR DRBs of a UE

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11179808`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Overview

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11179814`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
The Weighted Round Robin (WRR) algorithm has following inputs:

1. The list of UEs eligible for resource distribution (as per the sequence defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
2. The maximum reservation of resources possible for each UE. (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Various Steps in the WRR algorithm:

1. Calculating the scheduling weight for each UE in the list
2. Sorting the list of UEs input to the WRR algorithm
3. Resource distribution to each UE in the list.
4. Handling of unused resources.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-2**  (ID: `11179820`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Algorithm

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11179826`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Step 1: Calculating the scheduling weight for each UE in the list

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-2.0-1.0-8**  (ID: `12533628`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12533628:
DL resource assignment using FDM
*If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = False*

For a UE i, the weight is obtained by calculating the sum of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight of its non-GBR bearers that have data in the buffer. [CB014044-C] If NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdAlgoEnh= DL or DL\_UL, the weight is further multiplied the FD weighted metric of the UE, namely fdWeightedMetric[i,j,t]. For FD weighted metric description, please refer to [12532556: Calculation of FD Weighted Metric](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_zsP6ABuhEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [End CB014044-C]

*Else*

For a UE, the weight is obtained by calculating the sum of combined weight (i.e., the product of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight and resource group (RG) weight (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) of its non-GBR bearers that have data in the buffer. [CB014044-C] If NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdAlgoEnh= DL or DL\_UL, the resultant weight is further multiplied the FD weighted metric of the UE, namely fdWeightedMetric[i,j,t]. For FD weighted metric description, please refer to [12532556: Calculation of FD Weighted Metric](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_zsP6ABuhEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). [End CB014044-C] The resource group (RG) of a non-GBR bearer is equal to the weight of the RG to which the bearer belongs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note that a non-GBR bearer is associated with a RG.

When ext-gNB-DU receives from prime-gNB a notification to stop the specific non-GBR bearer(s) data scheduling of the ext-SCell, the DL FD-scheduler in ext-gNB-DU shall exclude the NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight for the specific non-GBR bearer(s) of the ext-SCell UE.

*Note:* *dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic bearers weight for the outer WRR shall be computed based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10158\_replaced([11181155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

*If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = False* *and (MRBTS/NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = disabled* *OR*\*\*\*\*

NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_LegacyWrr**or** NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = LegacyRange\_lowComplexityWrr)\*\*\*\*

If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false

if **NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = disabled or NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_LegacyWrr**

The round robin weights should be normalized with respect to the UE with the smallest weight.  Specifically the weights should be normalized via dividing the weights by the smallest weight then rounding the result to the nearest integer. This will result in a set of normalized weights where the smallest normalized weight equals 1.

Example: 3 UEs with round robin weights 100, 20, 60 should be normalized such that the weights are 5, 1, 3 respectively.

Example: 3 UEs with round robin weights 100, 30, 80 should be normalized such that the weights are 3, 1, 3 respectively.

else if NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = LegacyRange\_lowComplexityWrr

Please refer (5G\_UP\_ALG\_32372\_replaced([11180861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85cfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

end if

Else

The PRBs required based on the normalized scheduling weight of the UE is calculated as follows.

The normalised scheduling weight of the UE is calculated by as [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a fraction. The numerator is $w\_u^U$. The denominator is a summation symbol followed by "each UE i joining WRR" and then $w\_i^U$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where

[Before CB014044-C]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. It shows the letter "W" with a subscript "u" and a superscript "U". The letters are in a stylized font with a gradient of colors, appearing to be orange, yellow, and blue. The background is white, and there is a subtle shadow effect around the letters.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]: UE scheduling weight of UE [image-2][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract depiction of two figures or objects. The colors are muted, with shades of blue, yellow, and gray blending together. The figures appear to be standing side-by-side, with a sense of depth suggested by the way the colors fade and blend. The overall impression is one of movement or fluidity, as if the figures are in motion or are made of a liquid-like substance. The background is a bright, undefined white, which further emphasizes the abstract nature of the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[CB014044-C]

For [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the mathematical notation "w\_u^U". The "w" is large and in the foreground, with a subscript "u" below and to the right of it. Above and to the right of the "w" is a superscript "U". The colors are muted and appear to be a mix of orange, blue, and purple, with a white background. The overall image is out of focus.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13693.007.png[/image\_path][/image-3]definition, please refer to [11180562: Step 3: Resource distribution to each UE in the list.](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

[End CB014044-C]

Thus the PRBs required as per the normalisation is given by:

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$w\_{u}^{R} = \text{round}\left(\frac{w\_{u}^{U}}{\sum\_{\text{each UE } i \text{ joining WRR}} w\_{i}^{U}} \cdot R\_{u}\right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.004.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

where

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R u :". The letters and symbols are rendered in a blurry, colorful, and slightly distorted manner, with a gradient of colors, possibly blue, orange, and yellow, appearing within and around the characters.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.005.png[/image\_path][/image-5][image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, abstract representation of the letter "N". The letter is rendered with a gradient of colors, including shades of blue, orange, yellow, and black, creating a somewhat iridescent or prismatic effect. The edges of the letter are soft and diffused, contributing to the overall blurriness. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.006.png[/image\_path][/image-6]

where [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, abstract representation of the letter "N". The letter is rendered with a gradient of colors, including shades of blue, orange, yellow, and black, creating a somewhat iridescent or prismatic effect. The edges of the letter are soft and diffused, contributing to the overall blurriness. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.007.png[/image\_path][/image-7]is the remaining available PRBs in the largest BWP across **several PDSCH sub-area(s), if there are several dedicated BWPs configured in the cell. See Step 3.** Hint to implementation: Here the round function used should be roundup/ceil, however it is upto the implementation to consider the respective math function.\*\*\*\*

end if

Else if MRBTS/NRBTS.actSliceAwareScheduler*= False* *and (NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_EnhancedWrr or* *NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_lowComplexityWrr)*\*\*\*\*

If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false

if **NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_EnhancedWrr**

Please refer (5G\_UP\_ALG\_28960\_replaced([11180808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku8SbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

else if **NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_lowComplexityWrr**

end if

Else

Please refer (5G\_UP\_ALG\_28962\_replaced([11180935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85c_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

end if

*Else* *if enhanced slice aware scheduler is deactivated for a cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_12237\_replaced([11288194](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp5RdPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* *and (MRBTS/NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = disabled OR  NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_LegacyWrr or* *NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = LegacyRange\_lowComplexityWrr)***\***\*

If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false

if **NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = disabled OR  NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_LegacyWrr**

The round robin weight for each UE should be computed as follows:

[image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$w\_u^R = \max \left{ \text{round} \left( \frac{w\_u^U}{\sum\_{\text{each UE } i \text{ joining WRR}} w\_i^U} \cdot R\_u \right), 1 \right}$
This equation defines a variable $w\_u^R$ as the maximum of two values:
1. The rounded result of a fraction multiplied by $R\_u$. The fraction's numerator is $w\_u^U$ and its denominator is the sum of $w\_i^U$ for all UEs $i$ joining WRR.
2. The value 1.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.008.png[/image\_path][/image-8]

else if **NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = LegacyRange\_lowComplexityWrr**

Please refer (5G\_UP\_ALG\_32372\_replaced([11180861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85cfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

end if

Else

the **PRBs required based on the normalised scheduling weight of the UE is calculated as follows**

[image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression rendered in a stylized font. The expression is "W\_u^R". The "W" is large and bold, with a gradient of colors from orange to blue. The subscript "u" is smaller and positioned below and to the right of the "W", also in a similar gradient. The superscript "R" is smaller and positioned above and to the right of the "W", in a similar gradient. The entire expression is enclosed within a thick black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.009.png[/image\_path][/image-9][image-10][image\_desc]The image contains two horizontal bars, each divided into two sections. The left section of each bar is a light brown color, and the right section is a light blue color. The bars are positioned one above the other, with a small gap between them. The image is a close-up of these bars, and the background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.010.png[/image\_path][/image-10][image-11][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is enclosed in parentheses and starts with the word "round". Inside the parentheses, there is a fraction. The numerator of the fraction is "w^u\_u". The denominator is a summation symbol followed by "each UE i joining WRR W^u\_i". The fraction is multiplied by "R\_u".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.011.png[/image\_path][/image-11]

end if

where [image-23][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, abstract representation of the letter "N". See Step 3.**

Note: Here the round function used should be roundup/ceil.

For the case of MU WRR (NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True), [image-24][image\_desc]The image contains a stylized letter "N" with a gradient effect. The letter is rendered in a bold, sans-serif font. The gradient transitions from a dark blue at the bottom to lighter shades of blue and orange towards the top, giving it a somewhat metallic or iridescent appearance. The background is white, and there is a subtle black border around the entire image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.015.png[/image\_path][/image-24] is equal to the available PRBs in the largest BWP at the beginning of MU WRR execution, i.e., the total PRBs in the largest BWP minus the PRBs allocated for SU and MU re-Tx (see 5G\_L2\_5575\_replaced([11166705](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HK_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false and **(NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = disabled or NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_LegacyWrr)**

Example: Suppose the available resources are 18 RBGs, 3 UEs with UE weights 100, 30, 80 have round robin weights[image-25][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is:
max {round($\frac{100}{100+30+80} \cdot 18$), 1}, max {round($\frac{30}{100+30+80} \cdot 18$), 1}[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.018.png[/image\_path][/image-25] and *[image-26][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is:
max {round (80 / (100 + 30 + 80) \* 18), 1}[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.019.png[/image\_path][/image-26], i.e. 9, 3 and 7 respectively.*

Else

Let S be initialized as the set of all UEs that join the WRR and this list may change after each iteration

If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false

Let *N  be the available PRBs (excluding blanked PRBs) in the largest BWP.*

Else

Let *N  be the* *remaining available PRBs in the largest BWP across several PDSCH sub-area(s), if there are several dedicated BWPs configured in the cell.* *See Step 3.*\*\*\*\*

For the case of MU WRR (NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True), *N is equal to the available PRBs in the largest BWP at the beginning of MU WRR execution, i.e., the total PRBs in the largest BWP minus the PRBs allocated for SU and MU re-Tx (see 5G\_L2\_5575\_replaced([11166705](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HK_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*

If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false

if **NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = LegacyRange\_lowComplexityWrr or NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_lowComplexityWrr**

Please refer (5G\_UP\_ALG\_32372\_replaced([11180861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85cfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

else

Please refer (5G\_UP\_ALG\_28960\_replaced([11180808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku8SbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Example: Suppose the available resources are 128 PRBs, 3 UEs have UE weights 100, 2, 3. Each UE has nominal RBG size of 8. The first UE has buffered non-GBR data only for 48 PRBs (6 RBGs), the other two UEs both have full buffer non-GBR data. Then the WRR algorithm will give out resources as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | N (before iteration) | S (before iteration) | Round robin weight of UE1 [image-27][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.020.png[/image\_path][/image-27] | Round robin weight of UE2 [image-28][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.021.png[/image\_path][/image-28] | Round robin weight of UE3 [image-29][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.022.png[/image\_path][/image-29] | RBGs assigned to UE1 in the iteration | RBGs assigned to UE2 in the iteration | RBGs assigned to UE3 in the iteration |
| Iteration 1 | 128 | {UE1, UE2, UE3} | 15 | 0 | 0 | 6 | 0 | 0 |
| Iteration 2 | 80 | {UE2, UE3} | NA | 4 | 6 | NA | 4 | 6 |

end if

Else

Please Refer (5G\_UP\_ALG\_28962\_replaced([11180935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85c_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Example: Suppose the available resources are 128 PRBs, 3 UEs have UE weights 100, 2, 3. The first UE has buffered non-GBR data only for 48 PRBs, the other two UEs both have full buffer non-GBR data. Then the WRR algorithm will give out resources as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | N (before iteration) | S (before iteration) | Round robin weight of UE1 [image-30][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.023.png[/image\_path][/image-30] | Round robin weight of UE2 [image-31][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.024.png[/image\_path][/image-31] | Round robin weight of UE3 [image-32][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.025.png[/image\_path][/image-32] | PRBs assigned to UE1 in the iteration | PRBs assigned to UE2 in the iteration | PRBs assigned to UE3 in the iteration |
| Iteration 1 | 128 | {UE1, UE2, UE3} | 15 | 0 | 0 | 48 | 0 | 0 |
| Iteration 2 | 80 | {UE2, UE3} | NA | 4 | 6 | NA | 32 | 48 |

End If

The rationale of the above WRR algorithm is as follows:

1.   The above WRR algorithm makes sure that, If [image-33][image\_desc]The image is blurry and contains a logo with the letters "W" and "R" in a stylized font. The letters are multicolored and appear to be made of abstract shapes. There is also a smaller letter "u" below the "W" and "R". The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.026.png[/image\_path][/image-33]= 0,then keep UEs in WRR list(i.e. to be  schedule list) and wait for unused FD resources from other UEs with [image-34][image\_desc]The image is blurry and contains text that is difficult to read. The text appears to be "W, R, U" with some smaller characters below the "W" and "R". The colors are muted and the overall image quality is poor.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13685.027.png[/image\_path][/image-34]is not equal to zero, because it is possible that some FD resources are unused by UEs with high schedule weight due to limited buffer which can be distributed to the most deserving candidate which have either no reserved resources or not been fully satisfied with the reserved FD resources to drain their total buffered data

2. The resource allocation is done in one or more iterations until all UEs are assigned maximum resources or all PDSCH resources are given out. This makes sure that UEs with high UE weight are considered in the first iteration. If some resources are still available after the first iteration, other UEs that have small UE weight (i.e. normalized round robin weight of these UEs is equal to 0 in the first iteration) will be considered in later iterations for resource allocation.

3. If UEs with high UE weight have a high traffic demand, it is possible that UEs with small UE weight will not be given any resources for the current slot. This is to make sure that resources given to UEs shall be more proportional to their UE weights

4. The above WRR algorithm makes sure that either a UE is given 0 resource or it is given at least 1 If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **PRB, else** RBG.\*\*\*\*

5. Normalized round robin weights for UEs are computed in single iteration, where every UE shall get atleast 1 token.

---

DL resource assignment using FDM

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-2.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `12533619`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12533619:
DL resource assignment using FDM
If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false

if NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = disabled **or**

NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_LegacyWrr or

NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_EnhancedWrr

Before the resource distribution, the list of UEs input to the weighted round robin algorithm is shuffled (random order of UEs).

else if NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = LegacyRange\_lowComplexityWrr or NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_lowComplexityWrr

The UEs in the list of UEs, say I, which are put as input to the WRR are sorted based on the following:

- [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRBu^max". The text is dark blue and appears to be written in a sans-serif font. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The formula is a fraction. The numerator is $W\_{u}^{U}$. The denominator is a summation symbol followed by "each UE i joining WRR" and then $W\_{i}^{U}$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13689.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]is the normalized scheduling weight of the UE.

The list is first sorted in decreasing order of the BWP ‘*p’ and within each BWP ‘*p’ in increasing order of [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in dark blue text on a white background. The expression reads "PRBu^max / Yu". The letters are on a white background with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13689.005.png[/image\_path][/image-5]*)\*\*\*

I\* is finally used in the Step 3 during resource distribution.

end if

Else

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-2.0-3.0-7**  (ID: `13382876`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
13382876:
DL resource assignment using FDM
If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,
If NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = disabled or
NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_LegacyWrr or NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange\_EnhancedWrr
The FD resources are distributed *rdFdResPerRoundDl resource(s) at a time. (default value* rdFdResPerRoundDl=1)**If the available resources for initial transmission UEs is not multiples of *rdFdResPerRoundDl, the last round will distribute mod(*rdFdResPerRoundDl, available resources for Non-GBR initial transmission) resource(s).**A UE is not given more than the maximum FD resources it can get (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and should be excluded from any further FD resource giveaways if it has already acquired this many FD resources.
When NRCELL/eirpControl/actEpic is disabled for a group of UEs having the same beam, the sum of allocated FD resources should not exceed* rrmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp\*\*, the remaining number of FD resources that can be allocated to each scheduled beam (based on EIRP, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4349\_replaced([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and all those UEs should be excluded from any further FD resource giveaways if FD scheduler already assigned the remaining number of FD resources for that beam.***
 
If NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg is equal to ExtendedRange\_EnhancedWrr or If enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_12237\_replaced([11288194](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp5RdPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* if the available resources contains at least rdFdResPerRoundDl, each UE with nonzero normalized weight should get at least rdFdResPerRoundDl resource(s) before the normalized weights are taken into consideration
Else
\* Each UE with nonzero normalized weight should get at least *rdFdResPerRoundDl resource(s) from the available resources before the normalized weights are taken into consideration.*
End
 
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, after allocating FD resources to a UE in each round, gNB-DU shall determine the number of layer PRBs consumed (**rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and use it to keep track of the used, as well as remaining layer PRB resources (**rrmNumUsedPdschLayerPrbPerSlot, **rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*****\*
 
In the case that while allocating FD resources to a UE in a round, the additional required layer PRB resources (can be calculated using* \*numReqLayerPrb described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is greater than the available layer PRB resources, gNB-DU shall reduce the number of FD resources allocated to the UE such that the layer PRB restriction is satisfied (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26597\_replaced([11214606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).(The maximum usable FD resources based on the currently available layer PRB resources and the UEs rank can be calculated using** numReqFdPrb described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))***\*
 
The algorithm stops if either:
- all UEs with initial transmission have been allocated the maximum amount of resources they can get (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
- or all the available resources including the layer PRB resources (rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot; refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* \*have been allocated.**
- gNB-DU shall also ensure that the constraint related to layer PRBs mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26597\_replaced([11214606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is maintained.
 
After algorithm stops, the following check shall be executed 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14422\_replaced([11181129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85ePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Skip MU-MIMO Enhanced'.
The output of the algorithm is the amount of FD resources (RBGs) each initial transmission UE will get (for all bearers).
 
If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True, for non-GBR UE which is paired with HARQ re-Tx UE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7431\_replaced([11144204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7arPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), corresponding RBG allocation due to DL pairing shall be considered as piggyback RBGs. The WRR algorithm doesn't need to consider these piggyback RBGs for simplification purpose. The output of the WRR algorithm, allowedRbgUe(i), will be used by DL pairing algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7518\_replaced([11142428](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7431\_replaced([11144204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7arPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
else
Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32405\_replaced([11180977](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85dvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
End if
Else,
For the UEs sorted in Step 2, perform the following in order to distribute resources to the UEs in the list:
From Step 2, *I* is the list of sorted UEs.*Let* N, be the remaining PRBs available among all the PDSCH sub-area(s), if multiple PDSCH sub-areas exist, **across the largest BWP.***Calculate [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a blurry, italicized font. The equation reads:
totalMaxNeededNonGbrRes = $\sum\_{i=0}^{I-1} PR B\_{i,u}^{max}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
For each UE u in the list while* I*>0, and while* N > 0, gNB-DU shall do the following:**do,
1) Calculate:
       [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in blue text on a white background. The formula is:
$PRB\_u = \max(\min(PRB\_u^{\max}, w\_u^R, w\_u^R, PRB \text{ granularity} \times \lfloor (\max \text{Of } (nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea)) / PRB \text{ granularity} \rfloor, PRB \text{ granularity} \times \lfloor (\text{SumOf } (rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea)) / PRB \text{ granularity} \rfloor)), PRB \text{ granularity})$
The text is clear and legible. There are no tables or other visual elements in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
         where,
[image-2][image\_desc]PRBu^max[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]: the maximum number of PRBs required to drain out the  buffer for UE u. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB014045-F] In TRS slots, if NRCELL.act4prbAllocDlTrsSlotsForRat1 is set to True and NRBTS.actSubTRSRateMatch is set to True and ueHasFewDataInBuffer (see [11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) is not True, then this shall be equal to max(4PRBs, the maximum number of PRBs required to drain out the buffer from [11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB014045-F]
[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$w\_u^{R'} = \text{ceil}\left(\frac{W\_u^U}{\sum\_{j \in \text{Remaining UEs in BWP of the UE and all nested smaller BWPs}} W\_j^U} \cdot R'\right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
         If NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdAlgoEnh = (DL\_Only or DL\_UL):
[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. It shows the letter "w" with a subscript "u" and a superscript "U". The letters are in a stylized font and appear to be in a gradient of colors, with blues, oranges, and purples. The background is white, and there is a black border around the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]: UE scheduling weight of UE *u multiplied by the FD weighted metric of the UE u, namely FdWeightedMetric[u,j,t]. For FdWeightedMetric description, see [12532556: Calculation of FD Weighted Metric](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_zsP6ABuhEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).*Else**[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. It shows the letter "w" with a subscript "u" and a superscript "U". The letters are in a stylized font and appear to be in a gradient of colors, with blues, oranges, and purples. The background is white, and there is a black border around the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.005.png[/image\_path][/image-5] : UE scheduling weight of UE* u.R’: Remaining PRBs in the BWP of the UE *u, with all the nested smaller BWPs beneath given by* rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea. When there are multiple PDSCH sub-areas, the sum of the \*rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea would give the remaining PRBs in a BWP, across all of PDSCH sub-areas that contain PRBs of the BWP.****Hint to implementation: Here a ceil/roundup function is recommended**, however it is left for implementation to consider further.**[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" followed by a subscript "u". The text is dark blue and appears to be on a white background. The image is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]  : the number of PRBs to be reserved for the UE u and is given by the following:
[image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a blurry, italicized font. The formula is:
$PRB\_u = PRB \ granularity \* ceil(PRB\_u / PRB \ granularity)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]**Note: The result should make sure that the resources granted should not exceed the remaining PRBs in the sub-area or in the BWP.**Note: For PRB granularity definition please refer 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**[image-8][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8]: The PRBs based on the normalised scheduling weight of the UE u as determined in Step 1. This depends on the remaining PRBs given by* N.nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea : Remaining PRBs in the PDSCH subarea which includes the unused dedicated PRBs from the PDSCH subarea (*rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea) and unused CORESET #0 (or X with X>0)PRBs in the PDSCH subarea (*rmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea) across the largest BWP.***When there are several PDSCH sub-areas, the maximum of the* nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea would give the largest PDSCH sub-area available across the largest BWP.*For* rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea, rmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea and *rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23464\_replaced([11176110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When there are multiple PDSCH sub-areas, the sum of the* rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea would give the remaining PRBs in a BWP, across all of PDSCH sub-areas that contain PRBs of the BWP.***1.1) If NRCELL/eirpControl/actEpic is disabled and if the UE u even for a UE* *with valid subband BWVs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14111\_replaced([11156776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks3OyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),**belongs to a group of UEs having the same beam, [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" followed by a subscript "u". The text is dark blue and appears to be on a white background. The image is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.007.png[/image\_path][/image-9] should not exceed* rmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp**, the remaining number of FD resources that can be allocated to each scheduled beam else the UE u should be excluded from the resource distribution and take necessary actions (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4349\_replaced([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the UE u with valid subband BWVs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14111\_replaced([11156776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks3OyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is excluded from the FD resource distribution due to EIRP control (number of FD resources exceeding *rmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp ) , gNB-DU shall shall mark the UE as " WB SRS constraint UE" and start/restart rdWbSrsConstraintTimer for this UE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4349\_replaced([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).******1.2) For UEs with [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" followed by a subscript "u". The text is dark blue and appears to be on a white background. The image is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.007.png[/image\_path][/image-10]<* rdMinPrbForRat1Alloc PRBs (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29029\_replaced([11193977](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvxY2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if the sum of *rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea > [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains the text "total/MaxNeededNonGbrRes" in a blurry, stylized font. The text is white with a slight gradient and appears to be on a light background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.010.png[/image\_path][/image-11], then calculate the available PRBs for augmenting the granted resources for such UEs (given by* availablePrbHeadroom = rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea - [image-12][image\_desc]The image contains the text "total/MaxNeededNonGbrRes" in a blurry, stylized font. Note1: It must be made sure that the augmentation does not exceed the *rmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp when NRCELL/eirpControl/actEpic is disabled, in the event of which the augmentation is abandoned and the UE(s) would continue to use the original allocation.*
Note2: For the slots with PRB granularity < *rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb PRBs for a non-GBR High Reliability bearer, otherwise <* rdMinPrbForRat1Alloc PRBs, the algorithm must make sure PRB granularity be equal to  *rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb PRBs for a non-GBR High Reliability bearer, otherwise* rdMinPrbForRat1Alloc PRBs. *rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb shall be applicable to all bearers configured with High reliability profile, that would contend for resource distribution i.e bearers with DRB traffic which includes MC-nGBR(5QI70), normal non-GBR (including congestion deprioritized GBR) , large buffer non-GBR and NBR excessive data or the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class* *and/or deprioritized GBR/DC-GBR* *data.****\*****2)If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and the UE u is a MU-MIMO eligible user then assign the subarea as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26464\_replaced([11176645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) else if the UE u is a non MU-MIMO eligible user, assign the PDSCH sub-area as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23683\_replaced([11176637](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
3) Update* rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea =*rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea - [image-17][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" followed by a subscript "u". See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24165\_replaced([11176627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*4) Update the size of the selected PDSCH sub-area* nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea- = [image-19][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" followed by a subscript "u". The text is dark blue and appears to be on a white background. The image is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.011.png[/image\_path][/image-19] **Note that the PRBs deducted can be form the remaining dedicated PRBs or from the remaining CORESET0 (or X with X>0)** PRBs within the PDSCH subarea. **Also note that the PRBs deducted from the CORESET0 (or X with X>0)** part within a PDSCH sub-area or BWP within a PDSCH sub-area is counted only once in the above equations.***\******5) Update *N = *N - [image-20][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" followed by a subscript "M". The text is dark blue and appears to be on a white background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.012.png[/image\_path][/image-20].**6) Compute [image-21][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.013.png[/image\_path][/image-21]for the next UE in the list* *as in Step 1, with the updated* N**. While computing, the scheduling weight for the UE u needs to be removed** since this UE u has been served in this iteration and shall no longer join the WRR further.******\*
7) Compute [image-22][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry mathematical expression. The expression appears to be "w\_u^R'".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.014.png[/image\_path][/image-22]for the next UE in the list, with the updated* rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea** from step 3 for this BWP and the other nested smaller BWPs. While computing, the scheduling weight for the UE u needs to be removed **since this UE u has been served in this iteration and shall no longer join the WRR further.*****8) If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, after allocating FD resources to a UE, gNB-DU shall determine the number of layer PRBs consumed (*\*rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and use it to keep track of the used, as well as remaining layer PRB resources (**rrmNumUsedPdschLayerPrbPerSlot, **rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*****\*
In the case that while allocating FD resources to a UE, the additional required layer PRB resources (can be calculated using* \*numReqLayerPrb described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is greater than the available layer PRB resources, gNB-DU shall reduce the number of FD resources allocated to the UE such that the layer PRB restriction is satisfied (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26597\_replaced([11214606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).(The maximum usable FD resources based on the currently available layer PRB resources and the UEs rank can be calculated using** numReqFdPrb described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The allocation shall be stopped for any further remaining UEs.\*\*\*\*
EndFor
Note:
1. For a MU eligible user, if the above algorithm stops because N = 0 (leading to [image-23][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB" followed by a subscript "u". The text is dark blue and appears to be in a bold, italicized font. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13694.015.png[/image\_path][/image-23]= 0) still the subarea assignment rules shall be performed since for a MU user there is still possibility of pairing with the other MU eligible users and the MU user shall be assigned to PDSCH subarea with maximum potential PRBs for pairing. See example in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26543\_replaced([11144828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr9P2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. At the end of WRR, for a MU eligible user, gNB-DU shall perform PDSCH subarea re-selection for the user when the conditions under (3.1) as described in [11145129:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_FBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) are fulfilled.

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11180766`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
**Step 4: Redistribution of unused FD resources within the assigned PDSCH subarea**

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11180770`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
After the subarea assignment has been performed, there is a possibility that some PRBs are unused in the respective subareas which can be distributed to the most deserving candidate.

**[PR795459] For remaining PRBs allocation to vUEs refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7530\_replaced([11145129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_FBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The following rules apply to only SU mode users.** [End PR795459]\*\*\*\*

The rules to determine the deserving candidate is given as follows:

* gNB-DU shall mark the UEs which have not been fully satisfied with the reserved PRBs to drain their total buffered data.
* gNB-DU shall list the UEs in descending order according to the number of PRBs needed.

gNB-DU shall assign these unused PRBs to the top ranked UE provided that the checks defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are satisfied and the resulting number of PRBs not exceed *rmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp, i.e the remaining number of FD resources that can be allocated to each scheduled beam.*

Note that 5G L2-PS shall perform this step after the "dynamicswitch" to RAT0 functionality , if applicable to this slot and with allowable users existing among the scheduled users, is performed. For "dynamicwitch" to RAT0 for initial transmission users see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24995\_replaced([11181383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

[CB008927-A]- If NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, the PDSCH sub-area assigned to each UE with initial transmission. [End CB008927-A]

Overview

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11180798`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
The Enhnced WRR is applicable for Scheduling weight(MRBTS/NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight) to a broader range to accommodate sufficient differentiation in scheduling for different users.

Various Steps in the Enhanced WRR algorithm:

1. Calculating the scheduling weight for each UE in the list
2. Sorting the list of UEs input to the WRR algorithm
3. Resource distribution to each UE in the list.
4. Handling of unused resources.

---

DL resource assignment using FDM
Step1: Compute the round robin weight for each UE in the list
