**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-4.0-2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11180855`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11180855:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Let S be initialized as the set of all UEs that join the WRR and this list may change after each iteration.
 Let R be available PDSCH resources (RBG) for non-GBR initial transmission.
 Let N  be the available PRBs (excluding blanked PRBs) in the largest BWP.
[PR786526-25R1]
  loopId = 0
[End PR786526-25R1]
While (N > 0) and (S is not empty)
For each UE u in S
Compute the round robin weight for each UE u in S as follows:
[image-0][image\_desc]R\_u = ceil(N/P\_u)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
  [Before PR786526-25R1]
[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$w\_{u}^{R} = round\left(\frac{w\_{u}^{U}}{\sum\_{i \in I} w\_{i}^{U}} \cdot R\_{u}\right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
  [PR786526-25R1]
        [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$w\_u = \frac{w\_u^U}{\sum\_{i \in S} w\_i^U} \cdot R\_u$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
       [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a light blue background. The equation is: $w\_u^R = round(w\_u)$. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
                  [End PR786526-25R1]
                  where:
R\_u: number of RBGs available for non-GBR initial transmission. 
P\_u: The nominal RBG size of the active BWP of UE u. See also 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of P\_u.
w\_u^R: normalized scheduling weight of UE u.
w\_u^U: UE scheduleweight of UE u.
N = is the available PRBs (excluding blanked PRBs) in the largest BWP.
 
[PR786526-25R1]
[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "w" followed by a subscript "u". The letters are blurred and appear to be in a light orange-yellow color with a slight blue outline. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]: unrounded normalized round robin weight of UE u
[End PR786526-25R1]
[PR786526-25R1]
If (loopId = 0) and ([image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as "w\_u^R = 0". The letter "w" is in lowercase, followed by a subscript "u" and a superscript "R". This is followed by an equals sign and the number "0".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5])
Generate a uniformly distributed random number r in [0, 1)
       If  [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "r < W". The characters are in a dark color against a white background. The letter "r" is on the left, followed by a less than symbol "<", and then the letter "W" with a subscript "u" below and to the right of it.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]
  [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as "w\_u^R = 1". The "w" is a lowercase letter, the "u" is a subscript to the "w", and the "R" is a superscript to the "w". The equation is set equal to the number "1".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]
                 End If
                          End If
        [End PR786526-25R1]
 
       End For
      [PR786526-25R1]
          loopId = loopId + 1
        [End PR786526-25R1]
 
If [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as "w\_u^R = 0". The "w" is a lowercase letter, the "u" is a subscript to "w", and the "R" is a superscript to "w". The equation states that "w\_u^R" is equal to "0".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8] for each UE u in S
  For each UE u in S, set[image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as: $w\_u^R = 1$. The text is in a dark color against a light background. The characters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9];
 End If
 
Assign 0 RBG to all UEs u in S with w\_u^R=0.
 
Distribute resources to all UEs u in S  that have nonzero w\_u^R, as specified in the step of resource assignment
 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 23763), with normalized round robin weights w\_u^R and available PDSCH resources as inputs.
 
Remove from S the UEs that have got nonzero RBGs in this iteration.
 
Remove from S the UEs that have got zero RBG in this iteration due to the UE's active BWP has no available PDSCH RBGs.
 
N is reduced by the number of PRBs that have been distributed in this iteration;
 
      End While
          End If
 
Note, the step of resource assignment (5G\_UP\_ALG\_23763\_replaced([11180562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) can be executed for multiple times, depending on UE weights, their buffer status and available PDSCH resources. In each execution of resource assignment (5G\_UP\_ALG\_23763\_replaced([11180562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), either all UEs with nonzero round robin weights are given the maximum number of resources or the available PDSCH resources are exhausted.
 
 
The rationale of the above WRR algorithm is as follows:
1.The above WRR algorithm makes sure that, If [image-10][image\_desc]The image is blurry and contains a logo with the letters "W" and "R" in a stylized font. The letters are multicolored and appear to be made of abstract shapes. There is also a smaller letter "u" below the "W" and "R".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10]= 0,then keep UEs in WRR list(i.e. to be  schedule list) and wait for unused FD resources from other UEs with [image-11][image\_desc]The image is blurry and contains text that is difficult to read. The text appears to be "W, R, U". The letters are in different sizes and positions, with "W" being the largest, "R" slightly smaller and to the right of "W", and "U" being the smallest and below "W". The colors of the letters are varied and appear to be a mix of blues, purples, and oranges. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13705.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11]is not equal to zero, because it is possible that some FD resources are unused by UEs with high schedule weight due to limited buffer which can be distributed to the most deserving candidate which have either no reserved resources or not been fully satisfied with the reserved FD resources to drain their total buffered data
2. The resource allocation is done in one or more iterations until all UEs are assigned maximum resources or all PDSCH resources are given out. This makes sure that UEs with high UE weight are considered in the first iteration. If some resources are still available after the first iteration, other UEs that have small UE weight (i.e. normalized round robin weight of these UEs is equal to 0 in the first iteration) will be considered in later iterations for resource allocation.
3. If UEs with high UE weight have a high traffic demand, it is possible that UEs with small UE weight will not be given any resources for the current slot. This is to make sure that resources given to UEs shall be more proportional to their UE weights.
4. The above WRR algorithm makes sure that either a UE is given 0 resource or it is given at least 1 RBG.
5. Normalized round robin weights for UEs are computed in multiple iteration. They can take any nonnegative integer values.In first iteration, the resource assignment step may be executed on a set of UEs that have normalized round robin weights higher than Zero.
[PR786526-25R1]
6. In the first iteration of WRR, if a UE has relatively small weight such that the computed normalized round robin weight is equal to 0, the UE may get starved if other high weight UEs have enough traffic to exhaust resources. To avoid the starvation problem, the UE is given normalized round robin weight of 1 with a probability that is equal to the equivalent number of RBG (a fractional number) that the UE should get per slot.
[End PR786526-25R1]

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-4.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11180932`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11180932:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Let S be initialized as the set of all UEs that join the WRR and this list may change after each iteration.
Let R be available PDSCH resources (RBG) for non-GBR initial transmission.
 
Compute the round robin weight for each UE *u in* S as follows:**[image-0][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
[image-1][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
[Before PR836611]
[image-2][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
If ([image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3])
Generate a uniformly distributed random number *r in [0, 1);*if ([image-4][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4])
[image-5][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]
end if
end if
[PR836611]
     [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a light gray color against a white background. The equation is:
$w\_{u}^{R} = floor(w\_{u})$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/7651795a-e826-46e5-947f-05e389251011.png[/image\_path][/image-6]
Generate a uniformly distributed random number* r in [0, 1);*[image-7][image\_desc]if($r < w\_u - w\_u^R$)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/b23d5a35-75c5-4f6f-aec2-e025a9dc743b.png[/image\_path][/image-7]

      End if
[End PR836611]
If ([image-9][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.007.png[/image\_path][/image-9]for all UEs in S)
for each UE u in S, set [image-10][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.008.png[/image\_path][/image-10];
end if
 
where:
[image-11][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.009.png[/image\_path][/image-11]number of RBGs available for non-GBR initial transmission.* *Note: Considering partial RBG(s), [image-12][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.010.png[/image\_path][/image-12] may need to be fine tuned such that the number of PRBs corresponding to [image-13][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.011.png[/image\_path][/image-13] RGBs is closest to N, but not bigger than N.**N:  the remaining available PRBs in the largest BWP.
[image-14][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.012.png[/image\_path][/image-14]the nominal RBG size of the active BWP of UE* u. See also 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of[image-15][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.013.png[/image\_path][/image-15].*[image-16][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.014.png[/image\_path][/image-16]normalized scheduling weight of UE* u.*[image-17][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.015.png[/image\_path][/image-17]UE scheduleweight of UE* u.*Distribute resources to all UEs* u in *S  that have nonzero [image-18][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.016.png[/image\_path][/image-18], as specified in the step of resource assignment (5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 23763), with normalized round robin weights [image-19][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.017.png[/image\_path][/image-19]* and available PDSCH resources as inputs.***
 
Remove from *S the UEs that have got nonzero RBGs in this iteration.*
 
Remove from *S the UEs that have gotton zero RBG in this iteration due to the UE's active BWP has no available PDSCH RBGs or the UE has gotten the maximum resources it can get (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*
 
*N*is reduced by the number of PRBs that have been distributed in this iteration;\*\*
 
Note, the step of resource assignment (5G\_UP\_ALG\_23763\_replaced([11180562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) can be executed for multiple times, depending on UE weights, their buffer status and available PDSCH resources.
 
The rationale of the above WRR algorithm is as follows:
1. The above WRR algorithm makes sure that, If [image-20][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.018.png[/image\_path][/image-20]= 0,then keep UEs in WRR list(i.e. to be  schedule list) and wait for unused FD resources from other UEs with [image-21][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13707.019.png[/image\_path][/image-21] is not equal to zero, because it is possible that some FD resources are unused by UEs with high schedule weight due to limited buffer which can be distributed to the most deserving candidate which have either no reserved resources or not been fully satisfied with the reserved FD resources to drain their total buffered data
 
2. The resource allocation is done in one or more iterations until all UEs are assigned maximum resources or all PDSCH resources are given out. This makes sure that UEs with high UE weight are considered in the first iteration. If some resources are still available after the first iteration, other UEs that have small UE weight (i.e. normalized round robin weight of these UEs is equal to 0 in the first iteration) will be considered in later iterations for resource allocation.
 
3. If UEs with high UE weight have a high traffic demand, it is possible that UEs with small UE weight will not be given any resources for the current slot. This is to make sure that resources given to UEs shall be more proportional to their UE weights.
 
4. The above WRR algorithm makes sure that either a UE is given 0 resource or it is given at least 1 RBG.
 
5. Normalized round robin weights for UEs are computed in single iteration.
 
6. In the first iteration of WRR, if a UE has relatively small weight such that the computed normalized round robin weight is equal to 0, the UE may get starved if other high weight UEs have enough traffic to exhaust resources. To avoid the starvation problem, the UE is given normalized round robin weight of 1 with a probability that is equal to the equivalent number of RBG (a fractional number) that the UE should get per slot.

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-4.0-2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12533614`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12533614:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Let S be initialized as the set of all UEs that join the WRR and this list may change after each iteration.

Let R be available PDSCH resources (PRBs) for non-GBR initial transmission.

Compute the round robin weight for each UE *u in* S as follows:\*\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R u :". The text is blurry and has a chromatic aberration effect, with red, green, and blue fringes around the letters. The letters are in a serif font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13709.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0][image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, abstract representation of the letter "N". The colors are a mix of blues, oranges, and yellows, with a gradient effect that makes the letter appear to glow or shimmer. The edges of the letter are indistinct and feathered, contributing to the overall impression of blurriness.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13709.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$w\_{u}^{R} = round\left(\frac{w\_{u}^{U}}{\sum\_{i \in I} w\_{i}^{U}} \cdot R\_{u}\right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13709.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Where:

*R\_u : number of RBGs available for non-GBR initial transmission.*

**w\_u^R : normalised scheduling weight of UE *u.***

[Before CB014044-C]

**w\_u^U : UE scheduling weight of UE *u.***

[CB014044-C]

For [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the mathematical notation "w\_u^U". The "w" is large and in the foreground, with a subscript "u" below and to the right of it. Above and to the right of the "w" is a superscript "U". The colors are muted and appear to be a mix of orange, blue, and purple, with a white background. The overall image is out of focus.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13693.007.png[/image\_path][/image-3]definition, please refer to [11180562: Step 3: Resource distribution to each UE in the list.](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

[End CB014044-C]

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, abstract representation of the letter "N". The colors are a mix of blues, oranges, and yellows, with a gradient effect that makes the letter appear to glow or shimmer. The edges of the letter are indistinct and feathered, contributing to the overall impression of blurriness.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13709.002.png[/image\_path][/image-4] is the remaining available PRBs in the largest BWP across several **PDSCH sub-area(s), if there are several dedicated BWPs configured in the cell(See Step 3).**Note: Here the round function used should be roundup/ceil.\*\*\*\*

If [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as "w\_u^R = 0". The "w" is a lowercase letter, the "u" is a subscript to "w", and the "R" is a superscript to "w". The equation states that "w\_u^R" is equal to "0".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13709.004.png[/image\_path][/image-5] for each UE *u in* S\*\*

For each UE *u in* S, set[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as: $w\_u^R = 1$. The text is in a dark color against a light background. The characters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13709.005.png[/image\_path][/image-6];\*\*

End If

Distribute resources to all UEs *u in* S  that have nonzero *w\_u^R, as specified in the step of resource assignment*\*\*

(5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 23763), with normalized round robin weights *w\_u^R and available PDSCH resources as inputs.*

Remove from *S the UEs that have got nonzero PRBs in this iteration.*

Remove from *S the UEs that have got zero PRB in this iteration due to the UE's active BWP has no available PDSCH PRBs.*

*N*is reduced by the number of PRBs that have been distributed in this iteration.\*\*

**Note, the step of resource assignment (5G\_UP\_ALG\_23763\_replaced([11180562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) can be executed for multiple times, depending on UE weights, their buffer status and available PDSCH resources. In each execution of resource assignment (5G\_UP\_ALG\_23763\_replaced([11180562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), either all UEs with nonzero round robin weights are given the maximum number of resources or the available PDSCH resources are exhausted.**

The rationale of the above WRR algorithm is as follows:

1.The above WRR algorithm makes sure that, If [image-7][image\_desc]The image is blurry and contains a logo with the letters "W" and "R" in a stylized font. The letters are multicolored and appear to be made of abstract shapes. There is also a smaller letter "u" below the "W" and "R".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13709.006.png[/image\_path][/image-7]= 0,then keep UEs in WRR list(i.e. to be  schedule list) and wait for unused FD resources from other UEs with [image-8][image\_desc]The image is blurry and contains text that is difficult to read. The text appears to be "W, R, U". The letters are in different sizes and positions, with "W" being the largest, "R" slightly smaller and to the right of "W", and "U" being the smallest and below "W". The colors of the letters are varied and appear to be a mix of blues, purples, and oranges. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13709.007.png[/image\_path][/image-8]is not equal to zero, because it is possible that some FD resources are unused by UEs with high schedule weight due to limited buffer which can be distributed to the most deserving candidate which have either no reserved resources or not been fully satisfied with the reserved FD resources to drain their total buffered data

2. The resource allocation is done in one or more iterations until all UEs are assigned maximum resources or all PDSCH resources are given out. This makes sure that UEs with high UE weight are considered in the first iteration. If some resources are still available after the first iteration, other UEs that have small UE weight (i.e. normalized round robin weight of these UEs is equal to 0 in the first iteration) will be considered in later iterations for resource allocation.

3. If UEs with high UE weight have a high traffic demand, it is possible that UEs with small UE weight will not be given any resources for the current slot. This is to make sure that resources given to UEs shall be more proportional to their UE weights.

4. The above WRR algorithm makes sure that either a UE is given 0 resource or it is given at least 1 PRB.

5. Normalized round robin weights for UEs are computed in single iteration, where every UE shall get atleast 1 token.

12539266:
DL resource assignment using FDM
For the UEs sorted in Step 2 specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23762\_replaced([11180478](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), perform the following in order to distribute resources to the UEs in the list:

*I* is the list of sorted UEs.\*

Let [image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] be the remaining RBGs across BWP i.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is the nominal RBG size of BWP i. See also 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of [image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]. **Note: Considering partial RBG(s), [image-4][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.001.png[/image\_path][/image-4] may need to be fine tuned such that the number of PRBs corresponding to [image-5][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.001.png[/image\_path][/image-5] RGBs is closest to the remaining available PRBs across BWP i, but not bigger than the remaining available PRBs across BWP i.**

Let N be the remaining PRBs available across the largest BWP.

For each UE u in the list while *I*>0, and N > 0*, gNB-DU shall do the following:*\*

1)   Calculate:

[image-6][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.005.png[/image\_path][/image-6]

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = true (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[image-7][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.006.png[/image\_path][/image-7]

Where:

[image-8][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.007.png[/image\_path][/image-8]: the maximum number of PRBs required to drain out the buffer for UE u, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-9][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.008.png[/image\_path][/image-9]

[image-10][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.009.png[/image\_path][/image-10]UE scheduling weight of UE u.

[image-11][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.010.png[/image\_path][/image-11]the nominal RBG size of the active BWP of UE *u.*

[image-12][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.011.png[/image\_path][/image-12]Remaining RBGs in the active BWP of the UE *u, with all the nested smaller BWPs.*

[image-13][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.012.png[/image\_path][/image-13]The RBGs based on the normalized scheduling weight of the UE u as determined in Step 1 specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32372\_replaced([11180861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85cfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-14][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.013.png[/image\_path][/image-14]

rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-15][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.014.png[/image\_path][/image-15]

[image-16][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.015.png[/image\_path][/image-16]the rank of UE u.

2)  If UE u  is given more than the maximum resources it can get (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall reduce [image-17][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.016.png[/image\_path][/image-17] such that UE u is given the maximum resources it can get. **Note 1: [image-18][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.017.png[/image\_path][/image-18] shall be at least 0.** Note 2: If there is available partial RBG and UE u is still given the maximum resources it can get based on [image-19][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.018.png[/image\_path][/image-19] which contain partial RBG, then partial RBG shall be allocated to UE u.\*\*\*\*

3)   If the UE u belongs to a group of UEs having the same beam [CB013505-A] and NRCELL/eirpControl/actEpic is disabled [End CB013505-A] , the allocated FD resources shall not exceed *rrmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp: the remaining number of FD resources that can be allocated to each scheduled beam (based on EIRP, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4349\_replaced([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and UE u shall be excluded from any further FD resource giveaways if FD scheduler already assigned the remaining number of FD resources for that beam.*

4)   Update N as follows:

N shall be reduced by the number of PRBs [image-20][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.019.png[/image\_path][/image-20]corresponds to.

Update remaining RBGs in the active BWP of the UE *u as follows:*

[image-21][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.020.png[/image\_path][/image-21]

Update remaining RBGs for other affected BWPs as follows:

If (BWP i is bigger than the active BWP of the UE u)

[image-22][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.021.png[/image\_path][/image-22] shall be reduced by the number of RBGs in BWP i which overlap with [image-23][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.022.png[/image\_path][/image-23]. Note: [image-24][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.023.png[/image\_path][/image-24]  may not need to be updated in some cases.

Example: the active BWP for UE1 and UE2 are BWP2, initially the remaining RBGs across BWP1:[image-25][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.024.png[/image\_path][/image-25] = 4 and the remaining RBGs across BWP2:[image-26][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.025.png[/image\_path][/image-26] = 4. When UE1 was allocated with 1 RBG, updated with [image-27][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.026.png[/image\_path][/image-27] = 3 and [image-28][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.027.png[/image\_path][/image-28] = 3. When UE2 was allocated with 1 RBG, updated with [image-29][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.028.png[/image\_path][/image-29] = 3 and [image-30][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.029.png[/image\_path][/image-30] = 2.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BWP1 | RBG1 |  | RBG1 |  | RBG2 |  | RBG3 |  |
| BWP2 | RBG1 | RBG2 | RBG3 | RBG4 |  |  |  |  |
| 1st UE | UE1 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| 2nd UE |  | UE2 |  |  |  |  |  |  |

Else if (BWP i is smaller than the active BWP of the UE u)

If (RBGs in BWP of UE u which are outside BWP i have been exhausted by UEs with active BWP bigger than BWP i, note: RBGs in BWP of UE u which are outside BWP i are always assumed to be used firstly for UEs with active BWP bigger than BWP i)

[image-31][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.030.png[/image\_path][/image-31] shall be reduced by the number of RBGs in BWP i which overlap with the allocated RBGs for UE u.

*end if*

Example: the active BWP for UE1 and UE2 are BWP1, initially the remaining RBGs across BWP1: [image-32][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.031.png[/image\_path][/image-32] = 4 and the remaining RBGs across BWP2:[image-33][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.032.png[/image\_path][/image-33] = 3. When UE1 was allocated with 1 RBG, updated with [image-34][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.033.png[/image\_path][/image-34] = 3 and [image-35][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.034.png[/image\_path][/image-35] = 3. When UE2 was allocated with 2 RBGs, updated with [image-36][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.035.png[/image\_path][/image-36] = 1 and [image-37][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.036.png[/image\_path][/image-37] = 2.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BWP1 | RBG1 |  | RBG2 |  | RBG3 |  | RBG4 |  |
| BWP2 | RBG1 | RBG2 | RBG3 |  |  |  |  |  |
| 1st UE |  |  |  |  |  |  | UE1 |  |
| 2nd UE |  |  | UE2 |  | UE2 |  |  |  |

end if

5)   Compute [image-38][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.037.png[/image\_path][/image-38] for the next UE in the list **as in Step 1 specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32372\_replaced([11180861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85cfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), with the updated N**. While computing, the scheduling weight for the UE u needs to be removed **since this UE u has been served in this iteration and shall no longer join the WRR further.**\*\*\*\*

6)   Compute [image-39][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.038.png[/image\_path][/image-39]for the next UE in the list, with the updated [image-40][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13712.039.png[/image\_path][/image-40]for this BWP. While computing, the scheduling weight for the UE u needs to be removed **since this UE u has been served in this iteration and shall no longer join the WRR further.**

7)   If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, after allocating FD resources to a UE, gNB-DU shall determine the number of layer PRBs consumed (**rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and use it to keep track of the used, as well as remaining layer PRB resources (**rrmNumUsedPdschLayerPrbPerSlot, **rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**\*\*\*\*

In the case that while allocating FD resources to a UE, the additional required layer PRB resources (can be calculated using **numReqLayerPrb described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is greater than the available layer PRB resources, gNB-DU shall reduce the number of FD resources allocated to the UE such that the layer PRB restriction is satisfied (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26597\_replaced([11214606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).(The maximum usable FD resources based on the currently available layer PRB resources and the UEs rank can be calculated using** numReqFdPrb described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The allocation shall be stopped for any further remaining UEs.\*\*\*\*

End for

The following check shall be executed: 'Skip MU-MIMO Enhanced' (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14422\_replaced([11181129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85ePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The output of the algorithm is the amount of FD resources (RBGs) each initial transmission UE will get (for all bearers).

If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True, for non-GBR UE which is paired with HARQ re-Tx UE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7431\_replaced([11144204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7arPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), corresponding RBG allocation due to DL pairing shall be considered as piggyback RBGs. The WRR algorithm doesn't need to consider these piggyback RBGs for simplification purpose. The output of the WRR algorithm, allowedRbgUe(i), will be used by DL pairing algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7518\_replaced([11142428](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7431\_replaced([11144204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7arPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

After algorithm stops, there is a possibility that some RBGs are unused which can be distributed to the most deserving candidate.

The deserving candidates are sorted as step2 specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23762\_replaced([11180478](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

gNB-DU shall assign these unused RBGs to the top ranked UE that has not yet reach maximum required PRBs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and, [CB013505-A] When NRCELL/eirpControl/actEpic is disabled [End CB013505-A], the resulting number of PRBs shall not exceed *rrmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp: the remaining number of FD resources that can be allocated to each scheduled beam (based on EIRP, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4349\_replaced([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, in the case that while assigning unused RBGs to a UE, the additional required layer PRB resources are greater than the available layer PRB resources, gNB-DU shall reduce the number of FD resources allocated to the UE such that the layer PRB restriction is satisfied, and the assignment shall be stopped for any further remaining UEs.*

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-5**  (ID: `12532554`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
UE Scheduling Metric Calculation

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12532556`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Calculation of FD Weighted Metric

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12822917`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12822917:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Let S be initialized as the set of all UEs that join the WRR in carrier j in slot t

For each UE i in S, FD weighted metric of the UE i, namely fdWeightedMetric[i,j, t] is given by:

[Before PR871233]

If fdSchedMetric[i,j,t] > avgFdSchedMetric[j,t]:

fdWeightedMetric[i,j,t] = 0.1\* rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst \* fdSchedMetric[i,j,t]

Else [End PR871233]

fdWeightedMetric[i,j,t] = fdSchedMetric[i,j,t]

with

* fdSchedMetric[i,j,t] is the FD scheduling metric of UE i in carrier j in slot t calculated as below[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
  $fdSchedMetric[i,j,t] = \frac{rrmCurrentDIUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[i,j,t]}{rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i, j,t-1]} \* rrmUeSchedWeight[i,t]$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/29ebcdf7-f3b6-4dab-b20e-a6d0b7a1e9da.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
* rrmUeSchedWeight[i,t] is the UE i scheduling weight. If NRBTS.actSliceAwareScheduler = FALSE, It shall be calculated as the sum of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight of its non-GBR bearers in DL that have data in the buffer in slot t. Else, it shall be calculated as the sum of combined weight (i.e., the product of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight and rrmRGWeight) of its non-GBR bearers that have data in the buffer in slot t, where rrmRgWeight is the RG weight of the resource group associated to the non-GBR bearer, see [11188798: Slice/RG weights](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).  When ext-gNB-DU receives from prime-gNB a notification to stop the specific non-GBR bearer(s) data scheduling of the ext-SCell, the DL FD-scheduler in ext-gNB-DU shall exclude the NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight for the specific non-GBR bearer(s) of the ext-SCell UE.

*Note:* *dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic bearers weight for the outer WRR shall be computed based on* *[11181155: Resource distribution to Non-GBR bearers in the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class of initial transmission UEs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).*

* rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[i,j,t] is the the current DL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in slot t in bytes per slot as the described in [11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).
* rrmAvgDlUeDataRate[i,j,t-1] is the DL average data rate of the UE as described in [Before PR855011] [11175317: Calculation of the average 5G UE DL Data Rate](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [PR855011] [12822886: Calculation of the average 5G UE DL Data Rate for FD Weighted Metric](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_7UcGGanNEfCrANRYxAlR5A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End PR855011]
* avgFdSchedMetric[j,t] is the average value of FD scheduling metric among all UEs that join the WRR in carrier j in slot t calculated as below:[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
  $avgFdSchedMetric[i, j, t] = \frac{\sum\_{i \in S} fdSchedMetric[i, j, t]}{\sum\_{i \in S} 1}$
  The equation defines the average of a metric called "fdSchedMetric" for specific indices i, j, and time t. It is calculated by summing the values of "fdSchedMetric" for all elements i belonging to a set S, and then dividing by the total number of elements in set S (which is represented by the sum of 1 for each element in S).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/3556d25e-3f34-47bd-80d6-0fabf22afd34.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
* The filter constant rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst shall be implemented by an R&D parameter. For an FDD cell, it shall be rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConstFdd, see [12533643: rdAvgDlUEDataRateFilterConstFdd](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_SBKmwBxiEfCQJ5daVHVkgg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). For a TDD FR1 cell, it shall be rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConstTddFr1, see [12552044: rdAvgDlUEDataRateFilterConstTddFr1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_JBPwoSTBEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12822886`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
**Calculation of the average 5G UE DL Data Rate for FD Weighted Metric**

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-11.0-5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12822887`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12822887:
DL resource assignment using FDM
The average data rate of the 5G UE in DL shall be calculated as below:

rrmAvgDlUeDataRate computation on a per carrier basis:

When NRBTS.NRCELL.actFdAlgoEnh=DL or DL\_UL (enabled)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is:
$rrm Avg DIUe Data Rate[i, j,t] = (1 - \frac{1}{\beta})^n \* rrm Avg DIUe Data Rate[i,j,t-n] + \frac{1}{\beta} \* instDIUeData Rate [i, j, t]$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/5115dd2f-0731-4c71-91d2-76b86ab04646.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula is:
rrm AvgDlUe Data Rate[i, j,t] = max (avg DlUe Data Rate Lower Threshold[i,j,t], rrmAvgDlUe Data Rate[i, j, t])[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/644f3fc7-941b-478d-bf54-c5744babf87c.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

with:

i: Index of the 5G UE

t: NR slot the calculation relates to

n:  rrmNumSlotSinceLastDlSched

avgDlUeDataRateLowerThreshold[i,j,t]: The lower bound on the avgDataRate in DL for the UE i in carrier j in slot t. It shall be calculated as rdMinExpectedBwSharePerUe \* rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[*i,j,**t*]. Please refer to  [12811470: rdMinExpectedBwSharePerUe](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_E6Q9oKQ4EfCBvZf8dodgmQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for rdMinExpectedBwSharePerUe and [11175309: Definition of the current 5G UE DL Data Rate](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha definition.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract representation of the letter "B". The letter is rendered in a gradient of colors, transitioning from a light beige on the left to shades of blue, white, and purple in the center, and finally to a dark black on the right. The overall impression is one of soft, diffused light and color.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13056.002.png[/image\_path][/image-2]:   rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst: The filter constant rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConst shall be implemented by an R&D parameter. For an FDD cell, it shall be rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConstFdd, see [12533643: rdAvgDlUEDataRateFilterConstFdd](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_SBKmwBxiEfCQJ5daVHVkgg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). For a TDD FR1 cell, it shall be rdAvgDlUeDataRateFilterConstTddFr1, see [12552044: rdAvgDlUEDataRateFilterConstTddFr1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_JBPwoSTBEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

instDlUeDataRate[i,j,t]:The instantaneous DL data rate of a 5G UE i**, in bytes per slot, which is valid in NR slot t for the carrier j, shall be evaluated as function of 5G current UE data rate of the UE along with the FD scheduling cost due to the scheduling of the UE in the slot. In the slot when the UE is not scheduled, it shall be 0. The instantaneous DL UE data rate of the UE i on carrier j during the NR slot t calculated as below:**

When NRBTS.NRCELL.actFdAlgoEnh=DL or DL\_UL (enabled) [Before PR864769]

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is:
`instDlUe Data Rate[i, j,t] = current DlUe Data RateWithFdCost[i, j, t]`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/a02e23e6-b055-4f9b-bf8c-f2d94f423dbe.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is:
`currentDlUeDataRateWithFdCost[i, j, t] = rrmCurrentDlUeDataRateBytesPerSlotalpha[i, j, t] * allocFdResources[i, j, t] / usableFdResource[I, j, t]`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/0c3b39c6-2635-425a-b71f-2427fe03c845.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

[PR864769]

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is:
`instDlUe Data Rate[i, j, t] = max(current DlUeData RateWithFdCost[i,j,t], rrmDlSumOfTransport BlockSize[i,j,t])`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/20d91ed2-c15a-4b04-b0c0-8372d0eefc42.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a stylized font. The formula is:
`currentDlUeDataRateWithFdCost[i, j,t] = rrmCurrentDlUeDataRateBytesPerSlot[i, j, t] * allocFdResources[i, j,t]/usableFdResources[j, t]`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/d34cd18b-72b4-4d91-aa9b-1ac6e6223d8c.png[/image\_path][/image-6]

[End PR864769]

with

fdSchedulingCost[i,j,t] is the cost of scheduling the UE i in FD scheduler on carrier j in NR slot t calculated as below:

[Before PR864769] rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot\_alpha[i,j,t] is the the current DL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in slot t in bytes per slot as the described in [11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).[PR864769]

rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] is the the current DL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in slot t in bytes per slot as the described in [11175309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuiCuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).[End PR864769]

allocFdResources[i,j,t] is the number of allocated PDSCH FD resources for the UE i in the carrier j in NR slot t.

usableFdResources[i,j,t] is the maximum number of available PDSCH FD resources in the carrier j in NR slot t.

For the initialization of the exponential filter, the average data rate shall be derived from the scheduling conditions in the previous NR slot in the carrier j.

The exponential filter shall be initialized the 1st time that the PF metric has to be computed after the UE setup.

The exponential filter shall be re-initialized in the following cases:

* when the UE re-enters the pre-scheduler candidate set with new data in DRB buffer(coming back from nullBuffer state) after staying out for longer than MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL.avgDataRateReinitProhibitTimer; Reinitialization is done at most only once after the UE returns to nonNullBuffer state from nullBuffer state.
* with each change in the set of the non-GBR bearers and hence with each change of the overall schedulWeight.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12**  (ID: `12483324`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Exhaustive Scheduling for RAT1

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `12487237`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Overview

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12487239`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12487239:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Unlike Weighted Round Robin (WRR), where resources are allocated to users in proportion to their scheduling weights (refer to [11179805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lYBBYPvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), exhaustive scheduling (ES) assigns PRBs to a user until its buffer is fully drained, before moving on to the next user or the remaining PRBs get exhausted. Exhaustive Scheduling is exclusive to WRR and cannot be used in combination with WRR.  
Exhaustive Scheduling is specific to RAT1 and only for FR1,

DL ES activation/deactivation:

* DL ES shall only be activated when NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to 1 (DL) or 3 (DLAndUL).
* DL ES shall be deactivated if NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to a value other than 1 (DL) or 3 (DLAndUL).

Following flowchart depicts the ES for SU and MU eligible users.

The following folder has the flow diagrams for ES: [flow diagrams](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB014500%20-%20CB014999/CFAM/CB014861%20Exhaustive%20Scheduling%20in%20downlink/CP2/flow%20diagrams?csf=1&web=1&e=3E05aU)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a scheduling process. The flowchart begins with an input step labeled "Input list of UEs sorted per BWP and TD metrics." From this input, a decision diamond asks, "If Remaining Prbs And still UEs in the list, whose buffer have not been drained out."
If the condition in the diamond is met, the flow splits into two paths, labeled "SU" and "MU."
The "SU" path leads to a process box labeled "Perform SU user Exhaustive scheduling."
The "MU" path leads to another decision diamond asking, "If MU eligible UES And Remainin B. Prbs." If this condition is met, the flow proceeds to a process box labeled "Perform MU user Exhaustive scheduling."
Both the "Perform SU user Exhaustive scheduling" and "Perform MU user Exhaustive scheduling" processes, as well as the "No" branch from the "If MU eligible UES And Remainin B. Prbs" diamond, converge to a process box labeled "Perform sub-area assignment."
The flowchart ends with a terminal symbol, indicating the completion of the process. The overall structure suggests a conditional scheduling approach that considers both SU and MU users based on remaining resources and buffer status.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-04280956.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12.0-2**  (ID: `12487764`)

Algorithm

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12487768`)
