Resource distribution

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12.0-2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13322410`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Inputs:

* U: List of UEs selected based on TD metric order, including both SU and MU eligible users. Refer to [11174242: Request class drbTraffic](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and [11175151: Definition of per UE and per carrier scheduling metric for DL](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for calculation of TD metric for DRB traffic
* N: Number of Remaining PRBs available across all the PDSCH sub area(s) from the largest BWP, if multiple PDSCH sub-areas exist.

# Algorithm:

1. Initialization

---

* [CB015725-C]If rdDlEsEnh(refer to [13365298: rdDlEsEnh](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_XH4FAfZzEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is equal to false, firstly sort UEs in increasing order of their respective BWP sizes. [End CB015725-C]
* If CPU overload control conditions specified in [12436592](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_buMlQQf0EfCT35inaBGo7g) allow then gNB-DU shall create Initial virtual UEs and update their TD metrics (See [13343587](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_m8n-Md8cEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* Sort UEs in list U purely in decreasing order of their TD metrics (i.e priority/sub-priority). For the rest of the algorithm a UE can be seen as a vUE with more than one constituent UE or a degenerate vUE with just one constituent UE.
* Note:

  + There could be users (within request class criticalDrbTraffic, drbTraffic and dlMacCe) like NBR users with positive token and dlMacCe + non-GBR bearer from request class drbTraffic having data, who are either SU-only or MU eligible:
  + When the user is SU-only, partial resource reservation and PDSCH sub-area assignment are performed according to the prioritized resource allocated in [13404773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_sziNcgyeEfGu_4itz1o3RA) for critical nonGBR UE, and the positive token bucket for NBR category and Mac-CE due to it's higher priority traffic class.
  + When the user is MU-eligible, immediate PDSCH sub area assignment shall not be made, however largest PDSCH sub area shall be considered as initial PDSCH sub area but no pinning shall be made with start PRB. Rationale, is that the user could be potentially pairable with other users of DRB priority class in the future during the ES procedure as described below.
  + For both SU-only and MU-eligible cases, when a UE with an NBR bearer having a positive token enters the ES, it shall recalculate a temporary PF metric by applying the normal non-GBR weight (instead of the NBR weight, as referenced [11175240: Scheduling weight for non-GBR bearer](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) to be used solely for sorting the UEs. Rationale: The NBR weight is significantly larger than that of a typical non-GBR bearer, which causes UEs with an NBR bearer with positive token to consistently appear at the top of the UE list. As a result, other UEs have fewer opportunities to be scheduled. The issue is more prominent if the NBR users in SU-only.
  + When initiating the MU-MIMO pairing process in a given slot, the gNB-DU scheduler gives priority to UEs with critical non-GBR traffic. The scheduler shall select an eligible UE with critical non-GBR data as the root UE to begin the pairing process. After the pairing of critical user is complete, scheduler shall proceed to consider remaining un-paired UEs with NBR traffic as the next candidates for root UEs
  + For NBR user, which is MU-eligible, irrespective of the temporary PF metric calculated as described above, gNB-DU shall consider this NBR user as the root UE during the start of MU pairing in this slot. Note that the MU pairing with users belonging to higher traffic priority class must be completed already at this stage. Only when MU pairing with NBR user is finished, gNB-DU shall begin the MU pairing with other non-GBR user(s) as root UE (if present)
  + For users with Mac-CE to transmit and a non-GBR bearer in the drbTraffic request class that have data to transmit, the same scheduling steps described above shall be applied. Note that, unlike NBR users, the PF metric is not recalculated..
  + Only when MU pairing with critical nonGBR user and NBR user are finished, gNB-DU shall begin the MU pairing with other non-GBR user as root UE (if present).
  + For UEs under large buffer OR pairing group categories, the TD metric uses only the weighted PF metric from [11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_KXO7ofvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)to determine the UE order, without considering sub-priority introduced in [11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_EXHMQfvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
  + Denote the sorted list as U\*.
  + Set Remaining\_PRBs = N.
  + Main Reservation and Distribution Loop

  ---
* ### For each UE j = 1 to |U\*|:

  + If UE j is not paired with other UEs or an (initial) vUE and PRBs are available PRBs are available in UE j's BWP (Remaining\_PRBs > 0), go to Step 1; else, pick the next UE in the list.
  + ### Step 1: PRB Distribution
  + If the UE j is a MU eligible UE:
  + Perform the virtual UE j creation with other MU eligible users from the list (Refer to [11144070: Virtual UE construction](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). Note: The MU candidate list is already created before this step based on whether MU pairing is allowed in this slot. Refer to [11142605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_hyNfsvvLEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
  + If NRCELL.actCarPoolLaneMuPairingES is set to 'DL' or 'DLAndUL', the UE j can be a 'initial' virtual UE[i,m..} with constituent users {i,m..}.
  + Calculate PRBjmax which gives the maximum number of PRBs required to drain the buffer for UE j using MCS based on MU-SINR. Do the same for other constituent UEs. Note: For simplicity, with each virtual UE created, consider the index ‘j’ can be interchangeably used to indicate a virtual UE. Final PRB reservation to the virtual UE j is given by PRBj*.*
  + If the PRB requirements of not all UEs in the virtual UE j are satisfied and PRBs are still available, further MU pairing shall be performed. Create a new virtual UE j’ which includes only those UEs from virtual UE j whose requirement is not yet satisfied. When UE j is an 'initial' virtual UE{i,m,..} it can so happen that, while resource distribution to users in the (initial) vUE j, one of the constituent user ‘m’, gets drained out of its buffer completely thereby terminating the (initial) vUE j resource allocation, making the remaining yet unsatisfied user(s) in (initial) vUE j, to pair with other (initial) (v)UEs or other candidate UEs, which otherwise were not able to pair due to the presence of the user ‘m’ that just got drained. See the following for summary of each virtual UE creation:

    - If other pairable candidates with pending buffer exists in the list, then
    - Run the MU pairing algorithm to identify other MU candidate(s) k to add to virtual UE j’. In case of initial virtual UE with the remaining (yet unsatisfied) users, k can be another (initial) vUE such that either all the users of (initial) vUE k can pair with remaining users in vUE j, or just one user from the (initial) vUE k can pair, or other yet non-paired MU eligible users in the tbsList can pair.
    - Each time a virtual UE is created, recalculate the sub-priority of the vUE (See 13347880) and insert the vUE in the sorted list. Note: in case of existence of one or more, yet unsatisfied user(s) in the vUEj'/j'..,  to achieve contiguous allocation for the users, there shall be no need to resort these vUEs. Only when they are completely different UEs, forming a vUE j'/j'.. then resorting should happen for these vUEs.
    - The constituent UEs from virtual UE j’ are allocated the maximum possible PRBs to drain their buffer or remaining available PRBs whichever is exhausted earlier. Remove the UE(s) whose PRB requirement got satisfied from further pairing. For all constituent UEs in each virtual UE and the maximum total resource allocation for each subsequent virtual UE j/j’..given by PRBjmax, PRBj'max,…refer to PRB allocation rules defined in [11144618: PDSCH allocation with RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8oyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). Note: For each constituent UE in the virtual UEj/j’.., the required PRBs to drain out the remaining buffer shall be calculated using MU-SINR based MCS and shall be included in the calculation for the above.
    - For each finalized virtual UE j/j’.. calculate PRB*j as per Equation-1.*
    - Go to step 2
* Else if the UE j is SU UE:
* PRBjmax which gives the maximum number of PRBs required to drain the buffer for UE j using SU-SINR based MCS. In TRS slot, if NRCELL.act4prbAllocDlTrsSlotsForRat1 is set to True and NRBTS.actSubTRSRateMatch is set to True and ueHasFewDataInBuffer (see [11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) is not True, then PRBjmax shall be equal to max(4PRBs, PRBjmax).
* Calculate *PRB*jas per Equation-1\*\*
* Go to step 2
* Equation-1

PRB*j= max (min (PRBjmax, PRB\_granularity*floor (nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea/ PRB granularity), PRB\_granularity*floor (rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubAreaPinned /PRB\_granularity), PRB\_granularity*floor (SumOf (rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea)/ PRB\_granularity)), PRB\_granularity).\*

PRB*j*= PRB\_granularity*ceil (PRB*j/PRB\_granularity)\*\*\*

Where:

* rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea is the remaining PRBs in the PDSCH sub-area for the active BWP of the UE j.
* nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea is the remaining PRBs in the PDSCH subarea which includes the unused dedicated PRBs from the PDSCH subarea (rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea) and unused CORESET #0 (or X with X>0) PRBs in the PDSCH subarea (rmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea) across the largest BWP. When there are several PDSCH sub-areas, the maximum of the nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea would give the largest PDSCH sub-area available across the largest BWP.
* rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubAreaPinned is the remaining PRBs in the PDSCH sub-area for the active BWP of the UE j (rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea) if the UE j is already pinned to this sub-area before this step.
* For rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea, rmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea and rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea See [11176110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_QvsSgPvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* PRBjmax is the maximum number of PRBs required to drain the buffer for UE j using SU-SINR based MCS.
* PRB*j*is the number of PRBs to be reserved for UE j.
* Note: The result should make sure that the resources granted should not exceed the remaining PRBs in the BWP in the PDSCH subarea.
* Note: For PRB\_granularity definition please refer [11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_WpZoIPvfEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* Step-2: Sub-area assignment

Assign the SU UE j or virtual UE j to respective subareas. For virtual UE j/j’.., determine the PDSCH subarea if all the UEs in the virtual UE are satisfied, or finished with their allocation, e.g., due to max sub-area size. For PDSCH sub area assignment rules please refer to [11176637: PDSCH sub-area assignment rules for non MU-MIMO users](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for SU and [11142428: DL pairing algorithm in FD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for MU.

[CB015725-E]If multiple sub-areas provide an equivalent maximum PRB allocation (including cases where all can fully grant the required resources), the gNB shall prefer the sub-area that contains the highest amount of exclusive resources for the UE's respective BWP, refer to [13476994: PDSCH sub-area selection based on most exclusivity criteria](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_JDFX0DyAEfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).[End CB015725-E]

Note: If there exists a user 'j' for which the gNB-DU has already assigned a sub-area prior to this step, any subsequent PRB reservation shall be performed within the same previously assigned sub-area. This applies, for example, to (a) a vUE with a retransmission user, or (b) a user with multiple DRBs, where at least one DRB has had PRBs reserved before the resource distribution in this step. Note: if rdSubareaReselectActForMultiBearerUes ([12563046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ral0gisFEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is enabled, for b) there can be reselection of PDSCH subarea if found productive to avoid potential loss of PRBs in any subarea if exists.

* Step-3: Update resource availability
* rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea = rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea - PRB*j. for the active BWP of the UE j in the selected PDSCH sub-area.*
* Update rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea -= PRB*j, i.e the remaining PRBs that do not overlap with the reserved PRBs in all BWPs other than the above if needed, in case of several dedicated BWPs. See [11176627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_UBrnsPvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Note that, rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea could be the rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubAreaPinned.*
* nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea -= PRB*j. Note that the PRBs deducted can be from the remaining dedicated PRBs or from the remaining CORESET#0 (or X with X>0) PRBs within the PDSCH subarea. Also note that the PRBs deducted from the CORESET#0 (or X with X>0) part within a PDSCH sub-area or BWP within a PDSCH sub-area is counted only once in the above equations.*
* Remaining\_PRBs -=  PRB*j*
* Go to Step-1 for the next UE

# Additional Notes:

1. Resource constraints:

---

* If the UE j is a UE with valid sub band BWVs ([11156776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_jIlnUPvMEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), belonging to a group of UEs having the same beam, PRBj should not exceed rrmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp, the remaining number of FD resources that can be allocated to each scheduled beam else the UE j should be excluded from the resource distribution and take necessary actions (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4349\_replaced([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If the UE j with valid subband BWVs ([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_bgJkYPvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is excluded from the FD resource distribution due to EIRP control (number of FD resources exceeding rrmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp), gNB-DU shall mark the UE as "WB SRS constraint UE" and start/restart rdWbSrsConstraintTimer for this UE (see [11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_bgJkYPvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For virtual UE j, the above is applicable for all constituent UEs.
* Augmentation for low PRB allocation

---

* For UEs with PRBj < rdMinPrbForRat1Alloc PRBs (See [11193977](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_xo-nQPvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), if the sum of rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea > totalMaxNeededNonGbrRes, then calculate the available PRBs for augmenting the granted resources for such UEs (given by availablePrbHeadroom = rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea - totalMaxNeededNonGbrRes) which would be used to adjust the deficit, by adding to the existing PRBj extra PRBs, maximum up to the rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb PRBs for a non-GBR High Reliability bearer, otherwise rdMinPrbForRat1Alloc PRBs. If there are multiple BWPs, then availablePrbHeadroom = rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea – totalMaxNeededNonGbrRes. Each time some PRBs are distributed to such UEs, the availablePrbHeadroom is updated by deducting the exact number of extra PRBs granted to the user.
* Ensure that the augmentation does not exceed the rrmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp. In the event of which the augmentation is abandoned, the UE(s) would continue to use the original allocation.
* For the slots with PRB granularity < rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb PRBs for a non-GBR High Reliability bearer, otherwise < rdMinPrbForRat1Alloc PRBs, the algorithm must make sure PRB granularity be equal to rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb PRBs for a non-GBR High Reliability bearer, otherwise rdMinPrbForRat1Alloc PRBs. rdMinPrbForRat1AllocHrDrb shall be applicable to all bearers configured with High reliability profile, that would contend for resource distribution i.e bearers with DRB traffic which includes MC-nGBR(5QI70), normal non-GBR (including congestion deprioritized GBR), large buffer non-GBR and NBR excessive data or the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class and/or deprioritized GBR/DC-GBR data.
* Layer PRB allocation check
* If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, after allocating FD resources to a UE, gNB-DU shall determine the number of layer PRBs consumed (rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation described in [11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_bXYimvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and use it to keep track of the used, as well as remaining layer PRB resources (rrmNumUsedPdschLayerPrbPerSlot, rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot described in [11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_bXYimvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* In case that while allocating FD resources to a UE, the additional required layer PRB resources (can be calculated using numReqLayerPrb described in [11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_bXYimvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is greater than the available layer PRB resources, gNB-DU shall reduce the number of FD resources allocated to the UE such that the layer PRB restriction is satisfied ([11214606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_bbto8fvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (The maximum usable FD resources based on the currently available layer PRB resources and the UEs rank can be calculated using numReqFdPrb described in [11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_bXYimvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The allocation shall be stopped for any further remaining UEs.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12487786`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Redistribution of unused FD resources

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12487787`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
After the subarea assignment has been completed, there may be unused PRBs in the respective subareas. These PRBs can be redistributed to the most deserving candidates.

The following rules apply to only SU mode users:

* gNB-DU shall mark the UEs which have not been fully satisfied with the reserved PRBs to drain their total buffered data.
* gNB-DU shall redistribute unused PRBs according to the UE list sorted in [12487768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Rj_cYBBKEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Rj_cYBBKEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9") for the marked unsatisfied UEs.

Rationale: If rdSubareaReselectActForMultiBearerUes ([12563046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ral0gisFEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is enabled, then, in a multi-bear scenario (e.g., GBR + non-GBR scenarios), a UE may be re-selected to a more optimal subarea that better satisfies its buffer draining requirements. This re-selection can create a PRB hole in the original subarea, which can be reused for PRB redistribution among other UEs with unsatisfied PRB allocations.

gNB-DU shall assign these unused PRBs to these UEs provided that the checks defined in [11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), are satisfied and the resulting number of PRBs not exceed rmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp, i.e the remaining number of FD resources that can be allocated to each scheduled beam.

[CB014081-C]Note that gNB-DU shall perform this step before the "dynamicswitch" to RAT0 functionality, if applicable to this slot and with allowable users existing among the scheduled users, is performed. For "dynamicwitch" to RAT0 for initial transmission users see [11181383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).[End CB014081-C]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12.0-3**  (ID: `13343316`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Output

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-12.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13343318`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
The output of the algorithm is

* the amount of FD resources (PRBs)**each initial transmission UE will get (for all bearers)**
* the PDSCH sub-area assigned to each UE with initial transmission.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-13**  (ID: `11181129`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Skip MU-MIMO Enhanced

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-13.0-2**  (ID: `11181140`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
If all of the following conditions are fulfilled:

* NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh = True and
* NRCELL.actDlInterferenceAvoidance = True and
* [PR836556] If NRCELL/actRat1Alloc=False [End PR836556]

5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Resource distribution to Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs' algorithm has stopped, because all UEs received maximum amount of resource they can get

[PR836556]

Else

Evaluation of whether MU-MIMO pairing shall be performed in the slot based on the condition defined in [11142428: DL pairing algorithm in FD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), resulted in MU pairing to be not allowed

[End PR836556]and

* '**[CB008927-P] If NRCELL/actRat1Alloc=False,** [End CB008927-P]\*\*\*\*

Virtual UE construction and RBG allocation with reTx UEs as roots' step in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7518\_replaced([11142428](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'DL pairing algorithm in FD' resulted in no paired UEs

then skip MU-MIMO Enhanced flag shall be set to true.

Otherwise, it shall be set to false.

Note:

1. **[CB008927-P] If NRCELL/actRat1Alloc=False,**[End CB008927-P]\*\*\*\*

If both actDlMuMimoEnh and actDlInterferenceAvoidance are enabled, then MU-MIMO Enhanced algorithm will be only executed, when there was no enough RBGs to fulfill UEs needs in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Resource distribution to Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs' algorithm. Otherwise, CB008955 DL interference avoidance algorithm will be applied.

**[CB008927-P] If NRCELL/actRat1Alloc=True,**

if both actDlMuMimoEnh and actDlInterferenceAvoidance are enabled, and [PR836556] skip MU-MIMO Enhanced flag = False, i.e [End PR836556] in a slot if MU-MIMO pairing is made, then MU-MIMO Enhanced algorithm will be only executed i.e no CB008955 DL interference avoidance related algorithms are applied. Otherwise, CB008955 DL interference avoidance algorithm will be applied.

**[End CB008927-P]**

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-14**  (ID: `11181145`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Scaling of Weighted Round Robin weight  for DL CA using DPA

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-14.0-1**  (ID: `11181150`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
For CA UEs where the  increase of the UE’s PF metric for new transmission in the time domain scheduler by pre-defined scaling factor according 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8329\_replaced([11175264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuhbrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) has been applied in the current scheduling cycle, the UE weight to be applied in the Weighted  Round Robin algorithm for  PRB allocation according 5G\_UP\_ ALG\_4504 shall be multiplied by rdDpaWrrWeightFd, as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8835\_replaced([11193302](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvr5QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The scope of the scaling defined in this requirement ("only Scells" or "PCell and Scells") is controlled by the parameter rdDpaActPfboostPcell according 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8884\_replaced([11193312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvr5QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If the TD metric boost according 5G\_ALG\_8329 is not applied, i.e. , WPf, DPA = 1, then dDpaActPfboostPcell=1 shall be assumed.

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-15**  (ID: `11181155`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to Non-GBR bearers in the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class of initial transmission UEs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-15.0-5**  (ID: `12633197`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
[CB014861-A]If DL ES is disabled(see [12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ)) then,[End CB014861-A]

For a given UE within the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class, the overall weight of its non-GBR DRBs before multiplication by the slice weight (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23761\_replaced([11179826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), shall be assigned a weight of *rdDtxUeSchedulWeight (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10161\_replaced([11193406](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvsgV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the WRR algorithm for the PRB distribution (described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*

When ext-L2-PS receives from prime-gNB a notification to stop the specific non-GBR bearer(s) data scheduling, and calculates the overall weight of its non-GBR DRBs, the DL FD-scheduler in ext-L2-PS shall exclude those non-GBR DRB(s) from that calculation for an ext-SCell UE.

Note: As such, the weight of each of the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic bearers of a UE could be derived as: *rdDtxUeSchedulWeight /* numDtxBearers.\*\*

Where *numDtxBearers is the number of non-GBR DRB bearers within the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class with buffered data for that UE, not including bearers which belong to an* *RG of 0 RG weight  when* NRBTS/NRCELL/actEnhDtxOptimizedScheduler is set to true.\*\*\*\*

[CB014861-A]Else then,

For a given UE within the dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class with non-GBR DRBs, the PRB distribution shall be done in exhaustive manner as described in [12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ).

When ext-L2-PS receives from prime-gNB a notification to stop the specific non-GBR bearer(s) data scheduling, the DL FD-scheduler in ext-L2-PS shall exclude these bearers for an ext-SCell UE.

[End CB014861-A]

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-16**  (ID: `11181182`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution to Non-GBR of Initial Transmission UEs with Several Dedicated BWPs per Cell

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-16.0-4**  (ID: `12633208`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

5G-L2-PS shall distribute resources [CB014861-A] through Exhaustive Scheduling if enabled at per UE per BWP basis (see [12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ)), otherwise [End CB014861-A] to the round robin weights as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CB014861-A] or [12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ) [End CB014861-A] shall be conducted several times in the following manner.

5G-L2-PS shall distribute 1st BWP exclusive resources (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4507\_replaced([11181223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) until exhausted, before allocating smaller BWP resources, only to 1st BWP UEs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

5G-L2-PS shall distribute kth BWP exclusive resources (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4507\_replaced([11181223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) until exhausted, before allocating smaller BWP resources, only to (k-i)th BWP UEs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) where iϵ{0,1, 2, …, k-1}, kϵ{1, 2, …, n-1, n}~~, and (k-1) > 1~~.

5G-L2-PS shall only distribute nth BWP resources (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4507\_replaced([11181223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to nth largest BWP UEs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In the example for n = 3, that is:

5G-L2-PS shall distribute 1st BWP exclusive resources (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4507\_replaced([11181223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) until exhausted, before allocating smaller BWP resources, only to 1st BWP UEs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

5G-L2-PS shall distribute 2nd BWP exclusive (versus 3rd BWP) resources (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4507\_replaced([11181223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) until exhausted, before allocating smaller BWP resources, only to 1st and 2nd BWP UEs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

5G-L2-PS shall only distribute 3rd BWP resources (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4507\_replaced([11181223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to 3rd BWP UEs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1813\_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: For information, when DL PRB Pooling is activated, number of available FD resources(RBGs) to distribute to UEs for ith BWP (i ϵ {1, …, n}) exclusive resources may be 0 because number of available FD resources(RBGs) for ith BWP (i ϵ {1, …, n}) are restricted to the total amount of DL PRBs granted to the cell (numAllocCellPrb) by the BBRM (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4290\_replaced([11177227](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuto6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), iterations follow same procedure with 1st iteration starts from ith BWP exclusive resources (i ϵ {1, …, n}) which has number of available FD resources(RBGs) to distribute. **Only amount of available FD resources(RBGs) is distributed to UEs here, positions of distributed RBGs for a UE in a dedicated BWP are decided later and all positions in dedicated BWPs can be used for UEs.**

NOTE :

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE,  number of available FD resources(RBGs) to distribute to UEs for ith BWP (i ϵ {1, …, n}) exclusive resources may be 0 because number of available FD resources(RBGs) for ith BWP (i ϵ {1, …, n}) are restricted to the total amount of Layer PRBs granted to the cell (**rrmMaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by the BBRM. iterations follow same procedure with 1st iteration starts from ith BWP exclusive resources (i ϵ {1, …, n}) which has number of available FD resources(RBGs) to distribute.** Only amount of available FD resources(RBGs) is distributed to UEs here. The distributed PRBs for each UE are used to determine the consumed layer PRB resources (**rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and use it to keep track of the used, as well as remaining layer PRB resources (**rrmNumUsedPdschLayerPrbPerSlot, **rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**. Positions of distributed RBGs for a UE in a dedicated BWP are decided later and all positions in dedicated BWPs can be used for UEs.***\******\***

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-17**  (ID: `11181204`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource distribution with EIRP restriction

---

**3.29.7.2.0-10.0-17.0-3**  (ID: `12539245`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
[CB013505-A] When NRCELL/eirpControl/actEpic is disabled [End CB013505-A] L2-PS shall distribute the available resources considering EIRP restriction.

For a group of UEs having the same beam, the sum of allocated FD resources should not exceed *rrmNumberOfRemainingRbFromEirp**,**the remaining number of FD resources that can be allocated to each scheduled beam (based on EIRP, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4349\_replaced([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and all those UEs should be excluded from any further FD resource giveaways if FD scheduler already assigned the remaining number of FD resources for that beam.*\*\*\*\*

#### 3.29.7.2.0-11 DL resource assignment using FDM FD Resource Groups with Several Dedicated BWPs per Cell (ID: 11181223)

**3.29.7.2.0-11.0-4**  (ID: `11181257`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11181257:
DL resource assignment using FDM
5G\_UP\_ALG\_1811\_replaced([11110797](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpHXP_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) defines up to n dedicated BWPs per cell.
 
A ith BWP, let it have the FD resources PRBs/RBGs 0 to N\_i. Where N\_i > N\_(i+1), with i ϵ {1, …, n}.
For i<n, the ith BWP exclusive resources shall be defined by N\_(i+1) + 1 to N\_i, where for the nth BWP, the exclusive resources are from 0 to N\_n
Example in figure 1.
 
 
 
Where N\_top is the highest PRB, common for all dedicated BWPs.
 
NOTE: These resources can only be scheduled to (i-k)th BWP UEs, where k < i and k ϵ {0, 1, …, n-1}.
NOTE: The nth BWP UE can only be scheduled in the nth BWP. The ith BWP UE can also be scheduled in the nth BWP.
 
 
In the example of n = 3,
A 1st BWP, let it have the FD resources PRBs/RBGs 0 to N\_1, a 2nd BWP, let it have FD resources PRBs/RGBs 0 to N\_2, where N\_2 < N\_1, and a 3rd BWP, let it have FD resources PRBs/RGBs 0 to N\_3, where N\_3 < N\_2.
1st BWP exclusive resources shall be defined by PRBs/RGBs N\_2+1 to N\_1.
Note: These resources can only be scheduled to 1st BWP UEs.
2nd BWP exclusive resources shall be defined by PRBs/RGBs N\_3+1 to N\_2.
Note: These resources can only be scheduled to 1st or 2nd BWP UEs.
3rd BWP resources shall be defined by PRBs/RGBs 0 to N\_3.
Note: 3rd BWP UEs can only be scheduled here. 1st or 2nd BWP UEs can also be scheduled here.
 
Note:  For information, when DL PRB Pooling is activated, number of available FD resources(PRBs/RBGs) to distribute to UEs for ith BWP (i ϵ {1, …, n}) exclusive resources may be 0 because number of available FD resources(PRBs/RBGs) for ith BWP (i ϵ {1, …, n}) are restricted to the total amount of DL PRBs granted to the cell (numAllocCellPrb) by the BBRM (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4290\_replaced([11177227](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuto6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). All positions in dedicated BWPs can be used for UEs.
NOTE :
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE,  number of available FD resources(RBGs) to distribute to UEs for ith BWP (i ϵ {1, …, n}) exclusive resources may be 0 because number of available FD resources(RBGs) for ith BWP (i ϵ {1, …, n}) are restricted by the total amount of  Layer PRBs granted to the cell (rrmMaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by the BBRM. The distributed PRBs for each UE are used to determine the consumed layer PRB resources (rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and use it to keep track of the used, as well as remaining layer PRB resources (rrmNumUsedPdschLayerPrbPerSlot, rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  All positions in dedicated BWPs can be used for UEs.[CB010630-E]This constraint is valid also for PRB based RAT1 allocation scheme (NRCELL.actRat1Alloc=True)[End CB010630-E]
Figure 1.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a bar chart illustrating the relationship between three "Bandwidth Parts" (BWPs) labeled BWP1, BWP2, and BWP3. Each BWP is represented by a vertical bar of decreasing height, indicating a hierarchical or nested structure.
The tallest bar, labeled "BWP 1" and associated with "N\_1", represents the first and largest bandwidth part. To its right, a bracket indicates "BWP1 exclusive (N\_2+1;N1)", suggesting a portion of N\_1 that is exclusively allocated to BWP1.
The second bar, labeled "BWP 2" and associated with "N\_2", is shorter than BWP1. It is positioned within the conceptual space of BWP1. A bracket to its right denotes "BWP2 exclusive (N\_3+1;N2)", indicating a portion of N\_2 that is exclusively for BWP2. Below this, it states "Available also for BWP1", implying that BWP2 can also utilize resources from BWP1.
The shortest bar, labeled "BWP 3" and associated with "N\_3", is the smallest. It is positioned within the conceptual space of BWP2 and, by extension, BWP1. A bracket to its right signifies "BWP3 exclusive (0;N3)", indicating a portion of N\_3 that is exclusively for BWP3. Below this, it states "Available also for BWP1 and BWP2", meaning BWP3 can utilize resources from both BWP1 and BWP2.
The visual representation suggests that BWP1 encompasses BWP2, and BWP2 encompasses BWP3, with each subsequent BWP having exclusive portions and also being available for use by the preceding BWPs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13735.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-12 DL resource assignment using FDM Determining the maximum number of FD resources which initial transmission UEs can get (ID: 11181264)

**3.29.7.2.0-12.0-7**  (ID: `11181300`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
For all initial transmission UEs in the to-be-scheduled list, the maximum number of FD resources a UE can get for these bearers is the minimum number of FD resources that can contain the amount of buffered data for all bearers. **Exception: For initial transmission UEs with SRB traffic the maximum  number of FD resources the UE can get is the minimum number of FD resources that can contain the amount of buffered data for SRB traffic alone and does not consider any other bearer the UE might have.**

Note: The above calculation for the maximum number of FD resources facilitates multiplexing with multiple bearers, excluding signalling bearers as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4537\_replaced([11185465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvTew_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Though multiplexing with SRB traffic is not considered in this step during resource distribution, if resouce allocation is as per RAT0 with FD resource granularity of one RBG, the unused PRBs within the RBG(s) allocated for the user with SRB traffic can be considered for multiplexing later during the FD allocation phase as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4537\_replaced([11185465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvTew_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When resource allocation is as per RAT1 (NIDD.actRat1Alloc = True) please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) regarding SRB and DRB traffic.

This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4622\_replaced([11192072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the proposed MCS *rrmDlProposedMcs* *to be greater than* (sum (max { bufferedBytes, rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes } for all bearers with data*) + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes \* number of bearers with data) \*\* 8***\***

with:

- the proposed MCS *rrmDlProposedMcs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3045\_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*

- the user’s SLIV for initial PDSCH transmission in the slot **(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4226\_replaced([11174914](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kue_aPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

If the UE works in high reliability LA mode(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15603\_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or UE is configured with lowSE MCS table for PDSCH, and the buffer contains RLC status PDU, the number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4622\_replaced([11192072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the proposed MCS *rrmDlProposedMcs to be greater than* (sum ~~(max {~~ *of bufferedBytes~~, RLC status PDU size }~~* for all bearers with data~~)~~ *+ rdRrmRLCStatusPDUProtectionSize\_bytes + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes \* number of bearers with data) \*\* 8.***\***

*Note, if proposedMCS is 0, rdRrmRLCStatusPDUProtectionSize\_bytes(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23155\_replaced([11193667](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8kPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*) shall be regarded as 0.\*\*

Note, If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True, "all bearers with data" do not include non-GBR DRBs **and deprioritized GBR**[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR**[End CB008600-F] DRBs** of a resource group (RG) with 0 RG weight (rrmRGWeight = 0, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), note: DRB is not associated with an RG is handled by default RG).***\****\*

*(*bufferedBytes in *5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),* rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4550\_replaced([11185672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUs7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), *rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes  in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28749\_replaced([11187059](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvYXQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*\*\*\*\*

If rrmBufferStatusSplitNeeded == 1, the determination of the maximum number of FD resources need to take into account the buffer status split for CA UEs as defined in  5G\_UP\_ALG\_2846\_replaced([11122312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqepKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), instead of bufferedBytes.

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-13 DL resource assignment using FDM Determination of UE category for FD resource allocation (ID: 11181306)

**3.29.7.2.0-13.0-3**  (ID: `11181321`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
**[CB013709-B]**

(If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0 and the real-time (RT) scheduling processing load severity metric (specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31301\_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for DL scheduler is below the deactivation threshold of rdIntfAvdRbgRandOlcDlPerCent (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31830\_replaced([11216820](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxa-pvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) OR (If rdActExtendedUpOverloadHandling (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31311\_replaced([11216763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxaXn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **is set to FALSE**)\*\*\*\*

**[End CB013709-B]**

FD scheduler shall perform UE categorization into ‘normal’ and ‘high performing’ UEs.

FD scheduler shall treat those UEs as ‘high performing’ UEs, when for each of those UEs in the CS2 list, all of the following conditions are met:

* Downlink assignment for the UE in the slot is to be done using C-RNTI and signaled using DCI Format 1\_1

Note: this condition is added  to resolve terminological ambiguity to eliminate interpretaiton where scheduling Paging for the given UE or even common channels (e.g.: SIB1) is perceived as “scheduling UE”.

* rrmDlProposedMcs (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3045\_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is in the range given by two threshold parameters:
* dlInterferenceAvoidance.startMcs and dlInterferenceAvoidance.endMcs i.e. dlInterferenceAvoidance.startMcs <= rrmDlProposedMcs <= dlInterferenceAvoidance.endMcs
* rank from CSI report  (Rank1 or Rank2 or Rank3 or Rank4) is greater than or equal to a threshold parameter given by:

**- dlInterferenceAvoidance.rank i.e. selected rank >= dlInterferenceAvoidance.rank**

* Buffered bytes is greater than a threshold parameter given by:
* **dlInterferenceAvoidance.dataBufferThreshold i.e. buffered bytes > dlInterferenceAvoidance.dataBufferThreshold**
* if n > 1, where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then UE must be 1st BWP (largest BWP) UE

Otherwise, if any of the above-mentioned conditions are not met, those UEs are considered as ‘normal’ UEs

**[CB013709-B]**

Else

If (NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0**, HwVariant used is not ABIL, and the real-time (RT) scheduling processing load severity metric (specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31301\_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for DL scheduler is greater than or equal to the activation threshold of rdIntfAvdRbgRandOlcDlPerCent (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31830\_replaced([11216820](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxa-pvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))))**)\*\*\*\*

* The UEs in CS2 are considered as ‘normal’ UEs

**[End CB013709-B]**

Notes:

1. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4507\_replaced([11181223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'FD Resource Groups with Several Dedicated BWPs per Cell' for details about nth BWP definition.
2. CB008955 algorithm uses initial estimation of proposed MCS (based on value used in previous scheduled slot of the UE; prior to any rate matching related adjustments) during categorization of UEs.
3. The Rank is from CSI report to align with SW. Interworking of reported rank and rank switch block/probing refers to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23994\_replaced([11139686](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krpGxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-14 DL resource assignment using FDM Resource Allocation Using FDM (ID: 11181328)

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-1**  (ID: `11181334`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Overview

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11181344`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Tasks of DL FD Scheduler

[CB008927-A] Note: In the below text, a DL FD resource can be a RBG with RAT0 or a PRB with RAT1. [End CB008927-A]

gNB-DU shall apply the functions of the DL FD scheduler which are listed as follows:

* Allocate resources to UEs in Initial Bandwidth Part. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16468\_replaced([11181355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note that the DL RBG [Before CB008927-E] DL FD resource randomization is not done for the UEs whose transmissions are scheduled in the Initial BWP [CB008927-E] DL FD resource randomization is not done for RAT0 UEs whose transmissions are scheduled in the Initial BWP, but with RAT1 UEs Start PRB randomization for the first UE randomly picked is done whose transmissions are scheduled in the Initial BWP [End CB008927-E].
* Allocate resources to UEs  that are being scheduled in the dedicated BWP. FD scheduler shall allocate the FD resources to the UEs in the order defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Based on whether  randomization of FD resources is supported or not, the following functions are performed
* When enhanced DL RBG DL FD resource Randomization is disabled, resources are not randomized while allocating to UEs scheduled in the dedicated BWP. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21928\_replaced([11182170](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBK7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the set of requirements. The set of requirements cover both cases when single and multiple dedicated BWPs are configured in a cell.
* [CB008927-E] With DL RAT1(NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True), DL FD resource randomization shall be always enabled. FD scheduler shall perform the PRB randomization as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23995\_replaced([11182407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB008927-E]
* With RAT0 when basic DL RBG DL FD resource Randomization is enabled (NRCELL/actDlRbgRandomization = enabled), only the start RBG FD resource is randomized while allocating resources to UEs scheduled in the dedicated BWP. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22033\_replaced([11182374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the set of requirements.
* [CB008927-E]With DL RAT0, [End CB008927-E] when enhanced DL RBG randomization is enabled (NRCELL/actDlRbgRandomization = enhanced), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12657\_replaced([11182642](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvE1R_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for allocating resources to UEs when single or several dedicated bandwidth parts are configured per cell.
* When slowPrbPoolingAllowed or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed  is TRUE [CB012605-C]or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE[End CB012605-C], PRB pooling is enforced for a group of cells in order to efficiently use the L1 resources while resource allocation. In this case, not all the PRBs are available for resource allocation for the FD scheduler. The number of usable of FD resources will also be influenced by whether PRB blanking is enabled or not. Refer to the rules specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21811\_replaced([11183015](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvHRiPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for interaction with PRB pooling.
* Towards the end of FD scheduling, the scheduler generates an artificial load based on the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16626\_replaced([11183121](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvH4nvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Resource Continuity While Allocation

* Refer to rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16586\_replaced([11183066](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvH4k_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) on resource allocation contiguity to the UEs in the FD resource allocation with FDM.

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2**  (ID: `11181350`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
FD Resource Allocation Algorithms

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11181355`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource Allocation for RNTI in Initial Bandwidth Part (BWP) or Using a Common Search Space

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12643499`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
For RNTI with initial BWP transmission or using a common search space (excluding Paging RNTI with only Paging short message for SI change notification or CMAS Notification):

 DCI Format 1\_0 in a Common Search Space (Type0/0A/1/2/3), the constraints are teh followings:

 If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is False, only one RNTI using a DCI Format 1\_0 in a Common Search Space associated to CORESET(0) is scheduled in the CORESET(0) bandwidth.

If numOfUesFdmInitialBWP is equal to 1, UE with initial BWP transmission shall be allocated the largest PDSCH allocation according to the DCI Format 1\_0 described hereafter, including the largest number of RBs defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4489\_replaced([11179193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is True, several RNTI using a DCI Format 1\_0 in a Common Search Space associated to CORESET(0) could be scheduled in the CORESET(0) bandwidth.

On top of the constraints described hereafter, the whole PDSCH allocations (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16521\_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) due to DCI Format 1\_0 in a Common Search Space linked to the CORESET(0) must not be outisde the CORESET(0) bandwidth and not larger than the largest number of RBs defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4489\_replaced([11179193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) the U-Plane enforces the following rules.

 For a TDD cell or for a FDD cell which is not shared with LTE or for which Cat-M support is disabled (actLteNrDssLteCat) [CB007372-G] or when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true [End CB007372-G]. The first RB is defined by the one of the CORESET selected and the size is restricted by the minimum value between the remaining available RB within initial BWP (excluding the potential blanked PRB) and the CORESET(0) size and which does not overlap with potential CSI-IM area in the respective slot and when PRB pooling is activated (slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true'), the remaining consecutive RBs (not lower than 4 RBs to take into account the MAC and RLC headers and few bytes of data) after other allocation within the limit defined by the PRB pooling. In case of any initial/retransmission of RAT1 scheduling(Transient phase UE) in the initial BWP(coreset #0), and the required number of RBs are not contiguously available in coreset #0, the allocation shall be reduced to maximum number of RBs that is contiguously available.The largest allowed number of RBs is defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4489\_replaced([11179193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- For a FDD cell shared with LTE when Cat-M support is enabled (actLteNrDssLteCat) [CB007372-G] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-G]**the largest consecutive area of PRBs is used within the CORESET selected and which does not overlap with potential blanked PRBs, potential CSI-IM area (Type1 searchspace only, i.e. msg2), and Cat-M NBs resources (for paging, SIB1-BR, MPBCH, and data) in the respective slot. As allocation type 1 is used, allocation is on PRB granularity, not on RBG granularity. If there are several areas of equal size, an arbitrary one can be chosen. Note, Type0/0A/2 searchspace are in MBSFN slots. If a DCI 1\_0 is allocated in a CORESET 0 (or X with X>0) within a PDSCH sub area above a LTE CAT-M PRBs area, gNB-DU shall allocate the DCI 1\_0 at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  [CB007372-G] Note: For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true, Coreset0 and InitialBWP resources are in NR dedicated area and do not collide with CAT-M PRBs [End CB007372-G]**

For paging schedule slot, paging will preempt exact number of PRB resources calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3915\_replaced([11167805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if RRC paging (S-TMSI) need to be scheduled.

 DCI Format 1\_1:

 For TDD cell or for a FDD cell which is not shared with LTE or for which Cat-M support is disabled (actLteNrDssLteCat) [CB007372-G] or when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true [End CB007372-G] with allocation type 0 (RBG),**if the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,  the First available RBG is defined by the first RBG not overlapped by the potential blanked PRB within the initial downlink BWP and the largest pdsch allocation is restricted to the remaining RBG not impacted by the potential blanked PRB within the initial BWP.** When the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,  gNB-DU shall handle the FD resources in PDSCH sub areas as it is explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))\*\*\*\*

 For a FDD cell shared with LTE when Cat-M support is enabled (actLteNrDssLteCat) if the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, the first available RBG is defined by the first RBG not overlapped by the potential blanked PRB or potential  Cat-M NBs resources (for paging, SIB1-BR, MPBCH, and data) within the initial downlink BWP in the respective slot and the largest PDSCH allocation is restricted to the remaining RBGs not impacted by the potential blanked PRB or potential Cat-M NBs resources (for paging, SIB1-BR, MPBCH, and data) within the initial BWP in the respective slot. **When the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,  gNB-DU shall handle the FD resources in PDSCH sub areas as it is explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))   
[CB007372-G] Note: For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true, Coreset0 and InitialBWP resources are in NR dedicated area and do not collide with CAT-M PRBs. Also NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to true so this case is not applicable for asymmetric DSS. [End CB007372-G]**

The location and bandwidth of the downlink and uplink initial Bandwidth parts and the CORESET characteristics (size/position) are configured. If PRB blanking function is activated,  the downlink initial Bandwidth part shall consider the blanked PRBs. Only the resource in downlink initial Bandwidth nonblanked area shall be allocated to the UE. The blanked PRBs refer more to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2036\_replaced([11277297](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpMt4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, Initialization of the available RBGs in the BW is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4279\_replaced([11177002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11181378`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Resource Allocation for UEs in Dedicated BWP

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11181383`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation for initial transmission

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11181389`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Introduction and step overview for dynamicswitch RAT 0 (initial transmission)

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13440703`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
As explained in 5G\_UP\_11498\_replaced([11350860](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8DfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), if NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true, after the completion of ES(Exhaustive Scheduling, see 5G UP Algorithms ID: [12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ)) if enabled, otherwise WRR algorithm step and before the resource allocation in dedicated BWP(s) gNB-DU shall check if a UE can fullfill the conditions listed in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and then if a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 can be allocated in the list of UEs scheduled in one of the PDSCH sub areas(s).

Steps for dynamicswitch RAT0 (Initial transmission)

1) RBGs pre booking and PDSCH sub area update and creation

For an initial transmission, after the conditions are checked  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and if a UE candidate has been selected (if these conditions are fullfilled), gNB-DU shall determine the list of the candidate RBGs for the "dynamicswitch" RAT0 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_25094\_replaced([11181651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8wvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), shall select and pre book the RBGs which will be allocated later to the UE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_25096\_replaced([11181674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8xvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). **In each PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall update the size (and the startPrb) of the PDSCH sub areas which contain pre booked RBGs as it is described in the requirements below 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If UE belongs  to the BWP2 (see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),  if there is no exclusive PRB for the largest dedicated BWP1 at the top of the PDSCH sub area or no reserved PRBs in the largest dedicated BWP1, or there are vUE(s) scheduled in the slot and the candidate UE is either a MU paired user or not (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ,** gNB-DU shall create a new PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) too).\*\*\*\*

1bis) increase of reserved PRBs for other RAT1 UEs inside the PDSCH sub area where the dynamicswitch RAT0 had its reserved

Rationale: When gNB-DU has pre booked RBGs in the PDSCH sub area where the candidate UE has reserved PRBs as RAT1, due to RBG roundup, a few PRBs remain free (and then available) in the PDSCH sub area updated and in the new PDSCH sub area created if the candidate UE belongs to BWP2, if multi BWP is configured and if there are reserved PRBs in the largest dedicated BWP1 and there is an exclusive PRB area for the largest dedicated BWP1 at the top of the PDSCH sub area, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25676\_replaced([11176765](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) above and below the pre booked RBGs and then gNB\_DU shall re allocate these free PRBs to other RAT1 UEs if there are eligible UEs (listed and ranked in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25657\_replaced([11181438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_Vs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) amongst these other RAT1 UEs.

In the PDSCH sub areas which are below or above the pre booked RBGs which mapped with the previous reserved PRBs of the candidate UE when it was RAT1, when there are eligible UEs (listed and ranked in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25657\_replaced([11181438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_Vs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which could have been candidate for dynamicswitch RAT0, gNB-DU shall allocate these free PRBs to these eligible RAT1 UEs in order to fill these PDSCH sub areas (no more remaining PRB) provided the PRB pooling limit and EiRP checks are still fulfilled (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25658\_replaced([11181459](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_Vt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

When NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) scheduled in the slot, the gNB-DU shall reserve the MU PRBs pertaining to the top frequencies of the BWP1 (largest BWP for MU-MIMO) utilizing the exclusive PRBs of the BWP1 either fully or partially and shall update the respective PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the candidate user is not a MU paired user or an user from the BWP2, then the candidate UE belongs to this reduced PDSCH sub area and above mentioned RBG pre-booking rules shall be applied.

When the candidate UE is a MU paired user the modification of the respective PDSCH sub area depends upon the total number of PRBs reserved for the vUE ( nbPrbs(vUE)) since the prebooking and allocation of the candidate UE happens within these PRBs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Any free and hence available PRBs due to RBG roundup shall become part of the updated PDSCH sub area and can be utilized. See figures 1bis, 1bis', 1bis´´, 2bis in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25683\_replaced([11176810](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMq_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 1bis **in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25686\_replaced([11176935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

2) **Link adaption and TBS re computation**

When the UE is selected for dynamicswitch RAT0 with an updated RBG pre-book bitmap, before the RBG allocation, gNB-DU shall re call the Link Adaptation procedure for this UE as a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3001\_replaced([11125242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqtSpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). **Note: gNB-DU shall follow the same MCS selection procedure (including the TBS re computation) as with a RAT1 allocation (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24101\_replaced([11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and perform a MCS redetermination in case of a rate matching (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17189\_replaced([11129019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6G_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).** gNB-DU shall apply the same re call for the RAT1 UE(s) which get additional PRBs (see 1bis).\*\*\*\*

In the other PDSCH sub area gNB-DU shall not try to pair this UE with any other MU eligible user in that PDSCH sub area

**Rationale: Note 1:To reduce the compexity of MU pairing post the Exhaustive Scheduling(see [*12483324*](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ)) or MU-WRR it is recommended that gNB-DU shall not try to pair the candidate user in the other PDSCH sub area, as this can cause modifications to existing vUEs.**

Note 2:  The final MCS shall be recalculated for the MU paired users that are converted to dynamicswitch to RAT0 as per the rules defined by [11145579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6PPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). Likewise the the final TBS shall be recomputed based on the final MCS recalculated.

3) re update of the number of transmitted data for the dynamic Switch RAT0 UE ([CB014699-E-UPD001] critical GBR, critical non-GBR, [End CB014699-E-UPD001], DC-GBR, non GBR, GBR and NBR bearers)

After the completion of the Link adaptation procedure,

* gNB-DU shall update the amount of additional data transmitted by the candidate UE in dynamicswitch RAT0 compared to RAT1 **and by the RAT1 UE(s) which get additional PRBs (see 1bis) [CB014699-E-UPD001]in the critical GBR bearer (dataSizeSentForGbrBearer, see [11175497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kujQ1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), in the critical non-GBR bearer (see** [13404773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rJo3eQyeEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB014699-E-UPD001], **in the non GBR bearer (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4185\_replaced([11174341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the GBR bearer (dataSizeSentForGbrBearer, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8026\_replaced([11175497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kujQ1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or in the NBR bearer (txDataForNbrBearer\_bits, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22083\_replaced([11175539](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj34fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or in the DC-GBR bearer (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30517\_replaced([11185540](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUF0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and then for all [CB014699-E-UPD001]critical GBR, critical non-GBR, [End CB014699-E-UPD001] DC-GBR, GBR, NBR and non GBR bearers,**

gNB-DU shall start the RBG allocation in the startPrb allocation part (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23995\_replaced([11182407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note For retransmission users (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23663\_replaced([11177504](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) it is possible to retransmit using a different "RAT" than the intial transmission if conditions allow. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25033\_replaced([11176715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the conditions to allocate a retransmission as dynamicswitch to RAT0 or RAT1.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13340435`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Interworking with OCNS features

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13340436`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
When RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to trueand when NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true and when NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is not set to 0, when there are several PDSCH sub areas in a slot, gNB-DU shall select the fake UE (from artificial load) as candidate for dynamicswitch RAT0 if only no other real UE has not been selected for dynamicswitch RAT0, for initial transmission and retransmission in the slot (see [11181630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_VvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

If gNB-DU selects a fake UE as dynamicswitch RAT0:

* gNB-DU shall check for the fake UE that the conditions under [11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) are fulfilled.
* The fake UE follows the same rules for the pre booking and allocation for the RBGs within the PDSCH sub areas (see [11181389](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [11181630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_VvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)),

Note: gNB-DU handles the dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation for a fake UE in the same way for the OCNS features (that is when NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to non-zero value or when NRCELL.actEmfTestMode is set to true) .

DL resource assignment using FDM
Conditions needed for a UE candidate for a dynamicswitch RAT 0 allocation.

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11181417`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Conditions at slot level

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12681278`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
In order to select a UE as candidate for dynamicswitch RAT 0 allocation, gNB-DU shall check at slot level that all the following conditions are fulfilled:

1) gNB-DU shall check that the functionality dynamicswitch have been activated (NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc  = true)

2) gNB-DU shall check that there are at least 2 PDSCH sub areas in the slot, except when there is only one single UE scheduled in TRS slot or in CSI-IM slot in FR1 TDD FD beamforming (see a more detailed description below). gNB-DU shall check that there are enough PRBs available (for a RBG) in one PDSCH sub area at least. The number of PRBs shall be greater (or at least equal) than a RBG size . [CB014474-A] If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = False,[End CB014474-A] for ~~If~~ single dedicated BWP, the RBG size is the one of the dedicated BWP. If multi BWP is configured, the rbg size is the one of the largest dedicated BWP1, or if a BWP2 is configured, the one of the dedicated BWP2 (see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).[CB014474-A] If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = True, then the RBG size shall depend on the BWP of the candidate UE chosen from amongst the several dedicated BWPs configured in the cell. [End CB014474-A]

Note: the slot does not contain CSI-RS for beam refinement (FR1 TDD).

specific use case with only one PDSCH sub area for only one single UE scheduled in TRS slot or in CSI-IM slot in FR1 TDD FD beamforming:

If FR1 TDD FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and when NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking is set to false and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), in TRS slots when **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = false** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = false (note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]*****\* or in CSI-IM slots when CSI-IM is rate matched (that is if* \*[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]**\*\*\*\*), when the PRB\_GRANULARITY is greater than 1, when there is only one UE scheduled in a single PDSCH sub area in these slots and if this UE belongs to the largest dedicated BWP if the multi BWP is configured,

the PRB located at the top of carrier bandwidth and which is discarded from the single PDSCH sub area is added to this PDSCH sub area, and the gNB-DU shall allocate the last RBG of the dedicated BWP if mono BWP is configured or of the largest dedicated BWP if multi BWP is configured to the UE moved into dynamicswitch RAT0. (see [11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for details and rational).

Notes:

* this specific use case is not applicable for a dynamic retransmission RAT0 (only initial transmission), when there are several PDSCH sub areas (for instance the additional blanked PRBs in TRS slots (due to PR780308), DCI 1\_0 scheduled in CSI-IM slots)), when there are blanked PRBs at high edge or low edge.

3) gNB-DU shall check that there is not already a dynamicswitch RAT0 previously scheduled as a retransmission in this slot (the RBG pre book bitmap of this slot is equal to 0, no RBG pre booked). [CB014474-A] However if NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, then the above restriction does not hold true for MU paired users. Thus, if there has been a dynamicswitch RAT0 MU paired user previously scheduled as a retransmission in this slot (See [11176715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), another one or more user with initial transmissions in the same virtual UE as the retransmission user, can be subjected to dynamicswitch to RAT 0 procedure in the same slot. **[End CB014474-A]**

4) in FDD (slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true') or if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true in FR1 TDD, gNB-DU shall check that the PRBs limitation provided by BBRM has not yet been reached with the PRBs reservation in RAT1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24760\_replaced([11176430](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall check too that the PRB pooling inequations (1) and (2) in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24760\_replaced([11176430](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) allow at least a margin of one RBG size (see 2) for the RBG size).
