**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11181427`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Conditions at UE level (single UE)

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11181432`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
[Before CB008927-L]

In order to select a UE as candidate for dynamicswitch RAT 0 allocation, gNB-DU shall check that all the following conditions are fulfilled:

1) gNB-DU shall check that only one DCI 1\_1 RAT1 is scheduled in dedicated BWP in the largest PDSCH sub area and is configured as dynamicswitch allocation (see 5G\_UP\_11496\_replaced([11350683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt7fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall check that there is no other PRB reserved in the other PDSCH sub area(s) than the one is schedued the candidate UE.

2) If multi BWP is configured,  gNB-DU shall check that the candidate UE belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1 or the dedicated BWP2 ( see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

3) gNB-DU shall check that the UE contains a non GBR bearer [Before CB008927-J-UPD001]  or GBR bearer [CB008927-J-UPD001] (not applicable for GBR and NBR bearers) [End CB008927-J-UPD001]  (with mono or multi bearer) which sends DL data is  in the slot.

4) gNB-DU shall check that the UE cannot flush the number of PRBs reserved in its PDSCH sub area (its the buffer is not drained out ) as RAT1 as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008927-L]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11181438`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Conditions for UE ranking

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-3.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `13440668`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
During the previous FD scheduler steps, gNB-DU shall mark the UEs which could have not flushed their buffer (the FD resources requested in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) have not been satisfied by the number of PRBs reserved in a PDSCH sub area), provided that :

* The UE is configured as dynamicswitch (and not only RAT1).
* If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = False, then the UE belongs to the dedicated BWP if only one single dedicated BWP is configured, or to the  largest dedicated BWP1 or BWP2 (see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if multi BWP is configured.
* If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, then the UE may belong to any of the dedicated BWPs if several dedicated BWPs are configured in the cell.
* The buffer is not drained out due to [CB014699-E-UPD001] critical GBR bearer or a critical non-GBR bearer or [End CB014699-E-UPD001] a non GBR bearer or a GBR bearer **or a DC-GBR bearer**

Amongst all the marked UEs which have been pre selected, gNB-DU shall prioritize the UEs in ranking them (from the highest to the lowest)  according to the following criteria (decreasing order):

1) If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = False,The UEs which require the highest number of PRBs.

2)If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = False, then if multi BWP is configured, the largest dedicated BWP (compared to BWP2, see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = True, then, gNB-DU shall perform the following procedure for candidate selection. For each UE belonging to BWPi where i = {i..j..n} for n BWPs configured in the cell, that satisfies the conditions needed for dynamicswitch to RAT0 functionality mentioned above, calculate total TBS (based on UE's current MCS, rank and number of DL symbols with DMRS configuration in each PRB) each UE can achieve with the additional RBG(s) (worth the free PRBs) from the other PDSCH subarea. For multiple users in vUE(s) calculate the TBS of each eligible user and add them to reflect the total TBS across the level of all the vUE(s) the UE is present. Next sort the users first at BWP level from large to small and then within in each BWP in decreasing order of respective TBS and pick the top user as the candidate. Note that in case of the BWPi with i=1, the user picked can be a SU user or 'a set of multiple paired users' belonging to one or more vUE(s). For all other BWPj, where j=i+1..n there can be a single candidate SU user.

3) The UEs requiring additional PRBs due to [Before CB014699-E-UPD001] a non GBR bearer (compared to a GBR bearer and DC-GBR bearer) or a GBR bearer (compare to a DC-GBR bearer) [CB014699-E-UPD001] a bearer of priority ranked as:  critical GBR, critical non-GBR, DC-GBR, GBR and non-GBR [End CB014699-E-UPD001].

**Note: The UEs may belong to a vUE with all MU paired users with initial transmission. gNB-DU must guarantee that the PRB allocations of the other users in the vUE is not modified while choosing the UE (See rules defined under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25094\_replaced([11181651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8wvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = false, then If there are multiple UEs that qualify in the vUE any one can be chosen. If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, then if there are multiple UEs that qualify in the vUE, all the users can be chosen.**

Note:

-    If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = False, then If a UE where the buffer cannot be drained out in the largest dedicated BWP with a non GBR bearer has been marked, gNB-DU shall stop the ranking procedure for the UEs which belong to the same beam.

- If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = False, then If there is a UE where the buffer cannot be drained out, gNB-DU shall discard the UEs belonging to the same beam requesting a limited number of PRBs.

-     If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = True, then, gNB-DU shall discard subsequent UEs, requesting a limited number of PRBs, which belong to the same beam and also from other BWPj, when a UE belonging to a BWPi where i=1..j..n, and j>1, for whom the buffer cannot be drained out in the respective dedicated BWP has been found.

When the list of UEs marked have been sorted, gNB-DU shall start the checks in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25658\_replaced([11181459](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_Vt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) starting with the UE with the highest ranks and in the decreasing order.

11181594:

1) gNB-DU shall check that the number of PRBs from the RBGs allocated to the dynamicswitch RAT0 is greater than the initial number of PRBs reserved to this candiddate UE

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `11181601`)

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11181624`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11181624:
DL resource assignment using FDM
For each candidate UE as a candidate for dynamicswitch RAT 0 allocation, follow the step as below.
If there is an eligible HARQ retransmission for power boost in the slot, FD resource availability check shall also include the condition that the sum of FD resources used  for PRBs for PDSCH allocation of all the UEs and PRBs for Power boosting  the eligible retransmission is less than rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs. 
   Eligibility of HARQ retransmission for power boost defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 25136.
   Definition of PRBs for PDSCH allocation for a UE and PRBs for Power boosting is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24935\_replaced([11185393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
   rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs  defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced([11177271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
 
gNB-DU shall check that all the following conditions w.r.t for power boosting are fulfilled:
In the SSB slot, gNB-DU shall check that the sum of the number of the PRBs from the RBGs allocated to the dynamicswitch RAT0 shall not exceed the rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced([11177271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
(1)  [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation denoted by the Greek letter sigma ($\Sigma$), with a subscript 'k'. This is followed by the term 'nbPrbsRbg(k)'. The entire expression is enclosed in a white background with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13776.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]  +     [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by subscripts "p,n". The term being summed is "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13776.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]   +  powerBoostPrbs    <= rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs 
where  powerBoostPrbs is PRBs used to power boost all the eligible UEs in the slot[As explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25138\_replaced([11185420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3t_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]. These PRBs are from rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced([11177271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which can have both granted and non granted resources.
 
Otherwise in the other slots, gNB-DU shall check that the sum of the number of PRBs from the RBGs allocated to the dynamicswitch RAT0 and the PRBs allocated in the CORESET #0 (or X with X>0)  (of all the PDSCH sub areas  , for instance if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled  or if there are several CAT-M PRBs area in the slot) shall not exceed rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced([11177271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
(2)  [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation denoted by the Greek letter sigma ($\Sigma$), with a subscript 'k'. This is followed by the term 'nbPrbsRbg(k)'. The entire expression is enclosed in a white background with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13776.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]  + [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation denoted by the Greek letter sigma ($\Sigma$) with a subscript 'n'. This is followed by the text "nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n)". The text appears to be in a sans-serif font. The background is white, and the text is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13776.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]    +  [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by subscripts "p,n". The term being summed is "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13776.002.png[/image\_path][/image-4] +  powerBoostPrbs     <= rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs
where
 powerBoostPrbs is PRBs used to power boost all the eligible UEs in the slot[As explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25138\_replaced([11185420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3t_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]. These PRBs are from rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced([11177271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which can have both granted and non granted resources.
 nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n), nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p) , nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n)  are variables defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 nbPrbsRbg(k) are the number of PRBs of the RBGs contained in the PDSCH sub area k

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-4.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12642706`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
After the completion of Exhaustive Scheduling(see [*12483324*](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ)) or WRR step and for all the PDSCH sub areas before the resource allocation in dedicated BWP(s), for initial transmission only, gNB-DU shall check that the remaining FD resources in the PDSCH sub areas can be allocated to a UE which has not been flushed its buffer in RAT1 PRBs reservation in a PDSCH sub area through a dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation.

[CB012971-F] When NIDD:actDlMuPairingGroup is enabled, paired UEs in pairing groups shall not be considered as candidates to dynamicly switch to RAT0. [End CB012971-F]

In order to switch from a "dynamicswitch" RAT1 into a dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation for a candidate UE, gNB-DU shall check that the NIDD parameters NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc and NRCELL.actRat1Alloc are set to true.

[CB014474-A] If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, gNB-DU shall schedule more than one "dynamicswitch" RAT0 UE per slot only when these users are eligible candidates and in the same virtual UE(s). This "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation can be an initial transmission or a retransmission MU paired user. For non MU paired users, gNB-DU shall schedule at most one "dynamicswitch" RAT0 UE per slot. This "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation can be an initial transmission or a retransmission (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25033\_replaced([11176715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Otherwise if NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = false, [End CB014474-A]gNB-DU shall schedule at most one "dynamicswitch" RAT0 UE per slot. This "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation can be an initial transmission or a retransmission (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25033\_replaced([11176715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note: In case of retransmission gNB-DU shall not consider a MU paired user in a vUE.

gNB-DU shall check if there is an eligible UE amongst the UEs scheduled in order to switch from "dynamicswitch" RAT1 allocation into a "dynamicswitch" RAT 0 allocation. The conditions are listed under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

gNB-DU  shall identify which RBGs can be allocated for a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation, see generic rules 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25094\_replaced([11181651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8wvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and use cases:

* if 2 PDSCH sub areas due to DCI 1\_0 allocation within a CORESET 0 **or CORESET#X (with X>0) or in SSB slot : see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25499\_replaced([11181729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8yvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**

**- if 2 or 3  PDSCH sub areas due to an additional blanked PRBs area in TRS slots (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30501\_replaced([11175903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): gNB-DU shall handle in a similar way than in a SSB slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25499\_replaced([11181729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8yvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): the SSB will be replaced by the the blanked PRBs, there is no DCI 1\_0 since TRS slot, and in SSB with TRS symbols and the SSB is not adjacent neither overlapping the additional blanked PRBs area, gNB-DU shall handle the 2 PDSCH sub areas below the additional blanked PRBs area in the similar way than a SSB slot with a low and high edge blanked PRBs (still without DCI 1\_0).**

* the specific use case with only one PDSCH sub area and one single UE in TRS slots or CSI-IM slots in FR1 FDD beamforming (see [11181417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-usPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details and rationale).

Note: the use cases with CORESET 0 are applicable to CORESET#X (with X>0) when all the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are mapped to the CORESET#X (other than CORESET #0).

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11181651`)

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11181658`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Generic rules for RBG allocation for a UE candidate in a PDSCH sub area

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-4.0-2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12681437`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB-DU shall align dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation with RBG boundaries. In a PDSCH sub area, the RBG allocation is contiguous due to other RAT1 allocations in the PDSCH sub area. [CB014474-A] If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = false, [End CB014474-A]The candidate UE can belong to the single dedicated BWP or if multi BW is configured, the largest dedicated BWP1 or the dedicated BWP2 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25040\_replaced([11178662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB014474-A] else if NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, the candidate UE can belong to anyone of the several dedicated BWPs (1..n..p) configured in the cell. [End CB014474-A]. The RBG size used is the one of the BWP where the candidate UE belongs to. The RBG boundary can be below or above a blanked RBG, due to SSB, PRB blanking, the additional blanked PRBs area in TRS slots (see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), a DCI 1\_0 RAT1 allocation (in the CORESET 0 **or CORESET#X (with X>0) ) or DCI 1\_1  RAT1 grants.**

If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True, and vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot and the candidate UE is a non MU paired user the following generic rules for RBG allocation are followed.

Note that the respective PDSCH sub area shall be updated/reduced as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The reduction in the PDSCH sub area happens from the top since the vUE(s) are reserved and allocated from the top frequencies in high2Low direction.

The reduction process of the respective PDSCH sub area can induce partial RBG at the top. When multiple BWPs are configured in the cell, within the reduced PDSCH sub area, this partial RBG can belong to either:

- the exclusive area of the largest BWP1 if the reduction did not impact the PRBs of the next larger BWP or

- to next ~~larger~~ BWP(s) if the PRBs from BWP1 overlapped ~~the PRBs of the BWP2~~ with the smaller nested BWPs.

This RBG can be considered as a blanked RBG for a dynamicswitch candidate UE or just a partial RBG for other RAT1 users. Any reference to blank RBG or partial RBG within this updated PDSCH sub area, henceforth in the below defined rules shall also consider such a case.

1) If single dedicated BWP or if both dedicated BWP1 and BWP2 (if multi BWP is configured) [CB014474-A] or if NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, all the dedicated BWPs (1..n..p) [End CB014474-A] contain all the PRBs of the PDSCH sub area,

 If there are blanked RBGs at the top but not at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area,

If other RAT1 UEs are scheduled in this PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall count and locate the PRBs already reserved from the top of the PDSCH sub area. Then gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs of the candidate UE from the lowest full size RBG and shall continue in low2high direction and in a contiguous way to the highest full size RBG.

 If there are blanked RBGs at the bottom but not at the top of the PDSCH sub area or if there are blanked RBGs at the bottom and at the top of the PDSCH sub area

If other RAT1 UEs are scheduled in this PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall count and locate the PRBs already reserved from the bottom of the PDSCH sub area. Then gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs of the candidate UE from the highest full (or partial) size RBG and shall continue in high2low direction and in a contiguous way to the smallest full size RBG.

2) if the dedicated BWP~~2-~~n (if multi BWP is configured) does not contain the highest PRBs of the PDSCH sub area. [CB014474-A] Note: n =2, if NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = false, othersie or n= {2..p} [End CB014474-A]

 If there are blanked RBGs at the bottom and at the top of the PDSCH sub area,

If the candidate UE belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1, gNB-DU shall count and locate the PRBs already reserved from the bottom of the PDSCH sub area. Then gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs of the candidate UE from the highest full size RBG and shall continue in high2low direction and in a contiguous way to the lowest full size RBG.

If the candidate UE belongs to the BWP~~2-~~n, gNB-DU shall count and locate the PRBs already reserved for UEs which don´t belong to each of the larger dedicated ~~BWP1~~ BWP(n-1) from the bottom of the PDSCH sub area and shall count and locate the PRBs already reserved for UEs which belong to each of the larger dedicated ~~BWP1~~ BWP(n-1) from the top of the PDSCH sub area. Then gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs of the candidate UE **from the highest full size RBG of the dedicated BWP~~2-~~n if the PRBs reserved for each of the larger dedicated ~~BWP1~~ BWP(n-1) have overlapped the dedicated BWP~~2-~~n, otherwise from the highest (full size or partial) RBG of the dedicated BWP~~2-~~n and shall continue in high2low direction and in a contiguous way to the lowest full size RBG.**

 If there are blanked RBGs at the bottom but not at the top of the PDSCH sub area,

If the UE candidate belongs to the larger largest dedicated BWP1, gNB-DU shall count and locate the PRBs already reserved from the bottom of the PDSCH sub area. Then gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs of the candidate UE from the highest (full size or partial) RBG and shall continue in high2low direction and in a contiguous way to the lowest full size RBG.

If the candidate UE candidate belongs to the BWP~~2-~~n, gNB-DU shall count and locate the PRBs already reserved for UEs which don´t belong to each of the larger dedicated ~~BWP1~~ BWP(n-1) from the bottom of the PDSCH sub area and shall count and locate the PRBs already reserved for UEs which belong to each of the larger dedicated ~~BWP1~~ BWP(n-1) from the top of the PDSCH sub area. Then **gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs of the candidate UE from the highest full size RBG of the dedicated BWP~~2-~~n if the PRBs reserved for each of the larger dedicated ~~BWP1~~ BWP(n-1) have overlapped the dedicated BWP~~2-~~n, otherwise** from the highest (full size or partial) RBG of the dedicated BWP~~2-~~n and shall continue in high2low direction and in a contiguous way to the lowest full size RBG.\*\*\*\*

 The use case “no blanked RBG” at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area is not met.

When the candidate UE is a MU paired user, the gNB-DU shall perform the RBG selection and allocation for the dynamicswitch to RAT0 within the nbPrbs(vUE), i.e the number of PRBs reserved for the vUE previously with RAT1,  in the respective PDSCH sub area where the vUE is scheduled. **gNB-Du shall apply following generic rules**.\*\*\*\*

[CB014474-A] If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = false, [End CB014474-A] For the other PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall treat this candidate same as a non-MU user and thereby shall follow the same rules specified above and in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25096\_replaced([11181674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8xvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). See figure 1bis and 1bis' as examples when the candidate UE is a MU user.

[CB014474-A] else, if NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, if there are multiple MU paired users converted to dynamicswitch to RAT0, for the other PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall treat this as another vUE' with the users as partners. However this step does not mean new vUE creation, nor new pairing with exsiting MU paired users (vUE(s)) in the target PDSCH sub area. The MU-MCS for all the users shall be recalculated (See [11145579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6PPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) as the total number of allocated PRBs for the candidate user(s) increase which shall be taken into account for Mu-MCS recalculation for all the rusers including the other partner users that are paired with them in any of the previous vUEs in the original PDSCH sub area, when they were paired in the source PDSCH sub area with RAT1. [End CB014474-A]

1) When the candidate UE chosen ~~is the largest contributing user in~~ in the the vUE, such that the nbPrbs(vUE) correspond to the number of PRBs reserved for this user and the other users share these PRBs according to their respective share within the nbPrbs(vUE).

 If there is a blanked RBG at the top of the PDSCH sub area and the nbPrbs(vUE) contain the unblanked PRBs

 The gNB-DU shall select and allocate, for the candidate UE, the topmost full size RBG below this blanked RBG and continue in high2Low order till the lowest full-size RBG within the nbPrbs(vUE).

 If there is a partial RBG at the top of the PDSCH sub area and the nbPrbs(vUE) contain the PRBs in that partial RBG

 The gNB-DU shall select and allocate, for the candidate UE, the partial RBG and continue in high2Low order till the lowest full-size RBG within the nbPrbs(vUE).

 The nbPrbs(vUE) shall be updated such that the start PRB in the nbPrbs(vUE) from the top does not change but the lowest PRB within the nbPrbs(vUE) must be the lower most PRB in the lowest full-size RBG selected for the candidate UE without affecting the total allocations of the other paired users.

 The modified nbPrbs(vUE) is used to update the respective PDSCH sub area as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The PRBs hence made free can be utilized for other RAT1 users as described above within the updated PDSCH sub area.

 The start PRB of the vUE or the non-candidate users in the vUE does not change.

 If the reservation of the candidate UE is such that parts of it are scheduled as MU and rest as SU, after conversion to RBG only the RBGs corresponding to the number of nbPrbs(vUE) after the switch are MU paired and the remaining RBG(s) are SU.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11181674`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Selection of RBGs for a dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation for initial transmission

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-4.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12681502`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12681502:
DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB-DU shall select amongst the list of candidate RBGs which one are allocated for a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation.

When all the RBGs are identified for each PDSCH sub area, if all the conditions are fullfilled (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall compute the number of the RBGs needed for the allocation with these 2 inputs:

 gNB-DU shall check that the sum of the number of the PRBs from the RBGs (partial and full size) selected for the “dynamicswitch” RAT0 in addition of the PRBs of other signals in the slot shall not exceed the PRBs limitation provided by BBRM  (in FDD or if NRCELL.slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is set to True in FR1 TDD) in the slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 gNB-DU shall check that:

o if the UE required a limited number of PRBs (to drain out completely the buffered bytes), the sum of the number of PRBs from all the available RBGs is greater or equal than the number of PRBs requested by the UE.

o otherwise, if the buffer of the UE can not be drained out:

 if there is a PRB pooling limitation or an EIRP limitation (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) , the number of RBGs is the smallest number of RBGs which contains the maximum number of PRBs in addition of other signals which does not exceed the PRBs limitation provided by BBRM or EIRP (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 otherwise, it is the maximum number of RBGs candidate for a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation.

When gNB-DU has computed the number of RBGs allocated for a "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation, gNB-DU shall prebook the RBGs which will be allocated at the beginning of the start PRB allocation step (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25098\_replaced([11182422](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with a bitmap where each bit identifies a RBG (1 for allocated and 0 for free) taking care about the RBG size (if the UE belongs to the largest BWP1 or the BWP2, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25040\_replaced([11178662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

gNB-DU shall pre book all the RBGs selected **from the PDSCH sub area where the candidate UE** had reserved PRBs as RAT1**.**\*\*\*\*

When NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True, and vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot and the candidate UE selected for the "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation is a MU paired user, the gNB-DU shall pre-book the RBGs of the candidate user within the nbPrbs(vUE) that has been reserved previously as RAT1. The number of PRBs moved to RBG in the process depends upon the candidate user in the vUE contributing to the nbPrbs(vUE) and gets modified accordingly and hence the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). See figure 1bis and 1bis' below.

For the other PDSCH sub area(s), gNB-DU shall pre book the RBGs according to the number of RBGs needed for the allocation for the "dynamicswitch" RAT0 allocation.

 if the number of RBGs requested by the candidate UE is lower than the maximum number of RBGs available or a dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation, then in a PDSCH sub area (2nd or 3rd), all the RBGs within a PDSCH sub area are not selected in the allocation, so in this PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall select

 if there is no RAT1 UEs in the PDSCH sub area, first the highest full size RBG, then in a contiguous way and in high2low direction the RBGs (full size or partial) below till the number of RBGs needed for the allocation is reached.

 otherwise:

o If there is no reserved PRBs for RAT1 UEs at the top of the PDSCH sub area, first the highest (full size or partial) RBG, then in a contiguous way and in high2low direction the RBGs (full size or partial) below till the number of RBGs needed for the allocation is reached.

o If there is no reserved PRBs for RAT1 UEs at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area, **first the lowest (full size or partial) RBG, then in a contiguous way and in low2high direction the RBGs (full size or partial) below till the number of RBGs needed for the allocation is reached.**

o If there are reserved PRBs for RAT1 UEs at the bottom and at the top of the PDSCH sub area, **first the highest (full size or partial) RBG, then in a contiguous way and in high2low direction the RBGs (full size or partial) below till the number of RBGs needed for the allocation is reached.**

 if the number of RBGs candidate is equal to the maximum number of RBGs candidate, all the RBGs of the PDSCH sub area(s) are pre booked.

 [CB014474-A] If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = false, [End CB014474-A] If the candidate UE is a MU paired user in the other PDSCH sub area, the RBGs booked in this PDSCH sub area are not scheduled with MU but SU i.e the user is not paired with any other potential user in this PDSCH sub area. The user remains MU paired only in the original PDSCH sub area it belonged earlier with RAT1. There is no difference in handling for this PDSCH sub area and the user is treated same as a non MU paired user as described above.

 [CB014474-A] If NRCELL.actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, there can be more than one candidate user that are MU paired in the source/other PDSCH sub area and gNB-DU shall keep them paired in a new vUE' created in the target PDSCH sub area with both these user as partners. gNB-DU shall perform recalculation of MU-SINR and MU-MCS due to addition of the newer vUE' (See [11145579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6PPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). There is no difference in handling for this PDSCH sub area and the user is treated same as a non MU paired user as described above.[End CB014474-A]

gNB-DU shall update existing PDSCH sub area (and CORESET 0 within a PDSCH sub area) and create a new PDSCH sub area, according to the conditions and criteria described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25646\_replaced([11176742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

example 1: all the RBGs of the PDSCH sub area 1 are selected and only 1 RBG is needed in the PDSCH sub area 2, so the RBG in the lowest position is selected in the PDSCH sub area 2 and the other RBGs available are left free as PRBs for other RAT1 UEs.

example 1bis: MU paired user chosen to be the candidate UE and PRBs of the largest user moved to RBGs Nb1 within the nbPrbs(vUE) in the PDSCH sub area 1, and thereby updating the PDSCH sub area 1 accordingly based on the nbPrbs(vUE). The PRBs hence made free are available for the other RAT1 UEs. Then only 1 RBG needed in the PDSCH sub area 2, so the RBG in the lowest position is selected in the PDSCH sub area 2 **and the other RBGs available are left free as PRBs for other RAT1 UEs.**

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their allocation in a communication system. The diagram uses colored bars to represent these BWPs and text labels to explain their purpose and relationships.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side - BWPs:**
\* **CORESET#0:** Represented by a thin blue bar on the far left.
\* **initial BWP:** Represented by a yellow bar, positioned to the right of CORESET#0.
\* **dedicated BWP 1:** Represented by a tall orange bar, to the right of the initial BWP.
\* **dedicated BWP 2:** Represented by a tall green bar, to the right of dedicated BWP 1.
\* **PS BWP-3:** Represented by a light blue bar, to the right of dedicated BWP 2.
**Right Side - Allocation and Labels:**
\* **nbPrbs for the vUE:** A label pointing to a section of stacked orange and brown bars. This section appears to be related to the "Old PDSCH sub area 1".
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 1:** This is indicated by a dashed line encompassing the stacked orange and brown bars.
\* **Prbs made free:** A label with a double-headed arrow indicating a range of PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks) that are made free. This range is below the "Old PDSCH sub area 1".
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_1 BWPs:** A label pointing to a section of stacked light orange, green, and light purple bars. This section is below the "Prbs made free" line.
\* **new PDSCH sub area 1:** A label pointing to the stacked light orange, green, and light purple bars.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0:** A label pointing to a light purple bar, below the "nbPrbs DCI 1\_1 BWPs" section.
\* **POSCH sub area 2:** A label indicating a region below the "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0" section. This area contains a light orange bar and a light blue bar.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_1 BWP1:** A label pointing to the light blue bar within the "POSCH sub area 2".
\* **Nb1 + Nb2 RBGs Pre booked:** A label on the far right, connected by a blue line to the top of the stacked orange and brown bars ("Old PDSCH sub area 1"). This suggests a pre-booked allocation of RBGs.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs are utilized and how resources are allocated or freed up for different purposes, such as PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) transmissions. The stacked bars likely represent the distribution of PRBs within a given BWP or sub-area.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13787.002.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

~~example 1bis': MU paired user chosen to be the candidate UE and PRBs of the any user but the largest user moved to RBGs Nb1 within the nbPrbs(vUE) in the PDSCH sub area 1. The nbPrbs(vUE) does not change and hence no update of the PDSCH sub area required. Then only 1 RBG needed in the PDSCH sub area 2, so the RBG in the lowest position is selected in the PDSCH sub area 2 **and the other RBGs available are left free as PRBs for other RAT1 UEs.**~~

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11181725`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Initial transmission use cases for RBGs location of the candidate UE

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11181767`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11181767:
DL resource assignment using FDM
If the candidate UE as RAT1  belongs to the largest PDSCH sub area (above SSB/CORESET #0/Initial BWP or additional blanked PRBs area):
[CB013945-A] Note: If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDSCH sub area update. Below requirement defines the rules for further update of the reduced PDSCH sub area for non-MU paired users depending on whether the initial PDSCH sub area size was updated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or not.  [End CB013945-A]
1) If single dedicated BWP or the UE belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1 if multi BWP is configured and if several RAT1 UEs are scheduled in the dedicated BWP(s)  in the PDSCH sub area, 
gNB-DU shall determine the highest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for other RAT1 UEs in the PDSCH sub area computed in this way:  it is the sum of the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) and the number of PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 (if it is not a SSB slot) reduced by the number of previously reserved PRBs as RAT1 for the candidate UE in the PDSCH sub area n:
PrbNumOtherUesBwpPos = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]  +   [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation denoted by the Greek letter sigma ($\Sigma$) with a subscript 'p'. This is followed by the text "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP" and then a function call with arguments "(p, n)". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in italics. The overall image is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13793.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]  + nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) - nbReservedPrbsUe
Where:
- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) . If there is only one single dedicated BWP,  nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) = nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n).
- nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 within a PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If there is no PRB belonging to the CORESET #0 or in the SSB slot, it is equal to 0
- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] and rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] are the size and the start Prb of the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
- nbReservedPrbsUe is the number of PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area
Note:  If the candidate UE has reserved PRBs as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area (nbReservedPrbsUe > 0 ), this  highest PRB position can be computed inthis way too:
PrbNumOtherUesPos = rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]  + rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] –  nbReservedPrbsUe
If the candidate UE has no reserved PRB as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area,  the number of PRB to remove due to the candidate UE as RAT1 is NULL (nbReservedPrbsUe = 0).
gNB-DU shall start the allocation with the highest RBG (last partial RBG if no PRB blanked at higher edge of the carrier bandwidth) and shall continue in high2low direction and in a contiguous way to the smallest full size RBG such as the number of PRBs of the RBGs is below or equal to the number of PRBs previouX (with X>0)sly reserved as RAT1 for the candidate UE. The RBG size is the one of the single dedicated BWP or the largest dedicated BWP if multi BWP configured (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25040\_replaced([11178662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
This smallest full size RBG is
if no other RAT1 UE is scheduled in the PDSCH sub area, this
 above the highest PRB allocated of a DCI 1\_0 grants if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING), rach msg2 , rach msg4 or any DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in CORESET #0 (or CORESET#X (with X>0)) is scheduled in the slot
 above the highest PRB of the SSB, if SSB slot if no DCI 1\_0 in the SSB slot
  above the highest PRB of the additional blanked PRBs area, if TRS slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30501\_replaced([11175903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
otherwise
 above the highest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 grants for other RAT1 UEs in the PDSCH sub area
 
2) If the UE belongs to the BWP2 if multi BWP is configured (see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and if several RAT1 UEs are scheduled in the dedicated BWPs in the PDSCH sub area,
 gNB-DU shall determine the lowest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the largest BWP1 and the highest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the other dedicated BWP(p) (with p different from 1).
gNB-DU shall compute the lowest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the largest dedicated BWP1 in this way: it is the sum of of the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and the PDSCH sub area size reduced by the number of PRBs reserved for the RAT1 UEs  in the dedicated BWP1 in the PDSCH sub area n:
PrbNumOtherUesBwp1Pos = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] + rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] – nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n)
Where:
- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] and rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] are the size and the start Prb of the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(1,n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP1 within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
If the lowest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the largest dedicated BWP1 is greater than the highest PRB of the dedicated BWP2, gNB-DU shall set this lowest PRB position to the highest PRB of the dedicated BWP2 (a UE which belongs to the dedicated BWP2 cannot be granted with PRBs which don´t belong to the dedicated BWP2).
gNB-DU shall compute the highest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the other dedicated BWP(p) (with p different from 1) in this way: it is the sum of of the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area, the number of PRBs reserved in other dedicated BWP(p) (with p different from 1) and the number of PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 (if it is not a SSB slot) reduced by the number of PRBs previously reserved by the candidate UE as RAT1  in the dedicated BWP2  in the PDSCH sub area n:
PrbNumOtherUesOtherBwpPos =  [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by the index "p" with a subscript "p < > 1". The term being summed is "nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p, n)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13793.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] + nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n)+ rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]  –  nbReservedPrbsUe
Where:
- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] and rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] are the size and the start Prb of the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p<>1) within the PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
- nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 within a PDSCH sub area n (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If there is no PRB belonging to the CORESET #0 or in the SSB slot, it is equal to 0
- nbReservedPrbsUe is the number of PRBs previously reserved for the candidate UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area
If the candidate UE has no reserved PRB as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area,  the number of PRB to remove due to the candidate UE as RAT is NULL (nbReservedPrbsUe = 0).
If the candidate UE belongs to the BWP2 if multi BWP is configured, gNB-DU shall start the allocation with the highest RBG of the BWP2 (which can be partial) if there is no other RAT1 UE in the largest dedicated BWP1, or with the highest full size RBG below the lowest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other UEs which belong to the largest dedicated BWP1 and shall continue in high2low direction and in a contiguous way to the smallest full size RBG such as the number of PRBs of the RBGs is below or equal to the number of PRBs previously reserved as RAT1 for the candidate UE. The RBG size is the one of the dedicated BWP2 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25040\_replaced([11178662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
This smallest full size RBG is the RBG
If no other UE scheduled in the PDSCH sub area
 above the highest PRB allocated of a DCI 1\_0 grants if a common channel (SIB1, Si, PAGING), rach msg2 , rach msg4 or any DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in CORESET #0 (or CORESET#X (with X>0)) is scheduled in the slot.
 above the highest PRB of the SSB, if SSB slot if no DCI 1\_0 in the SSB slot
  above the highest PRB of the additional blanked PRBs area, if TRS slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30501\_replaced([11175903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
otherwise (if other UEs scheduled in the PDSCH sub area)
 above the highest PRB position (for a UE which belongs to BWP1 or BWP2 respectively, see their computation above) of the other DCI 1\_1 grants in the PDSCH sub area
The figures for these use cases are under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25000\_replaced([11181804](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvAj0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for only one candidate UE scheduled in the slot or under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25666\_replaced([11181890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvAj3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  several RAT1 UEs scheduled with the candidate UE.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11181798`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11181798:
DL resource assignment using FDM
If the candidate UE as RAT1 belongs to the largest PDSCH sub area (above SSB/CORESET #0/Initial BWP):

For the PDSCH sub area below SSB/CORESET #0/Initial BWP

If there is no other RAT1 UE scheduled in the PDSCH sub area,

if there is no blanked PRB at the bottom of PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall start the allocation with the lowest RBG at the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area (first full size RBG if no PRB blanked or first partial if specific bandwidth) and shall continue in low2high direction and in a contiguous way to the highest full size RBG:

 below the lowest PRB allocated of a DCI 1\_0 grants if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 or any other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in the CORESET 0 or in the CORESET#X (with X>0) is scheduled in the slot

 below the SSB, if SSB slot and if no DCI 1\_0

 below the lowest  PRB of the additional blanked PRBs area, if TRS slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30501\_replaced([11175903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note:  if a common channel is scheduled, the lowest PRB allocated for a DCI1\_0 can be lower than the start PRB of the common channel if a DCI 1\_0 is allocated below the common channel. It is true too without common channel when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

otherwise (blanked PRBs at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area), gNB-DU shall handle the RBGs allocation in the other direction (high2low) with same border RBG (from the same highest full size RBG to the lowest full size RBG above the blanked PRBs )

otherwise (if other RAT1 UEs are scheduled in the PDSCH sub area)

-     [CB013945-A] If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) are scheduled in this PDSCH sub area from the top below the lowest PRB allocated to a DCI\_1\_0 grant the PDSCH sub area is reduced by the nbPrbs(vUE) and henceforth the PDSCH sub area refered is the reduced PDSCH sub area. [End CB013945-A]

- if the first RBG at the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area is full or partial size (and no blanked PRB),

gNB-DU shall determine the lowest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the dedicated BWP(p) in this way: it is the sum of the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and the PDSCH sub area size reduced by the sum of the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) and the number of PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 (if there is a common channel scheduled (SIB1, SI, PAGING)) in the PDSCH sub area (n+1):

PrbNumOtherUesBwpPos = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] + rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]    - (  [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by the subscript 'p'. The term being summed is 'nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p, n + 1)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13796.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] + nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) )

Where:

- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1] and rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] are the size and the start Prb of the PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n+1) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 within a PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If there is no PRB belonging to the CORESET #0 if a common channel is scheduled or in another slot (SSB, no common channel scheduled), it is equal to 0.

gNB-DU shall start the RBGs allocation from the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and shall continue in low2high direction and in a contiguous way to the highest full size RBG below the lowest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other UEs which belong to the all the dedicated BWP(p).

- otherwise (if there are blanked PRBs at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area)

gNB-DU shall determine the highest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the dedicated BWP(p) in this way: it is the sum of the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) in the PDSCH sub area (n+1):

PrbNumOtherUesBwpPos = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] +   [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by the subscript 'p'. The term being summed is 'nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p, n + 1)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13796.001.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Where:

- rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] is the start Prb of the PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n+1) is the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

gNB-DU shall start the allocation with the highest full size RBG:

 below the lowest PRB allocated of a DCI 1\_0 grants if a common channel (SIB1, Si, PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 or any other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in the CORESET 0 or in the CORESET#X (with X>0)  is scheduled in the slot. [CB013945-A] If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) are scheduled in this PDSCH sub area from the top below the lowest PRB alocated to a DCI\_1\_0 grant the PDSCH sub area is reduced by the nbPrbs(vUE). [End CB013945-A]

 below the SSB, if SSB slot and if no DCI 1\_0

 below the lowest  PRB of the additional blanked PRBs area, if TRS slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30501\_replaced([11175903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note:  if a common channel is scheduled, the lowest PRB allocated of a DCI 1\_0 can be lower than the start PRB of the common channel if a DCI 1\_0 is allocated below the common channel. It is true too without common channel when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase are mapped on a CORESETX (with X>0)

and shall continue in high2low direction and in a contiguous way to the highest full size RBG above the highest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to all the dedicated BWP(p).

The figures for these use cases are under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25000\_replaced([11181804](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvAj0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for only one candidate UE scheduled in the slot or under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25666\_replaced([11181890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvAj3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  several RAT1 UEs scheduled with the candidate UE.

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11181804`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Single candidate UE scheduled in the slot

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11181809`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Single dedicated BWP without PRB blanking or specific bandwidth for slots with DCI 1\_0 allocated in CORESET 0 or SSB slots.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11181826`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11181826:
DL resource assignment using FDM
These use cases are applicable when only one UE is scheduled in the slot.

There is no PRB blanking ( NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = false), no specific bandwidth (NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = false).

In the largest PDSCH sub area where are located the reserved PRBs, gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb1) starting with the last partial RBG from the top of the carrier bandwidth to the smallest full size RBG which is above the highest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0 in the CORESET #0  in the PDSCH sub area that is:

* if there is no common channel scheduled in the slot, the PRB above the highest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0, knowing that all the DCI 1\_0 RAT1 PDSCH grants have been allocated in a contiguous way from the bottom of the CORESET #0.
* if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled in the slot, the PRB above the highest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0,  knowing that all the DCI 1\_0 RAT1 PDSCH grants have been allocated in a contiguous way from the common channel.

In the smallest PDSCH sub area,  gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb2) starting with the bottom of the carrier bandwidth to the highest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just below the PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 that is:

* if there is no common channel scheduled in the slot, the PRB below the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0.
* if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled in the slot, the PRB below the lowest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0. Note: the highest full size RBG can overlap the lowest PRBs of the largest PDSCH sub area.

To the right of these blocks, there are two large, vertical orange rectangles. The upper one is taller and labeled "Nb1 RBGs" on its right side. The lower one is shorter and labeled "Nb2 RBGs" on its right side.
Several dashed lines and arrows indicate divisions and relationships between these blocks. Text labels are placed around these elements.

dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation in single dedicated BWP no PRBs blanking, no specific bandwidth, no common channel scheduled

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and resource blocks (RBs) allocated for various purposes.
On the left side, there are several vertical bars representing different BWPs:
- A thin blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A wide orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A light orange bar above a purple bar, also part of the "dedicated BWP" area.
The diagram shows two main horizontal sections labeled "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2".
Within "PDSCH sub area 1":
- There is a text box indicating "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP". This area is visually represented by the light orange and purple bars on the left and a large orange rectangle on the right.
- An arrow labeled "Nb1 RBGs" points to the height of the large orange rectangle on the right.
- Below this, there's a smaller section with text "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with common channel)". This is indicated by a purple rectangle on the left and a smaller orange rectangle on the right.
- Text to the right of this section reads "Highest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant" and "Lowest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant", with arrows indicating the range.
Within "PDSCH sub area 2":
- There is a large orange rectangle on the right.
- An arrow labeled "Nb2 RBGs" points to the height of this orange rectangle.
The diagram uses dashed lines to delineate different resource areas and arrows to indicate sizes or ranges of these resources. The colors are used to differentiate between different types of allocated resources or BWPs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13799.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation in single dedicated BWP no PRBs blanking, no specific bandwidth, common channel scheduled

In the largest PDSCH sub area where are located the reserved PRBs, gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb1) starting with the last partial RBG from the top of the carrier bandwidth to the smallest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just above the highest PRB of the SSB.

In the smallest PDSCH sub area,  gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb2) starting with the bottom of the carrier bandwidth to the highest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just below the lowest PRBs of the SSB.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and resource blocks (RBs) allocated for various purposes.
On the left side, there are several vertical bars representing different BWPs:
- A thin blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A thick orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A light purple bar, positioned below the "dedicated BWP" and partially overlapping with the "SSB" region.
To the right of these bars, the diagram shows two large orange rectangular blocks, representing resource blocks. These blocks are associated with different regions and labels.
Key labels and regions identified in the diagram are:
- "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP": This text is positioned above the upper orange block, indicating a reserved number of physical resource blocks (PRBs) for the User Equipment (UE) within the dedicated BWP.
- "PDSCH sub area 1": This label is associated with the upper part of the diagram, spanning from the top of the "dedicated BWP" to a dashed line above the upper orange block.
- "Nb1 RBGs": This label is to the right of the upper orange block, indicating a quantity of resource block groups (RBGs) labeled "Nb1".
- "SSB": This label is in the middle of the diagram, between two dashed orange lines labeled "Highest PRB of SSB" and "Lowest PRB of SSB". This likely refers to the Synchronization Signal Block.
- "PDSCH sub area 2": This label is associated with the lower part of the diagram, spanning from the dashed line below the "SSB" region to the bottom of the lower orange block.
- "Nb2 RBGs": This label is to the right of the lower orange block, indicating a quantity of resource block groups (RBGs) labeled "Nb2".
The diagram uses dashed lines to delineate different sub-areas and the boundaries of the SSB. Arrows indicate the extent of these areas and the quantities of RBGs. The overall arrangement suggests a division of the available spectrum into different resource pools for control and data transmission.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13799.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation in single dedicated BWP in SSB slot

gNB-DU canl allocate Nb1+ Nb2 RBGs to the dynamicswitch RAT0 UE

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11181829`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Single dedicated BWP with PRB blanking for slots with DCI 1\_0 allocated in CORESET 0 or SSB slots.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11181846`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11181846:
DL resource assignment using FDM
These use cases are applicable when only one UE is scheduled in the slot.

In single dedicated BWP, PRBs blanking is configured and activated ( NRBTS/NRCELL/actPRBBlanking = True).

If PRBs blanking is activated at higher edge (NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL > 0), in the largest PDSCH sub area where are located the reserved PRBs, gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb1) starting with the highest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area to the smallest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just above the PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 that is:

* if there is no common channel scheduled in the slot, the PRB above the highest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0, knowing that all the DCI 1\_0 RAT1 PDSCH grants have been allocated in a contiguous way from the bottom of the CORESET #0.
* if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled in the slot, the PRB above the highest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0.

If PRBs blanking is not activated at higher edge  (NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL = 0), for the  largest PDSCH sub area , gNB-DU shall handle in the same way than 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2500\_replaced([11116648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp5aX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If PRBs blanking is activated at lower edge (/NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDLl > 0), in the smallest PDSCH sub area,  gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb2) starting with the smallest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area to the highest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just below the PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 that is:

* if there is no common channel scheduled in the slot, the PRB below the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0.
* if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled in the slot, the PRB below the lowest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0. Note: the highest full size RBG can overlap the lowest PRBs of the largest PDSCH sub area.

If PRBs blanking is not activated at lower edge  (NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL = 0), for the smallest PDSCH sub area , gNB-DU shall handle in the same way than 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2500\_replaced([11116648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp5aX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to cellular networks. It depicts different blocks representing various types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and resource grants.
On the left side, there are vertical colored bars. The leftmost bar is blue and labeled "CORESET#0". To its right is a yellow bar labeled "initial BWP". Next to that is a wider orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP". Further to the right, there's a light orange bar, and then a tall, dark orange rectangular block. Below the light orange bar and above the dark orange block, there's a small purple rectangular block.
The diagram uses horizontal dashed lines to demarcate different sections and indicate ranges. Text labels are placed around these blocks and lines.
Key text elements and their approximate locations are:
- "CORESET#0" (bottom left, on a blue bar)
- "initial BWP" (bottom left, on a yellow bar)
- "dedicated BWP" (left side, on an orange bar)
- "PRBs blanking" (top and bottom edges, indicated by dashed lines and arrows)
- "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP" (top right of the dedicated BWP bar, pointing to a section of the tall dark orange block)
- "Nb1 RBGs" (right side, indicating the height of the tall dark orange block)
- "PDSCH sub area 1" (center, indicating a region between two dashed lines)
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel)" (middle section, indicated by dashed lines and arrows)
- "Highest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant" (right side, below "Nb1 RBGs" and to the right of the purple block)
- "PDSCH sub area 2" (lower center, indicating a region between two dashed lines)
- "Nb2 RBGs" (right side, indicating the height of a smaller dark orange block)
The diagram visually represents how different parts of the available radio resources (PRBs - Physical Resource Blocks) are allocated or reserved for specific purposes, such as control regions (CORESET), initial access (initial BWP), dedicated data transmission (dedicated BWP), and specific grants (DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant). The concept of "blanking" suggests periods where resources are not used or are intentionally left idle. The "Nb1 RBGs" and "Nb2 RBGs" likely refer to the number of resource block groups within specific allocated bands.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13801.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation in single dedicated BWP with PRBs blanking, common channel scheduled

If PRBs blanking is activated at higher edge (NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL > 0), in the largest PDSCH sub area where are located the reserved PRBs, gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb1) starting with the highest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area  to the smallest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just above the highest PRB of the SSB.

If PRBs blanking is not activated at higher edge  (NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL = 0), for the  largest PDSCH sub area , gNB-DU shall handle in the same way than 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2500\_replaced([11116648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp5aX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If PRBs blanking is activated at lower edge (/NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL > 0), in the smallest PDSCH sub area,  gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb2) starting with the smallest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area to the highest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just below the lowest PRBs of the SSB.

If PRBs blanking is not activated at lower edge  (NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL = 0), for the smallest PDSCH sub area , gNB-DU shall handle in the same way than 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2500\_replaced([11116648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp5aX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

On the left side, there are several colored rectangular blocks stacked vertically. From bottom to top, these are:
- A thick red block at the very bottom, labeled "PRBs blanking" below it.

Several dashed lines and arrows indicate divisions and relationships between these blocks. Text labels are placed throughout the diagram to explain the different sections:
- "PRBs blanking" is indicated at the top and bottom, associated with the red blocks.

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11181851`)
