**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11181868`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11181868:
DL resource assignment using FDM
These use cases are applicable when only one UE is scheduled in the slot.

In single dedicated BWP, specific bandwidth is configured and activated (NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true)

In the largest PDSCH sub area where are located the reserved PRBs, gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb1) starting with the last partial RBG from the top of the carrier bandwidth to the smallest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just above the PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 that is:

* if there is no common channel scheduled in the slot, the PRB above the highest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0, knowing that all the DCI 1\_0 RAT1 PDSCH grants have been allocated in a contiguous way from the bottom of the CORESET #0.
* if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled in the slot, the PRB above the highest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0.

In the smallest PDSCH sub area,  gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb2) starting with the first partial RBG from the bottom of the carrier bandwidth to the highest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just below the PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 that is:

* if there is no common channel scheduled in the slot, the PRB below the lowest PRB of the CORESET #0.
* if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled in the slot, the PRB below the lowest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0. Note: the highest full size RBG can overlap the lowest PRBs of the largest PDSCH sub area.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and resource blocks (RBs) used for various channels and control information.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side:**
\* **CORESET#0:** A blue rectangular block labeled "CORESET#0". CORESET stands for Control Resource Set, which is used for control signaling.
\* **initial BWP:** A yellow rectangular block labeled "initial BWP". This represents the initial bandwidth part allocated to a User Equipment (UE).
\* **dedicated BWP:** A larger, light orange rectangular block labeled "dedicated BWP". This indicates a dedicated bandwidth part for the UE, likely for data transmission.
\* **Purple Block:** A small purple rectangular block is positioned within the "dedicated BWP", near its bottom. Its purpose is not explicitly labeled but it might represent a specific allocation within the dedicated BWP.
**Right Side and Vertical Annotations:**
\* **Top Red Bar:** A red horizontal bar at the very top, labeled "PRBs blanked due to specific bandwidth". This signifies that certain Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) are intentionally not used due to bandwidth constraints or specific configurations.
\* **"nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP":** A vertical arrow pointing from the top red bar down to the top of the "dedicated BWP". Text next to it explains that this section represents the number of PRBs reserved for the UE within its dedicated BWP.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 1":** A vertical line extending from the top of the "dedicated BWP" down to a dashed line. This area is labeled as "PDSCH sub area 1", indicating a region for Physical Downlink Shared Channel transmission.
\* **"Nb1 RBGs":** A large orange rectangular block is shown within "PDSCH sub area 1". A vertical arrow on the right side of this block is labeled "Nb1 RBGs", indicating the number of Resource Block Groups (RBGs) in this section.
\* **"nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel)":** A vertical arrow pointing from the dashed line separating "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2" down to the top of the purple block. Text next to it indicates "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel)", suggesting a number of PRBs allocated for DCI (Downlink Control Information) format 1\_0, and that this allocation does not involve a common channel.
\* **"Highest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant":** Text to the right of the "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0" annotation, indicating the highest PRB used for a DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant.
\* **"PDSCH sub area 2":** A vertical line extending from the dashed line below "PDSCH sub area 1" down to the bottom red bar. This area is labeled as "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* **Second Orange Block:** A smaller orange rectangular block is shown within "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* **"Nb2 RBGs":** A vertical arrow on the right side of the second orange block is labeled "Nb2 RBGs", indicating the number of Resource Block Groups in this section.
\* **Bottom Red Bar:** A red horizontal bar at the very bottom, labeled "PRBs blanked due to specific bandwidth". Similar to the top red bar, this indicates unused PRBs.
The diagram visually represents how the total bandwidth is divided and allocated, with specific regions designated for control signaling (CORESSET), initial access (initial BWP), dedicated data transmission (dedicated BWP), and different sub-areas within the PDSCH for various grants and configurations. The concept of blanked PRBs highlights resource efficiency by not utilizing certain frequency resources when not needed.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13803.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation in single dedicated BWP with specific bandwidth, no common channel scheduled

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and resource blocks (RBs) allocated to a User Equipment (UE).
The diagram is structured vertically, with different colored blocks representing various resource allocations.
On the left side, there are several vertical bars:
- A thin blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A thick orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A light orange bar above a purple bar.
The right side of the diagram contains text labels and two large orange rectangular blocks representing Resource Block Groups (RBGs).
Key elements and their descriptions:
1. **Top Section**:
\* A red horizontal bar at the very top.
\* Text: "PRBs blanked due to specific bandwidth" with a double-headed arrow pointing to the top of the diagram, indicating a region of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) that are not used due to bandwidth limitations.
\* Text: "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP" with a double-headed arrow pointing to the light orange and purple bars. This signifies a number of PRBs reserved for the UE within its dedicated BWP.
\* Text: "Nb1 RBGs" with a double-headed arrow spanning the upper large orange block. This indicates the first group of Resource Block Groups (RBGs) allocated.
\* Text: "PDSCH sub area 1" with a vertical arrow indicating a specific sub-area for Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) transmission.
2. **Middle Section**:
\* Text: "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with common channel)" with a double-headed arrow pointing to the purple bar. This indicates a number of PRBs allocated for DCI (Downlink Control Information) format 1\_0, possibly for common channels.
\* Text: "Highest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant" with a double-headed arrow pointing to the top of the upper orange block.
\* Text: "Lowest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant" with a double-headed arrow pointing to the bottom of the upper orange block.
3. **Bottom Section**:
\* Text: "PDSCH sub area 2" with a vertical arrow indicating a second sub-area for PDSCH transmission.
\* Text: "Nb2 RBGs" with a double-headed arrow spanning the lower large orange block. This indicates the second group of RBGs.
\* A red horizontal bar at the very bottom.
\* Text: "PRBs blanked due to specific bandwidth" with a double-headed arrow pointing to the bottom of the diagram, similar to the top section.
The diagram visually represents how the total system bandwidth is divided and allocated, with specific regions designated for control information, reserved resources, and data transmission (PDSCH), while also accounting for unused PRBs due to bandwidth constraints. The concept of BWPs (initial and dedicated) and RBGs (Nb1 and Nb2) are central to this allocation scheme.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13803.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation in single dedicated BWP with specific bandwidth, common channel scheduled

In the largest PDSCH sub area where are located the reserved PRBs, gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb1) starting with the last partial RBG from the top of the carrier bandwidth to the smallest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just above the highest PRB of the SSB

In the smallest PDSCH sub area,  gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb2) starting with the first partial RBG from the bottom of the carrier bandwidth to the highest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just below the lowest PRBs of the SSB.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different blocks of Resource Blocks (RBs) and their assignments.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Vertical Bars):**
\* **Red Bar (Top):** Labeled "PRBs blanked due to specific bandwidth". This indicates a portion of the total available RBs that are not usable due to bandwidth limitations.
\* **Blue Bar:** Labeled "CORESET#0". This likely represents a Control Resource Set (CORESET) used for control signaling.
\* **Yellow Bar:** Labeled "initial BWP". This signifies the initial Bandwidth Part (BWP) allocated to a User Equipment (UE).
\* **Orange Bar:** Labeled "dedicated BWP". This represents a dedicated BWP for the UE, which is larger than the initial BWP.
\* **Light Orange Bar:** This block is positioned above the "dedicated BWP" and seems to be part of the overall resource allocation.
\* **Purple Bar:** This block is located within the "dedicated BWP" and is positioned below the light orange bar.
\* **Red Bar (Bottom):** Labeled "PRBs blanked due to specific bandwidth". Similar to the top red bar, this indicates unusable RBs at the lower end of the spectrum.
**Right Side (Annotations and Orange Blocks):**
\* **Top Orange Block:** This large block is labeled with a double-headed arrow indicating "Nb1 RBGs". It also has an annotation "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP", suggesting these RBs are specifically reserved for the UE within its dedicated BWP.
\* **Middle Horizontal Dashed Lines:** These lines delineate different regions.
\* The top dashed line is associated with "PD$CH sub area 1".
\* The middle dashed line is associated with "SSB" (Synchronization Signal Block).
\* The bottom dashed line is associated with "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* **"Highest PRB of SSB" and "Lowest PRB of SSB":** These labels indicate the boundaries of the RBs used by the SSB.
\* **Bottom Orange Block:** This block is labeled with a double-headed arrow indicating "Nb2 RBGs".
**Overall Interpretation:**
The diagram shows how a total bandwidth is divided. Some parts are reserved for control signaling (CORESET#0), some are for initial UE access (initial BWP), and a larger portion is dedicated to the UE's data transmission (dedicated BWP). Within the dedicated BWP, there are specific regions for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) transmission, potentially divided into sub-areas. The SSB occupies a specific range of RBs. The diagram also highlights that certain RBs are unusable due to bandwidth constraints at both the top and bottom ends of the spectrum. The "Nb1 RBGs" and "Nb2 RBGs" likely refer to the number of Resource Block Groups allocated for specific purposes.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13803.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation in single dedicated BWP with specific bandwidth in SSB slot.

gNB-DU canl allocate Nb1+ Nb2 RBGs to the dynamicswitch RAT0 UE

Note : the first and last RBG size are defined and computed with the NIDD parameters NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl/cRbStartDl and NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl.cRbSizeDl and the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4968\_replaced([11199418](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11181885`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11181885:
DL resource assignment using FDM
These use cases are applicable when only one UE is scheduled in the slot when Multi BWP bottom aligned is configured.

If the UE candidate belongs to the largest BWP1, or for the smallest PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall handle the RBG allocation in the same way as described for single dedicated BWP without PRBs blanking or specific bandwidth (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2500\_replaced([11116648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp5aX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), with PRBs blanking (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2502\_replaced([11116669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp6BY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or with specific bandwidth (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25020\_replaced([11181851](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvAj2PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If the UE candidate belongs to the BWP2, in the largest PDSCH sub area where are located the reserved PRBs, gNB-DU shall allocate the RBGs (Nb1) starting with the highest RBG (partial or full size) in the PDSCH sub area in the BWP2 to the smallest full size RBG in the PDSCH sub area just above the PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 that is:

* if there is no common channel scheduled in the slot, the PRB above the highest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0, knowing that all the DCI 1\_0 RAT1 PDSCH grants have been allocated in a contiguous way from the bottom of the CORESET #0.
* if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING) is scheduled in the slot, the PRB above the highest PRB allocated to a DCI 1\_0.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system, likely related to cellular networks. It uses colored vertical bars to represent different types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and resource blocks (RBs).
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side - BWPs and CORESET:**
\* **CORESET#0:** A thin blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
\* **initial BWP:** A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
\* **dedicated BWP:** An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
\* **dedicated BWP 2:** A green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
\* **PS BWP-3:** A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
**Right Side - Resource Allocation and Labels:**
\* **PDSCH sub area 1:** Indicated by a vertical arrow and text.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP 1:** Text describing a reserved number of PRBs within a specific BWP.
\* **Nb1 RBGs:** Text indicating a quantity of Resource Block Groups (RBGs) labeled "Nb1". This is associated with a large orange bar on the right.
\* **Highest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant:** Text indicating the highest Physical Resource Block (PRB) associated with a specific grant.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel):** Text describing a number of PRBs for DCI 1\_0, explicitly stating it's not for a common channel. This is associated with a purple bar and a portion of an orange bar.
\* **PDSCH sub area 2:** Indicated by a vertical arrow and text.
\* **Nb2 RBGs:** Text indicating a quantity of Resource Block Groups (RBGs) labeled "Nb2". This is associated with a smaller orange bar at the bottom right.
**Visual Representation:**
The diagram uses stacked and adjacent vertical bars of varying heights and colors to visually represent the allocation of resources. The text labels and arrows explain the meaning and relationships between these allocated resources. The dashed purple lines likely delineate different regions or boundaries within the resource allocation. The orange bars on the right appear to represent larger blocks of resources, possibly related to the total available RBGs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13805.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation for a UE belonging to the largest BWP (BWP1) in multi dedicated BWP configration without PRBs blanking or specific bandwidth, no common channel scheduled

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs).
The diagram shows several vertical bars of varying colors, representing different BWPs. From left to right, these are:
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A light green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
- A purple bar.
- Two dark green bars.
Horizontal dashed lines and arrows indicate different regions and allocations within the PRB spectrum.
Text labels in the diagram explain the different sections:
- "PRBs blanking" is indicated at the top and bottom, suggesting periods where PRBs are not used.
- "PDSCH sub area 1" and "PDSCH sub area 2" denote different sub-areas for Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) allocation.
- "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP 2" describes a reserved set of PRBs within "dedicated BWP 2".
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with common channel)" refers to PRBs allocated via DCI (Downlink Control Information) format 1\_0, possibly for a common channel.
- "Nb1 RBGs" and "Nb2 RBGs" likely represent Resource Block Groups (RBGs) for different configurations.
- "Highest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant" and "Lowest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant" define the range of PRBs allocated by a specific DCI.
The diagram visually represents how PRBs can be partitioned and allocated for different purposes, including control signaling (CORESET), initial access (initial BWP), dedicated data transmission (dedicated BWPs), and potentially other services (PS BWP-3). The concept of "blanking" suggests that certain PRBs might be intentionally left unused to save power or for other operational reasons. The distinction between "sub area 1" and "sub area 2" for PDSCH might relate to different scheduling or transmission strategies.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13805.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation for a UE belonging to the BWP2 in multi dedicated BWP configration with PRBs blanking with common channel scheduled

gNB-DU can allocate Nb1+ Nb2 RBGs to the dynamicswitch RAT0 UE

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11181890`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Several RAT1 UEs and one candidate UE scheduled in the slot

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11181969`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11181969:
DL resource assignment using FDM
These use cases are applicable when several UE is scheduled in the slot and the UE candidate as RAT1 belongs to the largest PDSCH sub area

The CORESET 0 can contain DCI 1\_0 mapped with CORESET#X (with X>0) and DCI 1\_0 can be split in 2 PDSCH sub areas not only in slots with common channel when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

[CB013945-A] If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True, the candidate UE belonging to the largest PDSCH sub area can be a MU paired user (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25094\_replaced([11181651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8wvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). In case the user is not a MU paired user the PDSCH sub area is reduced by nbPrbs(vUE) to facilitate for the dynamicswitch to RAT0 proceedings within the reduced PDSCH sub area. In case of MU paired user being the candidate UE, the respective PDSCH sub area gets updated by nbPrb(vUE) as well but in order to facilitate other RAT1 users to utilize the free PRBs made available due to RAT0 conversion (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB013945-A]

fig1: A candidate UE in the largest PDSCH sub area where other RAT1  UEs are scheduled in the largest PDSCH sub area 1 and the PDSCH sub area 2 too below DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (without common channel) in the Coreset 0. Single dedicated BWP without PRB blanking or specific bandwidth.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the allocation of resource blocks (RBs) in a cellular network, likely for 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource allocations for a user equipment (UE) and other UEs.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Allocation Blocks):**
\* **CORESET#0 (Blue Block):** This likely represents a Control Resource Set (CORESET) which is used for control signaling.
\* **initial BWP (Yellow Block):** This indicates the initial bandwidth part allocated to the UE.
\* **dedicated BWP (Orange Block):** This signifies a dedicated bandwidth part for the UE.
\* **Purple Block:** This block is positioned within the dedicated BWP and is labeled as "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel)". This suggests a reservation of Narrowband Physical Resource Blocks (nbPrBs) for a specific DCI (Downlink Control Information) format, possibly without a common channel.
**Right Side (Explanations and RBG Counts):**
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP:** This text points to the upper portion of the dedicated BWP, indicating reserved nbPrBs for the UE itself within its dedicated BWP.
\* **PD$CH sub area 1:** This label indicates a specific sub-area within the PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) allocation.
\* **Nb1 RBGs:** This indicates a count of Resource Block Groups (RBGs) labeled as "Nb1". The arrow points to the upper orange block, suggesting this is the range of Nb1 RBGs.
\* **Highest PRB position of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 1:** This text describes the highest PRB position for PDSCH grants associated with DCI format 1\_1 for other UEs within PDSCH sub area 1. The arrow points to the upper boundary of the orange block.
\* **nbPrbs reserved For the other UEs in dedicated BWP in PDSCH sub area 1:** This text indicates reserved nbPrBs for other UEs within the dedicated BWP, specifically within PDSCH sub area 1. The arrow points to the region between the upper and lower orange blocks.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel):** This is a repetition of the label for the purple block, reinforcing its meaning.
\* **nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWP:** This text indicates reserved nbPrBs for other UEs within the dedicated BWP. The arrow points to the region below the purple block and above the lower orange block.
\* **In PDSCH sub area 2:** This label indicates a second sub-area within the PDSCH allocation.
\* **PDSCH sub area 2:** This is a repetition of the label for the second sub-area.
\* **Lowest PRB position of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 2:** This text describes the lowest PRB position for PDSCH grants associated with DCI format 1\_1 for other UEs within PDSCH sub area 2. The arrow points to the lower boundary of the lower orange block.
\* **Nb2 RBGs:** This indicates a count of Resource Block Groups (RBGs) labeled as "Nb2". The arrow points to the lower orange block, suggesting this is the range of Nb2 RBGs.
The diagram visually represents how different parts of the available spectrum (represented by the colored blocks) are allocated for control and data channels, and how these allocations are further divided or reserved for specific purposes or groups of UEs. The dashed lines indicate boundaries of these allocated regions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13808.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). The diagram uses colored blocks to represent different types of PRB allocations and includes text labels and arrows to explain the relationships and quantities.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Vertical Blocks):**
\* **Blue Block:** Labeled "CORESET#0", indicating a control resource set.
\* **Yellow Block:** Labeled "initial BWP", representing the initial bandwidth part.
\* **Orange Block:** Labeled "dedicated BWP", signifying a dedicated bandwidth part.
**Right Side (Vertical Blocks and Labels):**
\* **Red Block (Top):** Labeled "PRBs blanking", indicating a region where PRBs are not used.
\* **Orange Block (Upper):** This block is associated with "Nb1 RBGs" (Resource Block Groups).
\* An arrow points from the top of this orange block down to a dashed line, with the label "Nb1 RBGs".
\* A dashed line above this orange block is labeled "PRBs blanking".
\* A dashed line below this orange block is labeled "PDSCH sub area 1".
\* Text to the right of this orange block states "Highest PRB position of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants In PDSCH sub area 1".
\* **Orange Block (Lower):** This block is associated with "Nb2 RBGs".
\* An arrow points from the top of this orange block down to a dashed line, with the label "Nb2 RBGs".
\* A dashed line below this orange block is labeled "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* Text to the right of this orange block states "Lowest PRB position of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 2".
\* **Peach Block (Upper):** This block is positioned within "PDSCH sub area 1".
\* A dashed line above it is labeled "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP".
\* **Purple Block:** This block is positioned within "PDSCH sub area 1".
\* A dashed line above it is labeled "nbPrbs reserved For the other UEs in dedicated BWP".
\* A dashed line below it is labeled "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with common channel)".
\* **Peach Block (Lower):** This block is positioned within "PDSCH sub area 2".
\* A dashed line above it is labeled "nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWP".
\* A dashed line below it is labeled "In PDSCH sub area 2".
\* Below this, another label reads "+PRBs blanking".
**General Annotations:**
\* Dashed lines with arrows indicate ranges or positions of PRB allocations.
\* The diagram seems to be illustrating how PRBs are divided and allocated for different purposes, such as dedicated UEs, other UEs, and specific DCI (Downlink Control Information) grants, within different sub-areas of the PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13808.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different types of resource blocks (PRBs) and their allocation to different users or functionalities.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Blocks):**
\* **CORESET#0:** A blue block labeled "CORESET#0", likely representing a control resource set.
\* **initial BWP:** A yellow block labeled "initial BWP", indicating an initial bandwidth part.
\* **dedicated BWP:** A large orange block labeled "dedicated BWP", representing a dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **Purple Block:** A small purple block within the "dedicated BWP", its purpose is not explicitly labeled but it's positioned between the "dedicated BWP" and the SSB.
\* **Red Block:** A red block at the bottom, labeled "PRBs blanked due to specific bandwidth".
\* **Orange Blocks:** Two large orange blocks, one in the upper half and one in the lower half, positioned to the right of the "dedicated BWP". These are associated with "Nb1 RBGs" and "Nb2 RBGs" respectively.
**Right Side (Labels and Allocations):**
\* **PRBs blanked due to specific bandwidth:** Text at the top, indicating that some PRBs are intentionally not used due to bandwidth constraints.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP:** Text indicating a reservation of "nbPrbs" (number of PRBs) for a specific UE within the dedicated BWP. This is visually represented by a double-headed arrow spanning a portion of the "dedicated BWP".
\* **PDSCH sub area 1:** A label indicating a specific sub-area for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) allocation.
\* **Nb1 RBGs:** Text indicating "Nb1 Resource Block Groups" associated with the upper orange block.
\* **Highest PRB position of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 1:** Text describing the highest PRB position for PDSCH grants from another DCI (Downlink Control Information) within PDSCH sub area 1. This is visually indicated by a double-headed arrow pointing to the top of the upper orange block.
\* **nbPrbs reserved For the other UEs in dedicated BWP:** Text indicating a reservation of "nbPrbs" for other UEs within the dedicated BWP. This is visually represented by a double-headed arrow spanning a portion of the "dedicated BWP" below the first "nbPrbs reserved" section.
\* **SSB:** Text indicating "SSB" (Synchronization Signal Block). The purple block is positioned below the first "nbPrbs reserved" section and above the SSB label.
\* **nbPrbs reserved for the other UEs in dedicated BWP:** Text indicating a reservation of "nbPrbs" for other UEs within the dedicated BWP, specifically in relation to the SSB. This is visually represented by a double-headed arrow spanning a portion of the "dedicated BWP" below the SSB label.
\* **In PDSCH sub area 2:** Text indicating a specific sub-area for PDSCH allocation.
\* **Lowest PRB position of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 2:** Text describing the lowest PRB position for PDSCH grants from another DCI within PDSCH sub area 2. This is visually indicated by a double-headed arrow pointing to the bottom of the lower orange block.
\* **Nb2 RBGs:** Text indicating "Nb2 Resource Block Groups" associated with the lower orange block.
\* **PRBs blanked due to specific bandwidth:** Text at the bottom, similar to the top, indicating blanked PRBs.
The diagram visually separates the resource allocation into different sections, likely representing different operational modes or user groups, with specific reservations and constraints highlighted. The dashed lines indicate boundaries of these sub-areas and allocations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13808.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

fig4: A candidate UE which belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1 in the largest PDSCH sub area where other UEs are scheduled in the largest PDSCH sub area 1 and the PDSCH sub area 2 too below DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (without common channel) in the Coreset 0. Multi dedicated BWPs without PRB blanking or specific bandwidth.

[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the allocation of Resource Blocks (RBs) for different User Equipments (UEs) and their associated Bandwidth Parts (BWPs) in a cellular communication system. The diagram uses colored bars to represent these allocations.
On the left side, there are several vertical bars representing different BWPs and a CORESET. From left to right, these are:
- A dark blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
Following these bars, there are several horizontal dashed lines and colored blocks indicating different regions and reservations.
To the right of the bars, there are labels and arrows indicating the distribution of RBs:
- A large orange block is positioned above a dashed line labeled "PDSCH sub area 1". This block is associated with "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP 1".
- To the right of this, "Nb1 RBGs" is indicated.
- Below "PDSCH sub area 1", there is another dashed line. Between these two dashed lines, there is a region labeled "nbPrbs reserved For the other UEs in dedicated BWPs".
- Further down, there is a dashed line labeled "PDSCH sub area 2". Between the "PDSCH sub area 1" dashed line and the "PDSCH sub area 2" dashed line, there is a region labeled "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel)". Below this, there is another region labeled "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWPs".
- To the right of these regions, there are labels indicating "Highest PRB of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 1" and "Lowest PRB of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 2".
- A second large orange block is positioned below the "PDSCH sub area 2" dashed line. This block is associated with "Nb2 RBGs".
The diagram visually represents how PRBs are partitioned and allocated for different purposes, including dedicated BWPs for specific UEs, reservations for other UEs, and grants for PDSCH transmissions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13808.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

Multi dedicated BWP with PRB blanking .

[image-4][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated physical resource blocks (PRBs).
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (Resource Blocks Allocation):**
\* **CORESET#0:** A blue block, likely representing a control resource set.
\* **Initial BWP:** A yellow block, representing the initial bandwidth part.
\* **Dedicated BWP:** An orange block, indicating a dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **Dedicated BWP 2:** A green block, representing a second dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **PS BWP-3:** A light blue block, possibly indicating a Paging Service or a specific type of bandwidth part.
\* **Purple Block:** A small purple block, its specific function is not explicitly labeled but it's positioned within the PS BWP-3.
\* **Red Block:** A red block at the bottom, labeled as "PRBs blanking," indicating a region of unused or blanked PRBs.
**Right Side (Explanations and Allocations):**
\* **PRBs blanking:** Text indicating the blanked PRB region at the top.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 1:** Text describing reserved non-beacon PRBs for other UEs in the first dedicated BWP. This is associated with a dashed orange line.
\* **Lowest PRB position of DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants Of the BWP 1:** Text explaining the lowest PRB position for DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants for BWP 1. This is indicated by a dashed orange line.
\* **Nb1 RBGs:** Text indicating "Nb1 Resource Block Groups" with a vertical arrow showing a range.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP 2:** Text describing reserved non-beacon PRBs for a specific UE in the second dedicated BWP. This is associated with a dashed purple line.
\* **PDSCH sub area 1:** Text indicating a sub-area for PDSCH.
\* **Highest PRB position of DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants Of the BWP 2 and 3:** Text explaining the highest PRB position for DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants for BWPs 2 and 3. This is associated with a dashed orange line.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 2 and 3:** Text describing reserved non-beacon PRBs for other UEs in dedicated BWPs 2 and 3. This is associated with a dashed purple line.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with common channel):** Text describing reserved non-beacon PRBs for DCI 1\_0, possibly associated with a common channel. This is associated with a dashed orange line.
\* **PDSCH sub area 2:** Text indicating a second sub-area for PDSCH.
\* **Lowest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant:** Text explaining the lowest PRB for a DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant. This is associated with a dashed purple line.
\* **Nb2 RBGs:** Text indicating "Nb2 Resource Block Groups" with a vertical arrow showing a range.
\* **PRBs blanking:** Text indicating the blanked PRB region at the bottom.
The diagram visually represents how PRBs are segmented and allocated for different purposes and users within various bandwidth parts, highlighting reserved resources and grant positions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13808.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

[image-5][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) in a cellular network, likely 5G NR, for different types of data transmission and user equipment (UE) configurations. The diagram uses colored bars to represent PRB allocations and text labels to explain their purpose.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side (PRB Allocation Representation):**
\* **CORESET#0 (Blue Bar):** Represents a Control Resource Set (CORESET) for initial access or control signaling.
\* **Initial BWP (Yellow Bar):** Represents the initial Bandwidth Part (BWP) for a UE, likely used for initial data transmission.
\* **dedicated BWP (Orange Bar):** Represents a dedicated BWP for a UE, suggesting a more optimized or specific configuration.
\* **dedicated BWP 2 (Green Bar):** Represents a second dedicated BWP for a UE.
\* **PS BWP-3 (Light Blue Bar):** Represents a "PS" (likely Physical Shared) BWP, possibly for shared data channels, and designated as BWP-3.
\* **Purple Bar:** A smaller allocation within the PS BWP-3, its specific purpose is not explicitly labeled but is positioned below the "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0" section.
\* **Another Green Bar:** Located to the right of the light blue bar, it extends higher and is associated with "Nb1 RBGs" and "Highest PRB position of DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants".
\* **Another Orange Bar:** Located at the bottom right, it is associated with "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWPs" and "PRBs blanking".
\* **Red Bar:** A thick red bar at the very bottom, spanning across the entire width, labeled as "PRBs blanking".
**Right Side (Explanations and Labels):**
\* **PRBs blanking:** Indicates PRBs that are not used for transmission.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 1:** Refers to a number of PRBs reserved for other UEs using dedicated BWP 1.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the UE in PDSCH sub area 1 dedicated BWP 2:** Refers to PRBs reserved for the UE in a specific sub-area (PDSCH sub area 1) within dedicated BWP 2.
\* **Nb1 RBGs:** Likely refers to Resource Block Groups (RBGs) of size Nb1.
\* **Highest PRB position of DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants Of the BWP 2 and 3:** Indicates the highest PRB used for PDSCH grants with DCI format 1\_1, applicable to BWPs 2 and 3.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 2 and 3:** Refers to PRBs reserved for other UEs using dedicated BWPs 2 and 3.
\* **nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with common channel):** Refers to a number of PRBs reserved for DCI format 1\_0, possibly associated with a common channel.
\* **PDSCH sub area 2:** Indicates a second sub-area for PDSCH transmission.
\* **Lowest PRB of DCI 1\_0 PDSCH grant Nb2 RBGs:** Indicates the lowest PRB used for a DCI format 1\_0 PDSCH grant, associated with RBGs of size Nb2.
\* **Highest PRB position (PDSCH sub area 2) in dedicated BWPs:** Indicates the highest PRB position within PDSCH sub area 2, used in dedicated BWPs.
The diagram visually separates different PRB usage scenarios, highlighting how PRBs are allocated for control signaling, initial access, dedicated data transmission, and potentially shared data channels, while also indicating reserved PRBs for other UEs and blanked PRBs. The concept of "sub areas" within PDSCH transmission is also depicted.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13808.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

fig7: In TRS slot with an additional blanked PRBs area is created at TRS boundary, a candidate UE which belongs to BWP1 in the largest PDSCH sub area 2 where other UEs are scheduled in the both PDSCH sub areas  above and below this additional blanked PRBs area. PRB GRANULARITY is applied on all the PDSCH sub areas.

[image-6][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource block (RB) allocation and blanking in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different sub-areas and bandwidth parts (BWPs) with associated resource allocations and blanking strategies.
The diagram is structured vertically, with different colored blocks representing allocated or blanked resource blocks. Text labels and arrows explain the purpose and boundaries of these blocks.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side:**
\* **Dedicated BWP:** Represented by a yellow vertical bar.
\* **Dedicated BWP 2:** Represented by a green vertical bar, positioned next to the "Dedicated BWP".
\* **PS BWP:** Represented by a light blue vertical bar, positioned below the "Dedicated BWP 2".
**Right Side (Resource Allocation and Blanking Areas):**
The diagram is divided into two main sections, each with similar structures but potentially different configurations.
**Top Section:**
\* **High edge PRBs blanking:** Indicated by a red horizontal bar at the very top.
\* **Nb1 RBGs:** A tall, orange vertical block labeled "Nb1 RBGs".
\* Above this block, an arrow points to "Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in old PDSCH sub area 2".
\* Below this block, an arrow points to "Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs In old PDSCH sub area 2".
\* The text "Dynamicswitch RATO" is placed between these two labels.
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 2 PRB GRANULARITY:** This label is associated with a large, light orange rectangular block.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other Ues in BWP 1 and 2 in PDSCH sub area 2:** This describes a section of resource blocks.
\* A light orange block is shown above a green block, and this combined area is likely what's being described.
\* To the right of this area, "New PDSCH sub area 1 PRB GRANULARITY" is indicated.
\* An arrow points from the top of this reserved area to "Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 2".
**Bottom Section:**
\* **Additional PRBs blanking area (PDSCH):** Indicated by a red horizontal bar below the top section.
\* **Nb2 RBGs:** A shorter, orange vertical block labeled "Nb2 RBGs".
\* Above this block, an arrow points to "Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in old PDSCH sub area 1".
\* Below this block, an arrow points to "Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs In old PDSCH sub area 1".
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 1 PRB GRANULARITY:** This label is associated with a light orange rectangular block.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs In BWP 1, 2 and 3 in PDSCH sub area 1:** This describes a section of resource blocks.
\* A light orange block is shown above a green block, and this combined area is likely what's being described.
\* To the right of this area, "New PDSCH sub area 1 PRB GRANULARITY" is indicated.
\* An arrow points from the top of this reserved area to "Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1".
\* **PRBs blanking:** Indicated by a red horizontal bar at the very bottom.
The diagram visually represents how different types of data (dedicated, other UEs) and network configurations (old PDSCH sub-areas, new PDSCH sub-areas, RATO) influence the allocation and blanking of physical resource blocks. The use of color and distinct blocks helps differentiate these components.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13808.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]

fig8: In SSB slot with TRS symbols with an additional blanked PRBs area is created at TRS boundary, a candidate UE which belongs to BWP2 in the largest PDSCH sub area 3 where other UEs are scheduled in the  PDSCH sub areas 1, 2 and 3 above this additional blanked PRBs area, above and below SSB.

[image-7][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource blocks (RBs) or physical resource blocks (PRBs).
The diagram uses colored bars to represent different BWPs and their states. From left to right, there's a yellow bar labeled "CORESET#0", followed by a vertical orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP", and then a light green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2". These bars appear to represent different configurations or allocations.
The main part of the diagram shows stacked colored blocks, primarily shades of green, with some orange, purple, and red. These blocks are organized vertically and are associated with various labels and annotations indicating their purpose and allocation.
Key elements and their descriptions:
\* **High edge PRBs blanking**: Indicated by a red horizontal bar at the top, suggesting a region of PRBs that are not used or are intentionally blanked.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other Ues in BWP 1 in old PDSCH sub area 1**: Represented by a light orange block above a green block.
\* **Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in old PDSCH sub area 2**: An annotation pointing to the top of a green block.
\* **Nb1 RBGs Dynamicswitch RATO**: A green block labeled "Nb1 RBGs" and "Dynamicswitch RATO", suggesting a set of resource blocks that can be dynamically switched.
\* **Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs In old PDSCH sub area 2**: An annotation pointing to the bottom of a green block.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other Ues in BWP 2 in old PDSCH sub area 3**: Represented by a light green block.
\* **New PDSCH sub area 3**: An annotation indicating a new sub-area for PDSCH.
\* **Additional PRBs blanking area (PDSCH)**: Indicated by a red horizontal bar below the "nbPrbs reserved of the other Ues in BWP 2" block.
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 3**: An annotation indicating the starting PRB for a specific PDSCH sub-area.
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 2**: An annotation pointing to a horizontal dashed line.
\* **SSB**: A purple block labeled "SSB", likely representing a Synchronization Signal Block.
\* **Nb2 RBGs**: A green block labeled "Nb2 RBGs".
\* **Highest and lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in old PDSCH sub area 2**: Annotations pointing to the top and bottom of the "Nb2 RBGs" block.
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 2**: An annotation indicating the starting PRB for PDSCH sub-area 2.
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 1**: An annotation pointing to a horizontal dashed line.
\* **Nb3 RBGs**: A green block labeled "Nb3 RBGs".
\* **Highest and lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in old PDSCH sub area 1**: Annotations pointing to the top and bottom of the "Nb3 RBGs" block.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs In BWP 1, 2 and 3 in PDSCH sub area 1**: Represented by a light orange block above a green block.
\* **New PDSCH sub area 1**: An annotation indicating a new sub-area for PDSCH.
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1**: An annotation indicating the starting PRB for PDSCH sub-area 1.
\* **Low edge PRBs blanking**: Indicated by a red horizontal bar at the bottom, suggesting another region of blanked PRBs.
The diagram uses dashed lines to demarcate different sub-areas and ranges of PRBs. Arrows are used to indicate specific points or ranges within the resource allocation. The overall structure suggests a breakdown of the total available PRBs into different categories based on their usage, reservation, or blanking status for different BWPs and PDSCH sub-areas.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13808.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]

fig9: In SSB slot with TRS symbols with an additional blanked PRBs area is created at TRS boundary (SSB overlaps the additonal blanked PRBs area), a candidate UE which belongs to BWP1 in the largest PDSCH sub area 2 where other UEs are scheduled in the both PDSCH sub areas 1 and 1 above this additional blanked PRBs area and below SSB.

[image-8][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G NR (New Radio). It depicts different types of resource blocks (RBs) and their allocation across various sub-areas and bandwidth parts (BWPs).
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Vertical Bars (Resource Blocks):**
\* **Yellow Bar:** Labeled "dedicated BWP".
\* **Green Bar:** Labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
\* **Orange Bar (Top):** Represents "Nb1 RBGs" (likely Number of RBGs for Bandwidth Part 1) and is associated with "Highest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in old PDSCH sub area 2".
\* **Peach Bar (Top):** Part of the "Nb1 RBGs" allocation.
\* **Peach Bar (Middle):** Part of "nbPrbs reserved of the other Ues in BWP 1 and 2 in old PDSCH sub area 2".
\* **Green Bar (Middle):** Also part of "nbPrbs reserved of the other Ues in BWP 1 and 2 in old PDSCH sub area 2".
\* **Purple Bar:** Labeled "SSB" (Synchronization Signal Block).
\* **Orange Bar (Bottom):** Represents "Nb2 RBGs" (likely Number of RBGs for Bandwidth Part 2) and is associated with "Highest and lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in old PDSCH sub area 1".
\* **Peach Bar (Bottom):** Part of "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs In BWP 1, 2 and 3 in PDSCH sub area 1".
\* **Red Bars (Top and Bottom):** These appear to represent blanking areas. The top red bar is labeled "High edge PRBs blanking", and the bottom red bar is labeled "Low edge PRBs blanking".
**Text Labels and Annotations:**
\* **CORESET#0:** Indicates a control resource set.
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 2:** Refers to a specific region for Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) allocation.
\* **Old PDSCH sub area 1:** Another region for PDSCH allocation.
\* **Dynamicswitch RATO:** Suggests a dynamic switching mechanism.
\* **Lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs In old PDSCH sub area 2:** Defines the lower boundary of pre-booked RBs.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other Ues in BWP 1 and 2 in old PDSCH sub area 2:** Indicates reserved resource blocks for other users in specific BWPs and sub-areas.
\* **New PDSCH sub area 2:** Refers to a newer region for PDSCH allocation.
\* **Additional PRBs blanking area (PDSCH):** A blanking area specifically for PDSCH.
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 2:** Marks the starting resource block for PDSCH in sub-area 2.
\* **Highest and lowest PRB of the Pre booked RBGs in old PDSCH sub area 1:** Defines the range of pre-booked RBs in sub-area 1.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs In BWP 1, 2 and 3 in PDSCH sub area 1:** Reserved resource blocks for other users in BWPs 1, 2, and 3 within PDSCH sub-area 1.
\* **New PDSCH sub area 1:** Refers to a newer region for PDSCH allocation.
\* **Start PRB of PDSCH sub area 1:** Marks the starting resource block for PDSCH in sub-area 1.
**Arrows:**
Various double-headed arrows indicate the extent or range of different resource allocations or blanking areas. For example, arrows show the span of "Nb1 RBGs", "nbPrbs reserved of the other Ues", and "Additional PRBs blanking area (PDSCH)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13808.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8]

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11181980`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
In the PDSCH sub area below the CORESET 0 or SSB:

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11182021`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182021:
DL resource assignment using FDM
If the candidate UE as RAT1 does not belong to the largest PDSCH sub area (above SSB/CORESET #0/Initial BWP):

In the PDSCH sub area below the CORESET 0 or SSB:

If several UEs are scheduled in the dedicated BWPs in the PDSCH sub area,

[CB013945-A] Note: If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDSCH sub area update. Below requirement defines the rules for further update of the reduced PDSCH sub area for non-MU paired users depending on whether the initial PDSCH sub area size was updated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or not.  [End CB013945-A]

1) If PRBs are blanked at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area,

gNB-DU shall determine the highest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the dedicated BWP(p) in this way: it is the sum of the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p)  reduced by the number of previously reserved PRBs as RAT1 for the candidate UE in the PDSCH sub area (n+1):

PrbNumOtherUesBwpPos = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] +   [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by the subscript 'p'. The term being summed is 'nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p, n + 1)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13812.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] - nbReservedPrbsUe

Where:

- rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] is the start Prb of the PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n+1) is the number of reserved PRBs in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- nbReservedPrbsUe is the number of PRBs previously reserved for the UE in RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area

Note: If a common channel is scheduled (SIB1, SI, PAGING) and a DCI 1\_0 is allocated below the common channel, nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) is not handled for the highest PRB position, because the DCI 1\_0 are allocated below the common channel and not at the bottom of the PDSCh sub area.

Note: if there is no other RAT1 UE scheduled, the number  [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by the subscript 'p'. The term being summed is 'nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p, n + 1)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13812.001.png[/image\_path][/image-1] is equal to 0

gNB-DU shall start the allocation with the highest full size RBG:

 below the lowest PRB allocated of a DCI 1\_0 grants if a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING),  rach msg2, rach msg4 or any other DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode in the CORESET 0 or in the CORESET#X (with X>0)  is scheduled in the slot.

 below the SSB, if SSB slot and if no DCI 1\_0.

Note:  if a common channel is scheduled, the lowest PRB allocated of a DCI 1\_0 can be lower than the start PRB of the common channel if a DCI 1\_0 is allocated below the common channel. It is true too without common channel when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0).

and shall continue in high2low direction and in a contiguous way to way to the highest full size RBG above the highest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to all the dedicated BWP(p).

Note: If the candidate UE has no reserved PRB as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area,  the number of PRB to remove due to the candidate UE as RAT is NULL (nbReservedPrbsUe = 0) and gNB-DU shall follow the same rules than in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25663\_replaced([11181773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvAj0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

2) otherwise (no blanked PRB at the bottom of the PDSCH sub area)

gNB-DU shall determine the lowest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the dedicated BWP(p) in this way: it is the sum of the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and the PDSCH sub area size reduced by the sum of the number of PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) and the number of PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 (if there is a common channel scheduled (SIB1, SI, PAGING))  and reduced too by the number of previously reserved PRBs as RAT1 for the candidateUE in the PDSCH sub area (n+1):

PrbNumOtherUesBwpPos = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] + rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1]    - (  [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by the subscript 'p'. The term being summed is 'nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p, n + 1)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13812.001.png[/image\_path][/image-2] + nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) ) - nbReservedPrbsUe

Where:

- rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1] and rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] are the size and the start Prb of the PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p,n+1) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP(p) within the PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the CORESET #0 within a PDSCH sub area (n+1) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If there is no PRB belonging to the CORESET #0 if a common channel is scheduled or in another slot (SSB, no common channel scheduled), it is equal to 0.

- nbReservedPrbsUe is the number of PRBs previously reserved for the UE as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area n+1

Note:  If the candidate UE has reserved PRBs as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area (nbReservedPrbsUe > 0 ), this lowest PRB position can be computed inthis way too:

PrbNumOtherUesBwpPos = rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+1] + nbReservedPrbsUe

Note: if there is no other RAT1 UE scheduled, the number  [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation. The summation symbol is followed by the subscript 'p'. The term being summed is 'nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(p, n + 1)'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13812.001.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is equal to 0

gNB-DU shall start the allocation with the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area and shall continue in low2high direction and in a contiguous way to the highest full size RBG below the lowest PRB position of the DCI 1\_1 granted for the other RAT1 UEs which belong to the dedicated BWP(p).

Note: If the candidate UE has no reserved PRB as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area,  the number of PRB to remove due to the candidate UE as RAT is NULL (nbReservedPrbsUe = 0) and gNB-DU shall follow the same rules than in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25663\_replaced([11181773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvAj0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The figures for these use cases are under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25510\_replaced([11182036](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBK5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

gNB-DU shall apply the same rules than the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25662\_replaced([11181741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8zPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when the candidate UE has no reserved PRB as RAT1 in the PDSCH sub area.

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-5.0-1.0-2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11182097`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182097:
DL resource assignment using FDM
These use cases are applicable when several UE is scheduled in the slot and. the UE candidate belongs to the largest PDSCH sub area

The CORESET 0 can contain DCI 1\_0 mapped with CORESET#X (with X>0) and DCI 1\_0 can be split in 2 PDSCH sub areas not only in slots with common channel when the DCI 1\_0 for the common channel (SIB1, SI , PAGING), rach msg2, rach msg4 are mapped on CORESET 0 but the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode for transient phase (Type3) are mapped on a CORESET#X (with X>0)

[CB013945-A] Note: If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True and vUE(s) are scheduled in the slot refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDSCH sub area update. Below requirement defines the rules for further update of the reduced PDSCH sub area for non-MU paired users depending on whether the initial PDSCH sub area size was updated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27357\_replaced([11176769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or not.[End CB013945-A]

fig1 (partial view of PDSCH sub area 2): A candidate UE which belongs to the largest dedicated BWP1 in the PDSCH sub area 2 where  UEs are scheduled in the PDSCH sub area 2 too below DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode (without common channel) in the Coreset 0.Multi dedicated BWP without PRB blanking or specific bandwidth.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). It depicts different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated resource blocks (RBs) or resource groups (RGs).
The diagram is structured vertically, with different colored blocks representing different BWPs. From left to right, the blocks are:
\* **Blue block:** Labeled "CORESET#0". This is likely a control resource set used for initial access or control signaling.
\* **Yellow block:** Labeled "initial BWP". This represents the initial bandwidth part allocated to a User Equipment (UE).
\* **Orange block:** Labeled "dedicated BWP". This indicates a dedicated bandwidth part for a specific UE.
\* **Green block:** Labeled "dedicated BWP 2". This suggests a second dedicated bandwidth part for a UE.
\* **Light blue block:** Labeled "PS BWP-3". "PS" might stand for "Physical Shared" or a similar term, indicating a shared bandwidth part.
To the right of these blocks, there are annotations and further resource allocations:
\* **Purple block:** Positioned above the light blue block, it is associated with the text "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (no common channel)". "nbPrbs" likely refers to the number of physical resource blocks. DCI 1\_0 is a Downlink Control Information format. This section seems to represent resource allocation for a common channel or a specific DCI format.
\* **Green block (right side):** Positioned above the orange block (right side), it is associated with the text "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWPs in PDSCH sub area 2". This indicates reserved resources for other UEs within a specific PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) sub-area.
\* **Orange block (right side):** Positioned below the green block (right side), it is associated with the text "Lowest PRB position of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 2". This indicates the lowest physical resource block position for other UEs receiving PDSCH grants.
\* **Orange block (bottom right):** A distinct orange block is shown at the bottom right, labeled with "Nb2 RBGs". This represents a quantity of resource groups, possibly for a specific type of transmission or allocation.
Dashed lines with arrows indicate the extent of these resource allocations and their relationships. The text "PDSCH sub area 2" is also present, defining a specific region within the PDSCH allocation.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and control information allocations are mapped onto the available spectrum, highlighting reserved resources for different UEs and channel types.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13817.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

fig2  (partial view of PDSCH sub area 2) : A candidate UE which belongs to the largets dedicated BWP1 in the PDSCH sub area 2 where UEs are scheduled in the PDSCH sub area 2 too below DCI 1\_0 allocated below a common channel in the Coreset 0 . Multi dedicated BWP without PRB blanking or specific bandwidth

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the allocation of Resource Blocks (RBs) in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR) or a similar technology. The diagram uses colored blocks to represent different types of resource allocations and text labels to explain them.
On the left side, there are vertical colored bars representing different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and a control resource set (CORESET). From left to right, these are:
- A dark blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
- A light purple bar above a light green bar, which are part of a larger allocation.
- A light orange bar below the light green bar.
On the right side, text labels and arrows explain the resource allocation.
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 with common channel)" is associated with the top portion of the allocation, indicated by a dashed line and an upward arrow.
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 Below common channel)" is associated with a segment below the previous one, also indicated by dashed lines and arrows.
- "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWPs in PDSCH sub area 2" is a longer text label pointing to a larger section of the diagram.
- "Lowest PRB position of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 2" is another text label explaining a specific positioning.
- "PDSCH sub area 2" is a label indicating a specific region within the resource allocation.
- "nbPrbs reserved of the UE in dedicated BWP 1" is a label pointing to the light orange bar.
- A dark orange bar is shown on the far right, with a label "Nb2 RBGs" next to it, indicating the number of resource blocks in this allocation.
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and control information (DCI) are mapped onto physical resource blocks, with specific areas reserved for different users or purposes. The concept of "sub area 2" suggests a division of the PDSCH resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13817.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

- "initial BWP" is a yellow bar next to CORESET#0.

To the right of these bars, there are annotations and colored blocks indicating resource usage and reservations:

- Below "PDSCH sub area 2", there is an orange block and a green block.

- At the very bottom, there is a red bar, and an annotation "PRBs blanking" points to the space below the red bar.
The diagram visually represents how PRBs are allocated or reserved for different purposes and BWPs, distinguishing between common and dedicated channels, and indicating specific sub-areas within the PDSCH.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13817.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

[image-4][image\_desc]The image is a bar chart illustrating different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their allocation in a PDSCH sub-area. The bars are color-coded and labeled with text.
On the left side, there are several bars representing different BWPs:
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- An orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
- Below the light blue bar, there are three smaller stacked bars in purple, light blue, and peach colors.
The chart also indicates different sub-areas and resource allocations:
- A dashed purple line spans the top of the orange and green bars, and a label "PDSCH sub area 1" is placed below this line, indicating the extent of this sub-area.
- To the right of the "PDSCH sub area 1" label, there is a tall orange bar.
- A double-headed arrow connects the top and bottom of this orange bar, with a label "Nb1 RBGs" next to it.
- Below the "PDSCH sub area 1" line, there are several text labels and arrows indicating resource reservations:
- "nbPrbs reserved For the other UEs in dedicated BWPs" is written with a double-headed arrow pointing to the stacked bars below the "PS BWP-3" bar.
- "Highest PRB of other DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants in PDSCH sub area 1" is written to the right of the stacked bars, with a double-headed arrow pointing to the top of the tall orange bar on the right.
- "nbPrbs DCI 1\_0 (with or without common channel)" is written below the "nbPrbs reserved" label, with a double-headed arrow pointing to the bottom of the stacked bars.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13817.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

fig 6 (partial view of PDSCH sub area 2): A candidate UE which belongs to the dedicated BWP2 in the PDSCH sub area 1 where UEs are scheduled in the PDSCH sub area 1 above SSB. Multi dedicated BWP with  PRB blanking. The PRBs reserved for BWP1 overlap the BWP2

[image-5][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the allocation of Resource Blocks (RBs) in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). The diagram uses colored bars of varying heights to represent different types of resource allocations.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side - Resource Allocation:**
\* **CORESET#0 (Blue Bar):** This is the first element, indicating a control resource set.
\* **initial BWP (Yellow Bar):** Following CORESET#0, this represents the initial bandwidth part.
\* **dedicated BWP (Orange Bar):** This bar is taller than the initial BWP and is labeled as a dedicated bandwidth part.
\* **dedicated BWP 2 (Green Bar):** Another dedicated bandwidth part, significantly taller than the previous ones.
\* **PS BWP-3 (Light Blue Bar):** This bar represents a "PS BWP-3," likely a power-saving or secondary bandwidth part.
\* **Purple Bar:** Located below PS BWP-3, this bar's specific designation is not explicitly labeled with text but is positioned above the "SSB" label.
**Right Side - Explanations and Positions:**
\* **PRBs blanking (Red Bar at the top):** A red bar at the very top, indicating blanked-out PRBs.
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 1 (Light Orange Bar):** This bar is positioned above the "dedicated BWP 1" section and is labeled as reserved PRBs for other UEs in that specific BWP.
\* **PDSCH sub area 1 (Vertical Line):** A vertical line labeled "PDSCH sub area 1" divides the diagram, suggesting different sub-areas for PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) allocation.
\* **Lowest PRB position of DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants Of the BWP 1:** This text annotation points to the upper boundary of the "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 1" section, indicating the lowest PRB position for specific PDSCH grants within BWP 1.
\* **Nb1 RBGs (Vertical Arrow and Text):** A vertical arrow labeled "Nb1 RBGs" points from the "Lowest PRB position..." down to the "Highest PRB position..." line, indicating a quantity of Resource Block Groups (RBGs).
\* **nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 2 and 3 (Green Bar):** A green bar is shown in the lower half of the diagram, labeled as reserved PRBs for other UEs in dedicated BWPs 2 and 3.
\* **SSB (Small Blue Bar):** A small blue bar at the bottom, labeled "SSB," likely representing a Synchronization Signal Block.
\* **Highest PRB position of DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants Of the BWP 2 and 3:** This text annotation points to the lower boundary of the green bar representing reserved PRBs for BWPs 2 and 3, indicating the highest PRB position for specific PDSCH grants within those BWPs.
**Overall Structure:**
The diagram visually represents how different types of resources (control, data, reserved, blanking) are organized and allocated within a given spectrum. The vertical axis represents the PRB index, and the horizontal axis shows different types of allocations or sub-areas. The dashed lines indicate boundaries or reference points for these allocations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13817.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

fig 6 (partial view of PDSCH sub area 2): A candidate UE which belongs to the BWP2 in the PDSCH sub area 1 where UEs are scheduled in the PDSCH sub area 1 above SSB. Multi dedicated BWP with  PRB blanking. The PRBs reserved for BWP1 do not overlap the BWP2

[image-6][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) in a cellular network, likely 5G New Radio (NR). The diagram shows several colored bars representing different types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and control resources.
From left to right, the bars are:
- A blue bar labeled "CORESET#0".
- A yellow bar labeled "initial BWP".
- A tall orange bar labeled "dedicated BWP".
- A tall green bar labeled "dedicated BWP 2".
- A light blue bar labeled "PS BWP-3".
- A light green bar.
- A light purple bar.
Above the bars, there are annotations indicating different resource allocations and their positions:
- A red bar at the top is labeled "PRBs blanking".
- An orange rectangle is positioned above the "dedicated BWP" and "dedicated BWP 2" bars.
- A vertical line labeled "PDSCH sub area 1" extends from the top of the diagram down to the level of the "PS BWP-3" bar.
- A green bar is positioned to the right, representing a different allocation.
There are also several annotations with arrows indicating ranges and definitions:
- An arrow pointing down from the "PRBs blanking" level to the top of the orange rectangle is labeled "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP-1".
- A dashed purple line indicates the "Lowest PRB position of DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants Of the BWP 1".
- Below this, "Nb1 RBGs" is indicated with a vertical arrow.
- Another set of annotations on the right side relates to the green bar:
- A dashed purple line indicates the "Highest PRB position of DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants Of the BWP 2 and 3".
- On the left side, below the "PS BWP-3" bar, there are annotations related to other UEs and SSB:
- An arrow pointing down from the top of the light green bar to the top of the light purple bar is labeled "nbPrbs reserved of the other UEs in dedicated BWP 2 and 3".
- A short arrow pointing down from the top of the light purple bar to the bottom of the light purple bar is labeled "SSB".
The diagram visually represents how PRBs are partitioned and allocated for different purposes, including control signaling (CORESET#0), initial access (initial BWP), dedicated data transmission for specific BWPs, and potentially reserved resources for other users or system information (SSB). The concept of Bandwidth Parts (BWPs) and their associated PRB allocations is central to the diagram.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13817.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]
