**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11182489`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182489:
DL resource assignment using FDM
When NRCELL.actDlInterferenceAvoidance = True (DL Interference Avoidance) which is a TDD only feature that minimizes inter-cell interference for high performance (HP) UEs (high MCS, high rank, high data rate, in largest BWP) by allocating resources for HP UEs at one edge of the bandwidth (HighToLow), while allocating resources for non-HP UEs at the other edge of the bandwidth (LowToHigh).  Given that there are relatively few HP UEs at an instance in time it is typical that the HP portion of the bandwidth is empty in adjacent cells and therefore the inter-cell interference is reduced for the HP UEs.  See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8432\_replaced([11181306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8194\_replaced([11182952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details of the HP UE allocation algorithm.
 
When NRCELL.actRAT1Alloc = True, gNB-DU shall randomize the StartPrb for the first UE randomly picked, and when HP DL Interference Avoidance is enabled at the same time (NRCELL. actDlInterferenceAvoidance = True) the objectives of both functions are met, i.e., the HP UEs must be scheduled in a part of the bandwidth (HighToLow) separate from non-HP UEs (LowToHigh) according to DL interference avoidance algorithm, and the non-HP UEs need to be allocated PRBs according to StartPrb Randomization algorithm as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24819\_replaced([11182450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDnJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The following steps will be taken to achieve this goal in a cell with a single dedicated BWP.
 gNB-DU shall allocate PRBs to HP UEs from HighToLow according to the DL interference avoidance algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8194\_replaced([11182952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 The StartPrb randomization algorithms described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24819\_replaced([11182450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDnJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) will be followed when allocating PRBs contiguously from LowToHigh to the non-HP UEs with the following additional guidelines mentioned below:
o Corresponding number of PRBs equivalent to NIDD parameter NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs, is a percentage that specifies how many PRBs are to be reserved at the edge of the bandwidth used for HP UEs
 Number of PRBs equivalent to, Number of reserved RBGs = Floor (NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs \* (Number of RBGs in bandwidth) / 100),[PR783596] For calculation of Number of reserved RBGs (and the equivalent PRBs), please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12665\_replaced([11182890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End PR783596]
 The edge of the bandwidth in which the PRBs are reserved for the HP UEs is specified by NRCELL.dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection, which is always set to HighToLow
o If there are no HP UEs in the slot and the non-HP UEs do not need more PRBs than in exclusive region from the reserved HP PRBs, then the gNB-DU shall randomize the StartPrb for the first UE as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24819\_replaced([11182450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDnJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) followed by contiguous PRB allocations within the part of the bandwidth that is not reserved for HP UEs.  This ensures that the local cell will not generate inter-cell interference into adjacent cells that may have HP UEs. Refer to Scenario 1 below
o If there are no HP UEs in the slot and the non-HP UEs need more resources than in the non-HP area, then PRBs shall be taken from the HP reserved area to fulfil the non-HP UE resource needs. Refer to Scenario 2
 First all PRBs in the non-HP area are to be allocated to the non-HP UEs
 When PRBs are allocated to non-HP UEs in the HP reserved area they shall be allocated in contiguous order.
o When there are HP and non-HP UEs in the same slot the UEs shall be allocated PRBs as follows (Refer to Scenario 3):
 gNB-DU shall first allocate PRBs contiguously to the HP UEs from HighToLow as per the DL interference avoidance algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8194\_replaced([11182952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
 Then allocate PRBs to the non-HP UEs according to the StartPrb randomization algorithm described above
 The non-HP UEs are not allocated PRBs in the reserved HP area unless there are not sufficient PRBs in the non-HP area
 
The following diagram illustrates various scenarios of allocating PRBs to HP and non-HP UEs.  In these examples the top edge of the bandwidth is used for the HP UEs, i.e. NRCELL.dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection = highPerforming\_High2low.
 
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a comparison of three scenarios related to the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) for High Priority (HP) and Non-High Priority (Non-HP) User Equipments (UEs). Each scenario is presented with a description and a visual representation of PRB allocation.
**Scenario 1: No HP UEs, Non-HP UEs needs PRBs < 192 PRBs. No need to use reserved PRBs meant for HP UEs.**
This scenario shows a column labeled "PRBs" with a section for "Reserved HP PRBs" and "PRBs for non-HP UEs". The reserved HP PRBs range from 272 down to 193. The PRBs for non-HP UEs start from 89 down to 0. The visual indicates that Non-HP UEs are allocated PRBs from a lower range, and there's no overlap with the reserved HP PRBs.
**Scenario 2: No HP UEs, Non-HP UEs needs PRBs > 192 PRBs. Need to use reserved PRBs from PRB#192 till 210. No StartPrb randomization.**
In this scenario, the reserved HP PRBs are from 272 down to 210. The PRBs for non-HP UEs start from 192 down to 0. This implies that when Non-HP UEs require more PRBs than available in their usual allocation, they can utilize the lower end of the reserved HP PRBs (from 192 to 210).
**Scenario 3: Both HP UEs and Non-HP UEs needs PRB allocations.**
This scenario is the most detailed. It shows a column of PRBs with a section for "Reserved HP PRBs" and "PRBs for non-HP UEs". Next to this column, there are numbers representing the count of PRBs allocated to each category.
- The "Reserved HP PRBs" are from 272 down to 220. The counts associated with these are 6, 13, 1, and 16.
- The "PRBs for non-HP UEs" are shown from 192 down to 0. The counts associated with these are 2, 3, 9, 8, 15, 5, 14, 10, 4, 12, and 7.
- There is a specific block of PRBs from 45 down to 44, with counts 5 and 14 respectively, which are also part of the "PRBs for non-HP UEs".
- A text box explains "HP UEs PRB allocation from HighToLow without StartPrb Randomization".
- Another text box explains "non-HP UEs PRB allocation from LowToHigh with StartPrb Randomization. Note: this is an eg showing there is StartPRB randomized, but ensured that not spilled over to Reserved HP PRBs".
- A note at the bottom left states "StartPRB randomized. Note: Randomization is such that the allocation do not spill over to Reserved HP PRBs".
- A legend at the bottom indicates that yellow blocks represent "Non-HP UEs" and orange blocks represent "HP UEs".
The image uses color coding to distinguish between PRBs allocated to Non-HP UEs (yellow) and HP UEs (orange). The scenarios illustrate different conditions of UE demand and how PRB allocation strategies, including randomization, are applied to ensure efficient resource utilization without compromising reserved resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13880.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
diagrams available here:
 
Note: When there are multiple PDSCH sub-areas due to SIB1, Paging, or DCI1\_0 or for any other reason, it should be ensured that the PRBs are utilized fully in the PDSCH sub-area that does not contain Reserved HP PRBs. This is to ensure that in the PDSCH sub-area that contains Reserved HP PRBs, the UEs shall be satisfied within the region reserved for non-HP UEs
Additionally in a slot when more than one subarea is created, due to SIB1, Paging, or any other DCI1\_0 allocations, there is no modification in the subarea selection based on whether HP UEs or Normal UEs, and allocation direction mentioned above will be only applicable to the sub-area which is above CORESET 0 allocation (SIB1, Paging, or any other DCI1\_0 allocations) that has the HP PRB reserved region. i.e., during the reservation phase an HP UE could be pinned to SA below CORESET 0, then the allocation direction is low2high for all UE (HP or Normal UE)

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11182494`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Multiple Dedicated BWPs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11182500`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
When StartPrb randomization and HP DL Interference Avoidance are enabled at the same time (NRCELL.actRAT1Alloc = True AND NRCELL. actDlInterferenceAvoidance = True) and multiple dedicated BWPs are configured in the cell, the PRB allocation algorithms defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26236\_replaced([11182477](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDnKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are followed with the following extensions for multiple BWPs:

 With multiple BWPs, all BWPs line up at the bottom of the bandwidth and the HP reserved area is always positioned at the top of the bandwidth away from the smaller BWPs.

 After the HP UEs are allocated PRBs as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22120\_replaced([11182985](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvHRgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the non-HP UE PRB allocations are made starting with the smallest BWP.  Once UEs in the smallest BWP have been allocated PRBs the UEs in the next largest BWP are allocated PRBs, etc.

 NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs specifies the percentage of RBGs in the largest BWP that are to be reserved for high performance UEs.  Note that only UEs assigned to the largest BWP can be classified as HP UEs.

 To ensure that VoNR UEs in the power savings (PS) BWP are not impacted by the HP reserved area, the HP reserved area cannot overlap the PS BWP.   If NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs is configured to overlap the PS BWP the reserved area shall be reduced to only those RBGs that are exclusive of the PS BWP.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11182511`)

DL resource assignment using FDM

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11182527`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182527:
DL resource assignment using FDM
For FR1 FDD, when NRCELL.actRAT1Alloc = True and number of PRBs corresponding to NIDD parameter NRCELL::numOfDeprioritizedDlRBGs is greater than 0, gNB-DU shall de-priorotize a configurable number of PRBs at the top edge of the frequency carrier governed by the NIDD parameter numOfDeprioritizedDlRBGs. The PRBs in the large dedicated BWP are divided into two groups: prioritized PRBs at the bottom, and de-prioritized PRBs at the top of the spectrum. gNB-DU shall allocate the PRBs such that de-prioritized PRBs are always allocated once the prioritized PRBs are fully allocated. The rationale behind this feature is to minimize the interference in FDD at the top edge of the frequency carrier which is interfered from the satellite band.
 
When NRCELL.actRAT1Alloc = True, gNB-DU shall randomize the StartPrb for the first UE randomly picked as per the StartPrb Randomization algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24819\_replaced([11182450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDnJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The following steps will be taken to achieve this goal in a cell with a single dedicated BWP.
 
 Number of PRBs equivalent to NRCELL::numOfDeprioritizedDlRBGs specifies the #PRBs that are de-prioritized
 If there is no common channel (SIB1/Paging), RachMsg2, RachMsg4, or any other DCI1\_0, then there is single dedicated BWP. If there is any common channel or RachMsg2, RachMsg4, or any other DCI1\_0 then there can be 2 PDSCH sub-areas.
 Either for the Single or if multiple PDSCH sub-areas above CORESET 0, if the number of PRBs required for the UEs in the sub-area that contains de-prioritized PRBs is satisfied within the prioritized PRB area,  then the gNB-DU shall randomize the StartPrb for the first UE as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24819\_replaced([11182450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDnJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) followed by contiguous PRB allocations within the prioritized PRBs region and shall not spill over to de-prioritized PRB region as shown in the figure below Scenario 1.
 If the #PRBs to be allocated is greater than the number of PRBs in prioritized PRB region, then the PRBs shall be allocated from the starting (or bottom) of PDSCH sub-area and there is No StartPrb Randomization, and the PRBs are allocated contiguously from bottom to top as shown in Scenario 2.
 In a slot if there is a dynamic switch RAT 0 UE to be scheduled, then the above rules will not be applied. In that case dynamic switch RAT0 UE will be handled as mentioned in  5G\_UP\_ALG\_24995\_replaced([11181383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two scenarios illustrating the allocation of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) to users (UEs) based on prioritization. Each scenario presents a column labeled "PRBs" which is divided into "de-prioritized PRBs" and "prioritized PRBs". A legend at the bottom indicates that yellow-filled boxes represent PRBs reserved or allocated to UEs.
**Scenario 1: When #PRBs reserved/allocated to UEs < #Prioritized PRBs**
This scenario shows a PRB allocation where the total number of PRBs reserved or allocated to UEs is less than the number of prioritized PRBs.
\* **De-prioritized PRBs:** This section contains PRBs numbered from 106 down to 67. The PRBs from 106 down to 67 are not highlighted in yellow, indicating they are not reserved or allocated.
\* **Prioritized PRBs:** This section contains PRBs numbered from 66 down to 0. The PRBs from 64 down to 0 are highlighted in yellow, indicating they are reserved or allocated. The PRBs 66, 65 are not highlighted.
**Scenario 2: When #PRBs reserved/allocated to UEs > #Prioritized PRBs**
This scenario shows a PRB allocation where the total number of PRBs reserved or allocated to UEs is greater than the number of prioritized PRBs.
\* **De-prioritized PRBs:** This section contains PRBs numbered from 106 down to 67. The PRBs from 106 down to 67 are not highlighted in yellow, except for the PRB labeled '67' which is highlighted in yellow. The PRBs from 106 down to 68 are not highlighted.
\* **Prioritized PRBs:** This section contains PRBs numbered from 66 down to 0. All PRBs from 66 down to 0 are highlighted in yellow, indicating they are reserved or allocated.
**Legend:**
\* Yellow-filled boxes: PRBs reserved or allocated to UEs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13887.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
diagrams available here:
 
When multiple dedicated BWPs are configured in the cell, the PRB allocation is as follows:
 With multiple BWPs, all BWPs line up at the bottom of the bandwidth and the de-prioritized PRB area is always positioned at the top of the bandwidth.
 There is no PRB de-prioritization for small dedicated BWP, if any
 There is no de-prioritization on the Initial BWP. If necessary, initial BWP's bandwidth can already be restricted to not overlap with the de-prioritized PRBs
 All other criteria mentioned above for single dedicated BWP holds good for multiple dedicated BWP too
 
Note: while handling the de-prioritized PRBs for the partial RBG, the RBG size is not the full RBG size. Eg., if numOfDeprioritizedDlRBGs = 2, and last RBG is partial say 5 PRBs and other RBGs are 8 PRBs (i.e., rbg size), then the number of PRBs that needs to be de-prioritized for RAT1 should be 5+8 = 13 and not 2\*8 = 16.

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11182545`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182545:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Below figure is just an example to explain and relate the parameters & the basic principles before applying randomization to different possible scenarios

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a communication system. It depicts different "Bandwidth Parts" (BWPs) and "Common Resource Sets" (CORESETs) within sub-areas.
The diagram shows two main vertical blocks, one outlined in red labeled "Large-BWP Id 1" and another outlined in blue labeled "Small-BWP Id 2". Inside these blocks are smaller rectangular boxes representing different sub-areas (SA1 and SA2) and their associated resource configurations.
Within the "Large-BWP Id 1" block:
- The top section contains "N2\_Large SA1".
- Below that, a green highlighted box is labeled "N1 = nbPrbsCoresetOBWP(n)".
- The bottom section contains "N2\_Large SA2".
Within the "Small-BWP Id 2" block:
- The top section contains "N2\_Small SA1".
- The bottom section contains "N2\_Small SA2".
To the left of the main blocks, there are vertical arrows and labels indicating sizes:
- "rrmSizeCoreset0In PdschSubArea[n]" represents the size of CORESET#0 within PDSCH sub-area [n].
- "rrmSizeCoreset0InPds chSubArea[n+1]" represents the size of CORESET#0 within PDSCH sub-area [n+1].
To the right of the main blocks, there are vertical arrows and labels indicating sizes related to PDSCH sub-area sizes:
- "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] = rrmSizeBwplnPdschSubArea[n][1]" indicates the PDSCH sub-area size [n] is equal to the size of BWP [n][1].
- "rrmSizeBwplnPdschSubArea[n][2]" indicates the size of BWP [n][2].
- "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]" indicates the PDSCH sub-area size [n+1].
At the bottom, there are three labels corresponding to the colored outlines:
- "CORESET#0" (green)
- "Large-BWP Id 1" (red)
- "Small-BWP Id 2" (blue)
The diagram visually represents how different BWPs and CORESETs are organized and their relative sizes within sub-areas, potentially for scheduling or resource management purposes in a cellular network.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13890.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

N2\_Large\_SA1:   nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1] is the number of PRBs reserved for UEs in Large-Dedicated BWP [sub-area n][bwp-id 1]

N2\_Large\_SA2:   nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1] is the number of PRBs reserved for UEs in Large-Dedicated BWP [sub-area n+1][bwp-id 1]

N2\_Small\_SA1:   nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] is the number of PRBs reserved for UEs in Small-Dedicated BWP [sub-area n][bwp-id 2]

N2\_Small\_SA2:   nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2] is the number of PRBs reserved for UEs in Small-Dedicated BWP [sub-area n+1][bwp-id 2]

Note: N2 : In short refer to number of reserved PRBs in dedicated BWP which is the sum of all the dedicated BWPs and all sub-areas

N2= nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1] + nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1] + nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] + nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2]

 gNB-DU shall start randomization from the Smallest to Largest BWP in increasing order of the bandwidth Size (Initial BWP>Small BWP>Large BWP)

 gNB-DU shall apply randomization [Before CB008927-H] for the largest [CB008927-H] in all the [End CB008927-H] sub-area(s) and all the BWPs in Multiple BWP scenario.

 gNB-DU shall not apply PRB Randomization if: N1 + N2 = BWP\_PROFILE[x].locationAndBandwidthDl.cRbSizeDl, where x is the largest BWP.

 gNB-DU shall randomize the startPrb for the first UE randomly picked, for each BWP and each sub-areas associated with the BWP if it is possible. The subsequent UEs will be assigned the PRB after the highest PRB assigned to the first UE contiguously

o In the above example in sub-area [n]: Initial BWP is randomized first. Second, the randomization is done for N2\_Small\_SA1 since there is enough remaining PRBs in both Small and Large Dedicated BWPs of Sub-Area [n]. Finally, N2\_Large\_SA1 is randomized

o  [CB008927-H] For sub-area [n+1]: There is no startPrb randomization for N2\_Small\_SA2 and N2\_Large\_SA2, since the Remaining PRBs in sub-area [n+1] for dedicated BWP is 0  [End CB008927-H].

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11182551`)

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11182557`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Since RAT1 allocations are contiguous, if there is any DCI1\_0, SIB1/Paging allocated in CORESET#0, or in case of SSB slots, the dedicated BWP would be divided into 2 Sub-areas. Thus the objective of this section is to randomize the startPrb of first UE randomly picked for both DCI1\_0 & DCI1\_1 allocations i.e., to randomize both CORSET#0 within the initial BWP and dedicated BWPs (Single or Multiple). startPrb Randomization is done in all the BWPs and [Before CB008927-H] for the largest [CB008927-H] all the [End CB008927-H] sub-areas.

This section refers to the parameters definitions that would be used in defining startPrb algorithms for RAT1:

Note: There are 2 Sub-areas defined [n] and [n+1]. For all parameters where there is sub-area [n] defined, there exists sub-area [n+1] for all parameters but is not explicitly mentioned below eg., nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) and nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1)

N1-Resources (PRBs) reserved in Initial BWP or CORESET#0

nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) – is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the CORESET0 in sub-area [n], refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

N2-Resources (PRBs) reserved in dedicated BWP

If Single dedicated BWP:

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) - is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area [n]. It does not contain the PRBs reserved to PDSCH granted in the CORESET#0, refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

If Multiple dedicated BWP:

nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][p] - is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP[p] within the PDSCH sub area [n]. It does not contain the PRBs reserved to PDSCH granted in the CORESET #0. It does not contain the PRBs reserved to PDSCH granted in the other dedicated BWPs except for BWP [p], refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24758\_replaced([11176401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmFirstAvailablePrb – this is the first PRB from where the randomization can start

If Single dedicated BWP:

rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n], refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

If Multiple dedicated BWP:

rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p], refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24011\_replaced([11175971](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

for Initial BWP:

\*SIB1/Paging is already randomized as per the legacy – not required to define. (startPrbCC (5G\_UP\_ALG\_24016\_replaced([11176017](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is the lowest PRB allocated for the common channel, when there is CC allocated)

If NO SIB1/Paging, but RachMsg2/4 or any other DCI1\_0 present:  startPrbCoreset0 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_24013\_replaced([11175986](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is the first PRB from where the randomization can start.

Initial BWP Size

rrmSizeCoreset0 – is the size of the CORSET#0, refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmSizeCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – is the size of the CORESET#0 within the PDSCH sub-area [n], refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23465\_replaced([11176113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Dedicated BWP Size

If Single dedicated BWP:

rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] – parameter to track the size of each PDSCH sub-area, in this case the size of PDSCH within the PDSCH sub-area [n], refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If Multiple dedicated BWP:

rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] – size of the BWP [p], in pdsch sub-area [n]. (rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] is the size of largest BWP, which is also same as the size of PDSCH sub area: rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]), refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

rrmRemainingPrb - is the number of PRBs not allocated to any UE in the PDSCH subarea (Initial BWP or Dedicated BWP), where randomization can take place any where within this region (difference between size of PDSCH subarea and PRBs allocated to all UEs in it)

rrmNbRemainingPrbsCoreset0InPdschSubArea[n] – is the number of remaining PRBs in the CORSET#0 within the PDSCH sub-area [n] [5G\_UP\_ALG\_23464\_replaced([11176110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]

rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] – is the number of remaining PRBs in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub-area [n]. Number of PRBs reserved in CORESET#0 are not computed in this variable [5G\_UP\_ALG\_23464\_replaced([11176110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]

rrmNbRemainingPrbsBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p] – is the number of remaining PRBs in the BWP[p] within the PDSCH sub-area [n]. Number of PRBs reserved in CORESET#0 are not computed in this variable, also only BWP [p] is considered.

randomStartPrb: is the random number generated within a range between rrmFirstAvailablePrb and rrmRemainingPrb. Below are the parameters defined for this randomStartPrb.

In Coreset0: rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n]

If Single Dedicated BWP: rrmRandomStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]

If Multiple Dedicated BWP: rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][p]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-3**  (ID: `11182561`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Calculating a random startPrb in FR1

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11182565`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
randomStartPrb = rrmFirstAvailablePrb + min (randomStep \* stepSize, rrmRemainingPrb)

Where,

 stepSize = 4

 randomStep = (( rrmScheduledXSfn +1 + pciSmall ) \* primeLarge ) mod ( roundup(rrmRemainingPrb div stepSize) + 1)

 rrmScheduledXSfn is the extended SFN as defined in 5G\_L2\_233\_replaced([11148538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24RZ2fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 primeLarge = 997 (A prime number larger than rrmRemainingPrb)

 pciSmall = (physCellId mod 24) + 1

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11182579`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182579:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Reference to this use case can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23605\_replaced([11175956](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7PfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
Note: for all the parameters definition mentioned below, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24826\_replaced([11182551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
1.1 Single Dedicated BWP – Without SSB: this is a case with single sub-area
For a slot without SSB/TRS, there can be DCI1\_0 and DCI1\_1 traffic. But, this is a special case when there is no SIB1/Paging or no DCI1\_0. In such cases there is no segmentation of PDSCH and hence this is a case with single dedicated BWP with one sub-area.
Dedicated BWP Size : rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]
N2 -> nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) - is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area n
rrmRemainingPrb -> rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n] – parameter to track number of remaining PRBs within the PDSCH sub-area. This is nothing but (rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] – nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n])
rrmFirstAvailablePrb -> rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]
N1 -> nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) = 0
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating two related concepts, likely within a telecommunications or network context.
On the left side, a vertical blue rectangle labeled "N2" is shown within a larger red rectangle. Two smaller green rectangles are positioned on either side of the blue rectangle, partially overlapping it. A dotted blue line with an arrow indicates a "Randomized" process, pointing from the left diagram to the right. Above the blue rectangle, a dotted red line with a bracket and the label "RemainingPrb" signifies a remaining quantity or probability.
The right side of the diagram shows a similar vertical blue rectangle labeled "N2" within a red rectangle. This diagram is associated with two vertical arrows and labels. A long blue arrow points upwards from the bottom of the red rectangle to the top, with the label "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n]" to its right. A shorter blue arrow points downwards from the top of the red rectangle, with the label "rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]" to its right. The text "CORESET#0" is oriented vertically to the left of the right diagram.
The diagram appears to be explaining a process or configuration related to resource allocation or scheduling, possibly involving "N2" as a parameter, "RemainingPrb" as a remaining resource, and "Randomized" as a method of allocation. The right side seems to define the overall size and starting point of a sub-area within a larger system.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13896.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
gNB-DU shall randomize the start PRB of first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in the Single Dedicated BWP as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
 (rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]<= rrmRandomStartPrbPdschSubArea[n]<= rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n]) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n) PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] location
where,  rrmRandomStartPrbPdschSubArea[n] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-5**  (ID: `11182584`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Case 2: SSB/TRS Slot, and Multiple Dedicated BWP

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11182593`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182593:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Reference to this use case can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23462\_replaced([11176070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
Note: for all the parameters definition mentioned below, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24826\_replaced([11182551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
  gNB-DU shall first randomize the Small Dedicated BWP i.e., N2\_Small\_SA1, then Randomize N2\_Large\_SA1.
 Similarly in Sub-Area [n+1]
 Note: In an SSB [PR782200] with TRS symbols [End PR782200] or a TRS slot, there is no DCI1\_0 allocation (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
 
For Small BWP (BWP2) Sub-Area [n]:
  gNB-DU shall initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]
 rrmRemainingPrb = min( rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2],  rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] )
 gNB-DU shall randomize the start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 2 SA [n] as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
 ( rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] <= rrmRemainingPrb+ rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] location
where, rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a concept related to radio resource management, likely in a cellular network context. It depicts two main sections, separated by a dashed arrow labeled "Randomized". Each section contains a dark blue rectangular block labeled "SSB/PBCH", representing a Synchronization Signal Block/Physical Broadcast Channel.
Surrounding the "SSB/PBCH" block are several other rectangular blocks representing different resource allocations or configurations. These are labeled "N2\_Large SA1" and "N2\_Small SA1". The arrangement of these blocks and associated red and blue outlines suggests different sub-areas or configurations.
On the left side, there are two "N2\_Large SA1" blocks, one above and one below the "SSB/PBCH". There is also an "N2\_Small SA1" block positioned above and to the right of the "SSB/PBCH". Red and blue lines frame these blocks, with blue lines also indicating "RemainingPrb" above and below the "SSB/PBCH".
The right side of the diagram shows a similar "SSB/PBCH" block. Above and to the right of this block, there are two "N2\_Large SA1" blocks, one above the other. To the right of the "SSB/PBCH" block, there is an "N2\_Small SA1" block.
The right side also features several text labels with arrows pointing to different parts of the diagram, indicating parameters or calculations:

\* "rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]" points to a smaller vertical segment within the larger one.

For Large BWP (BWP1) Sub-Area [n]:
  gNB-DU shall initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = (rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]+ nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2])

( rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]+nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] <= rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] location
where, rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
[CB008927-H]

 rrmRemainingPrb = min( rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2],  rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2] )
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 2 SA[n+1] as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
( rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] <= rrmRemainingPrb+ rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] location
where, rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
For Large BWP (BWP1) Sub-Area [n+1]:

 rrmRemainingPrb = (rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1])
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 1 SA[n+1]  as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
 ( rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]+nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2] <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] <= rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] location

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11182609`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182609:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Reference to this use case can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23458\_replaced([11176013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuniSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
Note: for all the parameters definition mentioned below, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24826\_replaced([11182551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
In Current implementation SIB1/Paging is already randomized (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16548\_replaced([11179605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52JfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and then there is no need to further randomize this! Same is carry forwarded with RAT1 feature as well. Randomization of SIB1/Paging initializes & define the sub-area size.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a system with different components and their relationships. The diagram is organized into three main vertical blocks labeled "CORESET#0" (green), "Large-BWP Id 1" (red), and "Small-BWP Id2" (blue).
Within the "Large-BWP Id 1" block, there are stacked rectangular elements representing "N2\_Large SA1", "DCI1\_0", "SIB1/Paging", and "rachMsg2". Below these, there is another element labeled "N2\_Large SA2".
The "Small-BWP Id2" block contains "N2\_Small SA1" and "N2Small SA2".
There are several text labels and arrows indicating relationships and parameters.
On the left side, there are labels "rrmSizeCoresetoln PdschSubArea[n]" and "rrmSizeCoresetOInPds chSubArea[n+1]", each with a double-headed arrow pointing to different vertical sections of the diagram.
On the right side, there are text labels and dashed arrows:
- "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] =" followed by "rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1]"
- "rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]"
- "startPrbCC =" followed by a dashed arrow pointing to "rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]"
- "startPrbCoreseto"
- "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]" followed by a dashed arrow pointing to "rrmStartPrbBwpInPdsch SubArea[n+1][2]"
The diagram visually represents how different sub-areas and identifiers are related within a system, likely related to network resource management or communication protocols.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13900.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
CORESET#0 is already randomized as per the current implementation (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3748\_replaced([11165244](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktgH-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SIB and for Paging in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3921\_replaced([11168085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kttjXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) )
nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) & nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the CORESET0 sub-area n and n+1 resp.
 
For Small BWP (BWP2) Sub-Area [n]:
  Initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]+ nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n)
 rrmRemainingPrb = min( rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]- nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] , rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] - nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1] )
 Note: Idea is to subtract the #reserved PRBs from the own BWP and the Smaller BWPs
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 2 SA [n] as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
( rrmFirstAvailablePrb <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] <= rrmFirstAvailablePrb  + rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] location
where, rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
For Large BWP (BWP1) Sub-Area [n]:
  gNB-DU shall initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = (rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]+ nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2])
 rrmRemainingPrb = (NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl.cRbSizeDl- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1])
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 1 SA[n] as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
( rrmFirstAvailablePrb <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] <= rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] location
where, rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
Note: This block of equation is same as the corresponding block in Case2
 
[CB008927-H]
For Small BWP (BWP2) Sub-Area [n+1]:
  gNB-DU initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]
 rrmRemainingPrb = min( rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]- nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2] , rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] - nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1] )
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 2 SA [n+1] as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
( rrmFirstAvailablePrb <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] <= rrmFirstAvailablePrb  + rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]
where, rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
For Large BWP (BWP1) Sub-Area [n+1]:
  gNB-DU shall initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = (rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]+ nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2])
 rrmRemainingPrb = (rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1] – nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1])
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB  of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 1 SA[n+1] as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE. 
( rrmFirstAvailablePrb <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] <= rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] location
where, rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
[End CB008927-H]

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-7**  (ID: `11182614`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Case 4: No Common Channel (SIB1/Paging), but RachMsg2/4 and other DCI1\_0

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11182623`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182623:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Reference to this use case can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23469\_replaced([11176138](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
Note: for all the parameters definition mentioned below, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24826\_replaced([11182551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
In current implementation, i.e., in RAT0 there is no randomization done for rachMsg2/4 and other DCI1\_0 and the allocation starts from starting PRB of CORESET#0 from bottom.
But, in  CB008927-E gNB-DU shall randomize too the rachMsg2/4 and other DCI1\_0 within CORESET#0.
Note: during the initialization or the PRBs are reserved for rachMsg2/4 or DCI1\_0 (except for SIB1/Paging) from startPrbCorset0 and then two sub-areas are created, one from the startPrbCoreset0 and above, and other sub-area below the startPrbCoreset0.
 
Note: Case 3 i.e., when there is SIB1/Paging, the sub-area split is from the startPrbCC. However, in Case 4 when there is NO SIB1/Paging, but there is any other DCI1\_0, the sub-area split is from startPrbCoreset0. This difference in sub-area split is because, SIB1/Paging is allocated PRBs at the TD scheduler phase and these allocations are random within the initial BWP (or CORESET#0), which is same as legacy. So, at the instance there is randomization done for SIB1/Paging at the TD phase, there is fragmentation of PDSCH dedicated BWP. And thus we consider this random PRB location to be the point of sub-area split. But when there is NO SIB1/Paging, but there is any other DCI1\_0 allocation, basically PRBs are just reserved in TD phase but not allocated. And this reservation is initially assumed to be done from startPrb of CORESET#0, and thus we fragment the PDSCH dedicated BWP from the startPrb of CORESET#0.
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation concepts, likely within a telecommunications context. It depicts several rectangular blocks representing different resource configurations and their relationships.
Key elements and their textual labels are:
**Resource Blocks:**
\* A green rectangle labeled "CORESET#0".
\* A red-bordered rectangle labeled "Large-BWP Id 1".
\* A cyan-bordered rectangle labeled "Small-BWP Id2".
\* Within the red and cyan borders, there are smaller light blue rectangles:
\* "N2\_Large SA1" (within the red border)
\* "N2\_Small SA1" (within the cyan border)
\* A dark blue rectangle labeled "rachMsg2/ DCI1\_0" spanning across parts of the red and cyan bordered areas.
\* "N2 Large SA2" (below "rachMsg2/ DCI1\_0", within the red border)
\* "N2Small SA2" (below "rachMsg2/ DCI1\_0", within the cyan border)
**Textual Annotations and Equations:**
\* "rrmSizeCoreset0In PdschSubArea[n]" - Associated with a green vertical arrow indicating a size.
\* "rrmSizeCoreset0InPds chSubArea[n+1]" - Associated with a blue vertical arrow indicating a size.
\* "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n] ="
\* "rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1]"
\* "rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]" - Associated with a blue vertical arrow indicating a size.
\* "startPrbCoreset0 ="
\* "rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]" - Associated with a dashed green arrow pointing right.
\* "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+1]" - Associated with a blue vertical arrow indicating a size.
The diagram uses colored borders and blocks to visually represent different types of bandwidth parts (BWPs) and their associated configurations (like CORESETs and sub-areas). Arrows indicate sizes and relationships between these resources. The text labels provide specific identifiers and formulas related to these resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13902.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 gNB-DU shall first randomize Initial-BWP, followed by Small-BWP and then Large-BWP for sub-area [n] first when there is DCI1\_0 but No SIB1/Paging
 Next gNB-DU shall randomize sub-area [n+1]
 Change that is needed in this scenario is once the CORESET#0 is randomized, the size of the dedicated BWPs also changes and this shall be factored in the equations. (sub-area [n] dedicated BWP Size reduces, and for [n+1] it increases)
 
For Initial-BWP (or CORESET#0):
  gNB-DU shall initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = rrmStartPrbCoreset0
 rrmRemainingPrb = min( rrmSizeCoreset0- nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) , rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]- nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] , rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] - nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1] )
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB  of the first UE randomly picked from the list of DCI1\_0 UEs in CORESET#0 as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
( rrmFirstAvailablePrb <= rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n] <= rrmFirstAvailablePrb  + rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n] location
where,  rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
For Small BWP (BWP2) Sub-Area [n]: idea is to update the dedicated BWP size for small and large BWP, because of the CORESET#0 randomization
  gNB-DU shall initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n] + nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n)
 rrmRemainingPrb = min( (rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]- rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n]- rrmStartPrbCoreset0) - nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] , (rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] ]- rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n]- rrmStartPrbCoreset0) - nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1] )
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 2 SA [n] as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
( rrmFirstAvailablePrb <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] <= rrmFirstAvailablePrb  + rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] location
where, rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
For Large BWP (BWP1) Sub-Area [n]:
  Initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = (rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][2]+ nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][2])
 rrmRemainingPrb = (NRBTS/BWP\_PROFILE/locationAndBandwidthDl.cRbSizeDl- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1])
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 1 SA[n] as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
( rrmFirstAvailablePrb <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] <= rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n][1] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] location
where,  rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n][1] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
[CB008927-H]
For Sub-Area [n+1]:
 after the CORESET#0 is randomized, the rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] & rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] gets updated to rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n]
 Update:
 rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] = rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n]
 rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] = rrmRandomStartPrbCoreset0[n]
 
 
For Small BWP (BWP2) Sub-Area [n+1]:
  gNB-DU shall initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = rrmStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]
 rrmRemainingPrb = min( rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]- nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2] , rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] - nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2]- nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1] )
 Note: nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) - this is anyway equal to 0, but just kept to retain the same equation
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 2 SA [n+1]  as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
 ( rrmFirstAvailablePrb <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] <= rrmFirstAvailablePrb  + rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]
where,  rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
 
For Large BWP (BWP1) Sub-Area [n+1]:
  gNB-DU shall initialize rrmFirstAvailablePrb = (rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][2]+ nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][2])
 rrmRemainingPrb = (rrmSizeBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1]] – nbPrbsCoreset0BWP(n+1) - nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1])
 gNB-DU shall randomize start PRB of the first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in BWP 1 SA[n+1]  as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
( rrmFirstAvailablePrb <= rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] <= rrmRemainingPrb) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+1][1] PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] location
where,  rrmRandomStartPrbBwpInPdschSubArea[n+1][1] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
[End CB008927-H]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-8**  (ID: `11182629`)

DL resource assignment using FDM

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-9.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11182636`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182636:
DL resource assignment using FDM
Reference to this use case can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25677\_replaced([11176778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurMpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when the dynamicswitch RAT0 belongs to BWP2 (see 5G\_UP\_11522\_replaced([11351060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubqxPfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the RBGs are located at the top of the carrier bandwidth
Note: for all the parameters definition mentioned below, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24826\_replaced([11182551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
PDSCH sub area above RBGs allocation of a dynamicswitch RAT 0 at the top of the carrier bandwidth in multi BWP
In this PDSCH sub area (located at the top of the carrier bandwidth), there are only reserved PRBs of the largest dedicated BWP1 . There is no CORESET 0 PRB areas, and no other dedicated BWPs than the largest dedicated BWP in this PDSCH sub area. So BWP1 can be considered in this PDSCH sub area as a single dedicated BWP
Dedicated BWP1 Size : rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2]
N2 -> nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n+2) - is the number of PDSCH PRBs reserved in the dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area n+2
rrmRemainingPrb -> rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+2] – parameter to track number of remaining PRBs within the PDSCH sub-area. This is nothing but (rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2] – nbPrbsDedicatedBWP[n+2])
rrmFirstAvailablePrb -> rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+2]
 
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two vertical bars, each enclosed in a red rectangle. The bar on the left is partially filled with a light blue color, and the text "N2" is written inside the filled portion. Above the filled portion, there is a gap, and an arrow pointing downwards from the top of the rectangle to the top of the filled portion. The text "RemainingPrb" is written next to this arrow. Below the filled portion, there is another arrow pointing downwards, and the text "Randomized" is highlighted in yellow next to a dashed arrow pointing to the right.
The bar on the right is also enclosed in a red rectangle and is fully filled with a light blue color. The text "N2" is written inside this bar. A vertical double-headed arrow spans the entire height of the filled portion of this bar. To the right of this bar, there are two labels with wavy red borders. The upper label reads "rrmPdschSubAreaSize[n+2]", and it is associated with a vertical arrow pointing upwards from the top of the bar. The lower label reads "rrmStartPrbhdschSubArea[n+2]", and it is associated with a vertical arrow pointing downwards from the bottom of the bar.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13904.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
gNB-DU shall randomize the start PRB of first UE randomly picked from the list of UEs in the Dedicated BWP1 as below, and the Start PRB for all other UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to first UE.
 (rrmStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+2]<= rrmRandomStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+2]<= rrmNbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea[n+2]) and allocate nbPrbsDedicatedBWP(n+2) PRBs contiguously from the rrmRandomStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+2] location
where,  rrmRandomStartPrbPdschSubArea[n+2] can be computed from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24527\_replaced([11182561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11182656`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
PDSCH transmissions are less reliable when inter-cell interference is present due to the local cell and the adjacent cells transmitting on the same frequency resources.  Additionally PDSCH transmission reliability can be negatively impacted if a UE’s frequency resources are allocated in a contiguous manner when there is frequency selective fading in the cell.  By randomizing the location of the RBGs that are allocated to UEs in the cell reduces the probability that adjacent cells are transmitting on the same PDSCH resources thereby reducing the inter-cell interference.  Also, distributing a UE’s RBGs across the bandwidth in a randomized manner provides frequency diversity which helps to make the transmission more robust when frequency selective fading is present in the cell.

**[CB013709-B]If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0**, HwVariant used is not ABIL, and the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31301\_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is greater than or equal to the value set for **activation threshold of rdIntfAvdRbgRandOlcDlPerCent (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31830\_replaced([11216820](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxa-pvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then, gNB-DU shall [Before** PR848562] skip the following functions and shall perform DL RBG randomization as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11319\_replaced([11182354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [PR848562] allocate FD resources as below\*\*\*\*

* RBG allocations for a given slot for all the UEs belonging to all configured dedicated BWP(s), shall be based on a single startPRB index calculated per slot;
* The startPRB/startPRB index for the slot correspond to the first unused or "free" RBG (from all dedicated BWP(s)) in the direction of "low2high"
* Once startPRB for the slot is selected (that reflects the first RBG for allocation in the slot) complete allocation of required RBGs for all the UE shall be done in a contiguous manner

[End PR848562]

When the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31301\_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is less than the value set for **deactivation threshold of rdIntfAvdRbgRandOlcDlPerCent (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31830\_replaced([11216820](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxa-pvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then gNB-DU shall resume the below functions.**

**[End CB013709-B]**

The following functions are required to provide PDSCH RBG Randomization:

 Generate the RBG random allocation order sequence used to allocate RBGs to UEs.  Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12659\_replaced([11182662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvE1S_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Randomly allocate RBGs to transmissions in cells with one dedicated BWP.  Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12660\_replaced([11182732](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvFcVvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Randomly allocate RBGs to transmissions in cells with multiple dedicated BWPs.  Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12661\_replaced([11182756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvFcWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Randomly allocate RBGs to retransmissions.  Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12664\_replaced([11182875](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGDbPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Randomly allocate RBGs to transmissions in cells with DL Interference Avoidance enabled and one dedicated BWP.  Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12665\_replaced([11182890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Randomly allocate RBGs to transmissions in cells with DL Interference Avoidance enabled and multiple BWPs.  Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12666\_replaced([11182912](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that if Downlink MU MIMO is enabled (actDlMuMimo = TRUE) and there are MU MIMO paired UEs in the slot the above RBG randomization algorithms will not be used in that the slot.  Instead the DL MU MIMO algorithms will be use to allocate the RBGs in that slot.   This is in contrast to Enhanced Downlink MU MIMO which is compatible with RBG randomization.  If actDlMuMimoEnh = TRUE the RBG randomization algorithms shall be used regardless of whether or not there are MU MIMO paired UEs in a slot.

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11182662`)

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11182695`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
[CB008927-S] If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRAT1Alloc = False, [End CB008927-S] PDSCH RBGs will be allocated to UE’s in random allocation order.  The order in which RBGs are allocated to UEs is specified by a random allocation order sequence that is mapped to the physical RBGs.  The random allocation order sequence is calculated via a formula that produces different sequences depending on the cell’s PCI, the slot number and number of de-prioritized RBGs in the cell. Creating RBG allocation orders that differ based on the cell’s PCI ensures that the RBG allocations in adjacent cells will not overlap when the cells are lightly loaded thereby reducing the inter-cell interference.  Also by changing the allocation order from slot to slot provides increased frequency diversity for UEs that are scheduled in consecutive slots.  The following formula is used to generate the RBG random allocation sequences:

For prioritized area i.e. when 0 ≤ RBG# < prioritizedAreaLength:

1 + ((xSFN + 1 + PCI + RBG#)\* primeNumberFun(prioritizedAreaLength)) modulo (prioritizedAreaLength),

For deprioritized area i.e. when prioritizedAreaLength ≤ RBG# < NumOfWholeRBGs:

1 + (prioritizedAreaLength) + ((xSFN + 1 + PCI + RBG# - prioritizedAreaLength)\* primeNumberFun(deprioritizedAreaLength)) modulo (deprioritizedAreaLength)

Where:

* prioritizedAreaLength = NumOfWholeRBGs - dlDeprioritizedRBGs + partial
* deprioritizedAreaLength = dlDeprioritizedRBGs - partial
* xSFN = extended SFN (SFN \* N slotsframe,μ + slot).

Where N slotsframe,μ is the number of slots in one radio frame

with the following exception:

If (NumOfWholeRBGs - dlDeprioritizedRBGs + partial)  is equal 5 or 10 or (dlDeprioritizedRBGs-partial) is equal 5 or 10 then

N slotsframe,μ = 23

EndIf

* PCI = Physical cell ID
* NumOfWholeRBGs = number of whole RBGs (excluding partial RBG if it exists) in the largest dedicated BWP.  This value determines the length of the calculated random order sequence
* RBG# = the RBG ID, e.g. 0…NumOfWholeRBGs - 1
* primeNumberFun(x) =
* 991 if x = 4 or 12
* 997 otherwise
* dlDeprioritizedRBGs is parameter received during  the cell setup or cell reconfiguration.
* partial is the lowest of two numbers: dlDeprioritizedRBGs and number of partial RBGs in largest bandwidth part (0 or 1).

Notes:

1. Value of the dlDeprioritizedRBGs is derived from the system parameter numOfDeprioritizedDlRBGs associated with the cell
2. partial can take values  0 or 1 .
3. For the:
4. dlDeprioritizedRBGs = 0 or
5. dlDeprioritizedRBGs = 1 when partial RBG exists sequence generated according to this requirement is the same as sequence generated according to version of the requirement for before the CB012669.

Note: For bandwidths that contain either 5 or 10 full RBGs (FDD 30MHz, TDD 5MHz, and TDD 60MHz) the random allocation order repeats every 20ms.  Since a VoNR UE is typically scheduled a multiple of every 20ms this will result in a VoNR UE being given the same RBG every 20ms which is not desired.  To prevent the random allocation order from repeating every 20ms in these bandwidths N slotsframe,μ = 23 is used instead of the actual number of slots per frame.

If a partial RBG (RBG with fewer PRBs than a whole RBG at the top of the BW) exists the random order sequence will be extended by one value.  The additional value added at the end of the sequence will be equal to NumOfWholeRBGs + 1.

Note that the RBG allocation order sequences can be computed one time at cell startup with the proper sequence being selected based on the current xSFN: SequenceID = xSFN MOD NumOfRBGs.  Note that this is not a requirement, only a suggestion.  If the development team has a more efficient way to create the slot specific sequences that is fully acceptable also.

For examples of the sequence of order of allocation see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25822\_replaced([11182700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvFcUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008927-S] If NRCELL.actRAT1Alloc = True, and NIDD parameter NRCELL::numOfDeprioritizedDlRBGs is greater than 0 then refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26762\_replaced([11182513](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvEOMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: NRCELL::numOfDeprioritizedDlRBGs shall be converted to equivalent number of PRBs [End CB008927-S]

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11182714`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182714:
DL resource assignment using FDM
The following is an example that illustrates the use of RBG random allocation order sequence:
 
• The cell has 14 physical RBGs 0..13 with the last RBG being a partial RBG.
• The RBG random allocation order sequence changes per slot and cell.
• The length of the random allocation order sequence is 14 with values in the range 1…14. 
• The sequence is applied to the physical RBGs starting at RBG 0.
• In this example the RBG random order sequence for Cell 1, Slot 1 is:  4, 13, 9, 5, 1, 10, 6, 2, 11, 7, 3, 12, 8, 14
• For Cell 1, Slot 1 using the random allocation order the scheduler will allocate the physical RBGs in the following order: 4, 7, 10, 0, 3, 6, 9, 12, 2, 5, 8, 11, 1, 13
 
The following diagram illustrates the mapping of the RBG random allocation order sequences to two cells over two slots asuming SFN = 12.

The numbers range from 1 to 14, and some cells are colored with gradients of red, yellow, and green, suggesting a visual representation of the allocated RBG values or their status.

| 0 |  |  |  |  |

For example, the Physical RBG '13' in the first column is shown with a dotted pattern.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13916.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |  |

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11182727`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182727:
DL resource assignment using FDM
The following is an example that illustrates the use of RBG random allocation order sequence when non-zero RBGs are deprioritized:
 
• The cell has 14 physical RBGs 0..13 with the last RBG being a partial RBG.
• 8 RBGs are de-prioritized
• The RBG random allocation order sequence changes per slot and cell.
• The length of the random allocation order sequence is 14 with values in the range 1…14. 
• The sequence is applied to the physical RBGs starting at RBG 0.
\* In this example the RBG random order sequence for Cell 1, Slot 1 is:  4, 5, 6, 1, 2, 3, 8, 11, 7, 10, 13, 9, 12, 14
\* For Cell 1, Slot 1 using the random allocation order the scheduler will allocate the physical RBGs in the following order: 3, 4, 5, 0, 1, 2, 8, 6, 11, 9, 7, 12, 10, 13
The following diagram illustrates the mapping of the RBG random allocation order sequences to two cells over two slots asuming SFN = 12.

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-4**  (ID: `11182732`)

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11182749`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182749:
DL resource assignment using FDM
The FD scheduler will receive the following inputs prior to allocating specific RBGs to UEs:

 List of RBGs that are available for allocation to UEs

o RBGs may not be available due to initial BWP allocations, SSB transmissions, PRB Blanking, etc.

 If PRB Pooling is enabled, the number of PDSCH PRBs that can be allocated in the slot

o If the number of PRBs is restricted to less than all PRBs, i.e. all RBGs cannot be allocated in the slot, it may be possible to allocate the partial RBG with a reduced number of full RBGs and remain below the number of PRBs that can be allocated.  In this case the partial RBG can be allocated to a UE before all of the full RBGs in the bandwidth have been allocated.

 CS2 list that contains the list of UEs to be allocated RBGs

o The CS2 list must remain in TD priority order, i.e. the UE order shall not be randomized as is done when the RBG randomization feature is disabled.  This will ensure that high priority UEs such as VoNR and retransmissions will be allocated RBGs first and will have a lower probability of being allocated the partial RBG.  Also given that RBG allocations are randomized per UE it is no longer required to randomize UEs to get frequency diversity across slots.

o The CS2 list can contain enhanced MU MIMO paired UEs that are identified as virtual UEs.  The virtual UEs should be treated no differently than un-paired UEs.

 The number of RBGs that should be allocated to each UE

The FD scheduler shall map the RBG random allocation order sequence to the physical RBGs as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12659\_replaced([11182662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvE1S_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  The FD scheduler shall then:

1. Process the CS2 UEs per the order specified in the CS2 list.

2. For each UE the FD scheduler shall allocate the number of RBGs that was specified for that UE

3. The physical RBGs allocated to a UE are selected according to the RBG random allocation order in ascending order.

a. Using the random allocation order will result in non-contiguous RBGs being allocated to the UE which provides frequency diversity.

b. Note that a random allocation order sequence number will be mapped to all RBGs even if an RBG is unavailable for scheduling.  When an unavailable RBG is next in the random allocation order the FD scheduler will skip that RBG and proceed to the next RBG in the random allocation order.

4. [Before CNI-93043] If the cell contains a partial RBG, the partial RBG will be allocated last. [CNI-93043] L2 PS shall fulfill the rule described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24135\_replaced([11182274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvCZBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Partial RBG allocation for initial transmission'. [End CNI-93043]

5. When a UE with dlMacCe request class priority is allocated the partial RBG the following check must be made:

o If the number of PRBs in the considered partial RBG is less than 50% of the PRBs in a full RBG, and a UE which does not contain dlMacCe request class priority exists for initial transmission then both UEs shall be swapped in RBG allocation list.

For each UE that has been allocated RBGs, the FD scheduler shall:

1. Create an RBG bitmap for each UE specifying the location of the RBGs allocated to the UE

2. Provide the UE RBG bitmaps to L1 via the DlData\_ PdschSendReq message.

The following example illustrates the above algorithm when applied to two cells.  As can be seen in the diagram the random allocation order differs significantly for each cell largely avoiding transmissions on the same RBGs.  For UEs with multiple RBGs the random allocation order spreads the UE’s allocation across the bandwidth, i.e. the UE’s RBG allocation is not contiguous.  The following are the example details:

• FDD, BW = 20MHz,  RBG size = 8 PRBs

• RBG 13 is a partial RBG (2 PRBs) and is the last RBG to be allocated.

• UEs in example.  The UEs are listed in CS2 order.

• Cell 1: UE1:1RBG, UE2:1RBG, UE3:1RBG, UE4:1RBG, UE5:2RBGs, UE6:2RBGs

• Cell 2: UE1:1RBG, UE2:1RBG, UE3:1RBG, UE4:1RBG, UE5:2RBGs

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a visual representation of Resource Block Group (RBG) allocation in a cellular network. It is divided into three main sections: "Physical RBGs", "RBG Random Allocation Order", and "RBGs Allocated per UE".
The "Physical RBGs" section lists RBGs from 0 to 13. RBG 13 is indicated as a "Partial RBG" with a dotted pattern.
The "RBG Random Allocation Order" section is further divided into "Cell 1" and "Cell 2". Each cell shows a grid of numbers, representing the RBG allocation order for each physical RBG. The numbers are color-coded, with red indicating lower values and green indicating higher values, suggesting a priority or order. For example, in Cell 1, RBG 13 is allocated with the number 14, RBG 12 with 8, and so on. Similarly, in Cell 2, RBG 13 is allocated with 14, RBG 12 with 13, etc.
The "RBGs Allocated per UE" section is also divided into "Cell 1" and "Cell 2". This section shows which User Equipment (UE) is allocated which RBG. For instance, in Cell 1, UE6 is allocated RBG 14, UE3 is allocated RBG 12, and so on. In Cell 2, UE4 is allocated RBG 14, UE3 is allocated RBG 12, and so forth.
The table below represents the "RBG Random Allocation Order" and "RBGs Allocated per UE" sections.
**RBG Random Allocation Order**

| Physical RBGs | Cell 1 | Cell 2 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 13 | 14 | 14 |
| 12 | 8 | 13 |
| 11 | 12 | 4 |
| 10 | 3 | 8 |
| 9 | 7 | 12 |
| 8 | 11 | 3 |
| 7 | 2 | 7 |
| 6 | 6 | 11 |
| 5 | 10 | 2 |
| 4 | 1 | 6 |
| 3 | 5 | 10 |
| 2 | 9 | 1 |
| 1 | 13 | 5 |
| 0 | 4 | 9 |
| **RBGs Allocated per UE** |  |  |

| Cell 1 | Cell 2 |
| --- | --- |
| UE6 | UE4 |
| UE3 | UE3 |
| UE6 | UE2 |
| UE2 | UE5 |
| UE5 | UE1 |
| UE5 | UE5 |
| UE4 | UE1 |

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-5**  (ID: `11182756`)

Randomly allocate RBGs in cells with multiple dedicated BWPs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-5.0-5**  (ID: `12659676`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
The FD scheduler will receive the following inputs prior to allocating RBGs to UEs:

 List of RBGs that are available in each BWP for allocation to UEs

o RBGs may not be available due to initial BWP allocations, SSB transmissions, PRB Blanking, etc.

 If PRB Pooling is enabled, the number of PDSCH PRBs that can be allocated in the slot.

o If the number of PRBs is restricted to less than all PRBs, i.e. all RBGs cannot be allocated in the slot, it may be possible to allocate the partial RBG with a reduced number of full RBGs and remain below the number of PRBs that can be allocated.  In this case the partial RBG can be allocated to a UE before all of the full RBGs in the bandwidth have been allocated.

 CS2 list that contains the list of UEs to be allocated RBG

o The CS2 list must remain in TD priority order, i.e. the UE order shall not be randomized as is done when the RBG randomization feature is disabled.  This will ensure that high priority UEs such as VoNR and retransmissions will be allocated RBGs first and will have a lower probability of being allocated the partial RBG.  Also given that RBG allocations are randomized per UE it is no longer required to randomize UEs to get frequency diversity across slots.

o The CS2 list can contain MU MIMO paired UEs that are identified as virtual UEs.  The virtual UEs should be treated no differently than un-paired UEs.

 The number of RBGs that should be allocated to each UE

 The BWP that each UE is assigned to

The FD scheduler shall perform the following allocation steps per slot:

1. Map the RBG random allocation order sequence associated with the current slot to the RBG islands.  Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12662\_replaced([11182785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvFcXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for a definition of RBG islands  and partial island.

2. Process the UEs in the smallest BWP first.  Once the UEs for a given BWP have been allocated RBGs, process the UEs in the next largest BWP.

3. For each UE the FD scheduler shall allocate the number of RBGs that were specified for the UE in the BWP that the UE is assigned.  The allocation rules for RBG islands must be followed as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12662\_replaced([11182785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvFcXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

4. When a UE with dlMacCe request class priority is allocated the partial RBG the following check must be made:

o If the number of PRBs in the considered partial RBG is less than 50% of the PRBs in a full RBG, and a UE which does not contain dlMacCe request class priority exists for initial transmission then both UEs shall be swapped in RBG allocation list.

5. When the PS BWP is configured [Before CNI-137616-B] AND rdReservePsBwpForVoNR (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13165\_replaced([11193492](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))= TRUE [End CNI-137616-B] the following constraints are added:

The following order shall be used for non-PS BWP UEs:

[CB012299-A] If start CRBs of non-largest BWPs are not aligned to the start CRB of largest BWP and DL blanking is not set, then:

1) Allocate RBG islands (for non-largest BWPs) or full RBGs (for largest BWP) which does not overlap with PS BWP.

2) Allocate bottom partial RBG  (if exists for largest BWPs)

3) Allocate full RBG islands (for non-largest BWPs) or full RBGs (for largest BWP) overlapping with PS BWP.

4) Allocate partial islands (for non-largest BWPs) in descending order of island coverage factor (ICF, see [11182791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGDYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), i.e. partial island with the smallest ICF shall be the last one.

5) Allocate top partial RBG (if exists for largest BWP).

Otherwise: [End CB012299-A]

1) Allocate RBG islands (for non-largest BWPs) or full RBGs (for largest BWP) which does not overlap with PS BWP.

2) Allocate bottom partial RBG if exists.

3) Allocate full RBG islands (for non-largest BWPs) or full RBGs (for largest BWP) overlapping with PS BWP.

4) Allocate top partial island (for non-largest BWPs) or top partial RBG (for largest BWP).

Rationale: It is expected that both VoNR UEs and PS BWP UEs are allocated to PS BWP. As a result, avoiding of allocation RBGs overlapping with PS BWP prevents inter-cell interference for VoNR UEs.

6. [Before CNI-137616-B] When the PS BWP is configured AND rdReservePsBwpForVonr = FALSE there is no special handling for the PS BWP.  The PS BWP shall be treated like other BWPs. [End CNI-137616-B]

Note that the processing associated with the PS BWP is TDD only.  FDD does not support the PS BWP.

For each UE that has been allocated RBGs, the FD scheduler shall:

1. Create an RBG bitmap for each UE specifying the location of the RBGs allocated to the UE

2. Provide the UE RBG bitmaps to L1 via the DlData\_ PdschSendReq message.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12663\_replaced([11182862](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGDavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for an example of RBG allocation when multiple dedicated BWPs are configured in the cell.
