**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-6.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12435733`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
When multiple dedicated BWPs are configured in a cell the FD scheduler needs to allocate RBGs to UEs one BWP at at time starting from the smallest BWP to the largest BWP.  Because the RBG size can differ in each dedicated BWP, i.e. small RBGs for UEs in small BWPs, large RBGs for UEs in large BWPs, the algorithm must allocate RBGs to UEs in the smaller BWPs in a manner that avoids fragmentation that would block UEs in the larger BWP from being allocated large RBGs that overlap the smaller BWP.

To avoid fragmentation in the small BWPs the concept of an RBG island is used.  An RBG island has the following characteristics:

• The RBG islands are equal to the RBGs in the largest BWP.

• The RBG random allocation order sequence is mapped to the RBG islands.

• Partial island is such island in non-largest BWP for which RBGs constituting the island does not cover RBG associated to the island in the largest BWP. This means that a portion of the island extends beyond the current BWP (note that partial island can be located only at the edges of the BWP, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31648\_replaced([11182830](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGDZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Examples' for details).

[CB012299-A] When the start CRBs of non-largest BWPs are not aligned to the start CRB of largest BWP and DL blanking is not set, for every such partial island  X overage factor is defined:

Island coverage factor (ICF)  = number of PRBs in non-largest BWP  belonging to the island X / total number of PRB in island X

[End CB012299-A]

L2PS shall fulfill the following rules:

• When UEs assigned to the smaller BWPs, are allocated smaller RBGs, the smaller RBGs will be allocated to fill an island before proceeding to the next island.

• Smaller RBGs within an island will be allocated in either High2Low or Low2High direction.  The direction alternates per PCI according to the following formula:

• If (PCI mod 2 == 0 ) then direction = Low2High,  Else direction = High2Low

• Partial RBG inside island shall be allocated after all other RBGs of the island i.e.: ignoring above rule.

]

• Partial islands shall be allocated ignoring allocation order sequence:

[CB012299-A] When start CRBs of non-largest BWPs are not aligned to the start CRB of largest BWP and DL blanking is not set

* Partial islands shall be allocated last in that non-largest BWP in descendnig order of island coverage factor (ICF), i.e. partial island with the smalest ICF shall be the last one.

Otherwise

* ­ top partial island (located at higher frequencies) shall be allocated as last island.
* ­ bottom partial island (located at lower frequencies) shall be allocated as first island when corresponding RBG from largest BWP is not available for scheduling (e.g.: contains at least one blanked PRB).

Rationale: It is assumed that potential benefit of using PRBs, which are not available in largest BWP is greater than benefit from randomization. As a result, usage of the bottom partial island as first allocated RBG is done on purpose.

[End CB012299-A]

• The physical RBGs allocated to a UE are selected according to the RBG random allocation order in ascending order.

• Note that a random allocation order sequence number will be mapped to all RBG islands even if an RBG(s) in the island is unavailable for scheduling.  When an unavailable RBG is next in the random allocation order the FD scheduler will skip that RBG and proceed to the next RBG in the random allocation order.

* L2 PS shall fulfill the rule described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24135\_replaced([11182274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvCZBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Partial RBG allocation for initial transmission' for partial RBGs in the largest BWP.

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12436612`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
12436612:
DL resource assignment using FDM
This example demonstrates how the FD scheduler allocates RBGs to UEs in multiple BWPs using randomized RBG allocations (CB012299)

For this example the cell contains three BWPs with the following characteristics including the listing of UEs assigned to each BWP:

• BWP1 (40Mhz,  RBG = 8 PRBs)       UE1 needs 1 RBG, UE2 needs 3 RBGs

• BWP2 (20Mhz,starting at CRB28,  RBG = 4 PRBs)       UE3 needs 3 RBGs, UE4 needs 8 RBGs

* At the start of the slot, the island allocation order is determined (see column H )
* At the start of the slot, the island filling direction is determined, High2Low or Low2High.  For this example High2Low is used.
* Per existing FD algorithms UEs in smallest BWP are allocated physical RBGs first, then UEs in next largest BWP, etc.
* Starting with BWP 2 select the RBGs to be assigned to UEs 3 & 4, i.e. BWP2 UEs.  
        - Start the allocation from list of "full" island (i.e. id= 4,5,6,7,8) and follow  the allocation random order  
            - The BWP2 islands will be assigned from smallest to largest random number for the  islands in BWP2, i.e. id=8,4,7,6,5     
                - UE3 will be assigned 2 RBGs in island id=8, 1 RGB in island id=4.    
                - UE4 will be assigned 1 RBGs in island 4 (completes island 4) and then  6 RBGs in island id=7,6,5  
                - the last remaing RGB for UE4 will be selected based on the island coverage factor (ICF):  
                    island id=3 has ICF=50% (4 PRBs in BWP2 vs. 8 PRBs in island)  
                    island id=9 has ICF=87.5% (7 PRBs in BWP2 vs. 8 PRBs in island)  
                        therefore UE3 will be assigned the last 1 RBG in island id=9. This island will not be available in BWP1.
* The BWP1 islands will be assigned from smallest to largest random order number for unused islands in BWP1 that are not overlapping with BWP2, i.e. id=12,0,3,10,11,  
       - UE1 will be assigned 3 RBGs in islands id=12, 0, 3  
       - UE2 will be assigned 1 RBG in island id=10

The following diagram shows the results of the above processing.

Column C contains the numbers 105 and 28. Column D is labeled "BWP 1 RBGs" and contains numbers from 0 to 13, with some cells filled with green and dotted patterns. Column E is labeled "BWP 2 RBGs" and contains numbers from 0 to 12, with some cells filled with yellow and dotted patterns. Column F is labeled "UE RBG Allocations" and contains some cells filled with light blue and dark blue colors. Column G is labeled "RBG Islands" and contains numbers from 0 to 13. Column H is labeled "RBG Random Allocation Order" and contains numbers from 1 to 14, with some numbers in red.
The table also includes labels for "BWP 1 UEs" (UE1, UE2) and "BWP 2 UEs" (UE3, UE4) to the left of the table.
Here is the table in markdown format:

| B | C | D | E | F | G | H |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | CRB | BWP 1 RBGs | BWP 2 RBGs | UE RBG Allocations | RBG Islands |
|  | 105 | 13 (dotted) |  |  |  | 13 |
|  |  | 12 |  |  |  | 12 |
|  |  | 11 |  |  |  | 11 |
|  |  | 10 |  |  |  | 10 |

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-8**  (ID: `11182875`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
RBG random allocation to retransmissions

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11182884`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
[Before 03031401]

When NRCELL.actDlRbgRandomization = enhanced and NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs = False, retransmissions in a dedicated BWP will be allocated RBGs using the RBG random allocation order independent of the initial transmission.

If the number of PRBs in the retransmission is not equal to the number of PRBs in the initial transmission because the partial RBG was used in one and not the other, [CB009425-A] or due to enhanced retransmission scheme (NRBTS/NRCELL/actVoNRIncCodingGain set to TRUE) for eligible VoNR UEs as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14946\_replaced([11185337](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvSQp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB009425-A] the retransmission grant must use a reserved MCS (28, 29, 30, or 31) instead of the MCS that was used for the initial transmission.  The reserved MCS shall be selected so that the modulation order used for the retransmission is the same as the modulation order used for initial transmission. The reserved MCS is required because the code rate changed between the initial transmission and the retransmission, i.e. the original MCS is no longer applicable.  Refer to 3GPP 38.214, section 5.1.3.1 for details.

If DTX is indicated for the initial transmission, altering the properties for the retransmission shall not occur.  Instead the UE shall be rescheduled with the same MCS and the same number of PRBs that were used for the intial transmission.  If the UE did not receive the downlink grant for the initial transmission it will not be able to decode a retansmission with a reserved MCS.

Retransmissions in a dedicated BWP, for which enhanced retransmission scheme for VoNR does not apply (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14946\_replaced([11185337](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvSQp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), shall be allocated same partial RBGs as initial transmissions first, then RBG randomization shall apply to the remaining RBGs to allocate following the same sequence as initial transmissions.

When NRCELL.actDlRbgRandomization = enhanced and NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs = True the properties of the retransmission may be altered by the adaptive retransmission functionality (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  If this occurs, the RBG randomization function shall randomize the location of the retransmission RBGs while maintaining the retransmission properties (MCS and number of PRBs) defined by the adaptive retransmission functionality.

[03031401]

When NRCELL.actDlRbgRandomization = enhanced, if a retransmission requires allocation of one or more partial RBG(s), the RBG randomization function shall randomize the location of the retransmission RBGs while maintaining the retransmission properties (MCS and number of PRBs):

* If adaptive retransmission scheme does not apply to the retransmission, same partial RBG used for initial transmission shall be allocated first before applying randomization of remaining RBGs to be allocated as per initial transmissions allocation sequence.
* If adaptive retransmission scheme applies to the retransmission (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs = True or 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14946\_replaced([11185337](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvSQp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if NRBTS/NRCELL/actVoNRIncCodingGain set to TRUE), the RBG randomization function shall randomize the location of the retransmission RBGs while maintaining the retransmission properties (MCS and number of PRBs) defined by the applied adaptive retransmission scheme

[End 03031401]

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-9**  (ID: `11182890`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
RBG random allocation in cells with DL Interference Avoidance enabled and a single dedicated BWP

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11182907`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11182907:
DL resource assignment using FDM
CB008955 (DL Interference Avoidance) is a TDD only feature that minimizes inter-cell interference for high performance (HP) UEs (high MCS, high rank, high data rate, in largest BWP) by allocating resources for HP UEs at one edge of the bandwidth while allocating resources for non-HP UEs at the other edge of the bandwidth.  Given that there are relatively few HP UEs at an instance in time it is typical that the HP portion of the bandwidth is empty in adjacent cells and therefore the inter-cell interference is reduced for the HP UEs.  See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8194\_replaced([11182952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details of the HP UE allocation algorithm.
 
When RBG randomization and HP DL Interference Avoidance are enabled at the same time (NRCELL.actDlRbgRandomization = enhanced  AND NRCELL. actDlInterferenceAvoidance = True) the objectives of both functions need to be met, i.e. the HP UEs must be scheduled in a part of the bandwidth separate from non-HP UEs according to DL interference avoidance algorithms, and the non-HP UEs need to be allocated RBGs in random allocation order to achieve RBG randomization gains.   The following steps will be taken to achieve this goal in a cell with a single dedicated BWP. 
• The HP UEs are to be allocated RBGs according to the DL interference avoidance algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8194\_replaced([11182952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
• The RBG randomization algorithms described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12660\_replaced([11182732](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvFcVvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) will be followed when allocating RBGs to the non-HP UEs with the following additional guidelines.
• NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs is a percentage that specifies how many RBGs are to be reserved at the edge of the bandwidth used for HP UEs. 
• Number of reserved RBGs = Floor (NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs \* (Number of RBGs in bandwidth) / 100)
[PR783596]
Note: If there is a partial RBG at the top of the BWP, then it shall be included within the number of reserved RBGs. The Number of RBGs in bandwidth includes all the full and partial RBGs. The partial RBG is allocated as per the allocation rules given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24135\_replaced([11182274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvCZBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22504\_replaced([11182922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqdfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
[End PR783596]
• The edge of the bandwidth in which the RBGs are reserved for the HP UEs is specified by NRCELL.dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection
[PR783596]
• If PRB blanking is enabled and there are blanked PRBs configured at the top or bottom, those shall be included in the number of RBGs (and equivalent PRBs) derived from NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs.
[End PR783596]
• If there are no HP UEs in the slot and the non-HP UEs do not need more RBGs than is exclusive from the reserved HP RBGs, the FD scheduler will randomize the RBG allocations in the part of the bandwidth that is not reserved for HP UEs.  This ensures that the local cell will not generate inter-cell interference into adjacent cells that may have HP UEs.
• If there are no HP UEs in the slot and the non-HP UEs need more resources than is in the non-HP area, then RBGs shall be taken from the HP reserved area to fulfill the non-HP UE resource needs. 
• First all RBGs in the non-HP area are to be allocated to the non-HP UEs in random allocation order.
• When RBGs are allocated to non-HP UEs in the HP reserved area they shall be allocated in contiguous order from the end of the reserved area that is in the center of the bandwidth.
 When there are HP and non-HP UEs in the same slot the UEs shall be allocated RBGs as follows:
 Allocate RBGs to the UEs in CS2 list order
 Allocate RBGs to the HP UEs per the DL interference avoidance algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8194\_replaced([11182952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
 Allocate RBGs to the non-HP UEs according to the RBG randomization algorithms described above.
 The non-HP UEs are not allocated RBGs in the reserved HP area unless there are not sufficient RBGs in the non-HP area.
 
The following diagram illustrates various scenarios of allocating RBGs to HP and non-HP UEs.  In these examples the top edge of the bandwidth is used for the HP UEs, i.e. NRCELL.dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection = highPerforming\_High2low.
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a comparative analysis of Resource Block Group (RBG) allocation strategies across four different scenarios, each with varying requirements for High Priority (HP) and Non-High Priority (Non-HP) User Equipments (UEs). The analysis is presented in a tabular format, with each scenario occupying a distinct column.
Each column is further divided into sections: "Random Allocation Order" and "RBGs". The "RBGs" section is split into "Reserved HP RBGs" and "RBGs for non-HP UEs", visually demarcated by brackets. Within these sections, RBGs are listed with their corresponding assigned order numbers. A legend at the bottom indicates that yellow-filled cells represent "HP UEs" and yellow-bordered cells represent "Non-HP UEs".
**Scenario 1: No HP UEs, Non-HP UEs need 5 RBGs. No need to use reserved RBGs.**
This scenario shows an initial allocation of RBGs. The "Reserved HP RBGs" section lists RBGs from 15 down to 7, with corresponding order numbers. The "RBGs for non-HP UEs" section lists RBGs from 6 down to 0, with their respective order numbers.
**Scenario 2: No HP UEs, Non-HP UEs need 12 RBGs. Need to use reserved RBGs.**
This scenario also shows an allocation of RBGs. The "Reserved HP RBGs" section lists RBGs from 15 down to 7, with corresponding order numbers. The "RBGs for non-HP UEs" section lists RBGs from 6 down to 0, with their respective order numbers. Notably, some RBGs in the "Reserved HP RBGs" section are highlighted in yellow, indicating they are used by HP UEs.
**Scenario 3: Two HP UEs need 10 RBGs, Non-HP UEs need 4 RBGs.**
This scenario presents a different allocation. The "Reserved HP RBGs" section lists RBGs from 15 down to 7, with corresponding order numbers. The "RBGs for non-HP UEs" section lists RBGs from 6 down to 0, with their respective order numbers. Several RBGs within the "Reserved HP RBGs" section are highlighted in yellow, indicating their use by HP UEs.
**Scenario 4: HP UEs need 2 RBGs, Non-HP UEs need 12 RBGs.**
This scenario shows an allocation where HP UEs require 2 RBGs and Non-HP UEs require 12 RBGs. The "Reserved HP RBGs" section lists RBGs from 15 down to 7, with corresponding order numbers. The "RBGs for non-HP UEs" section lists RBGs from 6 down to 0, with their respective order numbers. Similar to Scenario 3, some RBGs in the "Reserved HP RBGs" section are highlighted in yellow.
The table below summarizes the RBG allocation for each scenario:

| Category | Scenario 1 (No HP UEs, Non-HP UEs need 5 RBGs) | Scenario 2 (No HP UEs, Non-HP UEs need 12 RBGs) | Scenario 3 (Two HP UEs need 10 RBGs, Non-HP UEs need 4 RBGs) | Scenario 4 (HP UEs need 2 RBGs, Non-HP UEs need 12 RBGs) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Reserved HP RBGs** | 15 (6), 14 (13), 13 (1), 12 (16), 11 (11), 10 (2), 9 (3), 8 (9), 7 (12) | 15 (6), 14 (13), 13 (1), 12 (16), 11 (11), 10 (2), 9 (3), 8 (9), 7 (12) | 15 (6), 14 (13), 13 (1), 12 (16), 11 (11), 10 (2), 9 (3), 8 (9), 7 (12) | 15 (6), 14 (13), 13 (1), 12 (16), 11 (11), 10 (2), 9 (3), 8 (9), 7 (12) |
| **RBGs for non-HP UEs** | 6 (15), 5 (5), 4 (14), 3 (10), 2 (4), 1 (8), 0 (7) | 6 (15), 5 (5), 4 (14), 3 (10), 2 (4), 1 (12), 0 (7) | 6 (15), 5 (5), 4 (14), 3 (10), 2 (4), 1 (12), 0 (7) | 6 (15), 5 (5), 4 (14), 3 (10), 2 (4), 1 (12), 0 (7) |
| **Legend:** |  |  |  |  |
| \* Yellow-filled cells: HP UEs |  |  |  |  |
| \* Yellow-bordered cells: Non-HP UEs[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_13945.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-10**  (ID: `11182912`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
RBG random allocation in cells with DL Interference Avoidance enabled and multiple dedicated BWPs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-5.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11182916`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
When RBG randomization and HP DL Interference Avoidance are enabled at the same time (NRCELL.actDlRbgRandomization = enhanced  AND NRCELL. actDlInterferenceAvoidance = True) and multiple dedicated BWPs are configured in the cell the RBG allocation algorithms defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12665\_replaced([11182890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are followed with the following extensions for multiple BWPs:

• The allocation direction specified by NRCELL.dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection is ignored.  With multiple BWPs, all BWPs line up at the bottom of the bandwidth and the HP reserved area is always positioned at the top of the bandwidth away from the smaller BWPs.

• After the HP UEs are allocated RBGs per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8194\_replaced([11182952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the non-HP UE RBG allocations are made starting with the smallest BWP.  Once UEs in the smallest BWP have been allocated RBGs the UEs in the next largest BWP are allocated RBGs, etc.   Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12661\_replaced([11182756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvFcWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for requirements specifying how to allocate resources in multiple BWPs.

• NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs specifies the percentage of RBGs in the largest BWP that are to be reserved for high performance UEs.  Note that only UEs assigned to the largest BWP can be classified as HP UEs.

• To ensure that VoNR UEs in the power savings (PS) BWP are not impacted by the HP reserved area, the HP reserved area cannot overlap the PS BWP.   If NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs is configured to overlap the PS BWP the reserved area shall be reduced to only those RBGs that are exclusive of the PS BWP.

• As an example if the largest BWP contains 16 RBGs, the PS BWP overlaps the lower 8 RBGs of the largest BWP, and NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs = 75% (12 RBGs), in this case the HP RBG reserved area would be reduced from 12 to 8 RBGs so that there is no overlap with the PS BWP.

• Ideally parameter PDL rules would prevent the operator from setting NRCELL.dlHpReservedRbgs to overlap with the PS BWP.  However this is not doable in PDL rules and therefore must be enforced in the software.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `11182922`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Partial RBG allocation constraint with Resource Allocation Type0 (RAT0)

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11182928`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
L2PS shall not allocate a single partial RBGs with less PRBs than 50% of a full RBG of UE's active BWP to UEs with dlMacCe or voipTraffic request class.

If such an allocation would be the result of the Resource Allocation Algorithm, the UE shall not be scheduled in the current slot but remain a candidate for scheduling in the next slot (as if it had not been added to the to-be-scheduled list).

Overview

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-7.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11182948`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
As specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the parameter n denotes the number of dedicated BWP supported by a cell. If n = 1,  the parameter actDlInterferenceAvoidance is set to TRUE and either one of the following conditions are satisified:

* The parameter NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh is  set to FALSE and no UEs have been paired (co-scheduled) in this slot by 5GC000524 DL MU-MIMO algorithm
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14422\_replaced([11181129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85ePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Skip MU-MIMO Enhanced' is set to TRUE,

then the FD scheduler shall apply the rules below to allocate resources to multiple UEs:

* Categorize the UEs as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8432\_replaced([11181306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Allocate resources to UEs in the order as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8194\_replaced([11182952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvGqe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Else, the resource allocation rules are applied on the basis of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22041\_replaced([11182238](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBx_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11182952`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
FD resource allocation for FDM for 'High performing' and 'normal' UEs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-7.0-1.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `11182979`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = TRUE

Then

gNB-DU shall follow the rules defined below:

For the UEs categorized as ‘high performing’ or ‘normal’ based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8432\_replaced([11181306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB-DU shall allocate resources to UEs with with initial transmissions (not retransmissions) within a single dedicated BWP following the rules below:

* dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection is set to highPerforming\_High2Low
* Normal UEs use PRB allocation direction as low2high as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26236\_replaced([11182477](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDnKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* High performing UEs use PRB allocation direction as high2low
* UEs with SRB traffic shall be allocated from high2low direction if it is categorized as HP UE, otherwise allocation direction would follow similar to normal UE. Rationale: in RAT0, RBG is allocated directly and in case of Multi BWP since SRB UE has higher priority if it gets allocated from Low2High, then there is a risk that Power Saving BWP UEs might not get its part of allocation. Thus in RAT0 it was decided to allocate the SRB traffic class from high2Low. But in RAT1, resources are reserved first, so the SRB UE can be treated as Normal UE.

Additionally in a slot when more than one subarea is created, due to SIB1, Paging, or any other DCI1\_0 allocations, there is no modification in the subarea selection based on whether HP UEs or Normal UEs, and allocation direction mentioned above will be only applicable to the sub-area which is above CORESET 0 allocation (SIB1, Paging, or any other DCI1\_0 allocations) that has the HP PRB reserved region. i.e., during the reservation phase an HP UE could be pinned to SA below CORESET 0, then the allocation direction is low2high for all UE (HP or Normal UE)

Elseif the condition set (If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = TRUE and NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = TRUE and the condition 'Skip MU-MIMO Enhanced' in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14422\_replaced([11181129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85ePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) = FALSE) is evaluated to be TRUE

Then,

Refer to the resource allocation rules described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23918\_replaced([11182175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBx8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22041\_replaced([11182238](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBx_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23986\_replaced([11182340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Else

If the parameter NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh is set to TRUE and the condition 'Skip MU-MIMO Enhanced' in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14422\_replaced([11181129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku85ePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is set to FALSE, below algorithm for 'high performing' or 'normal' UEs shall be disabled. FD resources shall always be allocated in direction of low2high

For the UEs categorized as ‘high performing’ or ‘normal’ based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8432\_replaced([11181306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), FD scheduler allocate resources to UEs  with initial transmissions (not retransmissions) within a single dedicated BWP following the rules below:

* FD scheduler shall sort the UEs according to their actual needed number of PRBs
* Based on the values of the parameter dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection the following resource allocation directions are used
* if dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection is set to highPerforming\_Low2High
* Normal UEs use PRB allocation direction as high2low
* High performing UEs use PRB allocation direction as low2high
* UEs with SRB traffic (high performing or normal)  use PRB allocation direction as low2high
* if dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection is set to highPerforming\_High2Low
* Normal UEs use PRB allocation direction as low2high
* High performing UEs use PRB allocation direction as high2low
* UEs with SRB traffic (high performing or normal)  use PRB allocation direction as low2high

EndIf

[PR809767]

If slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall always ensure that the sum of allocated PRBs to all UEs (In case of RAT0 based allocations, the RBGs shall be converted to PRBs) does not exceed the allocated number of PRB resources to the cell by the BBRM for the current pooling period (numAlloCellPrbDl)

[End PR809767]

[PR814985]

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, gNB-DU shall always ensure that the sum of allocated layer PRBs to all UEs shall not exceed the remaining available layer PRBs in a slot (rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot; refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The number of layer PRBs used for a given PRB allocation can be calculated as numReqLayerPrb mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End PR814985]

Note:

* If there are only high performing UEs to be scheduled in a slot, FD scheduler shall apply a limit based on dlInterferenceAvoidance.rbgLimit to block/reserve the contiguous block of upper RBGs while scheduling the high performing UEs. The limit is applied below blanked RBGs, i.e.: from first RBG, which does not contain any blanked PRB. This is applicable irrespective of whether dlInterferenceAvoidance.prbAllocationDirection is set to highPerforming\_Low2High or highPerforming\_High2Low. [PR803079] If NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh is set to true, then RBGs blocked by this limit might be still allocated if needed, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7530\_replaced([11145129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_FBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Allocation of remaining RBGs/PRBs'. [End PR803079] Note that when NRCELL.actRat1Alloc=True, then dlInterferenceAvoidance.rbgLimit must always be set to 0.
* If there are UEs to be scheduled in the list with traffic corresponding to request class of the priority gbrTraffic or higher according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9891\_replaced([11174018](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuYRsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) then FD scheduler shall not use the parameter dlInterferenceAvoidance.rbgLimit for those UEs. Such UEs shall be treated as Normal UEs.
* Note that gNB-DU shall apply the rbgLimit only in slots for which the corresponding bit is 1 in the bitmap rdRbgLimitedSlot (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30312\_replaced([11194051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If there are both normal and high performing UEs in a slot to be scheduled, then FD scheduler shall not apply any limit and the parameter dlInterferenceAvoidance.rbgLimit is not used at all because the normal UEs will occupy the upper RBG space.
* Handling of retransmissions is not modified by downlink interference avoidance feature: allocation of frequency resources for retransmission for the UEs that are classified as High Performing are handled in the same way as retransmission for others UEs (see requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Scheduling of a Re-transmission' for more details on retransmission processing).
* The details of low2high and high2low allocation directions are described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22041\_replaced([11182238](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBx_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Resource Allocation Direction'.

The rationale for the above mentioned limitation on FD resource usage for high performance UEs is a potential improvement in avoiding inter-cell DL interference (when allocating less than full PRBs on adjacent cells). Consequently, this leads to an improvement in DL throughput (higher the interference, higher the gains).

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11182991`)

DL resource assignment using FDM

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-2.0-2.0-7.0-2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11183011`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRAT1Alloc = False, 5G-L2-PS shall distribute resources for UEs with initial transmissions (not retransmissions) as follows:

UEs with SRB traffic (high performance or normal) shall be allocated using high2low direction, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21259\_replaced([11182302](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvCZDPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'FD resource allocation direction for UEs with multiple dedicated bandwidth parts per cell'.

For other UEs:

* i-th BWP exclusive resources (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4507\_replaced([11181223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku9gi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are distributed before j-th BWP exclusive resources where i > j.
* if i > 1, then i-th BWP resources are distributed among UEs such that:
* ­UE is classified as normal (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8432\_replaced([11181306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and
* ­UE needs are not satisfied by resources already allocated to it and
* ­UE is kth BWP UE where k in {1, ...,i}

according to the following rules:

* ­resources are allocated to UEs with higher BWP before UEs with lower BWP until all UEs needs are satisfied or no further allocation of i-th BWP resources is possible.
* ­use low2high PRB allocation method, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21259\_replaced([11182302](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvCZDPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'FD resource allocation direction for UEs with multiple dedicated bandwidth parts per cell'
* if i = 1, then 1st BWP resources are distributed among UEs such that

­- UE is classified as normal and

* ­UE needs are not satisfied by resources already allocated to it and
* ­UE is 1st BWP UE

according to the following rules:

* ­resources are allocated to UEs with 1st BWP until all UEs needs are satisfied or no further allocation of 1st BWP resources is possible.
* ­use low2high PRB allocation method, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21259\_replaced([11182302](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvCZDPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'FD resource allocation direction for UEs with multiple dedicated bandwidth parts per cell'
* After above steps, remaining 1st BWP resources are distributed among UEs classified as high performing (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8432\_replaced([11181306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) according to the following rules:
* ­if there are both normal and high performing UEs in a slot to be scheduled resources are allocated to UEs until all UEs needs are satisfied or no further allocation of 1st BWP resources is possible.
* ­if there are only high performing UEs in the slot, then resources are allocated to UEs until all UEs needs are satisfied or dlInterferenceAvoidance.rbgLimit is reached.Note that the gNB-DU shall apply the rbgLimit only in slots for which the corresponding bit is 1 in the bitmap rdRbgLimitedSlot (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30312\_replaced([11194051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* ­use high2low PRB allocation method, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21259\_replaced([11182302](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvCZDPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'FD resource allocation direction for UEs with multiple dedicated bandwidth parts per cell'

Notes:

1. Handling of retransmissions is not modified by downlink interference avoidance feature: allocation of frequency resources for retransmission for the UEs that are classified as High Performing are handled in the same way as retransmission for others UEs (see requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Scheduling of a Re-transmission' for more details on retransmission processing).

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRAT1Alloc = True, the resource allocations method would remain same as above mentioned rules, except that:

* for the normal UEs StartPrb randomization principle is used as while allocating resources from LowToHigh as mentioned in algorithm 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26236\_replaced([11182477](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDnKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* UEs with SRB traffic shall be allocated from high2low direction if it is categorized as HP UE, otherwise allocation direction would follow similar to normal UE. Rationale: in RAT0, RBG is allocated directly and in case of Multi BWP since SRB UE has higher priority if it gets allocated from Low2High, then there is a risk that Power Saving BWP UEs might not get its part of allocation. Thus in RAT0 it was decided to allocate the SRB traffic class from high2Low. But in RAT1, resources are reserved first, so the SRB UE can be treated as Normal UE.
* Additionally in a slot when more than one subarea is created, due to SIB1, Paging, or any other DCI1\_0 allocations, there is no modification in the subarea selection based on whether HP UEs or Normal UEs, and allocation direction mentioned above will be only applicable to the sub-area which is above CORESET 0 allocation (SIB1, Paging, or any other DCI1\_0 allocations) that has the HP PRB reserved region. i.e., during the reservation phase an HP UE could be pinned to PDSCH sub-area below CORESET 0, then the allocation direction is low2high for all UE (HP or Normal UE).

[PR809767]

If slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall always ensure that the sum of allocated PRBs to all UEs (In case of RAT0 based allocations, the RBGs shall be converted to PRBs) does not exceed the allocated number of PRB resources to the cell by the BBRM for the current pooling period (numAlloCellPrbDl)

[End PR809767]

[PR814985]

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, gNB-DU shall always ensure that the sum of allocated layer PRBs to all UEs shall not exceed the remaining available layer PRBs in a slot (rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot; refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The number of layer PRBs used for a given PRB allocation can be calculated as numReqLayerPrb mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End PR814985]

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11183025`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
FD resource allocation to UEs is limited in 2 different ways based on the type of pooling that is enabled.

-When stream PRB based pooling is enabled i.e. slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True, the limitations are mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26611\_replaced([11183029](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvHRi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-When layer PRB based pooling is enabled i.e. slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, the limitations are mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26612\_replaced([11183051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvH4kPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11183029`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Limitation by stream PRB pooling

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-3.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11183045`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
[CB008927-A] If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, [End CB008927-A] if slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), all RBGs may not be available for use. The number of useable RBGs could be limited as indicated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4279\_replaced([11177002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If the full RBG can not be used due to the usable PRB limit per cell is reached and if the first RBG or last RBG has less PRBs compared to the full RBG,  check shall be done if the first RBG or last RBG can be used instead and the usable PRB limit is respected. If both slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true' and PRB blanking is enabled, the RBGs can be used or not also depend on PRB blanking configuration. If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed set to 'true', first RBG shall be checked when actSpecificBwpSize is true in PsCell\_CellSetupReq. Please also refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008927-A] If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,  if slowPrbPoolingAllowed set to 'true', all PRBs may not be available for use and each time PRBs requires to be reserved for a RAT1 PDSCH grant within a PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall re evaluate the number of  remaining PRBs for the dedicated BWP and the CORESET #0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008927-L] and the number of RBGs of an allocated dynamicswitch RAT 0 [End CB008927-L] and shall proceed the constraints in order to check that the usable PRB limit is respected (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  [End CB008927-A]  [CB008927-B] The limitation due to PRB blanking and specific bandwidth size are handled in the computation of the size and the startPrb of the PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23455\_replaced([11175942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End CB008927-B]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11183051`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Limitation by layer PRB pooling

---

**3.29.7.2.0-14.0-3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11183061`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false AND if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, all RBGs may not be available for use. The number of useable RBGs could be limited depending on the usable layer PRB capacity (rrmMaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as indicated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4279\_replaced([11177002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If the full RBG can not be used due to the usable layer PRB limit per cell is reached and if the last RBG has less PRBs compared to the full RBG, check shall be done if the number of layer PRBs required with the last RBG can be used instead (by considering the number of PRBs in the last RBG when calculating rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the usable layer PRB limit is respected.

[CB010630-E]

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,  if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, all PRBs may not be available for use.The number of useable RBGs could be limited depending on the usable layer PRB capacity (rrmMaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as indicated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4279\_replaced([11177002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Each time PRBs are to be reserved for a RAT1 PDSCH grant within a PDSCH sub area, gNB-DU shall re evaluate the number of  remaining layer PRBs for and shall proceed the constraints in order to check that the usable layer PRB limit is respected (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26597\_replaced([11214606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End CB010630-E]
