* As many NBR UEs shall be selected such that sum of the DL-PDCCH CCE utilisation of the selected UEs for scheduling its NBR bearers is larger or equal to the excess of the PDCCH GBR+NBR load hysteresis limit.
* All NBR bearers of the de-prioritized NBR UEs shall be considered as normal non-GBR traffic (NBR configuration is ignored even if the bearers have positive NBR token bucket) and shall be treated as normal nonGBR category in scheduling is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NBR PDCCH congestion is considered to be resolved if the average PDCCH GBR+NBR load drops below the PDCCH GBR+NBR load hysteresis limit,  otherwise congestion is not considered resolved.

When NBR congestion is resolved, all de-prioritized NBR UEs will return to normal NBR scheduling. In other words, the set of de-prioritized NBR UEs is emptied.

If PDCCH GBR+NBR load further increases, additional NBR UEs shall be selected for de-prioritization and according handling shall apply in scheduling.

Note: If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), as the NBR congestion control mechanism is per dedicated BWP, it is possible that congestion might be detected in one BWP but not in the other and so on.

[Before CB008118-CB]

Note: As per NIDD description, maxNbrTrafficLimit represents the cumulative load limit of both GBR+NBR. However, since GBR PDCCH load montioring is not available till now, for PDCCH channel, maxNbrTrafficLimit is considered only as NBR load limit (by setting avgGbrDlPdcchLoad = 0). This will result in over-utilization of PDCCH resources for GBR+NBR and the number of PDCCH CCEs available for nonGBR traffic might be quite low. In extreme situations (depending on configuration and load), all the available CCEs might be utilized for GBR+NBR traffic resulting in no PDCCH resources available for nonGBR traffic. This current behaviour can be corrected once GBR congestion control is introduced in future release and thereby also including the GBR PDCCH load here in the calculation.

[CB008118-CB]

Note: As per NIDD description, maxNbrTrafficLimit represents the combined load limit of both GBR and NBR. Congestion is detected, if the periodic average DL PDCCH GBR+NBR load exceeds the NRCELL.maxNbrTrafficLimit. For GBR PDCCH(DL) load montioring(refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31283\_replaced([11183567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  Monitoring and counting of GBR PDCCH load shall be included into DL GBR PDCCH load. L2-PS shall monitor the PDCCH resources (CCEs) used for scheduling the prioritized (i.e. having positive GBR token bucket) DL GBR bearers configured in a given cell.  GBR traffic (including MC-GBR & DC-GBR) should not be greater than NRCELL.maxGBRTrafficLimit. GBR congestion control shall be triggered when total PDCCH(DL) load of GBR exceeds the congestion limit:NRCELL.maxGBRTrafficLimit.

[End CB008118-CB]

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4**  (ID: `11183503`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
DL NBR time domain congestion control(descoped from CB008227)

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11183507`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Monitoring of DL NBR Time Domain load

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11183512`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
L2-PS shall monitor the NBR DL time domain load and used for NBR congestion control on cell in FR2 TDD.

Activation: NBR congestion control is activated for DL (NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrPdschCongHandling = TRUE)

Metrics:

* Scope: per cell
* Units: percent
* Base:

- NbrAllocationsDl(P) = nAllocations\_made(P) / nAllocations\_usable(P), where:

- nAllocations\_made(P) =  number of allocations by L2-PS for PDSCH transport blocks that are determined for NBR category UE(UE having NBR configured Non-GBR bearers with positive token bucket and not de-prioritized, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), over the period P.

- nAllocations\_usable(P) = number of allocations usable by L2-PS for PDSCH for any DRB over the period P, rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl \* # of available slot in the period P. For FR2, the rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl=1.

- P = 100 ms (fixed)

-Average:

- avgNbrDlTdLoad(P) = ( 1/Ttdli ) \*NbrAllocations (P) + ( 1−( 1/Ttdli ) ) \* avgNbrDlTdLoad(P−1) ; where

- Ttdli = time constant for filter (number of P intervals), could be a R&D parameter, with the following suggested properties; - Range= 5...100; Step size= 5; Default= 20

* Reported load:

- Using periodic NBR Time domain DL load for congestion detection, with period = T

- avgNbrDlTdLoad(T) = is the latest value of avgNbrDlTdLoad (P) prior to T

- T refers to end of NBR congestion detection period provided at cell setup/reconfiguration (NRCELL.congDetectPeriod). Any change in value provided is immediately taken into use at end of current congestion detection interval

Exceptions:

* If NBR congestion control is deactivated for PDSCH (NRCELL.nbrPdschCongHandling = FALSE), or NBR congestion detection period NRCELL.congDetectPeriod = 0, L2-PS does not need to perform this NBR Load monitoring
* If there are no NBR bearers, avgNbrDlTdLoad(T) = 0%
* Slots without any PDSCH available shall be skipped.

Notes:

* NBR time domain load is per cell, so it reflects slot utilization over all configured dedicated BWPs

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11183517`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
NBR DL time domain congestion detection

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11183523`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
L2-PS shall support the NBR DL time domain congestion detection mechanism:

This requirement only applies to cell in FR2 TDD SA mode.

Activation: NBR congestion control is activated for DL (NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrPdschCongHandling = TRUE)

NBR DL time domain congestion detection requires the following inputs:

* NBR DL time domain periodic average load in the cell:  avgNbrDlTdLoad(T) refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22786\_replaced([11183507](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* DL time domain NBR congestion limit: marks a certain maximum percentage of resource allocation for NBR as defined by O&M parameter NRCELL.maxNbrTrafficLimit.
* DL time domain NBR congestion hysteresis: R&D parameter rdDlsTdCongHyst (5G\_UP\_ALG\_23326\_replaced([11193656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvuVkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* DL Time domain NBR congestion hysteresis limit: as product of the DL Time domain NBR congestion limit and the DL time domain congestion hysteresis factor.

NBR DL time domain congestion is detected if the calculated NBR DL time domain periodic average load exceeds the NRCELL.maxNbrTrafficLimit.

As soon as NBR DL time domain congestion is detected, the following handling shall apply:

* NBR bearers shall be selected for de-prioritization in scheduling
* Criteria for NBR bearer selection is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23165\_replaced([11183528](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Compute DL time domain resource utilization NBR UE is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22935\_replaced([11183537](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* As many NBR UE shall be selected such that sum of the NBR time domain resource utilization of the selected UE(s) is larger or equal to the excess of the DL NBR time domain congestion hysteresis limit.
* Deprioritize NBR category UE shall be treated as normal nonGBR UE in scheduling is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

NBR DL time domain congestion is considered to be resolved if the average NBR DL time domain load drops below the DL NBR time domain load hysteresis limit.

[Before CB008227-DA\_UPD001] When NBR congestion is resolved, all de-prioritized NBR bearers return to normal NBR scheduling. In other words, the set of de-prioritized NBR bearers is emptied.

[CB008227-DA\_UPD001]

When NBR congestion is resolved, re-prioritize NBR bearers to normal NBR scheduling according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23534\_replaced([11183546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB008227-DA\_UPD001]

If DL NBR time domain load further increases, additional NBR UE(s) shall be selected for de-prioritization.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11183528`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
NBR UE prioritization in DL Time domain congestion

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11183531`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
The DL NBR UEs to be de-prioritized and excluded from NBR scheduling list are determined in order of the following criteria:

  Step1: From higher to lower congestion priority value based on the NRCELL.congWeightAlg selection:

* If the NRCELL.congWeightAlg = ''priorityLevel ', the NBR bearer congestion priority is based on the NRBTS/NRDRB.priorityLevel configured value for the bearer, the lower priority values indicate higher priority.
* If the NRCELL.congWeightAlg = 'arpPrioPerVul', the bearer congestion priority is based on the ARP priority configured for the bearers (refer to 5G\_CP\_IF\_NTW\_5886\_replaced([11408282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__GmynPvmEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). First the bearers with ARP vulnerability "preemptable" are prioritized in order of the ARP priority, then the bearers with ARP vulnerability "not preemptable" also in order of ARP priority.
* The UE congestion priority value is equal to the highest congestion priority value among all NBR bearers within the UE

  Setp2: Then from higher to lower DL time domain load per NBR UE (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22935\_replaced([11183537](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  Step3: Then in random order.

This selection needs only be performed when NBR DL time domain congestion is detected or when following this detection as load level changes, additional UEs need to be de-prioritized. It is left to entity level/implementation to define which exact procedure has the least complexity (e.g., triggered search, managed sorted lists, etc).

The de-prioritisation shall be performed on UE basis in case of NBR Time domain congestion.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `11183537`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Estimation DL Time domain load per NBR UE

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11183542`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
L2-PS shall compute DL time domain resource utilisation per NBR UE that used for DL time domain congestion (de-)prioritization on cell in FR2 TDD.

Activation: NBR congestion control is activated for DL (NRBTS/NRCELL.nbrPdschCongHandling = TRUE), and the NBR time domain congestion is detected (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22871\_replaced([11222689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kx8jHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Metrics:

* Scope: per UE with NBR bearer(s)
* Units: percent

This can be expressed in the following equation

NbrDlTdLoad\_i = ∑k nbrDl[i,k] / [ ∑j maxTbsOnCarrier(i, j)\* NumDlSlotPerSecond] , where:

i:          index of the UE with NBR bearer.

j:         index of carrier

k:         index of the NBR bearer.

∑k  nbrDl[i,k]   The sum of the NBR setting (NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC.nbrDl) for all NBR bearers UE is having.

maxTbsOnCarrier(i,j): calculated max number of PRBs usable for PDSCH for any DRB, while MCS and rank are the ones that are selected for the UE i on carrier j, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11651\_replaced([11192714](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvmZvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note:

* If NBR congestion control is deactivated for DL (NRBTS/NRCELL.nbrPdschCongHandling = FALSE), or NBR time domain congestion is not detected, L2-PS does not need to calculate this NBR Time domain Load per UE.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `11183546`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
DL NBR time domain congestion reprioritization

---

**3.29.7.2.0-20.0-4.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11183551`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
[CB008227-DA\_UPD001]

L2-PS shall support the deprioritized NBR UEs controllable reprioritize to NBR scheduling when NBR time domain congestion is resolved.

The reprioritization should consider the candidate UEs which are deprioritized to nonGBR in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22817\_replaced([11183517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and sort the candidate UEs in order of increasing TD load from lower to higher, and select as many UEs to be reprioritized such that the sum of aggregate NBR time domain load still below the congestion threshold.

It can be expressed in the following steps:

* Aggregate cell NBR TD load = The cell NBR DL time domain load avgNbrDlTdLoad (T) which latest detected (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22786\_replaced([11183507](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* Sort the candidate NBR UEs which from lower to higher of UE DL time domain load calculated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22935\_replaced([11183537](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For UE in the candidate list as per sorted order,

If (aggregate cell NBR TD load + TD load of next candidate UE) < congestion threshold;

Convert next candidate UE from non-GBR to NBR

Aggregate cell NBR TD load += TD load of next candidate UE

Else

break

End

* Set the average cell NBR TD load avgNbrTdLoad(t) (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22786\_replaced([11183507](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) equal to the last estimated value of above 'Aggregate cell NBR TD load'.

[End CB008227-DA\_UPD001]

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-21 DL resource assignment using FDM DL GBR congestion control (ID: 11183557)

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-1**  (ID: `11183562`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
PDCCH GBR congestion control

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11183567`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Monitoring of DL GBR PDCCH  Load

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13396651`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB-DU shall monitor the PDCCH resources (CCEs) used for scheduling the prioritized (i.e. having positive GBR token bucket) DL GBR bearers [CB014699-D] & DL critical GBR bearers [End CB014699-D] configured in a given cell which is eventually used to perform DL GBR congestion control based on PDCCH channel.

This requirement is applicable only for FR1(both TDD and FDD). The rationale is that there is no benefit in executing PDCCH congestion control algorithm in FR2 without FDM & dynamic PDCCH.

Activation: GBR feature and DL PDCCH congestion control is activated:

(NRBTS/NRCELL.actNonVoNRGBRService = TRUE & NRBTS/NRCELL.gbrPdcchCongHandlingDl = TRUE) in the cell.

Metrics:

* Scope: per cell.
* Unit: percent
* Base:

IF n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

* GbrDlPdcchLoad (i, t) = nCCE\_used(i, t) / nCCE\_usable(i, t), where;
* For ith dedicated BWP, where i ϵ {2, …, n}:
* nCCE\_used(i, t) = number of CCEs within ith dedicated BWP allocated for PDCCH (DL grant) for UE(s) with highest eligible priority class = [CB014699-D] criticalVoipTraffic, criticalGbrTraffic  and [End CB014699-D] VoipTraffic and gbrTraffic and scheduling PDSCH transport block containing data from their prioritized (GBR positive token bucket) GBR bearer irrespective of their active BWP, otherwise, it shall be 0.
* If NRCELL/actDcGbrService = TRUE:
* nCCE\_used(i, t) = number of CCEs within ith dedicated BWP allocated for PDCCH (DL grant) for UE(s) with highest eligible priority class =[CB014699-D] criticalVoipTraffic, criticalGbrTraffic & [End CB014699-D] voipTraffic, gbrTraffic and dcGbrTraffic and scheduling PDSCH transport block containing data from their prioritized GBR (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8009\_replaced([11174223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) bearer or prioritized DC-GBR (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30353\_replaced([11174200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuZf3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  irrespective of their active BWP, otherwise, it shall be 0.
* nCCE\_usable(i, t) = number of CCEs available for DL NR scheduling within the ith dedicated BWP.
* For ith dedicated BWP, where i =1 (largest dedicated BWP):

-  nCCE\_used(i, t) = number of CCEs within nth dedicated BWP allocated for PDCCH (DL grant) for UE(s) with highest eligible priority class = [CB014699-D] criticalVoipTraffic, criticalGbrTraffic and [End CB014699-D] VoipTraffic & gbrTraffic and scheduling PDSCH transport block containing data from their prioritized (GBR positive token bucket) GBR bearer irrespective of their active BWP, otherwise, it shall be 0.

* nCCE\_usable(i, t) = number of CCEs available for DL NR scheduling within the nth dedicated BWP.
* Where:  t refers to scheduled slot.

ELSE

* GbrDlPdcchLoad(t) = nCCE\_used(t) / nCCE\_usable(t), where:
* nCCE\_used(t) = number of CCEs allocated for PDCCH (DL grant) for UE(s) with highest eligible priority class = VoIP traffic & GBR traffic and scheduling PDSCH transport block containing data from its prioritized (GBR positive token bucket) GBR bearer, otherwise, it shall be 0.
* nCCE\_usable(t) =  number of CCEs available for DL NR scheduling.
* Where: t refers to scheduled slot.

END

* Notes for nCCE\_usable calculation:
* nCCE\_usable shall calculate the number of CCEs available for DL scheduling in any given slot.
* The construction of coreset and PDCCH slot pattern for a given cell is explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11211\_replaced([11168405](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktv_kfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) & 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3958\_replaced([11168909](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktzp8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and split of CCEs, if any, between UL/DL is explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3955\_replaced([11169860](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt6Xp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) which can be used to calculate nCCE\_usable.
* The pre-empted CCEs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4032\_replaced([11169907](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt6-svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the available CCEs, if any, shall be considered in nCCE\_usable.
* With PRB pooling (if NRBTS.slowPrbPoolingAllowed or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is set to 'true'), the nCCE\_usable shall consider the number of CCEs that can be used which is limited by the total amount of usable PRB allocated for PDCCH by the BBRM and this grant may change every BB pooling period (5G\_UP\_ALG\_6960\_replaced([11169929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt6-t_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* Average:
* avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(t) = (1/Tprb) \* GbrDlPdcchLoad(t) + (1−(1/Tprb)) \* avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(t−1)
* If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, t) = (1/filterCoefficient) \* GbrDlPdcchLoad(i, t) + (1−(1/filterCoefficient)) \* avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, t−1)
* Where: i ϵ {1, 2, …, n} and n refers to the number of dedicated BWP per cell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Where: filterCoefficient could be a R&D parameter, with the following suggested properties:
* Range= 40...400; Step size= 10; Default= 200
* Periodic Load:

At the end of every GBR congestion detection period T (NRCELL.congDetectPeriod):

- avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(T) = the latest value of avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(t)  prior to T

- If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

- avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, T) = the latest value of avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, t)  prior to T

- Where: i ϵ {1, 2, …, n} and n refers to the number of dedicated BWP per cell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Exceptions:

* For gbrPdcchCongHandlingDl = FALSE or GBR congestion detection period NRCELL.congDetectPeriod = 0: L2-PS does not need to perform this GBR DL PDCCH Load monitoring
* If there are no GBR configured bearers, the avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(T) is set to 0%
* Slots without any CCEs available for DL NR PDCCH (PDCCH for DL grant) shall be skipped.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12495956`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Monitoring of DL PDCCH resources per GBR UE

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `13396653`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB-DU shall monitor the PDCCH resource (CCEs) used by UEs for scheduling the prioritized (i.e. having positive GBR token bucket) DL GBR bearers [CB014699-D] and DL critical GBR bearers [End CB014699-D] which is eventually used to de-prioritize those GBR UEs during PDCCH congestion control based on their GBR resource usage.

This requirement is applicable only for FR1(both TDD and FDD). The rationale is that there is no benefit in executing PDCCH congestion control algorithm in FR2 without FDM & dynamic PDCCH.

Activation: GBR feature and DL PDCCH congestion control is activated:   
(NRBTS/NRCELL.actNonVoNRGBRService = TRUE & NRBTS/NRCELL.gbrPdcchCongHandlingDl = TRUE) in the cell.

Metrics:  
- Scope: per UE  
- Unit: CCEs

Base:  
        - usedCCEsGbrDl(i, t) = number of CCEs allocated for PDCCH (DL grant) for UE i with highest priority class = [CB014699-D] criticalVoipTraffic, criticalGbrTraffic and [End CB014699-D] VoIP traffic and GBR traffic and scheduling PDSCH transport block containing data from its prioritized (GBR positive token bucket) GBR bearer in slot t, otherwise, it shall be 0.  
    - Where:  
        i refers index of the UE   
        t refers to the scheduled slot

* Average:  
      The average PDCCH CCE utilization by the UE i to schedule GBR data is then calculated via an exponential filter every DL scheduling slot t  
      - avgUsedCCEsGbrDl(i,t) = (1/filterCoefficient) \* usedCCEsGbrDl(i,t) + (1−(1/filterCoefficient)) \* avgUsedCCEsGbrDl(i,t−1)

filterCoefficient could be a R&D parameter constant for the averaging function, with the following suggested properties:  
    - Range= 40...400; Step size= 10; Default= 200

* Periodic Load:  
          At the end of every GBR congestion detection period T (NRCELL.congDetectPeriod):    
      - avgUsedCCEsGbrDl(i, T) = the latest value of avgUsedCCEsGbrDl(i) prior to T which is used for GBR UE de-prioritization (5G\_UP\_ALG\_31321\_replaced([11183615](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when GBR congestion is detected on the PDCCH channel (5G\_UP\_ALG\_31285\_replaced([11183590](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Exceptions:  
- For gbrPdcchCongHandlingDl = FALSE or GBR congestion detection period NRCELL.congDetectPeriod = 0: L2-PS does not need to perform this GBR DL PDCCH Load monitoring  
-  Slots without any CCEs available for DL NR PDCCH (PDCCH for DL grant) shall be skipped.  
- The exponential filter is updated DL slot (see above) or only when UE is scheduled. In later case, the filter update shall also be done in that slot for all missing DL NR slots (considering zero load in those slots) since the filter was last updated so that the UE GBR load is not artificially seen higher. It is left to entity level/implementation to define which manner has the least complexity.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11183590`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
DL PDCCH congestion detection

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13396656`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB-DU shall support the GBR DL PDCCH congestion detection mechanism.

This requirement is applicable only for FR1(both TDD and FDD). The rationale is that there is no benefit in executing PDCCH congestion control algorithm in FR2 without FDM & dynamic PDCCH.

**Activation: GBR feature and PDCCH congestion control is activated**

(NRBTS/NRCELL.actNonVoNRGBRService = TRUE & NRBTS/NRCELL.gbrPdcchCongHandlingDl = TRUE) in the cell.

Scope: GBR PDCCH congestion shall be detected

IF n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

* Per dedicated BWP i.e. 'n' independent PDCCH congestion detection mechanism per cell.

Note, there can be more than one dedicated BWP configured per cell. Power saving BWP is considered a dedicated BWP and GBR PDCCH congestion detection shall also be performed for power saving BWP.

ELSE

* per cell.

GBR DL PDCCH congestion detection requires the following inputs:

* NRCELL.actGBRAdmissionControl is set to 2(Corresponding to GBR services).
* GBR congestion limit: NRCELL.maxGBRTrafficLimit (I.e. ,marks a certain maximum percentage of resource allocation for GBR periodic average DL PDCCH CCE utilization for UEs scheduling its GBR bearers in the cell).
* For DL PDCCH GBR CCE load, namely avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(T) refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31282\_replaced ([11183562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi-_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For DL PDCCH DC-GBR CCE load, namely avgDcGbrDlPdcchLoad(T) refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32407\_replaced ([11183581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi_vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* DL PDCCH GBR congestion hysteresis factor (R&D parameter rdPdcchGbrCceCongHyst, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31290\_replaced ([11193635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvuVifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* DL PDCCH GBR hysteresis limit = (DL PDCCH GBR congestion limit) \* (DL PDCCH GBR congestion hysteresis factor)

Once every GBR congestion detection period (NRCELL.congDetectPeriod):

* DL PDCCH GBR congestion is detected, if the periodic average DL PDCCH GBR load exceeds the NRCELL.maxGbrTrafficLimit, otherwise congestion is not considered detected.

Note that when the PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell ([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) *GBR congestion limit takes the value of* maxGBRTrafficLimit\_L2PS (\*\*[12704521S](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_oy0TEGCREfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) ).

For DL PDCCH GBR load, following conditions is defined:

If NRCELL/actDcGbrService = TRUE,

* DL PDCCH GBR load = Sum of periodic average DL PDCCH GBR CCE load avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, T) and DL PDCCH DC-GBR CCE load avgDcGbrDlPdcchLoad(T)

Else

* DL PDCCH GBR  load = Periodic average  DL PDCCH GBR CCE load avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, T)

If  n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

* For i\_th dedicated BWP, where i ϵ {2, …, n}, DL PDCCH GBR load= Periodic average DL PDCCH GBR CCE load avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, T);
* For i\_th BWP, where i = 1 (largest dedicated BWP), DL PDCCH GBR load= DC-GBR PDCCH usage avgDcGbrDlPdcchLoad(T)+ DL PDCCH GBR  CCE load avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, T)

Rationale: To restrict the sum of CCE usage for all kinds of GBR traffic (including [CB014699-D] critical GBR, critical VoNR and [End CB014699-D] GBR, MC-GBR, DC-GBR) not exceed NRCELL.maxGBRTrafficLimit, as DC-GBR PDCCH usage avgDcGbrDlPdcchLoad(T) only report for largest BWP, so it shall be count together with DL GBR PDCCH usages of avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, T) for largest BWP GBR DL PDCCH congestion detection; and for other BWP(except largest BWP) the DL GBR PDCCH usages of avgGbrDlPdcchLoad(i, T) it counts all GBR(also including MC-GBR, DC-GBR) CCE usage that is used for GBR DL PDCCH congestion detection.

As soon as GBR PDCCH(DL) congestion is detected, the following handling shall apply:

* UEs configured with GBR bearers (having positive token bucket) shall be selected for de-prioritization in scheduling. GBR PDCCH congestion control shall not be applicable to 5QI1/65/66, [CB014699-D] critical 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB014699-D] and DC-GBR.
* Criteria for GBR UE selection is same as PDSCH defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31321\_replaced ([11183615](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* As many GBR UEs shall be selected such that sum of the DL-PDCCH CCE utilization of the selected UEs for scheduling its GBR bearers is larger or equal to the excess of the PDCCH GBR load hysteresis limit.
* All GBR bearers of the de-prioritized GBR UEs shall be considered as normal non-GBR traffic and shall be treated as normal non-GBR category in scheduling as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced ([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* [CB014699-D] All critical GBR bearers of the de-prioritized critical GBR UEs shall be considered as critical non-GBR traffic under criticalDrbTraffic request class (refer to [13393326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_lHKq4wZMEfGozuGzRUXHpg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB014699-D].

GBR PDCCH congestion is considered to be resolved, if the average PDCCH GBR load drops below the PDCCH GBR load hysteresis limit, otherwise congestion is not considered resolved.

When GBR congestion is resolved, all de-prioritized GBR UEs [CB014699-D] and Critical GBR UEs [End CB014699-D] will return to normal GBR scheduling. In other words, the set of de-prioritized GBR UEs is emptied.

If PDCCH GBR load further increases, additional GBR UEs/ [CB014699-D] critical GBR UE [End CB014699-D] shall be selected for de-prioritization and accordingly handling shall be applied in scheduling.

Note:

* If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced ([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), as the GBR congestion control mechanism is per dedicated BWP, it is possible that congestion might be detected in one BWP but not in the other and so on.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-2**  (ID: `11183601`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
PDSCH GBR congestion control

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11183605`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
GBR PDSCH PRB load congestion detection

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13396609`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB-DU shall support the GBR PDSCH congestion detection mechanism on the cell in FR1 TDD and FDD.

* Activation: GBR feature and PDSCH congestion control is activated in the cell

(NRBTS/NRCELL.actNonVoNRGBRService = TRUE & NRBTS/NRCELL.gbrPdschCongHandling = TRUE)

* Scope: GBR PDSCH congestion shall be detected
* IF n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

-  Per dedicated BWP i.e. 'n' independent PDSCH congestion detection mechanism per cell.

**Note, There can be more than one dedicated BWP configured per cell. GBR PDSCH congestion detection shall also be performed for power saving BWP similar to dedicated BWP.**

ELSE

* per cell.
* Input:
* GBR PDSCH congestion detection per cell or per dedicated BWP requires the following inputs:
* per cell, periodic average PDSCH GBR PRB load avgGbrPrbLoad\_n(T) (refers to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8168\_replaced ([11183175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvIfqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and periodic average PDSCH DC-GBR PRB load avgDcGbrPrbLoad\_n(t) (refer ro 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30857\_replaced ([11183283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvJGvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Rationale: Monitoring and counting of DC-GBR PDSCH usages shall be added into the GBR PDSCH usages as total PDSCH GBR load. Note- DC-GBR load monitoring is applied only to largest dedicated BWP.

-  per dedicated BWP , periodic average PDSCH GBR PRB load avgGbrPrbLoadDL(i, T) refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8168\_replaced ([11183175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvIfqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Power saving BWP shall additionally support the PRB load monitoring and its periodic average PDSCH GBR PRB load avgGbrPrbLoadDL(i, T) shall be used for GBR congestion detection.

- T refers to end of GBR congestion detection period provided at cell setup/reconfiguration

(NRCELL.congDetectPeriod). Any change in value provided is immediately taken into use at end of current congestion detection interval.

* PDSCH GBR congestion limit: Marks a certain maximum percentage of PDSCH resources to be utilized for GBR as defined by O&M parameter NRCELL.maxGbrTrafficLimit.

- PDSCH GBR congestion hysteresis factor: R&D parameter rdDlsGbrPrbCongHyst, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31563\_replaced([11193646](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvuVi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* PDSCH GBR load hysteresis limit: As product of the PDSCH GBR limit and the PDSCH GBR congestion hysteresis factor.

**Note that when the PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell ([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) GBR congestion limit takes the value of maxGBRTrafficLimit\_L2PS ([12704521](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_oy0TEGCREfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).**

* Congestion Detection:
* If any following conditions are fulfilled, the GBR PDSCH congestion is detected, otherwise congestion is not considered detected:
* GBR PDSCH congestion is detected per Cell / Per Dedicated BWP , if the periodic average PDSCH GBR load exceeds the GBR Congestion limit ( I.e. , NRCELL.maxGBRTrafficLimit). Note: If GBR PDSCH Congestion detection is performed per BWP, GBR PDSCH congestion control shall also performed per dedicated BWP(including PS-BWP). Because it is possible that some BWP is congested but still while other is not congested or have been resolved.

As soon as GBR PDSCH congestion is detected on cell or any dedicated BWP in the cell, the congestion deprioritize handling shall be performed according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31321\_replaced ([11183615](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). GBR congestion control shall not be applicable to [CB014699-D] critical 5QI-1/65/66 & [End CB014699-D] 5QI1/65/66**and DC-GBR.**

* Congestion Resolved:
* If following conditions are fulfilled, the GBR PDSCH congestion is resolved, otherwise congestion is not considered resolved:
* Largest dedicated BWP periodic average PDSCH GBR PRB load avgGbrPrbLoad(T) drops below the PDSCH GBR load hysteresis limit, Otherwise congestion is not considered to be resolved. The rationale of deprioritize GBR load to the load hysteresis limit is to avoid ping-ping congestion happen.

When GBR congestion is resolved, re-prioritize GBR bearers to normal GBR scheduling according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31323\_replaced ([11183626](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB014699-D] When GBR congestion is resolved, re-prioritize critical GBR bearers (If deprioritized due to congestion) to GBR scheduling according to ([11183626](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB014699-D].

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11183615`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
DL GBR bearer/UE de-prioritization in congestion

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13396627`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
When GBR PDSCH congestion is detected on a cell or any dedicated BWP in the cell, gNB-DU shall deprioritize GBR bearer(s) [CB014699-D] and deprioritize critical GBR bearer(s) [End CB014699-D] or UE(s) as following:

* The DL GBR category bearers/UEs are selected to de-prioritize from Cell or each dedicated BWP in the cell which are detected congestion (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8168\_replaced ([11183175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvIfqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), as the GBR congestion control mechanism is performed per dedicated BWP, it is possible that congestion might be detected in one BWP but not in the other and so on. If GBR congestion is detected in multiple BWPs (including PS-BWP), GBR bearer selection for de-prioritization shall be performed starting from smallest to the largest BWP so that the predicted GBR load reduction resulting from bearer de-prioritization in smaller BWP can already be considered in the larger BWP as the smaller BWPs are always nested within the larger BWP.
* As many GBR bearers [CB014699-D] and critical GBR bearers [End CB014699-D] shall be selected such that sum of the GBR PDSCH resource utilization of the selected bearers (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31329\_replaced([11183635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is larger than or equal to the excess of the PDSCH GBR load hysteresis limit per Cell or dedicated BWP in the cell. The rationale of deprioritize GBR load to the load hysteresis limit is to avoid ping-ping congestion happen. GBR congestion control shall not be applicable to [CB014699-D] critical 5QI-1/65/66 & [End CB014699-D] 5QI1/65/66 and DC-GBR.
* The DL GBR category bearers/UEs deprioritize selection are determined by following criteria:

Step1: From higher to lower congestion priority value based on the NRCELL.congWeightAlg selection:

* If the NRCELL.congWeightAlg = ' priorityLevel ', the GBR bearer congestion priority is based on the NRBTS/NRDRB.priorityLevel configured value for the bearer, the lower value indicates higher congestion priority.
* If the NRCELL.congWeightAlg = ' arpPrioPerVul ', the bearer congestion priority is based on the ARP priority configured for the bearers (refer to 5G\_CP\_IF\_NTW\_1869\_replaced ([11355256](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__A7oaPvmEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) & 5G\_CP\_IF\_NTW\_8457\_replaced ([11355289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__A7ocPvmEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The deprioritize start from the bearers with ARP vulnerability "preemptable" and sorted by the ARP priority, then from the bearers with ARP vulnerability "not preemptable" and sorted by the ARP priority.
* The UE's congestion priority value is equal to the highest congestion priority value among all GBR bearers within the UE.
* Step2: Then from higher to lower PDSCH GBR resource utilization of a bearer for PDSCH Congestion Control (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31329\_replaced ([11183635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) OR higher to lower PDCCH GBR resource utilization of a UE for PDCCH Congestion Control (See artifact to [12495956](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_M5bgsBQ6EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* Step3: Then in random order.

This selection only needs be performed for congested cell or dedicated BWP(s) in the cell. When GBR PDSCH/PDCCH congestion is detected or when there are any load level changes following this detection, additional bearers/UEs need to be de-prioritized or some de-prioritized bearers/UEs need to be removed from the de-prioritized list. Priority level of bearers/UEs de-prioritization shall be considered within its active BWP which is going under congestion control.

GBR bearers [CB014699-D] and critical GBR bearers [End CB014699-D] (having GBR positive token bucket) have been selected to be de-prioritized shall be handled in DL scheduling as follows until congestion is resolved:

* Delay based prioritization values (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8024\_replaced ([11175486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kujQ1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are no longer applied for the deprioritized GBR [CB014699-D] and deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-D] bearers due to GBR congestion; instead, a priority value and fix schedule weight: rdCongDepriorGbrSchedulWeight  (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31697\_replaced ([11193461](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of standardized 5QIs with resource type: Normal non-GBR shall be used. It is to handle in "drbTraffic" request class (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced ([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as same as normal non-GBR but using specific scheduling weight. [CB014699-D] When a critical GBR bearer (having positive token bucket) is de-prioritized, it is handled as critical non-GBR traffic and moved to the criticalDrbTraffic request class (refer to [13393326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_lHKq4wZMEfGozuGzRUXHpg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and using the fix schedule weight rdCongDepriorCriticalGbrSchedulWeight ([13409680](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ejv5aBCNEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB014699-D].

The de-prioritization shall be performed on bearer(s) basis for PDSCH congestion and be performed on UE basis in case of PDCCH congestion.

If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8240\_replaced ([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the DL GBR UE(s)/bearer(s) considered here for GBR de-prioritization [CB014699-D] and critical GBR de-prioritization [End CB014699-D] shall only be among UEs with active BWP same as BWP in which congestion is detected.

UE is not considered for de-prioritization, if the UE is in the "inactive BWP" like during the DL BWP scanning procedure (see [12682751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_7Vn41VGcEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11183626`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
PDSCH GBR congestion reprioritization

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13407518`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB shall select as many deprioritized GBR bearer(s) [CB014699-D] and deprioritized critical GBR bearer(s) [End CB014699-D] or UE(s) as possible to back to GBR scheduling, when GBR PDSCH congestion (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31297\_replaced ([11183601](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is resolved with following conditions met:

* For a cell or each dedicated BWP in the cell, the average PDSCH GBR PRB load plus estimated load of reprioritize bearer(s) (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31329\_replaced([11183635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which belongs to them would not exceed PDSCH GBR congestion limit.
* After reprioritization, the gNB shall update the average PDSCH GBR PRB load of a cell or each dedicated BWP in the cell with the estimated load of the reprioritized bearer(s).

The priority of bearers to be reprioritized with following steps :

Step1: The congestion priority value based on the NRCELL.congWeightAlg selection (same as de-prioritization step1 defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31321\_replaced([11183615](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Step2:  If value is same in step 1, estimated PDSCH PRB load per GBR bearer (5G\_UP\_ALG\_31329\_replaced([11183635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in increasing order.

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11183635`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
Estimation PDSCH PRB load per GBR bearer for deprioritization and reprioritization

---

**3.29.7.2.0-21.0-2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13396644`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
gNB-DU shall estimate PDSCH PRB load per GBR bearer that used for PDSCH congestion de-prioritization and re-prioritization on FR1 TDD and FDD cell.

Activation: GBR feature and PDSCH congestion control is activated (NRBTS/NRCELL.actNonVoNRGBRService=true & NRBTS/NRCELL.gbrPdschCongHandling = TRUE)

Metrics:

* Scope: per GBR bearer
* Units: PRB
* The instantaneous GBR UE DL spectrum efficency calculated at every slot t:

InstUeDlSE (i,t)  = TbsDl(i,t) / PrbDl(i,t),

where:

i: index of the UE with an GBR bearer/[CB014699-D] critical GBR bearer [End CB014699-D].

t: slot of UE is scheduled for PDSCH.

PrbDl(i,t): The overall number of PRBs used for UE i in slot t for all bearers

TbsDl(i,t): DL TBS of UE i at slot t which is equal to the sum of the TBS of the transmitted codeword(s)

If the UE is not scheduled in a slot with DL transmission, the calculation shall not update.

The average GBR UE DL spectrum efficency is then calculated via an exponential filter on slot basis.

avgUeDlSE(i,t) = (1/Tprb) \* InstUeDlSE (i,t) + (1−(1/Tprb)) \* avgUeDlSE(i, t−1),

where

i: index of the UE with an GBR bearer/[CB014699-D] critical GBR bearer [End CB014699-D].

t: slot of UE is scheduled for PDSCH.

Tprb:  R&D parameter constant for the averaging function, with the following suggested properties:

- Range= 40...400; Step size= 10; Default= 200

The PDSCH PRB load per GBR bearer j is calculated when PDSCH GBR congestion is detected or resolved (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31297\_replaced([11183601](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- GbrDlPrbLoad(i,k)= GFBR Dl(i,k) /(avgUeDlSE(i,T) \* normalization\_factor) , where:

i: index of the UE with an GBR bearer/ [CB014699-D] critical GBR bearer [End CB014699-D].

k: index of the GBR bearer belong to UE i.

T: slot the congestion is detected or resolved.

GFBR Dl(i,k): derive from GFBR value received from AMF or source cell (in case of Handover) of GBR bearer k for UE i .

**Note: Refer to the C-Plane requirement(5G\_CP\_OTH\_17416\_replaced([11598203](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y3Aj0_wOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the GFBR**

avgUeDlSE(i, T):  The latest value of avgUeDlSE(t) when congestion is detected or resolved.

normalization\_factor: Normalization based on the total number of DL slot per second (see 5G\_UP\_54\_replaced([11346921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIls_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TDD and 5G\_UP\_77\_replaced([11347090](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz0_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for DSS).

Exceptions:

* For gbrPdschCongHandling  = FALSE or GBR congestion detection period NRCELL.congDetectPeriod = 0, gNB  no need to perform this GBR Load calculation.

**Note: The PDSCH PRB load calculation needs only be performed when GBR PDSCH congestion is detected/resolved OR when any cell GBR load changes that trigger additional UEs need to be de-prioritized/re-prioritized. It is left to entity level/implementation to define which exact procedure has the least complexity (e.g., triggered search, managed sorted lists, etc).**

---

#### 3.29.7.2.0-22 DL resource assignment using FDM FD resource availability check  for retransmission (ID: 11183646)

**3.29.7.2.0-22.0-2**  (ID: `11183691`)

DL resource assignment using FDM
11183691:
DL resource assignment using FDM
For FDD cell shared with LTE and actLteNrDssLteCatM equals true, the  RBGs [CB008927-Q]/ PRBs [End CB008927-Q]may contain rate matched areas therefore retransmisions can have a different number of REs than initial transmision depending on the allocated area.
 If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled, L2PS shall calculate number of available REs for given allocation according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2967\_replaced([11124515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):
[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of text. The text appears to be a mathematical or scientific notation. The visible characters are "N", "ReT", and "RF". The "N" is larger and appears to be a subscript. The "ReT" is above the "RF", and the "RF" is below the "ReT". The expression is: $N\_{PRB} \* N\_{RE} - N\_{RE}^{PTRS} - N\_{RE}^{TRS}$. The characters are black and appear to be on a white background. It shows the expression $N\_{RE}^{CRS} - N\_{RE}^{CSI-IM} -$. There is also a trailing minus sign at the end of the expression.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14089.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N RateMatched RE". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters "N" and "RE" are smaller and positioned below and to the left of "RateMatched".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14089.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
and compare this value with number of RE from the initial allocation (calculated using the same formula)
if the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled and if [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is: $N\_{RE}^{ReTx} < N\_{RE}^{Initial} \* rdDiRetransMinFraction$. The text is in a dark color against a light background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14089.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]  the retransmission is blocked in this slot. If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, the retransmission follows the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11953\_replaced([11184310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRplPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

### 3.29.7.3 DL resource assignment for 5G UEs with carrier aggregation

#### 3.29.7.3.0-1 DL resource assignment for 5G UEs with carrier aggregation PUCCH token check during PDSCH scheduling (ID: 11183702)

**3.29.7.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11183717`)

DL resource assignment for 5G UEs with carrier aggregation
MSG2 can always be scheduled irrespective the cell has free PUCCH token or not.

FD scheduler shall make sure all scheduled UEs have PUCCH token or can get PUCCH token at the cell that report PUCCH. If short PUCCH is configured and UE has no PUCCH token and the cell that UE report PUCCH has no free token available, the UE can't be scheduled. [PR777253] In FR2, the beam constraint on the PUCCH token shall also be checked as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29065\_replaced([11177731](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuxTSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End PR777253]

---

### 3.29.7.4 Slot blanking calculation due to RF high temperature

### 3.29.7.4-1 Slot blanking calculation due to RF high temperature

**3.29.7.4-1.0-2**  (ID: `13317557`)

Slot blanking calculation due to RF high temperature
If [Before CB014044-A-UPD004] NRBTS/NRCELL.actBestNList [CB014044-A-UPD004] NRBTS/NRCELL.bestNK.actBestNList [End CB014044-A-UPD004] is set to DL or DL\_UL,

Post scheduling, follow below algorithm to create Best NK list; If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True, if the current slot is the end of virtual slot in slice volume control adaptations (see [11188771: Slice Volume Control / Enhanced Slice Volume Control per Virtual Slot](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)), then the creation of best NK list shall be done after the execution of slice volume control to have the latest rrmRGWeight which shall be applicable for the next slot.

Best N-K List

* Best NK List only contains Non-GBR initial transmission UEs with few high metric N  UEs and few high buffer K UEs, selected from CS1 list.
* BestNKListSize (DL)=[Before CB014044-A-UPD004]dlBestNListRatio [CB014044-A-UPD004] NRBTS/NRCELL.bestNK.dlBestNListRatio [End CB014044-A-UPD004] \* averageCs1ListMaxSize (gNB-Du shall estimate the maximum possible CS1 size for the next scheduling slot. For the avgCs1Size please see  [11212749: Control based on run-time measurements](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-n5cPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA))
* Best NK List contains N UEs with high TD metrics and K UEs with highest remaining bytes in buffer selected from CS1 list.
* [CB014044-A-UPD004] Activating BestKList (DL): NRBTS/NRCELL.bestNK.actBestKList shall set to DL or DL\_UL. [End CB014044-A-UPD004]
* BestNKListRatio (DL): [Before CB014044-A-UPD004] NRBTS/NRCELL.dlBestNListRatio [CB014044-A-UPD004] NRBTS/NRCELL.bestNK.dlBestNListRatio [End CB014044-A-UPD004]
* BestKRatio (DL) [Before CB014044-A-UPD004] is an RnD param, rdDlHighBuffBestKRatio (see [12532524](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_bfiC4BuXEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), [CB014044-A-UPD004] NRBTS/NRCELL.bestNK.dlBestKListRatio [End CB014044-A-UPD004] which is used to calculate the number of high buffer K UEs in Best NK List.

Post scheduling, update TD metrics of UEs which are picked for scheduling in this slot and still with data remaining in the buffer.

Step 1: Select K non-GBR UEs of the largest BWP and with the highest remaining buffer bytes from the CS1 list, and add these UEs to the Best NK List. K includes non-GBR UEs whose priorities are equal to  drbTraffic.

Note: If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True, bufferedBytes in non-GBR DRBs **and deprioritized GBR/DC-GBR**DRBs **of a resource group (RG) with 0 RG weight (rrmRGWeight = 0, see** [11188798: Slice/RG weights](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall be excluded in the remaining bytes calculation.\*\*\*\*

Step 2: Select N = BestNKListSize(DL) - K non-GBR UEs with the highest TD metric from the CS1 list, and add them to the Best NK List after sorting by TD metric in descending order. UEs that are no longer eligible, i.e., fully drained with no remaining data in the buffer, shall not be added to the Best NK List. N includes drbTraffic (see [12550648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Q5B2YyQrEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Q5B2YyQrEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9"))

Same UE shall not be picked in above step 1 and 2.

This updated list becomes the new Best NK List for the next slot.

---

#### 3.29.7.4.0-1 Slot blanking calculation due to RF high temperature achievedMutingRatio calculation (ID: 11183727)

**3.29.7.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11183742`)

Slot blanking calculation due to RF high temperature
11183742:
Slot blanking calculation due to RF high temperature
[image-0][image\_desc]achievedMutingRatio[j] =[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14097.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]  
[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is enclosed in parentheses and consists of a fraction subtracted from 1, multiplied by another term. The fraction has 1 in the numerator and "rdMutingRatioFilterConst" in the denominator. The term outside the parentheses is "achievedMutingRatio[i-1]". The multiplication is indicated by a cross symbol.
The expression can be written as:
$(1 - \frac{1}{\text{rdMutingRatioFilterConst}}) \times \text{achievedMutingRatio[i-1]}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14097.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]  
[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is:
+ 1 / rdMutingRatioFilterConst × SlotmutingOutcome[i-1][/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14097.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

With:

i                  index of DL/special slot

where,

rdMutingRatioFilterConst refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18290\_replaced([11193586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtufvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

achievedMutingRatio shall be initialized to tgtMutingRatio refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18285\_replaced([11175107](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0kvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

SlotmutingOutcome refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20825\_replaced([11183747](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMxGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: achievedMutingRatio shall reset to the new tgtMutingRatio if tgtMutingRatio is changed (the final tgtMutingRatio shall be always applied based on the latest values which sent from CPRT to L2-PS).

---

#### 3.29.7.4.0-2 Slot blanking calculation due to RF high temperature SlotmutingOutcome calculation (ID: 11183747)

**3.29.7.4.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13340066`)

Slot blanking calculation due to RF high temperature
SlotmutingOutcome[i] = nSym\_not\_used(i) / nSym\_total(i) \* normalization\_factor

With:

i                                 index of DL/special slot

nSym\_not\_used           number of not scheduled DL symbols per DL/special slot

nSym\_total                  total DL symbols per DL/special slot

normalization\_factor     Normalization based on the total number of symbols for DL slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2313\_replaced([11114697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). **normalization\_factor is not applicable for FDD case.**

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE [CB007372-M-UPD010][CB007372-M] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M][End CB007372-M-UPD010], symbols applicable for NR DL transmissions (see [11171912](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are counted for the calculation.  
[CB007372-M-UPD010][CB007372-M] On an FDD carrier shared with LTE and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true all symbols are applicable for NR DL transmissions (see [11171912](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB007372-M][End CB007372-M-UPD010]

---

## 3.29.8 Artificial load generation in the DL slots

### 3.29.8.1 Legacy

#### 3.29.8.1.0-1 Legacy Artificial load generation Overview of Legacy DL artificial load generation (ID: 11183777)

**3.29.8.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13462177`)

*Legacy* Artificial load generation
L2PS shall generate artificial load in the DL direction as per the following:

If legacy artificial load is enable (when NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is configured and set to '100'):

- Only PDSCH channel is affected by the artificial load.

- NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is any arbitrary value between 0-100 by step 1.(0 means not configured)

- After FD scheduling, DL scheduling needs to generate the load based on the rules which is described in artifact [11183791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvNYI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- Artificial load measurement settings are described in artifact [11183823](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvNYKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- DL FD scheduler allocates any remaining resources after actual UEs allocation to the artificial load in the limit of EIRP control and BB pooling.(see [11183858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvN_MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- Beams selection is described in artifact [11183891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvN_OfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

**Notes:**

* In case of ABIL board, gNB-DU shall handle the interworking when RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and when NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is not set to 0

**- When NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is expected not to be configured or to be set to 0 if it is not an ABIL board**.\*\*\*\*

[CB013712-A] Not valid for NewFR1 [End CB013712-A]

---

#### 3.29.8.1.0-2 Legacy Artificial load generation DL Scheduling for the artificial load generation (ID: 11183791)

**3.29.8.1.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `13376980`)

*Legacy* Artificial load generation
Following preparations are not valid when NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is non-zero value**or artificial load for EMF is enabled (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17426\_replaced([11183931](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**.\*\*\*\*

If artificial PDSCH load needs to be generated in a given cell (5G\_L2\_1813\_replaced([11212150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-joA_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) (when NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is configured), after FD scheduling, DL scheduling needs to generate the load based on the following rules:

**1. Analog beamforming**

a) If there is no UE scheduled in any cell of the cell group in given slot, artificial load needs to be scheduled. Beam is selected according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1898\_replaced([11183891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvN_OfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

b) If there is no UE scheduled in given cell, but one or more UEs are scheduled on other cells of the cell group, and the beam for these UEs is among the beams configured for artificial load, artificial load needs to be scheduled with the same beam as the UE(s).

c) if neither a) nor b) is satisfied, no artificial load is generated.

**2. Digital beamforming**

If cell is in RF high temperature (blanking indicator due to RF high temperature (see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_13501\_replaced([11611355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5LuEvwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) received), no artificial load needs to be generated.

Else

a) If there is no UE is scheduled in the slot, artificial load needs to be scheduled. Beam is selected according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1898\_replaced([11183891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvN_OfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

b) If one or more UEs are scheduled in the slot and an L1 DL subcell is empty, artificial load needs to be co-scheduled in frequency domain on this empty subcell.

c) If one or more UEs are scheduled in the slot and no L1 DL subcell is empty, no artificial load needs to be generated.

**[CB015174-B]**

**3. FDD non-beamforming**

If cell is in RF high temperature (MIMO muting with hopping is received according to RF thermal management indicator ([13333900](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GgklQNaaEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) from CPRT), no artificial load needs to be generated.

**[End CB015174-B]**

No HARQ logical channel handling and no retransmissions are needed for the artificial load. Only PDSCH channel is affected by the artificial load; no other channels or physical signals are needed.

**Note: NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is any arbitary value between 0-100 by step 1. (0 means not configured)**

[CB013712-A] Not valid for NewFR1 [End CB013712-A]

---

#### 3.29.8.1.0-3 Legacy Artificial load generation Artificial load measurement (ID: 11183823)

**3.29.8.1.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `11183852`)

*Legacy* Artificial load generation
Following measurement settings are not valid when NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is non-zero value (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17659\_replaced([11183961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB010944-A] or artificial load for EMF is enabled[End CB010944-A].

When NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is configured, artificial load may be required in a cell as described in 5G\_L2\_1813\_replaced([11212150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-joA_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). There are some limitations in 5G18A:

1. For analog beamforming, only one beam is allowed for one cell group at a time.
2. No partial PRB allocation is allowed in 5G18A.

Accordingly, the total load (normal or artificial) condition on the beams as given by artificialLoadInfo may not be satisfied:

1. For digital beamforming,  if no L1 DL subcell is empty after scheduling normal UEs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4292\_replaced([11177281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), no artificial load is generated.
2. For analog beamforming, if normal UEs are scheduled in the same cell group, the UE may use a different beam than configured in artificialLoadInfo.

If cell is in RF high temperature (blanking indicator due to RF high temperature (see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_13501\_replaced([11611355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5LuEvwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) received), no artificial load needs to be generated.

A PM counter is designed to measure this situation.

Name: DL\_SLOT\_DUMMY\_PDSCH

Description: The number of slots where artificial PDSCH load is scheduled.

Together with beamforming counters DL\_SERV\_BEAM\_ID\_BEAM\_00...DL\_SERV\_BEAM\_ID\_BEAM\_63 (which are not affected by the artificial load) it can be used to check how many slots had PDSCH load (normal or artificial) on the beam(s) configured in artificialLoadInfo.

---

#### 3.29.8.1.0-4 Legacy Artificial load generation Number of PRBs for artificial PDSCH load (ID: 11183858)

**3.29.8.1.0-4.0-9**  (ID: `13462180`)

*Legacy* Artificial load generation
For the artificial PDSCH load (when NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is configured) (5G\_L2\_1813\_replaced([11212150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-joA_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), DL FD scheduler allocates resources according to the NRCELL.targetTotalLoad after allocating resources to actual UEs, in the limit of the available PRBs for the given beam due to EIRP restriction (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4349\_replaced([11177928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

When NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is set to non zero value, to generate artificial load in the cell in downlink direction for PDSCH only, The operator can configure  targetTotalLoad  to define the desired load on the cell.

Assuming N as total number of DL PRBs  as per DL BW.

ConfiguredLoadInPRB =Roundup ((NRCELL.targetTotalLoad \* N) / 100)

ConfiguredLoadInPRB: Calculated from NRCELL.targetTotalLoad and DL BW. It gives the number of PRBs to achieve the configured desired load.

If RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, since RAT0 allocation is used, the PRB scheduling will be based on RBG. So round-up to higher RBG size is required for PRBs which are used for dummy load. Otherwise, if RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, and {if NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to false, or (if NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true,  and if there is only one PDSCH sub area in the slot)} the PRB scheduling will be based on PRBs within this (or these) PDSCH sub-area(s). When RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true and if NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true, if there are several PDSCH sub area in the slot, then gNB-DU shall select the fake UE as a candidate for dynamicswitch RAT0 provided the conditions in artifact [11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) are fulfilled. Note: gNB-DU selects the fake UE s dynamicswitch RAT0 only if no real UE is selected with a dynamicswitch RAT0 allocation for initial transmission or retransmission if RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, if NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true and when NRCELL.targetTotalLoad is set to non zero value.

In slots where UEs scheduled with power boost for HARQ retransmissions (as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24934\_replaced([11185387](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3sfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then the PRBs reserved for power boost shall be excluded from artificial PDSCH load generation.

In case of PRB pooling enabled(that is, slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' , or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed and actBbPoolExtensionTddFr1 are set to 'true' ), or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE )), the available PRBs, If RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, within PDSCH sub-areas for artificial load shall also consider the available PRBs used in the TTI , as it is described in the conditions in artifacts [11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (if the fake UE (that is from artificial load)  is RAT1 PRBs allocation or a dynamicswitch RAT0  RBGs allocation) (refer more to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6839\_replaced([11213362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxG1mvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ) when slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' , or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed and actBbPoolExtensionTddFr1 are set to 'true'; refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27908\_replaced([11213385](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxG1nvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in case slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE).

In case of PRB pooling is enabled(i.e., slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' **, or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed and actBbPoolExtensionTddFr1 are set to 'true', or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE),  and RAT0 is used (NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false), the available PRBs for artificial load shall also consider the available PRBs used in the TTI (refer more to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6839\_replaced([11213362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxG1mvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true'** , or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed and actBbPoolExtensionTddFr1 are set to 'true'; refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27908\_replaced([11213385](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxG1nvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in case slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE).\*\*\*\*

If RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, or {if RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to true, if NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true and there are several PDSCH sub areas  in the slot and if the fake UE (that is from artificial load) is a dynamicswitch RAT0  RBGs allocation (that is the conditions are fullfilled in artifacts [11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))}, the PRBs belonging to a RBG which is not fully available(for example, due to PRB blanking and/or due to PRB pooling) shall be excluded from artificial load. If the left PRBs after allocation is still greater than or equal to last RBG and full last RBG is still available and not used, then the last RBG shall be used for artificial load. This is also valid when layer PRB pooling is enabled (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE).

When (NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize is set to 'true') and PRB pooling enabled(actSlowPrbPooling is set to 'true' **, or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed and actBbPoolExtensionTddFr1 are set to 'true'** ), if **RAT-1** NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, PRBs **within PDSCH sub-areas have been used for artificial load in the respect of BBRM limit,** (that is the conditions are fullfilled in artifacts [11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).***\****\*

If **RAT-1** NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,**or {if RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to true, if NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true and there are several PDSCH sub areas  in the slot and if the fake UE (that is from artificial load) is a dynamicswitch RAT0  RBGs allocation (that is the conditions are fullfilled in artifacts [11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))} , full RBG have been used for artificial load in the respect of BBRM limit, if this BBRM limit still allows non-full RBG, then the first partial RBG or the last partial RBG can be used.** Even more the first and last partial RBG can be used together.\*\*\*\*

Artificial load generated with layer PRB pooling enabled (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE) shall also ensure that the layer PRB based constraints mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26597\_replaced([11214606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are always respected. Note: this statement is valid too if RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true.

The number of layer PRBs required for a certain FD allocation can be computed using the function numReqLayerPrb defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).This is also valid for PRB based RAT1 allocation scheme (i.e. NRCELL.actRat1Alloc=True)

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed and actBbPoolExtensionTddFr1 are set to 'true' and **actTddCsiRsOpt = false (note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator)**, in case the cell does not have ZAB privilege, L2PS shall not schedule PDSCH in the slots that might be allocated to CSI-RS for TRS/CSI-RS for Channel Acquisition in other CsiRsPooling Groups, and thus artificial load shall not be generated in these slots. List of such slots can be obtained from 5G\_UP\_ALG**\_11299.**

When NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to non zero value or artificial load for EMF is enabled, the number of PRBs for artificial load is determined by configuredLoadInPRB as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17661\_replaced([11183983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In the slot with PRB muting by IM(refer to [12707684: SSB Interference Mitigation for non-BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Vz03IGLOEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), the muted PRBs(refer to nbPrbsMutedIMFinal in [12709089: Basic functionality](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_pAtj4GOsEfCtwYrh0KQ_DA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) shall be excluded from artificial load generation.

[CB013712-A] Not valid for NewFR1 [End CB013712-A]

---

#### 3.29.8.1.0-5 Legacy Artificial load generation Beam control for artificial PDSCH load (ID: 11183891)

**3.29.8.1.0-5.0-4**  (ID: `13462181`)

*Legacy* Artificial load generation
For TDD BF: Beam selection for the artificial PDSCH load is controlled by NRBTS.NRCELLGRP.beamIndexListForArtificialLoad and NRBTS.NRCELL.nrBtsBeamRefinementP2.

First, a list of candidate beams shall be constructed according to the following rules:

* The list of candidate beams is initialized as empty list.
* If beamIndexForArtificialLoadList is non-empty, all beamId values from beamIndexForArtificialLoadList are added to the candidate beams. Note: These beamIds correspond to SSB beams.
* If nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 == true, SSB beamIds from the candidate list are replaced by beamIds of refined beams that correspond to the SSB beamIds. Note: The relationship between SSB beams and refined beams is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1934\_replaced([11111723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpP6G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1939\_replaced([11111782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpQhIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11982\_replaced([11111700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpP6FfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1938\_replaced([11111762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpP6IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Second, beam(s) shall be selected from the candidate list according to the following rules:

* If dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2, if nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 = true, one beam shall be randomly selected from the candidate list;
* elseif dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 OR dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4, if nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 = true, two beams shall be randomly selected from the candidate list.
* If there is not enough beamIDs in the candidate list,
* if nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 == false, the remaining beams shall be randomly selected from all remaining SSB beams configured to the cell;
* elseif nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 == true, the remaining beams shall be randomly selected from all remaining refined beams configured to the cell.

For FDD BF:

if dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_32x4, the beams shall be randomly selected from all PMI beams of PDSCH rank1/2 based on 32 ports CSI-RS configured to the cell, refers to [11112756: PatternIDs for PMI beams](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZECvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

If no beamforming mode, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2183\_replaced([11112736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZEBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_907\_replaced([11353384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub-6UvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or **supercell mode, see 5G\_UP\_5471\_replaced([11353418](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub_hVfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the beam index in *DlData\_PatternConfigReq is not relevant for CPRI deployment and for eCPRI deployment patternId is set to 0.***

[CB013712-A] Not valid for NewFR1 [End CB013712-A]

---

#### 3.29.8.1.0-6 Legacy Artificial load generation Rank and precoder selection for legacy artificial PDSCH load (ID: 11183902)

**3.29.8.1.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `13462182`)

*Legacy* Artificial load generation
For the artificial PDSCH load (see 5G\_L2\_1813\_replaced([11212150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-joA_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), rank is selected based on parameter rdArtificialLoadRank (5G\_L2\_1307\_replaced([11189518](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28Qh8PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or rdArtificialLoadRankNonBf (5G\_L2\_1308\_replaced([11189533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28Qh8_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If rdArtificialLoadRankNonBf = 5, this means maximum possible rank is to be used. The maximum rank can be determined as follows:

* If dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2, then maximum rank is 2
* If dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4, then maximum rank is 4

If non-BF mode is used (5G\_UP\_908\_replaced([11353388](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub_hUPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): The precoding matrix is to be set in 5G\_L2\_1816\_replaced([11212192](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-joCvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else:

The precoding matrix selection is controlled according to the following rules:

* In case of open loop MIMO (dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4), the default behaviour from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3178\_replaced([11139281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3183\_replaced([11139297](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is followed.
