* In case of closed loop MIMO (dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_32x4), because PMI report is not available, the selection is hardcoded as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3196\_replaced([11184070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvP0Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB013712-A] Not valid for NewFR1 [End CB013712-A]

---

### 3.29.8.2 Configurable artificial load generation

### 3.29.8.2-1 Configurable artificial load generation

#### 3.29.8.2-1.0-3 Configurable artificial load generation For the artificial PDSCH load, the mcsIndexForArtificialLoad in artificialLoadInfo defines the MCS index used. Since 256QAM is not supported for the artificial load, Table 5.1.3.1-1 defined in 38.214 is always used. [Before PR783731]In case L1 does not support given MCS, the standard procedure from 5G_L2_642_replaced( 11164067 ) is used.[End PR783731] When NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to non zero value [CB010944-A] or artificial load for EMF is enabled[End CB010944-A], mcsIndexForArtificialLoad shall be used for fake UE. (ID: 11184067)

### 3.29.8.2-2 Configurable artificial load generation

**3.29.8.2-2.0-3**  (ID: `12981227`)

Configurable artificial load generation
For artificial PDSCH load, the following Precoding & PTRS parameters are used:

DL PTRS disabled for FR1 TDD and FDD and enabled for TDD FR2.

* spatialMode:
* If non-BF mode is used (5G\_UP\_908\_replaced([11353388](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub_hUPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): open loop spatial multiplexing
* Else:

align with configuration of the cell

* PdschPrecodingConfig

If non-BF mode is used: openLoopScheme=NoPrecoding

Else:

* [CB008577-D] If dlMimoMode is ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_32x4: precoding shall follow the description as UEs with 32ports CSI-RS configuration [11139234: PDSCH precoding and spatial mode settings for different transmission schemes](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krlca_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). Hardcoded precoding according to rank is designed by CP3. [End CB008577-D]
* codebookIndex (relevant only for dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4 ~~[CB008577-D] OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_32x4: [End CB008577-D]~~):  the codebook index shall be fixed to 0
* i1Codebook4AntPorts (relevant only for dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 **~~OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4~~** ): all entries fixed to 0\*\*\*\*
* i2Codebook4AntPorts (relevant only for dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 **OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4 ~~[CB008577-D] OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_32x4: [End CB008577-D]~~): fixed to 0**
* pdschPrecodingOption4x4 (relevant only for dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 OR dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4):
* if actBeamforming == true AND numOfSubSectors == 0, then pdschPrecodingOption4x4 is set to rdPdschPrecodingOption4X4 + 4 \* rdCSIRSprecoding4X4;
* else pdschPrecodingOption4x4 is set to 0

---

### 3.29.8.2-3 Configurable artificial load generation

#### 3.29.8.2-3.0-1 Configurable artificial load generation Dummy load generation for field testing activation/deactivation and UE/Cell selection (ID: 11184093)

**3.29.8.2-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11184097`)

Configurable artificial load generation
gNB shall able to be configured to support dummy load function at runtime for testing need. gNB-DU shall generate DL non-GBR dummy load.

* rdEnableDummyLoadDl (5G\_L2\_12664\_replaced([11189570](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28QiAPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is specified for online modification of the functionality to activation/deactivation.After the dummy load testing in the field, the function shall be deactivated manually, or gNB reset will deactivate all the configuration.

gNB shall be able to select UEs with dummy load support at cell level, or UEs over all the cells in gNB;

* rdDummyLoadDlLcrIdAndRnti (5G\_L2\_12666\_replaced([11189582](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28QiAvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is specified to be able to select UE/all UEs and Cell/all Cells.

The dummy load shall work well for BF/MIMO testing while UEs are scheduled over single carriers, for CA testing while UEs are scheduled over multiple carriers;

In case of CA, If dummy load is enabled for selected UE/all UEs over P(S)Cell and there are/is SCell(s)/Ext-SCell(s) already activated for this UE,dummy load shall be generated over such activated SCells as well.

UE and Cell selection could be as below:

1 UE in 1 cell

or all UEs in 1 cell

or all UEs in all cells (CA case)

or 1 RNTI (i.e. potentially several UEs) in all cells, this applies of the selected RNTI in a Shared RNTI (i.e. not selectivable for CA)

but cannot select 1 UE/RNTI in 1 cell and at the same time another UE/RNTI on this cell or any another cell.

* rdDummyLoadDlBsr (5G\_L2\_12668\_replaced([11189594](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28QiBPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is specified to ensure that artificially generated DL MAC throughput is always above a minimum configurable threshold.

This feature maximizes user’s DL MAC throughput artificially by ensuring its cell-specific buffer status is always above a configurable minimum value . It supplements buffer status handling described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). UE(s) and cell(s) are selectable.

Rational:

As per Legacy communication through LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd (5G\_L2\_IF\_874\_replaced([10984647](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2WBxx_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) message, 5G-L2-LO shall report to 5G-L2-PS the amount of data awaiting for transmission per radio bearer/ logical channel as the sum of data in RLC control and data queue. If the UE is selected for dummy load, then gNB tries to maintain the buffer size in L2Lo per Brear (bufferedBytes) as maximum TBS so for the UE with dummy load, bufferedBytes shall not be decreased after each DL scheduling. (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10555\_replaced([11185711](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note:

With the solution UE shall be selected before dummy load, Once the call released, need to configure the UE again in case the dummy load is only applied to selected UE but not for all UE.

---

#### 3.29.8.2-3.0-2 Configurable artificial load generation Share UE RNTI selected for dummy load from P(S)Cell to SCell(s) or ext-SCell(s) (ID: 11184105)

**3.29.8.2-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11184109`)

Configurable artificial load generation
If UE/All UEs selected for dummy load and rdEnableDummyLoadDl (5G\_L2\_12664\_replaced([11189570](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28QiAPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to 'true':

* In FR1 (both intra and inter-gNB) CA, L2PS instance of SCell(s)/ext-SCell(s) know(s) the lcrId of P(S)Cell.This information shall be used in order to start/stop dummy load distribution over selected SCell(s)/ext-SCell(s) for selected UE/All UEs.
* In FR2 CA, the PCell and SCell(s) shall belong to the same L2PS Cell Group. Thus, a UE context on FR2 SCell(s) shall also be known the nrCellIdentity or its peer PCell.This information shall be used in order to start/stop dummy load distribution over selected SCell(s) for selected UE/All UEs.

For both FR1 and FR2, implementation of abovementioned is left to CP3.

Note: P(S)Cell L2PS shall make decision for SCell activation based on buffer level (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2715\_replaced([11120998](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqVfPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 3.29.8.2.0-1 Configurable artificial load generation Overview of DL configurable artificial load generation (ID: 11183915)

**3.29.8.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12583649`)

Configurable artificial load generation
L2PS shall generate artificial load in the DL direction as per the following:

If artificial load on sector beam is enabled (when NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to non-zero value)

- All DL slots are affected by the artificial load, PDSCH, PDCCH and TRS+SSB slots.

- NRCELL.targetTotalLoadSectorBeam is any arbitary value between 0-100 by step 1.

- After FD scheduling, DL scheduling needs to generate the load based on the rules which is described on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17426\_replaced([11183931](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- Artificial load measurement settings are described on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17659\_replaced([11183961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- Resource allocation is determined by configuredLoadInPRB as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17661\_replaced([11183983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- Transmission is done via sector beam as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17337\_replaced([11184003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvPNUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Note: If NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to 'PDSCH' or 'PDSCH+PDCCH' or 'PDSCH+PDCCH+TRS+SSB', then NRCELL.targetTotalLoad shall be not configured.**

If artificial load for EMF is enabled (when NRCELL.actEmfTestMode is set to true and beam candidate list from gNB-CU is not empty)

- Only PDSCH channel is affected by the artificial load.

- NRCELL.emfTargetTotalLoad is any arbitrary value between 0-100 by step 1.

[CB014534-A]- If the real UE(s) traffic ( as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16626 ([11183121](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvH4nvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not enough to reach the target load defined by parameter NRCELL.emfTargetTotalLoad, then the gNB-DU shall select a direction (A, B or C) based on the input weights configured to the parameter NRCELL.emfLoadSplit.targetLoadDirectionA, B & C and load balancing algorithm defined in [12635121](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_b50YIT-nEfCal_QUJn4xrg) [End CB014534-A]

[CB014534-E]- If the NIDD parameters NRCELL.emfBurstyArtificialLoad.onPeriod and NRCELL.emfBurstyArtificialLoad.offPeriod are configured and the value set to non-zero, artificial load shall be generated for the duration of onPeriod, while refrains from sending any artificial load in offPeriod. [End CB014534-E]

- After FD scheduling, DL scheduling needs to generate the load based on the rules which is described on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17426\_replaced([11183931](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- Artificial load measurement settings are described on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17659\_replaced([11183961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- Resource allocation is determined by configuredLoadInPRB as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17661\_replaced([11183983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- Transmission is done via beam candidate list as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17337\_replaced([11184003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvPNUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- The fake UE which generates the artificial load shall be treated as same as real UE for EIRP control.

---

#### 3.29.8.2.0-2 Configurable artificial load generation DL Scheduling for configurable artificial load generation (ID: 11183931)

**3.29.8.2.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `12583707`)

Configurable artificial load generation
After real UE scheduling is done for a slot, and  if NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to a non-zero value or artificial load for EMF is enabled, and cell is in RF high temperature (blanking indicator due to RF high temperature (see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_13501\_replaced([11611355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5LuEvwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) received [CB015174-B] or MIMO muting with hopping is received according to RF thermal management indicator ([13333900](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GgklQNaaEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) from CPRT [End CB015174-B]), no artificial load needs to be generated. Else if NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to a non-zero value or artificial load for EMF is enabled,**and cell is not in RF high temperature, L2 shall calculate the number of PRBs where artificial load needs to be generated (PRBsForArtificialLoadGeneration) as per (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17661\_replaced([11183983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If it is not possible to generate artificial load, counters shall be updated as per (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17659\_replaced([11183961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If it is possible to generate artificial load, L2 shall schedule a fake UE on the PRBsForArtificialLoadGeneration PRBs**

1. mcsIndexForArtificialLoad shall be used for fake UE. (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17728\_replaced([11184052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvPNX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

2. One extra and the reserved C-RNTI shall be used  for the purposes of artificial PDSCH load.(5G\_UP\_ALG\_3399\_replaced([11152029](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksdmJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

3. Fake UE data shall be generated on

If dlMimoMode=1x1:

1 layer and precoding W=1.(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3165\_replaced([11139212](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krlcZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Else:

If  energySavingState = reducedEnergySaving enabled and rank reduced to 1 (according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3165\_replaced([11139212](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krlcZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

1 layer and precoding W=1.

Else:

2 layers with precoder settings as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3196\_replaced([11184070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvP0Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

4. if RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true,

If  there exists a single PDSCH sub-area in a scheduling slot, a fake UE is created when there exists available PRBs in the PDSCH sub-area, and the allocation is performed in RAT1,

else if there exist multiple PDSCH sub-areas ~~exist~~ in a scheduling slot, if there are available PRBs in one PDSCH sub-area while other PDSCH sub-areas are either fully utilized or not fully utilized so that the maximum possible cell utilization can be achieved, then the following can be undertaken:

 PRBs from the PDSCH sub-area that has [CB014534-B] highest [End CB014534-B] available PRBs are allocated to a fake UE, and resource allocation is performed in RAT1

else

gNB-DU determines the list of available PRBs from different PDSCH sub-areas in a slot from the largest dedicated BWP when multiple BWPs are configured. In addition, gNB-DU shall adopt RBG size and indicate RBG with a bitmap, with each bit indicating an RBG from different PDSCH sub-areas.

gNB-DU shall also allocate RBGs indicated in the RBG bitmap to a fake UE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note1: Due to PRB blanking, it is possible that all available PRBs from different PDSCH sub-areas after conversion to RBG cannot be allocated to the fake UE. Thus, the maximum PRB utilization for the artificial PDSCH load may not be achieved.

Note2: if NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to a non-zero value or artificial load for EMF is enabled, when an artificial PDSCH load is generated for a fake UE on the slot, the [CB014534-B] start PRB randomization [End CB014534-B] is disabled for RAT1 real UEs and skipped in the slot.

Else RAT0, NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, allocation shall be used for frequency domain allocation.

5. DL DCI corresponding to PDSCH transmission for fake UE shall not be sent.

6. Fake UE data shall be generated using beam indicated by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17337\_replaced([11184003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvPNUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

7.   For DL test measurement, artificial load on sector beam shall be considered in the power accumulation calculation.

---

#### 3.29.8.2.0-3 Configurable artificial load generation Artificial load measurement on DL slots (ID: 11183961)

**3.29.8.2.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `12583749`)

Configurable artificial load generation
When NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to 'PDSCH' or 'PDSCH+PDCCH' or 'PDSCH+PDCCH+TRS+SSB', or artificial load for EMF is enabled, then artificial load may be required on DL slots in a cell.

It is possible “ConfiguredLoadInPRB” (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17661\_replaced([11183983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not fully generated for artificial load, e.g. artificial load UE is not scheduled or some PRBs are not available for PDSCH.

If cell is in RF high temperature (blanking indicator due to RF high temperature (see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_13501\_replaced([11611355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5LuEvwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) received), no artificial load needs to be generated.

The PRBs belonging to a slot which is not fully available due to PRB pooling or blanking, shall be excluded from artificial load.

When NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is not zero, artificial load shall not be generated during L2 overload control.

If artificial load for EMF is enabled and EIRP control is enabled and no PRB can be allocated to artificial load due to existing PRB allocation already restricted by EIRP control, no artificial load needs to be generated.

[CB014534-E]- If the NIDD parameters NRCELL.emfBurstyArtificialLoad.onPeriod and NRCELL.emfBurstyArtificialLoad.offPeriod are configured for bursty artificial load generation, artificial load shall not be generated for the duration of offPeriod. [End CB014534-E]

[CB014534-B] If RAT1 is enabled, and if DCI1\_0 (SIB1, Rach Msg2/4 etc.,) is allocated in the Intial BWP or in case of SSB or TRS slots, the large BWP will be split into multiple PDSCH SAs. [End CB014534-B]

PM counters are designed to measure this situation:

**Name: DL\_SLOTS\_BELOW\_TGT\_LOAD**

Description: DL slots with total load below target load.

**Name: DL\_SLOTS\_ARTIFICIAL\_LOAD**

Description: DL slots with artificial load for interference generation

---

#### 3.29.8.2.0-4 Configurable artificial load generation Number of PRBs for configurable DL artificial load generation (ID: 11183983)

**3.29.8.2.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `13462865`)

Configurable artificial load generation
When NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to non zero value, to generate artificial load in the cell in downlink direction for both PDCCH & PDSCH, The operator can configure targetTotalLoad **= NRCELL.targetTotalLoadSectorBeam to define the desired load on the cell. The transmission is done via sector beam (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17337\_replaced([11184003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvPNUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

When artificial load for EMF (Electro Magnetic Field) is enabled, to generate artificial load in the cell in downlink direction for PDSCH only, The operator can configure targetTotalLoad **= NRCELL.emfTargetTotalLoad to define the desired load on the cell. The transmission is done via beam candidates configured by gNB-CU (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17337\_replaced([11184003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvPNUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

When there is regular traffic in the cell, the level of dummy data to be transmitted is decreased by real traffic to make sure unnecessary load is not generated in DL.

If there are UEs in the cell, gNB shall decide the dummy load to be generated based on following calculations:

Assuming N as total number of DL PRBs If RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, in PDSCH sub-areas **as per DL BW.**

ConfiguredLoadInPRB =Roundup ((targetTotalLoad \* N) / 100)

PRBsForArtificialLoadGeneration = ConfiguredLoadInPRB – PRBConsumedByscheduledRealUEs [PR880973]– PRBConsumedByCommonChannels [End PR880973]

PRBsForArtificialLoadGeneration: Number of PRBs on which artificial load needs to be generated.

ConfiguredLoadInPRB: Calculated from targetTotalLoad **and DL BW. It gives the number of PRBs If RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, in PDSCH sub-areas** to achieve the configured desired load.\*\*\*\*

PRBConsumedByscheduledRealUEs: Number of PRBs If RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, in PDSCH sub-areas **consumed by real UEs as per existing scheduling algorithm + PRBConsumedByScheduledRealUEsForPowerBoost.**

[PR880973] PRBConsumedByCommonChannels: PRBs used for transmissions not associated with real UEs, such as System Information Blocks (SIBs) and Paging. [End PR880973]

If NRCELL/actDlPowerBoost is set to (dlVoNRPowerBoost/dlSrbPowerBoost/dlVoNRAndSrbPowerBoost)

PRBConsumedByScheduledRealUEsForPowerBoost: Number of PRBs **reserved for real UEs for power boost of eligible HARQ retransmissions (as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24934\_replaced([11185387](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3sfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

else,

PRBConsumedByScheduledRealUEsForPowerBoost = 0;

If ConfiguredLoadInPRB is already less than PRBConsumedByscheduledRealUEs[PR880973]+ PRBConsumedByCommonChannels [End PR880973] , there is no need to generate artificial load.

Otherwise, artificial load shall be generated on PRBsForArtificialLoadGeneration PRBs.

If RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, since RAT0 allocation is used, the PRB scheduling will be based on RBG. So round-up to higher RBG size is required for PRBs which are used for dummy load. Otherwise, **if RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, the PRB scheduling will be based on PRBs within PDSCH sub-areas.**

For the artificial load there is no need to perform HARQ logical channel handling and retransmissions.

When NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to non-zero value and **in case of PRB Pooling** enabled (that is, slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'True' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is set to 'True' for 7.2a PRB pooling and slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed is set to 'True' for 7.2e PRB pooling), If it can’t be ensured that the available resources to a cell/subcell is enough to generate the desired load all the time, then an alarm shall be raised for that cell. When either the feature is disabled, or when it can be ensured that the cell will always have enough resources to generate the load, alarm should be cleared.**Note that even if 7.2e PRB pooling is at layer level here computation remains at PRB level assuming all UE's are rank 1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**\*\*\*\*

When artificial load for EMF (Electro Magnetic Field) is enabled and **in case of PRB Pooling** enabled (that is, slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'True' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is set to 'True'), the cell configured to artificial load for EMF shall be prioritized for PRB allocation in pooling algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_13342\_replaced([11212500](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxB9EPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).\*\*\*\*

When artificial load for EMF (Electro Magnetic Field) is enabled and **in case of 7.2e PRB Pooling** enabled (that is,  slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed is set to 'True'), the cell configured to artificial load for EMF shall be prioritized for PRB allocation in pooling algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_25717\_replaced([11214316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxM8M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).\*\*\*\*

If RAT-1 NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, the PRBs belonging to a RBG which is not fully available(for example, due to PRB blanking and/or due to PRB pooling) shall be excluded from artificial load.(see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_10106\_replaced([11280808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpY7IfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

#### 3.29.8.2.0-5 Configurable artificial load generation Beam configuration for DL artificial load (ID: 11184003)

**3.29.8.2.0-5.0-6**  (ID: `12819278`)

Configurable artificial load generation
When NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set as non-zero and "MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = True", full sector beam (see [11288965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp-J8fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be used to generate artificial load over dummy PDSCH transmission and dummy PDCCH transmission on downlink slots.

When NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to non-zero value and "MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = True" and "NRBTS/actNr​L​B​mMimoR​T​X​Power​Saving = TRUE", while cell is in power saving mode (See [11193094](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvpdCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11193157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvqEFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then the constant value equal to pattern ID of full sector beam + PowerSavingBeamOffset (see in [11288965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp-J8fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be used for artificial load(i.e. 8193), otherwise full sector beam (see [11288965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp-J8fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be applied for artificial load.

When artificial load for EMF is enabled, gNB-DU shall use the beam provided from gNB-CU [CB014534-B] for one of the directions depending on the settings of the parameter NRCELL.emfLoadSplit.targetLoadDirectionX, where X can be direction A, B or C [End CB014534-B] for dummy PDSCH transmission. If gNB-CU provides more than one beam candidate, gNB-DU shall randomly choose a beam per slot among all beam candidates.

If "MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = False", defaults are used as described in [11112739](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZEBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) & [11111439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

If NRBTS/NRCELL/eirpControl/actEpic is enabled, then gNB-DU shall evaluate if the dummy PDSCH transmission impacts a segment under EIRP control in which the allowance planned for the current slot (see [12526294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-_HiABrIEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is 0. If it the case, the dummy PDSCH transmission shall be dropped.

If tiltOffset / leftEdgeAngle / rightEdgeAngle / upperEdgeAngle / lowerEdgeAngle / basicBeamSet is/are online changed, the full sector beam(including powerSavingBeamOffset for power saving if power saving is activated) and/or the beams for artifical load for EMF are online updated. gNB-DU shall use the latest ones provided from gNB-CU.

---

#### 3.29.8.2.0-6 Configurable artificial load generation Fake UE scheduling on TRS and SSB Slots (ID: 11184029)

**3.29.8.2.0-6.0-5**  (ID: `13340321`)

Configurable artificial load generation
Applicable only when (NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to not zero OR artificial load for EMF is enabled) AND (NRBTS/NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking is not zero OR actSubTRSRateMatch is set to TRUE)

* fake UE shall be scheduled as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17426\_replaced([11183931](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvOmQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* numRateMatchingRes shall be calculated as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10885\_replaced([11124985](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsEg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* TBS shall be calculated based on available number of resources.
* Configured MCS “mcsIndexForArtificialLoad” shall be used for PDSCH on CSI-RS symbols. (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17728\_replaced([11184052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvPNX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

**[Before PR865373]**

Additionally, when NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen AND NRBTS/NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking is not zero,

* There is no need to rate match fake UE.

**[PR865373]**

* Rate match on fake UE is needed if actSubTRSRateMatch is set to TRUE, otherwise rate match on fake UE is not needed.

**[End PR865373]**

---

#### 3.29.8.2.0-7 Configurable artificial load generation Load balancing algorithm to distribute artificial load fairly across multiple directions over time (ID: 12635121)

**3.29.8.2.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12635130`)

Configurable artificial load generation
parameter initializations:

alpha = 0.001

for (direction i, where i is A or B or C)  
  initialize acheivedFairShare(i, t-1) to 0.001  
  initialize I(i, t-1) = 1 for direction i  
endfor

algorithm description:

**[CB014534-E] if((at least one of the two periods (onPeriod or offPeriod) is 0) or (for the duration of onPeriod))**[End CB014534-E]\*\*\*\*

if ((current slot t is a DL slot) and (real UE(s) traffic load in slot t (as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16626 ([11183121](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvH4nvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) < NRCELL.emfTargetTotalLoad))  
   if (only one direction i is configured with a non-zero NRCELL.emfLoadSplit.targetLoadDirection(i), where i is A or B or C)  
     select direction i *to i  
   else  
     for (each direction i, where i is A or B or C)  
         weight(i) =  NRCELL.emfLoadSplit.targetLoadDirection(i)  
         if (weight(i) is zero)   
             skip direction i  
         else  
             acheivedFairShare(i, t) = (1-alpha) \* acheivedFairShare(i, t-1) + alpha \* I (i, t-1) / weight(i)  
         endif  
      endfor  
      select direction i* that has the least value of acheivedFairShare(i, t) among directions i with non-zero weight(i)  
    endif  
    artificial load shall be generated in the selected direction i\* in slot t     
 else  
   skip slot t  
 endif

**[CB014534-E] endif**[End CB014534-E]\*\*\*\*

where,

I(i, t-1) = 1, if direction i is chosen at time t-1

0, otherwise

Note: if the weights are are reconfigured i.e., changed online in the run time, then the parameters are re-initialized to the initial values.

example, refer to link: [RAN A&S- 5G-CB & 2/3/4G Spec. Repository of Architecture and Specification - CP2 - All Documents](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FASCOP%2FAS%20Documents%2FFS1TAs%2DCFAMs%2FCB%2FCB014500%20%2D%20CB014999%2FCFAM%2FCB014534%20Load%20generator%20for%20EMF%20%2D%20multiple%20segments%2FCP2&viewid=73435348%2D8379%2D4262%2Da3aa%2Dffe0b7322e0c&FolderCTID=0x012000D96F54C70C4C3547AE19DA5D399C6414)

---

#### 3.29.8.2.0-8 Configurable artificial load generation Rank and precoder selection (ID: 13399020)

**3.29.8.2.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `13399022`)

Configurable artificial load generation
When NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to non zero value, to generate artificial load in the cell in downlink direction for both PDCCH & PDSCH, The gNB-DU shall select rank based on parameter rdArtificialLoadRank (5G\_L2\_1307\_replaced(11189518)) or rdArtificialLoadRankNonBf (5G\_L2\_1308\_replaced(11189533)).

If rdArtificialLoadRankNonBf = 5, this means maximum possible rank is to be used. The maximum rank can be determined as follows:

* If dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2, then maximum rank is 2
* If dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4, then maximum rank is 4

If non-BF mode is used (5G\_UP\_908\_replaced([11353388](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub_hUPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): The precoding matrix is to be set in 5G\_L2\_1816\_replaced([11212192](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-joCvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else:

The precoding matrix selection is controlled according to the following rules:

* In case of open loop MIMO (dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4), the default behavior from [11139281: Default precoder settings CL/OL (2x2 transmissions)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_SvMzhvvLEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11139297: Default precoder settings CL/OL (4Tx transmissions)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_S0b40vvLEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) is followed.
* In case of closed loop MIMO (dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 OR ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4), because PMI report is not available, the selection is hardcoded as defined in [11184070: Spatial mode & Precoding for artificial PDSCH load](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_9EKGkPvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

## 3.29.9 DL HARQ handling

### 3.29.9.0-1 DL HARQ handling Basic requirements and multiplicities (ID: 11184119)

**3.29.9.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11184133`)

DL HARQ handling
There shall be one DL HARQ handler per configured serving subcell per UE context created in the DL scheduler.

The DL HARQ handler shall support adaptive asynchronous HARQ processing.

Each DL HARQ handler shall support the handling of a maximum of 16 HARQ processes per UE context created for each subcell the UE can support until the UE context is terminated.

The number of HARQ Processes is configured by MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/nrOfHARQProcessesForPDSCH.

[Before CB010544-A] ; Until the UE dedicated configuration is activated, the 5G-L2-PS restricts the number of HARQ in parallel to the 8 first. [CB010544-A] When UE dedicated configuration is disabled including the UE in the initial BWP without dedicated configuration, the 5G-L2-PS restricts the number of HARQ in parallel to the 8 first. [End CB010544-A]

The DL HARQ entity shall support bookkeeping of the free, allocated HARQ processes with first transmissions and the HARQ processes with pending retransmissions for all HARQ processes of all UEs and all subcells.

There is one TB allocated per HARQ process.

HARQ Process Identifiers 0..15 shall be used. 5G-L2-PS shall only use the first numOfHarqProcessesForPdsch HARQ Processes.

If DL MU-MIMO (FR1, TDD, conduction mode only) is activated, Each DL HARQ were expected to receive positive feedback and HARQ was to be freed without retransmissions.

---

### 3.29.9.1 DL HARQ processing

#### 3.29.9.1.0-1 DL HARQ processing DL HARQ processing overview (ID: 11184145)

**3.29.9.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11184150`)

DL HARQ processing
HARQ processing, logical channel prioritization and multiplexing shall be defined by the DL scheduler after the DL TD scheduler has determined the candidate set 2 and the assignment of UEs to subcells for the next scheduling 5GTF-subframe/NR Slot according to request classes:

A retransmission shall be done in a subcell if either NACK or DTX has been received from L1-UL for a HARQ process on PUCCH in the subcell and reported to 5G-L2-PS. If ACK has been received the HARQ process is freed. If a retransmission has to be done for the assigned UE the DL scheduler will inform 5G-L2-LO about the required retransmission.

If the maximum number of HARQ transmissions ([Before CB009189-B]NRBTS/NRCELLGRP.maxDlHarqTx [CB009189-B]UE specific maxDlHarqTxNum value per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12742\_replaced([11185319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvSQpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB009189-B]) has been reached the transmission of the related transport block shall be stopped and the HARQ process shall be freed.

For each subcell where a new transmission shall be done based on the resource requests of the UE the following procedure shall be executed:

The scheduler determines the TB see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4621\_replaced([11192057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kviITvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The DL scheduler shall select the next available free HARQ process of the UE in the subcell for the transmission of the TB. The DL scheduler will toggle the NDI value in the DCI sent for the transmission. 5G-L2-PS shall inform 5G-L2-LO about the new transmission, the TB and the HARQ process id.

See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4531\_replaced([11184177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

---

#### 3.29.9.1.0-2 DL HARQ processing Speculative DTX Algorithm (ID: 11184154)

**3.29.9.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11184159`)

DL HARQ processing
If {(actSpeculativeDtx is set to 'shortPUCCHMode' and pucchModeSelect is set to 'shortPucch') or actSpeculativeDtx is set to 'enabled‘} and UE is not in transient state of Scell Addition (refer to TS38.133 section 8.2 for interruptions at Scell addition/Release for SA and NSA mode while transient state is defined as period between L2PS received Scell addition request for the UE and L2PS received RRC reconfiguration complete for the UE Scell addition request), a DTX can be considered as an ACK and the HARQ process is freed if the MCS used in the transmission is:

* greater than or equal to rdSpeculativeDtxAckThreshold
* & less than rdSpeculativeDtxDtxThreshold

PM counters related to the HARQ process are also updated based on the above decision to pass a DTX as an ACK.

DTX of MAC-CEs remain DTX even if the above is satisfied (excluded from speculative DTX procedure).

Note: Upon the modification of actSpeculativeDtx received, the modified value is taken into use immediately for new and existing UEs.

---

#### 3.29.9.1.0-3 DL HARQ processing Incremental Redundancy for PDSCH HARQ Transmissions (ID: 11184166)

**3.29.9.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11184170`)

DL HARQ processing
If rdHARQDisableIncrRedundancyDl is TRUE (see 5G\_L2\_1060\_replaced([11181843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27XKEvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall disable incremental redundancy on PDSCH.

5G-L2-PS shall use RV0 in all initial transmissions and in all HARQ retransmissions. 5G-L2-PS shall always set rvIndex = 0 in DlData\_PdschSendReq (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3763\_replaced([11165485](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kthWEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and Redundancy version (TB1) = 0 in DCI Format 1\_1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10502\_replaced([11171547](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuFWwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If rdHARQDisableIncrRedundancyDl is FALSE, 5G-L2-PS shall proceed as follows:

* 5G-L2-PS shall indicate in each DL DCI the RV index corresponding to the data transmitted in the PDSCH. The RV index used with a HARQ transmission is derived as follows:

- If rdRvReuseLastValueInCaseOfDtxForDL(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13536\_replaced([11193512](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))= 1

- If Initial HARQ Transmission resulted in a DTX:

- RV 0 shall be repeated for all subsequent HARQ transmissions until a non-DTX response is received

- The RV index order subsequent to non-DTX response point shall proceed starting with RV index # 2 in the order of RV 2, 3, 1, 0, 2, 3, 1, 0, etc. (repeating RvSequence), as the HARQ TX number is increased

- If a HARQ Re-Transmission resulted in a DTX when previous HARQ Transmission did not:

- The RV index used in the transmission resulting in DTX shall be repeated for all subsequent HARQ transmissions until a non-DTX response is received.

- The RV index order subsequent to non-DTX response point shall proceed starting with next RV index in repeating RvSequence. as the HARQ TX number is increased. See example below.

- Else If rdRvReuseLastValueInCaseOfDtxForDL(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13536\_replaced([11193512](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))= 0:

- RV = RvSequence [N+1) modulo 4] (i.e. next RV in RvSequence mod 4)

Notes:

* RvSequence corresponding to RV0, RV2,RV3,RV1. Repeating RvSequence means RV0, RV2, RV3, RV1, RV0, RV2, RV3, RV1, etc.
* As long as no DTX is received, the repeating RvSequence is followed
* Intent of rdRvReuseLastValueInCaseOfDtxForDL(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13536\_replaced([11193512](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))= 1 is to minimize complete HARQ failure in edge-of-cell cases by 1st making sure RV0 gets through to UE and as a 2nd enhancement to get other RVs to UE.
* Example(s) illustrated below

**Example(1) Only Inital Tx results in DTX:**

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| HARQ Tx |  | Normal RV Seq |  | DTX Result |  | RV Used |  | Notes |  |  |
| 1 |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | 0 |  |  |  |  |
|  | 2 |  | 2 |  | Yes |  | 0 |  | Initial Tx resulted in DTX, so repeat RV0 |  |
|  | 3 |  | 3 |  | No |  | 0 |  | Previous Tx resulted in DTX, so repeat RV0 |  |
|  | 4 |  | 1 |  | No |  | 2 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so resume RvSequence with RV2 |  |
|  | 5 |  | 0 |  | No |  | 3 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so continue with RvSequence |  |
| 6 |  | 2 |  | No |  | 1 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so continue with RvSequence |  |  |
|  | 7 |  | 3 |  | No |  | 0 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so continue with RvSequence |  |
| 8 (max) |  | 1 |  | N/A |  | 2 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so continue with RvSequence |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

**Example(2) Inital Tx and subsequent HARQ Tx result in DTX:**

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| HARQ Tx |  | Normal RV Seq |  | DTX Result |  | RV Used |  | Notes |  |  |
| 1 |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | 0 |  |  |  |  |
|  | 2 |  | 2 |  | Yes |  | 0 |  | Initial Tx resulted in DTX, so repeat RV0 |  |
|  | 3 |  | 3 |  | No |  | 0 |  | Previous Tx resulted in DTX, so repeat RV0 |  |
|  | 4 |  | 1 |  | No |  | 2 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so resume RvSequence with RV2 |  |
|  | 5 |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | 3 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so continue with RvSequence |  |
| 6 |  | 2 |  | Yes |  | 3 |  |  |  |  |
|  | 7 |  | 3 |  | No |  | 3 |  | Previous Tx resulted in DTX, so repeat RV3 |  |
| 8 (max) |  | 1 |  | N/A |  | 1 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so resume RvSequence with RV1 |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

**Example(3) Only HARQ Re-Tx results in DTX:**

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| HARQ Tx |  | Normal RV Seq |  | DTX Result |  | RV Used |  | Notes |  |  |
| 1 |  | 0 |  | No |  | 0 |  |  |  |  |
|  | 2 |  | 2 |  | No |  | 2 |  | Initial Tx was NON-DTX, so continue with RvSequence |  |
|  | 3 |  | 3 |  | No |  | 3 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so continue with RvSequence |  |
|  | 4 |  | 1 |  | Yes |  | 1 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so continue with RvSequence |  |
|  | 5 |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | 1 |  | Previous Tx resulted in DTX, so repeat RV1 |  |
| 6 |  | 2 |  | Yes |  | 1 |  |  |  |  |
|  | 7 |  | 3 |  | No |  | 1 |  | Previous Tx resulted in DTX, so repeat RV1 |  |
| 8 (max) |  | 1 |  | N/A |  | 0 |  | Previous Tx was NON-DTX, so resume RvSequence with RV0 |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

---

#### 3.29.9.1.0-4 DL HARQ processing HARQ handling of xPDSCH transmission (ID: 11184177)

**3.29.9.1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11184188`)

DL HARQ processing
For each scheduled xPDSCH transmission (excluding RA Msg2 transmissions), the corresponding HARQ handling shall be carried on as below:

Scheduling of an Initial Transmission:

* The transport block size is calculated considering the MCS indicated by the Link adaptation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4621\_replaced([11192057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kviITvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the amount of data waiting for transmission (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4559\_replaced([11186978](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which can lead to an MCS downgrade (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4630\_replaced([11192270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvjWafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* The DL scheduler selects the next (in the sense of increasing HARQ process ID) available free HARQ process of the UE in the subcell for the transmission of the TB (that’s recommended in order to make the check in the TTI trace easier);
* The DL scheduler will toggle the NDI value in the DCI of this xPDSCH transmission;
* The DL Scheduler shall store for the selected HARQ process:
* the MCS recommended by the link adaptation (rrmDlProposedMcs) and the one finally used (rrmDlUsedMcs), which can be different in case of MCS downgrade.
* other information needed to handle possible retransmissions;
* 5G-L2-PS shall indicate the correct dlHarqBufferIndex in LoCtrl\_PduMuxReq towards L2-LO (see 5G\_L2\_282\_replaced([11149470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24WSVfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

[CB013199-B]

* If slow pooling of instantaneous throughput is enabled i.e. tddInstTputPoolingAllowed is TRUE (refer 5G\_RAN\_OP\_3986\_replaced([11693605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D33F-P9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), for every initial transmission, gNB-DU shall add the TBS(tbSize) value to rrmUsedCellThroughputPerSlot (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29086\_replaced([11215102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to keep track of the cell's throughput per slot. Before every PDSCH initial transmission, the scheduler shall also ensure that the throughput limit is not exceeded as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case that scheduling the current UE increases the throughput beyond the limit, the scheduler shall take necessary actions as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB013199-B]

---

#### 3.29.9.1.0-5 DL HARQ processing Scheduling of a Re-transmission (ID: 11184193)

**3.29.9.1.0-5.0-8**  (ID: `12585914`)

DL HARQ processing
Scheduling of a Re-transmission:

* The same HARQ process is used as for the initial transmission;
* NDI value of that HARQ process is not toggled;
* 5G-L2-PS shall use the same dlHarqBufferIndex as for the initial transmission to address the TB towards L2-LO;
* The same or different CDM group(s) without data as initial transmission; The different CDM group(s) without data can be used only when reserved MCS(refer to 5G\_UP\_IF\_1897\_replaced([11515718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUO_Wvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) used in retransmission.
* Scheduling of a retransmission with increased coding gain for eligible VoNR capable users configured with 5QI1/65/66 bearer for voice service is defined by the rules specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14946\_replaced([11185337](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvSQp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For ineligible users the scheduling of a retransmission shall follow as defined hereafter.
* Scheduling of a retransmission with increased transmit power for UEs which fullfill eligibility criteria according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24934\_replaced([11185387](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3sfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For ineligible users (which doesn't meet the criteria of power boost) the scheduling of a retransmission shall follow as defined hereafter.

For a (TDD FR1 cell and NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True) or (TDD / FDD FR1 cell and NRCELL/actDlRbgRandomization = enhanced):

* If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled and PDSCH allocation is made with RAT0 then, **if initial transmission uses the last RBG but re-transmission doesn’t (or vice versa), the reserved MCS (refer to 5G\_UP\_IF\_1897\_replaced([11515718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUO_Wvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be used in retransmission.** The same number of PDSCH symbols shall be used as for the initial transmission. **If PDSCH allocation is made with RAT1 (NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True), then partial RBG is not relevant.**\*\*\*\*
* If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, the adaptive re-transmission scheme is applied as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11948\_replaced([11184286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and reserved MCS could be used as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12911\_replaced([11184825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRpnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For a FDD cell which is shared with LTE and actLteNrDssLteCatM equals true:  
      · TBS shall be calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4622\_replaced([11192072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

· The same Rank is used as for the initial transmission.

* If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled, a re-transmission is not allowed in SSB slot with TRS

· If TBS calculated for retransmission differs from initial TBS the reserved MCS (refer to 5G\_UP\_IF\_1897\_replaced([11515718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUO_Wvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 3GPP 38.214, clause 5.1.3.1) shall be used in retransmission. Specific reserved MCS shall be chosen according to modulation order.

Modulation order should be the same as in the initial transmission. The same number of PDSCH symbols shall be used as for the initial transmission.

For such retransmission 5G-L2-PS shall send to L1 TBS value from the initial transmission.

* If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, the adaptive re-transmission scheme is applied as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11948\_replaced([11184286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and reserved MCS could be used as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12911\_replaced([11184825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRpnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* If NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true and if the UE is configured in dynamicswitch, gNB-DU shall try first the retransmission as a dynamicswitch RAT1 allocation and if there is not enough PRBs available in PDSCH sub areas, then shall retry the retransmission as dynamicswitch RAT0 whatever the initial transmission was a dynamicswitch RAT1 or RAT0 allocation (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23663\_replaced([11177504](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note: if the initial transmission was a dynamicswitch RAT1, the adaptive re-transmission scheme shall be activated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be fulfilled, otherwise gNB-DU shall not allow a retransmission as a dynamicswitch RAT0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25033\_replaced([11176715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

For other cell type:

* The same TBS and Rank is used as for the initial transmission
* If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled
* The same number of PDSCH symbols is used as for the initial transmission. If there are less PDSCH symbols available in a slot a retransmission is blocked as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4258\_replaced([11175719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kulGCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If there are more PDSCH symbols available in the slot in which the retransmission is scheduled only the number of PDSCH symbols according to the initial transmission shall be used**.**
* If the DL PRB sharing in subframe 0/5/9 is enabled (*rrmLteDLPrbSharing=true, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4722\_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) re-transmission is not allowed in neither slots 0 nor slots 5*
* If actRateMatchedCsiIm is set to 'true' re-transmission in slots with ZP CSI-RS (CSI-IM)  is not allowed
* If**[Before CB015668-A]** actTddCsiImRm is set to 'true' **[CB015668-A] actTddCsiImRm = TRUE (note: actTddCsiImRm is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator)** [End CB015668-A]\*\*\*\*: 1) for the UEs whose DL grant is DCI 1\_1, re-transmission traffic can't be scheduled on ZP cell specific CSI-RS (CSI-IM) slots no matter initial transmission traffic is scheduled on ZP cell specific CSI-RS (CSI-IM) slots or not.    2) for the UEs whose DL grant is DCI 1\_0, if initial traffic is scheduled on ZP cell specific CSI-RS (CSI-IM) slots, re-transmission traffic could be scheduled on ZP cell specific CSI-RS (CSI-IM) slots or normal data slot.  CSI-IM symbols are excluded in both of initial and re-transmission traffic. Note: Reserved MCS shall be used if tbSize in re-transmission and initial transmission traffic is different
* Re-transmission is not allowed in SSB slot with TRS and TRS slot.
* If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, the adaptive re-transmission scheme is applied as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11948\_replaced([11184286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and reserved MCS could be used as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12911\_replaced([11184825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRpnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For a TDD cell, if slow pooling of instantaneous throughput is enabled i.e. tddInstTputPoolingAllowed is TRUE (refer 5G\_RAN\_OP\_3986\_replaced([11693605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D33F-P9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), for every re-transmission, gNB-DU shall add the TBS(tbSize) value to rrmUsedCellThroughputPerSlot (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29086\_replaced([11215102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to keep track of the cell's throughput per slot. Before every PDSCH re-transmission, the scheduler shall also ensure that the throughput limit is not exceeded as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case that scheduling the current UE increases the throughput beyond the limit, the scheduler shall take necessary actions as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29088\_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB007372-AA]   
Note: For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) the NIDD ensures that adaptive re-transmission is activated (NRBTS.NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE)  
Note2: For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) there will be RLC retransmission if the initial transmission with code rate >0.83 is in the NR dedicated area and the retransmission after DTX is in the LTE shared area ([2620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). In such case the maximum code rate will be exceeded and UE will not decode data.  
[End CB007372-AA]

---

#### 3.29.9.1.0-6 DL HARQ processing HARQ feedback processing for a retransmission (ID: 11184237)

**3.29.9.1.0-6.0-5**  (ID: `11184264`)

DL HARQ processing
At the reception of the HARQ Feedback (message UlData\_PucchReceiveRespHarqD or UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD, For  FR1 CA UE ,  HARQ feedback for Scell PDSCH scheduling will be received from message from P(S)Cell through PsPeerCtrl interface according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2680\_replaced([11120547](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqR02fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).) from 5G-L1-UL for a given UE, subcell and HARQ process:

If DL CRC = OK is derived from UlData\_PucchReceiveRespHarqD or if UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD is received indicating HARQ non-DTX and HARQ Ack, or HARQ feedback forwarded from P(S)Cell for FR1 CA UE:

* The HARQ process can be freed, the dlHarqBufferIndex is freed;

If DL CRC = NOK or DL CRC = DTX is derived from UlData\_PucchReceiveRespHarqD or if UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD is received indicating HARQ DTX or HARQ Nack, or HARQ feedback forwarded from P(S)Cell for FR1 CA UE :

If the maximum number of HARQ transmissions (UE specific maxDlHarqTxNum value per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12742\_replaced([11185319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvSQpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) has been reached (HARQ Failure):

* The retransmission scheduling of the related transport block shall be stopped.
* the HARQ process is freed, the dlHarqBufferIndex is freed;

Otherwise:

* If HARQ feedback for PCell or Scell which is not in overheating status according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2874\_replaced([11123313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqiThPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB-DU shall consider the UE as candidate to retransmit the data of that HARQ process. Retransmission can be on any subcell. Subcell is decided in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If HARQ feedback for Scell which is in overheating status according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2871\_replaced([11123293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqiTgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or FR1 TDD Scell or FDD SCell is in suspension scheduling status according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2878\_replaced([11123344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqiTivvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB-DU shall stop the retransmission of the related transport block, free the HARQ process and dlHarqBufferIndex.

If slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is True (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if a retransmission is skipped due to insufficient usable RBG or number of PRBs in a slot as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4311\_replaced([11177467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (not enough RBGs or PRBs per slot during the bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6781\_replaced([11211947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-StfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) retransmission is scheduled),  the HARQ process is freed, the dlHarqBufferIndex is freed;

* If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, for a UE with retransmission, the number of layer PRBs used shall not exceed the remaining available layer PRBs in a slot (rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot; refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The number of layer PRBs used for a given PRB allocation can be calculated as numReqLayerPrb mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case the required number of layer PRBs are not available in the current slot, the retransmission shall be skipped and tried in a later slot. However, if the required number of layer PRBs for the retransmission are greater than the maximum number of available layer PRBs per slot in a pooling period (refer rrmMaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),  the retransmitting UE shall be skipped and the HARQ process is freed.[CB010630-E]This layer PRB constraint is also valid for PRB based RAT1 allocation scheme (i.e. NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True)[End CB010630-E]

If long PUCCH is configured and for a UE one or two A/N bits are expected in same slot as SR occasion then gNB-DU sends two UlData\_PucchReceiveReq (entries) as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4602\_replaced([11187378](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvazhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). It is considered HARQ DTX feedback only if both corresponding UlData\_PucchReceiveHarqD (entries) indicate DTX. It is expected and considered HARQ non-DTX if one of the two corresponding UlData\_PucchReceiveHarqD (entries) indicates non-DTX feedback.

If single A/N bit is expected in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD then gNB-DU shall evaluate IE dtxAck1Bit and IE ackNack1BitUci for DTX and A/N feedback, respectively (see details in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1720\_replaced([11109204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1DVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). IE dtxAckMoreThan1Bit and IE ackNackUciMoreThan1Bit shall be ignored.

If two or more A/N bits are expected in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD then gNB-DU shall evaluate IE dtxAckMoreThan1Bit and IE ackNackUciMoreThan1Bit for DTX and A/N feedback, respectively.

If two A/N bits are expected in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD and the corresponding PUSCH has been granted by DCI 0\_0 and IE dtxAckMoreThan1Bit indicated HARQ DTX then gNB-DU shall evaluate IE dtxAck1Bit and IE ackNack1BitUci for DTX and A/N feedback, respectively. Rationale is that in UL DCI 0\_0 the UE doesn’t get a DAI value and therefore would not recognize that it missed the PDCCH of the second PDSCH and consequently it would transmit single A/N bit only whereas gNB expects two A/N bits. L1 is provided the required information. The double decoding is only applicable for the 1 vs. 2 HARQ A/N bit, not 1 vs. more than 2 HARQ A/N bits as not supported by L1.

---

#### 3.29.9.1.0-7 DL HARQ processing Scheduling of adaptive Re-transmission (ID: 11184268)

**3.29.9.1.0-7.0-4**  (ID: `13458103`)

DL HARQ processing
13458103:
DL HARQ processing
For UE with SRB1 traffic, gNB-DU shall not apply adaptive retransmission and UE shall be scheduled with the legacy retransmission method.

if /NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE, 5G-L2-PS shall use the adaptive retransmission algorithm for the allocation of resources for the retransmissions. If a NACK is received for the HARQ feedback of a single DL transmission by the gNB, the adaptive retransmission shall be applied.

If a DTX is indicated for the initial transmission of the HARQ process, the adaptive retransmission shall not be applied. Instead, the UE shall be rescheduled with the legacy retransmission method.

The following flow chart illustrates the general conditions for the provision of reserved MCS (refer to 5G\_UP\_IF\_1897\_replaced([11515718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUO_Wvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to adaptive retransmission schemes. The flowchart begins with a "Start" oval. From "Start," an arrow points to a diamond-shaped decision box labeled "actAdaptiveReTx ResMcs?".
If the answer to "actAdaptiveReTx ResMcs?" is "True," an arrow leads to another diamond-shaped decision box labeled "HARQ feedback is DTX'ed?".
If the answer to "HARQ feedback is DTX'ed?" is "False," an arrow leads to a diamond-shaped decision box labeled "rdAdaptiveReTxNackCount(ReTx Number) == 1".
If the answer to "rdAdaptiveReTxNackCount(ReTx Number) == 1" is "True," an arrow leads to a green rectangular process box labeled "Apply algorithm of the adaptive retransmission scheme".
If the answer to "rdAdaptiveReTxNackCount(ReTx Number) == 1" is "False," an arrow leads to the "End" oval.
If the answer to "HARQ feedback is DTX'ed?" is "True," an arrow leads to a diamond-shaped decision box labeled "Valid feedback of the HARQ process received before?".
If the answer to "Valid feedback of the HARQ process received before?" is "True," an arrow leads to the green rectangular process box "Apply algorithm of the adaptive retransmission scheme".
If the answer to "Valid feedback of the HARQ process received before?" is "False," an arrow leads to a green rectangular process box labeled "Use legacy method for reTx".
If the answer to "actAdaptiveReTx ResMcs?" is "False," an arrow leads to the green rectangular process box "Use legacy method for reTx".
Both "Use legacy method for reTx" and "Apply algorithm of the adaptive retransmission scheme" process boxes have arrows pointing to the "End" oval.
The flowchart uses standard flowchart symbols: ovals for start/end, diamonds for decisions, and rectangles for processes. Arrows indicate the flow of control. Text labels are present within each shape and on the arrows to denote decision outcomes ("True" or "False").[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/DL Adaptive reTx flowchart.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

If there is the reception of more than one bit in UCI carried on PUCCH or PUSCH, global DTX indication is not sufficient because the UE changes a DTX of one HARQ to a NACK by use of the HARQ codebook. In such case, the evaluation to decide if the adaptive retransmission shall be used is based on the following method which is applicable to decide how the HARQ retransmission needs to be scheduled.

The NACKs received shall be mapped to the bitmap *rdAdaptiveReTxNackCount (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12915\_replaced([11193480](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), such that if the bit corresponding to the retransmission number is set to 1, the MCS/PRB of the initial transmission shall be used for HARQ retransmission. If rdRvReuseLastValueInCaseOfDtxForDL (see [11193512](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) is set to True, both DTX and NACKs shall be counted for mapping to* rdAdaptiveReTxNackCount.\*\*

* If DTX and no Nack received before for the Harq process

- Adaptive retransmission shall not be used

* else If the bit in *rdAdaptiveReTxNackCount corresponding to the retransmission number == 1*

- Adaptive retransmission shall not be used

[PR876290-26R3] - else If rdActAdaptiveReTxFakeAckMitigationAlg*(see [13458108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Hi6VaS5lEfGxldjOZQLezQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) is set to TRUE AND there are more than one HARQ feedback (ACK/NACK) bits bundled in UCI AND gNB-DU receives a  NACK for initial transmission*

- Adaptive retransmission shall not be used

[End PR876290-26R3]

* else

- Adaptive retransmission shall be used

---

#### 3.29.9.1.1 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation

#### 3.29.9.1.1-1 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation

**3.29.9.1.1-1.0-2**  (ID: `11185330`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
The gNB shall support the configuration of the maximum number of downlink HARQ transmissions including initial transmission and retransmissions (maxDlHarqTxNum), as following:

* If NRCELL/actServiceAwareConfig is TRUE,

• maxDlHarqTxNum = Max (maxDlHarqTx\_Bearers, NRCELLGRP/maxDlHarqTx) if any MAC Control Element is transmitted in the TB, maxDlHarqTx\_Bearers otherwise.

Where:

- maxDlHarqTx\_Bearers = Max (maxDlHarqTx\_Bearer[i]), where the 'i' represents all bearers (including SRBs and DRBs) of this UE multiplexed in the TB,

-     maxDlHarqTx\_Bearer[i] = NRDRB\_MAC/maxDlHarqTxDrb for bearers with maxDlHarqTxDrb configured [CB008140-GA] or, when it runs in an ext-DU for a connection in Inter-gNB CA mode, from the parameter "MaxDlHarqTransmission" received from prime-DU (see 5G\_IF\_NTW\_9078) if NRCELL/actServiceAwareConfig is true in PCell and NRBTS/NRDRB\_MAC/maxDlHarqTxDrb is configured in Prime-gNB [End CB008140-GA] , NRCELLGRP/maxDlHarqTx otherwise.

* Else (i.e. NRCELL/actServiceAwareConfig is FALSE)

• maxDlHarqTxNum = NRCELLGRP/maxDlHarqTx

---

#### 3.29.9.1.1-2 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation

##### 3.29.9.1.1-2.0-1 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Algorithm (ID: 11185342)

**3.29.9.1.1-2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11185363`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
If NRBTS/NRCELL/actVoNRIncCodingGain is set to TRUE, then a VoNR UE scheduled for retransmission is eligible to get allocated number of PRBs greater than their initial transmission provided that the following criterion are met;

- **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI1**[CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] bearer configured with active voice session and is scheduled for retransmission in the particular slot.**\*\*\*\*

- For this UE, if a particular HARQ process has received continuous NACKs for transmission attempts given by NRBTS/NRCELL/numTxPrbVoNRIncCodingGain. HARQ transmission attempts lesser than numTxPrbVoNRIncCodingGain, should not be considered for PRB augmentation.

-     Number of PRBs allocated durig initial transmission does not exceed configured valued for NRBTS/NRCELL.maxPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain.

-     The UE must not have been selected as a candidate for adaptive retransmissions as per the rules defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15568.

[PR820861-25R3]

-     The value of the bit from *rdAdaptiveReTxNackCount (see [11193480](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) corresponding to the retransmission number being scheduled is equal to 0.  [End PR820861-25R3]*

The number of PRBs that can be allocated over and above the initial transmissions if available, is given by a scaling factor relative to the number of PRBs allocated during the initial transmission and is configured by an O&M parameter NRBTS/NRCELL.stepUpNumPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain and shall be capped against the configured value NRBTS/NRCELL.maxPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain. Availability of PRBs shall depend upon size of channel bandwidth or specific BWP,**available PRBs for VoNR in PDSCH sub area according to PDSCH sub area definition in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to TRUE,** PRB blanking restrictions, limitations due to PRB pooling in that slot or PRB share available when the cell is FDD FR1 with actLteNrDssLteCatM set to TRUE. When such restrictions are met, the UE shall be allocated with as many PRBs possible, which may result in lesser than the configured value (i.e number of PRBs that could be augmented as per the configured value given NRBTS/NRCELL.stepUpNumPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain) but higher than the PRBs granted during the initial transmission.\*\*\*\*

After allocation of PRBs for retransmission as described above, and the number of PRBs allocated during the retransmission is higher than that used during the initial transmission, then;

- the UE is scheduled for retransmission with reserved MCS (refer to 5G\_UP\_IF\_1897\_replaced([11515718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUO_Wvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 3GPP 38.214, clause 5.1.3.1)

- Specific reserved MCS shall be chosen according to modulation order. Modulation order should be the same as in the initial transmission.

- For such retransmission 5G-L2-PS shall send to L1, TBS value from the initial transmission

- Same rank as initial transmission is used.

Once allocated to the user, the number of PRBs shall remain same for subsequent transmission attempts (on reception of a NACK) if PRBs are available during scheduling on that slot. Otherwise, the scheduler should try and grant as many PRBs possible , which may result in lesser than the configured value (i.e number of PRBs that could be augmented as per the configured value given NRBTS/NRCELL.stepUpNumPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain  but higher than the PRBs granted during the initial transmission.

Examples for clarification are demonstrated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23978\_replaced([11185368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvSQsPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: It is possible that (one of) previous retransmission attempt before the transmission attempts have reached numTxPrbVoNRIncCodingGain, was carried out in adaptive retransmission scheme and resulted in a different number of PRBs compared to the initial transmission and was NACKed. Following retransmission attempt(s) can be carried out in enhanced retransmission scheme provided the UE becomes eligible, with the extra PRBs relative to the number of PRBs granted in the initial transmission, given by the scaling factor stepUpNumPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain, and not relative to the retransmission that was adaptive.

Rationale: The idea is to increase coding gain by increasing more coded bits and making the HARQ attempts more robust. This improves the situation with VoNR packet loss by decreasing the occurences of complete HARQ failure and thereby packet drops. Avoidance of HARQ failures helps in reducing the residual BLER (rBLER).

---

##### 3.29.9.1.1-2.0-2 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Examples (ID: 11185368)

**3.29.9.1.1-2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11185380`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
Following is an example to depict the above concept:

Case1: if NRBTS/NRCELL/numTxPrbVoNRIncCodingGain = 2

NRBTS/NRCELL.maxPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain = 32

Cell is FDD FR1 20MHz (106 PRBs, RBG size=8)

NRBTS/NRCELL.stepUpNumPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain = 2 [Before CB008927-C-UPD001] [CB008927-C] if NRBTS/NRCELL/actRat1Alloc is set to FALSE [End CB008927-C] [End CB008927-C-UPD001].

From the 2nd ReTx, the RBG/PRBs are augmented [CB008927-C] if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to FALSE [End CB008927-C] as shown below:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Init Tx | 1st ReTx | 2nd ReTx | 3rd ReTx | 4th ReTx | 5th ReTx |
| NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | ACK |
| 1 RBG | 1 RBG | 2 RBGs | 2 RBGs | 2 RBGs |  |
| 8 PRBs | 8 PRBs | 16 PRBs | 16 PRBs | 16 PRBs |  |

[CB008927-C] Else if actRat1Alloc is set to TRUE [End CB008927-C] [Before CB008927-C-UPD001] and NRBTS/NRCELL/incNumPrbsHarqRat1VoNR = 8 PRBs [End CB008927-C-UPD001]

[CB008927-C] From the 2nd ReTx, the PRBs are augmented as shown below:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Init Tx | 1st ReTx | 2nd ReTx | 3rd ReTx | 4th ReTx | 5th ReTx |
| NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | ACK |
| 8 PRBs | 8 PRBs | 16 PRBs | 16 PRBs | 16 PRBs |  |

The incremental factor for adding PRBs - in case of PRB allocation to PDSCH - shown above is adding 8 PRBs to the initial number of VoNR PRBs. [End CB008927-C].

The scaling factor for augmetation shown above is double the initial number of PRBs.

Note, that for certain channel bandwidth (FDD 5MHz) or during certain slots the available PRBs for augmentation may not be as much as given by this scaling factor or 'maxPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain'.

Case2: if NRBTS/NRCELL/numTxPrbVoNRIncCodingGain = 2

NRBTS/NRCELL.maxPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain = 32

Cell is TDD FR1 100MHz (273 PRBs, RBG size=16)

NRBTS/NRCELL.stepUpNumPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain = 2 [Before CB008927-C-UPD001] [CB008927-C] if actRat1Alloc is set to FALSE [End CB008927-C] [End CB008927-C-UPD001]

Here the initial PRBs were already at the limit, hence the augmenation cannot work here, so the number of PRBs remain same. This UE shall be also considered as ineligible [CB008927-C] if actRat1Alloc is set to FALSE [End CB008927-C].

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Init Tx | 1st ReTx | 2nd ReTx | 3rd ReTx | 4th ReTx | 5th ReTx |
| NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | ACK |
| 2 RBGs | 2 RBGs | 2 RBGs | 2 RBGs | 2 RBGs |  |
| 32 PRBs | 32 PRBs | 32 PRBs | 32 PRBs | 32 PRBs |  |

[CB008927-C] Else if actRat1Alloc is set to TRUE [End CB008927-C] [Before CB008927-C-UPD001] and incNumPrbsHarqRat1VoNR = 32 PRBs  [End CB008927-C-UPD001] [CB008927-C]. Here the initial PRBs were already at the limit, hence the augmenation cannot work here, so the number of PRBs remain same. This UE shall be also considered as ineligible.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Init Tx | 1st ReTx | 2nd ReTx | 3rd ReTx | 4th ReTx | 5th ReTx |
| NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | ACK |
| 32 PRBs | 32 PRBs | 32 PRBs | 32 PRBs | 32 PRBs |  |

[End CB008927-C]

Case3: if NRBTS/NRCELL/numTxPrbVoNRIncCodingGain = 2

NRBTS/NRCELL.maxPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain = 32

Cell is TDD FR1 100MHz (273 PRBs, RBG size=16)

NRBTS/NRCELL.stepUpNumPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain = 2 [Before CB008927-C-UPD001] [CB008927-C] if actRat1Alloc is set to FALSE [End CB008927-C] [End CB008927-C-UPD001]

Here the initial PRBs were already exceeding the maxPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain, hence the augmenation cannot work here, so the number of PRBs remain same. This UE shall be also considered as ineligible [CB008927-C] if actRat1Alloc is set to FALSE [End CB008927-C].

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Init Tx | 1st ReTx | 2nd ReTx | 3rd ReTx | 4th ReTx | 5th ReTx |
| NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | ACK |
| 3 RBGs | 3 RBGs | 3 RBGs | 3 RBGs | 3 RBGs |  |
| 48 PRBs | 48 PRBs | 48 PRBs | 48 PRBs | 48 PRBs |  |

[CB008927-C] Else if actRat1Alloc is set to TRUE [End CB008927-C] [Before CB008927-C-UPD001] and incNumPrbsHarqRat1VoNR = 48 PRBs [End CB008927-C-UPD001] [CB008927-C]

Here the initial PRBs were already exceeding the maxPrbsVoNRIncCodingGain, hence the augmenation cannot work here, so the number of PRBs remain same. This UE shall be also considered as ineligible.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Init Tx | 1st ReTx | 2nd ReTx | 3rd ReTx | 4th ReTx | 5th ReTx |
| NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | NACK | ACK |
| 48 PRBs | 48 PRBs | 48 PRBs | 48 PRBs | 48 PRBs |  |

[End CB008927-C]

[Before CB008927-C-UPD001] [CB008927-C]

Different simulation results for the performance of HARQ retransmission at different values of above NIDD parameters are available in : <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB008500%20-%20CB008999/CFAM/CB008927%20DL%20Resource%20Allocation%20Type%201/Simulations/VoNR%20Simulations/DL%20RAT1RAT0%20simulation%20for%20CB008927.pptx?d=w3d2681d36e7648ea9a0c2ae21b04b8af&csf=1&web=1&e=LZWmgQ>

[End CB008927-C] [End CB008927-C-UPD001]

---

#### 3.29.9.1.1-3 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation

##### 3.29.9.1.1-3.0-1 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Overview (ID: 11185393)

**3.29.9.1.1-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11185399`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
To increase the reliability of the retransmissions, additional power boost is applied to the PRBs used for eligible HARQ retransmissions (eligibility is defined as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25136\_replaced([11185404](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3tPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For such HARQ retransmissions, in addition to the PRBs  allocated for PDSCH , additional PRBs are reserved to power boost in a slot. gNB-DU shall not schedule any PDSCH on the PRBs reserved for power boost, instead share of the power corresponding to these PRBs used for power boost of an eligible retransmission, are spread over other allocated PRBs used for PDSCH allocation for the UE. PRBs allocated to power boost a UE shall not be used by any other UE’s of the cell in the slot.

Definitions

* PRBs for PDSCH allocation  is defined as the number of PRBs [CB008409-D], if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false, [End CB008409-D] in the RBGs that are allocated to the UE, corresponding to the FDRA(as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* powerBoostPrbs: PRBs used to power boost all the eligible UEs in the slot[As explained in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25138\_replaced([11185420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3t_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))].

---

##### 3.29.9.1.1-3.0-2 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Eligibility criteria to find the HARQ retransmissions that needs to be power boosted (ID: 11185404)

**3.29.9.1.1-3.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12728418`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
Eligibility criteria to schedule UE's HARQ retransmission with power boost shall satisfy:

1.  If NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlPowerBoost is set to DlVoNRPowerBoost,

- UE scheduled for retransmission with 5QI1/65/66 bearer (with DCI1\_1) data in the particular slot.

Or

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlPowerBoost is set to DlSrbPowerBoost,
