- UE scheduled for retransmission with SRB1/SRB2(with DCI1\_1) data in the particular slot.

Or

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlPowerBoost is set to DlVoNRAndSrbPowerBoost,

- UE scheduled for retransmission with 5QI1/65/66 bearer/SRB1/SRB2 (with DCI1\_1) **data in the particular slot.**

1. If the rrmDlUsedMcs for initial HARQ transmission is less than or equal-to,
2. rd64QamMcsThreshForPowerBoostEligibility defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25346\_replaced([11193828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwKs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  (if UE is using 64Qam MCS index Table), or
3. rd256QamMcsThreshForPowerBoostEligibility defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25347\_replaced([11193839](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwKtfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  (if UE is using 256Qam MCS indexTable).
4. rdLowSeMcsThreshForPowerBoostEligibility defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25561\_replaced([11193848](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwKt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  (if UE is using Low SE MCS indexTable).

[CB008949-A]

* rd1024QamMcsThreshForPowerBoostEligibility defined in [12728312: rd1024QamMcsThreshForPowerBoostEligibility](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DKfi4nHUEfCRE7eh4Ndkrw)  (if UE is using 1024Qam MCS indexTable).

[End CB008949-A]

**Rationale: It is intended that only the UEs at the cell edge with poor channel condition are eligible for power boost, hence the UEs with QPSK modulation are recommended for power boost.**

1. If this HARQ process has received successive NACKs/DTX greater then number of transmission attempts given by numTxThreshForPowerBoost. Where transmission attempts include the initial transmission which is not successful and following retransmission attempts.
2. Bi-directional slots are not considered. Please check 5G\_UP\_57\_replaced([11346935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIltvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for slot definition.
3. Slots blanked due to RF high temperature[5G\_UP\_ALG\_18889\_replaced([11175091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kugNk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))], are not considered.

**NRBTS/NRCELL/dlRetxPowerBoostCtl.numTxThreshForPowerBoost**

---

##### 3.29.9.1.1-3.0-3 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Resource allocation (ID: 11185420)

**3.29.9.1.1-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11185424`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
**Allocation of number of PRBs for PDSCH transmission of eligible HARQ = Number of** [Before CB008409-D]PRBs (RBG) **[CB008409-D] PRBs within RBGs if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc= FALSE,**\*\*\*\*

elseif NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc = FALSE, PRBs,

else NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc = TRUE, RBG (for the case dynamic switch RAT0) or PRB (for the case dynamic switch RAT1) **[End CB008409-D] allocated during  retransmission of the HARQ.**

**[CB008409-B-UPD001]**

Check if it's possible to perform  the retransmission with same TBS and MCS as initial transmission (such that number of PRBs (for PDSCH) and code rate used for retransmission is same as during initial HARQ transmission ), [PR829282] note that such re-transmission is not allowed in SSB slot with TRS and TRS slot [End PR829282]. If not, then the adaptive retransmission scheme is applied as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11948\_replaced([11184286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and reserved MCS could be used as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12911\_replaced([11184825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRpnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[End CB008409-B-UPD001]**

**Reserving number of PRBs****for power boost, over and above what is allocated for the PDSCH transmission as mentioned above, if available, is given by a scaling factor(configured by an O&M parameter powerBoostScalingFactor) relative to the number of PRBs allocated during the** [CB008409-B-UPD001] retransmission, and rounding up to nearest PRB. PRBs needed for power boosting shall be corrected based on the available FD resources. **[End CB008409-B-UPD001]Also, max number of PRBs that can be allocated per UE eligible for power boost, shall be capped against the configured value** [Before PR781598]maxPrbsForPowerBoost**[PR781598]maxPrbsIncludingPowerBoostPrbs** [End PR781598].***\*******\****\*\*

Availability of PRBs (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25085\_replaced([11177271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **[CB008409-D] and in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26756\_replaced([11176544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-kfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc =TRUE** [End CB008409-D]) shall depend upon the size of channel bandwidth, PRB blanking restrictions, PRBs for PDSCH allowed in a slot based on SSB, CSI-RS for beam management, CSI-RS for tracking. When such restrictions are met, the UE shall be allocated with as many PRBs possible, which may result in fewer PRBs than the configured value (i.e. number of PRBs that could be increased as per the configured value given by powerBoostScalingFactor).\*\*\*\*

If there are multiple eligible HARQ retransmission in a slot for power boost, then the PRBs for power boost are allocated  to the UE's with eligible HARQ retransmission in the same order as in CS1 list.

After allocation of PRBs for retransmission as described above, then for such retransmission in gNB-DU,

* *PdschTbTransmitPower, shall be calculated as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10176\_replaced([11118372](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqCkR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*

**NRBTS/NRCELL/dlRetxPowerBoostCtl.maxPrbsIncludingPowerBoostPrbs**

**NRBTS/NRCELL/dlRetxPowerBoostCtl.powerBoostScalingFactor**

Examples for clarification are demonstrated in following requirement [5G\_UP\_ALG\_24946\_replaced([11185429](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvS3ufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))].

---

##### 3.29.9.1.1-3.0-4 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Examples  of resource allocation (ID: 11185429)

**3.29.9.1.1-3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11185434`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
How PRBs used for power boost shall be calculated, when adaptive ReTxn is disabled?

---

**3.29.9.1.1-3.0-4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11185439`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
PRBs used for initial Txn = 8 PRBs = [CB008409-D] and if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc= FALSE [End CB008409-D] 2RBG(as RBG size = 4)

Multiplierfactor(powerBoostScalingFactor) for ReTxn = 1.2

[CB008409-B-UPD001] PRBs needed for retransmission = PRBs used for initialTxn [End CB008409-B-UPD001]

PRBs to be used for eligible retransmission with power boost = 8\* 1.2 = 9.6 PRBs = 10 PRBs

PRBs needed for power boost = ReTxn PRB with PowerBoost “minus” initial Txn PRBs = 10 - 8 = 2 PRBs

PRBs needed for PDSCH transmission = 8 PRB ([CB008409-D] and if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc= FALSE [End CB008409-D] 2 RBG), same as initial Txn.

---

**3.29.9.1.1-3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11185444`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
How to handle the retransmission when adaptive retransmission is applicable as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11953\_replaced([11184310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRplPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.29.9.1.1-3.0-4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11185447`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
PRBs used for initial Txn = 8 PRBs = [CB008409-D] and if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc= FALSE [End CB008409-D] 2RBG(as RBG size = 4)

Multiplierfactor(powerBoostScalingFactor) for ReTxn = 1.5

[Before CB008409-B-UPD001]

PRBs to be used for eligible retransmission with power boost = 8\* 1.5 = 12 PRBs

PRBs needed for power boost = ReTxn PRB with PowerBoost “minus” initial Txn PRBs = 12 - 8 = 4 PRBs

PRBs needed for PDSCH transmission = 8 PRB (2 RBG), same as initial Txn.

PRBs needed with adaptive retransmission = 6

If Only 7 PRBs remaining

 Then it’s not eligible for power boost, as available PRBs less then the PRBs used in initial transmission. Use Adaptive transmission scheme to schedule.

If only 10 PRBs remaining

 It’s eligible for power boost, as available PRBs more then used in initial transmission, even if power boost can be done with only 2 PRBs where the need was actualy of 4 PRBs

If only 15 PRBs remaining

 It’s eligible for power boost, as available PRBs more then used in initial transmission, also complete requirement of power boost PRBs as per powerBoostScalingFactor is available.

[CB008409-B-UPD001]

Assuming that retransmission can't be performed with same TBS as initial transmission (such that number of PRBs (for PDSCH) and code rate used for retransmission is same as during initial HARQ transmission )[5G\_UP\_ALG\_]25138

PRBs needed for adaptive retransmission = 6 => 8 PRB(2 RBG)

PRBs to be used for eligible retransmission with power boost = 8\* 1.5 = 12 PRBs

PRBs needed for power boost = ReTxn PRB with PowerBoost “minus” PRBs needed for adaptive retransmissio = 12 - 8 = 4 PRBs

PRBs needed for PDSCH transmission = 8 PRB (2 RBG), as needed for adaptive ReTxn.

If only 10 PRBs remaining

 Use adaptive ReTxn to schedule the Ue and 2 PRB to be used for power boost.

If only 12 PRBs remaining

* Use adaptive ReTxn to schedule the Ue and 4 PRB to be used for power boost.

[End CB008409-B-UPD001]

---

**3.29.9.1.1-3.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11185453`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
How to calculate the remaining resources while scheduling [Adaptive ReTxn disabled]

---

**3.29.9.1.1-3.0-4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11185458`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
Case 1

dlBandwidth = 100 PRBs

granted pooled PRB resources = 60 PRBs

non-granted pooled PRB resources = 40 PRBs

rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs = 100 (considering no PRB blanked)

Scheduling on a non-SSB/TRS slot

Multiplierfactor(powerBoostScalingFactor) for ReTxn = 2

UE1

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 20 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 20 PRBs to power boost

UE2

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 20 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 20 PRBs to power boost

Check condition if resources remaining to schedule next UE.

PRB allocated for all the UEs for PDSCH = 20(UE1) + 20(UE2) = 40 PRBs

PRBs reserved for Power boosting = 20(UE1) + 20(UE2)  = 40 PRBs

Legacy condition check: Remaining FD resources for PRB allocation for PDSCH = granted pooled PRB resources “minus” PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH = 60 – (20 + 20) = 20   [greater then ZERO]

CB008409 condition: If [(PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH + PRBs reserved for Power boosting) < rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs ]  = If (( 40 + 40) less then 100)= TRUE

As both the conditions above are true, another UE can be scheduled with

 Maximum 20 PRBs for physical PRB allocation for PDSCH

 Maximum 20 PRBs for sum of PRB allocation for PDSCH and power boost if the UE has any HARQ retransmission eligible  to power boost.

Note: There are many other conditions, for example PRBs remaining in  active BWP of the UE  check etc that is done in legacy, current example is just a simplification to show the additional check brought in by CB008409.

Case 2 [Granted pooled resources consumed by sacrificed PRBs]

dlBandwidth = 100 PRBs

granted pooled PRB resources = 90 PRBs

non-granted pooled PRB resources = 10 PRBs

rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs = 100 (considering no PRB blanked)

Scheduling on a non-SSB/TRS slot

Multiplierfactor(powerBoostScalingFactor) for ReTxn = 2

UE1

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 20 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 20 PRBs to power boost

UE2

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 20 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 20 PRBs to power boost

Check condition if resources remaining to schedule next UE.

PRB allocated for all the UEs for PDSCH = 20(UE1) + 20(UE2) = 40 PRBs

PRBs reserved for Power boosting = 20(UE1) + 20(UE2)  = 40 PRBs

Legacy condition check: Remaining FD resources for PRB allocation for PDSCH = granted pooled PRB resources “minus” PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH = 90 – (20 + 20) = 50   [greater then ZERO]

Additional condition check when power boost done in this slot: If [(PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH + PRBs for Power boosting) < rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs ]  = If (( 40 + 40) less then 100)= TRUE

As both the conditions above are true, another UE can be scheduled with

 Maximum 50 PRBs for PRB allocation for PDSCH

 Maximum 20 PRBs for sum of physical PRB allocation for PDSCH and power boost if the UE has any HARQ retransmission to power boost.

Here you could notice that the PRBs of power boost are mapped to granted pooled resources, as non-granted pooled resources are consumed. So eventhough  there are 50 PRBs that can be used for allocation to PDSCH, only 20 can be used, as 30 is consumed by sacrificed PRBs.

Note: There are many other conditions, for example PRBs remaining in  active BWP of the UE  check etc that is done in legacy, current example is just a simplification to show the additional check brought in by CB008409.

Case 3 [UE3 doesn’t get sufficient sacrificed PRB]

dlBandwidth = 100 PRBs

granted pooled PRB resources = 70 PRBs

non-granted pooled PRB resources = 30 PRBs

rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs = 100 (considering no PRB blanked)

Scheduling on a non-SSB/TRS slot

Multiplierfactor(powerBoostScalingFactor) for ReTxn = 1.5

UE1

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 20 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 10 PRBs to power boost

UE2

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 20 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 10 PRBs to power boost

Check condition if resources remaining to schedule next UE.

PRB allocated for all the UEs for PDSCH = 20(UE1) + 20(UE2) = 40 PRBs

PRBs reserved for Power boosting = 10(UE1) + 10(UE2)  = 20 PRBs

Legacy condition check: Remaining FD resources for PRB allocation for PDSCH = granted pooled PRB resources “minus” PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH = 70 – (20 + 20) = 30   [greater then ZERO]

Additional condition check when power boost done in this slot: If [(PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH + PRBs reserved for Power boosting) < rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs ]  = If (( 30 + 30) less then 100)= TRUE

As both the conditions above are true, another UE can be scheduled with

     Maximum 30 PRBs for physical PRB allocation for PDSCH

     Maximum (100 – (30 + 30) = 40) PRBs for sum of PRB allocation for PDSCH and power boost if the UE has any HARQ retransmission eligible  to power boost.

UE3

Initial transmission needs 30 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 30 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 15 PRBs to power boost

30 PRBs shall be allocated for PDSCH

Need 30 + 15 = 45 PRBs to accommodate fully the power boost PRBs, and remaining PRBs are only 40(as per calculated at the end of previous UE alloction). So only 10 PRBs is used for power boost.

So this UE gets PRB distribution as below,

PRBs for allocation of PDSCH = 30

PRBs reserved for power boost = 10

Scheduling summary

Total PRBs allocated for PDSCH in this slot = 20 + 20 + 30 = 70

Total PRBs allocated for power boost = 10 + 10 + 10 =  30

PRBs from non-granted pooled resource used for power boosting = 30 PRBs

PRBs from granted pooled resource used for power boosting = 0

Note: There are many other conditions, for example PRBs remaining in  active BWP of the UE  check etc that is done in legacy, current example is just a simplification to show the additional check brought in by CB008409.

Case 4 [Granted pooled resources can’t be used for PRB allocation as it is consumed by sacrificed PRBs, (power restriction)]

dlBandwidth = 100 PRBs

granted pooled PRB resources = 90 PRBs

non-granted pooled PRB resources = 10 PRBs

rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs = 100 (considering no PRB blanked)

Scheduling on a non-SSB/TRS slot

Multiplierfactor(powerBoostScalingFactor) for ReTxn = 2

UE1

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 20 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 20 PRBs to power boost

UE2

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 20 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 20 PRBs to power boost

Check condition if resources remaining to schedule next UE.

PRB allocated for all the UEs for PDSCH = 20(UE1) + 20(UE2) = 40 PRBs

PRBs reserved for Power boosting = 20(UE1) + 20(UE2)  = 40 PRBs

Legacy condition check: Remaining FD resources for PRB allocation for PDSCH = granted pooled PRB resources “minus” PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH = 90 – (20 + 20) = 50   [greater then ZERO]

Additional condition check when power boost done in this slot: If [(PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH + PRBs for Power boosting) < rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs ]  = If (( 40 + 40) less then 100)= TRUE

As both the conditions above are true, another UE can be scheduled with

     Maximum 50 PRBs for PRB allocation for PDSCH

     Maximum 20 PRBs for sum of physical PRB allocation for PDSCH and power boost if the UE has any HARQ retransmission to power boost.

Here you could notice that the PRBs of power boost are mapped to granted pooled resources, as non-granted pooled resources are consumed. So eventhough  there are 50 PRBs that can be used for allocation to PDSCH, only 20 can be used, as 30 is consumed by sacrificed PRBs.

UE3 (initial transmission, non-eligible for power boost)

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

So this UE gets PRB distribution as below,

PRBs for allocation of PDSCH = 20

PRBs reserved for power boost = 0

Check condition if resources remaining to schedule next UE.

PRB allocated for all the UEs for PDSCH = 20(UE1) + 20(UE2)  + 20(UE3)= 60 PRBs

PRBs reserved for Power boosting = 10(UE1) + 10(UE2)  + 0(UE3) = 20 PRBs

Legacy condition check: Remaining FD resources for PRB allocation for PDSCH = granted pooled PRB resources “minus” PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH = 90 – (20 + 20 + 20) = 30   [greater then ZERO]

Additional condition check when power boost done in this slot: If [(PRB allocation for all the UEs for PDSCH + PRBs for Power boosting) < rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs ]  = If (( 40 + 40 + 20) less then 100)= FALSE

As the second condition has failed, no more UEs can be scheduled even though there are 30 PRBs remaining in granted pooled resources, which could have been used for PDSCH allocation if they were not used for power boosting.

Scheduling summary

Total PRBs allocated for PDSCH in this slot = 20 + 20 + 20 = 60

Total PRBs allocated for power boost = 20 + 20 + 0 =  40

PRBs from non-granted pooled resource used for power boosting = 10 PRBs

PRBs from granted pooled resource used for power boosting = 30

Note: There are many other conditions, for example PRBs remaining in  active BWP of the UE  check etc that is done in legacy, current example is just a simplification to show the additional check brought in by CB008409.

Case 5 [Restriction due to maxPrbsIncludingPowerBoostPrbs]

dlBandwidth = 100 PRBs

granted pooled PRB resources = 90 PRBs

non-granted pooled PRB resources = 10 PRBs

rrmPrbPoolForPdschAndPowerboostPrbs = 100 (considering no PRB blanked)

Scheduling on a non-SSB/TRS slot

Multiplierfactor(powerBoostScalingFactor) for ReTxn = 2

maxPrbsIncludingPowerBoostPrbs = 32

UE1

Initial transmission needs 20 PRB.

HARQ retransmission needs 20 PRBs to schedule the PDSCH and 20 PRBs to power boost

As maximum number of PRBs that can be allocated to the eligible HARQ retransmission for power boost,maxPrbsIncludingPowerBoostPrbs is set as 32, hence only 12 PRBs can be used to power boost 20 PRBs allocated for PDSCH

So this UE gets PRB distribution as below,

PRBs for allocation of PDSCH = 20

PRBs reserved for power boost = 12

---

##### 3.29.9.1.1.0-1 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Overview (ID: 11184290)

**3.29.9.1.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11184305`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is TRUE, the adaptive retransmission algorithm shall be applied for the evaluation of the resources. The algorithm shall evaluate on slot basis the number of available physical channel bits for data transmission per PRB and accumulate the resources over all available PRBs. Guard symbols and resources occupied by e.g., DMRS, TRS, CSI-RS, CSI-IM or SRS and for the shared spectrum with LTE (DSS) LTE-CRS and LTE-PBCH shall not be used for the data transmission which reduces the number of useable resources differently per slot.

If the PRB pooling feature [CB012605-E](stream PRB based pooling, which is not used for eCPRI 7-2e based configurations)[End CB012605-E] is activated, the number of available PRBs before the first PRB assignment with adaptive retransmission shall correspond to the number of PRBs provided by the PRB pooling feature (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6500\_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB012605-E]

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, the required layer PRBs for the resource allocation with adaptive retransmission shall not be greater than the max layer PRBs allocated by the BBRM in every pooling period. The value is defined by rrmMaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB012605-E]

---

##### 3.29.9.1.1.0-2 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Definitions for adaptive retransmission algorithm (ID: 11184310)

**3.29.9.1.1.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `12688998`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
12688998:
Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
The following definitions shall be used for the resource allocation algorithm in adaptive retransmission.
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains two mathematical equations written in a sans-serif font.
The first equation is:
`rePerPRB_reTx = (12 x nbSymbols_reTx - N^TRS_RE,reTx - N^DMRS_RE,reTx - N^LTECRS_RE,reTx - N^RateMaching_RE,reTx - N^CSI-IM_RE,reTx)`
The second equation is:
`rePerPRB_ini = (12 x nbSymbols_ini - N^TRS_RE,ini - N^DMRS_RE,ini - N^LTECRS_RE,ini - N^RateMaching_RE,ini - N^CSI-IM_RE,ini)`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
The parameters [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N LTECRS RE,reTx". The text is in a mathematical notation, with "N" as a subscript to "LTECRS" and "RE,reTx". The font is sans-serif and bold. The image is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1], [image-2][image\_desc]N\_{RE,reTx}^{RateMachining}[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2], and [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a subscript format. The main term is "N", and the subscript is "CSI-IM" on the top and "RE,reTx" on the bottom.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] are assumed 0 for simplification, since the PRB location/position is unknown. For DSS case, it could be possible that [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRBmin" in a blurry, light gray font against a white background. The letters "PRB" are larger and bolder than the letters "min", which are smaller and appear as a subscript to "B".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]and [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB\_cand". The text is in a sans-serif font and is slightly blurry. The letters are black, and the background is white. The subscript "cand" is smaller than the other letters.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]are computed lower than the expected value (see below for the definition of both).
 
DL PTRS is disabled in FR1 TDD and FDD, so   [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains text that appears to be a logo or a watermark. The text is blurry and has a gradient effect, making it difficult to read clearly. The visible text seems to be "NPTRS" on the top line and "RE.inf" on the bottom line. The letters are in a sans-serif font. The background is white with some subtle color variations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] and [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N PTRS RE, reTx". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be a mathematical or scientific notation. The letters are slightly blurred, but the overall meaning is discernible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7] are removed.
 
The number of retransmission symbols allowed for the current slot shall be calculated based on the TDRA (defined as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2396\_replaced([11115520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpvCRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
The following inequalities are defined to derive the PRBcand and PRBmin, which are the number of PRBs required to meet the target and minimum number of PRBs for retransmission.
 
Inequality 1:
[image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula is presented on two lines.
The first line reads:
$PRB\_{cand} \times rePerPRB\_{reTx} \times Q\_{m,reTx} \times V\_{reTx} \geq (PRB\_{ini} \times rePerPRB\_{ini} \times Q\_{m,ini} \times V\_{ini}) \times$
The second line reads:
$targetPctResourceReservedMCS/100$
The formula appears to be a condition or an inequality used in a technical or scientific context, likely related to resource allocation or performance metrics in a communication system. The variables and subscripts suggest concepts like "Physical Resource Blocks" (PRB), "re-transmission" (reTx), "Quality of Service" (Q), and "Value" (V). The term "targetPctResourceReservedMCS" likely refers to a target percentage of reserved resources for a specific Modulation and Coding Scheme (MCS).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8]
 
Inequality 2:
[image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula is presented on two lines.
The first line reads:
$PRB\_{min} \times rePerPRB\_{reTx} \times Q\_{m,reTx} \times V\_{reTx} \geq (PRB\_{ini} \times rePerPRB\_{ini} \times Q\_{m,ini} \times V\_{ini}) \times$
The second line reads:
$minPctResourceReservedMCS/100$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9]
 
 
Metric 1 (derived from inequality 1;
[image-10][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula defines `PRB_cand` as the rounded-up value of an expression. The expression is a product of several terms: `PRB_ini`, a ratio of `rePerPRB_ini` to `rePerPRB_reTx`, a ratio of `Qm,ini` to `Qm,reTx`, a ratio of `V_ini` to `V_reTx`, and a fraction involving `targetPctResourceReservedMCS` divided by 100.
The formula is:
$PRB\_{cand} = \text{roundup}\left(\left(PRB\_{ini} \times \frac{rePerPRB\_{ini}}{rePerPRB\_{reTx}} \times \frac{Q\_{m,ini}}{Q\_{m,reTx}} \times \frac{V\_{ini}}{V\_{reTx}}\right) \times \frac{targetPctResourceReservedMCS}{100}\right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10]
 
Metric 2 (derived from inequality 2);
[image-11][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is for calculating `PRBmin`. It is defined as the rounded-up value of `PRBini` multiplied by several ratios. The ratios involve `rePerPRBini` and `rePerPRBreTx`, `Qm,ini` and `Qm,reTX`, and `Vini` and `VreTx`. Finally, it is multiplied by `minPctResourceReservedMCS` divided by 100.
The formula is:
`PRBmin = roundup((PRBini × (rePerPRBini / rePerPRBreTx) × (Qm,ini / Qm,reTX) × (Vini / VreTx) × (minPctResourceReservedMCS / 100)))`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11]
[image-12][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$PRB\_{MaxCodeRate} = \text{roundup} \left( \frac{TBS\_{ini} + CRC}{\text{maxCrReTransmissionDI} \times (\text{rePerPRB}*{reTx} - \text{Nxoh}) \times Q*{m,reTx} \times V\_{reTx}} \right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.013.png[/image\_path][/image-12]
Nxoh is the xOverhead parameter (38.214 §5.1.3.2) calculated according to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2201\_replaced([11325202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tsrfu_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The values supported for Nxoh are {0,6,12,18}. For Nxoh value used, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4639\_replaced([11192414](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvkkhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
[image-13][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRB\_cand". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in a dark color against a white background. The "B" is slightly larger than the "P" and "R", and the "cand" is in a smaller font size and appears to be a subscript to the "B".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.014.png[/image\_path][/image-13]: Candidate PRBs to be scheduled for the retransmission to fulfil Metric 1
[image-14][image\_desc]The image contains the text "PRBmin". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in a dark color, possibly black or dark blue, against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred, and there is a subtle gradient effect on the letters, giving them a somewhat three-dimensional appearance. The "min" is subscripted to the "B".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14210.015.png[/image\_path][/image-14]: Minimum PRBs to be scheduled for the retransmission to fulfil Metric 2

##### 3.29.9.1.1.0-3 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Maximum allowed code rate of adaptive retransmissions in DL (ID: 11184814)

**3.29.9.1.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11184819`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
[CB008662-A-UPD005]

The maximum allowed code rate of adaptive retransmissions in DL maxCrReTransmissionDl is defined differently depending on the HW architecture of the baseband card. For non-Loner based baseband cards the maximum allowed code rate is defined directly by the R&D parameter rdMaxCrReTransmissionDl (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12047\_replaced([11193470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the cell is served by a Loner based baseband card the maximum allowed code rate of the adaptive retransmission shall be set for all system bandwidths of TDD and FDD configurations to 0.95.

L1 hardware type is derived from the subcell configuration received from the C-Plane (note that for the same cell all the L1 subcells shall have the same L1 hardware)

[End CB008662-A-UPD005]

---

##### 3.29.9.1.1.0-4 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Resource allocation for adaptive retransmission (ID: 11184825)

**3.29.9.1.1.0-4.0-7**  (ID: `12816726`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
12816726:
Adaptive retransmission resource allocation
If conditions given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, gNB-DU shall calculate the PRBs (referenced as PRBtarget) for allocation of a retransmission in DL in the following steps.
 
The algorithm shall first find the PRBs to fulfil Metric1 while keeping the maximum code rate for retransmission limitation. If the maximum code rate for retransmission allows, and the available PRBs for scheduling are not enough to fulfil Metric1, the algorithm shall allocate remaining amount of resources that fulfil Metric2. Furthermore, the algorithm shall check the available resources if the DL baseband pooling is activated. UEs, which are blocked in scheduling with its adaptive retransmission due to the reduced number of available PRB, shall be taken out of the scheduling set and the corresponding HARQ process shall be freed. The retransmission shall be postponed, if the minimum number of PRBs to fulfil Metric2 are not available for scheduling.
If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, **if the value of PRBmaxCodeRate (as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11953\_replaced([11184310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRplPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is calculated to be more than initial transmission PRBs and resource elements per PRB are same for initial (rePerPrbini) and retransmission (rePerPrbreTx), gNB-DU shall assign the value of initial transmission PRBs to PRBmaxCodeRate.**
**Rationale: In the calculation of PRBs for adaptive retransmission, due to simplifications on TRS symbols evaluation and the maximum code rate restriction for retransmission, the calculated number of PRBs can be greater than the initial transmission PRBs due to rounding of float number. gNB-DU shall perform a check to mitigate the issue created by this rounding.  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12913\_replaced([11202887](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwd8aPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and PR709174).**
[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is:
$PRB\_{min} = max(PRB\_{maxcodeverate}, PRB\_{min})$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
If [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality. The text in the image is: PRB\_cand <= PRB\_min.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
               [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a white background. The equation is: PRB\_cand = PRB\_min. The text is in a dark color, likely black or dark blue. The font is sans-serif and appears to be in a bold style. The subscript 'cand' is written below 'B' in 'PRB', and the subscript 'min' is written below 'B' in the second 'PRB'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
Endif
 
If actDlMuMimoEnh = TRUE and the UE with an adaptive reTx is eligible for DL MU MIMO pairing (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7454\_replaced([11142605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
 If [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "PRB\_cand <= BWP". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in a dark color against a light background. The expression is written horizontally.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
 [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation reads: PRB\_target = PRB\_cand. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
 Elseif slowPrbPoolingAllowed = TRUE or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = TRUE and the UE is scheduled in a DL slot without SSB or TRS and the number of DL OFDM symbols of current slot is identical to the initial transmission slot
  If [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "PRBmin > BWPBBPooling". The text is in a dark color against a light background. The letters are clear and legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]
   UE shall be taken out of scheduling and HARQ process as well as dlHarqBufferIndex shall be freed
  Endif
 Elseif slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE [PR882465]and the UE is scheduled in a DL slot without SSB or TRS and the number of DL OFDM symbols of current slot is identical to the initial transmission slot[End PR882465]
  If [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the following text: PRBmin \* Rank > rrm.MaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]
   UE shall be taken out of scheduling and HARQ process as well as dlHarqBufferIndex shall be freed
  Endif
 Else
  Postpone the retransmission
 Endif 
Else
 If [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "PRB\_cand <= BWP and PRB\_remaining". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The expression uses standard mathematical notation with subscripts and comparison operators.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]

 Elseif [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in a light gray font on a white background. The expression is:
$PRB\_{min} \le BWP \text{ and } PRB\_{remaining}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.009.png[/image\_path][/image-9]

$PRB\_{target} = PRB\_{remaining}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14220.010.png[/image\_path][/image-10]
 Elseif slowPrbPoolingAllowed = TRUE or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = TRUE and the UE is scheduled in a DL slot without SSB or TRS and the number of DL OFDM symbols of current slot is identical to the initial transmission slot

rrmMaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot = Layer PRB capacity provided by the BBRM in the current pooling period (refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, this number for DCI 1\_1 PDSCH grants is the number of remaining PRBs inside the largest BWP or a dedicated BWP within the PDSCH sub area selected and for DCI 1\_0 allocations it is the **number of remaining PRBs in the CORESET0 within a PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23464\_replaced([11176110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall check this number of remaining PRBs for any PRBs reservation through the list of constraints described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23481\_replaced([11176397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kup-g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**
Note about DCI 1\_0 retransmission:
- if the DCI 1\_0 retransmission in CSS mode is the first one DCI 1\_0 transmission (initial or retransmission) in CSS mode in the TD scheduler list, gNB-DU shall check the creation of a PDSCH sub area (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23607\_replaced([11176609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in order to allow this PRBs reservation. If the check fails, gNB-DU shall postpone the DCI 1\_0 retransmission in CSS mode.
-  if the DCI 1\_0 retransmission in CSS mode is scheduled in the same slot than a common channel (SIB1, SI, PAGING, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the CORESET #0 is split into 2 PDSCH sub areas (for details see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23623\_replaced([11175867](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23558\_replaced([11176156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuoJXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and gNB-DU shall select one of the 2 PDSCH sub areas within the CORESET #0 in order to allocate the DCI 1\_0 according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23683\_replaced([11176637](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
- if the DCI 1\_0 in CSS mode are not mapped on CORESET #0 but on a CORESET X (with X>0) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23452\_replaced([11175855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumUKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_10912\_replaced([11350814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubo8BvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall handle the CORESET X (with a PRB area for the DCI 1\_0 allocations equal to the size of the CORESET #0, see 5G\_UP\_10874\_replaced([11350654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubnt5vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the same way than the CORESET #0. The note about the check of a PDSCH sub area creation is valid for CORESET X case too.

In TRS slots when ***\*[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = false* \*[CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = false (note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator)** [End CB015668-A]***\**\*** or in CSI-IM slots when CSI-IM is rate matched (that is if **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt = true and NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = true** [CB015668-A] actTddCsiRsOpt = true and actTddCsiImRm = true (note: actTddCsirsOpt and actTddCsiImRm are not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]*****\*)*\*, if FR1 TDD FD beamforming (NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True), gNB-DU shall grant any PDSCH grant as a multiple of a PRB granularity (for PRB\_GRANULARITY definition, see 5G\_UP\_11022\_replaced([11350898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*****\*

## 3.29.10 Bearer Prioritization and Multiplexing

### 3.29.10.1 Priority handling between logical channels of one UE

#### 3.29.10.1.0-1 Priority handling between logical channels of one UE (ID: 11185475)

**3.29.10.1.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `13365821`)

Priority handling between logical channels of one UE
gNB-DU shall allocate DL TB resources to the UE's logical channels (SRBs, DRBs). gNB-DU shall follow these rules:

- A sufficient amount of resources shall be reserved for MAC CEs which have to be included in the same DL TB.

- The remaining resources shall be allocated to a single or to multiple logical channels (multiplexing) according to the request class priority of each bearer.

- The DL TB shall be filled as much as possible.

To fulfill these rules, gNB-DU shall execute the following steps.

Step 1: gNB-DU shall initialize rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes to the TB size (TBS). This is a variable for the available number of bytes in the DL transport block. **Note that for the user with SRB traffic the DL TBS calculated correspond to minimum number of FD resources that can contain the amount of buffered data for SRB traffic alone (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4502\_replaced ([11179653](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and does not consider any other bearer the UE might have. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4509\_replaced ([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Step2: gNB-DU shall subtract the number of bytes required for MAC CEs in the same DL TB from rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes. Therefore, 5G-L2-PS shall consider the 5G-MAC sub header size, given in 5G\_L2\_1970\_replaced ([11215653](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-5mSPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and the MAC CE payload size, given in 5G\_L2\_1971\_replaced ([11215683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-5mTfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If MAC CEs need to multiplex with MAC PDU, please refer 5G\_L2\_315\_replaced ([11149629](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24W5cfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to check the sequence.

For each of the following steps if data exists for an SRB/ [CB014699-B] critical SRB [End CB014699-B] or DRB/ [CB014699-B] critical DRB [End CB014699-B], after allocating resources to the SRB/ critical SRB or DRB/Critical DRB subtract the number of bytes allocated from rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes.  Continue until rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes equals 0 or all of the UE’s SRBs and DRB buffers have been drained. **Note: With RBGs used for FD resource allocation as per RAT0, it is possible to have PRBs within the RBG, that constitute the free bytes inside the DL TBS which can be used to multiplex other bearers the UE might have, but with FD resource allocation as per RAT1 (NIDD.actRat1Alloc = True) the DL TBS calculated correspond exactly to the minimum number of FD resources that can contain the amount of buffered data for SRB traffic alone (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4502\_replaced([11179653](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and hence cannot provide free bytes for further multiplexing.**

[CB014699-B]

Step 3: gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's critical SRB1**and critical 5QI5/69 DRBs, see [11185513](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvTezvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). Note: This existing requirement is equally applied for critical SRB1 or Critical 5QI-5/69.**

Step 4: gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's critical voipTraffic request class (refer to [11185562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUF1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note: This existing requirement is equally applied for critical 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB014699-B].

Step 5: [CB014699-C] gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's DRBs with criticalGbrTraffic request class (refer to [11179713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB014699-C].

Step 6: [CB014699-F] gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's DRBs with criticalDrbTraffic request class (refer to [13404773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rJo3eQyeEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB014699-F].

Step 7: gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's SRBs **and 5QI5/69 DRBs - see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4543\_replaced ([11185517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvTez_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Step 8: gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's dcGbrTraffic request class - see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30517\_replaced ([11185540](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUF0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 9: gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's DRBs with 5QI1/65/66  voipTraffic request class - see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4545\_replaced ([11185562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUF1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 10:  gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's DRB with 5QI1/65/66 deprioritizedVoip request class - see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4545\_replaced([11185562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUF1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 11: gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's DRBs with gbrTraffic request class - see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8033\_replaced([11179713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 12: gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UE's NBR category non-GBR DRBs within drbTraffic request class - see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced ([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 13: gNB-DU shall allocate resources for the UEs with congestion deprioritized GBR DRBs**, [CB014699-F] criticalDrbTraffic class for critical non-GBR,**[CB014699-F] non-GBR DRBs with the drbTraffic**or dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class, and/or Deprioritized GBR/[CB014699-C] Deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-C] DRBs, and/or deprioritizedGbr traffic request class. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4550\_replaced ([11185672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUs7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**\*\*\*\*

*Note:  A UE may be configured with up to two 5QI1 bearers and two 5QI5 bearers in case of emergency calls**. If NRCELL.actMcQosSupport is set to True, a UE may be configured with up to two voice bearers: 5QI1 bearer with 5QI5 for SIP signaling and 5QI65/66 bearer with 5QI69 for SIP signaling.*\*\*

---

### 3.29.10.2 Resource allocation to signaling bearers of the UE

#### 3.29.10.2.0-1 Resource allocation to signaling bearers of the UE (ID: 11185517)

**3.29.10.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13387297`)

Resource allocation to signaling bearers of the UE
When requested in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10510\_replaced([11185475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvTexfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G-L2-PS shall allocate DL TB resources to the UE's SRBs and 5QI5 5QI5/69 DRB, provided that resources are still available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes > 0). 5G-L2-PS shall serve the SRBs in the order of their priorities. The order is:

1) SRB1 (logicalChannelIdentity = 1).

2) SRB3 (logicalChannelIdentity = 3).

3) SRB2 (logicalChannelIdentity = 2).

4) 5QI5 **or 5QI69 bearer(s) starting with DRB with oldest data in buffer**

*Note: SRB2 has a lower priority than SRB3 even though the numbers 2 and 3 suggest the opposite.*

One by one, 5G-L2-PS shall allocate resources to all SRBs as described below. SRBs without pending data can be skipped. If no more resources are available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = 0) the process shall be stopped.

* Determine the amount of data to be allocated as:

5G-L2-PS shall always allocate enough bytes to transmit RLC status PDUs with 1 transmission because they can't be segmented. 5G-L2-PS know whether an RLC status PDU is pending and also the length of RLC status PDU in L2-LO via LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd. While 5G-L2-PS want to schedule an initial data transmission for RLC status PDU with LowSE MCS table, rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes shall be set as the value of the length of RLC status PDU + Mac header.

5G-L2-PS shall allocate enough data for transmitting the RLC status PDU within one TB, because the RLC status PDU cannot be segmented. 5G-L2-PS shall know the length of the RLC status PDU. In case the initial data transmission of RLC status PDU is triggered, the rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes size shall be set the as the value of the size of RLC status PDU + MAC header.

If the size of rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes allows, 5G-L2-PS shall allocate enough bytes to transmit the SRB's remaining data in DL buffer with a single transmission. Therefore, 5G-L2-PS shall allocate an extra rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes (see definition below) for MAC and RLC headers which might have been added to the DL buffer in 5G-L2-LO due to RLC segmentations in the previous slot (if scs = 15 or 30) or in the 2 previous slots (if scs = 120). This compensates an inaccuracy of bufferedBytes[i,j], which can be up to rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes smaller than the buffer level in 5G-L2-LO since 5G-L2-PS receives LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd with 1 slot (if scs = 15 or 30) or 2 slots (if scs = 120) delay.

5G-L2-PS shall proceed as follows:

IF rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes > rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes

Stop and keep the pointer at the current DRB.

ELSE

IF rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes >= bufferedBytes[i,j] + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes

amountBytes[i,j] = bufferedBytes[i,j] + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes

ELSE

{

amountBytes[i,j] = min{ bufferedBytes[i,j],  rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes }

}

amountBytes[i,j] = max{ amountBytes[i,j], rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes }

With:

* j is the C-RNTI of the UE.
* i is the logicalChannelIdentity of the SRB.
* bufferedBytes[i,j] is the pending data volume of the SRB, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes = 8 (if scs = 15 or 30) or 16 (if scs = 120). For scs = 15 or 30 it is the sum of the maximum MAC subheader size (3 bytes, see 38.321 Section 6.1.2) and the maximum RLC header size (5 bytes, see 38.322 Section 6.2.2.4). For scs = 120 the number is doubled because LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd is received 1 slot later than with scs = 15 or 30.
* rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28749\_replaced([11187059](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvYXQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Update the buffer level of the SRB as:

bufferedBytes[i,j] = bufferedBytes[i,j] - amountBytes[i,j]

* Update the amount of available bytes in the DL TB as:

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes - amountBytes[i,j]

* Proceed with next SRB.

[CB014699-B] Note: This functionality of resource allocation to critical SRB or critical 5QI-5/69 is equally applied [End CB014699-B]

---

### 3.29.10.3 Resource allocation to DC GBR bearers

#### 3.29.10.3.0-1 Resource allocation to DC GBR bearers (ID: 11185546)

**3.29.10.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11185548`)

Resource allocation to DC GBR bearers
gNB-DU shall allocate DL TB resources to the UE's DC GBR DRBs in the dcGbrTraffic request class with pending data, provided that resources are still available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes > 0), before serving any other DRB.

One by one, gNB-DU shall allocate resources to the DC GBR DRBs in the dcGbrTraffic request class as described below, starting with the DRB with the highest TD scheduling criteria.  If no more resources are available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = 0) the process shall be stopped.

Determine the amount of data to be scheduled for the DRB as follows:

If the size of rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes allows, gNB-DU shall allocate enough bytes to transmit the DRB's data in DL buffer according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30511\_replaced([11179689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L2-PS shall proceed as follows:

If (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes >= dcGbrDesiredBytes[i,j])  Then

amountBytes[i,j] = dcGbrDesiredBytes[i,j]

Else

amountBytes[i,j] = rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes

where:

amountBytes[i,j]                    amount of data scheduled

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes        remaining resources available in the DL TB related to the UE.

dcGbrDesiredBytes[i,j]            desired amount of data to schedule, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30511\_replaced([11179689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

with:

i          index of the UE

j          index of the DC GBR DRB to allocate resources to

Update buffer level of the DC GBR DRB as:

bufferedBytes[i,j] = max{bufferedBytes[i,j] - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

Update available bytes in the DL TB as:

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = max{rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

Proceed with next DC GBR DRB.

---

### 3.29.10.4 Resource allocation to 5QI1 bearers

#### 3.29.10.4.0-1 Resource allocation to 5QI1 bearers L2-PS shall allocate resources to the UE's 5QI1 DRBs when resources are available (ID: 11185562)

**3.29.10.4.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11185584`)

Resource allocation to 5QI1 bearers
gNB-DU shall allocate DL TB resources to the UE's [Before CB008118-A]5QI1[CB008118-A]5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] DRBs with pending data, provided that resources are still available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes > 0), before serving any other DRB.

One by one, gNB-DU shall allocate resources to all [Before CB008118-A]5QI1[CB008118-A]5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] DRBs as described below, starting with DRB with oldest data in its buffer. DRBs without pending data can be skipped. If no more resources are available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = 0) the process shall be stopped.

 Determining the amount of data to be scheduled for the DRB as follows:

If the size of rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes allows, gNB-DU shall allocate enough bytes to transmit the DRB's remaining data in DL buffer with a single transmission. Therefore, gNB-DU shall allocate an extra rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes (see definition below) for MAC and RLC headers which might have been added to the DL buffer in 5G-L2-LO due to RLC segmentations in the previous slot (if scs = 15 or 30) or in the 2 previous slots (if scs = 120). This compensates an inaccuracy of bufferedBytes[i,j], which can be up to rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes smaller than the buffer level in 5G-L2-LO since 5G-L2-PS receives LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd with 1 slot (if scs = 15 or 30) or 2 slots (if scs = 120) delay.

gNB-DU shall proceed as follows:

IF (rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes > rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes)

Stop and proceed with next logical channels

ELSE IF (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes >= bufferedBytes[i,j] + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes)

amountBytes[i,j] = bufferedBytes[i,j] + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes

ELSE

amountBytes[i,j] = min{ bufferedBytes[i,j],  rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes }

with:

amountBytes[i,j] is the amount of data to be scheduled in bytes for the DRB j of the 5G UE i.

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes represents the remaining resources available in the DL TB related to the UE.

bufferedBytes[i,j] is the pending data volume of the DRB, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes = 7 (if scs = 15 or 30) or 14 (if scs = 120). For scs = 15 or 30 it is the sum of the maximum MAC subheader size (3 bytes, see 38.321 Section 6.1.2) and the maximum RLC header size for RLC UM (4 bytes, see 38.322 Section 6.2.2.4). For scs = 120 the number is doubled because LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd is received 1 slot later than with scs = 15 or 30.

rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes = 0, assuming no maximum VoIP segmentation is enforced with RBG allocation if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to FALSE, else PRB allocation if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to TRUE, Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28749\_replaced([11187059](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvYXQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for original definition of rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes.

 Update buffer level of the [Before CB008118-A]5QI1[CB008118-A]5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] DRB as:

bufferedBytes[i,j] = max{bufferedBytes[i,j] - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

 Update available bytes in the DL TB as:

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = max{rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

* Calculate allocated [Before CB008118-A]5QI1[CB008118-A]5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] PDSCH PRB as:

IF (amountBytes[i,j] > 0)

[Before CB008118-A]

rrm5QI1Prb[i] = MAX(1, numOfPrb \* amountBytes[i,j] / TBS);

ELSE

rrm5QI1Prb[i] = 0;

[ CB008118-A]

rrm5QI1/65/66Prb[i] = MAX(1, numOfPrb \* amountBytes[i,j] / TBS);

ELSE

rrm5QI1/65/66Prb[i] = 0;

[End CB008118-A]

where:

* numOfPrb,is the number of allocated PDSCH PRB as filled to DlData\_PdschSendReq.
* TBS, is the total allocated TBS of UE[i], which is the initial value of rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes.

PM counter PRB\_USED\_PDSCH\_5QI1 is calculated based on rrm5QI1Prb of the cell, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6272\_replaced([11230269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kytYEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for counter definition.

[CB008118-A]Note: PM counter: DL\_PRB\_USED\_DATA\_PMQAP configured for 5QI65/66 and should be updated in the same way as 5QI1 specific counter mentioned above.[End CB008118-A].

Proceed with next [Before CB008118-A]5QI1 [CB008118-A]5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] DRB.

---

### 3.29.10.5 Resource allocation to Non-5QI-1 GBR bearers

#### 3.29.10.5.0-1 Resource allocation to Non-5QI-1 GBR bearers (ID: 11185595)

**3.29.10.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11185605`)

Resource allocation to Non-5QI-1 GBR bearers
5G-L2-PS shall allocate DL TB resources to the UE's [Before CB008118-A] non-5QI1 [CB008118-A] non-5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] GBR DRBs in the gbrTraffic request class with pending data, provided that resources are still available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes > 0), before serving any other DRB.

One by one, 5G-L2-PS shall allocate resources to the GBR DRBs in the gbrTraffic request class as described below, starting with the DRB with the highest TD scheduling criteria.  If no more resources are available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = 0) the process shall be stopped.

Determine the amount of data to be scheduled for the DRB as follows:

If the size of rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes allows, 5G-L2-PS shall allocate enough bytes to transmit the DRB's data in DL buffer according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8033\_replaced([11179713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L2-PS shall proceed as follows:

If (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes >= gbrDesiredBytes[i,j])  Then

amountBytes[i,j] = gbrDesiredBytes[i,j]

Else

amountBytes[i,j] = rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes

where:

amountBytes[i,j]                    amount of data scheduled

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes        remaining resources available in the DL TB related to the UE.

gbrDesiredBytes[i,j]               desired amount of data to schedule, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8033\_replaced([11179713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

with:

i          index of the UE

j          index of the GBR DRB to allocate resources to

Update buffer level of the GBR DRB as:

bufferedBytes[i,j] = max{bufferedBytes[i,j] - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

Update available bytes in the DL TB as:

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = max{rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

Proceed with next GBR DRB.

---

### 3.29.10.6 Resource allocation to Deprioritized GBR bearers

#### 3.29.10.6.0-1 Resource allocation to Deprioritized GBR bearers (ID: 11185617)

**3.29.10.6.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13387289`)

Resource allocation to Deprioritized GBR bearers
For the non-GBR resource allocation requirements, including the WRR algorithm that allocates the UE’s FD resources **( RBG or PRB)** to the UE’s DRBs (inner loop WRR), the term “non-GBR” shall be interpreted as “non-GBR or deprioritized GBR [CB014699-C] or deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-C]”.\*\*\*\*

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True, the deprioritizedGbr DRBs are treated the same way as non-GBR DRBs and the slice aware InnerWRR algorithm is used for scheduling non-GBR and deprioritized GBR DRBs per UE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_11918\_replaced([11185800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvV7BvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Implementation note:  The UE's WRR DRB list should be updated to include the GBR bearer when the GBR bearer is added to the UE.  The GBR bearer, [CB014699-C] critical GBR bearer [End CB014699-C] will have a scheduling weight = rdDepriorGbrSchedulWeight.  When the UE is scheduled, if the GBR bearer is not classified as deprioritized GBR [CB014699-C] OR critical GBR bearer is not classified as critical deprioritized GBR [End CB014699-C] then the GBR bearer is skipped just like any other bearer would be skipped that does not have data in the buffer.  If the GBR/ [CB014699-C] critical GBR [End CB014699-C] bearer is classified as deprioritized GBR and does contain data then it will be given resources as part of the UE’s inner loop WRR algorithm.

---

### 3.29.10.7 Resource allocation to Deprioritized DC GBR bearers

#### 3.29.10.7.0-1 Resource allocation to Deprioritized DC GBR bearers (ID: 11185636)

**3.29.10.7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11185640`)

Resource allocation to Deprioritized DC GBR bearers
For the non-GBR resource allocation requirements, including the WRR algorithm that allocates the UE’s FD resources (RBG or PRB) **to the UE’s DRBs (inner loop WRR), the term “non-GBR” shall be interpreted as “non-GBR or deprioritized DC GBR”.**

Implementation note: The UE's WRR DRB list should be updated to include the DC GBR bearer when the DC GBR bearer is added to the UE. The DC GBR bearer will have  a scheduling weight = rdDepriorGbrSchedulWeight. When the UE is scheduled if the DC GBR bearer is not classified as deprioritized DC GBR then the DC GBR bearer is skipped just like any other bearer would be skipped that does not have data in the buffer.  If the DC GBR bearer is classified as deprioritized DC GBR and does contain data then it will be given resources as part of the UE’s inner loop WRR algorithm.

---

### 3.29.10.8 Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers

#### 3.29.10.8.1 Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers with positive NBR token bucket

##### 3.29.10.8.1.0-1 Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers with positive NBR token bucket (ID: 11185658)

**3.29.10.8.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11185661`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers with positive NBR token bucket
If UE belongs to drbTraffic class, 5G-L2-PS shall allocate DL TB resources to the UE's NBR configured non-GBR DRBs with positive token bucket having pending data, provided that resources are still available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes > 0)..

One by one, 5G-L2-PS shall allocate resources to the NBR configured non-GBR DRBs with positive token bucket described below, starting with the NBR configured non-GBR DRB with the highest rrmNbrTokenBucket (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22083\_replaced([11175539](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuj34fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  If no more resources are available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = 0), the process shall be stopped.

Determine the amount of data to be scheduled for the NBR DRB i as follows:

amountBytes[i,j] = min(nbrDesiredBytes[i,j], rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes)

where:

amountBytes   amount of data scheduled for the NBR bearer as part of NBR positive token bucket category. The same NBR DRB may still be allocated in further step too.

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes  remaining resources available in the DL TB related to the UE.

nbrDesiredBytes   Tx bytes computed for NBR bearer j of UE i as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21689\_replaced([11179765](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ERvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

with:

i          index of the UE

j          index of the NBR configured Non-GBR DRB with positive token bucket to allocate resources to

Update buffer level of the NBR DRB as:

bufferedBytes[i,j] = max{bufferedBytes[i,j] - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

Update available bytes in the DL TB as:

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = max{rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

Proceed with next NBR configured non-GBR DRB with rrmNbrTokenBucket > 0.

---

#### 3.29.10.8.2 Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)

##### 3.29.10.8.2.0-1 Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket) Scheduling Data Volume Calculation for Selected non-GBR DRBs (ID: 11185672)

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11185677`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Flowchart

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11185686`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
11185686:
Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
The main structure of the algorithm of scheduling data volume calculation for a UE’s DRBs is as follows:

Check preconditions for DRB scheduling (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10550\_replaced([11185692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

IF (rrmSchedFinished = TRUE)

RETURN

Initialize the selected DRB (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10553\_replaced([11185702](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

WHILE (rrmSchedFinished = FALSE) {

Serve the selected DRB (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10555\_replaced([11185711](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

IF (rrmSchedFinished = TRUE)

BREAK

Update the selected DRB (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10558\_replaced([11185734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

}

Save DRB for next scheduling (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10560\_replaced([11185743](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT-_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

With:

* rrmSchedFinished: whether scheduling data volume calculation for the UE’s DRBs has been finished.

The flowchart of the algorithm is as follows:[vsd\_desc]=== Diagram Content ===
Start
Check preconditions for DRB scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10550)
rrmSchedFinished?
Initialize the selected DRB (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10553)

True

False
Serve the selected DRB (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10555)
rrmSchedFinished?

Save DRB for next scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10560)
End

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11185692`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Check preconditions for DRB scheduling

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11185697`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
When requested in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10510\_replaced([11185475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvTexfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G-L2-PS shall allocate DL TB resources to the UE's non-GBR DRBs, provided that resources are still available in the DL TB (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes > 0).

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True,

* 5G-L2-PS shall use inner weighted round-robin algorithm introduced in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7536\_replaced([11185805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvV7B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to determine non-GBR DRB to be scheduled,

Else

* 5G-L2-PS shall use weighted round-robin algorithm introduced in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to determine non-GBR DRB to be scheduled.

IF (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes > 0)

rrmSchedFinished = FALSE

ELSE

rrmSchedFinished = TRUE

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11185702`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Initialize the selected DRB
===========================

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11185707`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
5G-L2-PS shall allocate resources to selected non-GBR DRB as described below.

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True,

* the selected non-GBR DRB is determined from inner weighted round-robin algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7536\_replaced([11185805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvV7B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), whose logical channel identity is saved by 5G-L2-PS as the DRB to be scheduled;

Else

* the selected non-GBR DRB is determined from weighted round-robin algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), whose logical channel identity is saved by 5G-L2-PS as the DRB to be scheduled or the pointer is pointing to (depending on implementation options in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11185711`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Serve the selected DRB

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11185728`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Serve the DRB if the data of the selected DRB and the remaining resources of the TB are available (a DRB without data for transmission can be skipped for the step)

 Determining the amount of data to be scheduled for the DRB as following:

[Before PR765688-24R3]

5G-L2-PS shall always allocate enough bytes to transmit RLC status PDUs with 1 transmission because they can't be segmented. Since 5G-L2-PS doesn't know whether an RLC status PDU or data PDU is pending in 5G-L2-LO, it shall always allocate a minimum number of bytes (see rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes below). This avoids allocations which are too small and hence impossible to transmit and it preserves the correctness of the buffer level in 5G-L2-PS. 5G-L2-PS won't subtract a number of bytes from the buffer level which can't be transmitted and which are in fact still pending in the DL buffer in 5G-L2-LO.

5G-L2-PS shall always allocate enough bytes to transmit RLC status PDUs with 1 transmission because they can't be segmented. 5G-L2-PS know whether an RLC status PDU is pending and also the length of RLC status PDU in L2-LO via LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd. While 5G-L2-PS want to schedule an initial data transmission for RLC status PDU with LowSE MCS table, rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes shall be set as the value of the length of RLC status PDU + Mac header.

[PR765688-24R3]

5G-L2-PS shall allocate enough data for transmitting the RLC status PDU within one TB, because the RLC status PDU cannot be segmented. 5G-L2-PS shall know the length of the RLC status PDU. In case the initial data transmission of RLC status PDU is triggered, the rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes size shall be set the as the value of the size of RLC status PDU + MAC header.

[End PR765688-24R3]

If the size of rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes allows, 5G-L2-PS shall allocate enough bytes to transmit the DRB's remaining data in DL buffer with a single transmission. Therefore, 5G-L2-PS shall allocate an extra rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes (see definition below) for MAC and RLC headers which might have been added to the DL buffer in 5G-L2-LO due to RLC segmentations in the previous slot (if scs = 15 or 30) or in the 2 previous slots (if scs = 120). This compensates an inaccuracy of bufferedBytes[i,j], which can be up to rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes smaller than the buffer level in 5G-L2-LO since 5G-L2-PS receives LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd with 1 slot (if scs = 15 or 30) or 2 slots (if scs = 120) delay.

5G-L2-PS shall proceed as follows:

rrmSchedFinished = FALSE

IF (rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes > rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes) {

rrmSchedFinished = TRUE

Keep logical channel identity of the current DRB as selected DRB or keep the pointer at the current DRB

}

ELSE

{

IF (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes >= bufferedBytes[i,j] + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes)

{

amountBytes[i,j] = bufferedBytes[i,j] + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes

}

ELSE

{

amountBytes[i,j] = min{ bufferedBytes[i,j],  rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes }

}

amountBytes[i,j] = max{ amountBytes[i,j], rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes}

}

with:

amountBytes[i,j] is the amount of data to be scheduled in bytes for the DRB j of the 5G UE i.

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes represents the remaining resources available in the DL TB related to the UE.

bufferedBytes[i,j] is the pending data volume of the DRB, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If rrmBufferStatusSplitNeeded == 1, for CA UEs L2-PS shall consider the buffer status split as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2846\_replaced([11122312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqepKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to calculate the amount of bytes to be transmitted from each DRB, instead of bufferedBytes.

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes = 8 (if scs = 15 or 30) or 16 (if scs = 120). For scs = 15 or 30 it is the sum of the maximum MAC subheader size (3 bytes, see 38.321 Section 6.1.2) and the maximum RLC header size (5 bytes, see 38.322 Section 6.2.2.4). For scs = 120 the number is doubled because LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd is received 1 slot later than with scs = 15 or 30.

[Before PR765688-24R3]

rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes = 26 is the minimum allocation size. It includes 2 bytes for a MAC subheader plus 24 bytes for an RLC PDU. The latter is large enough for an RLC status PDU with 1 ACK (3 bytes) plus 3 NACK SN segments (7 bytes), see 38.322, Section 6.2.2.5. It is assumed that larger RLC status PDUs don't occur.

[PR765688-24R3]

rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28749\_replaced([11187059](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvYXQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End PR765688-24R3]

[CB008227-B] If selected DRB is an NBR configured DRB with positive token bucket and it was already allocated bytes according to NBR positive token category as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22204\_replaced([11185658](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUs6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes shall be coinsidered as zero for the same DRB allocation in this step. Else, rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes is determined as above. The rationale is that rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize\_bytes shall not be reserved twice for the same DRB in the slot. [End CB008227-B]

 Update buffer level of the DRB as:

bufferedBytes[i,j] = max{bufferedBytes[i,j] - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

If rrmBufferStatusSplitNeeded == 1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2851\_replaced([11122336](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UE i is a CA UE, the FD-Scheduler shall update the buffer split of the UE as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2848\_replaced([11122320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqepLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Reason: the buffer split represents the amount of data available for transmission from the set of UE's serving cells which FD scheduling is handled sequentially on the same core. After scheduling the UE on one carrier, the update provides the amount of data still available for transmission on the next UE carrier(s) of the set.

 Update available bytes in the DL TB as:

rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = max{rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes - amountBytes[i,j], 0}

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11185739`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
 Determine next DRB
====================

rrmSchedFinished = FALSE

IF (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes > 0)

Determine next DRB as selected DRB (if sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7536\_replaced([11185805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvV7B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)); Else 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

ELSE

Determine next DRB as selected DRB (if sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7536\_replaced([11185805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvV7B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)); Else 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), unless the current DRB is not the only DRB served in the slot for the UE and it still has pending data.

IF (rrmTbFreeAmount\_bytes = 0) OR (all the DRBs have been served)

rrmSchedFinished = TRUE

ELSE

rrmSchedFinished = FALSE

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
