============================

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-1.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `11185759`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
At the end,

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True,

* 5G-L2-PS shall save selected DRB's logical channel identity;

Else

* 5G-L2-PS shall save selected DRB's logical channel identity or save pointer to selected DRB (depending on implementation options in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

When the UE is selected in a later slot for user data scheduling, the procedure shall be resumed with the DRB which was selected last, if (sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = False) or ((sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True) and [Before CB008828-C] (the DRB still belongs to a configured slice in the later slot)); [CB008828-C] (the DRB still belongs to a configured resource group (RG) in the later slot));[End CB008828-C] otherwise, the procedure shall be start with a DRB that is selected by the inner weighted round-robin algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7536\_replaced([11185805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvV7B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If several carriers of one UE are handled sequentially in one slot, it is left to implementation whether DRB sequence is handled per carrier or common for sequentially handled carriers. In latter case, procedure shall be resumed with the DRB which was selected last for the next carrier. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4556\_replaced([11186935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXJNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 3.29.10.8.2.0-2 Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket) Weighted round-robin algorithm for scheduling non-GBR DRBs per UE (ID: 11185763)

**3.29.10.8.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11185770`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Introduction

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11185775`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
For each UE, 5G-L2-PS shall maintain a weighted round-robin algorithm for the allocation of DL TB resources to non-GBR DRBs. The algorithm shall handle all non-GBR DRBs of a UE with priority introduced by schedulWeight (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2209\_replaced([11281916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpbXbPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The non-GBR DRBs shall be scheduled in an order as described below. The scheduling order shall be newly determined each time a non-GBR DRB is established or removed from the set of established DRBs.

The principles of building the scheduling order are:

 Each DRB is scheduled as often as corresponding schedulWeight

 The scheduling occurrences of a DRB are spaced as far as possible

Note: dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic bearers weight for the inner WRR shall be computed based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11256\_replaced([11186945](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXJOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For example, if three non-GBR DRBs A, B and C with the schedulweight 4, 3 and 2 are established for the UE, they would be scheduled in the order ABCABCABA.

Implementation note: The scheduling order or next DRB to be scheduled can be determined by applying the weighted round-robin algorithm whenever the UE's DRBs are to be scheduled (implementation option 1) or alternatively by calculating whole scheduling sequence in advance (implementation option 2). It is up to implementation which alternative to use as long as determined order corresponds to the algorithm. For implementation option 1, each time when a DRB other than current one is needed for scheduling, 5G-L2-PS shall determine the DRB to be scheduled. For implementation option 2, a pointer needs to be maintained indicating the DRB to be scheduled inside the scheduling sequence.

See chosen Option in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22283\_replaced([11186883](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXJIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11185781`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Algorithm Calling Condition

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11185784`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Upon reception of a PsUser\_UserSetupReq or PsUser\_BearerSetupReq,

 If at least one non-GBR DRB contained in the message is setup for the UE, 5G-L2-PS shall initialize the weighted round-robin algorithm for all the DRBs of the UE

 For implementation option 1, 5G-L2-PS shall execute Step 1~4 of the algorithm to determine the DRB to be scheduled and save the DRB's logical channel identity

 For implementation option 2, 5G-L2-PS shall execute Step 1 once, and then execute Step 2~4 of the algorithm recursively to generate whole scheduling sequence until all the elements in the bearersArray become zero after Step 4. Then 5G-L2-PS shall set the pointer to a random position in the sequence

Upon reception of a PsUser\_BearerDeleteReq,

 If at least one non-GBR DRB contained in the message is deleted for the UE and there is still at least one non-GBR DRB left for the UE after bearer deletion procedure, 5G-L2-PS shall initialize the weighted round-robin algorithm for all the DRBs left

 For implementation option 1, 5G-L2-PS shall execute Step 1~4 of the the algorithm to determine the DRB to be scheduled and save the DRB's logical channel identity

 For implementation option 2, 5G-L2-PS shall execute Step 1 once, and then execute Step 2~4 of the algorithm recursively to generate whole scheduling sequence until all the elements in the bearersArray become zero after Step 4. Then 5G-L2-PS shall set the pointer to a random position in the sequence

 If at least one non-GBR DRB contained in the message is deleted for the UE and there is no non-GBR DRB left for the UE after bearer deletion procedure, 5G-L2-PS shall stop the weighted round-robin algorithm

 For implementation option 1, 5G-L2-PS shall discard saved logical channel identity for all the DRBs

 For implementation option 2, 5G-L2-PS shall set the pointer to invalid

Upon reception of a PsUser\_UserDeleteReq,

 If UE context is deleted for the UE, 5G-L2-PS shall quit the weighted round-robin algorithm and release all the relevant resources for the algorithm

Each time the UE goes into or out of DTX optimized scheduling state (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8232\_replaced([11174950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kufmc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 5G-L2-PS shall initialize the weighted round-robin algorithm for all the DRBs of the UE

Each time next DRB is needed to be determined (Step 2 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4550\_replaced([11185672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUs7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), without reception of any one of PsUser\_UserSetupReq, PsUser\_BearerSetupReq, PsUser\_BearerDeleteReq or PsUser\_UserDeleteReq,

 For implementation option 1, 5G-L2-PS shall execute Step 2~4 of the algorithm to determine next DRB to be scheduled and save the DRB's logical channel identity

 For implementation option 2, 5G-L2-PS shall move the pointer to the next DRB in the scheduling sequence

The weighted round-robin algorithm The weighted round-robin algorithm is in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16080\_replaced([11185790](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvV7BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11185790`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Algorithm

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11185795`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
The weighted round-robin algorithm is as following:

 Step 1: Create an array bearersArray with size of number of all established non-GBR DRBs (denoted as N below), and set all elements in bearersArray to zero.

bearersArray[i] = 0, i=0...N-1

j = 0

NOTE: The order for DRBs in bearersArray is based on descending order of their schedulWeight, i.e. first element corresponds to the DRB with highest schedulWeight and last element corresponds to the DRB with lowest schedulWeight.

 Step 2: Select the DRB with the lowest value (zero) in bearersArray.

Select DRB k, where bearersArray[k] <= bearersArray[i], i=0…N-1

If there are more than one element with the same lowest value (zero) in bearersArray, a DRB shall be selected from Set A based on the following rule, assuming Set A denotes the set of those DRBs with the same lowest value (zero) in bearersArray.

IF (j<N)

Select the DRB from Set A with lowest index in bearersArray

ELSE

Select the DRB from Set A, which has not been selected for longest duration since its last selection

 Step 3: For the selected DRB in Step 2, add reciprocal of its schedulWeight to corresponding element in bearersArray. Limit the element so that it is not bigger than 100.

bearersArray[k] = bearersArray[k] + 1/schedulWeight[k], where k is the index of selected DRB in Step 2

 Step 4: Decrease all the elements in bearersArray by the lowest value of bearersArray.

bearersArray[i] = bearersArray[i] - bearersArray[m], where bearersArray[m] <= bearersArray[i], i=0…N-1

IF (all the elements in bearersArray are zero)

j = 0

ELSE

j = j + 1

Note: Step 1 above is for initialization based on a updated set of established DRBs (i.e. with a DRB added or removed).

---

##### 3.29.10.8.2.0-3 Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket) Inner Weighted Round-Robin (WRR) algorithm for scheduling non-GBR and deprioritized GBR DRBs per UE for slice aware scheduler (ID: 11185800)

**3.29.10.8.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11185805`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Algorithm

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-3.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11185830`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
For each UE, 5G-L2-PS shall maintain a weighted round-robin algorithm for the allocation of DL TB resources to non-GBR and deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRBs. The algorithm shall handle all non-GBR and deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRBs of a UE by randomly selecting DRBs with probability proportional their combined weight (RG weight \* scheduling weight).

For example, if in a slot, three non-GBR and deprioritized GBR DRBs A, B and C with the combined weight 3, 2 and 5 are established for the UE, they would be selected for scheduling with probability of 0.3, 0.2 and 0.5.

When NRBTS/NRCELL/actEnhDtxOptimizedScheduler is set to true dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic bearers schedulWeight for the inner WRR shall be computed based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11256\_replaced([11186945](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXJOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), excluding each DRB which belongs to a resource group (RG) of 0 RG weight, Note: DRB is not associated with an RG or associated with an unconfigured RG is handled by default RG.

Each time the UE goes into or out of DTX optimized scheduling state (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8232\_replaced([11174950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kufmc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 5G-L2-PS shall initialize the weighted round-robin algorithm for all the DRBs of the UE

The inner weighted round-robin algorithm is described in the following.

Each time the selected DRB needs to be updated (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10558\_replaced([11185734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall execute Step 3 of the algorithm to determine next DRB to be scheduled and save the DRB's logical channel identity.

Assume that the current slot is t.

---

**3.29.10.8.2.0-3.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `11186770`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
11186770:
Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
[Before PR780895]

Step 2 is executed after the FD scheduling is complete (i.e. after PRBs are assigned and allocated as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4487\_replaced([11179166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4A_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Step 2 is executed in each slot t for each scheduled UE u that requires initial transmissions for its non-GBR and deprioritized GBR DRBs.

a. Update the actual and expected average number of PRBs allocated for every non-GBR  and deprioritized GBR DRB j of u if in slot t, j belongs to a configured slice and has a non-zero normalized weight in Step 1.d and a non-empty buffer before u is scheduled:

Assume in slot t, we have allocated [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrm InstDrbPrbAllocj(t)". If DRB j is a non-GBR DRB, [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc\_j(t) = rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc\_{j,drbTraf,fic}(t)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2], else [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, sans-serif font. The equation is:
`rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc,j(t) = rrmInstDrbPrbAllocj,deprioritizeddbr(t)`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] (computed according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7577\_replaced([11190661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6K_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  If basic MU-MIMO is activated for a cell (NRCELL.actDlMuMimo = True), [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text that appears to be a mathematical expression. The text reads "rrmInstUeNgbrProbAllocu(t)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] shall be computed according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8336\_replaced([11191471](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvhhOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, italicized font. The equation is:
`rrmInstDrbProbExp(t) = rrmDrbNormalizedWeight_j * rrmInstUeNghbrProbAlloc_u(t)`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

Then update the moving averages. Let s be the last slot in which [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmAvgDrbPrbExpⱼ". The text is blurry and appears to be in a mathematical or scientific context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] gets updated.

Note that when a DRB is newly assigned to a configured resource group (RG) or a DRB whose RG is changed from the default RG state to the configured RG state, then s, the last slot in which [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmAvgDrbPrbExpⱼ". The text is blurry and appears to be in a mathematical or scientific context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.007.png[/image\_path][/image-7] gets updated for the DRB, is set to nonexistent.

[image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation defines a variable `rrmAvgDrbPrbAlloc_j(t)` using a piecewise function.
The equation is as follows:
$$
rrmAvgDrbPrbAlloc\_j(t) =
\begin{cases}
rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc\_j(t) & ; s \text{ does not exist} \
\left(\frac{\epsilon}{1+\tau}\right) \cdot rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc\_j(t) + \left(1 - \frac{\epsilon}{1+\tau}\right)^{t-s} \cdot rrmAvgDrbPrbAlloc\_j(s) & ; \text{Otherwise}
\end{cases}
$$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.008.png[/image\_path][/image-8]

[image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation defines a function `rrmAvgDrbPrbExp_j(t)` using a piecewise definition.
The first case, which applies when 's' does not exist, states that `rrmAvgDrbPrbExp_j(t)` is equal to `rrmInstDrbPrbExp_j(t)`.
The second case, labeled "Otherwise", applies when 's' exists. In this case, `rrmAvgDrbPrbExp_j(t)` is defined as:
$$
\left( \frac{\epsilon}{1+\tau} \cdot \text{rrmInstDrbPrbExp}\_j(t) + \left(1 - \frac{\epsilon}{1+\tau}\right)^{t-s} \cdot \text{rrmAvgDrbPrbExp}\_j(s) \right)
$$
The equation is presented with a large curly brace indicating the piecewise nature of the definition. The text "; s does not exist," and "; Otherwise" are placed to the right of their respective conditions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.009.png[/image\_path][/image-9]

If rdAccurateInstDrbPrbAllocComp = 1

[image-10][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a white background. The equation is presented in three lines.
The first line defines a variable `rrmInstUeNgbrPrbAllocu(t)` and sets it equal to a summation. The summation is over `jeju` and `jeju^NCBR`. Inside the summation, there is a term `rrmInstDrbPrbAllocj,c(t)`.
The second line shows a fraction. The numerator is a summation over `ce(drbTraffic,deprioritizedGbr)` and `jeju^NCBR`, with the term `amountBytesj,c(t)`. The denominator is `payloadBytesu(t)`. This fraction is multiplied by `rrmInstUePrbAllocu(t)`.
The third line defines `rrmInstUeNgbrPrbRatio(t)` multiplied by `rrmInstUePrbAllocu(t)`.
The equation appears to be related to resource allocation in a telecommunications context, possibly involving user equipment (UE), neighbor cells (Ngbr), and different types of traffic (drbTraffic, GBR). The notation suggests a time-dependent calculation.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.010.png[/image\_path][/image-10]

Else

[image-11][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a white background. The equation is presented in three lines.
The first line is:
`rrmInstUeNgbrPrbAlloc(t)`
The second line is an equality followed by a summation:
`= Σ ∑rrmInstDrbPrbAllocje(t)`
`ce(drbTraffic,deprioritizedGbr) jeNGBR`
The third line is a fraction:
`Ece(drbTraffic,deprioritizedGbr) jejNGBR INGBR amountBytesj.c (t)`
`TBS(t)`
`= rrmInstUeNgbrPrbRatio (t) rrmInstUePrbAlloc(t)`
The fourth line is:
`rrmInstUePrbAlloc(t)`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.011.png[/image\_path][/image-11]

where,

 [image-12][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrm AvgDrbPrbAllocj(t)". The text is in a mathematical or scientific context, likely representing a variable or function. The font is a standard sans-serif font, and the text is rendered in a blurry, low-resolution manner. The letters are dark gray or black, and the background is white. There are no other discernible features in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.012.png[/image\_path][/image-12]: The exponential moving average of the actual number of PRBs allocated to initial transmissions of DRB j, computed at slot t.

 [image-13][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstDrbPrbExp,(t)". The text is blurry and appears to be in a mathematical or scientific context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.013.png[/image\_path][/image-13]: The expected number of PRBs allocated to initial transmissions of DRB j in slot t, which is equal to the product of normalized weight of DRB j and the actual number of PRBs allocated to initial transmissions of non-GBR and deprioritized GBR DRBs of UE u in slot t

 [image-14][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmAvgDrbPrbExpⱼ(t)". The text is blurry and appears to be a mathematical expression or a variable name.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.014.png[/image\_path][/image-14] The exponential moving average of the expected number of PRBs allocated to initial transmissions of DRB j, computed at slot t

 [image-15][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstUeNgbrPrbRatio\_u(t)". The text is in a dark color against a light, slightly blurred background. The characters are mostly lowercase, with the exception of "N" and "P" which are uppercase. The subscript "u" is smaller than the surrounding characters. The text appears to be a mathematical or scientific formula.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.015.png[/image\_path][/image-15]the percentage of PRBs allocated to UE u for traffic classes drbTraffic and deprioritizedGbr.

 [image-16][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, pixelated mathematical expression. The expression appears to be a subscripted integral symbol followed by the letters "NCBR". The subscript is the number "11".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14330.016.png[/image\_path][/image-16]nonGBR and deprioritized GBR DRBs of UE u excluding each DRB which belongs to a default RG or a configured RG of 0 RG weight.

 τ = rdInnerWrrFilterWindow : is the exponential filter time window (default value=100)

 ε = rdInnerWrrFilterFactor: is the exponential filter smoothing factor (default value=1)

 amountBytes\_{j,c}(t): the amount of bytes allocated to the transmissions of a DRB j for traffic class c in slot t (For DRB retransmissions see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for non-GBR DRB initial transmissions see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4548\_replaced([11185645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvUs5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for GBR DRB initial transmissions see below)

* TBS\_u(t): the DL transport Block size allocated to UE u in slot t,
* rdAccurateInstDrbPrbAllocComp: R&D parameter, for parameter definition, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16210\_replaced([11193547](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtudvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* payloadBytes\_u(t): the amount of bytes of UE u transmitted in its transport block in slot t,

[End PR780895]

11186878:
Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
Step 3 is executed each time next DRB needs to be determined for a UE u.

If [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrm.SkipInnerWrr\_u = True". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be a variable assignment in a programming context. The background is a light, slightly yellowish-white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

If [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrm Drbs". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] is empty

The scheduling of non-GBR and deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRBs for UE u shall finish,

Else

Return the logical channel identity of the first DRB in [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrm Drbs". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.002.png[/image\_path][/image-2] and remove it from [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrm Drbs". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.002.png[/image\_path][/image-3].

End If

The following computation shall be skipped.

End If

If the R&D parameter rdEnablePendingDataPrioritization = 1 (for parameter definition, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10983\_replaced([11193437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvsgXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmPdDrbsu". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are in lowercase, except for "P" and "D" which are capitalized. The letter "u" at the end of the text is a subscript.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.003.png[/image\_path][/image-4] is not empty

J refers to [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmPdDrbsu". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are in lowercase, except for "P" and "D" which are capitalized. The letter "u" at the end of the text is a subscript.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.003.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

Else

J refers to [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmNpdDrbs\_u". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are in lowercase, except for the "N" and "D" which are capitalized. The "u" at the end of the text is a subscript.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.004.png[/image\_path][/image-6].

End If

Else

J refers to [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrm Drbs". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.002.png[/image\_path][/image-7].

End If

If J is empty

The scheduling of non-GBR and deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRBs for UE u shall finish.

Else if J contains only one DRB

Return the logical channel identity of the DRB in J and remove it from J.

Else

L2PS executes the following procedure.

End If

Set A = 0

For each DRB j in J

Compute normalized weight for DRB j as [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmDrbNormalizedWeight\_j = \frac{rrmDrbCombinedWeight\_j}{\sum\_{k=j} rrmDrbCombinedWeight\_k}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.005.png[/image\_path][/image-8].

A = A + [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrm DrbNormalizedWeight,". The text is in a light color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred, and there is a faint shadow or gradient effect on some of the letters. The overall image is a close-up of the text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.006.png[/image\_path][/image-9].

Compute cumulative weight for DRB j as [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmCumWeightj = A". The text is slightly blurry and appears to be in a mathematical or scientific context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.007.png[/image\_path][/image-10].

End For

Generate a uniformly distributed random number r in [0, 1).

For each DRB j in J

If [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text that appears to be a mathematical expression. The visible characters are "rrmCumWeightj > r".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14334.008.png[/image\_path][/image-11]

Return the logical channel identity of DRB j and remove j from J.

End If

End For

Return the logical channel identity of the last DRB in J and remove it from J.

---

##### 3.29.10.8.2.0-4 Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket) Applied Option for Weighted round-robin algorithm for scheduling non-GBR DRBs per UE (ID: 11186883)

**3.29.10.8.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11186897`)

Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
11186897:
Resource allocation to Non-GBR bearers (excluding positive NBR token bucket)
The scheduling order or next DRB to be scheduled mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be determined by applying the weighted round-robin algorithm whenever the UE's DRBs are to be scheduled (implementation option 1). Each time when a DRB other than current one is needed for scheduling, 5G-L2-PS shall determine the DRB to be scheduled.

The DRB sequence shall be re-initialized and re-started each time a DRB is added or deleted from the UE.

Examples for 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))'s DRB order determination algorithm:

a. Equal scheduling weights for the bearers of a UE

For a exemplary UE three bearers A ( QCI = 9, schedulingWeight = 1 ), B ( QCI = 9, schedulingWeight = 1 ) and C ( QCI = 9, schedulingWeight = 1) are setup.

The following picture shall show the relevant bearerArray[] values from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))' algorithm over time and the DRB scheduling order pattern generated for above DRB configuration.

According to the rules of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) the DRB with the smallest bearerArray value is scheduled.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a bar chart with a legend and a table on the left. The bar chart displays the values of bearers A, B, and C over time. The x-axis represents time, and the y-axis represents the bearer values, ranging from 0.00 to 1.20. There are three lines representing the bearers: bearer A is blue, bearer B is orange, and bearer C is red. The table on the left lists the DRB scheduling order, with entries A, B, and C repeated in a cyclical pattern.
The bar chart shows that bearer A and bearer B have values that oscillate between 0 and 1, with peaks at 1. Bearer C's values are consistently at 0. The pattern for bearers A and B is a repeating sequence of peaks and troughs.
The table on the left shows the following DRB scheduling order:

The resulting DRB scheduling order has a length of 3, which corresponds to the sum of scheduling weights.

The resulting DRB sequence for this UE with above configuration is: ABC.

b. Different scheduling weights for the bearers of a UE

For a exemplary UE three bearers A ( QCI = 7, schedulingWeight = 10 ), B ( QCI = 8, schedulingWeight = 5 ) and C ( QCI = 9, schedulingWeight = 1) are setup.

The following picture shall show the relevant bearerArray[] values from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))' algorithm over time and the DRB scheduling order pattern generated for above DRB configuration.

According to the rules of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4552\_replaced([11185763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvVT__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) the DRB with the smallest bearerArray value is scheduled.

The x-axis represents time and is labeled with numbers from 1 to 81, with increments of 2.
