NaN 75 11 16 16.00 16.00 16.00 12.00 9.0 7.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN 76 11 16 16.00 16.00 16.00 13.00 10.0 7.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN 77 11 16 16.00 16.00 16.00 13.00 10.0 7.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN 78 11 16 16.00 16.00 16.00 13.00 10.0 7.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN[/xlsx\_desc][xlsx\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/attachments/m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file14441\_1.xlsx[/xlsx\_path]  
 
**[End CB014465-B]**
 
Note that the values given in the table above are calculated as follows:
the number of PRBs of the PUCCH F2 or F3 is the lowest number of PRBs such that:
UCI payload size + CRC bits <= maxCodeRate *Qm* NbSymbol \* N\_RB\_SC \* NbPrb, (1)
 
with
\* maxCodeRate corresponds to the configured maximum code rate provided in the user setup message
\* Qm is equal to 2 for PUCCH format 2, **Qm = 1 for PUCCH format 3 if PI/2 BPSK is applied and Qm = 2 for PUCCH format 3 otherwise.**
\* NbSymbols: **number of OFDM symbols for PUCCH F2 or F3 reduced by the number of DM-RS symbols for PUCCH format 3. Number of DM-RS symbol for PUCCH F3 is according to 38.211 table 6.4.1.3.3.2-1, e.g. when number of OFDM symbols is 14, number of DM-RS symbols is 2 without additional DM-RS, and is 4 with additional DM-RS.**when number of OFDM symbols is 8 **[CB014465-B] or 6** [End CB014465-B], number of DM-RS symbols is 2 with or without additional DM-RS***\*****\* N\_RB\_SC is equal to 8 for PUCCH format 2, N\_RB\_SC is equal to 12 for PUCCH format 3.
\* NbPrb: number of PRB used to report UCI.
\* CRC bits depends on number of UCI payload size.
 
The formula:
NbPrb = Min {maxNrOfPrbs, RoundUp [(UCI payload size + CRC bits)/(maxCodeRate* Qm *NbSymbol \* N\_RB\_SC)]} (2),
shall be used by L2PS  to calculate the number of PUCCH F2 PRBs passed to L1 in UlData\_PucchReceiveReq>PucchResourceAllocationConfig, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1186\_replaced([11104617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn_V2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
The maximum number of PRBs is configured by higher layers (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_829\_replaced([11313420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrqMDPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2845\_replaced([11309733](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrJ1wvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the selected PUCCH Resource (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4330\_replaced([11177663](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
 
For PUCCH format 3, if the number of PRBs is not equal to (2^alpha2 \* 3^alpha3 \* 5^alpha5) then the number of PRBs is increased to nearest allowed value of harqNumOfPrbs. Note, C-Plane considers this rule when determining harqNumOfPrbs.* *Valid values are [1,2,3,4,5,6,8,9,10,12,15,16].**Notes:
 
1. For UCI reports with at most 11 bits there is no CRC. 6 bits of CRC are added for UCI payload of 12-19 bits, and 11 bits for more than 20 bits of UCI payload.
2. The table is extended to 64* *bits for long PUCCH F3.**3. The table is extended to support NbSymbols 8* *[CB014465-B] or 6* *[End CB014465-B] for FDD cell.*\*\*\*
4. If actHighNumberOfUEs = TRUE  and rdActIncreasedUciBits = TRUE, the gNB-DU shall support UCI payload size of upto 78 bits for PUCCH F2. The table has been extended to support this case.

---

### 3.29.11.3 Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL

#### 3.29.11.3.0-1 Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL Force PUCCH Format 1 of SR (Scheduling Request) only to DTX (ID: 11187503)

**3.29.11.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11187507`)

Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL
L2PS shall force the PUCCH Format 1 carrying only a SR bit (Scheduling Request) to DTX if the corresponding SINR is smaller than a configurable threshold. The threshold is defined by the R&D parameter rdPucchLowRxPowerThreshold (see 5G\_L2\_1491\_replaced([11181948](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27XxKPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 3.29.11.3.0-2 Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL Scheduling Request (SR) reception with PUCCH format 1 (ID: 11187513)

**3.29.11.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11187522`)

Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL
Successful reception of positive SR:

If the UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs (entry) indicates non-DTX feedback for the PUCCH format 1 resource configured in perSrConfig structure (5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_4873\_replaced([11358147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wZAdHvvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then L2-PS shall consider it positive SR received.

Successful reception of negative SR:

If for slots with SR occasion and one or two expected HARQ A/N bits for the same UE, the UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs (entry) indicates non-DTX feedback for the PUCCH format 1 resource selected from PUCCH Resource Set 0 then L2-PS shall consider it negative SR received.

If for slots with SR occasion and zero expected HARQ A/N bits, the UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs (entry) indicates DTX feedback for the PUCCH format 1 resource of perSrConfig structure then L2-PS shall consider it negative SR received.

Unsuccessful reception of SR/ PUCCH format 1:

In slots with SR occasion and one or two expected HARQ A/N bits for the same UE, L2-PS sends two UlData\_PucchReceiveReq (entries) as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4602\_replaced([11187378](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvazhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). It is expected and not considered a failure (successful) if only one of the two corresponding UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs (entries) indicates DTX feedback. It is considered a failure (unsuccessful) if both UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs indicate DTX.

Forced positive SR during transient phase:

The gNB-DU shall shall force a positive SR during the reconfiguration transient phase, i.e. between the reception of RRC Reconfiguration and RRC Reconfiguration Complete messages, for slots with the occurrence of an SR resource considering the new and the old configuration. [PR803999] Note: The forced positive SR may cause DTX reception during transient phase because it may not be synchronized with UE. [End PR803999]

Note, from PR551924 on (fixing the interworking of Long PUCCH and DSS), the IE srBit is used to distinguish the PUCCH related to SR and the PUCCH related to PUCCH Resource Set0.

In the case that a positive SR is received via pucch format 1 the R&D counter handling for SR described in (5G\_L2\_298\_replaced([11150577](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24dAC_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be applied.

Note: Forced positive SR during RRC Reconfiguration Transient Phase (as described above) is required for load based dynamic SR resource allocation, where blocks of UEs are reconfigured from one SR Periodicity pool to another as cell load changes and possibility of slots with consideration of the new and the old configuration is possible

Note, a SR is not expected if /NRBTS/NRCELL/actSharedSr is set to TRUE and /NRBTS/NRCELL/pucchModeSelect is set to longPucch and the cell-related parameter sharedSrPrb (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_7088\_replaced([11278984](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpTbkfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal to the parameter startingPrb of the UE’s SR resource (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2973\_replaced([11311385](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrYfOPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and only the UE’s shared SR resource is configured.

---

#### 3.29.11.3.0-3 Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL Scheduling Request (SR) and HARQ A/N reception on a DSS carrier (ID: 11187527)

**3.29.11.3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11187533`)

Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL
On a FDD carrier shared with LTE (DSS), 5G-L2-PS shall expect and evaluate SR in accordance with 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2460\_replaced([11116030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

 If the perSrConfig structure in 5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_4873\_replaced([11358147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wZAdHvvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) provides SR scheduled in the same slots and with the same starting symbol as the PUCCH F2 for the same UE, then the SR is prepended to the CSI report   
(which may include also HARQ A/N for this UE, if scheduled with the same starting symbol).   
For the prepeding scheme, s. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3603\_replaced([11159969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 2Tx or 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3180\_replaced([11139272](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 4Tx.

 Else, if perSrConfig structure in 5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_4873\_replaced([11358147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wZAdHvvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) provides SR scheduled in the same slot and with the same starting symbol as HARQ A/N scheduled for the same UE, and there is no CSI scheduled for this UE in this slot, then the SR is multiplexed with the HARQ A/N in the same UCI.

 Else, SR is provided as a single bit from the resource configured for SR.

* As per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18087\_replaced([11187133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvY-UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), an SR colliding with the second hop of msg4 A/N of another UE shall be ignored.

Following the same logic, 5G-L2-PS shall expect and evaluate HARQ A/N in accordance with 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2460\_replaced([11116030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

 Preferably, for HARQ A/N the PUCCH F0 resource is scheduled with the same starting symbol as the PUCCH F2 configuration for the same UE. Then the HARQ A/N bit in CSI slots is prepended to the CSI report   
( which may include also SR, possibly, s. above).

 Else, if SR is configured on a different slot than the PUCCH F2 for the same UE and HARQ A/N is scheduled in the same slot and with the same starting symbol as SR, then SR is multiplexed with the HARQ A/N in the same UCI, like stated above.

 Else, HARQ A/N is provided as a single bit from the resource configured for SR.

---

#### 3.29.11.3.0-4 Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL CSI Invalidity Check (ID: 11187538)

**3.29.11.3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11187543`)

Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL
A CSI report with numOfBitsOfUciInformation > 11 shall be considered as DTX if:

* PUCCH reception is explicity marked as DTX

or

* report CRC is not ok

---

#### 3.29.11.3.0-5 Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL CSI plausibility Check (ID: 11187546)

**3.29.11.3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12659470`)

Reception and Handling of xPUCCH Information from 5G-L1-UL
Upon reception of UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs, [Before CNI-137616-C] if parameter rdEnableCsiPlausibilityCheck is set to TRUE, [End CNI-137616-C] L2PS shall check if the CQI value indicated in the received CSI-report (if included) is plausible. This plausibility check and CQI acceptance is done as follows:

If SINR of received PUCCH is greater than or equal rdPucchLowSinrThreshold, or the Scell where the CQI is reported is in overheating status (the CQI value in UE context is 0), or the CQI value reported from Scell is 0,

- accept the CQI value as the new CQI value in UE context

Otherwise,

- accept the CQI value if

 the difference between it and the CQI value in UE context is less than or equal rdAllowedCqiStep.

 Or the difference between it and the previously not accepted CQI value (if any) is less than or equal rdAllowedCqiStep.

- If the difference is larger than rdAllowedCqiStep, CQI value is not updated until another report with a new CQI value is obtained.

- If the rank reported in the received CSI-report is less than the previous rank (from UE context), the allowed CQI difference is rdAllowedCqiStep + rdAllowedadditionalCQIStepOnRankDowngrade (instead of only rdAllowedCqiStep).

---

## 3.29.12 PLMN and Slice Awareness

### 3.29.12.1 Resource Partition Volume Control

#### 3.29.12.1.0-1 Resource Partition Volume Control Determine Low Buffer Traffic States of RGs (ID: 11187568)

**3.29.12.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11187804`)

Resource Partition Volume Control
11187804:
Resource Partition Volume Control
To decide the low buffer traffic states of all RGs, gNB-DU needs to execute computeLowBufferStateForRGs(). **[Before CB008250-AB]after executing computeFullBufferTempTargetShares().**
**[CB008250-AB]**
Precondition for determining low buffer states of RGs: full buffer temporary target shares are computed [Before PR841286] per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31093\_replaced([11188307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, if** PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), **otherwise [End PR841286] per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26713\_replaced([11189763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvgTF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*****\**\*[End CB008250-AB]**
An RG **j is considered to have low buffer traffic,** i.e. hasLowBuffer(j) = True, if either of the following two conditions has been satisfied in the past rdMinLowBufferSlotsDL slots. Once both conditions are unsatisfied, the RG is considered not to have low buffer traffic, i.e. hasLowBuffer(j) = False***\*
a) rrmRGFullBufferTempTargetShare\_j for RG j is equal to 0 and no resource is assigned to RG j in the slot.
b) rrmRGFullBufferTempTargetShare\_j for RG j is greater than 0 and the average resource share of RG j rrmRGAvgShare\_j(t) is below rdAvgShareToleranceDL \* rrmRGFullBufferTempTargetShare\_j in the slot.
hasLowBuffer(j) returns the current low buffer traffic state of RG* j.*//After executing computeLowBufferStateForRGs(),* rrmRGLowBufferState\_j stores the low buffer state of RG *j. rrmRGLowBufferState\_j = True, if RG* j is in low buffer state.***[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains text that appears to be "rrrmRGlowBufferState,". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14460.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], for every RG* j*// compute the low buffer traffic state for all RGs. The states are stored in* rrmRGLowBufferState*Function computeLowBufferStateForRGs()
Make a list S that contains all configured RGs in the cell.
For each RG [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "f ∈ S". The letters and symbol are stylized with a gradient effect, transitioning from blue to yellow and then to purple. The text is set against a white background with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14460.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
If [image-2][image\_desc](rmRGFullBufferTempTargetSharej ≤ 0 and rmRGInstSharej(t) ≤ 0)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14460.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2][image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a single line of text, which appears to be code or a mathematical expression. The text is:
`or (rrmRG AvgShare,(t) < rdAvgShareToleranceDL · rrmRGFullBuf)/ferTempTax.getShare)`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14460.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
  [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the following text: rrrnRGlowBufSlot,(t) = True[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14460.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
 Else

 End If
 If (t < rdMinLowBufferSlotsDL) or ([image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmRG LowBuf Slot j(t')". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are white with a slight yellow tint, and the background is a light blue and white gradient.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14460.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] is false for any slot [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is: $t' \in [t - rdMinLowBuf \int \text{ersSlotsDL} + 1, t]$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14460.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7])

 End If
End For
If ([Before PR841286]**[CB008250-AB] PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* *or* *[End CB008250-AB] [End PR841286]**rdSvcControlTypeDL = 1) and (at least one RG* j has *rrmRGLowBufferState\_j changed from true to false or from false to true)****\******Set rrmRgIterm\_k(t) = 0 for each RG k in S.
End If
End Function
Output: low buffer traffic state of RG j. True, if RG j is in low buffer traffic state, Otherwise, return False
 
Function hasLowBuffer([image-10][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract composition of gradients and soft light. The dominant colors are warm beige and light blue, transitioning into white and then a dark, almost black, vertical band on the right side. There are subtle hints of darker tones within the lighter areas, suggesting depth or shadow. The overall impression is one of soft focus and diffused light, creating a gentle and ethereal atmosphere. There is no discernible text or table in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14460.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10])
 Return [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains text that appears to be "rrrmRGlowBufferState,". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14460.001.png[/image\_path][/image-11]
End Function
 
Notations:
\* rrmRGLowBufSlot\_j(t): whether RG *j has passed low buffer state check *for slot* t.***
\* rdAvgShareToleranceDL: R&D parameter, the tolerance level for checking average resource share for an RG, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23513\_replaced([11193707](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8mPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rdMinLowBufferSlotsDL: R&D parameter, the minimum number of consecutive slots for low buffer traffic state checking, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23515\_replaced([11193715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8m_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rrmRGLowBufferState\_j: current low buffer traffic state of RG *j*
\* rrmRGFullBufferTempTargetShare\_j: temporary target share (including both high priority and low priority traffic) for RG *j, assuming each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and the actual high priority traffic. [Before PR841286]**[CB008250-AB] If* *PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31093\_replaced([11188307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, else* *[End CB008250-AB][End PR841286] see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26713\_replaced([11189763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvgTF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).****\*****\* rrmRGInstShare\_j(t): the instantaneous resource share used by RG j in slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rrmRgIterm\_k(t): the integral term of PI control for RG j. [Before PR841286]**[CB008250-AB] If* *PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31226\_replaced([11188517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, else* *[End CB008250-AB] [End PR841286] see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23508\_replaced([11189878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvgTGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).****\****
\* rrmRGAvgShare\_j(t): Average resource share for RG *j at slot* t (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*.*\*\*
 
When PI control is applied for RG weight computation, L2PS shall reset integral term for all RGs if at least one RG switches between low buffer state and high buffer state. The rationale is to reset integral term when there is an abrupt change in the expected low priority traffic target share rrmRGExpLowTargetShare which improves the transient behavior (having RG weight stabilize faster and with less overshoot).

---

#### 3.29.12.1.0-2 Resource Partition Volume Control Compute the Set of Non-empty Buffer RGs of a Cell or a PLMN (ID: 11187810)

**3.29.12.1.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `11187947`)

Resource Partition Volume Control
11187947:
Resource Partition Volume Control
To compute the set of non-empty buffer RGs of a cell, gNB-DU needs to execute computeNonempBufRGs() **[CB008250-AB] with empty input parameter.**
To compute the set of non-empty buffer RGs of PLMN p, gNB-DU needs to execute computeNonempBufRGs(p) with input parameter p.
**[End CB008250-AB]**
**[CB012238-A-UPD002]**
computeNonempBufRGs() is called by SVC or enhanced SVC algorithm while computeNonempBufRGs(p) is called by PVC algorithm for a PLMN p. All variables with a subscript p are only used in computeNonempBufRGs(p) for PLMN aware scheduler. The corresponding variables without subscript p are used in computeNonempBufRGs() for legacy or enhanced slice aware scheduler. The purpose of merging computeNonempBufRGs() and computeNonempBufRGs(p) into one piece of algorithm is to avoid duplicating the same logic.
**[End CB012238-A-UPD002]**
An RG is considered to have empty buffer if no resource has been assigned to the RG in the past rdMinEmptyBufferSlotsDL slots. computeNonempBufRGs() is executed once every virtual slot. **Let t be the current slot.**
 
Function Q = computeNonempBufRGs(**[CB008250-AB] p** [End CB008250-AB])***\*
 Set Q to be an empty set.*\*[CB008250-AB]**
// empty parameter corresponds to computing the set of non-empty buffer RGs of a cell which is executed by (enhanced) SVC. Non-empty parameter p corresponds to computing the set of non-empty buffer RGs of PLMN p which is executed by PVC.
 If (p is empty)
**[End CB008250-AB]**
Make a list S that contains all configured RGs in the cell.
**[CB012238-A-UPD002]**
rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs = False
rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch = True
**[End CB012238-A-UPD002]**
**[CB008250-AB]**
 Else
Make a list S that contains all configured RGs of PLMN p.
**[CB012238-A-UPD002]**
rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs\_p = False
rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch\_p = True
**[End CB012238-A-UPD002]**
End If
 **[End CB008250-AB]**

Notations:
\* rrmRGEmptyBufSlot\_j(t): whether RG *j has passed empty buffer state check* for slot *t.***\* rdMinEmptyBufferSlotsDL: R&D parameter, the minimum number of consecutive slots for RG empty buffer state checking, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28687\_replaced([11193696](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rrmRGInstShare\_j(t): the instantaneous resource share used by RG j in slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rdSvcExecutionPeriodDL: the period to execute SVC algorithm, in number of slots, see see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30522\_replaced([11193737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvvjofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**[CB012238-A-UPD002]**\* rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs: whether any of the RGs in the cell is in empty buffer state.
\* rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs\_p: whether any of the RGs in PLMN p is in empty buffer state.
\* rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch: whether the set of empty buffer RGs match exactly the set of low buffer RGs in the cell.
\* rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch\_p: whether the set of empty buffer RGs match exactly the set of low buffer RGs in PLMN p.**[End CB012238-A-UPD002]\*\*

#### 3.29.12.1.0-3 Resource Partition Volume Control Skip Algorithm Execution (ID: 11187953)

**3.29.12.1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11187974`)

Resource Partition Volume Control
11187974:
Resource Partition Volume Control
The following logic is performed only when slice aware scheduler is activated (sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True) or enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_12237\_replaced([11288194](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp5RdPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008250-AB] or PLMN aware scheduler is activated (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008250-AB] for a cell.
If the number of PRBs available for slice aware scheduler or enhanced slice aware scheduler [CB008250-AB] or PLMN aware scheduler [End CB008250-AB] in virtual slot v is less than or equal to 0 [image-0][image\_desc]($\sum_{\text{each slot } t \text{ in } v}$ rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) $\le$ 0)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14469.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7577\_replaced([11190661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6K_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then the SVC or enhanced SVC [CB008250-AB] or PVC [End CB008250-AB] algorithm execution for virtual slot v shall be skipped and rrmRGWeight\_i shall keep the value of virtual slot v-1, for every RG i. In each slot t, if rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) <= 0, rrmRGAvgHighShare\_i(t), rrmRGAvgLowShare\_i(t), rrmRGAvgShare\_i(t) shall keep the corresponding values of slot t-1, for every RG i. [CB008250-AB] If PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), rrmPlmnAvgHighShare\_p(t), rrmPlmnAvgShare\_p(t) shall keep the corresponding values of slot t-1 for every PLMN p. [End CB008250-AB]

---

#### 3.29.12.1.0-4 Resource Partition Volume Control RG Weight Update with PI Control (ID: 11187980)

**3.29.12.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11188008`)

Resource Partition Volume Control
11188008:
Resource Partition Volume Control
RG weight update with PI control for RG j shall be done by executing the function rgWeightUpdateWithPiControl(j). RG weight is updated according to PI (proportional-integral) control law. The set-point of the PI control is the expected target share (including both high and low priority traffic) for RG j, rrmRGExpTargetShare\_j(t). The I-term in PI control is limited within [-rdEnhSvcPiPGainDL, rdEnhSvcPiPGainDL], to avoid integral windup.
 
Function rgWeightUpdateWithPiControl(j)
[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
rrmRGDeltaj (t) = rrmRGExpTargetShare; (t) - rrmRGAvgShare」(t)
The equation appears to be a formula defining a variable `rrmRGDeltaj` at time `t` as the difference between two other variables: `rrmRGExpTargetShare` at time `t` and `rrmRGAvgShare` at time `t`. The subscript 'j' is present on `rrmRGDeltaj` and `rrmRGExpTargetShare`, and the subscript 'j' is also present on `rrmRGAvgShare` with a closing parenthesis. The characters are slightly blurred, but the overall structure of the equation is clear.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14471.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
$r\_{mm}R\_{g,j} = r\_{mm}R\_{g,j} + rdEnhSvcPtGainDL \cdot r\_{mm}RGDelta\_j(t)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14471.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
If [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text. The text appears to be: "rrrm R glterm, > rdEnhSvePtPGain DL".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14471.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation is:
$rrrmRglterm\_j = rdEnhSvcPtPGain DL$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14471.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
End If
If [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is: `rrrnRglterm, < -rdEnhSvcPvPGainDL`. The text is in a light color against a white background, with a black border around the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14471.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font against a white background. The equation appears to be:
`rrrmRgltermj = -rdEnhSvcPtPGainDL`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14471.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]
End If
[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a stylized font. The formula appears to be defining a variable `rrmRGWeight` at time `t` as the maximum of several terms.
Here is the extracted text:
rrmRGWeight; (t) = max rrmRGExpLowTargetShare, (t) + rdEnhSvcPiPGainDL.
rrmRGDelta; (t) + rrmRgIterm;, rrmRGExpLowTargetShare;(t)/
rdRGTinyWeightReciDL}[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14471.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]
End
 
Notations:
\* rrmRGWeight\_j(t): RG weight computed for RG j at slot t
\* rrmRGExpTargetShare\_j(t): the expected target share (including both high and low priority traffic) for RG j, computed at slot t. If PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31226\_replaced([11188517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), else see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23508\_replaced([11189878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvgTGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rrmRGAvgShare\_j(t): average resource share of RG j at slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rrmRGDelta\_j(t): the difference between rrmRGExpTargetShare\_j(t) and rrmRGAvgShare\_j(t).
\* rdEnhSvcPiPGainDL:  R&D parameter, the proportional gain of PI control used in the PVC algorithm, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29759\_replaced([11193759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvvjpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rdEnhSvcPiIGainDL:  R&D parameter, the integral gain of PI control used in the PVC algorithm, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29667\_replaced([11193769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvvjp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rdRGTinyWeightReciDL: R&D parameter, the reciprocal of tiny RG weight for DL, to avoid cell resource waste, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29668\_replaced([11193780](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvvjqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rrmRgIterm\_j: the integral term of PI control for RG j. If PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31226\_replaced([11188517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), else see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23508\_replaced([11189878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvgTGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* rrmRGExpLowTargetShare\_j(t): the expected low priority traffic target share for RG j, computed at slot t.  If PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31424\_replaced([11188061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), else see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23504\_replaced([11189551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvgTFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.29.12.2 PLMN Aware Scheduler

#### 3.29.12.2.0-1 PLMN Aware Scheduler Introduction (ID: 11188019)

**3.29.12.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11188022`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
If PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall be aware of the available PLMNs and their target shares. For each PLMN, gNB-DU shall be aware of the RGs under the PLMN, their quotas, and the UEs/DRBs belonging to each **RG.**

DL PLMN aware scheduler is only supported for FR1 (both TDD and FDD).

gNB-DU shall obtain the following info during cell setup (& cell reconfig):

* The number of configured PLMNs and their IDs
* The number of resource groups (RGs) and theirs IDs
* The target share for each PLMN *p (0 <* rrmPlmnTargetShare\_*p <= 1.0, 0 means 0% and 1.0 means 100%).*\*\*
* The prioritized minimum quota and maximum quota for each RG.
* Whether resource sharing is allowed between PLMNs of the cell (*rrmAllowPlmnResourceSharing = True/False).*

where

*rrmPlmnTargetShare\_*p: The target share of PLMN *p, it is the target percentage of cell resources for PLMN aware scheduler that is allocated to a PLMN. It is set to NIDD parameter* NRBTS/NRPLMNSHARE\_PROFILE/NRPLMNSHARE.targetShare.\*\*\*\*

*rrmAllowPlmnResourceSharing: Whether resource sharing is allowed between PLMNs of the cell. It is set to NIDD parameter* NRBTS/NRPLMNSHARE\_PROFILE/allowPlmnToShareResources.\*\*

The RG quotas are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7387\_replaced([11188675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd21PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[CB012238-C]If QoS fairness slice aware scheduler is not activated on parent gNB(NRBTS.actSliceAwareSchQosFairness = False)**[End CB012238-C]\*\*\*\*

using the provided RG quotas, gNB-DU shall calculate for each RG the target share (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7390\_replaced([11189080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvfE9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the cell level maximum quota (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31404\_replaced([11188028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcoufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

**[CB012238-C]else, using both provided quotas and average total scheduling weight for RG, L2-PS shall calculate for each RG a target resource share using the PLMN Volume Control (SVC) Qos fairness algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_33006\_replaced([11189488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvfsCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** and the cell level maximum quota (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31404\_replaced([11188028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcoufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**[End CB012238-C].**\*\*\*\*

To achieve the target share, gNB-DU shall update the RG weight for all RGs after each PVC algorithm execution (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31092\_replaced([11188047](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). PVC algorithm is executed once per virtual slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30750\_replaced([11188771](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The enhanced SVC (5G\_UP\_ALG\_23499\_replaced([11189521](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvfsDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and SVC algorithms (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall not be executed if PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Other parts of DL scheduler work in the same way as for the enhanced slice aware scheduler (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7386\_replaced([11188670](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd20_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) unless specified otherwise.

---

#### 3.29.12.2.0-2 PLMN Aware Scheduler Cell Level Maximum Quota of a RG (ID: 11188028)

**3.29.12.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11188032`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
For each RG j (RG j belongs to PLMN p), L2PS shall compute the cell level maximum quota rrmCellLevelRGMaxQuota\_j which is the maximum resources that the RG is allowed to use in average, measured in the percentage of cell resources available to PLMN aware scheduler. 0 <= rrmCellLevelRGMaxQuota\_j <= 1.0.

If rrmAllowPlmnResourceSharing = True (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7385\_replaced([11188019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcot_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmCellLevelRGMaxQuota\_j = rrmRGMaxQuota\_j (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7387\_replaced([11188675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd21PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Else

rrmCellLevelRGMaxQuota\_j = rrmRGMaxQuota\_j \* rrmPlmnTargetShare\_p (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7385\_replaced([11188019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcot_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

End If

The rationale is that if PLMN resource sharing is allowed, the max percentage of cell resources that a RG can use is equal to the max quota of the RG, otherwise, it is equal to the product of the max quota of the RG and the PLMN target share.

---

#### 3.29.12.2.0-3 PLMN Aware Scheduler Cell Level Target Share of a RG (ID: 11188037)

**3.29.12.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11188042`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
For each RG j (RG j belongs to PLMN p), L2PS shall compute the cell level target share rrmCellLevelTargetShare\_j which is the expected resources that the RG shall get in average, measured in the percentage of cell resources available to PLMN aware scheduler, assuming each RG in the cell has full buffer traffic. 0 <= rrmCellLevelTargetShare\_j <= 1.0.

rrmCellLevelTargetShare\_j = rrmRGTargetShare\_j \* rrmPlmnTargetShare\_p.

Notations:

rrmPlmnTargetShare\_p: The target share of PLMN p, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7385\_replaced([11188019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcot_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

rrmRGTargetShare\_j: target share of RG j, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7390\_replaced([11189080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvfE9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.29.12.2.0-4 PLMN Aware Scheduler PLMN Volume Control (PVC) (ID: 11188047)

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11188053`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
Algorithm of PVC

---

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11188056`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
If PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_16121\_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2PS shall compute an RG weight for each RG in the cell with the PVC algorithm at the end of each DL virtual slot where a virtual slot is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30750\_replaced([11188771](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The PVC algorithm consists of the following steps:

* **[CB012238-C] If QoS fairness slice aware scheduler is activated on parent gNB(NRBTS.actSliceAwareSchQosFairness = True), computation of target shares of all RGs of each PLMN, assuming each PLMN has 100% of resource, according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_33006\_replaced([11189488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvfsCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [PR839489] To compute target share of RG j, the input param---set of RGs S shall be set to all RGs of the PLMN to which RG j belongs. [End PR839489] The target share of RG j is stored at rrmRGTargetShare\_j.** [End CB012238-C]\*\*\*\*

**[Before CB012238-C-UPD001]**

* computation of dynamic target shares for non-empty buffer RGs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31503\_replaced([11188561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[CB008250-C] will be used for computation of RG load for NBR congestion control per RG and** [End CB008250-C] the computation of estimated throughput per DRB for flow control.\*\*\*\*

**[End CB012238-C-UPD001]**

* computation of the low priority traffic target share for RGs of all PLMNs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31424\_replaced([11188061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* computation of temporary target share of PLMNs and RGs assuming each RG has full buffer low priority traffic (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31093\_replaced([11188307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The computed values will be used as thresholds to determine low buffer traffic state of PLMNs and RGs.
* determination of low buffer traffic states of PLMNs **[Before PR841286] and RGs** [End PR841286] (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31318\_replaced([11188465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **[Before PR841286] and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23506\_replaced([11187568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcBpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**[End PR841286]). If resource sharing among PLMNs is allowed, the unused resources of low buffer PLMNs are shared by other PLMNs in proportional to their target shares. The unused resources of low buffer RGs of a PLMN are shared by other RGs of the PLMN.***\****\*
* computation of low priority traffic target shares for RGs of all PLMNs based on the following inputs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31424\_replaced([11188061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): 1) low buffer traffic state of PLMNs and RGs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31318\_replaced([11188465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and **[Before PR841286] 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23506\_replaced([11187568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcBpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [PR841286] [12711058](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_44W3YGYwEfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End PR841286]), 2) PLMN and RG target shares (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7385\_replaced([11188019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcot_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 3) RG cell level maximum quotas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31404\_replaced([11188028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcoufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 4) whether resource sharing is allowed among PLMNs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7385\_replaced([11188019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcot_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and 5) the average resource usage of PLMNs and RGs for high priority and low priority traffic (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31465\_replaced([11188649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd20fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**
* feedback control based on the low priority traffic target share (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31226\_replaced([11188517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This step updates the RG weight of each RG of the cell.

**[**CB012238**-C-UPD001]**\*\*\*\*

* computation of dynamic target shares for non-empty buffer RGs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31503\_replaced([11188561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[CB008250-C] will be used for computation of RG load for NBR congestion control per RG and** [End CB008250-C] the computation of estimated throughput per DRB for flow control.\*\*\*\*

**[End CB012238-C-UPD001]**

---

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11188061`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
Computation of low priority traffic target shares for RGs of all PLMNs

---

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11188138`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
11188138:
PLMN Aware Scheduler
This step computes the low priority traffic target shares for RGs of all PLMNs. The procedure is listed as follows. The current slot is denoted *t.*
 
Output: the expected low priority traffic target share rrmRGExpLowTargetShare\_j(t) for each RG *j*
 
// compute the temporary target shares of all PLMNs and RGs, assuming that each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and the measured average high priority traffic. The computed values serve as thresholds for PLMN and RG low buffer state check.
Execute computeFullBufferTempTargetShares(), defined at 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31093\_replaced([11188307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
[Before PR841286]
// compute the low buffer traffic state for all RGs
Execute computeLowBufferStateForRGs(), defined at 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23506\_replaced([11187568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcBpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
[PR841286]
// whether low buffer state of any PLMN or RG is changed in this algorithm execution
rrmLowbufStateChanged = False
[End PR841286]
// compute the low buffer traffic state for all PLMNs
Execute computeLowBufferStateForPLMNs(), defined at 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31318\_replaced([11188465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
Make a list *P that contains all PLMNs in the cell.*
Make for each PLMN *p a list [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the letters "S" and "p" in a stylized font. The letters are rendered with a gradient effect, giving them a metallic or iridescent appearance. The "S" is larger and positioned to the left, while the "p" is smaller and positioned to the right and slightly below the "S". The background is white, and the letters are outlined with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] that contains all RGs of the PLMN.*
Initialize the remaining quota to be 100%, *R = 1.0.*
R0 = R.
// Find all PLMNs that are in the low buffer state. Set the expected low priority traffic target share to a high value for all RGs of each low buffer PLMN. Remove all low buffer PLMNs. The temporary target shares of other PLMNs are increased in proportion to their target shares.
For each PLMN [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "p ∈ P". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be rendered in a way that gives it a slightly metallic or iridescent sheen, with colors ranging from blue to orange and yellow. The letters are capitalized and lowercase, with the lowercase "p" on the left, followed by the symbol "∈" (element of), and then the uppercase "P" on the right. The text is set against a white background, and there is a black border around the entire image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
If *hasLowBufferPLMN(p) = True*
[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
R = R - rrmPlmnAvgSharep(t)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
For each RG [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "j ∈ Sₚ". The text is blurry and has a colorful halo effect around the letters.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a stylized font. The formula appears to be:
`rrmRG Exp LowTargetShare; (t) = max (rrmCellLevelRGMaxQuotaj - rrmRG Avg HighShare; (t), rdRGMin LowShareDL}`
The text is presented on two lines. The first line reads "rrmRG Exp LowTargetShare; (t) = max". The second line reads "(rrmCellLevelRGMaxQuotaj - rrmRG Avg HighShare; (t), rdRGMin LowShareDL}". The formula uses variables with subscripts and time dependencies indicated by `(t)`. The `max` function indicates that the value of `rrmRG Exp LowTargetShare; (t)` is the greater of the two expressions within the parentheses.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
[PR841286]
//Set per-slot low buffer state and low buffer state of each RG to be true
rrmRGLowBufSlot\_j(t) = True
rrmRGLowBufferState\_j = True
[End PR841286]
End For
Remove PLMN *p from* P**End If
End For
For each PLMN [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "p ∈ P". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be rendered in a way that gives it a slightly metallic or iridescent sheen, with colors ranging from blue to orange and yellow. The letters are capitalized and lowercase, with the lowercase "p" on the left, followed by the symbol "∈" (element of), and then the uppercase "P" on the right. The text is set against a white background, and there is a black border around the entire image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]
 If R <= 0
  [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, stylized font. The equation appears to be:
`rrrmPlm nTempTargetShare p(t) = 0`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]
 Else
  [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$rrmPlmTempTargetShare\_i(t) = \frac{rrmPlmTargetShare\_{e\_p} \cdot R}{\sum\_{l \in \epsilon} rrmPlmTargetShare\_l}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]
 End If
End For
// Find all PLMNs for which the average resource usage for high priority traffic is larger than the temporary PLMN target share. Set the expected low priority traffic target share to the smallest value for all RGs of such a PLMN. Remove all identified PLMNs. The temporary target shares of other PLMNs are decreased in proportion to their PLMN target shares. If resource sharing among PLMNs is not allowed, PLMN temporary target share is limited by the maximum of average resource usage for high priority traffic and the PLMN target share of the cell resource.
While (True)
For each PLMN [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains the text "p ∈ P". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be rendered in a way that gives it a slightly metallic or iridescent sheen, with colors ranging from blue to orange and yellow. The letters are capitalized and lowercase, with the lowercase "p" on the left, followed by the symbol "∈" (element of), and then the uppercase "P" on the right. If ([image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality. The text in the image is: `rmmPlmnAughShare, (t) >= rmmPlmnTempTargetShare, (t)`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9])
[image-10][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font against a white background. The equation appears to be:
$rrmPlmNtTargetShare\_{p}(t) = rrmPlmNAvgHighShare\_{j}(t)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10]
[image-11][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
R = R - rrmPlmnAvgHighSharej(t)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11]
For each RG [image-12][image\_desc]The image contains the text "j ∈ Sₚ". The text is blurry and has a colorful halo effect.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.013.png[/image\_path][/image-12]
[image-13][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font against a dark background. The equation appears to be:
`rrrmRG Exp Low TargetShare(t) = rdRGM(nLowShareDL)`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.014.png[/image\_path][/image-13]
[PR841286]
//Set per-slot low buffer state and low buffer state of each RG to be false
rrmRGLowBufSlot\_j(t) = False
rrmRGLowBufferState\_j = False
[End PR841286]
End For
Remove PLMN *p from* P**
End If
End For
If at least one PLMN was removed from *P in the last for loop and* P is nonempty**For each PLMN [image-14][image\_desc]The image contains the text "p ∈ P". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be rendered in a way that gives it a slightly metallic or iridescent sheen, with colors ranging from blue to orange and yellow. The letters are capitalized and lowercase, with the lowercase "p" on the left, followed by the symbol "∈" (element of), and then the uppercase "P" on the right. The text is set against a white background, and there is a black border around the entire image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.015.png[/image\_path][/image-14]
If R <= 0
[image-15][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, stylized font. The equation appears to be:
`rrrmPlm nTempTargetShare p(t) = 0`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.007.png[/image\_path][/image-15]
Else

End If
End For
Else
If rrmAllowPlmnResourceSharing = False
For each PLMN [image-17][image\_desc]The image contains the text "p ∈ P". The letters are capitalized and lowercase, with the lowercase "p" on the left, followed by the symbol "∈" (element of), and then the uppercase "P" on the right. End For

Break
End If
End While
// Compute expected low priority traffic target share for each RG of each PLMN contained in P, according to the computed PLMN temporary target share.
For each PLMN [image-19][image\_desc]The image contains the text "p ∈ P". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be rendered in a way that gives it a slightly metallic or iridescent sheen, with colors ranging from blue to orange and yellow. The letters are capitalized and lowercase, with the lowercase "p" on the left, followed by the symbol "∈" (element of), and then the uppercase "P" on the right. The text is set against a white background, and there is a black border around the entire image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14483.009.png[/image\_path][/image-19]
 Execute computeExpLowTargetSharesForRGsOfPLMN(*p), defined at 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31450\_replaced([11188146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*
End For
[PR841286]
If (rrmLowbufStateChanged = True)
For each RG j in the cell
rrmRgIterm\_j(t) = 0
End For
End If
[End PR841286]
 
Notations:
rrmRGExpLowTargetShare\_j(t): the expected low priority traffic target share for RG j, computed at slot t.
computeFullBufferTempTargetShares(): compute the temporary target shares of all PLMNs and RGs, assuming that each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and the actual high priority traffic, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31093\_replaced([11188307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
computeLowBufferStateForRGs(): compute the low buffer traffic state for all RGs, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23506\_replaced([11187568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcBpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
computeLowBufferStateForPLMNs(): compute the low buffer traffic state for all PLMNs, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31318\_replaced([11188465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
P: the set of PLMNs in the cell, whose temporary target share hasn’t been computed.
S\_p: the set of RGs of PLMN p.
R: remaining quota, 0 <= R <= 1.0
R0: initial quota, 0 <= R0 <= 1.0
hasLowBufferPLMN(p): whether PLMN p is in the low buffer traffic state, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31318\_replaced([11188465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmPlmnAvgShare\_p(t): average resource share of PLMN p at slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31465\_replaced([11188649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd20fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmCellLevelRGMaxQuota\_j: cell level maximum quota of RG j, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31404\_replaced([11188028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcoufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmRGAvgHighShare\_j(t): average resource share for high priority traffic of RG j at slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rdRGMinLowShareDL: R&D parameter, minimum resource share given to an RG for its low priority traffic, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23511\_replaced([11193687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmPlmnTargetShare\_p: The target share of PLMN p, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7385\_replaced([11188019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcot_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmPlmnTempTargetShare\_p(t): The computed temporary target share of PLMN p at slot t.
rrmPlmnAvgHighShare\_p(t): average resource share for high priority traffic of PLMN p at slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31465\_replaced([11188649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd20fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmAllowPlmnResourceSharing: whether PLMN resource sharing is allowed or not, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7385\_replaced([11188019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcot_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
computeExpLowTargetSharesForRGsOfPLMN(*p): compute the low priority target share for all RGs of PLMN p, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31450\_replaced([11188146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*
[PR841286]
rrmLowbufStateChanged: whether low buffer state of any PLMN or RG is changed in this algorithm execution. The variable is also used/changed in other requirements.
rrmRgIterm\_j: the integral term of PI control for RG j, see [11188517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
rrmRGLowBufSlot\_j(t): whether RG j has passed the per-slot low buffer state check in slot t, see [12711058.](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_44W3YGYwEfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)
rrmRGLowBufferState\_j: where RG j is in low buffer state, see [12711058.](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_44W3YGYwEfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)
[End PR841286]

---

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11188146`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
Computation of low priority traffic target shares for RGs of a PLMN

---

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11188299`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
11188299:
PLMN Aware Scheduler
This function computeExpLowTargetSharesForRGsOfPLMN(*p) computes the low priority traffic target share for all RGs of a PLMN* p, which is a PLMN that has neither low buffer traffic nor larger high priority traffic than the temporary target share.**The procedure is listed as follows. The current slot is denoted *t.*
 
Output: the expected low priority traffic target share rrmRGExpLowTargetShare\_j(t) for each RG *j of PLMN* p.**
 
[PR841286]
//Compute low buffer state for all RGs of PLMN p
Execute computeLowBufferRGStateForPlmn(p) as per [12711058](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_44W3YGYwEfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
[End PR841286]
// initialize the resource available for all RGs of the PLMN to be equal to the temporary PLMN target share.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font on a white background. The equation is:
$R\_p = rrmPlmnTargetShare\_p(t)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14485.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
// Find all RGs in the PLMN that have low buffer traffic. Set the expected low priority traffic target share of all identified RGs to a high value. Remove all identified RGs.
[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$f = \frac{R\_p}{\sum\_{\text{each } RG \in S\_p} \text{rrmRGTtargetShare}*k}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14485.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
rrmExistLowBufferRGs = False
For each RG [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "j ∈ Sₚ". The text is blurry and has a colorful halo effect around the letters.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14485.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
 [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains text that appears to be a variable assignment in a programming context. The text is blurry and difficult to read with certainty, but it seems to be:
`rrmRG DynamicTargetShare = rrmRG TargetShare`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14485.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
 [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a blurry, light-colored font against a dark background. The formula appears to be:
`rrnRGTempTar getShare, = min {rrnRGTarget getShare, f, rrnC&illLevelRGMaxQuota, }`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14485.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
 If hasLowBuffer(*j) = True*
  [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font against a white background. The equation is:
$R\_p = R\_p - rrrRG AvgShare\_j(t)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14485.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]
[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a stylized font. The formula is:
rrmRG Exp LowTargetShare; (t) = max {rrmCell LevelRGMaxQuota; rrmRGAvgHighShare; (t), rdRGM inLow ShareDL}
The formula appears to define a variable `rrmRG Exp LowTargetShare` at time `t` as the maximum of two values: `rrmCell LevelRGMaxQuota` and the result of `rrmRGAvgHighShare` at time `t` minus `rdRGM inLow ShareDL`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14485.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]

The background is white, and the text is enclosed within a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14485.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]*
  rrmExistLowBufferRGs = True
 End If
End For

The first line reads:
`rrmRG Exp LowTargetShare; (t)`
The second line starts with an equals sign and a "max" function, indicating that the value is the maximum of the two arguments provided within the curly braces:
`= max{rrmRGTempTargetShare;`
`rrmRG AvgHighShare; (t), rdRGM inLow ShareDL}`
The formula appears to be defining a variable `rrmRG Exp LowTargetShare` at time `t` as the maximum of two values: `rrmRGTempTargetShare` and a combination of `rrmRG AvgHighShare` at time `t` and `rdRGM inLow ShareDL`. The subscript `j` is present in `TargetShare`, `TempTargetShare`, and `AvgHighShare`, suggesting these variables might be indexed by `j`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14485.029.png[/image\_path][/image-42]
End For
 
Notations:
rrmPlmnTempTargetShare\_p(t): The temporary target share of PLMN p at slot t, it is computed in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31424\_replaced([11188061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
R\_p: remaining quota for PLMN p, 0 <= R\_p <= 1.0.
S\_p: the set of RGs of PLMN p, whose low priority traffic target share hasn’t been computed.
rrmRGTargetShare\_j: target share of RG j, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7390\_replaced([11189080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvfE9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmExistLowBufferRGs: if at least one RG is in low-buffer state for the PLMN, the value is True.
rrmRGDynamicTargetShare\_j: The dynamic target share computed for an RG j *in S\_p* according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28248\_replaced([11189319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvfE_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**rrmRGTempTargetShare\_j: temporary target share (including both high priority and low priority traffic) for RG j
rrmCellLevelRGMaxQuota\_j: cell level maximum quota of RG j, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31404\_replaced([11188028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcoufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
hasLowBuffer(j): whether RG j is in the low buffer traffic state, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23506\_replaced([11187568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcBpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmRGAvgShare\_j(t): average resource share of RG *j at slot* t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**
rrmRGAvgHighShare\_j(t): average resource share for high priority traffic of RG *j at slot* t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*
rrmRGExpLowTargetShare\_j(t): the expected low priority traffic target share for RG j, computed at slot t.
rdRGMinLowShareDL: R&D parameter, minimum resource share given to an RG for its low priority traffic, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23511\_replaced([11193687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `11188307`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
Compute temporary target share of PLMNs and RGs assuming each RG has full buffer low priority traffic

---

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11188456`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
11188456:
PLMN Aware Scheduler
This procedure computes the temporary target shares of all PLMNs and RGs, assuming that each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and actual (current) high priority traffic. These temporary target shares are stored in variables rrmPLMNFullBufferTempTargetShare\_p for every PLMN p, rrmRGFullBufferTempTargetShare\_j for every RG j, which will be used later for low buffer state check. The procedure is listed as follows. The current slot is denoted t.
 
Function computeFullBufferTempTargetShares()
Make a list P that contains all configured PLMNs in the cell.
Make for each PLMN [image-0][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, and abstract representation of a person's face, possibly a woman, with a focus on the eye area. The colors are muted, with shades of beige, brown, and white, and there are hints of blue and orange. The lighting creates a soft, diffused effect, with highlights and shadows that add depth and dimension. The overall impression is one of a dreamlike or ethereal quality, with the features of the face blending into one another. The image is highly stylized and artistic, with a focus on form and color rather than precise detail.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14487.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] a list [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Sp" in a stylized font. The letters are rendered with a gradient effect, transitioning from a warm orange-brown at the bottom left to a cool blue at the top right. The letters are composed of small, pixelated squares, giving them a digital or mosaic appearance. The background is white, and the text is enclosed within a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14487.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] that contains all RGs of the PLMN.
Initialize the remaining quota to be equal to 100%, R = 1.0.
// initialize full buffer temporary target share of each PLMN to the PLMN target share of the cell resource.
For each PLMN [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "p ∈ P". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be rendered in a way that gives it a slightly metallic or iridescent sheen, with colors ranging from blue to orange and yellow. The letters are capitalized and lowercase, with the lowercase "p" on the left, followed by the symbol "∈" (element of), and then the uppercase "P" on the right. The text is set against a white background, and there is a black border around the entire image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14487.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
 [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation is:
`rmmPfullBuf f erTempT ar getSharep = R · rmmPT ar getSharep`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14487.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
End For
// Find all PLMNs for which the average resource usage for high priority traffic is larger than the full buffer temporary PLMN target share. Set the full buffer temporary target share to the average resource usage for high priority traffic, for all RGs of such a PLMN. Remove all identified PLMNs. The full buffer temporary target shares of other PLMNs are decreased in proportion to their PLMN target shares. This process runs continuously until all PLMNs satisfying the condition are removed.
While (True)
 For each PLMN [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "p ∈ P". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be rendered in a way that gives it a slightly metallic or iridescent sheen, with colors ranging from blue to orange and yellow. The letters are capitalized and lowercase, with the lowercase "p" on the left, followed by the symbol "∈" (element of), and then the uppercase "P" on the right. The text is set against a white background, and there is a black border around the entire image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14487.003.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

Notations:
P: the set of PLMNs in the cell, whose temporary target shares under full buffer low priority traffic condition haven’t been computed.
S\_p: the set of RGs of the PLMN p, whose temporary target shares under full buffer low priority traffic condition haven’t been computed.
R: remaining quota, 0 <= R <= 1.0
R\_p: remaining quota for PLMN p, 0<=R\_p<=1.0
rrmPlmnFullBufferTempTargetShare\_p: temporary target share (including both high priority and low priority traffic) for PLMN p, assuming each PLMN has full buffer low priority traffic and the actual high priority traffic.
rrmRGFullBufferTempTargetShare\_j: temporary target share (including both high priority and low priority traffic) for RG j, assuming each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and the actual high priority traffic.
rrmPlmnTargetShare\_p: target share of PLMN p, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7385\_replaced([11188019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcot_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmRGTargetShare\_j: target share of RG j, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7390\_replaced([11189080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvfE9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmRGMaxQuota\_j: maximum quota of RG j.
rrmCellLevelRGMaxQuota\_j: maximum quota of RG j measured in terms of cell resource, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31404\_replaced([11188028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcoufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmPlmnAvgHighShare\_p(t): average resource share for high priority traffic of PLMN p at slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31465\_replaced([11188649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd20fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmRGAvgHighShare\_j(t): average resource share for high priority traffic of RG j at slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
rrmPlmnAvgShare\_p(t): average resource share for PLMN p at slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31465\_replaced([11188649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd20fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmRGAvgShare\_j(t): average resource share of RG j at slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
rrmAllowPlmnResourceSharing: whether PLMN resource sharing is allowed or not. The value is True or False, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7385\_replaced([11188019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcot_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `11188465`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
Determine Low Buffer Traffic States of PLMNs

---

**3.29.12.2.0-4.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11188511`)

PLMN Aware Scheduler
11188511:
PLMN Aware Scheduler
The low buffer traffic state check for PLMNs is similar to that for RGs.
To decide the low buffer traffic states of all PLMNs, gNB-DU needs to execute computeLowBufferStateForPLMNs() after executing computeFullBufferTempTargetShares(). 
A PLMN p is considered to have low buffer traffic, i.e. hasLowBufferPLMN(p) *= True, if either of the following two conditions has been satisfied in the past rdMinLowBufferSlotsDL slots. Once both conditions are unsatisfied, the PLMN is considered not to have low buffer traffic, i.e. hasLowBufferPLMN(p)* = False.**a) rrmPlmnFullBufferTempTargetShare\_p for PLMN p is equal to 0 and no resource is assigned to PLMN p in the slot.
b) rrmPlmnFullBufferTempTargetShare\_p for PLMN p is greater than 0 and the average resource share of PLMN p rrmPlmnAvgShare\_p(t) is below rdAvgShareToleranceDL \* rrmPlmnFullBufferTempTargetShare\_p in the slot.
hasLowBufferPLMN(p) returns the current low buffer traffic state of PLMN *p.*//After executing computeLowBufferStateForPLMNs(),* rrmPlmnLowBufferState\_p stores the low buffer state of PLMN *p. rrmPlmnLowBufferState\_p = True, if PLMN* p is in low buffer state.***
[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrm PlmmLowBufferStatep". The text is in a dark color against a light, slightly blurred background. The letters are mostly clear, but some are slightly distorted or have a slight blur. The word "rrrm" appears at the beginning, followed by "PlmmLowBufferStatep".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14489.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], for every PLMN *p*

Output: return True, if PLMN *p is in low buffer traffic state, Otherwise, return False*
 
Function hasLowBufferPLMN(*p)*
 Return [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrm PlmmLowBufferStatep". The text is in a dark color against a light, slightly blurred background. The letters are mostly clear, but some are slightly distorted or have a slight blur. The word "rrrm" appears at the beginning, followed by "PlmmLowBufferStatep".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14489.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11]
End Function
 
Notations:
rrmPlmnAvgShare\_p(t): Average resource share for PLMN *p at slot* t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31465\_replaced([11188649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd20fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**rdAvgShareToleranceDL: R&D parameter, the tolerance level for checking average resource share for an RG, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23513\_replaced([11193707](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8mPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rdMinLowBufferSlotsDL: R&D parameter, the minimum number of consecutive slots for low buffer traffic state checking, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23515\_replaced([11193715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvu8m_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
rrmPlmnLowBufSlot\_p(t): whether PLMN *p has passed low buffer state check *for slot* t.***
rrmPlmnLowBufferState\_p: current low buffer traffic state of PLMN *p*
rrmPlmnFullBufferTempTargetShare\_p: temporary target share (including both high priority and low priority traffic) for PLMN *p, assuming each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and the actual high priority traffic, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31093\_replaced([11188307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*
rrmPlmnInstShare\_p(t): the instantaneous resource share used by PLMN p in slot t, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31465\_replaced([11188649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd20fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
[PR841286]
rrmLowbufStateChanged: whether low buffer state of any PLMN or RG is changed in this algorithm execution, see [11188061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvdPw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
[End PR841286]
