* The estimated average amount of resource for nonGBR and deprioritized GBR iniTx which satisfies the intent throughput of RG i, rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t) (see [13484174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qrm7qT-3EfG4gL1xLdIWUw)).
* The estimated average amount of resource demanded by high priority traffic of RG i, rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t).
  + rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t) = min{rrmRGAvgHighShare\_i(t), rrmRGMaxQuota\_i}. For rrmRGAvgHighShare\_i(t), see [11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* [CB016273-B] The estimated average amount of resource demanded by NBR (in NBR category) traffic of RG i, rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t).
  + rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t) = rrmRGEstAvgHighNbrDemand\_i(t) - rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t). For rrmRGEstAvgHighNbrDemand\_i(t), see [12483734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_neRxYw-lEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). [End CB016273-B]

Output: the RG target share rrmRGTargetSharei for each RG i.

Algorithm Description:

Step 1: Reserve minQuota. For each RG, set rrmRGTargetShare = rrmRGMinQuota to guarantee rrmRGMinQuota for this RG. Update each RG’s rrmRGEstAvgHighResource by subtracting the reserved rrmRGMinQuota.

Step 2: Allocate remaining common pool to each RG based on estimated demand from high priority traffic. If the sum of rrmRGEstAvgHighResource of all RGs exceeds the remaining common pool, then rrmRGEstAvgHighResource of each RG shall proportionally decrease to match the remaining common pool. rrmRGTargetShare is increased by rrmRGEstAvgHighResource. Update each RG's rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource by subtracting the available resources in rrmRGTargetShare for nonGBR and deprioritized GBR iniTx.

Step 3: Allocate remaining common pool to each RG based on the estimated resource for satisfying intent throughput. If the sum of rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource of all RGs exceeds the remaining common pool, then rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource of each RG shall proportionally decrease to match the remaining common pool. rrmRGTargetShare is increased by rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource. [CB016273-B] Update each RG's rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource by subtracting the available resources in rrmRGTargetShare for nonGBR and deprioritized GBR iniTx.

Step 4: Allocate remaining common pool to each RG based on estimated demand from NBR category traffic. If the sum of rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource of all RGs exceeds the remaining common pool, then rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource of each RG shall proportionally decrease to match the remaining common pool. rrmRGTargetShare is increased by rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource.

[End CB016273-B]

Step 5: Distribute the remaining common pool to the target shares of all RGs in proportion to their average total scheduling weights. This is the same as Step 2 of RG target share calculation of QoS fairness slice aware scheduler (see [11189488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvfsCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

**Detail of Algorithm (Pseudo-code is reference for implementation, detail can be decided in EFS)**

Step 1: Reserve minQuota.

R = 1.0

For each RG i in S

rrmRGTargetShare\_i = rrmRGMinQuota\_i

R -= rrmRGMinQuota\_i

ngbrResource\_i = max{rrmRGMinQuota\_i – rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t), 0}

rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t) = max{0, rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t) - rrmRGMinQuota\_i}

End For

Step 2: Allocate remaining common pool to each RG based on estimated demand from high priority traffic.

If (R > 0) and (sum\_{RG i in S} {rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t)} > 0)

y = sum\_{RG i in S} {rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t)} / R

If (y > 1.0)

For each RG i in S, set rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t) /= y

End If

For each RG i in S

rrmRGTargetShare\_i += rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t)

R -= rrmRGEstAvgHighResource\_i(t)

rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t) = max{0, rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t) - ngbrResource\_i}

rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t) = min{rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t), rrmRGMaxQuota\_i - rrmRGTargetShare\_i}

End For

End If

Step 3: Allocate remaining common pool to each RG based on the estimated resource for satisfying intent throughput.

If (R > 0) and (sum\_{RG i in S} {rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t)} > 0)

y = sum\_{RG i in S} {rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t)} / R

If (y > 1.0)

For each RG i in S, set rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t) /= y

End If

For each RG i in S

rrmRGTargetShare\_i += rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t)

R -= rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t)

ngbrResource += rrmRGEstAvgIntentResource\_i(t)

[CB016273-B]

rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t) = max{0, rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t) - ngbrResource\_i}

rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t) = min{rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t), rrmRGMaxQuota\_i - rrmRGTargetShare\_i}

[End CB016273-B]

End For

End If

[CB016273-B]

Step 4: Allocate remaining common pool to each RG based on estimated demand from NBR category traffic.

If (R > 0) and (sum\_{RG i in S} {rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t)} > 0)

y = sum\_{RG i in S} {rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t)} / R

If (y > 1.0)

For each RG i in S, set rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t) /= y

End If

For each RG i in S

rrmRGTargetShare\_i += rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t)

R -= rrmRGEstAvgNbrResource\_i(t)

End For

End If

[End CB016273-B]

Step 5: Distribute the remaining common pool to all RGs in proportion to their average total scheduling weights.

If (R > 0)

Sort RGs in S, the RGs that tend to reach maximum quota earlier during resource distribution are put at the front of S.

totW = 0

For each RG i in S,

w\_i = max(10^(-6), rrmRgAvgSchedulWeight\_i(t))

totW += w\_i

End For

Sort RGs in S, in increasing order of (rrmRGMaxQuota\_i - rrmRGTargetShare\_i) / w\_i for RG i

For each RG in the sorted list, distribute R in proportion to w\_i, while ensuring that resulting target share doesn’t exceed the max quota.

For k = 1 to size(S)  (k is the RG index in the sorted list S)

d = R \* w\_k / totW

d = min(d, rrmRGMaxQuota\_k - rrmRGTargetShare\_k)

rrmRGTargetShare\_k += d

R -= d

totW -= w\_k

End For

End If

---

##### 3.29.12.3.4.0-1 PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO Introduction (ID: 11191477)

**3.29.12.3.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11191500`)

PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
11191500:
PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
If [PR715212] basic [End PR715212] MU-MIMO is activated for a cell, a specialized PRB bookkeeping method needs to be applied. [Before PR715212] If NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = true, we use the spectral efficiency based (SE-based) PRB bookkeeping (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8339\_replaced([11191551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kviIQPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); otherwise, [End PR715212] if NRCELL/actDlMuMimo = true, we use the pair size based (PS-based) PRB bookkeeping (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8156\_replaced([11191504](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvhhPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

With PS-based PRB bookkeeping, if N UEs are spatially multiplexed for one PRB, then for each of the N participating UEs, the PRB is counted as 1/N.

[Before PR715212]

With SE-based PRB bookkeeping, if a UE has a spectral efficiency of su\_se and a rank of su\_rank under SU-MIMO and it has a spectral efficiency of mu\_se and a rank of mu\_rank under MU-MIMO, then each PRB allocated to the UE under MU-MIMO is counted as the equivalent number of PRBs under SU-MIMO, i.e., (mu\_se*mu\_rank)/(su\_se*su\_rank).

[End PR715212]

To facilitate the PRB bookkeeping for MU-MIMO, the PRB bookkeeping factor [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmPrbBkFactor\_u(t)". The text is in a light color against a white background, with some blurriness. The characters are in a sans-serif font. The subscript "u" is present below and to the right of "Factor". The entire expression is enclosed in parentheses with "t" inside.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14593.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] is introduced. It takes a positive value lower than or equal to 1.0. One PRB allocated under MU-MIMO for a UE u in slot t is counted as [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmPrbBkFactor\_u(t)". The text is in a mathematical or scientific context, likely representing a variable or function. The characters are rendered in a serif font and appear to be in a dark color against a light background. The subscript "u" is present below and to the right of "Factor", and the entire expression is followed by "(t)" in parentheses, suggesting a dependence on time.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14593.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] PRB.

---

##### 3.29.12.3.4.0-2 PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO Pair Size based PRB Bookkeeping (ID: 11191504)

**3.29.12.3.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11191548`)

PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
11191548:
PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
Suppose a number of PRBs are allocated to UE u in slot t and these PRBs can be divided into K parts. The k-th (k=1…K) part contains [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the letters "P" and "R" in a stylized font. The letters are rendered in a gradient of colors, with shades of orange, yellow, and blue visible. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] PRBs and is spatially multiplexed by [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N" followed by a subscript "k". The letters are stylized with a gradient of colors, appearing to be a blend of orange, yellow, blue, and purple. The background is white, and the text is enclosed within a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] UEs (see PDSCH allocation at 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Then the PRB bookkeeping factor is

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmPrbBkFactor\_u(t) = \frac{\sum\_{k=1}^{K} P\_k / N\_k}{\sum\_{k=1}^{K} P\_k}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]  .

For the special case that no MU-MIMO DL communication is happening in slot t, the PRB bookkeeping factor of each scheduled UE u is

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "rrmPrbBkFactoru(t) = 1.0". The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3].

Considering the effects of MU-MIMO, the two PRB related metrics [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstUePrbAlloc\_u(t)". The text is in a light color against a white background, and it is slightly blurry. The characters are in a sans-serif font. The subscript "u" is smaller than the other characters and is positioned below the line of text. The entire text is enclosed in parentheses.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7577\_replaced([11190661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6K_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstUeNgbrPrbAllocu(t)". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7536\_replaced([11185805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvV7B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be multiplied by the PRB bookkeeping factor [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "rrmPrbBkFactor\_u(t)". The text is in a light color against a white background, with some blurriness. The expression appears to be a function of 't' and has a subscript 'u'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6].

In addition, the computation of [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstSasPrbAlloc(t)". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are in lowercase, except for the "S", "P", and "A" which are capitalized. The text is enclosed in a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7] should be done according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7577\_replaced([11190661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6K_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The bookkeeping factors of all UEs are implicitly considered because [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstSasPrbAlloc(t)". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are in lowercase, except for the "S", "P", and "A" which are capitalized. The text is enclosed in a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.008.png[/image\_path][/image-8] is derived from [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstUePrbAlloc\_u(t)". The number of PRBs that are free in PDSCH [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmInstPrbFree(t)" in a blurry, italicized font. The text is dark blue with a slight yellow and white hue, set against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14595.010.png[/image\_path][/image-10] should be computed according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7577\_replaced([11190661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6K_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

##### 3.29.12.3.4.0-3 PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO Spectral Efficiency based PRB Bookkeeping (deprecated) (ID: 11191551)

**3.29.12.3.4.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11191660`)

PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
11191660:
PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
[Before PR715212]

Suppose a UE u is spatially multiplexed with other UEs in slot t, the selected MCS index of UE u for MU-MIMO scheduling is rrmDlMuMimoMcs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7435\_replaced([11145579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6PPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the selected MCS index of UE u for SU-MIMO scheduling is rrmDlProposedMcs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3045\_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3050\_replaced([11129035](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Based on the PDSCH configuration and the MCS index (rrmDlMuMimoMcs or rrmDlProposedMcs), the spectral efficiency can be determined from the tables in Clause 5.1.3.1 of 3GPP specification 38.214. Let the spectral efficiency of UE u in slot t for MU-MIMO scheduling and SU-MIMO scheduling be [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmMuseu(t)". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] and [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmsuseu(t)". The text is in a light color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1], respectively. Due to DL rank adaptation, the DL rank of a UE may downgrade after it is paired (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7563\_replaced([11142677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5ldPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Let the DL rank of UE u in slot t for MU-MIMO scheduling and SU-MIMO scheduling be [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rankMuUe\_u(t)". The text is blurry and appears to be in a mathematical context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] and [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rankSuUe\_u(t)". The text is in a light color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred, but are still legible. The text is written in a mathematical notation, with "u" as a subscript to "e".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3], respectively.

The PRB bookkeeping factor of UE u is

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmPrbBkFactor\_u(t) = \frac{rrmMuSe\_u(t) \cdot rankMuUe\_u(t)}{rrmSuSe\_u(t) \cdot rankSuUe\_u(t)}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]  .

For the special case that no MU-MIMO DL communication is happening to UE u in slot t in the final schedule, the PRB bookkeeping factor of UE u is

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "rrmPrbBkFactoru(t) = 1.0". The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5].

Considering the effects of MU-MIMO, the metrics [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstUePrbAlloc\_u(t)". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in a mathematical or technical context. The letters are slightly blurred, but the overall meaning is discernible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7577\_replaced([11190661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6K_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal to the total number of PRBs allocated to UE u in slot t under MU-MIMO (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7431\_replaced([11144204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7arPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7530\_replaced([11145129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_FBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) multiplied by the PRB bookkeeping factor [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "rrmPrbBkFactor\_u(t)". The text is in a light color against a white background, with some blurriness. The expression is written in a standard mathematical font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]. The metric [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstUeNgbrPrbAllocu(t)". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8] (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7536\_replaced([11185805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvV7B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal to the total number of PRBs allocated to initial transmissions of non-GBR and deprioritized GBR DRBs of UE u under SU-MIMO (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In addition, the computation of [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstSasPrbAlloc(t)". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are in lowercase, except for the "S", "P", and "A" which are capitalized. The text is enclosed in a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9] should be done according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7577\_replaced([11190661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6K_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The bookkeeping factors of all UEs are implicitly considered because [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstSasPrbAlloc(t)". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are in lowercase, except for the "S", "P", and "A" which are capitalized. The text is enclosed in a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.010.png[/image\_path][/image-10] is derived from [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrrmInstUePrbAlloc\_u(t)". The text is in a light color against a white background, and it is slightly blurry. The characters are in a sans-serif font. The subscript "u" is smaller than the other characters and is positioned below the line of text. The entire text is enclosed in parentheses.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.011.png[/image\_path][/image-11].

[image-12][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmInstPrbFree(t)" in a blurry, italicized font. The text is dark blue with a slight yellow and white hue, set against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14598.012.png[/image\_path][/image-12] is equal to the total number of unallocated PRBs in slot t under MU-MIMO (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7577\_replaced([11190661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6K_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End PR715212]

##### 3.29.12.3.4.0-4 PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO Pair Size based PRB Bookkeeping Example (ID: 11191665)

**3.29.12.3.4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11191741`)

PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
11191741:
PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
Consider the following example of PDSCH allocation for 5 UEs in a slot t. Assume the number of PRBs for PDSCH is equal to 16.

In the following figure, the shaded PRBs are allocated to a UE but are not of interest to the slice aware scheduler (belong to a traffic class other than the high and low priority traffic classes as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). On the other hand, the unshaded PRBs are of interest to the slice aware scheduler.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a bar chart that displays the allocation of Resource Blocks (PRBs) to different User Equipments (UEs). The x-axis represents the PRB number, ranging from 0 to 16. The y-axis implicitly represents the UEs, with each horizontal bar corresponding to a specific UE.
Here's a breakdown of the allocation for each UE:
\* **UE1:** Occupies PRBs from 0 to 5.
\* **UE2:** Occupies PRBs from 5 to 14, with the last PRB (14) having a hatched pattern, possibly indicating a partial allocation or a different type of resource.
\* **UE3:** Occupies PRBs from 0 to 8.
\* **UE4:** Occupies PRBs from 8 to 12.
\* **UE5:** Occupies PRBs from 0 to 1, with a hatched pattern on PRB 1. It then occupies PRBs from 2 to 7.
The chart visually represents how PRBs are distributed among different UEs, likely in a cellular network context. The hatched patterns on PRB 1 for UE5 and PRB 14 for UE2 might signify specific configurations or limitations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

In computing rrmPrbBkFactor, all PRBs allocated to a UE in a DL slot should be considered.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
`rrmPrbBkFactorUE1(t) = 5/3 / 5 = 0.33`
The equation appears to be calculating a factor related to `rrmPrbBkFactorUE1` at time `t`. The calculation involves a fraction `5/3` divided by `5`, resulting in `0.33`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmPrbBkFactor\_{UE2}(t) = (\frac{5}{3} + \frac{3}{2} + 2) / (5 + 3 + 2) = 0.52$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a sans-serif font. The equation reads:
`rrmPrbBkFactor_UB3(t) = 7/3 / 7 = 0.33`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
rrmPrbBkFactorUE4(t) = (3/3 + 3/2) / (3 + 3) = 0.42[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
`rrmPrbBkFactor_UES(t) = 10/3 / 10 = 0.33`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

rrmInstUePrbAlloc is equal to the product of the bookkeeping factor and the number of PRBs allocated to a UE in the MU-MIMO schedule.

[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmInstUePrbAllocUE1(t)". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be mathematical notation. The letters are slightly blurred, but are still legible. The text is black and is set against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]= [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a light gray font on a white background. The equation reads: `rrmPrbBkFactorUE1(t) · 5 = 1.65`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]

[image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
`rrmnInstUePrbAllocUE2(t) = rrmuPrbBkFfactorUE2(t) * 10 = 5.2`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8]

[image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, stylized font. The equation reads:
`rrmlnstUePrbAlloc_UE3(t) = rrmPrbBkF actor_UE3(t) · 7 = 2.31`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9]

[image-10][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, sans-serif font. The equation reads:
`rrmmlnstUePrbAllocUE4(t) = rrmuPrbBkFfactorUE4(t) · 6 = 2.52`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10]

[image-11][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
`rrmnInstUePrbAlloc_UES(t) = rrmuPrbBkFactor_UES(t) * 10 = 3.3`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11]

According to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7577\_replaced([11190661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6K_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), [image-12][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation is:
$rmInstUeSasPrbAlloc\_u(t) = rmInstUeSasPrbRatio\_u(t) \cdot rmInstUePrbAlloc\_u(t)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.013.png[/image\_path][/image-12].

[image-13][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a light blue background. The equation is: `rrrmInstUeSasPrbAllocUE1(t) = 1.0 * 1.65 = 1.65`. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.014.png[/image\_path][/image-13]

[image-14][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
`rrrmInstUeSasPrbAllocUE2(t) = 9/10 * 5.2 = 4.68`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.015.png[/image\_path][/image-14]

[image-15][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font on a white background. The equation reads: "rrrmInstUeSasPrbAllocUES(t) = 1.0 \* 2.31 = 2.31".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.016.png[/image\_path][/image-15]

[image-16][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a light blue color on a white background. The equation reads: "rrrmInstUeSasPrbAllocUE4(t) = 1.0 \* 2.52 = 2.52".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.017.png[/image\_path][/image-16]

[image-17][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a blurry, light-colored font on a white background. The equation reads: "rrrmInstUeSasPrbAllocues(t) = 8/10 \* 3.3 = 2.64".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.018.png[/image\_path][/image-17]

In computing rrmInstUeSasPrbAlloc, only PRBs allocated to a UE and of interest to the slice aware scheduler in a DL slot should be considered.

[image-18][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmInstSasPrbAlloc(t) = \sum\_{each UE u} rrmInstUeSasPrbAlloc\_u(t) = 13.8$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.019.png[/image\_path][/image-18]

[image-19][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rrmInstPrbFree(t) = 1". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in a mathematical or programming context. The background is a light, slightly textured white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.020.png[/image\_path][/image-19]

[image-20][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
`rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) = rrmInstSasPrbAlloc(t) + rrmInstPrbFree(t) = 14.8`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14600.021.png[/image\_path][/image-20]

##### 3.29.12.3.4.0-5 PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO Spectral Efficiency based PRB Bookkeeping Example (deprecated) (ID: 11191746)

**3.29.12.3.4.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11191967`)

PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
11191967:
PRB Bookkeeping for MU-MIMO
[Before PR715212]

Consider the following example of PDSCH allocation for 6 UEs in a slot t. DL rank adaptation is applied for MU pairing. The total number of PRBs for PDSCH is equal to 20. The upper figure shows the SU-MIMO PRB allocation while the lower figure shows the MU-MIMO PRB allocation. The spectral efficiencies and DL ranks for both cases are listed beside the figures. The shaded PRBs are allocated to a UE but are not of interest to the slice aware scheduler (belong to a traffic class other than the high and low priority traffic classes as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7392\_replaced([11190186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvg6I_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). On the other hand, the unshaded PRBs are of interest to the slice aware scheduler. Suppose that in the example (see both figures), all allocated PRBs that are of interest to the slice aware scheduler are only used by initial transmissions of non-GBR DRBs.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams illustrating resource allocation for SU-MIMO and MU-MIMO, alongside two tables showing spectral efficiency and rank for different UEs.
The top diagram, labeled "SU-MIMO", shows a timeline from #PRB 0 to 20. Several User Equipments (UEs) are allocated different segments of this timeline. From left to right, UE1 is allocated PRBs 1-4, UE2 is allocated PRBs 5-6, UE3 is allocated PRBs 7-8, UE4 is allocated PRBs 9-10, UE5 is allocated PRBs 11-12, and UE6 is allocated PRBs 13-19. The first PRB (0) and the last PRB (20) are hatched, indicating they are not allocated.
The bottom diagram, labeled "MU-MIMO", also shows a timeline from #PRB 0 to 20. This diagram depicts a more complex allocation with multiple UEs sharing resources.
Row 1: UE1 is allocated PRBs 1-2, 9-10, and 12. UE4 is allocated PRBs 4-8. UE6 is allocated PRBs 13-19. PRB 0 and 3 are hatched.
Row 2: UE2 is allocated PRBs 1-3. UE5 is allocated PRBs 4-7. UE3 is allocated PRBs 8-9.
Row 3: UE3 is allocated PRBs 0-2. UE5 is allocated PRBs 3-5.
To the right of the SU-MIMO diagram is a table with two columns: "Spectral Efficiency" and "Rank". It lists six UEs (UE1 to UE6) with their corresponding spectral efficiency values and ranks.
To the right of the MU-MIMO diagram is a similar table, also with "Spectral Efficiency" and "Rank" columns. This table lists five UEs (UE1 to UE5) with their spectral efficiency values and ranks.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmPrbBkFactor\_{UE1}(t) = \frac{rrmMuSe\_{UE1}(t) \cdot rankMuUe\_{UE1}(t)}{rrmSuSe\_{UE1}(t) \cdot rankSuUe\_{UE1}(t)} = \frac{1.9141 \cdot 2}{3.0293 \cdot 2} = 0.63$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14603.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmPrbBkFactor\_{UE2}(t) = \frac{rrmMuSe\_{UE2}(t) \cdot rankMuUe\_{UE2}(t)}{rrmSuSe\_{UE2}(t) \cdot rankSuUe\_{UE2}(t)} = \frac{2.7305 \cdot 2}{3.6094 \cdot 3} = 0.50$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14603.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
$rrmPrbBkFactor\_{UE3}(t) = \frac{rrmMuSe\_{UE3}(t) \cdot rankMuUe\_{UE3}(t)}{rrmSuSe\_{UE3}(t) \cdot rankSuUe\_{UE3}(t)} = \frac{2.4063 \cdot 2}{4.2129 \cdot 2} = 0.57$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14603.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmPrbBkFactor\_{UE4}(t) = \frac{rrmMuSe\_{UE4}(t) \cdot rankMuUe\_{UE4}(t)}{rrmSuSe\_{UE4}(t) \cdot rankSuUe\_{UE4}(t)} = \frac{3.9023 \cdot 2}{4.8164 \cdot 3} = 0.54$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14603.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$rrmPrbBkFactor\_{UES}(t) = \frac{rrmMuSe\_{UES}(t) \cdot rankMuUe\_{UES}(t)}{rrmSuSe\_{UES}(t) \cdot rankSuUe\_{UES}(t)} = \frac{4.5234 \cdot 2}{5.3320 \cdot 3} = 0.57$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14603.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
`rrmPrbBkFactorUE6(t) = 1.0`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14603.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] (No MU pairing for UE6)

rrmInstUePrbAlloc is equal to the product of the bookkeeping factor and the number of PRBs allocated to a UE in the MU-MIMO schedule.

## 3.29.13 Transport Block Size Calculation

### 3.29.13.0-1 Transport Block Size Calculation DMRS configuration type for DL transmission (ID: 11192062)

**3.29.13.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11192066`)

Transport Block Size Calculation
gNB-DU shall determine the DL DMRS configuration type for PDSCH transmission as below:

If PDSCH is scheduled by DCI 1\_0,

DL DMRS configuration type is type 1

Else

DL DMRS configuration type is given by DMRS-DownlinkConfig->drms-Type from latest configured RRC setup or reconfiguration.

Endif

---

### 3.29.13.0-2 Transport Block Size Calculation Transport block size (TBS) for DL transmissions (ID: 11192072)

**3.29.13.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11192094`)

Transport Block Size Calculation
The transport block size (TBS) computation for new DL transmissions is based on requirement from 5G\_UP\_IF\_1905\_replaced([11515773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUPmaPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using gNB-DU decision such as the MCS, rank for the transmission, and the number of PRBs.

The Time Domain Resources Allocation selected during the PDCCH scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4452\_replaced([11178134](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku0WnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_ALG\_4453\_replaced([11178188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku09pfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4455\_replaced([11178202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku09qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22376\_replaced([11178213](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku09qvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22377\_replaced([11178224](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku09rPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is also used to compute the TBS and also sent to the L1 in the DlData\_PdschSendReq.

**[CB008927-A] If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false,** [End CB008927-A] the number of PRBs for initial transmissions is the number of RBGs allocated to the UE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) times the RBG size *P (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) Note: First and last RBG might have different size.* *[CB008927-A] If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true, the number of FD resources is a contiguous number of PRBs with a startPrb and a number of PRBs (see RAT1 allocation in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* *[End CB008927-A]****\****\*

In case of retransmissions the same TBS is used as determined for the first transmission.

In case of  scheduling of Transient phase UE using RAT1 in the initial BWP(coreset #0), if the number of RBs used in retransmission is not same as RBs in initial transmission, then reserved MCS shall be used to schedule the UE  to avoid retransmission failure.

In case of retransmission in TDD FR1 with NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh set to True, for the eligible VoNR UEs as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14946\_replaced([11185337](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvSQp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), different number of PRBs are scheduled compared to initial transmission. For the ineligible users follow the rule define below instead.

For the case of TDD FR1 and NRCELL/actDlMuMimoEnh = True, if retransmission has different number of PRBs compared with initial transmission (for example, initial transmission uses last RBG and retransmission uses normal RBG, or vice versa **[CB008927-P] if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to FALSE, else the number of FD resources is a contiguous number of PRBs with a startPrb and a number of PRBs, when NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to TRUE )** [End CB008927-P], gNB-DU shall schedule PDSCH with reserved MCS and TBS size is from the initial transmission. If adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, UEs follow the rule defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11948\_replaced([11184286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) instead and reserved MCS could be used as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12911\_replaced([11184825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRpnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

For a FDD cell which is shared with LTE and actLteNrDssLteCatM equals true, in case of retransmission  for which number of  usable REs differs from the assignment in the initial transmission, effective N\_RE\_retx is calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2967\_replaced([11124515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). TBS size for retransmission shall be derived based on  N\_RE\_retx REs in the allocated PRBs. For such retransmission 5G-L2-PS shall send to L1 TBS value from the initial transmission (not the effective TBS size).

The value of the parameter nb\_cdm\_no\_data is derived in two steps as follows:

* **Step1: In 38.212, determine the right table (from 7.3.1.2.2-1 to 7.3.1.2.2-4) according to the selected DL DMRS configuration (using IE dlDmrsConfigType for *DL-DMRS-config-type and dlDmrsLen for \*DL-DMRS-max-len)***\*
* **Step2: In the right table,** according to the value of the ‘antenna port’ field in the DCI (i.e. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10502\_replaced([11171547](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuFWwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), derive nb\_cdm\_no\_data from the column “Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data”\*\*\*\*

nb\_cdm\_no\_data shall be determined with dlDmrsConfigType according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24898\_replaced([11192062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kviIT_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), dlDmrsLen = 1 and the antenna port value according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10502\_replaced([11171547](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuFWwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.29.13.0-3 Transport Block Size Calculation Transport block size (TBS) (ID: 11192099)

**3.29.13.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12728495`)

Transport Block Size Calculation
[Before CB008949-A] Note: this artifact is out of date, clean up

The TBS computation shall also take into account the number of layers not supported before (3 or 4) and 256 QAM ​​​​​. Please be refer to :

- ([11192394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvkkgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with tables description for TBS computation,

* For TBS for RACH msg2, modification of Ninfo due to the introduction of a scaling factor (see [11192394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvkkgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- ([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for parameters to send to 5G-L1-DL. [End CB008949-A]

**3.29.13.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11192201`)

Transport Block Size Calculation
11192201:
Transport Block Size Calculation
[Before CNI-103796-AA]

For DL TBS determination of PDSCH, the detailed configurations can bee seen in 5G\_UP\_IF\_1905\_replaced([11515773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUPmaPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This requirement provide the configurations of [image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14622.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14622.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1],  [image-2][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14622.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2], and V for TBS cacluation, where:

- [image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_14622.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]  stands for the number of PDSCH symbols including DMRS.

### 3.29.13.0-5 Transport Block Size Calculation RA-Msg2 (ID: 11192210)

**3.29.13.0-5.0-4**  (ID: `13405981`)

Transport Block Size Calculation
If the UE is scheduled for RA-Msg2, the transport block size is calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4622\_replaced([11192072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with:

 If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is set to FALSE, MCS = 0, **Else,** If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is set to TRUE, MCS = as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16531\_replaced([11152757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksh3lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

 [Before 05283414-26R1] ; If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is set to FALSE, **PRBs = size in PRB of the initial BWP defined 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1817\_replaced([11110897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (considering restrictions described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4463\_replaced([11178704](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),** **Else,** If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is set to TRUE, PRBs = number of PRBs as calculated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16542\_replaced([11179347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [05283414-26R1] The number of RBs allocated is computed according to the number of RAR to transmit or the number of RBs allowed in the CORESET(0) bandwidth and the number of PDSCH symbols defined by the TDRA selected (see **[11179347: FD resource calculation for UEs with RACH Msg2](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). [End 05283414-26R1]**

 Single layer (numOfLayers = 1).

 [05283414-26R1] the number of PDSCH symbols of the TDRA selected (see [13404901: RA-Msg2 symbol allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_MM2K8gylEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). [End 05283414-26R1]

  [Before 05283414-26R1]  ; 3 DMRS OFDM Symbol (to be compliant with 5G\_UP\_IF\_1973\_replaced([11516290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUUe5vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 3GPP requirement, dlDmrsAddPos=2). [05283414-26R1]  The number of additional DM-RS is linked to the start symbol, the length of the TDRA selected. [End 05283414-26R1]

 **DL DMRS configuration type 1 is used.**

 number of CDM groups without data (nb\_cdm\_no\_data) shall be set to 2.

 An additional scaling  factor is added , its value is set according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3423\_replaced([11152739](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksh3k_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If RA-Msg2 containing the UL grant for Msg3 is scheduled for a UE in a contention based random access procedure, the timer for contention resolution handling, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12681\_replaced([11152993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), shall be started in the slot which is scheduled for Msg3

---

### 3.29.13.0-6 Transport Block Size Calculation RA SRB0 message(Msg4) (ID: 11192229)

**3.29.13.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `11192244`)

Transport Block Size Calculation
If the UE is scheduled for RA SRB0 message/Msg4, the transport block size is calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4630\_replaced([11192270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvjWafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with

* MCS is determined as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18139\_replaced([11153435](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note for SRB0 message/Msg4, MCS is selected from 64QAM MCS table.
* If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is set to FALSE, PRBs = size in PRB of the initial BWP (indeed CORESET(0) size, as long as it is configured in the cell) defined 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1817\_replaced([11110897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), Else, PRBs = number of PRBs as calculated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16521\_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Single layer (numOfLayers = 1)
* 3 DMRS OFDM Symbol (to be compliant with 5G\_UP\_IF\_1973\_replaced([11516290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUUe5vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 3GPP requirement, dlDmrsAddPos=2).
* [CB008830-A] DL DMRS configuration type 1 is used [End CB008830-A]
* number of CDM groups without data (nb\_cdm\_no\_data) shall be set to 2.

---

### 3.29.13.0-7 Transport Block Size Calculation RA SRB1 message(Transient UE) (ID: 11192250)

**3.29.13.0-7.0-4**  (ID: `12464638`)

Transport Block Size Calculation
This requirement defines TBS calculation for below cases:

 For any PDSCH that is scheduled in the transient phase (which is corresponding to the period between receiving PsUser\_UserModifyReq and receiving PsUser\_UserReconfCompletedReq) and if any of the following configurations are updated in PsUser\_UserModifyReq:

o mcs-table

o maxNumOfDlLayers

o dmrsAdditionalPosition

o enableDlPtrs

o DL DMRS configuration type

 OR,**[Before CNI-137616-B] if *rdActCr3574 == 1 (5G\_L2\_1340\_replaced([11187360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28AqU_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-137616-B], for any PDSCH that is scheduled just after a beam failure recovery which is corresponding to the period from  the receiving time of Msg3 with CRNTI until the updated beam ID can be applied (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2032\_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22395\_replaced([11174363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).***

For above cases, PDSCH shall be scheduled with **DCI 1\_0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4463\_replaced([11178704](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the TBS shall be calculated with:**

 MCS proposed from LA input which is defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3045\_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3050\_replaced([11129035](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), final MCS  correction is defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4630\_replaced([11192270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvjWafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(MCS is selected from 64QAM MCS table):

 PRBs are granted within CORESET0. If /NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is set to TRUE, PRBs = number of PRBs as calculated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4502\_replaced([11179653](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 single layer

 dmrs-AdditionalPosition equals to 'pos2'

 DL DMRS configuration type 1 is used

 number of CDM groups without data (nb\_cdm\_no\_data) shall be set to 2

---

### 3.29.13.0-8 Transport Block Size Calculation Correction of the MCS for DL scheduling (ID: 11192270)

**3.29.13.0-8.0-10**  (ID: `13455034`)

Transport Block Size Calculation
With activation of the MCS reduction in DL by using the R&D parameter rdEnableMcsPrbReduction (see 5G\_L2\_21166\_replaced([11183340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iwR_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if the legacy FD scheduler is used or by using the R&D parameter rdEnableMcsPrbReductionFdm (see 5G\_L2\_1179\_replaced([11182832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27ee3_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if the FDM scheduler is used **for basic reliability, or by NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMcsDowngradeForHr is set to "enabled" or "enhanced" for high reliability, the scheduler adapts the DL MCS as follows:**

For SRB0 message/Msg4, following shall be performed regardless if rdEnableMcsPrbReduction or rdEnableMcsPrbReductionFdm is enabled/disabled in DL.

For DL slots, for which the transport block size (TBS) provides more transmission capacity than required by the buffered user data volume, the scheduler shall downgrade the DL MCS. The applied MCS shall allow the transmission of all buffered user data by minimizing the amount of padding bits.

If the maximum MCS step-down restriction is enabled by rdEnableDlLaMcsMaxReduction (5G\_UP\_ALG\_30231\_replaced([11194060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvx_8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then the DL MCS reduction will be limited to a maximum number of MCS steps specified by [Before 05601742-26R3] NRBTS/NRCELL/tmpParam19 (target NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMcsDowngradeMaxReduction)[05601742-26R3] NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMcsDowngradeMaxReduction. [End 05601742-26R3]

Please note: [Before 05601742-26R3] NRBTS/NRCELL/tmpParam19 [05601742-26R3] NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMcsDowngradeMaxReduction [End 05601742-26R3] set to 0 will switch off the buffer based MCS downgrading. A value of 28 will allow full buffer based MCS downgrading. Any value in between will limit the MCS downgrading accordingly.

For VoNR users configured with 5QI1/65/66 bearer for voice service rules defined hereafter shall be followed

If NRBTS/NRCELL.dlMcsDropLimitForVoNR is set to 0 // No MCS downgrade allowed

disable DL MCS downgrade for VoNR users with configured 5QI1/65/66 bearer

else // MCS downrade allowed

the DL MCS reduction will be limited to a maximum number of MCS steps specified by the parameter NRBTS/NRCELL.dlMcsDropLimitForVoNR. If rdEnableDlLaMcsMaxReduction is true, the applied limit is min{dlMcsDropLimitForVoNR, [Before 05601742-26R3] NRBTS/NRCELL/tmpParam19 (target NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMcsDowngradeMaxReduction) [05601742-26R3] NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMcsDowngradeMaxReduction. [End 05601742-26R3]}.

The below defined procedure to determine the downgraded MCS value shall be followed hereafter.

For UE works in high relilability mode shall be followed

If NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMcsDowngradeForHr is set to "disabled" // No MCS downgrade for HR allowed

disable DL MCS downgrade for HR users works in high reliability mode

else

The below defined procedure to determine the downgraded MCS value shall be followed hereafter.

In below MCS downgrade procedue, number of cdm groups without data shall be detemined as below for every MCS

If (PDSCH is scheduled by DCI 1\_0)

number of cdm groups without data is 2

else

if UE is configured to DL DMRS type 1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_24898\_replaced([11192062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kviIT_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

if ( (MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlMuMimo is true) and (dlDataDmrsFdmAllowed decided in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4405\_replaced([11142329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr3wSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is FALSE))

or (number of cdm groups without data determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3045\_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for rrmDlProposedMcs is 2) // if data DMRS FDM is disabled by link adaptation, then keep it disabled

number of cdm groups without data is 2

else

number of cdm groups without data is determined by MCS value according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3054\_replaced([11129150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

else // UE is configured to DL DMRS type 2 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_24898\_replaced([11192062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kviIT_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

if  (number of cdm groups without data determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3045\_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for rrmDlProposedMcs is 3)  // if data DMRS FDM is disabled by link adaptation, then keep it disabled

number of cdm groups without data is 3

else

number of cdm groups without data is determined by MCS value according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3054\_replaced([11129150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

If NRBTS.NRCELL.actRat1Alloc is set to true and if rdIsDlRat1McsDowngradeSkipping is set to true (see [13393891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Anr_1AZ9EfGozuGzRUXHpg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) , for PDSCH allocated with RAT1 DCI 1\_1 or dynamicswitch RAT1 allocation if NRBTS.NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true, gNB-DU shall skip the buffer based MCS downgrade procedure (see below rrmDlMcsAfterDowngrade conditions) when for a given MCS, the number of PRBs granted is above the max PRB size provided in the LUT tables in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32879\_replaced([11192320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvj9cvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: the parameter rdIsDlRat1McsDowngradeSkipping is set by default to false in order to disable the MCS downgrade skipping procedure for RAT1 allocation.

5G-L2-PS shall try to find the smallest MCS value rrmDlMcsAfterDowngrade which fulfills all of the following conditions:

* It is smaller than rrmDlProposedMcs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3045\_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **with downgrading limited up to NRBTS/NRCELL.dlMcsDropLimitForVoNR (when set to non-zero value) for VoNR users with active 5QI-1/65/66 bearer for voice service**
* For msg4/SRB0 message,  rrmDlProposedMcs =19.
* It is included in list of supported MCSes (see 5G\_L2\_642\_replaced([11164067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25qg9fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* With an allocation determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

it maps to a DL TB size rrmDlTbsAfterDowngrade larger than or equal to ***max(sum of bufferedBytes of all LGC* , rrmMinDlAllocationSize\_bytes)  + the total data volume needed for DL MAC CE. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4622\_replaced([11192072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the TBS mapping table. 5G-L2-PS shall assume one sub-header per MAC CE (see 5G\_L2\_1960\_replaced([11216443](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2--exPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the size of the MAC CE given in 5G\_L2\_1971\_replaced([11215683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-5mTfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**\*\*

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True, "all LGC" do not include non-GBR DRBs and deprioritized GBR DRBsof unconfigured slices or configured slices with 0 slice weight (rrmSliceWeight = 0, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If rrmBufferStatusSplitNeeded == 1, the sum of buffered bytes for CA UEs is according to the buffer status split as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2846\_replaced([11122312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqepKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* If the DL PRB sharing in subframe 0/5/9 is enabled (rrmLteDLPrbSharing=true, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4722\_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [PR880139] or (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true and any of used PRBs is within the LTE shared area ([12771248](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_5LKyJIjAEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) the [End PR880139] modulation order for rrmDlMcsAfterDowngrade shall be the same as the modulation order for rrmDlProposedMcs. This ensures that coding rate of Dl transport block rrmDlTbsAfterDowngrade does not exceed 0.95.
* Coding rate of Dl transport block rrmDlTbsAfterDowngrade does not exceed 0.95 for the slot which PDSCH is rate matching with CSI-RS for tracking.
* In case of initial transmission of PDSCH for high reliablity, if UE is configured with LowSE MCS table it shall be not higher than corresponding NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/pdschHrLaConfig/dllaMcsMax64qamLowSERANKxHR. if UE is configured with 64QAM MCS table and it shall be not higher than corresponding NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/pdschHrLaConfig/dllaMcsMax64qamRANKxHR. x value is the UE RANK.

If such a MCS value is found 5G-L2-PS shall set in the HARQ process:

* rrmDlUsedMcs = rrmDlMcsAfterDowngrade.
* tbSize = rrmDlTbsAfterDowngrade.
* cdmGroupsWithoutData= number of cdm groups without data for rrmDlMcsAfterDowngrade.

Otherwise,

For Msg4/SRB0, if payload can’t fit, the gNB-DU shall skip the Msg4/SRB0 scheduling on this slot (due to Msg4/SRB0 can’t be segmented).

5G-L2-PS shall set in the HARQ process:

* rrmDlUsedMcs = rrmDlProposedMcs.
* Keep the current value of tbSize  and number of cdm groups without data.

The MCS/PRB reduction of Msg5 can be activated/deactivated by a dedicated bit of rdEnableMcsPrbReduction or rdEnableMcsPrbReductionFdm and shall follow the same rules as defined above when activated.

After the MCS/PRB reduction, if NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdPolite = “true”, gNB-DU shall adapt rrmDlUsedMcs and tbSize via FD-POLITE  PRB widening processing in [12439014](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_CO0dEgiSEfCT35inaBGo7g) or, if NRBTS/NRCELL/actEpic is enabled, this adaptation shall be done after FD-POLITE EPIC algorithm (detailed in [12533532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_08efoBxNEfCQJ5daVHVkgg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) instead.

Whenever the scheduler downgrades the MCS, an indication shall be sent to the DL link adaptation.

The DL LA does the evaluation of rrmDlUsedMcs to allow for adequate evaluation of HARQ feedback for the OLLA, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3060\_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L2-PS shall include the following parameters in the TTI trace:

* rrmDlProposedMcs
* rrmDlUsedMcs

---

### 3.29.13.0-9 Transport Block Size Calculation LUT tables for PRB threshold versus MCS indexes to skip MCS downgrade for RAT1 allocations (ID: 11192320)
